Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Flappy. Flappy was a very special bird, because he had a very special tail. His tail was made of colourful feathers and it always waved in the wind.

One day Flappy and his friends had a picnic at the park. Flappy was so excited! He flew around and played while the others were eating their lunch.

Suddenly, Flappy saw something interesting! He flew down to take a closer look. It was a pot full of beautiful feathers! Flappy kept looking and saw something else. It was a big spoon!

Flappy hopped closer and saw that the spoon was stirring the feathers inside the pot. He watched for a few moments and then he had an exciting idea. Flappy flew into the pot, grabbed a feather and hopped out. He waved it in the wind and it made a cool sound.

Flappy and his friends had a lot of fun with the feather. It was very interesting! They spent the rest of the day playing and stirring the feathers in the pot.

Jake was very adventurous. He saw an exit and he wanted to explore it. He asked his mom if he could go and she said it was okay.

Jake excitedly went to the exit. He collected some sticks and leaves. He also found a beautiful butterfly.

He put all of his collections into his pocket and went back to his mom. She was so happy to see all of his collections.

Jake had such a great time. He was so happy to be able to explore and collect things. He couldn't wait for his next adventurous day!

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play catch with a ball. They throw the ball to each other and catch it with their hands. Sometimes they miss and the ball falls on the ground. Then they run to get it and start again.

One day, they play catch in the park. There are many people and animals in the park. Anna throws the ball to Ben, but Ben does not catch it. The ball flies over his head and lands near a stand. The stand has many flowers and pots. A lady is selling the flowers and pots. She is not happy to see the ball near her stand.

She picks up the ball and shouts at Anna and Ben. She says they are bad and careless. She says they could break her flowers and pots. She says she will not give them back the ball. She puts the ball in a big pot and turns away.

Anna and Ben are sad and scared. They want their ball back. They say they are sorry and they did not mean to hurt the lady or her stand. They ask her to be kind and compassionate. They say they will be more careful next time.

The lady hears them and looks at them. She sees their tears and their sorry faces. She feels sorry for them too. She remembers that she was once a child who liked to play catch. She decides to be compassionate and give them back the ball. She takes the ball out of the pot and hands it to Anna and Ben. She tells them to be more careful and to have fun. She smiles and they smile too.

Anna and Ben thank the lady and hug her. They take their ball and run to a different place in the park. They play catch again, but they watch out for people and animals and stands. They are happy and grateful. They are friends.

Today was a fun day. Momma and Daddy took Sam to the park. Sam ran around and fit all the way there. He fit on the swings and the slides too.

When Sam got hungry, Momma gave him a tasty snack. He loved the apples. They were so sweet and juicy.

Next, they fit in a big sandbox. Sam filled up a bucket with sand and then carried it around. He made towers and filled up big trucks.

After the park, they went back home. Sam was so tired. He ate dinner and it was the best food ever. It was so yummy and tasty.

Today was a fun day and Sam fit in a lot of fun activities.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful little girl. She wanted to do something nice for her family, so she decided to make muffins. She gathered all the ingredients, read the recipe and got to work.

She performed each step carefully, adding flour, milk and eggs together until she had a smooth batter. She poured the batter into muffin pans and put them into the oven.

The muffins baked in the oven, making the house smell really good. Finally, the timer dinged and the muffins were ready. She carefully took them out of the oven, and they smelled delicious!

The little girl was so pleased with her muffins that she shared them with her family. Everyone was so happy and grateful for her helpful work. The muffins were a delicious treat, and it was a wonderful family moment!

Lila and Ben were playing outside with their toys. They liked to make roads and bridges with the sand and the stones. They had a lot of fun together.

But then, the sky became dark and grey. They heard a loud noise. It was thunder. They saw a bright light. It was lightning. They felt something wet on their faces. It was rain.

"Let's go inside, Lila!" Ben said. "The rain is too much. It can hurt us."

"No, Ben!" Lila said. "The rain is fine. It is just water. It is not bad. It is a gift from the spirit."

"What spirit?" Ben asked.

"The spirit of the sky. He makes the rain and the thunder and the lightning. He is very powerful and kind. He gives us water to drink and to grow. He cleans the air and the earth. He makes the flowers and the grass and the trees happy. He is our friend." Lila said.

Ben looked at Lila. He was not sure. He was scared of the noise and the light. He wanted to go inside and be safe and warm.

But he also saw that Lila was not scared. She was smiling and laughing. She opened her arms and spun around. She caught the raindrops with her tongue. She looked happy and free.

Ben thought for a moment. He decided to try. He opened his arms and spun around. He caught the raindrops with his tongue. He felt the water on his skin and hair. He felt a tickle in his belly. He started to smile and laugh.

He looked at Lila. She looked at him. They both giggled. They felt the spirit of the sky in their hearts. They felt fine.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Anna. She was very brave and always wanted to explore. One day, she decided to take a walk in the forest.

As Anna was walking, she saw a big tree and started to walk towards it. Suddenly, she felt something slippery under her feet, and she slipped and fell on the ground.

Anna was very upset and she started to cry. She was scared and thought she would never be able to get back home. But then something magical happened.

A small group of friendly forest animals came to help her. They helped Anna to get back up and took her back home.

From then on, Anna always made sure to be careful when walking in the forest.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and catch butterflies. One day, she saw a farmer harvesting wheat in the field. She asked the farmer what he was doing, and he explained that he was collecting the wheat to make bread.

Lily thought it would be fun to catch some wheat too. She tried to grab a handful, but it was too hard and inflexible. The farmer showed her how to gently hold the wheat and flex it to make it easier to catch.

Lily learned that being gentle and flexible can help you catch things better. She also learned that wheat is an important ingredient in making bread, which is a delicious and nutritious food. From that day on, Lily made sure to be gentle and flexible in everything she did, and she always appreciated the value of wheat.

Once upon a time, there was a group of friends. They were playing hide and seek in the park. It was getting dark outside and they knew it was time to end the game.

One of the friends said, "I want to play more. Can we play one more time?"

The other friends said, "No, it's too dark and we need to go home. We can play again tomorrow."

The friend who wanted to play more felt sad, but knew it was time to end the game. They all said goodbye and went home. The end.

Lily and Max were going on a safari with their mom and dad. They were very excited to see the animals. They packed their hats, water, and snacks in a big bag.

On the way to the safari park, Max was grumpy. He did not want to share his snacks with Lily. He said, "These are mine! Go away!" Lily was sad. She wanted to have some snacks too. She said, "That's not nice, Max. We are supposed to share."

Mom and dad heard them fighting. They said, "Stop it, both of you. We are almost there. You can have your snacks later. Now, look out the window and see the animals."

Lily and Max looked out the window. They saw zebras, giraffes, elephants, and lions. They forgot about their snacks. They said, "Wow! Look at them! They are so big and beautiful!"

They got to the safari park. They got out of the car and followed a guide. The guide showed them more animals and told them stories. Lily and Max listened and asked questions. They learned a lot.

At the end of the safari, they went back to the car. They were hungry and thirsty. They remembered their snacks. They looked at each other and smiled. They said, "Sorry, we were grumpy. Do you want to share?" They nodded and hugged. They opened their bag and shared their snacks. They were happy. They had a fun day.

Once there was a little girl who was feeling embarrassed. She was embarrassed because she didn't know how to get to the barn. Everyone was already there and it seemed like she was the only one who didn't know.
She looked around to find someone who could help her. Suddenly, she saw a scary looking man walking towards her. The little girl was very scared as he came closer. She didn't know what to do.
The man smiled and said, "I can help you get to the barn. It's not too far away, let's go!" The little girl was so relieved and followed the man to the barn. When they arrived, everyone clapped to welcome her. She was so happy and no longer felt embarrassed.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. He loved to ride his bike in the park. One day, he saw his friend Billy and they decided to ride together. They rode and rode until they saw a big tree. Timmy wanted to look up at the tree, but he couldn't see the top.

Suddenly, Timmy fell off his bike and hurt his knee. He was upset and started to cry. Billy asked him, "Are you okay?" Timmy shook his head and said, "No, it hurts." Billy helped him up and they walked to a nearby bench. Timmy's mom came over and gave him a band-aid. He felt much better and they all laughed and played together. The end.

One day, a little girl named Lucy was playing in her backyard. She was having lots of fun in the dirt. Lucy saw a bug, so she bent down and picked up some soil. She threw it in the air and then she kicked it. It flew really high! She laughed and laughed.

The noise was very loud, and her mom came to the door to see what was going on. She saw Lucy kicking the soil and said, "No, Lucy! Don't kick the soil!"

Lucy stopped. She looked at the soil and then at her mom. She said "OK" and put the soil back on the ground. She promised her mom that she would not kick it again.

Then, she started playing with some toys. She was having lots of fun! Lucy forgot all about the soil, but she always remembered to be careful with it - and not to kick it again.

Once upon a time there was a small boy called Peter. Peter loved to eat squash. He went to his garden to pick the best squashes. He found a big, dry squash so he decided to take it home for lunch.

Peter took the squash to the kitchen and washed it carefully. Then he cut it into small pieces with a knife. He put all the pieces into a pan and added some salt and pepper.

Next he put the pan on the stove and cooked the squash until it was soft. He stirred it again and again until everything tasted delicious.

At last he took the pan out of the stove and ate his squash. He loved it! Every bite tasted like sunshine.

The End

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sarah. Sarah was three-years-old and very curious. She loved to explore the world around her.

One day, she went out for a walk with her mommy. They went through a field of tall grass and saw a beautiful landscape. Sarah fell in love with it and looked at it with an adorable expression.

Sarah turned to her mommy and said, "Mommy, why do we not have a landscape like this at home?"

Her mommy smiled and said, "We can get one, Sarah, but we need to study a little bit first. We need to know how to take care of it before we can have one of our own."

Sarah was excited and started to study the landscape some more. Every day she went out with her mommy and learned something new. After some time, they were ready to have a landscape of their own at home.

When they got home, Sarah was thrilled to see the beautiful and adorable landscape they had created. She ran around it, playing and laughing.

And they all lived happily ever after.

Dave was a creative three-year-old boy who was always on the move. One day, he went to his dad's office. He looked around and noticed all the interesting things in the room. He started to roll around the office, having fun exploring the big room. Then he rolled to the big desk and saw a jar of pens and paper. He started to roll the pens around on the desk, feeling creative.

Suddenly, the door to the office opened and Dave's daddy shouted, "What are you doing?" Dave just looked scared. His dad said, "You must not roll here, this is my office now go to your room!" Dave started to cry and rolled to his bedroom.

That day, Dave felt horrible. He did something naughty and got in trouble. He realized he should not have rolled around the office.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who really wanted an automobile. She kept asking her mom and dad for one, but they just kept telling her no. She was very sad and angry about that.

One day, Lily and her parents were walking in the park. Suddenly, she saw a bright red automobile by the lake. She was so happy and excited that she started running towards it.

"Don't act silly, Lily," said her Dad.

But she didn't listen and kept running towards the car. When she reached it, she was surprised to see a man sitting in the driver's seat. Suddenly, that man started and drove away quickly.

Lily was very scared, but her Dad was there to help her. He hugged her tight and reassured her that everything was okay. He told her she always has to be careful and think before acting.

Lily felt better hearing her Dad's words. Even though the car was gone, she was still happy because she was safe with her Dad. She hugged him and they started walking back home, feeling happy together.

Ben was three years old and he loved to organize things. One day he found his dad's old poem book. It was weird and he wanted to organize it.

He went to his mom and asked, "Mom, can I organize the poems?

"Yes, why not? she said. "It'll make your dad happy.

So Ben took the book and started to organize it. The poems were strange and exciting. He read them over and over again.

When he was done, Ben gave the book to his dad. His dad was so happy to see how Ben had organized the poems.

"Thanks Ben, he said. "I love it! This is so cool.

Ben was proud of what he had done and he was happy to make his dad proud.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Molly and Lucy, who loved to play with their soft toys. One day, Lucy was looking for the perfect match for her favourite toy, a blue teddy bear. She searched everywhere but couldn't find the right one.

Molly then noticed and said, "Don't worry Lucy, I have a match for your teddy bear!"

Lucy was delighted and replied, "Really? Where is it?"

Molly smiled and said, "I knew it would be a perfect match when I saw it. Come with me and I'll show you."

So, the two friends went off together, hand in hand, and after a short walk in the park, they reached a special shop. Inside, there was an amazing selection of toys and Lucy could hardly contain her excitement.

Molly then showed Lucy a soft, yellow teddy bear that was the perfect match for her own blue one. Molly and Lucy both smiled, and united their two teddy bears together. From that day on, the two best friends were inseparable.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Max. One day, Max saw a broken cloud in the sky. He wanted to have it, but he could not reach it. So he hopped and he flapped his wings until he was floating high up in the sky. Max was so happy! He laughed and clapped his hands.

Max flew closer and closer to the broken cloud. When he was right in front of it, he jumped right in. He was lost in the broken cloud. It was so big and fluffy. Max looked around him and enjoyed the clouds.

But then something happened. Suddenly the wind blew and the broken cloud was no longer broken. It was together again. Max looked all around him, and he was happy. He had his own cloud! He sang and danced and he was so excited.

And then Max flew away, and he never forgot his broken cloud.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys, but he didn't like taking baths. His mom would always tell him to wash his hair with shampoo, but Timmy thought it was yucky.

One day, Timmy's mom noticed that his hair was getting very messy and she decided to give him a bath. Timmy didn't want to take a bath, so he was being very naughty and refused to get in the tub.

His mom tried to convince him by showing him his favorite toy on display in the bathroom, but Timmy still didn't want to take a bath. Finally, his mom had an idea. She put some bubbles in the tub and made it into a game. Timmy loved playing with bubbles, so he got in the tub and washed his hair with shampoo.

After the bath, Timmy's hair was clean and shiny. He was happy that he took a bath and he even thanked his mom for making it fun. From that day on, Timmy didn't mind taking baths anymore.

Once upon a time there was a wise owl who loved to carry spaghetti. Every day, he gathered as much spaghetti as he could carry and flew away. As he flew, he would drop some of the spaghetti in different places. He wanted to share it with everyone!

One day, he noticed three little birds on the ground. They were huddling together and they were very cold. So the wise owl decided to drop all of his spaghetti down to the three little birds. They clapped their hands together with delight!

The three little birds thanked the wise owl and everyone had a happy spaghetti feast! From that day on, the wise owl carried spaghetti every day and spread lots of happiness. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a fat princess. She lived in a tall castle, with high walls around it. Every day, she would look out of her window and admire her kingdom.

But one day, the princess noticed something strange. All the trees and animals were walking backwards! Even the waves on the lake reversed themselves. She was so surprised and she wanted to know what had happened.

The princess asked the gardener, but he had no idea. Then she asked the cook, but he too was baffled. In the end, the princess decided to find out the mystery herself.

So the princess grabbed her long cloak and stepped out of her castle. She walked around her kingdom but couldn't find an answer. Suddenly, she saw a wise old owl perched on a nearby tree.

"Oh, wise owl," the princess said. "Why is everything walking backwards?"

The wise owl smiled and said, "My dear princess, you have used the magical power of reverse. Everything knows the princess' royal command and will always obey it!"

The princess was so happy! From that day on, she used the magical power of reverse as she liked. She even used it to make her kingdom more beautiful than ever before.

The end.

It was a bright and sunny day. Johnny was out with his dad and they had come to the swamp. Dad wanted to show Johnny the animals and plants that lived in the swamp. Johnny was excited but also a bit scared.

Dad took Johnny to the edge of the swamp, where the water was still and calm. But soon the calm water started to get rougher, and dad said to be careful. Dad pointed out all the different animals and explained why they chose to live in the swamp.

Suddenly, dad stopped and said it was time to raise their voices and make some noise. Johnny was confused, but then he heard something: a loud splash coming from the swamp! Dad had warned him it was mating season, and sure enough there were two big alligators raising a little baby!

Johnny couldn't believe his eyes and he was so excited. Dad looked proud, and told Johnny that it was ok to be scared sometimes, but it was also ok to be curious and learn about nature. Johnny nodded and said that he wanted to come back to the swamp soon.

Once upon a time, there was an amazing otter. He liked to explore and was always curious. One day he was swimming around looking at all of the new things he could find. Then suddenly he heard a loud sound. It scared him and he swam away as fast as he could.

He rushed around a corner until he found a cave. The otter popped his head in and he saw something he had never seen before. There were little fish swimming around and the water sparkled in the sunlight. Then suddenly he heard a noise. It was almost like singing. It was so amazing!

The otter looked around the cave and he saw something he had never seen before. There was a family of otters singing and playing together. The otter was so happy and he joined in with the singing and playing.

They all had so much fun. After a while, the sun went down and the otter swam back home. He had had a really amazing day!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing in the snow. One day, she saw two boys fighting in the snow. She felt scared and didn't know what to do.

She went up to the boys and said, "Please don't fight. It's not nice."

The boys listened to Lily and stopped fighting. They apologized to each other and started playing in the snow together. Lily was happy that she could help make peace. From that day on, Lily knew that she could make a difference by using her words instead of being scared.

Sam was a little dog with short white hair. He had a big nose and he was very curious.

One day he decided to take a walk in the woods. As he was walking he noticed something shiny in the snow. It was an icy crystal. Sam was fascinated and he decided to pick it up.

When he touched the icy crystal he heard a sound. Someone was talking! It said "Touch me and I will be your friend.

Sam was scared, but he wanted to make a new friend. He put his nose closer and he said "Hello?

Then, a very friendly voice answered "Hello, Sam! I am the icy crystal. I can help you find the lost numbers in your skin. Would you like that?

Sam was so excited to find out that the icy crystal could help him find the lost numbers! He smiled and he said "Yes, I would like that!

From that day, Sam and the icy crystal became very good friends and they went on many adventures together.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little mouse named Max. Max loved to sing and dance. One day, Max practiced a song for his friends. He sang so well that his friends applauded him.

Max felt very happy when he heard his friends clapping. He took a bow and said "thank you" to them. His friends then gave him a note that said "we love your singing, Max". Max felt even happier when he read the note.

From that day on, Max practiced his singing even more. He wanted to make his friends happy and hear them applaud him again. And when he sang for them again, they did!

Ben and Mia liked to play with magnets. They had many magnets of different shapes and colors. They liked to stick them on the fridge, on the door, on the table. They liked to see how the magnets pulled or pushed each other.

One day, they found a new magnet in their toy box. It was a ring magnet. It was shiny and silver. It looked very modern. Ben and Mia wondered what it could do.

"Let's try it on the fridge," Ben said.

"OK," Mia agreed.

They ran to the kitchen and put the ring magnet on the fridge. It stuck very well. They tried to pull it off, but it was hard.

"Wow, this is a strong magnet," Ben said.

"Maybe it can stick to other things too," Mia said.

They looked around the room and saw a phone on the counter. It was their mom's phone. It was also shiny and silver. It looked very modern too.

"Let's try it on the phone," Mia said.

"OK," Ben agreed.

They took the ring magnet and put it on the phone. It stuck very well too. They tried to pull it off, but it was harder.

"Wow, this is a very strong magnet," Mia said.

But then, something strange happened. The phone started to make a loud noise. It was ringing. Ben and Mia were scared. They did not know what to do.

"Mom, mom, help!" they shouted.

Their mom came running from the living room. She saw the phone and the magnet. She looked angry.

"Ben, Mia, what have you done?" she asked.

She took the phone and the magnet. She tried to separate them, but it was very hard. She had to use a lot of force. She finally managed to pull them apart.

She looked at the phone and saw that it was broken. The screen was cracked. The buttons were stuck. The sound was distorted.

She looked at Ben and Mia and said, "This was a very bad idea. You should never put magnets on phones. Magnets can damage phones. Phones are not toys. They are important and expensive. You have to be careful and respectful with them. Do you understand?"

Ben and Mia nodded. They felt sorry and ashamed. They did not mean to break the phone. They did not know that magnets could hurt phones. They just wanted to play and have fun.

They said, "We are sorry, mom. We did not know. We will not do it again. We love you."

Their mom sighed. She was still angry, but she also loved them. She said, "I love you too. But you have to be more responsible and sensible. You have to learn from your mistakes. And you have to help me fix the phone. Come on, let's go to the store and see what we can do."

Jack was walking on the farm. Suddenly, he heard a noise. He looked around and saw a big stable full of animals. He moved closer and noticed a small horse. It was slow and gentle.

Jack: Hello little horse!

Little Horse: Moo!

Jack smiled and petted the horse. He wanted to stay with it, but he had to move on. He said goodbye to the little horse and moved towards the sun that was setting in the horizon.

Dave was playing outside when he heard a loud noise and looked around. A bee flew right over his head and he watched it fly away. He smiled and started running after it, but he knew he couldn't catch it. He kept running until he couldn't see the bee anymore.

Suddenly, he heard a loud noise again. This time it was a loud bang and Dave stopped running. He looked around and saw his Dad shooting. His Dad had a gun and he was shooting insects with it. Dave watched as the insects fell from the sky.

He felt scared and he wanted to go to his Dad, but he was too scared to move. His Dad finished shooting and smiled at Dave. He said, "It's okay. You can come here. I won't shoot any more insects."

Dave felt relieved and he ran to his Dad. Together they watched the insects fly away and Dave was happy that the loud noises were gone.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was brave and fun. One day, she was playing outside with her friends when something scary happened.

She saw a big, dark card floating through the sky. It was so big and so scary! She was so scared she ran to her house and hid under her bed.

Her mom found her and asked what had happened. The little girl told her mom about the card. Mom said "That's okay, sweetie. You don't have to be scared. It's just a card...

The little girl felt better after hearing her mom's words. She looked out the window and there it was - the card had flown away. The little girl was so happy and she ran outside to play with her friends again.

The End!

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Millie. She had long brown hair and liked to rub it when she was feeling sleepy. Millie had a big friend named Bob.

One day they decided to go to the park. Millie was so excited to go and asked her friend to count with her.

Bob said, "1,2,3,4,5,6,7, let's go!"

Millie rubbed her hair and said, "Yes, let's go!"

Once they arrived at the park, Millie and Bob counted all of the trees. They counted and counted until they were very sleepy.

Millie said, "Let's go home now and take a nap. We can count again tomorrow!"

Bob nodded his head and said, "Yes, let's go home."

So Millie and Bob went home and quickly fell asleep. They dreamed of counting things all night long. The end!

Anna and Ben are playing hide and seek in the kitchen. Anna is the seeker and Ben is the hider. Ben looks for a good place to hide. He sees the fridge and opens it. He sees a big bottle of milk and thinks it is a good hiding spot. He climbs into the fridge and closes the door behind him. He hopes Anna will not find him.

Anna counts to ten and then says, "Ready or not, here I come!" She looks around the kitchen and tries to find Ben. She does not see him under the table, behind the curtains, or in the cabinets. She wonders where he is. She hears a small noise from the fridge. She walks to the fridge and opens it. She sees Ben sitting in the fridge, holding the bottle of milk. She laughs and says, "I found you, Ben! You are hiding in the fridge with the milk!"

Ben feels embarrassed. He did not think Anna would find him so easily. He also feels cold and wet. The milk has spilled on his clothes and hair. He says, "I'm sorry, Anna. I thought it was a good hiding spot. But it was not. It was a bad hiding spot. And now I'm cold and wet and sticky."

Anna says, "It's okay, Ben. Don't be embarrassed. It was a funny hiding spot. But not a smart one. Come on, let's get you out of the fridge and clean you up. And then we can play another game. Maybe you can be the seeker and I can be the hider. And I will not hide in the fridge with the milk."

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Millie. She was three years old and lived on a farm with her parents. Millie was very curious and often asked a lot of questions. One day, Millie asked her dad what a teaspoon was.

"Well Millie," said her dad, "a teaspoon is a small spoon that we use to scoop up things and measure ingredients when we are cooking."

Millie was so curious that she decided to go on an adventure around the farm. She was looking for a teaspoon. She guessed that there were many hiding on the farm somewhere.

So Millie roamed around all day, searching for a teaspoon. The farm was so big and Millie searched in all the different places, but she couldn't find any teaspoon.

Finally, at the end of the day, Millie stopped and sat down outside a old, rich house. As she was sitting there, she spotted a large silver teaspoon on a table in the house.

Millie jumped up with joy and ran over to the house. She reached out to grab the teaspoon but a woman came out of the house and said, "What are you doing here little girl?"

Millie told the woman why she was there and the woman smiled kindly and said, "I am happy to give this teaspoon to you. It was given to me by my grandmother and I think it would be nice for you to have it

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Benny. Benny loved to play outside in the forest, but he was always scared of the big animals that lived there. One day, Benny found a magazine that had a picture of a strong and brave bear on it. Benny wanted to be like the bear and protect himself from the scary animals.

Benny asked his mommy to help him make a tight cape just like the bear's. His mommy made it for him and Benny felt very brave wearing it. When he went outside to play, he saw a big wolf coming towards him. Benny remembered what he learned from the magazine and put on his cape, feeling very strong.

The wolf tried to scare Benny, but Benny stood tall and protected himself with his cape. The wolf ran away and Benny felt very proud of himself. From that day on, Benny knew that he didn't have to be scared anymore. He had his tight cape to protect him and he felt very brave. The moral of the story is that we can protect ourselves by being brave and using the things we have.

Tuesday was a sunny day. Mum and Dad took Alex outside to go for a walk in the park. Alex saw lots of birds and he wanted to feed them some bread. Dad said, "Let's make a humble request to the birds," and he put his hands together and bowed his head. Alex did the same.

Dad said, "Please birds, would you like some bread?" One of the birds flew close to them and and accepted the request! Alex was excited and said, "Me too, me too!"

Dad gave some bread to the bird and then he shared some with Alex. Alex had so much fun feeding the birds that he asked to do it on Tuesdays from then on.

The family smiled. "That's a lovely request, Alex," said Mum. They agreed to go to the park every Tuesday to feed the birds.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who wanted to go and play outside. She looked out the window, but the sky seemed very gloomy. She begun to get a bit sad, so she decided to peek around and search for something fun to do. Then she saw it! It was a big mug on the floor!

She quickly ran over to it and picked it up. She immediately saw that it was empty, so she decided to go search for something to put inside. She carefully opened the cupboard door and peeked inside. A big smile appeared on her face. She had found something to put into the mug!

The little girl carefully took out the shiny stones and filled the mug. She put the mug on the floor and began to throw the stones around. She laughed and giggled as they sparkled and danced around the room.

She knew the gloomy skies outside would not stop her from having a fun day after all.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jane. She loved to play in the backyard with her friends. One day, she and her friends wanted to have a conversation. Jane could hear everyone perfectly, but her best friend Sarah could not. Sarah was deaf.

Jane wanted to help her friend. She realized that mail was a way for people to communicate without having to hear or talk. So she wrote a letter to Sarah and sent it off in the mail.

When Sarah received the letter, she was so excited! She couldn't believe Jane had thought of it. She wrote back and sent it back in the mail.

Back and forth they wrote letters. Jane and Sarah could now talk even though they lived far apart. They realized they could write letters to each other every day!

Since then, Jane and Sarah have been the best of friends. They wrote letters to each other all the time, even when they lived far away. They were so happy that they figured out how to communicate without hearing or talking.

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Lily. She loved to play her flute in the park. One day, as she was playing her flute, a young boy came up to her.

"Can I poke your flute?" he asked.

Lily giggled and said, "Sure, but be gentle."

The boy poked the flute and listened to the beautiful sound it made. "Wow, that's so cool!" he exclaimed.

Lily smiled and said, "Thank you! Do you want to learn how to play the flute too?"

The young boy nodded excitedly and Lily taught him how to play a simple tune. They played together and had a lot of fun. From that day on, they became good friends and would often play their flutes together in the park.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Mary and Joe. Mary and Joe wanted to see a new view. They decided to follow a path and see what it could show them.

They followed the path for a long time until they came to a big hill. Joe wanted to go up the hill, but Mary was a bit scared. She thought it looked a bit too serious.

Joe encouraged her to come with him. He promised that they wouldn't go too high and that they would have a great view. So, they decided to go and climbed the hill together.

At the top, the friends were happy to see the beautiful view. All around them they could see trees, flowers and birds. Even though they were a bit scared, they both agreed it was worth it!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sarah. She was very excited, because her mom said that today they were going to the park. It sounded like so much fun!

When Sarah and her mom arrived at the park, Sarah was amazed. She ran around and played on the swings and the slides for hours.

Before she knew it, Sarah had lost her thumb! She started to cry, but then she saw her mom. Sarah's mom hugged her tightly and wiped away her tears.

"Mommy, I've lost my thumb! said Sarah.

"Don't worry! said her mom. "It's safe here in the park. We'll find it soon.

So Sarah and her mom started to look for Sarah's thumb. After a few minutes, mom found it in the grass. Sarah was very happy - now she was safe with her thumb once again!

Sara and Tom were friends who liked to play with their toys. One day, they found a whip in the garage. It was long and black and had a handle. Sara thought it was pretty and wanted to try it.

"Can I have the whip, Tom?" Sara asked.

"No, it's mine. I found it first," Tom said.

"Please, Tom. Just for a little bit. I'll give it back to you," Sara begged.

"Fine, but only for a minute. Don't delay, or I'll be angry," Tom said.

Sara took the whip and ran outside. She swung it in the air and made a loud noise. She felt powerful and happy. She forgot about Tom and his warning.

Tom waited and waited for Sara to come back. He got bored and angry. He decided to go outside and get his whip back. He saw Sara playing with the whip and not paying attention to him.

"Hey, Sara! Give me back my whip! You delayed too long!" Tom shouted.

Sara turned around and saw Tom. She was scared and sorry. She tried to give him back the whip, but it was too late. Tom was so mad that he grabbed the whip and hit Sara with it. Sara cried out in pain and dropped the whip. She had a red mark on her arm where the whip hit her.

Tom felt bad and sorry. He didn't mean to hurt Sara. He just wanted his whip back. He picked up the whip and ran back to the garage. He hid the whip and hoped no one would find it.

Sara ran back to her house. She had tears in her eyes and blood on her arm. She told her mom what happened. Her mom was angry and sad. She called Tom's mom and told her what happened. Tom's mom was angry and sad too. She scolded Tom and took away his toys. She made him apologize to Sara and say sorry.

Sara and Tom were not friends anymore. They learned a hard lesson. They learned that whips are not toys. They learned that sharing is better than fighting. They learned that delaying can cause trouble. They learned that pretty things can be dangerous.

So they went inside and began to sort the toys. It was very fun and took a lot of time, but eventually all the toys were in the right places.

Then the mommy said, "I have one more thing for you to sort. It is a hoop!"

Sue was so happy and she started putting the hoop into different shapes. She was so excited. She made a circle, a square, a triangle and a diamond.

At the end of the day, Sue felt proud because she had sorted her hoop very slowly and carefully. She was really proud of her work.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Bob and Ben. They wanted to play together. But when they went outside, they saw a big, glistening puddle. Bob was jealous and frowned. He didn't want Ben to have all the fun.

Bob made an idea. He asked Ben if he could bring a bucket. Ben agreed and they went inside. Ben came back with a big yellow bucket. Bob filled it up with the water from the puddle.

The two friends ran back outside with the bucket. They had so much fun splashing in the water, laughing and smiling.

But Bob still felt jealous of Ben's fun. He frowned again and didn't join in. Ben felt bad for leaving his friend out and asked Bob to join in. Bob smiled and happily joined the game.

The two friends had lots of fun playing together in the puddle. They laughed and smiled until their parents called them inside.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sarah. She loved to explore the world around her. One day Sarah decided to go for a walk.

As Sarah was walking she saw a man in the distance. He was not moving at all. The man seemed to be frozen. Sarah knew that this was not normal so she decided to take a closer look.

When Sarah got closer to the man she noticed the look on his face was dull. He was not moving like the other people that she had seen walking around. She knew that something was wrong and felt very scared.

Sarah wished that the man could move again, so she started to clap her hands together. To her surprise the man started to move again. The man said thank you and gave Sarah a big hug.

Sarah was so happy that she was able to help the man. From then on whenever Sarah was scared she would clap her hands together, just in case!

One day he saw something special. A big white bird flew over the sea and hovered above the man. The man was amazed! He wanted to talk to the bird so he whispered to it.

The bird heard him and flew lower. It flew around the man's head and looked into his eyes. The man smiled. He waved goodbye to the bird as it flew away.

The man never forgot his special day. Every morning he went to the beach and whispered to the sky. Maybe the bird would visit him again...

The bully said 'What do you want?!' The nut said 'I want to help the ball'.

The bully said 'No! I'm kicking the ball and you can't stop me!' The bully kicked the ball again and again, making the nut so sad.

The nut was brave and honest, so he tried to stop the bully. The bully didn't like that so he kicked the nut very hard and the nut rolled away. The nut was so sad, but he never got to help the ball. The bad bully had won.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful ant. He liked to hang around and see what he could find. One day, he saw an apple hanging in a tree. The apple looked so juicy and yummy, he wanted to get it! But the apple was very high up in the tree and he couldn't reach it. Suddenly, he saw a helpful bird. The bird said, "I can help you. I can fly up to the tree and get the apple". The ant was so glad. He thanked the bird and hopped up and down with joy. The helpful bird flew up to the tree and brought the apple back down. The ant was so happy to get the apple that he shared it with his friends. Everyone enjoyed a tasty snack.

Mia and her aunt mixed the frosting together. It was pink and sweet. They put it on the cake when it was done baking. Mia was so happy she got to help her aunt in the modern kitchen. They both enjoyed the yummy cake together.

Dad picked one up and said, "Open your mouth and take a bite." The little one did and it was so yummy! "Do you want another one?" Dad asked.

The little one smiled and nodded. But this time, they were a bit more careless. Some of the chocolate crumbs from the bun fell from the plate onto the carpet.

Mum said, "No more buns! You have to be careful. It's time for bed now." The little one looked very sad.

But then Mum said, "If you promise to be careful, I'll allow you to have one more bun." The little one was so relieved and made sure to take his time eating it.

One day, John's family got a dog. John was very excited. He loved his new dog and wanted to play with it all day. He ran to the dog and gave it a big hug. But the dog felt uncomfortable and started to bark.

John's dad stepped in. He told John, "We need to value this dog's feelings. He might be scared because he's new to the family."

John thought about it and nodded. He said, "I understand. I'll be more careful and gentle the next time I play with him."

John's dad smiled and said, "That's my boy. You showed good understanding and empathy. Now why don't you two go for a walk together?"

John was happy. He grabbed the leash and ran out the door with the dog. He was careful to walk slowly so the dog felt safe. John knew he valued the dog's feelings, and this made him happy.

But then, a big wind came and took the toy away. The duck and the frog were sad. They did not have the toy to play with anymore. And they never found the expensive toy again.

His mom smiled and said, "Of course, Timmy. We can add the moon to our collection. It's a very rare thing to see, so we should cherish it."

Anna likes lizards. She thinks they are cool and friendly. She wants to buy a lizard for her birthday. She asks her mom and dad if she can buy a lizard.

"Please, please, can I buy a lizard?" Anna says. "I will take good care of it. I will feed it and play with it and love it."

Mom and dad think about it. They know Anna likes lizards, but they are not sure if she is ready for a pet. They ask Anna some questions.

"What do lizards eat?" mom asks.

"Lizards eat bugs and worms and plants," Anna says. "I can find them in the garden or buy them at the store."

"Where will the lizard sleep?" dad asks.

"The lizard will sleep in a big tank with rocks and water and plants," Anna says. "I can put it in my room or in the living room."

"How will you keep the lizard safe and happy?" mom and dad ask.

"I will keep the lizard safe and happy by giving it a name and a toy and a friend," Anna says. "I will talk to it and pet it and watch it."

Mom and dad are impressed by Anna's answers. They see that she knows a lot about lizards and that she really wants one. They decide to buy a lizard for Anna for her birthday.

They go to the pet store with Anna and look at the lizards. Anna sees a green lizard with brown spots and a long tail. She likes it very much. She asks the pet store lady if she can hold it.

"Sure, you can hold it," the pet store lady says. "This is a friendly lizard. It likes people and other lizards. It is a good lizard for a beginner."

Anna holds the lizard gently in her hands. She feels its smooth skin and its warm body. She looks into its eyes and smiles. She knows this is the lizard for her.

"I want this lizard," Anna says. "Can I buy this lizard?"

Mom and dad nod and smile. They pay the pet store lady and get a tank and some food and some toys for the lizard. They help Anna carry everything to the car.

Anna is very happy. She has a lizard for her birthday. She thinks of a name for her lizard. She decides to call it Spot.

"Hello, Spot," Anna says. "You are my lizard and I am your friend. I love you very much."

"I also bring a calmness to the meadow. Everyone who comes here can find peace and quiet, answered the flower.

But after a while, the little girl didn't see anything. Just when she was about to give up, she saw a pair of eyes peeking out from the grass. It was an alligator!

The little girl was excited and said, "Mommy, there's an alligator!"

The alligator was hidden in the grass but as soon as the little girl said it, the alligator stood up and bowed.

The little girl smiled and was about to go closer but mommy said, "No, sweetheart, that's dangerous."

Bill and Sam were two old friends who loved playing together. One day, Bill had a crazy idea to flip a switch that increased its volume. He thought it was funny, but Sam didn't want to do it.

"Hey, let's just flip this switch! It's gonna be so much fun!", Bill said.

"No, Bill, I don't like that idea! I bet it will make a lot of noise," Sam replied.

But Bill didn't listen. He was too excited to think straight. He flipped the switch and they heard a loud noise. Everything suddenly increased in volume and Bill got a bit scared.

"Okay, okay, let's just turn off the switch," Bill said.

But it was too late. Everything got crazier and noisier and they couldn't turn the switch off. In the end, they had to run away and never came back.

The moral of the story is that it's important to be careful when you increase something. Too much of anything can be dangerous and lead to bad outcomes.

Lila and Ben are twins. They like to play in their room. They have many toys and books. One day, they find a big book on the shelf. It has many pictures of animals and plants.

"Look, Ben, a big book!" Lila says. "Let's read it together."

"OK, Lila, but it is very heavy. We need to be strong to carry it." Ben says. He tries to lift the book, but it is too hard. He drops it on the floor.

"Ouch! That hurt!" Ben says. He rubs his foot.

"I'm sorry, Ben. Are you OK?" Lila says. She hugs him.

"I'm OK, Lila. Thank you." Ben says. He smiles.

"Let's try again. We can do it together. We are strong." Lila says. She holds one side of the book, and Ben holds the other. They lift the book slowly and carefully. They bring it to the bed.

"Yay! We did it!" Lila and Ben say. They high-five each other.

They sit on the bed and open the book. They see many colorful pages. They point at the animals and plants and say their names. They learn new words and facts. They have fun.

They also rock on the bed. They pretend they are on a boat. They make waves with their arms. They laugh and sing.

"Rock, rock, rock the boat. Don't let the book fall out." Lila and Ben sing.

They rock too much. The book slides off the bed. It lands on the floor with a thud.

"Oh no! The book!" Lila and Ben say. They jump off the bed and pick up the book. They check if it is OK.

"Phew! The book is OK. It is not broken." Lila says.

"Good. The book is strong. Like us." Ben says.

They hug the book and each other. They are happy. They love the book. They love to read. They love to rock. They love to play. They love each other.

Once upon a time, there was a big bad bear. He had a sack and he wanted to take it everywhere. One day, he was walking through the woods when he heard a wonderful rhythm coming from nearby. When he looked to see what it was, he saw a little rabbit playing with a pink drum. The bear knew he must have it!

The bear went over to the rabbit and said in a gruff voice, "Give me your drum! The rabbit was scared and shook his head no. The bear then grabbed the drum and put it into his sack.

The little rabbit was very upset. He ran over to the bear and tried to get his drum back, but the bear was too big and strong. The rabbit decided to try a different tactic. He began to tap out a steady beat on the ground with his foot, which made the bear dance with joy.

After dancing for a while, the bear realised what he had done and he felt really bad. He handed the drum back to the rabbit, apologised and ran off into the woods.

The little rabbit was very relieved and happy. He was able to enjoy his rhythm again and he never saw the big bad bear again.

"Is it a new animal?" Ben asked.

"Is it a big toy?" Lily asked.

Mom smiled and said, "You will see when we get there. It is something very special."

Ben and Lily were anxious to find out. They tried to guess all the way to the zoo. They looked out the window and saw cars, trees, and houses. They did not see anything that looked like a surprise.

When they got to the zoo, Mom bought the tickets and gave them a map. She said, "Follow me. I will show you the surprise. It is at the end of this path."

Ben and Lily followed Mom. They saw many animals on the way. They saw the lions, the monkeys, and the elephants. They were happy, but they still wanted to see the surprise.

They reached the end of the path. There was a big gate with a sign that said, "Penguin View". Mom opened the gate and said, "Here we are. This is the surprise. We can go inside and see the penguins."

Ben and Lily gasped. They loved penguins. They had seen them on TV, but they had never seen them in real life. They ran inside the gate and saw a big pool with many penguins. The penguins were swimming, diving, and sliding on the ice. They were black and white and very cute.

Ben and Lily were amazed. They clapped and laughed. They thanked Mom for the surprise. They said, "This is the best view ever. We love the penguins. They are so fun and cool."

Mom hugged them and said, "I'm glad you like it. I knew you would. I guessed right."

Tom and Sam were brothers who liked to play in the kitchen. They had many toys, but they also liked to open the cabinets and see what was inside. One day, they found a big jar of cookies in one of the cabinets. They wanted to eat the cookies, but they knew they had to ask Mom first.

"Mom, can we have some cookies?" Tom asked.

"Not now, boys. It's almost time for lunch. You can have one cookie each after you finish your food," Mom said.

Tom and Sam were not happy. They wanted the cookies now. They decided to sneak some when Mom was not looking. They opened the jar and took out two cookies each. They ate them quickly and put the jar back in the cabinet.

But they did not close the cabinet door properly. It was still a little bit open. Tom saw it and tried to push it with his hand. But it did not close. He got angry and kicked the cabinet door hard.

Bang! The cabinet door slammed shut, but the jar of cookies fell down and broke. Cookies and glass pieces were everywhere on the floor. Mom heard the noise and came to the kitchen.

"What happened here?" Mom asked.

Tom and Sam were scared. They knew they did something bad. They did not say anything.

"Did you boys take the cookies without asking me?" Mom asked.

Tom and Sam nodded. They felt sorry.

"And did you kick the cabinet door and break the jar?" Mom asked.

Tom nodded. He felt more sorry.

Mom was not happy. She was angry and sad. She told Tom and Sam to go to their room and think about what they did. She also told them they could not have any more cookies for a week. She said they had to learn to listen to her and to be careful with things.

Tom and Sam went to their room. They did not want to play. They wanted to hug Mom and say they were sorry. They learned that kicking things and taking things without asking were not good. They learned that they had to be fine boys who respected Mom and the kitchen.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved to play outside. One day, Lily looked out the window and saw that the weather was very cloudy. She wondered if it was going to rain. Lily put on her raincoat and boots and went outside to play.

As she was playing, Lily saw a snail crawling on the ground. She was curious and wanted to touch it, but her mom had told her to never touch snails. Lily didn't listen and picked up the snail. Suddenly, the snail squirted slimy goo all over her hand.

Lily learned her lesson and realized that sometimes it's better to listen to the advice of others. She also wondered if the snail was trying to protect itself from her curiosity. From then on, Lily was more careful and respectful of the creatures she encountered outside.

Max jumped into the car and sat on the carpet in the backseat. He looked out the window and saw many trees and flowers. When they arrived at the park, Max ran around and played with other dogs. He even found a new furry friend named Bella.

After playing for a while, Max's owner said it was time to go home. Max was tired but happy. He was grateful that his owner allowed him to come along in the car. When they got home, Max fell asleep on the furry carpet, dreaming of his fun day at the park.

Then one day, the mom had a wonderful idea. She said to the child, "Let's go and get something to help us make the clothes clean."

So the mom and the child went to the store and got a big box of soap. Then they took it home and Mom poured some of the soap into a bowl with water.

Mom said, "Let's use our hands to help make the clothes clean. So the child put the dirty clothes in the soapy water, and then he splashed and swished them around.

Mom and the child worked together and soon all the clothes were clean and dry. The child was very happy!

Tom saw a small bird who was not healthy. The bird could not fly. Tom felt sad for the bird. He wanted to help the bird get better. Tom thought of using the stick to help the bird.

Tom put the stick near the bird. The bird used the stick to rest. Tom gave the bird some water and food. Soon, the bird got healthy and could fly again. The bird was very happy and so was Tom.

The moral of the story is to always help others when they need it.

One day Brody went for a walk on the road. He saw a car coming and he ran back home to check on Abby. As he walked into his house he saw that Abby was getting married to a new dog!

Brody was so sad and he didn't know what to do. But he knew that he had to be dependable and he decided to stay and help his new friend.

Brody was happy to be the ring bearer at the wedding. But on the way back, the car they were travelling in had an accident! Poor Brody was hurt and his friend Abby was hurt too.

In the end, Brody and Abby had to go to the hospital to be looked after. They never married, but they were very dependable to each other in their sad time.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big animal. It has four legs, a long neck and spots. It is a giraffe.

"Wow, look at that animal!" Lily says. "It is so tall and pretty. I want to touch it."

"Me too," Tom says. "But it is too far. How can we reach it?"

They think and think. Then they have an idea. They see a big slide. They run to the slide and climb up. They slide down very fast. They hope to fly to the giraffe.

But they do not fly. They land on the ground. They are sad. They cannot touch the animal.

The giraffe sees them. It is a jolly animal. It likes children. It bends its neck and comes closer. It gives them a lick with its tongue.

Lily and Tom are surprised. They laugh. They feel the animal's soft fur. They are happy. They thank the animal.

"Thank you, jolly animal," Lily says. "You are very kind. We like you."

"Thank you, jolly animal," Tom says. "You are very fun. We like you too."

The giraffe smiles. It is happy too. It likes Lily and Tom. They are friends. They play in the park.

Once upon a time, in a big tree, there was a pretty dove. The dove had a shiny stone. She loved her stone a lot. One day, she thought, "I must keep my stone safe."

The dove flew down to the ground. She found a deep hole. The dove said, "This is a good spot to bury my stone." So, she put the stone in the hole and covered it with dirt. The stone was hidden and safe.

Later, the dove went back to the tree. She told her friend, a little bird, about what she did. The bird said, "That's a great idea! Now your stone is safe and sound." They both felt happy and played together in the tree.

Once upon a time, there was a stubborn little girl named Lily. She was three years old and loved to explore.

One day, Lily decided to play in the elevator. She started pushing all the buttons. But her mom said that she couldn't play in the elevator and asked her to come out. Lily was so stubborn that she wouldn't listen.

So her mom said, "If you don't come out, I will bury the elevator." Lily thought that was funny.

But her mom meant it! She got a shovel and started to dig around the elevator. After a while, soon the elevator was buried under a giant pile of dirt!

Lily laughed and laughed. She learned her lesson to not be so stubborn and she never played in the elevator again.

Once there was a girl named Jane. She loved taking care of animals. She always helped the little ones if they were feeling sick. Jane wanted to become a veterinarian when she grew up.

One day, she was feeling like she was busy all the time. She had a lot of things to do and didn't know where to start. So she asked her mom for help. Her mom suggested getting organized.

Jane created a list of things to do and made sure it was in the right order. When she was done, Jane felt much better. She was no longer overwhelmed and was confident she would get things done.

The moral of this story is that getting organized helps you stay on top of things. It also gives you more time to take care of the things that really matter. Jane was able to help the animals being a veterinarian because she learned how to stay organized.

Once upon a time there was a nosy bird named Bob. Bob always wanted to know what other animals were doing. He decided to go for a fly around the woods. But when he flew too close to the cliff, a strong wind suddenly started to blow. Bob struggled to stay in the air, but the wind was too strong! He was going to pass the cliff!

Bob was scared and he started to flap his wings, even harder than before. But it was too late, the wind was too strong. Bob flew passed the cliff and fell far down.

After he had fallen for what seemed like a long time, Bob squeaked with happiness as he saw something big, soft and green below him. He landed safely on a big, green field. Bob happily fluttered around the field, enjoying his adventure.

Not long after, Bob saw a nosy cat. The cat had been watching him while he flew. Bob chirped and said hello to the cat. The cat meowed back and they became good friends.

From then on, Bob never flew too close to the cliff again. He had learned his lesson, and never wanted to pass the cliff again!

Once upon a time there was a raven. He was happy and fit. He sat on a branch of a tree and looked around. He noticed something funny. On the ground below was a funny looking animal. It was bouncing and laughing. The raven wanted to laugh too. He cawed loudly and it made the animal laugh even harder. The raven spread his wings and jumped down from the tree. He flew around the animal and they laughed and played together. The two were having so much fun that they played until the sun went down. The raven gave the animal a big hug and flew back up to the tree. They waved goodbye and the raven said he would come back soon. The animal waved until the raven was out of sight. The animal smiled and was filled with joy. As the sun disappeared, the raven's last words echoed in the night: "Let's laugh together again."

John sat down and started to cry. He had really tried his best but it wasn't enough. He felt so down but he knew he had to keep going. He slowly got up and headed home with a heavy heart.

Bob and Sue played with the comb all day. They took turns flying and carrying the comb. They had lots of fun. But then, the comb got stuck in a tree. Bob and Sue tried to get it out, but it was not easy. They pushed and pulled, but the comb would not move.

Bob and Sue were very sad. They could not play with the comb anymore. They had to say goodbye to the comb and fly away. The comb stayed stuck in the tree, and Bob and Sue never saw it again.

Sara was a bossy girl. She liked to tell her brother Tom what to do. She liked to play with his toys and set them where she wanted. She did not like to share or say sorry.

One day, Sara found a big box in the garage. She wanted to make a fort with it. She told Tom to help her. She said he had to cut the box and tape it. She said he had to make a door and a window. She said he had to draw pictures on it. She said he had to do it fast.

Tom did not like to be bossed. He wanted to play with his cars. He did not want to cut and tape and draw. He did not want to make a fort. He wanted to make Sara stop. He had an idea.

He cut the box and taped it. He made a door and a window. He drew pictures on it. He did it fast. He did not tell Sara what he did. He said the fort was ready. He said Sara could go in first.

Sara was happy. She ran to the fort. She went in the door. She did not see what Tom did. She did not see the hole in the floor. She did not see the bucket of water under the hole. She did not see the string on the bucket. She did not see Tom pull the string.

Splash! Sara got wet. She got very wet. She was mad. She yelled at Tom. She said he was mean. She said he had to say sorry. She said he had to dry her.

Tom did not say sorry. He did not dry her. He laughed. He said she was bossy. He said she had to learn. He said he had to teach her. He said he had a fun game. He said it was called "Inch Worm". He said Sara had to crawl on the floor and say "inch, inch, inch" until she got to the door.

Sara did not like the game. She did not want to crawl. She did not want to say "inch, inch, inch". She wanted to get out. She wanted to get dry. She wanted to get Tom. She learned a lesson. She learned not to be bossy. She learned to share and say sorry. She learned to be nice to her brother. She learned the hard way.

Once upon a time, there was a big parade in town. A little girl named Lily was excited to see it. She asked her mom, "Can we go see the parade, please?"

Her mom said, "Yes, we can go see the parade. But we have to be careful and stay close to each other."

As they watched the parade, Lily saw a man who couldn't hear. He was waving his hands and trying to talk, but no one could understand him. Lily asked her mom, "Why is he waving his hands like that?"

Her mom explained, "He is deaf, which means he can't hear. He uses his hands to talk to other people who also can't hear."

Lily felt sad for the man and said, "I wish I could help him." Her mom replied, "You can help him by being kind and understanding. Sometimes, that's all someone needs to feel better."

Once upon a time, there was a young boy with a big, brave heart. He loved to explore the world around him and would often go on long adventures with his family.

One day, his family decided to go on a hike through a nearby forest. Everything was calm and peaceful until suddenly, a loud sound filled the air. It was thunder! The boy was terrified and didn't know what to do. He started to shake and cry.

But his family members quickly came to the rescue. They held him tight and told him that it was just thunder and nothing to be scared of. They led him through the forest and stayed close by, comforted him until he was calmed down.

Gradually, the boy began to feel more brave and the fear that had taken over him slowly disappeared. With his family by his side, he was able to lead himself through the forest and make it out safely.

This experience taught the young boy a valuable lesson-sometimes life can be a bit frightening, but if you keep a steady and brave heart, you'll eventually make it out okay!

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl named Lily. She loved to explore the world around her. One day, she decided to go outside and melt some snow. She grabbed her measuring cup and went to the back yard.

Lily looked at the snow and shouted, "What a lot of snow there is to melt!" She filled the cup with snow and placed it on the ground.

Lily then noticed her mom coming to the back yard. "Mom!" she shouted in delight.

"What are you doing, Lily?" Mom asked.

"I'm melting snow! I'm being so brave," Lily replied.

Mom smiled and said, "That's wonderful, Lily! I'm proud of you for being so brave."

Lily smiled back, then she continued to measure and melt the snow until all of it was melted. She was so proud of her work and knew she was really brave.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Emily. She loved riding her scooter. One day, she decided to go for a ride.

As she rode, she noticed something distinctive from the usual. She found a pot cooking on the side of the road. There was something familiar about it's aroma. Emily decided to take a closer look.

As she got closer, Emily saw a strange old man with a dull expression, stirring inside the pot. She said, "What are you cooking?". The man gave her a wicked smile and replied, "I'm making a stew".

The man asked Emily excitedly, "Do you want to try it?". Emily was scared, but she said yes. The man added some ingredients to the pot, and Emily nervously tasted it.

It was disgusting! Emily ran away, scared and crying. She never returned to the road and played with her scooter again. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Daniella. She was only three years old and loved to explore the world around her.

One morning, Daniella put on her favourite pink dress. It had a yellow buckle on the front. She waved goodbye to her mum and set off for a walk.

As she skipped down the street, Daniella noticed a very strange bird on a fence. It had a blue crest and a long red beak. The bird was waving its wings up and down. It wasn't behaving normally.

Daniella looked around and noticed several other birds, all behaving the same way. She clapped her hands and said, "Hello birds! Why are you waving your wings like that?"

The birds looked at her and one of them made a funny noise. It was her talking! The bird flapped its wings and said, "It's morning and we like to wave to welcome the day."

The other birds nodded in agreement and kept waving. Daniella smiled and waved back. She said goodbye to the birds, skipped down the street, and waved to her mum when she came back home.

Once upon a time there was a boy called Timmy. Timmy was very obedient and he always followed what his mum said. One day, his mum said to him, "Timmy, please don't go near that spot over there". Timmy nodded and said "okay, mum."

But then, Timmy got curious. He really wanted to know what was at the spot his mum had warned him about. So he crept over to it and when he got there he saw that it was a beautiful lake with fish swimming in it. It was so beautiful that he couldn't resist jumping in.

When Timmy got out, he realised that he had disobeyed his mum and he immediately felt really bad. He began to regret it and promised himself that he would never disobey his mum again.

From then on Timmy was always obedient and he never went against what his mum told him. Even though he felt bad about his mistake, he learned an important lesson and was now a very good and obedient boy.

Sara and Ben were playing with paper and glue. They wanted to make a sign for their mom. They cut out letters and shapes and stuck them on a big piece of paper. Sara wrote "We love you, Mom" with a crayon.

"Look, Ben, our sign is done!" Sara said. "It is very pretty and colorful. Mom will be happy when she sees it."

Ben nodded. He liked the sign too. He wanted to help Sara hang it on the wall. He took the sign and some tape and walked to the wall. But he was clumsy and tripped over a toy. He fell down and the sign flew out of his hands. It landed on the floor, face down.

"Oh no!" Sara cried. "Our sign is ruined! The glue is not dry yet and the paper is stuck to the floor. What will we do?"

Ben felt bad. He was sorry he was clumsy and messed up the sign. He got up and looked at Sara. He had an idea.

"Don't worry, Sara. We can fix it. We can peel off the paper and glue it again. And we can add some more letters and shapes. It will be even prettier and more colorful. Mom will still be happy when she sees it."

Sara smiled. She was glad Ben had an idea. She hugged him and said, "You are right, Ben. We can fix it. And we can be more careful next time. You are a good brother and I love you."

They worked together and fixed the sign. They hung it on the wall with tape. They waited for their mom to come home. They were proud of their sign and of each other.

One day, Mom comes to their room. She sees the wall. She is very angry. She says, "Who did this? This is ugly! You have to clean the wall now!" She gives them a sponge and a bucket of water. She says, "No more drawing on the wall!"

Lily and Tom are sad. They do not want to clean the wall. They like their drawings. They think Mom is mean. They start to cry. They say, "We are sorry, Mom. We just wanted to have fun."

Mom sees their tears. She feels sorry too. She says, "I know, my loves. But the wall is not for drawing. You have paper and books for that. The wall is for hanging pictures and posters.
