Once there was a tiny little mouse who lived in a cozy cardboard box. The mouse was very lively and loved to explore the world around him.

One morning, the mouse decided to take a walk outdoors. He came across a group of children playing in the garden. The Mouse was curious about what they were doing, so he hid behind a bush to watch.

The children had all brought with them a snack to eat between their playing. The snacks were all shapes and sizes and the mouse thought they looked delicious. He wanted to fit in with the children, so he grabbed one of the snacks off the ground and ran away with it.

At first the mouse was very happy eating his snack in a safe place, away from the children. But then he noticed something. The other children weren't eating their snacks; they were sharing them with each other, laughing and talking happily. He felt very sad, realizing he had missed out on the chance to fit in with the other children.

The moral of the story is that it is important to share and be inclusive. If we all work together, we can make the world a happier and more lively place!

Lila and Ben were playing in the garden. They liked to dig holes and look for bugs. They found a big worm, a shiny beetle and a funny spider. They put them in a jar and showed them to Mom.

"Look, Mom, we found some friends!" Lila said.

Mom smiled and said, "They are very interesting, but you should let them go soon. They need to live in the soil and the plants, not in a jar."

"OK, Mom, we will," Ben said.

They went back to the garden and dug some more holes. Then they saw something moving under a rock. They lifted the rock and saw a small, brown animal with a long nose and tiny eyes. It was a mole.

"Wow, what is that?" Lila asked.

"It's a mole," Ben said. "It lives under the ground and eats worms and bugs."

"Can we keep it?" Lila asked.

"No, we can't," Ben said. "It's not a pet. It's wild and it might bite us. And it's ugly."

The mole heard Ben and felt sad. It did not think it was ugly. It thought it was beautiful and clever. It could dig tunnels and find food and sense vibrations. It did not like the bright light and the loud noises. It wanted to go back to its home.

"Please, let me go," the mole said in a soft voice.

Lila and Ben heard the mole and were surprised. They did not know moles could talk.

"Did you hear that?" Lila asked.

"Yes, I did," Ben said.

They looked at the mole and saw that it was scared and unhappy. They felt sorry for it.

"OK, we will let you go," Lila said. "We are sorry we bothered you. You are not ugly. You are special."

"Thank you," the mole said. "You are kind."

Lila and Ben gently put the mole back under the rock and covered it with soil. They wiped their hands and went to find Mom. They told her about the mole and what it said. Mom hugged them and said, "You did the right thing. You are good children."

Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved to read books. One day a special book caught her eye. It looked incredible - bigger than the others and more colourful. She did not know what it was but wanted to see it closer. She asked her mum what it was and she said it was a novel. The little girl was very excited to hold this novel in her hand.

As she opened the book she could not believe what she saw. There were amazing pictures and so many words that she had never seen before. She knew it was a special book and she read it every day. As she read she began to see things that she did not understand. She asked her mum what some of the things meant and when she heard the answers, the little girl eyes lit up with wonder and delight.

The little girl kept reading this incredible novel and each time she picked it up she was filled with excitement. As she read, she discovered a new world full of surprises, adventure and wonder. She couldn't believe how things from earlier in the book made sense later on. The novel was amazing and the little girl loves it so much.

Lily liked to eat avocado. She liked the green and soft part inside. She did not like the brown and hard part outside. She used a spoon to scoop the avocado from the skin.

One day, Lily's mom gave her a white bowl with avocado and cheese. Lily was happy. She said, "Thank you, mom. I love avocado and cheese."

She started to eat with her spoon. But then she saw something in her bowl. It was a small black thing. Lily did not know what it was. She did not like it. She said, "Ew, what is this?"

Her mom looked at her bowl. She said, "That is a seed, Lily. It is from the avocado. It is not for eating. You can leave it in the bowl."

Lily did not want to leave it in the bowl. She did not want to see it. She said, "No, mom. I don't like it. It is yucky. I want to quit."

Her mom said, "Lily, don't quit. You still have a lot of avocado and cheese. They are yummy. The seed is not yucky. It is just different. You can try to touch it. It is smooth and round."

Lily was curious. She used her spoon to poke the seed. It was smooth and round. She said, "It is smooth and round. Can I play with it?"

Her mom said, "Yes, you can play with it. But don't put it in your mouth. It is not for eating. It is for growing new avocados."

Lily smiled. She said, "Okay, mom. I will play with it. And I will eat the avocado and cheese. They are yummy."

She finished her bowl. She was happy. She said, "Thank you, mom. I like avocado and cheese. And I like the seed. It is fun."

Once upon a time, there was a gray cat named Fluffy. Fluffy liked to play outside in the garden. One day, Fluffy saw a big crystal on the ground. The crystal was shiny and pretty. Fluffy wanted to play with it, so she pushed it with her paw. The crystal started to slide on the ground. Fluffy chased after it and had so much fun playing with the crystal. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny who wanted to be independent. He wanted to do things on his own, without help from his mommy or daddy. One day, the bunny saw an envelope on the ground. He wanted to pick it up, but he couldn't stand up on his hind legs.

The bunny felt sad and frustrated, but he didn't give up. He practiced standing every day until he could finally stand on his own two feet. One day, he saw another bunny who was struggling to stand up with a heavy load of carrots. The independent bunny went over to help him and together, they carried the carrots to their burrows.

From that day on, the independent bunny knew that it was good to be able to stand on his own, but it was even better to help others who couldn't stand on their own. He felt happy and proud of himself for being able to help others. The moral of the story is that being independent is good, but helping others is even better.

Once upon a time, there were two little brothers, Mark and Tom. Every day, Mark and Tom played together happily. One day, Mark wanted to mix a special potion. He asked Tom to help him. But Tom was worried about the potion, so he said no.

Mark was sad, but he still wanted to mix the potion. So he decided to go ahead and mix it all by himself. He took some water, some leaves and a few other things.

The potion smelled strong and strange. Suddenly, a bright light surrounded Mark and the room. When the light disappeared, Mark had vanished!

Tom was so scared that he ran away. He looked for an exit and managed to escape.

The moral of this story is that one should be thoughtful and think before doing something. Just because something is fun or exciting doesn't mean it's a good idea. Sometimes it brings bad consequences.

Once, there was a bird. The bird sang a very happy song, and it echoed all around. Everyone who heard it was filled with joy.

The bird flew high above the trees and looked down. Everything looked so beautiful! Everywhere it looked, it could see bright colors and movement.

As it flew by, the bird sang its song. People smiled when they heard it, and some danced to the melody. The bird's voice was so sweet, it was like a lullaby.

Soon the bird came across a park. Everything here was so noisy! Kids were laughing and running around, music was playing, and dogs were barking. Everyone was singing, too. It was a very happy sight.

The bird joined in the singing, and its voice was the loudest and happiest of all. People stopped and looked up at the bird in awe. The bird had made them even happier, and everyone was filled with joy.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tweet. Tweet was very excited because she was going to visit her friend, Flutter. Flutter lived on the other side of the cliff, but Tweet didn't mind. She loved to fly and see new things.

As Tweet flew over the cliff, she saw something she had never seen before. It was a big, scary bird with sharp claws. Tweet was scared, but she kept flying. She knew she had to tell Flutter about the scary bird.

When Tweet arrived at Flutter's house, she was out of breath. She told Flutter all about the scary bird and how she had flown over the cliff to get to her house. Flutter listened carefully and then told Tweet that she had seen the scary bird before. She knew how to avoid it.

From then on, Tweet and Flutter always flew around the cliff instead of over it. They were happy to have each other to tell their secrets and keep each other safe.

Molly was walking outside with her mom. The sky was bright and sunny. Then suddenly, Molly heard a loud sound. She said to her mom, "What was that?" Her mom replied, "Oh, that was just thunder." Molly knew that sound. She said, "I like thunder!" Then, she looked up at the sky and saw the clouds moving.

Molly was proud of herself for knowing what thunder was. She smiled and then asked her mom, "Can I look for thunder again? Please?" Her mom was proud too and said, "Of course! You can look for thunder whenever you want."

Molly kept looking up at the sky. Every time she heard thunder, she was so happy and proud. She was proud that she knew what thunder was. She felt like she could do anything and had the biggest smile on her face.

One day, Tom went to a party. There was a big cake on the table. It was a dry cake, but it looked yummy. Tom was happy to see the cake.

At the party, Tom saw a little bug. The bug wanted to eat the cake too. Tom did not want the bug to eat the cake. He tried to pinch the bug, but it was too fast.

Tom asked his friends for help. They all tried to pinch the bug together. The bug was too fast for them too. Then, they had a good idea. They gave the bug a small piece of the dry cake. The bug was happy, and it did not bother them anymore.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Jazz. She was three years old and very wise. Every day she would go running in the park with her mom. Jazz loved to feel the grass and wind on her face.

One day, Jazz found a big tree in the park. Its bark was smooth and its branches were high. She wanted to see what was at the top of the tree, so she started climbing.

Halfway up the tree, Jazz looked around and saw how small her mom seemed from where she was. She felt brave and wise. She felt so proud of herself for climbing the big tree.

When Jazz reached the top of the tree, she could see everything! She could see all the people in the park, the sunny sky, and everything else. Jazz felt so happy up there.

In the distance, Jazz could hear a kind of music she had never heard before. It was called jazz. Jazz smiled and moved her body to the music. The music made her feel so good.

Jazz felt brave and wise that day. She always remembers it and remembers how the jazz music made her feel.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Abi. She was three years old and she loved to play outside.

One day, Abi set off to look for her will. She searched everywhere but she couldn't find it. Then, she remembered that it was pink so she looked in the pink flowers and the pink sky. No luck.

Finally, she heard a big, deep voice. "Hello there Abi," it called out. Abi looked up and saw a big purple dragon. "Have you seen my will?" she asked.

The dragon nodded. "It is over there," he said, pointing to a pink bush. Abi went over to the pink bush and sure enough, she found her will.

"Thank you!" she said to the dragon. Abi was so happy to have found her will.

The dragon smiled. "You are welcome," he said.

Abi set off home with her will in hand, feeling very happy and thankful.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play in the garden. They have a big rock that they use as a table. They put their toys and snacks on the rock.

One day, they see a big worm on the rock. It is brown and slimy. Lily screams and runs away. Ben laughs and picks up the worm. He says, "Look, Lily, it is a friend. It is not scary. It is soft and wiggly."

Lily does not like the worm. She says, "No, Ben, it is not a friend. It is disgusting. It is dirty and icky. Put it down. It will make you sick."

Ben does not listen. He says, "No, Lily, it is fun. It tickles my hand. I want to keep it. I will name it Wally."

Lily shakes her head. She says, "You are silly, Ben. You cannot keep a worm. It needs soil and water. It does not belong on the rock. And you need to wash your hand. Come on, let's go to the shower."

Ben makes a face. He says, "I do not want to go to the shower. The shower is cold and wet. It is not fun. I want to stay with Wally."

Lily grabs Ben's hand. She says, "You have to go to the shower, Ben. Mommy said so. She said we have to be clean and tidy. And Wally has to go back to the garden. He will be happier there. Come on, Ben, please."

Ben sighs. He says, "Okay, Lily, fine. But I will miss Wally. He is a good worm. Bye, Wally. See you later."

Lily and Ben go to the shower. They wash their hands and faces. They rinse off the dirt and the slime. They feel fresh and happy. They hug each other and say, "We love you, Wally. But we love each other more."

The fairy smiled and said, "This paper has special powers. Congratulations, your wish has been granted". The girl felt so happy and thanked the fairy before she disappeared.

The girl was so excited that she opened the paper. It contained all of her dreams, written down in beautiful colours! She smiled and danced around with joy.

The girl was no longer nervous. Today she had learned that anything is possible, as long as you believe!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and explore the world around her. One day, she found a dirty, yucky cell in the grass. It was so filthy that she didn't want to touch it.

Lily decided to mail the cell to her friend, Timmy. She put it in a box and sent it off in the mail. Timmy was surprised when he received the box, but he was happy to have a new toy to play with.

After a few days, Lily and Timmy went on an adventure to find more dirty cells to mail to each other. They had so much fun exploring and discovering new things together. From that day on, they were the best of friends and always looked forward to receiving new surprises in the mail.

Once there was a girl named Ali. She was three years old. One day she wanted to take a bath. She filled up the tub with water and jumped in. It felt so good! She used lots of bubbles and played with her toys until she got tired.

After her bath, Ali went to her room. There, she opened up her treasure box. Inside was a shiny trophy. She had won it for being the best dancer at the talent show. It was very expensive and she was very proud of it.

The next day, Ali went to the park to play with her friends. She was so happy that she had her trophy to show them. That night, after a long day, Ali got ready for bed. She took another bath and washed her trophy until it was sparkly clean.

Ali was proud of her trophy and she knew she had worked hard to get it. She was excited to show it off to her friends and show them how special it was.

Lila and Ben liked to play knights and dragons in the park. Lila had a big shield made of cardboard and Ben had a stick for a sword. They ran around the trees and pretended to fight the bad dragons. Sometimes they met other kids who wanted to play with them, but sometimes they were alone.

One day, they saw a new kid in the park. He had a blue coat and a red hat. He looked at Lila and Ben and said, "Can I play with you?" Lila and Ben nodded and said, "Sure, what's your name?" The new kid said, "I'm Max, and I have a special power. I can freeze things with my breath." Lila and Ben were curious and asked, "How do you do that?" Max said, "Watch this." He blew on a flower and it turned white and stiff. Lila and Ben gasped and said, "Wow, that's cool!"

They played with Max for a while, and he showed them how he could freeze other things, like rocks, leaves, and bugs. Lila and Ben thought he was fun and friendly, but they also felt a little scared of his power. They wondered what would happen if he froze them. They hoped he would not do that.

But Max had a secret. He was not a nice kid. He was a mean kid who liked to hurt others. He only pretended to be nice to trick Lila and Ben. He wanted to freeze them and take their shield and sword. He waited for the right moment, when they were not looking. He sneaked behind them and said, "Hey, look at this!" He blew on them with his harsh breath and they felt a cold wind on their backs. They turned around and saw Max smiling wickedly. They tried to move, but they could not. They were frozen!

Max laughed and said, "Ha ha, I got you! Now I have your shield and sword, and you can't do anything!" He grabbed their shield and sword and ran away. Lila and Ben felt sad and angry. They wished they had not trusted Max. They wished they could break free. They wished someone would help them. They wondered how long they would stay frozen. They hoped not for long.

Once upon a time, there was a fat cat. The cat wanted to play with a penny. It found a penny on the ground and picked it up with its paw. The cat tried to bite the penny, but it was too hard. So, the cat decided to twist the penny with its paw. Suddenly, the penny became flat and the cat was happy. It played with the penny all day long. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a generous man. He liked to give things away to others. He was the nicest man around.
One day, a little girl was thirsty. She was walking down the street and she saw the generous man's house. She knocked on the door, and the generous man let her in. He smiled at her, and then asked her what she was looking for.

The little girl said she wanted a drink of water. Then, the generous man showed her to the faucet. There was a drinking fountain there, and it was just what she wanted. She filled her cup with cold, clear water and drank it up.

The generous man was so happy to help the little girl. He clapped his hands together and everyone around him applauded. They were all very happy for the generous man and for the little girl with the fresh cup of water.

The little girl thanked the generous man for being so kind and for showing her to the faucet. She thanked him for the water and then said goodbye. The generous man smiled, waved, and then watched her go.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Max. She loved playing with her friends and was always very kind. One day Max wanted to surprise her parents with a special gift. She went to the store and picked out a thermometer.

When Max got home she was very excited to give her mom and dad the surprise. But when she opened the bag, her mom said, "Why did you buy this thermometer?" The little girl said, "I wanted to show how much I love you."

Her dad was very happy and said, "Thank you! We love you too." But her mom just said, "That was a bit selfish of you, Max." Max was very sad to hear this and didn't understand why her mom thought that.

The next day, Max decided to surprise her mom with a special breakfast in bed. She put a flower on the tray, made her mom's favorite breakfast, and put a loving note on the tray.

When her mom woke up, she saw the special breakfast and read the note. A big smile spread across her face and said, "What a sweet girl!" Max smiled back and said, "I just wanted to show you how much I love you!" Her mom hugged Max tight and said, "I love you too!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big wish to go to the park and play on the swings. Her mommy said they could go, but only if Lily ate all her vegetables. Lily didn't like vegetables, but she wanted to go to the park so she ate them all.

When they got to the park, Lily was so happy. She went straight to the swings and started to swing higher and higher. But then, a big boy came and pushed her off the swing. Lily was upset and started to cry. Her mommy came over and hugged her, but Lily still felt sad.

Suddenly, a bee came and stung Lily on the arm. She cried even more and her mommy took her home. Lily's arm got very swollen and she had to go to the hospital. The doctor gave her medicine, but it didn't help. Lily's wish to play on the swings was ruined and she was very upset.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy. He liked to play with boats in the lake near his house. One day, his mom warned him not to go near the lake because it would get him in trouble. But the boy ignored her. He went to the lake and took a boat. The boat was very dirty, but he didn't care.

He sailed the boat, but the water was too deep. Suddenly, the boat started to sink. The boy was scared and wanted to go home. He shouted for help but no one heard him.

Soon, his mom found out about the boat. She was angry and punished the boy. His punishment was that he had to stay in his bedroom for the whole day.

The boy was very sad. He knew he should have listened to his mom. From that day onwards, he never went near the lake again.

Once upon a time there was an old woman. She wanted to get some water from a nearby stream, so she took an empty jug with her. She was walking through the forest, but it was getting dark. Suddenly, a dangerous animal appeared in front of her!

The animal growled and said, "Give me your jug or I will attack you!"

The poor old woman was scared but she bravely replied, "No! Please don`t hurt me!"

The animal stormed closer and demanded once more, "I said give me the jug!"

The old woman quickly grabbed the jug and put it in front of the animal. It happily picked up the jug and ran away.

The old woman was relieved, but then she realized that she had no jug and she'd have to go home without any water. Luckily, there were plenty of other jugs at home.

The old woman learned her lesson. The forest was dangerous, and it was not safe to wander around there at night.

Lily looked up at her mom. "Can I watch a video?" she asked.

Her mom smiled. "Yes, you can watch a video," she said.

Lily clapped her hands in excitement and ran to the living room. She saw the video was already playing. It had some harsh sounds, but they were fun! She sat in front of the television and watched the video.

Suddenly, a loud noise made Lily jump. "What was that?" asked Lily.

Her mom looked over to her. "It's just the video," she said. "It can be a bit harsh at times."

Lily smiled and turned back to the television. She watched the rest of the video and enjoyed it very much.

When it was over, Lily looked up at her mom again. "Can I watch another one?" she asked.

Her mom chuckled and said, "Sure, why not?"

Lily smiled and ran to the living room again to watch another video.

Once upon a time, there lived a small black bird. He all alone in the world and his wings were broken. He often wished he could fly like the other birds and so he kept trying to make his wings work.

But one day, the black bird found a skull on the ground and the skull was shiny and black. The skull looked strange and it made him wary.

Despite this, the black bird hobbled closer, and then he noticed that the skull was not just black and shiny, but it had a strange form of power. He suddenly felt safe.

The black bird felt that the skull had the power to heal his wings and so he touched the skull gently with his beak.

Suddenly, the black bird felt a jolt of energy that coursed through his body.

His wings grew strong and he could feel that he was healing. He then flew away happily, realising that he had been healed by the skull.

This story teaches us that although there may be unknown dangers in the world, we may find strength and courage in the most unexpected places.

The bird flew down and showed the girl how to hold the vine. The girl watched and learned from the bird. She held the vine with her hands and climbed up, just like the bird. The girl was very happy to play on the vine with her new friend, the unique bird. And they played together in the big forest every day.

Tim and Sue worked together to make a sign. They used the ink to draw pictures of their toys. When they finished, they hung the sign on a tree. All their friends came to see the sign and thought it was very pretty. Tim and Sue were very happy and proud of their work.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play and have fun. One day, they see a big sausage on the table. It smells good.

"Can we eat it?" Anna asks.

"No, it is for dinner," Mom says. "You can have some later."

But Anna and Ben are hungry and curious. They want to test the sausage. They are adventurous.

When Mom goes to the bathroom, they sneak to the table. They grab the sausage and run to their room. They close the door and laugh.

"Let's test it!" Ben says. He bites a piece of the sausage. It is juicy and spicy.

"Mmm, it is yummy!" he says.

Anna takes a bite too. She likes it. They eat more and more of the sausage. They are happy.

But then, they hear Mom coming back. She opens the door and sees them. She is angry.

"Anna! Ben! What have you done?" she says. "You ate the whole sausage! That was for dinner!"

Anna and Ben feel bad. They are sorry.

"We are sorry, Mom," they say. "We wanted to test the sausage. It was good."

Mom sighs. She is not happy, but she loves them.

"I know you are adventurous, but you have to listen to me," she says. "You can't eat things that are not for you. You have to wait and share."

Anna and Ben nod. They hug Mom and say sorry again.

Mom hugs them back. She smiles a little.

"Maybe next time, I will make two sausages," she says. "One for you and one for dinner."

Once upon a time, there was a clever little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to visit his grandma's house because she always had yummy cookies. Tom is a smart boy. He likes to learn new things. He has a tutor who comes to his house every week. The tutor helps him with reading and writing and math.

One day, Tom is playing with his toys when the tutor arrives. He hears the doorbell and runs to open it. He is happy to see the tutor.

"Hello, Tom. Are you ready for our lesson?" the tutor asks.

"Yes, I am. Come in, please," Tom says.

They go to the living room, where Tom has a table and a chair and some books. The tutor takes out a paper and a pencil. He writes some words on the paper.

"Tom, can you read these words for me?" the tutor says.

Tom looks at the paper. He knows some of the words, but not all of them. He tries to sound them out.

"Cat, dog, sun, fun, red, bed," he reads.

"Very good, Tom. You are doing well. Now, can you write these words on another paper?" the tutor says.

Tom nods. He takes another paper and a pencil. He starts to write the words. He writes cat, dog, sun, fun, red, but then he stops. He forgets how to write bed. He looks at the tutor's paper, but he can't see it well. He feels sad.

"I forget how to write bed," he says.

The tutor smiles. He moves his paper closer to Tom.

"That's okay, Tom. Everyone forgets sometimes. Look at my paper. Can you see the letters in bed?" he says.

Tom looks at the paper. He sees the letters b, e, and d. He remembers how to write them.

"Oh, I see. B, e, d. Bed," he says.

He writes bed on his paper. He shows it to the tutor.

"Good job, Tom. You remembered. You are a smart boy. I am proud of you," the tutor says.

Tom smiles. He feels happy. He likes the tutor. He likes to learn new things. He does not want to forget.

Once there was a tall girl called Jill. Every day she wanted to try something new. One day she decided to take a step that she thought was too hard. But she didn't want to resist because she wanted to try something new. So she stepped out bravely and took one more step.

She kept taking more and more steps and she was getting more and more brave. She felt a little scared, but she was determined to not resist the challenge. Eventually she made it to the top of the tall mountain.

When she looked back at the steps she had taken she smiled - she had done it! She was so glad that she had not resisted, but had actually taken the steps that she needed to take.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park with their mom. They saw a big statue of a lion. It was brown and had a long tail. Lily wanted to touch it, but Ben was scared.

"Mom, can I touch the lion?" Lily asked.

"No, Lily, you can't. The lion is too high and you might fall. You have to be obedient and stay with me," Mom said.

But Lily did not listen. She ran to the statue and tried to climb it. She wanted to pick the lion's nose. Ben followed her and tried to stop her.

"Lily, come down! You are being naughty! Mom will be angry!" Ben said.

But Lily did not listen. She reached the lion's nose and tried to pick it. But the nose was not soft. It was hard and cold. And it moved. The lion was not a statue. It was a real lion. It roared and opened its mouth. Lily and Ben were very scared. They screamed and ran back to Mom.

Mom heard them and saw the lion. She was very shocked and angry. She grabbed Lily and Ben and hugged them. She was glad they were safe, but she also scolded them.

"Lily and Ben, you were very foolish and disobedient. You could have been hurt by the lion. You have to listen to me and never touch strange things. Do you understand?" Mom said.

Lily and Ben nodded and said sorry. They learned their lesson. They never tried to pick a statue's nose again.

Luckily, there was an old man nearby who noticed the sad little girl. "Don't cry little one, he said in a gentle voice. The old man took out a key from his pocket and said, "This is the key that can unlock the door .

He then explained to Jenny that the door was a special one. If unlocked, a rainbow of butterflys would come out. "You can't reach the keyhole with your little fingers, but it's not hard to do. Just try your best . With that, the old man placed the key in Jenny's hand and encouraged her to unlock the door.

Jenny thought hard and tried again to reach the keyhole. This time she was successful with a little bit of help. She slowly inserted the key and through her courage and determination, the door was opened! Out came a long and colorful rainbow of butterflies that sparkled in the air.

Once upon a time there was a boy. He was troubled. He did not feel happy. He did not know why.

One day he heard a story. This story was about a kind princess. She was kind to everyone. Everyone in the land was happy when she was around.

The boy was interested in the story. He decided to find the princess. He walked and walked until he found her palace.

The princess saw the boy and she smiled. She could tell he was troubled so she asked him what was wrong. The boy told her his story and she listened carefully.

The princess was very kind. She hugged the boy and told him everything would be alright. She showed him around the palace and had fun.

The boy smiled and felt happy. He was no longer troubled. He thanked the princess and went home, happy and content.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mommy. They brought a plastic bag to put their snacks in. While they were eating, a careless boy threw his candy wrapper on the ground.

Lily saw it and said, "That's not good. We should pick it up."

Her mommy agreed and they both got up to wander around the park and pick up any other litter they saw. They found lots of plastic bottles and bags, but they made sure to put them in the trash can.

When they were done, Lily felt happy because she helped make the park clean. Her mommy was proud of her and said, "You're such a good helper!"

Lily smiled and said, "I like to wander and find things to pick up."

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Mary. One day Mary and her mom decided to visit the woods. Mary was excited and whispered, "I hope we will see an owl.

When they arrived, her mom said, "Let's look for the owl . As they walked around, they saw a nest in the tree. Then, they heard a noise! It was an owl!

"It's an owl! shouted Mary, jumping with joy. The owl looked at them with its big brown eyes. It was very spoiled, because it was the only owl in the woods.

Suddenly, the owl flew away. Mary and her mom watched it fly off into the sky. "Let's come back and visit the owl again soon, said Mary. They smiled and went home.

His dad smiled and said "Yes, you may. I permit it!"

The cat was so happy. He thanked his dad then ran off to go and visit his friend. He was so excited to tell his friend all about his visit!

Once there was a delicate flower in a deep pit. The flower had many petals and it was very small. Its petals were a beautiful white color, and they were so soft that they seemed to twinkle in the sunlight. Every day, the flower would look up to the sky and hope that it would one day be able to bloom and enjoy the sunshine.

One morning, the sun shone brighter than usual and the flower began to feel a warmth it had never felt before. Suddenly, a powerful force reached down from the sky and filled the little flower with energy. The petals began to stretch and open, and the flower bloomed for the very first time.

The flower was overjoyed. It stretched its petals to the sky, soaking up the warm and powerful sunshine. The deep pit was suddenly filled with light and beauty and the flower said, "thank you for this wonderful day and for helping me bloom."

But his mommy said, "If you take a nap now, you'll have a successful day of playing later." Benny thought about it and decided to take a nap.

"No, I want to take the net!" Lily said. She also wanted to catch the butterfly first.

"Go away, Lily! You are not my friend!" Tom said.

Once upon a time there was a wise bear who was part of a team.

One day the bear was walking around with his team when he saw something scary. He stopped and said to his team, "I see something that looks scary. What should we do?"

The team huddled together and discussed how to handle the scary thing. After a few minutes, the wise bear stepped forward and said, "I think we should just run away - fast."

So the team ran away as fast as they could. They ran until they were far away from the scary thing.

The wise bear was proud of the team. He reassured them that the scary thing was far away now, but to always be careful in the future.

The team thanked the wise bear for his advice and continued on their way. They lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a mighty pumpkin. It was so big that even a grown-up wouldn't be able to pick it up. Every day a small boy named George would come and look at the huge pumpkin. One morning George had a clever idea. He ran off and got some of his toys. He asked his dad for some help and together they lifted the big and mighty pumpkin onto the wagon.

George was so happy, he took the wagon and drove it off. He found some friends on the way and stopped to show them the pumpkin. They all laughed and waved as he drove on.

When he got home, George wanted to get the big and mighty pumpkin off the wagon. He needed help so he asked his mum and dad to lift it off the wagon into the garden. Together they lifted the pumpkin and George planted it in the ground.

Now, every day George would come and check on his mighty pumpkin and one day it would be perfect to pick.

One summer, two children went to the park. One was called Jack and the other was called Emily. While playing, they noticed a big pile of leaves.

Emily said, "Let's bury ourselves in the leaves!"

Jack thought it was a great idea and they both quickly began to bury themselves.

Suddenly, they heard a horrible smell. It was so disgusting that they decided to stop burying.

Jack and Emily got out of the leaves and saw a dead bird, which had been buried there a while ago.

They both looked at each other with sad eyes and realised that something once alive was now gone.

They decided it was better to admire the beauty of nature by looking but not touching.

The moral of the story is that we should never touch or take anything from nature unless we understand what it does for us and the consequences of taking from it.

Once upon a time there was a small girl named Lucy. She was happy and wanted to explore the world.

One day, Lucy found a valley that she had never seen before. She decided to explore the valley and when she reached the bottom, she noticed it was hot.

Lucy asked a nearby butterfly, "Why is it so hot in this valley?" The butterfly explained that inside the valley it was getting too hot and she had to solve the problem.

Lucy thought and thought and came up with an idea. She made a fan out of some sticks and some leaves she had found and put it by the entrance of the valley. The fan blew the hot air away and cooled the valley down.

The butterfly was very pleased and thanked Lucy. From then on Lucy explored the valley with the butterfly and they had many great adventures.

Charlie was a smart, three year-old boy. He hoped to find the perfect barber to wow his family and friends. He looked all around his neighbourhood for the right person for the job.

Finally, he found an intelligent barber who had just opened a shop. She welcomed Charlie into her chair and began to work her magic. She trimmed, cut and sculpted Charlie's hair until it was perfect.

Charlie knew he had found the perfect barber. He happily thanked her and ran home, excited to show off his new style.

His family and friends were amazed at how smart the barber must have been to make Charlie's hair look so fantastic. Charlie smiled, knowing that he had chosen the best barber in town.

He hoped to go to her again soon. It was the best decision he had ever made.

Tom and Mia are twins. They like to play with their toys. But sometimes they fight over them. One day, they find a big car in the garage. It is red and shiny. It has four wheels and a horn.

"Wow, look at this car!" Tom says. "I want to drive it!"

"No, I want to drive it!" Mia says. "You always get the best toys!"

They both run to the car and try to get in. But the car is too small for both of them. They push and pull and shout.

"Let me in, let me in!" Tom says.

"Go away, go away!" Mia says.

They are very selfish. They do not want to share the car.

Dad hears the noise and comes to the garage. He sees Tom and Mia fighting over the car. He is not happy.

"What are you doing?" he asks. "Why are you fighting over the car?"

"Because it is mine!" Tom says.

"No, it is mine!" Mia says.

Dad shakes his head. He has an idea.

"Listen, kids," he says. "The car is not yours. It is mine. I bought it for a special reason. Do you want to know what it is?"

Tom and Mia stop fighting. They are curious.

"What is it, Dad?" they ask.

Dad smiles. He opens the trunk of the car. He shows them a big bag of toys. There are dolls, cars, balls, books, and more.

"I bought this car to fill it with toys," he says. "I want to give them to the children who have no toys. They are very poor and sad. They need some joy in their lives. Do you want to help me?"

Tom and Mia look at the bag of toys. They look at each other. They feel ashamed. They have so many toys, but they are not happy. They are selfish.

They nod at Dad. They say sorry to each other. They hug.

"Yes, Dad," they say. "We want to help you. We want to share our toys with the children who have no toys. We want to make them happy."

Dad is proud of them. He hugs them back.

"Good," he says. "Let's go then. Let's fill the car with toys and drive to the orphanage. It will be a fun and kind adventure."

Tom and Mia smile. They are excited. They help Dad fill the car with toys. They get in the car and buckle up. They honk the horn.

"Beep beep!" they say.

They drive away. They are not selfish anymore. They are generous. They are happy.

Once there was a slow little frog. One day, he decided to go exploring. He hopped around in the sunshine, enjoying the season. Later on, he came across a pond. It was filled with water and little fish.

The frog wanted to poke the fish, so he hopped closer. He slowly reached out his little finger and gave the fish a gentle poke. The fish swam away. The frog laughed and hopped around some more.

He was so excited to explore every corner of the pond. He poked at all the different things he found. He found a flower and poked it. He found a log and poked it too.

Soon, he was so tired. He hopped out of the pond and started hopping back home. He had had so much fun! The slow little frog was glad he went exploring.

Lily hears Tom cry. She opens her eyes. She sees Tom. He is shaking. He is still dreaming. Lily shakes Tom. She says, "Tom, wake up! It is only a dream. The dragon is not real. You are safe."

Tom opens his eyes. He sees Lily. He sees the poppies. He sees the park. He is not dreaming. He is glad. He hugs Lily. He says, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend. You helped me. I had a bad dream. I don't like dragons."

A little girl named Amy walked by and saw Tim hiding. She said, "Why are you hiding?" Tim said, "I like to hide. It's fun!" Amy thought for a moment and then said, "I want to hide too. Can you show me the way?"

Tim smiled and said, "Yes, I can show you the way!" He took Amy's hand and led her to another big tree. They both hid behind the trees and laughed. They played hide and seek all day long, and had lots of fun together.

One day, Carl was walking around outside. It was a sunny day and Carl was happy. He saw a tall tree in the distance and ran over to it.

When he got to the tree Carl saw something funny. A big alligator was standing there, looking around. Carl smiled and said, "hello alligator!"

The alligator didn't answer. He was quiet and still. Carl was worried, so he stepped closer to the alligator and saw why he wasn't moving. The alligator was stuck in the tree! He must have been clumsy and fallen into it.

Carl knew he had to help. He searched around the tree for something to help him get the alligator out, but he could not find anything. Just then Carl noticed a stick leaning against the tree. He picked it up and stuck it between the alligator's tail and the tree. He wiggled the stick around until the alligator finally moved.

Carl had done it! The alligator thanked Carl for his help and said goodbye. Carl felt happy that he had been able to help. He watched the alligator walk away and smiled.

As the sun started to set, Lily began to shiver. She was cold and scared, and she wished she had her teddy bear to keep her warm. Suddenly, she saw a shadowy figure in the distance. It was coming closer and closer, and Lily's heart started to race.

But then, the figure got closer and Lily realized it was her mom! Her mom had found her teddy bear and was holding it out for her. Lily's face lit up with joy and she ran towards her mom with open arms. From that day on, Lily never lost her teddy bear again and always remembered to keep it close.

Once upon a time there was a brave little puppy called Mark. Mark always loved to wrap himself in his soft, warm blanket when he was feeling a bit cold or scared.

One day, Mark was walking through the forest when he heard a noise coming from deep in the woods. He was scared and thought he should duck and hide beneath his blanket. But his brave spirit told him to go and find out what was making the noise.

So slowly and carefully, Mark made his way deeper into the woods. He came to a clearing and saw a fox. The fox looked scared too.

"What are you doing here? , asked Mark.

"I was looking for a safe place to sleep, said the fox.

Mark thought for a moment, then held out his blanket. "You can come and sleep on my blanket, he said.

The fox gratefully curled up on the blanket beside Mark. The fox fell asleep and Mark lay awake, keeping watch over his brave new friend.

He made a big tower with her blocks, a race track with her cars and a mess with her crayons. He did not care about Lily's things.

Lily was angry and sad. She wanted her toys back. She said to Max, "Stop! Those are my toys! You are ruining them!" But Max did not listen. He said, "No! I want to play with them! They are fun!" He pushed Lily away and kept playing with her toys.

Lily cried and ran to her mom. She told her mom what Max did. Her mom was not happy. She said to Lily, "Don't worry, sweetie. I will talk to Max. He needs to learn to share and respect your toys." You also need to say sorry to her. How would you feel if she did the same to you?"

Max felt bad. He realized he was wrong. He said sorry to Lily and gave her back her toys. He helped her create things again and clean up the mess. He learned to share and respect her toys. Lily forgave him and hugged him. They played together nicely and had fun. They both created happy things and happy time.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park. One day, Lily went to the park with her mom. The wind was blowing hard, making the leaves on the trees rustle and dance.

Lily noticed that the swings were moving back and forth by themselves. She thought it was scary, but her mom told her not to worry. They went to play on the slide, but Lily couldn't stop thinking about the scary wind.

Suddenly, a gust of wind blew Lily's hat off her head and it flew away. Lily started to cry, but her mom hugged her and said, "Don't worry, we'll find your hat." They looked all around the park, but they couldn't find it. Just when Lily thought she would never see her hat again, a kind stranger came up to them and gave her back her hat. Lily was so happy and she realized that the wind wasn't so scary after all.

Emily reached out her little hand and pushed the car. It moved around in circles and she squealed with joy.

Just then, a little boy walked up to her. He had a toy truck and smiled at Emily.

He said, "Hi! I see you're playing with the car. Do you want to join me and race our cars?"

Emily smiled back at the boy. "Sure, that sounds like much fun!"

The two raced around the park. Emily was determined to win. She pushed her car with all her might, but the little boy was too fast. He won the race!

Emily smiled. She said, "That was a great race! You're a pretty good racer!"

The little boy smiled back. "Thanks! Maybe we can race again sometime!"

Bob looked around until he found the perfect jar. He put it down on the counter ready to make some jam.

Bob and his mum were busy making jam for most of the day. At the end of the day, Bob was so exhausted that he forgot to take the jar with him.

His mum smiled as she said, "Don't forget Jamie"! Bob smiled, ran over and grabbed the jar. He couldn't believe he'd almost forgotten it.

The jam was a success and Bob and his mum shared it with the whole family. Everyone loved it and they went home with happy memories.

Bob was a very lucky young boy. He learned a valuable lesson that day. Never forget the jam!

Once upon a time there was a boy named Tom. Tom was very restless and always wanted to explore. One day Tom went outside to play and he found an old box. Tom was so excited, he wanted to know what was inside. But before he could open it, his mum called him in for lunch.

Lunch was delicious. Tom had warm bread with butter and he yummed it up. After he finished, he quickly ran outside to look at the box again. This time, he decided to open it.

There were lots of treasures inside the box. Tom found a shiny toy car, a wooden drum and lots of cool rocks. He was so happy he wanted to take them home. But then he noticed that there was something that looked like a toy buried in the ground.

Tom knew he had to find out what it was. So he quickly grabbed a stick and started digging. Soon, he found a beautiful teddy bear. Tom was so excited, he couldn't believe his luck. He quickly buried the box again and ran home with his new special toy. He was very happy and thankful.

Once upon a time, there was a small flower called Blossom. Blossom was born into a garden full of other vibrant flowers, but it was the weakest of the bunch. Everyday, Blossom looked around and saw how the other flowers were blooming and wished that it had the strength to bloom too.

"Why can't I be like the other flowers?" Blossom asked one day. "I just want to be able to bloom too!"

The other flowers in the garden heard Blossom's cries and decided to help it. "We can help you," said the other flowers. They gathered around the weakest flower and gathered their energy to help it bloom.

Slowly, Blossom began to open up. A beautiful pink petal reached up to the sky and then another one on the other side. Before long, Blossom had bloomed into a beautiful flower and it looked so happy.

The garden was filled with joy and everyone was amazed at the sight of the little flower that had bloomed from the weakest side of the garden. From that moment onward, Blossom never felt weak again.

John was playing in the park with his friends. He saw a delicious looking cupcake in a nearby mug. He pointed at it and said, "I want that!". His friend Chloe said, "No, you can't eat that. It's not yours."

John stood and looked angry. He folded his arms and said he wanted it. Chloe just shook her head. John started feeling frustrated and he kicked the ground.

John's mom saw the children from a distance and joined them. She asked what was happening and they told her. She looked at the delicious cupcake and smiled.

Then she said, "I can give you something just as delicious, John. Then everyone will be happy." She bought John an ice cream cone and everyone enjoyed it.

Sara and Lily were sisters who liked to play together. One day, they found a bottle of perfume in their mom's room. It smelled like flowers and candy.

"Can we try it?" Sara asked.

"OK, but be careful. Mom said perfume is for special days, not for everyday," Lily said.

They sprayed some perfume on their wrists and necks. They felt very pretty and grown-up.

"Let's go show Dad," Sara said.

They ran downstairs to find their dad in the kitchen. He was making pancakes for breakfast.

"Hi, girls. You look very nice today," he said.

He hugged them and kissed their cheeks. Then he smelled something.

"What is that?" he asked.

"It's perfume, Dad. We found it in Mom's room. Do you like it?" Lily said.

Dad smiled. He was a compassionate dad. He knew the girls were curious and wanted to have fun.

"It's very nice, but it's also very strong. Maybe you used too much. How about we wash it off and save it for another day?" he said.

"OK, Dad. We're sorry. We didn't mean to make you sneeze," Sara said.

They went to the bathroom and washed their faces and hands. They closed the bottle of perfume and put it back in Mom's room.

They felt a little sad, but they also felt happy. They knew their dad loved them and wanted them to be happy too.

They went back to the kitchen and ate pancakes with their dad. They told him stories and jokes. They laughed and hugged.

They forgot about the perfume. They had a special day anyway.

Grandma was having a party. All the kids were so excited! There were balloons and candy and even a clown. Everyone was having so much fun.

After a while, Grandma brought out some toys. All the kids ran over to see their favorite toys. They all wanted to play with them.

Suddenly, one of the kids found a toy hammer. He grabbed it and started to destroy all of the other toys. He had so much fun smashing them!

The kids started to cry, but Grandma was not mad. She just said that it was an important lesson. We should always be careful with our toys and not destroy them.

The kids all agreed and the party went on. Soon, everyone was excited and playing again. Grandma was glad that the party was happy once more.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to run around and play outside. One day, she put on her favorite red shoes and ran outside to play. Her shoes were so comfy and she loved how fast she could run in them.

As she was running, she saw a little harmless bug on the ground. She thought it was so cute and wanted to pick it up. But just as she was about to, she heard a loud buzzing noise. It was a bee! Lily got scared and started to run away as fast as she could in her speedy shoes.

When she got home, Lily's mom asked her why she was running so fast. Lily told her about the bee and how she was scared. Her mom hugged her and told her that bees are harmless if you don't bother them. From that day on, Lily knew to be careful around bees, but she still loved her speedy red shoes.

Tom and Mia like to play in the park. They see many birds in the trees and on the grass. They want to catch a bird and make it their pet.

"Look, there is a red bird!" Tom says. "It is very pretty. I want to catch it."

He runs after the bird, but the bird flies away. Tom tries again and again, but the bird is too fast.

"This is difficult," Tom says. "The bird is smart. It knows I want to catch it."

Mia sees a blue bird on a bench. She walks slowly and quietly. She holds out her hand with some bread. The bird hops closer and closer. It pecks at the bread. Mia smiles and gently grabs the bird.

"I win!" Mia says. "I catch a bird. It is very soft. I will call it Blue."

Tom comes back and sees Mia with the bird. He feels sad and angry.

"That is not fair!" Tom says. "You cheat. You use bread. I want a bird too."

Mia shakes her head. "No, I do not cheat. I am clever. You are loud. The birds do not like loud. You can share my bird. We can be friends."

Tom thinks for a moment. He does not want to be mean. He wants to be friends. He nods and smiles.

"OK, we can share. Your bird is nice. Can I hold it?"

Mia nods and gives Tom the bird. The bird chirps and flaps its wings. Tom and Mia laugh and hug. They are happy. They have a bird.

Once there was a frog. He was bright green and he had big eyes. One day he was hopping around and he saw a leaf. The leaf was big and green. It looked great. So the frog jumped on to it. The leaf started to float away. The frog was so excited.

He bounced on the leaf. It felt like he was flying. The wind blew and the leaf swayed. Soon the leaf came to rest on a big pond. The frog jumped off the leaf and into the cool water. He swam around and around the pond. It was so much fun.

Soon the frog jumped back onto the leaf. He sailed across the pond. He felt like he was going on a great adventure. The leaf took him to a nearby tree. The frog hopped off the leaf and went looking for lunch. He was so happy and full of fun. The end.

One day, Sarah was walking through the park. But all around her, other children were playing. Sarah wanted to play too, but she remembered the task she had to do.

She had to clean her room. All morning she had been putting off this dull task. But now it was time, Sarah knew. She sighed and trudged up the hill, her feet dragging.

She opened the door and stopped for a moment. Looking at the room, Sarah knew it was going to be a struggle. She decided to start small, so she grabbed a toy and put it in the toy box. She quickly stuffed three more inside, feeling triumphant.

But soon, she began to tire. The next task seemed nearly impossible. But Sarah remembered all the fun she had while playing at the park. She found her motivation, and the dull task didn't seem so bad.

So Sarah worked hard. She scrubbed and swept and folded and put away her toys. After what seemed like an eternity, the task was complete. Pleased with her hard work, Sarah left her room with a smile.

Once there was a family who wanted to go out to eat. So they put on their shoes and went to the restaurant.

When they arrived, the parents noticed that the restaurant looked quite lovely. The windows were sparkly and the tablecloths were bright white.

The family walked inside and went to the counter to order. The server seemed very friendly and gave them a menu.

All of the food looked yummy! Mum and Dad ordered eggs and pancakes. The child got a cheese sandwich. They all enjoyed the food!

When they were finished, they thanked the server and left the restaurant. It had seemed like a lovely place. The family were all very happy and content.

Once upon a time there was a small rabbit named Fluffy. Fluffy was very cute and had big, floppy ears.

One day, Fluffy was hopping along through the forest on a very foggy day. The fog was so thick that Fluffy couldn't see very far. He hopped and hopped for a long time, until he stumbled across a pretty little meadow.

Fluffy was very excited when he saw the meadow. He hopped up and down, looking for something to do. Suddenly, he found a berry bush! Fluffy had never seen a berry bush before. He could hardly believe his luck.

"I have to have these berries!", Fluffy said to himself. He hopped over and grabbed a few of the berries. But just as he was about to take a bite, a loud voice yelled: "Stop!"

Fluffy was so startled that he jumped back. There was a small woman standing in front of him. She had gray hair and pointy ears. She was a fairy!

"You can have these berries if you promise to yield your first harvest to me when the time comes," the fairy said.

Fluffy thought it over and agreed. The fairy smiled, thanked him, and disappeared into the fog.

Fluffy left the meadow with a pocket full of pretty berries, proud that he had yielded for the fairy.

Mia likes to look at the stars at night. She has a big book with pictures of stars and planets. She learns their names and shapes. One night, she sees a bright light in the sky. It is a comet. A comet is a star with a long tail. Mia is very happy. She runs to tell her mom and dad.

Mom and dad are in the kitchen. They are making a cake. It is Mia's birthday tomorrow. They want to surprise her with a special cake. They have candles, sprinkles, and frosting. They are making an original cake. An original cake is a cake that no one else has. Mia loves original things. She likes to draw, sing, and make stories.

Mia tells mom and dad about the comet. They are happy too. They say the comet is a special gift for Mia. They say the comet is delivering a wish for her. Delivering means bringing something to someone. Mia thinks hard. What does she wish for? She wishes for a happy birthday with her family and friends. She wishes for a fun party with games and balloons. She wishes for a slice of the original cake.

Mom and dad hug Mia. They say they love her very much. They say they are proud of her. They say she is original too. Mia smiles. She feels warm and cozy. She thanks the comet for delivering her wish. She hopes the comet will come back again. She says good night to the comet and the stars. She goes to bed and dreams of her birthday.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys, especially her doll. One day, she found a pin on the floor and picked it up. She knew the pin was sharp and could be dangerous, so she put it away in a safe place where nobody could get hurt.

Later that day, Lily went outside to play on the swings. She loved to swing back and forth, feeling the wind in her hair. Her dad came out to push her and they had so much fun together. As they swung higher and higher, Lily felt like she was flying.

Suddenly, a big storm came and the wind started to rock the swings back and forth. But Lily and her dad were safe because they had put away the sharp pin and made sure everything around them was harmless. They held onto each other and laughed as the swings rocked in the wind.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily was playing outside when she saw a big, bright lightning bolt in the sky. She got scared and ran to her mom.

"What's wrong, Lily?" her mom asked.

"I saw a big, bright lightning in the sky. It scared me," Lily said, starting to weep.

"It's okay, sweetie. Lightning is just a big flash of light in the sky. It's loud, but it won't hurt you," her mom said, trying to calm her down.

Lily felt better knowing that lightning wasn't going to hurt her. She took a deep breath and looked up at the quiet sky. She felt safe knowing her mom was there to protect her.

Mia is a chubby cat who likes to sleep on the sofa. She has soft fur and big eyes. She thinks she is the best cat in the world. She does not like to share her sofa with anyone.

One day, a new cat comes to the house. His name is Leo and he is skinny and fast. He likes to play with toys and run around. He thinks he is the best cat in the world. He does not care about Mia's sofa.

Mia does not like Leo. She thinks he is noisy and rude. She tries to scare him away from her sofa. She hisses and scratches and puffs up her fur. But Leo does not get scared. He thinks Mia is funny and silly. He jumps on her sofa and tries to play with her.

Mia and Leo fight on the sofa. They make a lot of noise and mess. They do not see that their owner is watching them. She is not happy. She thinks they are naughty and bad. She picks them up and puts them in different rooms. She says they have to learn to get along.

Mia and Leo are sad and bored. They miss their sofa and their toys. They think about each other. They wonder if they can be friends. They decide to try. They meow and scratch at the door. They want to see each other.

Their owner hears them and opens the door. She sees Mia and Leo looking at each other with big eyes. She thinks they are cute and sweet. She smiles and lets them out. She says they can share the sofa and the toys. She says they have to be nice and gentle.

Mia and Leo go to the sofa. They sniff and nuzzle each other. They play and cuddle. They think they are the best cats in the world. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big, hairy dog named Ben. Ben loved to play with his ball. One day, Ben saw a small, hairy cat named Tom. Tom had a toy mouse. Ben wanted to play with Tom's toy mouse, but Tom did not want to share.

Ben and Tom started to fight over the toy mouse. They pulled and pulled, but neither would let go. They both loved their toys so much, they did not want to share.

Just then, a little girl named Lily came outside. She saw Ben and Tom fighting and said, "Stop! You can both play with the toys if you share." Ben and Tom stopped fighting and decided to share their toys. They played together and became best friends. From that day on, they always shared their toys and had lots of fun.

One day, a girl named Katie and her daddy went to the garage, which was very big and made out of pink bricks. Katie was very happy to go to the garage with her daddy!

They opened the garage door and went inside. Katie was amazed by all the things she saw! Daddy showed her the shelves with tools and boxes.

Then Daddy took her to a corner of the garage. There was a big pink bike! Katie smiled and clapped when she saw it. She asked Daddy if she could do it. Daddy said yes and helped her get on the bike.

Katie and Daddy went around the garage in circles. They do went back and forth and laughed every time. It was lots of fun!

At the end of their ride, Katie thanked Daddy for taking her to the garage. She said she had a great time, and she wanted to do it again soon!

Once upon a time, there was a strong winter. It was so cold that it made the wind rush around. One day, a brave little girl named Amy decided to go out into the cold. She wore a big warm coat and rushed around in the snow. She played tag with her friends, built igloos and threw snowballs. Everyone was so happy and the winter was so strong she did not get cold.

But then it started to snow and the snowflakes got bigger and bigger. Knowing it was too dangerous to stay out in the cold, Amy rushed back home. Even though she was safe and warm indoors, she still liked to look out of the window and watch the flakes fall. All of a sudden, she saw a bright yellow leaf fluttering in the snow - a sure sign of the end of winter.

Amy was so excited. She loved winter but she knew that a new season was coming soon and she wanted to explore it too. She waved goodbye to the snow, knowing that the strong winter was over.

Once there was a little girl who loved to play. She was so happy! One day she was playing with her hands in the garden. Suddenly, she saw a sweet butterfly fly by. She wanted to catch the butterfly, so she reached out with her hands. But, just as she was about to touch it, the butterfly disappeared. She looked everywhere for it, but it was gone. She couldn't believe it had disappeared so quickly. She sat down, feeling so sad that the butterfly had gone away. Suddenly, she heard a tiny voice whisper in her ear, "Don't worry. I'll be back soon." The little girl looked around and smiled, feeling happy again. Sure enough, when she looked up, the sweet butterfly had returned!

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily was playing in her backyard. She saw a bird flying in the sky and said, "Look, Mommy! A bird!"

Her mommy replied, "Yes, Lily. Birds are good animals. They can fly high in the sky and sing beautiful songs."

Lily asked, "Can we watch the bird together?"

Mommy said, "Of course, we can watch the bird together. But did you know that there are many more things to watch in the universe? The universe is very big and has many stars and planets."

Lily looked up at the sky and said, "Wow! The universe is so pretty. I want to watch it every day with you, Mommy."

Mommy smiled and said, "I would love that, Lily. Watching the universe with you is one of my favorite things to do."

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tweetie. Tweetie loved to sing songs. One day, Tweetie's favorite tree fell down and she couldn't sing her songs anymore. Tweetie was sad and didn't know what to do.

Then, a kind squirrel came along and saw Tweetie was sad. The squirrel asked, "What's wrong, little bird?" Tweetie replied, "My tree fell down and now I can't sing my songs." The squirrel said, "Don't be angry, little bird. We can replace your tree with a new one."

So, the squirrel helped Tweetie find a new tree to sing her songs in. Tweetie was so happy and sang a beautiful song to thank the squirrel. From that day on, Tweetie sang her songs every day in her new tree and was never sad again.

Once upon a time, there was a big jungle. In the jungle, there was a gorilla. The gorilla was very big and strong. But one day, a young gorilla came to the jungle. He was not as big as the other gorilla.

The young gorilla seemed scared of the other animals in the jungle. But the big gorilla saw this and decided to help him. The big gorilla showed the young gorilla how to swing from tree to tree and how to find food.

After some time, the young gorilla wasn't scared anymore. He seemed happy and strong, just like the big gorilla. They became friends and played together in the jungle every day. The end.

Lily was a curious girl who loved animals. She always asked her mom and dad to take her to the zoo, where she could see the lions, the elephants, and her favorite, the gorillas. She liked to watch them swing from the trees, eat bananas, and make funny noises.

One day, Lily received a big surprise. It was her birthday, and her mom and dad gave her a huge box wrapped in colorful paper. Lily tore off the paper and opened the box. Inside, she found a stuffed gorilla that looked just like the ones at the zoo. It had soft fur, big eyes, and a friendly smile.

"Wow, thank you, mom and dad!" Lily exclaimed. "I love it! Can I name it Gigi?"

"Of course, sweetie," her mom said. "Gigi is your new friend. You can play with her, hug her, and take her wherever you go."

Lily was so happy. She picked up Gigi and gave her a big kiss. Then she ran to her room, where she had a tea party with Gigi and her other toys. She told Gigi stories about the zoo, and pretended that Gigi could talk back to her.

"Gigi, you are the best birthday present ever," Lily said. "You are a curious gorilla, just like me. We are going to have so much fun together."

Once upon a time, there were two little girls called Lily and Sarah. They were playing in the garden and having lots of fun. They liked to run around, jump and laugh.

Then suddenly, they both felt a splash on their feet. It was mud!

Lily laughed and said to Sarah, "Let's go play in the mud!"

Sarah replied, "But it's warm!"

Lily smiled. "That's even better!"

So the two little girls stopped playing and ran off to the mud patch in the garden. They laughed and dipped their hands in the mud, making mud pies and mud cakes.

They were having lots of fun until Sarah said it was time to stop. They both knew that their parents would not be happy if they got too muddy! So Lily and Sarah said goodbye to the mud and went back to playing in the garden.

Once there was a bunny. He was very small and loved to crawl around. One day, he decided to go on an adventure. He crawled through the tall grass, jumping over logs and hopping over stones. He kept going and going until he reached a beautiful creek.

The bunny was amazed at how pretty it was. He watched the fish swim in the sparkly water and the birds fly above him. He wanted to take a closer look so he started to crawl along the side of the creek. Suddenly, he heard a sound.

He looked around and saw a little puppy stuck in the mud. The bunny was very compassionate and didn't want the puppy to stay stuck, so he inched closer and closer and started to help her get out. With his efforts, the puppy was finally back on solid ground.

The puppy was so grateful to have been rescued and the bunny was very proud of himself. They both raced off back to the bunny's burrow and since then, they have been the best of friends.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. He was on a journey and he had a very special job to do.

Every day he carried a pot of boiling soup and a wooden spoon. He stirred the soup with the spoon and it smelled so yummy.

He went along a big road and through a dark forest. He was going to a very special place. He was so excited for his journey.

When he arrived at the special place, he saw a little village. He knew he was almost there. He smiled as he stirred his soup and said "I'm almost finished!"

The little boy decided to take a break and enjoy the sights. After a while, he said goodbye to the village, got back on the road and continued his journey.

He arrived at the special place just as the sun was setting. He gave the soup to the people in the village and they were so happy.

The little boy had finished his journey and he was very proud. He made a little soup every day and stirred it with his special spoon. He was so brave and so little, but he had done a very important job.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Bob and Lucy. They were both playing with a bouncy ball in the park. It was a warm and sunny day, so Bob and Lucy decided to have a picnic. Lucy picked up a pan and filled it with her favourite treats.

"I can't wait to eat this", said Bob as he saw the treats. But then Lucy tasted some of them and made a face.

"This doesn't taste good," Lucy said. "It's very bitter!"

Bob tasted it too, and with a disappointed expression he said: "I don't like this either."

Lucy thought for a moment and then she said: "Let's go and get an ice-cream! That's always sweet and delicious!"

The two friends smiled and ran off to get some ice-cream. On their way, they bounced the bouncy ball around together. They were both so happy and excited!

When they arrived at the ice-cream shop, they were thrilled to discover that there were so many different flavours to choose from. They each chose their favourite flavour and enjoyed them together in the sun.

The two friends had a great day together, and the next day they both agreed that the ice-cream was much sweeter and more delicious than the bitter treats in the pan!

Once upon a time there was a rich elephant who wanted a ball. He looked high and low, in the sky and on the ground. Everywhere he looked, he just couldn't find the right ball.

So the elephant decided to search a little bit further. He walked through the forest and then he saw it! In the river was a big, round, colourful ball. He was so excited, he wanted the ball straight away.

The elephant ran to the river and took a deep breath. He dived right in and grabbed the ball. He lifted the heavy ball to the surface, and it was his.

He was so happy, he jumped and shouted. With the ball in his trunk, he skipped out of the forest and went back home with a smile.

Tom and Mia liked to play outside with their toys. They had a big truck, a doll, a ball, and a kite. One day, they saw a big cloud in the sky. It was dark and scary. It made a loud noise like a train.

"Look, Mia, a tornado!" Tom said. He pointed at the cloud.

"A tornado?" Mia asked. She did not know what a tornado was.

"It is a big wind that spins and breaks things," Tom said. He had seen a tornado on TV. "We have to go inside. It is not safe."

Tom and Mia grabbed their toys and ran to the house. They did not want the tornado to take their toys. They loved their toys. They were expensive. Their mom and dad had bought them for their birthday.

But they forgot one thing. They forgot their kite. The kite was still in the air. The string was tied to a tree. The tornado saw the kite. It liked the kite. It was colorful and shiny. The tornado wanted the kite.

The tornado came closer and closer to the kite. It reached out with its wind and pulled the string. The string broke. The kite flew away with the tornado.

Tom and Mia saw what happened. They were sad. They liked their kite. It was their favorite toy.

"Bye, kite," they said. They waved at the sky.

They went inside and hugged their mom and dad. They were safe. They still had their other toys. They decided to play with them in their room.

They put their truck and their doll on the floor. They threw their ball in the air. They pretended to march with their toys. They sang a song.

They were happy. They forgot about the tornado and the kite. They had fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. He was a bright and happy three-year-old. He was always exploring and learning new things.

One day, Timmy wanted to go on an adventure. He wanted to cross the nearby river to explore the other side. But he was scared and hesitated.

"Mommy, Timmy said, "I don't trust myself to cross the river.

"Oh, Timmy, his mommy said, "You don't need to be afraid. Just trust your own wisdom. Your bright mind will guide you.

Hearing his mommy's encouraging words, Timmy's fear melted away. He stepped onto the bridge and crossed the river to explore the other side. He found many new friends and exciting places!

Timmy learned that day that he can trust himself and his own wisdom. He was a bright and courageous boy!

Lucy was three years old and she loved to play. One day, she was playing in the garden when she got a surprise.

A fine, white letter fell from the sky. Lucy was amazed. She picked up the letter and opened it. Inside there was a smooth stone.

Suddenly, Lucy started to sneeze. She put the letter down and went inside. Soon, the sneezes stopped.

She looked out of the window and saw a magnificent rainbow in the sky. Then she knew - the stone was a gift from the rainbow.

Lucy smiled and held the stone tight. She was the luckiest three year old in the world!

The animals went to see what was happening. To their surprise, they found Fluffy. He had spread out all his food, and he was teaching other turkeys how to pick up food. Fluffy was being very wise, sharing his knowledge with the other turkeys!

The animals were so proud of Fluffy, and they clapped for him. From that day on, Fluffy shared his knowledge with all the turkeys in the forest, teaching them to be wise.
