Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a brown teddy bear that she loved very much. She would always carry him in her pocket when she went out to play.

One day, Lily went to the park with her parents. She was having so much fun playing on the swings and the slide that she forgot about her teddy bear in her pocket. When it was time to go home, Lily realized that her teddy bear was missing. She searched and searched but he was nowhere to be found.

Lily was very sad and cried all the way home. Her parents promised to help her find her teddy bear the next day. The next morning, Lily woke up early and went back to the park with her parents. They looked everywhere but still couldn't find her teddy bear.

Lily was heartbroken and missed her teddy bear so much. She never forgot to check her pocket again before leaving the house. From that day on, Lily learned that it's important to take care of the things you love or you might miss them forever.

Tim and Sue are friends. They like to play in the field near their home. The field has many flowers and bugs and birds. Tim and Sue run and laugh and have fun.

One day, a big dog comes to the field. The dog is not healthy. He has dirt and cuts and fleas on his fur. He is hungry and angry. He sees Tim and Sue and barks loud.

"Go away, dog!" Tim says. "This is our field. You cannot play here."

But the dog does not listen. He runs fast to Tim and Sue. He bites Tim on his leg. Tim cries and falls down. Sue is scared. She tries to help Tim.

"Help, help!" Sue shouts. "Someone come and help us!"

But no one hears Sue. The field is far from the houses. The dog bites Sue on her arm. Sue cries and falls down too. The dog eats their food and toys. He leaves Tim and Sue alone. They are hurt and sad.

The end.

Once there was a little girl named Emmy. She was only three years old, and one day she decided to make something special. She wanted to make cheese!
So Emmy started making the cheese, but it ended up looking really silly. It was a smushed, lumpy mess. Emmy's mom saw it and laughed, which made Emmy feel really embarrassed.

The next day, Emmy decided to try again. She made a more rounded cheese this time, and it looked better. It was still lumpy, though. Emmy's mom saw it again and this time she said something very harsh.

Emmy was so embarrassed, she felt like she wanted to hide. But she was brave and held her head up high, even though her mom's words felt very mean.

Finally, after a few more tries, Emmy got the cheese just right. She proudly showed it to her mom and waited for her reaction. Her mom said it was perfect and gave her a big hug. Emmy was so happy that all the embarrassment had gone away!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to swim in the pool with her friends. One day, Lily's friend dared her to jump off the high diving board. Lily was scared because it was very high, but she didn't want to look like a scaredy-cat in front of her friends, so she agreed.

Lily climbed up the ladder and stood at the edge of the diving board. She looked down and it seemed very far away. She felt scared and didn't want to jump anymore. But her friend shouted, "Come on, Lily! You can do it!" So, Lily took a deep breath and jumped into the pool.

After she got out of the pool, Lily's friend said, "Wow, you're so brave! I could never do that." Lily felt happy that she had dared to do something scary. She learned that sometimes it's okay to be scared, but it's important to be brave too. And she also learned that being harsh to someone is not a good idea, because it can hurt their feelings.

Once upon a time, there was a graceful bell. The bell glistened in the sun and sparkled in the night. It was so beautiful that everyone who walked by it stopped to stare. One day, a little boy walking by the bell stopped and said, "I want to hear the bell!" He took his hand and closed the bell. Then, he heard a beautiful sound that he'd never heard before. He was so happy that he laughed and smiled. Then, he closed the bell again and heard the same beautiful sound. Everyone around him clapped and cheered for the little boy's bravery. From then on, the bell was closed but the beautiful sound was never forgotten.

Kathy and her friends were playing in the park. They were looking for adventure. Suddenly, they saw a big maze. It looked like a harmless puzzle.

"Let's dive in and explore the maze," Kathy said to her friends.

So they went in and were amazed by the many twists and turns. They laughed and ran and found lots of fun things.

After a while, they came to a clearing in the center of the maze. There, they found a sparkling pond. The pond was so inviting that they decided to dive in.

The water was cool and refreshing. Kathy and her friends had so much fun diving and playing in the pond. They stayed in the pond until they got too tired.

"Let's go home now," Kathy said.

So, they waved goodbye to the harmless maze and went home with happy memories.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tommy. Tommy was very hungry and he wanted to find something to eat. He looked outside and saw the sunshine. Tommy walked outside and saw that the sunshine was warm. He removed some of his clothes and lay down in the sunshine. The sunshine felt so nice and he forgot about being hungry.

He lay there for a long time, until he decided to eat. So he got up and went for a walk. He was happy because the sunshine was with him. He soon saw some fruit trees and he was so excited. He removed some of the fruit and ate it. He felt so much better. The sunshine had made him forget about being hungry!

It was a bright and sunny day. Mary was outside with her dad in the garden. She saw a strange pipe in the grass.

"What is that? asked Mary.

"That's a pipe, said her dad.

"What's it for? she asked.

"Nobody knows. It's an unknown pipe.

Mary was curious. She walked over to the pipe and stretched out her arms. She couldn't reach it, so she stretched her legs too. Her dad walked over and picked her up, and together they stretched over to the pipe.

Mary grabbed the pipe. She was so happy! "Let's take it home! she said excitedly.

Her dad smiled. They grabbed the pipe and went home. Mary and her dad never found out what the pipe was for, but it was fun to try and find out.

One day, two friends named Jack and Jill went on a walk. Jack had a big bag of candy in his pocket. Jill wanted some candy, but Jack wanted to trade for something else. Jill thought it was a difficult decision, so she walked away and ignored Jack's offer.

Jack was sad that Jill didn't want to trade with him. He thought of all the fun things they could have done with the candy, like make a delicious treat. But Jill was still ignoring him, so he shouted, "Hey Jill, why are you ignoring me?"

Jill looked back and said, "Trading candy is too difficult for me. I just don't want to think about it right now." Jack was disappointed, but he understood why Jill didn't want to trade. He tried to cheer her up by offering her a big hug, and she smiled and said, "Let's just take a break and go for a walk again."

They had a great time on their walk, and Jack and Jill eventually forgot about their trade. They were best friends forever and would never ignore each other again.

Anna and Ben liked to play pretend. They liked to pretend they were grown-ups and had a big house and a big car. One day, they decided to pretend they were getting married. They put on their best clothes and found some flowers and rings.

They went to the living room, where there was a big bookshelf. They said the bookshelf was the church, and the books were the people. They asked Anna's teddy bear to be the priest. The teddy bear said, "Do you, Anna, take Ben to be your husband?" Anna said, "I do." The teddy bear said, "Do you, Ben, take Anna to be your wife?" Ben said, "I do."

Then they put the rings on each other's fingers and kissed. The books clapped and cheered. They were very happy. They said they wanted to have a cake and a dance. They went to the kitchen, where Anna's mom was baking cookies. They told her they had just got married and asked for some cake. Anna's mom smiled and said, "That's very sweet, but you are too young to get married. You are just playing pretend. And you can't have cake, but you can have some cookies."

Anna and Ben were a little sad, but they understood. They said thank you and took some cookies. They went back to the living room and ate their cookies. They said they still loved each other and wanted to stay together. They hugged and danced and laughed. They were very silly and very happy. They said they would play pretend again tomorrow.

Once upon a time there lived a little girl called Annie. She was only three years old and very curious. Whenever she went outside with her mom, she liked to explore the world around her.

One day, Annie and her mom went for a walk in the park. As they passed the market, Annie got excited and asked her mom if she could go and take a look. Her mom said yes and Annie ran forward, eager to discover all the wonders in the market.

When she reached the market, Annie saw a vendor selling yummy treats. She was fascinated and wanted to try every one. Annie asked her mom if she could purchase one, but her mom said, "No, not today.

Annie was helpless. She went back to her mom feeling sad and disappointed. But her mom had a surprise for her--a special treat that she had packed. Annie was overjoyed and couldn't stop thanking her mom. She ate the treat eagerly, and the hot sun and the lovely walk in the park made her sleepy. Annie hugged her mom and dozed off in her arms.

It had been a wonderful day.

Once upon a time, there was a young girl named Amy. She loved to sail in her little boat. She had big dreams and was determined to make them come true.

One day, Amy found a blackboard. She took the blackboard and put it in her boat, so she could sail with it. She was so excited to have something new to explore.

The next day, they set sail. The water was calm and the journey was safe. As they sailed, Amy used the blackboard to draw pictures of the places they were passing.

Suddenly, they heard a voice calling out to them. It was a mermaid! She said, "If you can make it across the sea, I will give you a prize!"

Amy was so excited. She sailed as fast as she could, but the journey was long. Eventually they made it, and Amy and her boat were safe. The mermaid kept her word and rewarded Amy with a magical trinket.

Amy was so happy. She thanked the mermaid for this special prize and sailed back home.

Amy enjoyed her adventure and remembered it for a long time.

The end.

Sara and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. Sara has a red bow in her hair. Tom has a blue bow on his shirt. They like to bow to each other and say hello.

One day, Sara and Tom go to the park. They see a big dog. The dog is black and brown. The dog has a green bow on its collar. The dog is very friendly. It wags its tail and licks their faces.

"Hello, dog!" Sara and Tom say. They bow to the dog. The dog bows back. They laugh and pet the dog.

But then, a man comes. The man is the dog's owner. He has a leash in his hand. He calls the dog.

"Come, Rex!" the man says. "It's time to go home!"

The dog hears the man. It runs to the man. It barks and jumps. The man puts the leash on the dog.

"Bye, dog!" Sara and Tom say. They wave to the dog. The dog waves back with its paw.

But Sara and Tom are sad. They like the dog. They want to play with the dog more.

"Can we see the dog again?" Sara asks the man.

"Maybe," the man says. "We come to the park sometimes. Maybe you can find us again."

Sara and Tom nod. They hope to see the dog again. They hug each other and go home. They still have their bows. They are still friends. But they miss the dog.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jill. She was very cute and liked to play outside. One day, she decided to go explore the woods. As she walked, she saw a shiny red object. She wanted to see what it was, so she reached out for it. It was an emergency button! Jill was so excited. She quickly pushed the button and suddenly, a big red truck came. It was the fire truck! A firefighter came out and thanked Jill for her help. She was so proud of herself. From then on, when Jill was out playing, she made sure to never forget to reach for the emergency button if she ever needed help.

Molly was very lonely. She had no friends to play with her so she often sat alone in her room. One day, she decided to move. She went to her living room and found an old picture frame. It was covered with dust and had a few scratches on the back. She picked it up and moved it to the sunny corner by her favourite window.

Molly moved the frame around until it caught the first glimpse of sunshine. She sat in front of it and had a nice time. The warmth of the sun made her feel happy and less lonely. She moved a few small things around to make the room prettier. Molly smiled and really enjoyed the change.

She moved the frame around until it gave her the best view of the neighbourhood. Her lonely afternoons were filled now with the beauty of the nature surrounding her. Molly was so happy with her new environment and she even started to make friends with some of the kids around.

Molly understood that changing things can make a big difference and made sure to keep her frame in the sunny corner of her living room.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby bunny living in the forest. He loved jumping up and down and bouncing all day.

He had lots of fun hopping around and playing with his friends. He would hop through the tall trees and hide in the bushes where no one could find him. All the animals could hear him giggling and bouncing around.

One day, the bunny spotted a big, red hoop. He hopped over to it, and saw that it was just perfect for bouncing! He hopped in and was surprised to find that he could bounce higher than ever before. He bounced so high that eventually he touched the clouds!

Filled with excitement, the bunny hopped and bounced around the forest all day. He bounced with his friends and even made up some new games to play.

At the end of the day, the chubby bunny was tired and happy. He had so much fun jumping and bouncing around the forest!

Lily was very tired after playing all day. She wanted to relax and read her favorite book. But when she went to her room, she saw a big mess. Her toys, clothes and books were everywhere. She could not find her book in the drawer.

"Who made this mess?" she asked in a loud voice.

"It was me, Lily. I'm sorry," said her little brother Ben. He came out from under the bed. He had a guilty look on his face.

"Why did you do that, Ben?" Lily asked. She was angry and sad.

"I wanted to play with your toys and read your books. But I could not put them back. I did not know where they belong," Ben said. He looked down.

Lily sighed. She loved her brother, but he was very naughty sometimes. She decided to help him clean up the mess. She showed him where to put the toys, the clothes and the books. She found her book in the drawer and gave it to him.

"Here, Ben. You can read this book with me. But next time, please ask me first. And don't make a mess in my room," she said.

"OK, Lily. Thank you. I'm sorry. I love you," Ben said. He hugged her.

Lily smiled. She hugged him back. She was not angry anymore. She was happy to share her book with him. They sat on the bed and read the book together. They relaxed and enjoyed the story.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely rose. It lived in a big garden with no other flowers. The rose was very sad because it had no friends.

One day, a little girl named Lily came to the garden. She saw the lonely rose and said, "Why are you so sad, rose?" The rose replied, "I am lonely. I have no friends to play with."

Lily wanted to help the rose. She went to her mom and asked for help. Her mom said, "Let's heal the rose's sadness by planting more flowers in the garden." So, Lily and her mom planted many flowers around the lonely rose.

Soon, the rose had lots of friends to play with. The rose was very happy and thanked Lily for her help. From that day on, the rose was never lonely again.

Jack put his hand on her shoulder and said, "No, no! You have to be careful!

Jill listened to Jack and put the rock down. After that they explored the camp and had a lot of fun!

John said: "Mom, can I pick the flower?"

His mom nodded and said: "Okay, but be careful. Remember it is delicate."

John carefully picked the flower. He put it carefully in the drain. It was a big drain.

John felt proud. He had picked a delicate flower and put it in the drain.

John smiled. He had done a good job.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lisa. She wanted to do something special. She went to look for a ladder. Lisa found a ladder which was very tall. She wanted to use the ladder, but she knew she couldn't do it on her own. She looked around and asked for help.

Suddenly she saw a man. He was independent. He helped Lisa carry the ladder. Lisa was very happy.

With the help of the man, Lisa and the ladder arrived at her destination. When they got there, they were both surprised to see what was revealed - a beautiful garden with colorful flowers and vegetables.

Lisa and the man were happy and excited. Lisa thanked the man for his help. She felt proud and independent for doing something on her own.

Molly was excited. She had been waiting all afternoon for her mom to come home from work. As soon as she heard the door open, Molly clapped her little hands and ran to greet her.

"Molly, I have a surprise for you!" said Mommy, pulling out a cup of yogurt. She opened it up and the aroma filled their kitchen. Molly's eyes lit up. But then Mommy said "But you can not have it just yet! You must wait until after dinner". Molly groaned, but Mommy was firm.

At dinner, Molly was so hungry that she ate quickly. When she finished, Mommy said "Now it's time for your surprise!" She smiled and gave Molly the yogurt.

Molly dug in and it was delicious. She ate until the cup was empty. Then Mommy said, "Do you remember why I asked you to wait until after dinner?". Molly shook her head. Mommy said: "It's because the yogurt was too cold and harsh for your tummy. If you had eaten it too early, it could have made you feel bad!"

Molly nodded, understanding the lesson. She smiled and clapped for her mommy for being so wise.

Once upon a time there were two best friends. Sarah was small and gentle and Lily was fierce and blue. One day Sarah asked Lily to join her on an adventure.

They set off on a wild and exciting journey that took them over hills, through woods and around rivers. After they had been travelling for some time, Sarah said, "Let's stop, there is something I want to show you."

Lily was amazed. In front of them was a giant mountain with shining jewels on it. Sarah said, "Let's try and remove these jewels from the mountain, so we can take them home and make something special with them."

Lily was very fierce and tried hard to remove the jewels from the mountain, but it was too strong. Sarah was small but her heart was big. She thought of an idea, and said, "Why don't we work together? If we work together, we can do it."

Lily and Sarah joined hands and tried to remove the jewels. They pulled, they tugged and they pushed. After a long time of struggling, the jewels were finally removed from the mountain.

The two friends were so happy that they had succeeded. They took the jewels home and made something truly special - a necklace of shimmering blue jewels, just for Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy had a pet cat named Mittens. One day, Timmy and Mittens went for a walk in the park. They saw a big tree with a patch of flowers underneath it. Timmy picked a flower and gave it to Mittens to smell. Mittens liked the flower, but Timmy accidentally touched a sharp thorn on the stem and it hurt his finger. He said "ouch!" and Mittens licked his finger to make it feel better.

After a while, Timmy and Mittens got thirsty, so they went to a lemonade stand. The lemonade was too sour for Timmy, but Mittens liked it. They sat down on a bench and watched the people walking by. Suddenly, Timmy saw a little girl crying because she had lost her doll. Timmy and Mittens went to help her look for it. They searched and searched until they found the doll in a patch of grass. The little girl was very happy and thanked Timmy and Mittens. They all became friends and played together in the park. The end.

Once upon a time there was a hunter. He wanted to catch a bear today so he got up early in the morning and prepared his modern tools and equipment. He was all ready, he just had to hurry.

The hunter began walking through the woods. He had been walking for over an hour, when suddenly he heard a noise. He stopped, listened and heard it again. It was the sound of a big bear!

The hunter picked up his pace and started hurrying. Faster and Faster.

The hunter soon saw the bear and quickly yelled, "Stop! I'm a hunter and I have to catch you!"

The bear looked at the hunter and shouted back, " I won't be caught easily. Hurry and you might still get me!"

The hunter ran faster and faster, trying to outrun the bear, but it was no use. The bear was too fast.
In the end, the hunter had to slow to a walk and go back home. He knew he had to come up with a better plan before he could catch the bear.

Emma was so excited today! She was getting ready to go to a costume party. She had the silliest costume she could find: a bright pink elephant with grey spots. Emma laughed as she put it on.

When she arrived to the party, Emma noticed all the other kids were in fancy costumes. Emma was embarrassed and wished she had a nicer costume. But soon enough, she forgot all about it as she started playing games and eating sweet treats.

At the end of the party, Emma looked for her costume. But it was gone! Emma didn't know what happened. The host said someone must have taken it by accident. Emma was so sad to have lost her silly costume, but she was happy to learn that the host would give her a new one so she can still go to the next costume party.

Tom likes to help his dad fix things. He has his own tool box with a hammer, a screwdriver and a wrench. He wants to be a good helper.

One day, dad is working on the car. He needs a tool from his big tool box. He asks Tom to bring him the tool. "Tom, can you bring me the pliers, please? They are in the big tool box, next to the car."

Tom is happy to help. He runs to the big tool box and looks for the pliers. He sees many tools, but he does not know which one is the pliers. He sees a shiny tool with a sharp end. He thinks it looks cool. He picks it up and runs to dad.

"Here, dad, I found the tool!" he says, holding the shiny tool.

Dad looks at the tool and frowns. "Tom, that is not the pliers. That is a knife. A knife is a bad tool for fixing cars. It can cut you or the car. Put it back in the tool box, please. And be careful with it."

Tom feels sad. He did not want to bring a bad tool. He did not want to hurt dad or the car. He says sorry to dad and puts the knife back in the tool box. He looks for the pliers. He sees a tool with two handles and a metal mouth. He thinks it looks like the pliers. He picks it up and runs to dad.

"Here, dad, I found the pliers!" he says, holding the tool.

Dad smiles and takes the tool. "Yes, Tom, that is the pliers. Thank you, Tom. You are a good helper. You found the right tool. Now we can fix the car together."

Tom feels happy. He likes to help dad. He likes to learn about tools. He likes to fix things. He hugs dad and says, "I love you, dad. You are the best."

Lily and Max liked to play with water. They had a big hose in the garden that could spray water very far. They also had a skeleton toy that they found in a box. The skeleton was made of plastic and had bones that could move.

One day, they decided to play a trick on their dad. They hid the skeleton behind a bush and waited for him to come home. When they saw his car, they turned on the hose and got ready to spray him.

But their dad was very smart. He saw the hose and the bush and guessed what they were doing. He sneaked behind them and grabbed the hose. Then he sprayed Lily and Max with water and made them wet.

Lily and Max screamed and laughed. They saw the skeleton and ran to get it. They wanted to spray their dad back. But their dad was faster. He took the skeleton and sprayed it too. He made the skeleton wet and dirty.

Lily and Max were angry. They thought their dad was rude. They wanted their skeleton back. They said, "Dad, give us our skeleton! You are rude! You made it wet and dirty!"

Their dad smiled and said, "I'm sorry, kids. I was just playing. Here is your skeleton. But you have to promise me something. No more spraying me with water, OK?"

Lily and Max nodded and said, "OK, dad. We promise. But can we spray each other?"

Their dad said, "Sure, you can. But only if you are careful and have fun. And don't forget to dry the skeleton later. It might catch a cold."

Lily and Max hugged their dad and said, "We love you, dad. You are not rude. You are funny."

Then they took the skeleton and the hose and played with water. They sprayed each other and the skeleton and had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time there was a fierce sheep called Oat. Oat lived in the fields and loved to play in the sunshine. Every day, Oat grazed and gamboled with the other sheep and barnyard animals.

One day Oat noticed a strange sound in the air. That sound was so fierce that it made all the animals scared. Oat remain still, in case the noise was dangerous. Then a big bird flew by and the animals all remained still until it had gone.

Oat felt brave and decided to remain in the field. He watched the bird as it soared away and became a tiny speck in the sky. As time went on, the animals started to get back to their playing and the fierce sound faded away.

Oat started to graze again and found some tasty oats to enjoy. He curled up in the sunshine and enjoyed being safe and warm. Oat knew that, no matter what happened, it was important to remain brave and never give up.

John was three years old and he loved playing outside. One day he was out exploring and he saw a yellow ball. He was so excited he ran over to it.

John shouted, "It's a yellow ball! I want to play with it!"

He picked it up, but oh no! It was broken. He started to cry.

Then he heard a voice from above. It was his mom.

She said, "John, don't cry. Let me help you." She took the yellow ball and held it tightly in her hand.

John watched as she blew on it three times. Then mom said, "Now try it again."

John was amazed! The yellow ball worked perfectly! He was so happy that he started to repeat, "Thank you mom, thank you."

His mom smiled and said, "You're welcome, now go play!"

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sarah. She wanted to be an ambulance driver one day but she thought it was too difficult. Every day as she drove her toy ambulance around the garden, Sarah's mom would try to encourage her.

"You can do it, Sarah," said her mom. "If you keep trying and never give up, you will succeed."

So Sarah kept driving her toy ambulance and, over time, she began to get better at it. She learned how to be careful and follow the rules. Even when things got hard, Sarah kept going.

One day, Sarah got a call from the real ambulance. Someone needed help, but the roads were blocked. Everyone was worried, but Sarah remembered what her mom said. She bravely worked hard and eventually she managed to get the ambulance to the person who needed help.

Sarah had succeeded in her task and everyone was so happy. From then on, Sarah decided that she would never give up, no matter how difficult things seemed. Learning to never give up and to keep trying is the most important lesson of all.

Once upon a time there was a little bunny. He was very hungry, so he wanted to go and find some food. He hopped out of his burrow and trotted along in search of food.

As he hopped along, he came across a big, comfy couch. He hopped up onto the couch and lay down, exhausted from his journey. Just then, he heard a voice coming from the couch.

"Hello, little bunny," the voice said. "Why are you so hungry?"

The bunny said, "I'm very hungry. Can you help me find some food?"

The voice said, "Of course, I can help. Jump down and follow me, I know where the food is!"

The little bunny hopped down from the couch and followed the voice. After a short journey, the voice pointed out some yummy carrots growing in a nearby garden.

The bunny hopped over to the carrots and started to eat. He was so happy that he was no longer hungry. He thanked the voice and then hopped back home.

Lily and Tom were twins who liked to fold paper. They could fold paper into many shapes, like birds, boats and hats. One day, they decided to fold paper for their grandma, who was coming to visit them.

They took out many papers of different colors and started to fold them on the floor. Lily folded a red paper into a heart and said, "This is for grandma, because I love her." Tom folded a blue paper into a star and said, "This is for grandma, because she is bright and kind."

They folded many papers until they had a big pile of shapes. They put them in a basket and waited for grandma to arrive. They heard a knock on the door and ran to open it. But instead of grandma, they saw a big dog with sharp teeth and loud barks. The dog had escaped from the neighbor's yard and was looking for food.

Lily and Tom were frightened and screamed. They dropped the basket and the paper shapes flew everywhere. The dog saw the paper and thought it was food. He jumped on the paper and tore it with his teeth. Lily and Tom cried and hid behind the couch.

Their mom heard the noise and came to see what was wrong. She saw the dog and the paper mess and quickly called the neighbor. The neighbor came and took the dog away. He said he was sorry and that the dog was not mean, just hungry.

Lily and Tom were still scared and sad. They hugged their mom and said, "Mom, the dog ruined our paper shapes for grandma. We wanted to give them to her as a gift. Now we have nothing."

Their mom hugged them back and said, "Don't worry, my loves. Grandma will understand. She will be happy to see you, not the paper. You can still give her your hugs and kisses. That is the best gift of all."

Lily and Tom felt a little better and nodded. They helped their mom clean up the paper and waited for grandma again. When grandma finally came, they ran to her and gave her their hugs and kisses. Grandma smiled and said, "Thank you, my darlings. You are the best gift of all."

Once upon a time, there was a mischievous seal named George. Every day, George liked to play pranks on his neighbors and cause trouble! One day, he decided to spray orange paint on the homes of his friends and family.

"George, why did you spray orange paint on everyone's houses?" asked his mother.

George giggled and said, "I just wanted to have fun!" His mother looked very unhappy.

"George, you have to stop this bad behavior!" his mom warned.

But George was stubborn and he continued to spray paint on the homes of everyone he knew. His friends and family got very angry and decided to hatch a plan to stop him.

They decided to capture George and convince him to stop his mischievous behavior. One day, they finally managed to catch George and showed him how his misbehavior had affected others.

George felt really sad about what he had done and he vowed never to spray orange paint again. He apologized to everyone and from that day on, he was a good seal and was no longer causing trouble in the neighborhood.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Toby and Lola. Toby was only three years old, but he had a bright mind. They were walking together in the park when Toby saw an old rock on the ground. "Look at that old rock," Toby said to his friend.

"Let's roll it to the top of the hill," suggested Lola. "We can make it to the top together and the view will be beautiful."

Toby excitedly agreed and the two started rolling. At first it seemed impossible, as if the rock was too heavy. But the more they pushed and rolled, the lighter the rock felt.

After a while, they managed to meet the top of the hill, and the view was astonishingly beautiful. The sky was bright, and they admired it with admiration.

Lola turned to Toby and said, "See? If you work hard together with your friends, anything seems possible!"

Toby and Lola went back home with this moral lesson. Whenever Toby worked together with his friends, and never thought things to be too hard, then he learned that anything was possible.

One day his dad asked him, "What do you want to do with your wand?"
The little boy had a good idea. He wanted to use the wand to make all his toys dance.
So his dad said, "OK, let's try it, but be careful."
The boy smiled and then he paused for a moment. He closed his eyes and waved the wand around, saying a special magic word.
Suddenly, the boy's toys started to move. They twirled and turned and the boy started to laugh.
He clapped his hands and said, "That was awesome!"
His dad smiled and said, "Yes it was. Now let's take a break and have a snack!"
The little boy nodded and together they enjoyed their snack. He was so happy to have a magic wand and he was determined to be careful with it!

Once there was a girl named Lucy. She was only three years old, but already she was so beautiful. She was always full of laughter and smiles.

One day she was walking by the beach when she saw a tiny box with something inside of it. She opened the box and inside she found a small puppy! Lucy was so happy and wanted to keep the pup. She named it emergency because she knew it needed a home.

Lucy was so excited to have emergency in her life. She wanted the pup to belong with her always. She took emergency home and gave it lots of love and attention.

emergency fit right in with Lucy's family and they all loved him. Everywhere Lucy went, emergency followed closely behind. Lucy and emergency were best friends and even though emergency was a tiny pup, he always found ways to bring lots of joy to Lucy's life. All of Lucy's days were made even more beautiful with emergency by her side.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She went to the movies with her mom and dad. They sat in the back row of seats. Lily loved sitting in the back row because she could see everything!

During the movie, Lily spilled her popcorn all over the floor. Her mom said, "Oh no, Lily! That's a terrible mess!" Lily felt sad, but her dad said, "Don't worry, we'll clean it up after the movie."

After the movie, Lily and her family went to the front row to ask the movie theater worker for a broom. The worker said, "Sure, I'll get you one." Lily said, "Thank you!" and her mom said, "That was very kind of you." They cleaned up the popcorn and went home happy.

When Lucy saw the green vest, she said to Jake "That vest looks really nice, Jake! Can I try it on?"

Jake said "Sure!" and he handed Lucy the vest. Lucy put it on and smiled. She said "It looks so pretty. What do you think, Jake?"

Jake suggested that Lucy keep the vest. He said "It looks nice on you. Why don't you keep it?".

Lucy was so happy. She thanked Jake and hugged him. Jake felt good that he had helped make his best friend so happy. From then on, Lucy always wore her green vest.

Once upon a time, there were two friends - a bear and a fox. The fox was always filled with energy and very active. The bear, on the other hand, was not as playful, but was very kind and compassionate.

One day, the fox had an idea - let's play some music! But the bear was not sure about this. He thought it was a silly idea, and he quit. The fox was disappointed, but he respected the bear's decision and suggested to take a break instead.

The bear liked the idea and they went for a walk together. As they walked, the fox sang a tune. The bear was touched by this and smiled. He suddenly realized what his friend was trying to do - he was trying to bring joy to the bear - by giving him music! This made the bear even more compassionate towards the fox.

From that day on, the two friends were inseparable. Whenever the fox sang a tune, the bear remembered why he was so lucky to have such a caring friend.

As soon as they got there, the little boy jumped out of the car and ran to the porch. The mom shouted, "careful!" But the little boy did not listen.

He ran around and around the porch. It was so big and colorful. Mom called out, "It's time for lunch!" But the little boy ignored her.

Suddenly, the little boy stopped and saw a big colorful butterfly. "Look, mommy!" he said. Mom smiled and said, "I think it's time for lunch now."

But the boy was too busy looking at the butterfly and ignored her again. Mom knew she couldn't ignore it, so she said, "Okay, let's go find something to eat now".

The little boy smiled, took his mom's hand and they went off to find lunch.

One day, Tom and Mia went to the park with their mom. They saw a big round thing that they could sit on and spin. They ran to it and climbed on. Tom pushed hard and they started to spin fast.

"Wow, this is fun!" Mia said.

"Yes, it is!" Tom said.

But then, a rude boy came and jumped on the round thing too. He pushed harder and made them spin faster and faster. Tom and Mia felt dizzy and scared.

"Stop, stop, stop!" they cried.

But the rude boy did not stop. He laughed and said, "No, no, no! I like to spin!"

Tom and Mia wanted to get off, but they could not. They held on tight and closed their eyes. They wished the rude boy would go away.

Then, their mom saw them and ran to them. She grabbed the rude boy and pulled him off the round thing. She scolded him and said, "That is not nice! You are being rude! You have to share and be careful!"

The rude boy looked down and said, "Sorry."

Tom and Mia stopped spinning and got off the round thing. They felt sick and fell to the ground. Their mom hugged them and said, "Are you okay, my loves?"

They nodded and said, "Yes, mom. Thank you."

They decided to play something else and never spin again. They hoped the rude boy would learn to be nice.

This made Jane very sad, so she would weep. But she was too scared to tell Joe how she felt.

One day Joe came over with a new toy: it was a spike. He showed it off to Jane and then took away all of her toys again. Jane stared down at the ground and her eyes filled with tears.

Joe saw this and started to feel a little bit guilty. He offered Jane the spike so they could both play together. Jane smiled and hugged it tight.

Joe saw that Jane was happy, and felt much better. From then on, Joe was a lot less selfish and they both had lots of fun playing together!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ben. He was very alert and always kept an eye out for danger. One day, he found some strange liquids near the stream. He was curious and opened the bottle, hoping to learn its secret.

The smell of the bottle was so strong that Ben got scared. He decided to call his friend Lily. When she arrived, she saw the bottle on the ground and knew it was poison.

She grabbed the bottle and said to Ben, "Do not lie to me, Ben! What did you put in your mouth?

Ben said, "Nothing! I was just smelling the bottle. I promise!

But Lily did not believe him. She said, "You should always be alert, Ben. This bottle has poison in it, and if you had taken a sip of this, you would have been very sick!

Ben learnt an important lesson and was careful from that day on. He always stayed alert and reminded himself that some things can be dangerous even if they look nice.

Mouse was very sad. He tried to cover his food, but in the end, he lost everything. He put the covers on too late, so all of his picnic things were ruined.

Mouse was so upset. He ran back home and he cried and cried. He was so sad that he had lost his picnic. All the fun he had planned was gone. He just wanted to be happy again, but he knew it was too late. He had lost his picnic and it was all gone.

Once there were two brothers, John and Jack. John was very careful, while Jack was mischievous. They were playing outside one day, and Jack noticed a rope on the ground.

Jack was inquisitive, so he asked John, "What's that rope for?" John replied, "It's open and it's there for us to don't disturb it so that nothing bad happens".

But Jack didn't listen and he grabbed the rope. Suddenly, there was a loud noise! Jack was very scared and he ran away quickly.

John followed him and they both ended up back at home. John said to Jack, "We have to be careful and be aware of our surroundings. Don't go disturbing things that don't belong to us".

The moral of this story is to think before you do something that might be dangerous, and never disturb things that are not yours.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to learn new things. One day, he saw a big storm outside his window. There was loud thunder and bright lightning. Tim felt a little scared.

Tim's mom saw him and said, "Don't be scared, Tim. The lightning is just light in the sky. It can be loud, but it won't hurt you." Tim listened to his mom and felt better. He wanted to learn more about lightning.

So, Tim and his mom went to the library. They found a book about storms. They read the book together. Tim learned that lightning comes from the sky when it is very cloudy. He also learned that it can make a loud noise, but it is far away.

After they read the book, Tim felt tired. He said, "Mom, I'm tired. Can we go home now?" His mom smiled and said, "Of course, Tim. Let's go home and rest."

They went home, and Tim took a nap. He was happy that he learned about lightning. Now, when he saw lightning, he wasn't scared anymore.

Once upon a time there were two friends. One was a lion and one was an elephant. The lion's name was Lyle and the elephant's name was Everett. Every morning, the two friends would get together and play by the big tree in the forest.

One day, Lyle noticed something strange up in the tree. It was a cobweb with lots of sparkles. "What's that?" he asked Everett. Everett didn't know for sure, but he said his mother had told him that when two friends help each other, something good can happen. So Everett and Lyle decided to try and unite with the cobweb.

They worked together to try and get the cobweb down. Everett used his trunk to hold onto it while Lyle used his sharp claws to cut the strings that came out of the web. With their help, the web started to unravel. Everett was excited to see what would come out of it!

Suddenly, a sparkly bear appeared from the web. It flew around the two friends and then landed in front of them. Everett and Lyle were amazed! They thought the sparkly bear was so good and it made them happy. The bear thanked Everett and Lyle for uniting it with the cobweb. From that day forward, the three friends would come together to play by the big tree in the forest.

Ben and Lily are twins. They like to play with their toys. They have many toys, but their favorite is a big red car. The car can make noises and move by itself. Ben and Lily take turns to drive the car.

One day, the car stops working. Ben and Lily are sad. They try to push the car, but it does not move. They look at the car and see a number on it. The number is zero.

"What does zero mean?" Ben asks.

"I don't know," Lily says. "Maybe it means the car has no power."

They look for their mom. They hope she can fix the car. They find her in the kitchen. She is making cookies.

"Mom, mom, our car is broken!" Ben and Lily say.

"Oh, no, what happened?" Mom says.

She follows them to their room. She sees the car and the zero.

"Zero means the car needs new batteries," Mom says. "Batteries are things that make the car go. I have some in the drawer. Let me get them for you."

Mom opens the drawer and takes out two batteries. She opens the car and puts the batteries inside. She closes the car and turns it on. The car makes noises and moves by itself.

"Yay, the car is working!" Ben and Lily say.

They hug their mom and thank her.

"You're welcome, my sweeties," Mom says. "You are very helpful for showing me the problem. Now you can play with your car again. But don't forget to share and be nice to each other."

Ben and Lily nod. They are happy. They drive the car together. They share and are nice to each other. They hope Mom will give them some cookies later.

She took a pepper out of her mom's kitchen and started sprinkling it onto her food. Suddenly, her mom came in to the kitchen. Mom saw what Amelia was doing and tried to prevent her from eating the pepper.

But Amelia was too smart. She knew that the pepper wasn't bad, so she sprinkled more of it onto her meal anyway.

Mom just smiled and watched Amelia eat the pepper. After Amelia was done, she thanked her mom for letting her try something new. Mom was proud of Amelia for being so intelligent and brave.

Once upon a time, there was a bird called Tilly. Tilly was a reliable bird and a very special one too. She could soar high up in the sky, higher than any other bird.

Tilly loved to fly and zip around the clouds. She didn't like to stay in one place too long and was always on the move.

One day, she decided to soar even higher and explore the world. Up and up, she flew, until she could see everything.

Tilly felt so free and happy, as she zoomed around, enjoying her adventure. But, eventually time passed and it was getting dark. So she started flying back down and back home to where she belonged.

By the time Tilly arrived, it was nearly nighttime. She was glad to be home and to be with her family again.

The next morning, Tilly was delighted when she saw that her reliable wings were ready to take her on more adventures. She knew she could always soar high and zip around the clouds whenever she wanted.

Lily and Tom were playing in the sand. They had a bucket and a spade each. They wanted to make a big castle with towers and a moat.

"Look, my tower is taller than yours!" Lily said, putting more sand on top of her spade.

"No, it's not!" Tom said, doing the same with his spade. "Mine is taller and stronger!"

They kept adding more and more sand, until their spades were too heavy to lift. They tried to pull them out, but they were stuck.

"Help!" Lily cried. "My spade is trapped!"

"Me too!" Tom said. "I can't get my spade out!"

They pulled and pulled, but nothing happened. They started to cry.

A man was walking by with his dog. He saw the children crying and came over.

"What's wrong, little ones?" he asked.

"Our spades are stuck in the sand!" Lily and Tom said.

The man smiled and bent down. He took hold of their spades and pulled them out with ease.

"Here you go," he said, handing them their spades. "You were trying to make a castle, right?"

"Yes, but we made it too big and our spades got stuck," Lily said.

"Well, sometimes less is more," the man said. "Why don't you try to make a smaller castle, but with more details? Like windows and doors and flags?"

"That sounds fun!" Tom said.

"Thank you, mister!" Lily said.

"You're welcome," the man said. "Have fun, but be careful not to dig too deep. The sand can be bitter if you taste it."

He waved and walked away with his dog.

Lily and Tom looked at each other and smiled. They decided to make a smaller castle, but with more details. They used their spades to carve windows and doors and flags. They were happy and proud of their castle. They did not stop until it was done.

Joe had the most wonderful adventure and saw so many new things. After a few hours he realised he was far from home, but he was not scared since he had his fierce belt with him.

Joe eventually made his way back home and went to bed exhausted but happy. He smiled as he snuggled his belt close and fell asleep knowing he was brave and he could go on any adventure he desired.

"Can we go outside and see the plants and flowers?" Tom asked. He liked the rain. He wanted to splash and jump.

"Not now, Tom. It's too wet and cold. Maybe later, when the rain stops." Mommy said. She smiled at Tom. "Why don't we stay in the kitchen and play some more? Maybe we can make some real food and eat it together."

"Okay, mommy. That sounds good." Tom said. He went back to the kitchen. He found a fake carrot and a fake knife. He pretended to cut the carrot.

Lily and Tom played in the kitchen with mommy. They forgot about the rain. They had fun.

Tom and Sam were brothers who liked to play in the park. They had a ball, a kite and a whistle. They took turns to kick the ball, fly the kite and blow the whistle. They had a lot of fun.

One day, they saw a big dog in the park. The dog was brown and furry, and had a long tail. The dog ran towards them, wagging its tail. Tom and Sam were scared. They thought the dog wanted to bite them.

"Run, Sam, run!" Tom shouted. He grabbed the ball and the kite and ran away. He left the whistle on the ground.

Sam was too afraid to run. He picked up the whistle and blew it as hard as he could. He hoped someone would hear him and help him.

The dog heard the whistle and stopped. It tilted its head and looked at Sam. It did not want to bite him. It wanted to play. It barked and wagged its tail.

Sam saw that the dog was friendly. He stopped blowing the whistle and smiled. He threw the whistle and the dog chased it. They played together for a while.

Tom saw them from a distant place. He felt bad. He had left his brother alone. He had been selfish and cowardly. He ran back to Sam and the dog. He said sorry to Sam and gave him the ball and the kite. He also petted the dog and said hello.

Sam forgave Tom and hugged him. He shared the ball and the kite with him. The dog licked them both and barked happily. They all played together in the park. They had a lot of fun.

The moral of the story is: Do not be afraid of things you do not know. Do not leave your friends behind. Be kind and share.

Once upon a time, there was a little triangle. It was very fragile and didn't have any friends.

The triangle went to the park one day and saw two other shapes, a circle and a square. The triangle wanted to join them, but the two shapes didn't pay attention. The triangle called out "Hello!

The circle and square finally saw the triangle and the triangle asked them to be friends. The circle and the square grinned and said "Sure!

The three shapes started playing tag. They ran around the park, having a wonderful time. The triangle urged the other two to keep playing and they ran around until they were all out of breath.

Then they went back home with happy hearts. From then on, they were the best of friends and spent lots of time playing together.

First, his rabbit friend looked around the forest, but there was no room. Then his dragon friend searched the sky, but it was too cramped.

Finally, his bear friend had an idea. He told the prince to come back to the forest and find a hollow tree. When the prince arrived, he peeked inside the tree and saw that it was just the right size.

So the prince climbed inside the tree and settled down. From then on, it was a snug home. The prince was so pleased with his cozy new home, and he thanked his friends for their help.

Tom is a boy who likes to play. He has many toys in his room. He likes to make noise with his toys. He does not like to share his toys with his sister, Ann.

One day, Tom is playing with his toys in his room. He hears his mom call him. She says it is time to eat. Tom does not want to eat. He wants to play more. He says no to his mom. He makes more noise with his toys.

Ann is in the living room. She hears Tom make noise. She wants to play with him. She goes to his room. She sees his toys. She likes his toys. She tries to take one of his toys. Tom sees her. He gets angry. He says no to her. He pushes her away.

Ann falls down. She hits her head on the floor. She cries. Tom does not care. He keeps playing with his toys. He does not help her. He does not say sorry.

Mom hears Ann cry. She runs to the room. She sees Ann on the floor. She sees Tom with his toys. She sees the blood on Ann's head. She is scared. She is mad. She picks up Ann. She takes her to the car. She says they have to go to the doctor. She says Tom is a terrible boy. She says he has to stay in his room. She says he has no toys. She says he has no dinner. She says he has no mom.

Tom is alone in his room. He has no toys. He has no dinner. He has no mom. He is sad. He is sorry. He wants to say sorry to Ann. He wants to say sorry to mom. He wants to hug them. He wants to play with them. He wants to eat with them. He wants to be a good boy.

He gets up from his bed. He goes to the door. He tries to open it. It is locked. He knocks on it. He calls for mom. He calls for Ann. He hears no answer. He cries. He goes to the window. He sees the car. He sees mom and Ann. They are leaving. They do not see him. They do not hear him. He cries more.

He sees the stair. He thinks maybe he can go down the stair. Maybe he can catch the car. Maybe he can say sorry. Maybe he can be a good boy. He goes to the stair. He tries to go down. He slips. He falls. He hits his head on the stair. He bleeds. He hurts. He cries. He stops. He is still. He is gone.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. Her name was Jane and she was very excited. She loved playing outside and exploring in the yard.

One day, Jane was outside playing in the grass. Suddenly, she saw a big insect. She had never seen an insect like it before! She was so excited that she let out a big scream.

Jane's mom heard her daughter's loud scream and came to see what was wrong.

"What happened, sweetheart?" her mom asked.

Jane pointed towards the insect and said "Mommy, look! A bug! It's huge!"

Her mom smiled and said: "Yes, that's a grasshopper. He's much bigger than the other bugs you've seen before!"

Jane smiled and said "He's so cool! Can I catch him?"

Her mom nodded and Jane ran to catch the grasshopper. She was so excited that she was jumping up and down with joy!

The end.

Once upon a time, there were two young friends: Mia and Ben. Mia and Ben were playing at the ancient festival. It was a beautiful day and they were having a lot of fun.

Suddenly, Ben started to ignore Mia. He didn't want to play with her anymore. Mia felt sad. She didn't understand why Ben was acting so mean.

Mia started to walk away. Then, a flock of birds flew above her head. Mia followed the birds and they led her to the top of an ancient hill. There she saw a magnificent view of the festival.

Mia smiled. Suddenly, Ben appeared at the top of the hill. He asked for her forgiveness, and Mia said yes. The two young friends hugged each other. They enjoyed the scenic view, together.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved to pick different things from the garden. She spent her day eagerly picking flowers and leaves. One day she stumbled upon something different from the usual things she picked. This was a pattern!

The little girl was so excited. She picked it carefully and brought it back home. She was mesmerized by its regular structure and marvelled at its beauty. She kept it safe in her jar of treasures and continued to admire it.

The little girl soon learnt that having even the simplest of joys can bring pleasure if they were picked with care. The moral of the story is to appreciate and treasure things that are picked regularly regardless of how small they may seem.

Lily and Ben were best friends. They liked to play in the park every day. They liked to swing on the swings, slide on the slides, and run on the grass. But they did not like the maze. The maze was big and dark and scary. They were fearful of the maze.

One day, Lily and Ben were swinging on the swings. They saw a new boy in the park. He was swinging on the swing next to them. He had a big smile and a red hat. He said hello to them.

"Hi, I'm Tom. Do you want to play with me?" he asked.

"Sure, we can play with you. What do you want to play?" Lily said.

"Let's play in the maze. It's fun and exciting. Come on, follow me!" Tom said.

He jumped off the swing and ran to the maze. Lily and Ben looked at each other. They did not want to play in the maze. They were fearful of the maze.

"Tom, wait! We don't like the maze. It's big and dark and scary. We want to play something else." Ben said.

But Tom did not hear them. He was already inside the maze. He was laughing and shouting.

"Come on, don't be scared. The maze is not scary. It's just a game. You can find your way out. It's easy. Trust me!" he said.

Lily and Ben did not trust him. They were fearful of the maze. But they did not want to lose their new friend. They wanted to play with him. They decided to be brave and try the maze.

They held hands and walked slowly to the maze. They entered the maze. They saw many paths and walls and turns. They did not know which way to go. They tried to follow Tom's voice, but it was hard to hear. They felt lost and alone. They were fearful of the maze.

They wished they were back on the swings. They wished they had never met Tom. They wished they could get out of the maze. They started to cry.

"Help! Help! We want to get out! We don't like the maze!" they cried.

Then they heard a familiar voice. It was Tom. He had found them. He had a big smile and a red hat. He was holding a map.

"Don't cry, don't cry. I'm here to help you. I have a map. I know the way out. Follow me!" he said.

He took their hands and led them through the maze. He showed them the right paths and turns. He made jokes and songs. He made them laugh and smile. He was not fearful of the maze. He was fun and kind.

Soon they reached the exit. They saw the sun and the sky and the park. They felt happy and relieved. They were out of the maze.

"See, that was not so bad. That was fun and exciting. You did it! You were brave and smart. You should be proud of yourselves!" Tom said.

He hugged them and gave them the map.

"Here, you can keep the map. Next time, you can play in the maze again. Or you can play something else. I don't mind. I just want to be your friend." he said.

Lily and Ben smiled and thanked him. They looked at the map. They looked at the maze. They looked at each other. They were not fearful of the maze anymore. They were curious and adventurous. They wanted to play with Tom again.

They decided to give the maze another try. They followed the map and explored the maze. They had fun and exciting. They were friends.

Once there was a cat named Puss who was very troubled. His owner wanted to help but didn't know what to do. So he decided to take Puss to see the veterinarian.

The veterinarian said to the owner, "Puss is not feeling well. I think you should borrow some medicine from me so he can start feeling better."

The owner was overjoyed and said, "Thank you, veterinarian! I will borrow some of your medicine right away." He then took the medicine back home and gave it to Puss.

In no time at all, Puss started to feel better. He thanked the owner and the veterinarian for helping him. From then on, Puss was his cheerful, happy self again.

The boy said yes and Lily was so happy. They played together for a while and then Lily's phone rang. It was her mom calling to ask if she was having fun at the park. Lily told her mom that she was playing with a new friend and having a great time.

After the call, the boy asked Lily if he could see her red phone. Lily happily showed him and they both admired the shiny red color. They continued to play together until it was time for Lily to go home. She said goodbye to her new friend and promised to come back to the park to play again.

Then one day, she saw something glittering far off in the distance. It was an ornament! She wanted it very much, but couldn't go very far to get it. She begged her parents to go get it for her, but her parents said no.

At this time, the princess felt sad. One evening, she heard a stranger talking outside the castle gates. The stranger said, "If anyone can get me this ornament from far away, I will give them anything they want!"

The next morning, the princess decided she wanted to go get the ornament for the stranger. Her parents said no, but the princess didn't listen. She packed a small bag and left the castle.

The princess went on a long and hard journey to get the ornament. She climbed mountains and ran across deserts, but never gave up. Finally, after three days and nights of traveling, she arrived at her destination.

When the princess held out the ornament to the stranger, they bowed down and said "thank you". In return, the stranger gave the princess a golden key. As the princess looked down at the key, she realized what the stranger had planned all along.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy fox who wanted to see what everyone else was doing. One day, the fox happened to notice a group of animals playing a game. The fox was curious and he wanted to join in, but the other animals said no.

The fox was all alone and he was feeling sad, when the wise owl flew by and said, "You can't play their game, but you can play your own game."

The fox was confused, he didn't know how to make a game. The owl said, "You need to find something that only you know and make a game out of it."

The fox was so excited! He immediately began searching for something that only he knew and finally, he found a perfect idea.
The fox created a game and the other animals loved it.

The moral of the story is that everyone has something inside of them that nobody else knows. We should never be discouraged or give up just because we are not like everyone else. We can use our special knowledge to make something amazing and share it with the world!

Tom and Lily were twins who liked to play with different things. Tom loved to look at tiny things with his microscope. He could see bugs, hairs, and even dust. Lily liked to draw and paint with her crayons and brushes. She could make colorful pictures of flowers, animals, and stars.

One day, Tom found a new thing to look at with his microscope. It was a shiny rock that he picked up from the park. He put it on the slide and turned on the light. He saw many sparkles and shapes inside the rock. He was very happy and excited.

"Look, Lily, look!" he shouted. "I found a magic rock! It has so many pretty things inside!"

Lily came over and looked at the rock. She saw the sparkles and shapes, too. But she was not happy and excited. She was jealous. She wanted to have a magic rock, too. She wanted to look at it with her own microscope. But she did not have one. She only had crayons and brushes.

"Tom, can I have your microscope?" she asked. "I want to see the magic rock better."

"No, Lily, you can't," Tom said. "This is my microscope. I got it for my birthday. You can use your crayons and brushes. They are fun, too."

Lily felt angry and sad. She did not want to use her crayons and brushes. She wanted to use Tom's microscope. She grabbed the microscope and tried to pull it away from Tom. Tom held on to the microscope and tried to push Lily away. They both started to yell and cry.

"Stop, stop, stop!" their mom said. She heard the noise and came into the room. She saw Tom and Lily fighting over the microscope. She was not happy. She took the microscope and the rock from them. She put them on the shelf.

"Tom, Lily, you are both being very naughty," she said. "You need to share and be nice to each other. You have many things to play with. You don't need to fight over one thing. Now, you both need to rest and calm down. Go to your beds and take a nap."

Tom and Lily did not want to go to their beds. They wanted to play with the microscope and the rock. But they knew they had to listen to their mom. They went to their beds and lay down. They were still feeling jealous and angry. But they were also feeling tired and sorry. They closed their eyes and fell asleep. They dreamed of sparkles and shapes.

Once upon a time there were two friends named Pepper and Tom. Tom was feeling very guilty and so Pepper wanted to help him feel better.

They decided to go outside and play. Tom started to run around and feel silly.

But then something strange happened, another little boy came up and tried to take away Tom's toy. Tom was not happy and he started to fight with the other boy.

Pepper saw this and ran to help. He bravely stepped in between Tom and the other boy. He reminded them both of the rules and suggested they share the toy.

Tom and the other boy agreed to share. Tom and Pepper were both very proud that they were able to find a solution without fighting. Tom no longer felt guilty and the two friends were able to play peacefully. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a small fish called Tiny. Tiny had a big dream; she wanted to dive deep into the big ocean. So one day, Tiny took a deep breath and swam down to the bottom of the sea.

When she reached the bottom, Tiny saw something sparkly. She opened her eyes wide and saw a beautiful pearl. She quickly opened her small lips and grabbed the pearl. Then she started to swim back to the top.

Suddenly, Tiny was confronted by a big fish. He said, "Please give me back my pearl!
Tiny replied, "But I found it first!
The big fish said, "You can keep it, but I must have something in exchange.

Tiny thought for a moment. Then she said, "Let's dive together!
The big fish smiled. "That sounds like so much fun! You have a deal!

So Tiny and the big fish dove deep into the ocean. As they dove, they found all kinds of beautiful things. In the end, they had a wonderful adventure and kept the pearl as a reminder of it. And they remained friends forever after.

Once upon a time, there was a big, strong bear. He went for a walk in nature. He felt so happy and brilliant! He was looking forward to a nice, peaceful day in nature.

Suddenly, something strange appeared. It was a tall, dark creature that he had never seen before. He was scared, but he decided to talk to it. He said "Hi there, who are you?"

The creature didn't answer. Instead, it started to growl. The bear was so scared. He quickly ran away. The creature followed him!

The bear was fast, but the creature was faster. It caught up with him and ate him in one bite! The bear had a bad ending and never made it back home.

Lila likes to play with her hoop. She can roll it, spin it, and jump through it. She likes to make it go fast and slow. She likes to make it go near and far.

One day, she takes her hoop to the park. She sees many other children playing there. Some are on the swings, some are on the slide, some are on the seesaw. Lila wants to play with them, but she is shy.

She finds a spot under a big tree and sits with her hoop. She rolls it on the grass and watches it come back to her. She spins it on her arm and feels it tickle her. She jumps through it and laughs.

Then, she sees a boy with a ball. He is lively and friendly. He runs and kicks the ball, and catches it with his hands. He sees Lila and smiles. He comes over to her and says, "Hi, I'm Ben. Do you want to play with me?"

Lila nods and smiles back. She says, "Hi, I'm Lila. This is my hoop. Do you want to try it?"

Ben says, "Sure, I like hoops. They are fun and round. Can I show you my ball?"

Lila says, "Yes, I like balls. They are bouncy and colorful. Can we share them?"

Ben says, "Yes, we can share them. We can be friends."

Once there were two friends named Martha and Jack. Martha and Jack decided to go outside and play. But the sky was looking gloomy, so they decided to go down the river on a raft.

Martha and Jack hopped on the raft, and began paddling down the river. The sun was beginning to peek through the clouds, so they both started to laugh. The water sparkled in the sunlight, and all around them birds were singing.

Martha and Jack had a lot of fun on the raft. They floated down the river, laughing and chatting. Eventually, the river got narrower, and the sun started to go down, so Martha and Jack decided it was time to go home.

Martha and Jack stepped off the raft and said goodbye to a wonderful day. They hugged each other and laughed. As Jack and Martha walked home, they waved to the raft, still floating on the river, and smiled.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play with their fish. They have a big tank with many fish. Some fish are red, some are blue, some are yellow.

One day, Lily and Ben see a new fish in the tank. It is big and gray. It has sharp teeth. It looks mean. It swims fast and chases the other fish.

"Look, Ben, a new fish!" Lily says.

"I don't like it, Lily. It is scary. It hurts the other fish." Ben says.

They watch the new fish bite the red fish and the blue fish and the yellow fish. The fish are scared and hide behind the plants and the rocks.

Lily and Ben are sad and angry. They want to help their fish. They have an idea.

They find an old box in the garage. They cut a hole in the side. They fill it with water. They use a net to catch the new fish. They put the new fish in the box.

They separate the new fish from the other fish. The new fish is alone in the box. It cannot bite the other fish anymore.

The other fish are happy and safe in the tank. They swim and play and eat.

Lily and Ben are happy too. They hug each other.

"We did it, Lily. We saved our fish." Ben says.

"Yes, Ben. We are good twins." Lily says.

One day, Bob saw a little rabbit. He waved at her, and she came over.

Bob said, "Hello, rabbit. I'm looking for something special, but I don't know what it is."

The rabbit smiled. She said, "You should look for something that makes you happy and stops you feeling worried. Try looking at the sky!"

Bob thanked the rabbit and said goodbye. He wandered around, looking up at the sky. It was so big and blue, and it made him feel so peaceful. Suddenly, he felt happy and his worries melted away.

And from that day on, Bob never felt worried again.

Joe was eager to try something new. He ran up to his Mom and asked if they could go outside. His Mom put her hands on her hips and said, "You can go outside, but you have to wipe your feet first!" Joe eagerly ran to the door and wiped his feet on the mat.

When Joe stepped outside, he saw the big round earth before him. He beamed with excitement and shouted out, "Look, Mom! The earth!" His Mom smiled and nodded as she watched him run off happily into the garden.

Joe hopped over to the garden bed and started to dig. He scooped up the dirt and tossed handfuls in the air. He enjoyed making a mess of the garden for a few minutes. Right when he was about to make another pile, the ground beneath Joe rumbled. He was shocked and quickly ran back to his Mom.

The earth shook and crumbled beneath them. Joe's Mom tried to grab hold of him, but it was too late. They were both thrown from their home as the earth quaked.

When the rumble died down, Joe held onto his Mom tightly. He looked up to see a world that was now destroyed. All of his eagerness and joy had been wiped away in an instant.

Lila was a princess who liked to play with her dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was a prince doll. She liked to pretend that he was her friend and they went on adventures together.

One day, she was playing with her prince doll in her room, when she heard a loud noise. She paused and listened. It was her brother, Tom, who was angry. He wanted to play with her prince doll, too. He knocked on her door and shouted, "Lila, give me your prince doll! I want to play with him!"

Lila was scared. She did not want to share her prince doll with Tom. She knew that he was rough and mean. She said, "No, Tom, this is my prince doll. You have your own toys. Go away!"

Tom did not listen. He kicked the door and broke it. He ran into her room and grabbed her prince doll. He said, "Ha, ha, now he is mine! I will make him fight with my dragon toy!"

Lila cried. She tried to get her prince doll back, but Tom was stronger. He pushed her away and ran out of the room. He laughed and said, "Bye, bye, princess! Your prince is gone!"

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who wanted to sail. He built a boat out of wood and found some sails. It was sunny, so he decided to go sailing.
