The little girl was so cheerful that she smiled brightly and said "Yes!"

So they made a long sand slide and went down it, laughing and giggling. They made sand angles and imagined they were birds flying in the air. After playing in the sand all day, they were so happy.

The little girl hugged her little friend and said, "We had such a great time today!"

The little boy smiled and replied, "Yes, it was the best day ever!"

And they laughed together, a cheerful little laugh, one that only the best of friends could make.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to eat candy every day. His mom always told him that he needed to eat healthy food, but he didn't want to listen. One day, Timmy found a magic bean in a sack. He ate the bean and suddenly he started to shrink!

Timmy was so small that he could fit inside the sack. He climbed inside and found himself in a magical land. There he met a friendly giant who said, "Hello little one, what brings you here?" Timmy explained what had happened and the giant said, "You need to eat healthy food to grow big and strong again."

Timmy realized that the giant was right. He asked the giant if he could have some of his healthy food. The giant gave Timmy a big carrot and some fresh apples. Timmy ate the healthy food and soon he grew back to his normal size. Timmy thanked the giant and went back home to tell his mom all about his adventure. From that day on, Timmy ate healthy food every day and never wanted to shrink again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim loved basketball. He had a great basketball that he loved to play with. One sunny day, Tim went to the park to play with his basketball. He ran all around the park, dribbling his basketball. Tim was so happy because he loved to run and play with his basketball.

Suddenly, a group of children came to the park. They saw Tim playing with his great basketball and asked if they could play too. Tim was happy to share and they all played together. They ran and passed the basketball to each other, having so much fun. Tim was happy to make new friends and play with his great basketball.

When it was time to go home, Tim said goodbye to his new friends and ran all the way back to his house with his basketball. He was tired but happy because he had a great day playing with his great basketball and new friends at the park.

Once upon a time there was a great king called Colin. He was very humble and kind. One day, Colin was worried because his castle was not safe. Someone could get in very easily!

So Colin asked his wise advisor for advice. The advisor said:
"We must lock all the doors and windows. That way, nobody can get in!"

Colin was delighted. He locked all the doors and windows so nobody could break in. He felt so safe, he was no longer worried.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play in the garden with his spade. One day, Timmy's mom asked him to organize his toys in his room. Timmy didn't want to do it because he thought it was hard, but his mom said it was easy.

Timmy went to his room and saw that his toys were all over the place. He started to organize them one by one. Suddenly, he found his spade. He was happy to see it and said, "Look, Mommy! I found my spade! Now I can play in the garden again."

His mom smiled and said, "Good job, Timmy! You organized your toys so well. Now you can play with your spade in the garden." Timmy was happy to hear that and ran outside to play with his spade.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Benny. Benny loved to eat radishes, but he couldn't find any in his garden. So, he went to his friend, a little bird named Billy, and asked him if he knew where he could find some radishes.

Billy said, "I saw some radishes in Farmer Joe's garden. But be careful, he doesn't like animals taking his vegetables."

Benny thanked Billy and went to Farmer Joe's garden. He saw some radishes, but they were too high for him to reach. He leaned against a big rock and tried to jump, but he still couldn't reach them.

Just then, Farmer Joe came out and saw Benny. "What are you doing here?" he asked.

Benny said, "I want some radishes, but they're too high. Can you help me?"

Farmer Joe smiled and said, "Sure, I have a ladder available. Let me get it for you." He got the ladder and helped Benny pick some radishes.

Benny was so happy and thanked Farmer Joe. He went back to his garden and shared the radishes with Billy. They both had a yummy snack and were happy they helped each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was an obedient child who always listened to his mom and dad. One day, his mom asked him to help her in the kitchen. She wanted to make rice for dinner.

"Tim, can you please repeat after me? First, wash the rice. Then, put it in the pot with water. Lastly, turn on the stove," said his mom. Tim listened carefully and repeated, "Wash rice, put in pot with water, turn on stove."

Tim did as his mom told him. He washed the rice, put it in the pot with water, and turned on the stove. The rice began to cook. The smell of the rice filled the kitchen, making Tim and his mom very hungry.

Soon, the rice was ready. Tim and his mom put the rice on their plates and sat down to eat. They both enjoyed the delicious rice that Tim had helped to make. Tim felt proud that he was able to help his mom in the kitchen and be an obedient child.

Once upon a time, there was a little green hanger. He was always very careful when he hung clothes on the line.

One day, he decided to swing as high as he could and let go. He felt like he could trust the wind. But, as he swung, the wind became stronger and stronger. Soon, the hanger lost his grip on the clothesline and flew away.

The hanger was so scared. He tried to find something to hold on to, but he couldn't see anything. He yelled out, "Help me! I'm falling!"

But, no one came to save the small green hanger. He kept falling until he landed on the ground. He was never seen again.

The little green hanger had learned the hard way that he couldn't always trust the wind. Sometimes, trusting can lead to bad things.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy who loved playing with his best friend, a rubber duck.
One day, when they were out in the park, Timmy noticed a butterfly resting on a tree. He was eager to get it.
He whispered, "Come to me, butterfly! Come to me!
But the butterfly flew away.
Timmy got a bit sad and his rubber duck said, "Don't worry, Timmy. I'll come to you!
The little boy became so eager and he whispered again, "Fly to me, rubber duck! Fly to me!
The rubber duck jumped into the air, and flew directly into Timmy's arms. They hugged each other and laughed.
The End.

Once upon a time, there was a nice little girl named Amy. Her mom and dad loved her very much and always wanted to support her.
One day, Amy's dad told her about the fine prize she could win in the competition she had entered. He said if she worked really hard, she would win the prize.

Amy was so excited. She put in lots of effort to prepare for the competition, and soon it was time for the big day. The judges were so impressed with Amy and announced that she was the winner!

Amy's dad hugged her tight and said, "I'm so proud of you! You worked so hard and it paid off. I'm glad I could support you."

Amy smiled and replied, "Thanks, dad! I'm so happy I won the fine prize!"

Anna was very happy. It was her birthday. She had a cake with four candles. She had balloons and a hat. She had many friends to play with.

But Anna wanted a present. She asked her mom, "Mom, where is my present?"

Mom smiled and said, "Wait a little, Anna. I have a surprise for you."

Anna waited and waited. She ate some cake and sang some songs. She played with her friends and her toys. But she still wanted a present.

Then mom came with a big box. It was wrapped in shiny paper. It had a bow on top. It was very thick.

"Here is your present, Anna. Happy birthday!" mom said.

Anna was very excited. She tore the paper and opened the box. Inside was a big teddy bear. It was soft and brown. It had a red bow around its neck. It had a tag that said, "I love you, Anna."

Anna hugged the teddy bear. She said, "Thank you, mom. I love you too. This is the best present ever!"

Johnny was walking in the park. He saw a big worm on the ground. Johnny wanted to pick it up, but his mom yelled at him.

"Johnny, don't touch that worm!" said his mom.

Johnny didn't listen. He picked up the worm, and put it in his pocket. His mom scolded him and told him to put the worm back on the ground.

"No," said Johnny. "I want to keep it."

His mom was starting to get angry. "Johnny," she said, "I recommend that you put that worm back on the ground. It's not safe to keep it."

Johnny thought about it for a moment. But then he said, "No, I want to keep the worm."

His mom was getting frustrated. "Johnny, please put it down. It's not safe to keep it," she said again.

But Johnny refused to listen. In the end, his mom had to take the worm out of his pocket, and put it back on the ground. Johnny watched as the big worm disappeared into the tall grass.

Once there was a little girl who was very hungry. She was looking for something to eat, so she decided to make a sandwich. She grabbed two slices of bread and put some meat and cheese in between.

The little girl felt very happy with her sandwich, but then she saw something very ugly. It was a big, slimy insect that was touching the sandwich. She screamed and jumped away!

The insect didn't mean any harm and just wanted to eat the sandwich. So, the little girl helped the bug safely away from the sandwich.

But she still didn't want to touch the sandwich, so she made another one without any touch from the bug. It tasted even better than the first one!

Once upon a time, there was a little red bird. She wanted to go to the stage, so she flew to find it.

She saw the stage and hopped on it. But she didn't know how to make it move.

Suddenly, she heard a voice behind her. "You need to lock the stage!" said the voice. The little red bird turned around and saw a blue bird.

The little red bird asked, "What do you mean?"
The blue bird said, "You have to press this button to lock the stage. Then, it will move."

The little red bird was so happy and said, "Thank you!" She pressed the button, and the stage started to move. The little red bird had so much fun dancing and singing on the stage.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly little mouse named Max. Max lived in a big maze with lots of twists and turns. One day, Max got lost in the maze and he didn't know which way to go. He started to yell for help, but no one could hear him.

As Max kept wandering through the maze, he met a kind cat named Mittens. Mittens saw that Max was lost and offered to help him find his way out of the maze. Max was so happy that he started to jump up and down with joy.

Mittens led Max through the maze, and they finally found the exit. Max was so grateful to Mittens for helping him that he gave her a big hug. From that day on, Max and Mittens became the best of friends and had many more jolly adventures together.

Lily and Max were twins who liked to play in the garden. One day, they saw a big white thing in the ground. It looked like a flower, but it was not pretty or colorful.

"What is that?" Lily asked.

"It is a cauliflower," Max said. He had learned the word from their mom.

"Can we pick it?" Lily asked.

"No, we have to wait until it is ready," Max said. He wanted to show the cauliflower to their mom and dad.

But Lily was curious and impatient. She pulled the cauliflower out of the ground. It was heavy and hard.

"Look, I have it!" Lily said. She ran to the house with the cauliflower.

Max was angry and sad. He chased after Lily. He wanted to put the cauliflower back in the ground.

"Stop, Lily, stop!" Max shouted. "You are not supposed to do that!"

But Lily was too fast. She reached the house before Max. She showed the cauliflower to their mom and dad.

"Look what I found in the garden!" Lily said.

Mom and dad looked at the cauliflower. They looked at Lily. They looked at Max.

They were not happy.

"Lily, you should not have picked the cauliflower," mom said. "It was not ready yet. You have to be patient and respectful of nature."

"Lily, you should not have run away from me," Max said. "I wanted to show the cauliflower to mom and dad too. You have to be fair and share with your brother."

Lily felt sorry. She realized she had made a mistake. She hugged Max and gave him the cauliflower.

"I am sorry, Max," Lily said. "You can show the cauliflower to mom and dad. I will wait for the next one to grow."

Max smiled and hugged Lily back. He showed the cauliflower to mom and dad. They praised him for being smart and kind.

Lily and Max learned a lesson. They learned to be patient, respectful, fair and share. They played in the garden again, but this time they did not pick anything until it was ready. They enjoyed watching the cauliflower and other plants grow. They were happy.

Mummy was reading when Lily came in. "It's snack time," Lily said.

Mummy smiled. "Yes, it is. What do you want to eat?"

"Cookies!" Lily said. She remembered that she always loved eating cookies.

Mummy laughed. "That's a very naughty snack. How about some fruit instead?"

"Okay," Lily said reluctantly. She remembered that she had to be good if she wanted more cookies.

Mummy gave her a bowl of apples, oranges, and grapes. Lily ate them all and felt much better. She remembered that eating healthier snacks was important.

Mummy gave her a big hug as Lily finished her snack. "Good job, Lily," Mummy said. She was so proud that Lily was so naughty and remembered to eat healthy snacks.

Once upon a time there was a smart bunny. He loved to pick flowers in the garden. One morning, he found a very special flower. He used his paws to squeeze it gently. When he opened his hand, a burst of colour appeared. Inside the flower was a tiny fairy.

The fairy was so happy to be free that she flew around the bunny's ears and hugged him. Together, the smart bunny and the fairy danced in the garden for a long time. They played hide and seek and chased each other through the flower petals.

At the end of the day, the bunny and the fairy said goodbye. They hugged each other tightly and the bunny gave the fairy a big squeeze. Then the fairy returned to her flower and the bunny smiled. He was happy to have a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a brave bunny who lived in a small house with his family. Every day, the bunny's mother would give him a request to help around the house. One day, the mother asked the bunny to lift a heavy box in the corner of the room. The bunny was surprised. He was only three and it seemed impossible!

He asked his mother: "How can I lift that box? It looks so heavy!"

His mother looked at him with a kind smile and said: "I know you are very small, but use your strength and you can do it! And just try to lift your weight, nothing more."

The bunny was determined to obey his mother's request. He had to try! He took a deep breath and bent down to lift the box. It felt surprisingly light! He was able to move it easily and put it in the right spot. His mother was proud, and the bunny was happy too. He had proved that he could do it!

Mia and Kai love to swim in the sea. They have a big reef near their home. The reef has many fish and corals of different colors. Mia and Kai like to look at them and learn their names.

One day, they see a new fish. It is long and thin and has stripes. It can bend its body in many ways. It is very flexible.

"Look, Kai, what is that fish?" Mia asks.

"I don't know, Mia. It is very pretty and funny. Let's follow it and see where it goes," Kai says.

They swim after the fish. The fish goes through small holes and cracks in the reef. It twists and turns and hides. Mia and Kai have to be careful not to touch the sharp corals. They have fun trying to catch the fish.

The fish leads them to a big cave. Inside the cave, there are many other fish like it. They are all flexible and striped. They swim around Mia and Kai and make bubbles.

"Wow, Mia, look at all these fish. They are a family. They have a home in the cave," Kai says.

"Yes, Kai, they are amazing. They are so friendly and playful. I think they like us," Mia says.

They smile and wave at the fish. The fish smile and wave back. Mia and Kai have a new friend in the reef. They are happy.

Billy went and found some of his treasure and put it under the carpet. He was very happy and felt proud of himself for being so honest.

Billy had so much fun playing hide and seek with his treasures under the carpet. He had lots of fun and became an expert at hiding things. Soon his mum found out about the treasures and was very proud of him for being so honest.

The End

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, her mommy took her to the mall to buy a new dress. At the mall, they saw a rude man who pushed past them without saying sorry.

Lily and her mommy went to a candy store and the nice lady who worked there gave them some candy to taste. Lily loved the sweet taste of the candy and wanted to buy more.

After they left the candy store, they went to a restaurant where Lily tasted some yummy pizza. She was so happy that she asked her mommy if they could come back to the mall every day to eat pizza and candy. Her mommy laughed and said they could come back sometimes, but not every day. And they both went home happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved wearing her favorite jacket, but she couldn't zip it up by herself. One day, Lily's mom was busy doing some work, so she asked her dad for help with the zip.

Dad came over and helped Lily zip up her jacket. He did it slowly, so Lily wouldn't get hurt. Lily was happy and warm in her jacket. She hugged her dad and thanked him for his help.

From that day on, Lily tried to zip up her jacket by herself. It was hard at first, but with practice, she got better and better. Now, Lily is a big girl who can zip up her jacket all by herself!

Lily liked to play in the garden. She liked to dig in the dirt and look for bugs. She found a big black beetle with long legs and shiny wings. She was very happy. She put the beetle in a jar and ran to show her mom.

"Look, mom, look what I found!" Lily said. "Can I keep it? Please, please, please?"

Mom looked at the jar and frowned. She did not like bugs. She thought they were icky and dirty. She did not want them in the house.

"No, Lily, you can't keep it," mom said. "It's not a pet. It belongs in the garden. You have to let it go."

Lily felt sad and angry. She loved her beetle. She wanted to keep it. She did not want to let it go.

"But mom, I promise I will take good care of it," Lily said. "I will give it water and leaves and a name. I will be nice to it. Please, mom, please."

Mom shook her head. She did not change her mind. She said Lily had to let the beetle go or she would be in trouble.

Lily did not listen. She hid the jar under her bed and hoped mom would not find it. She thought she was clever. She thought she could keep her beetle.

But she was wrong. The next day, when she came home from school, she saw the jar on the table. The lid was off. The beetle was gone. Mom was waiting for her with a stern look.

"Lily, what did you do?" mom asked. "You broke your promise. You lied to me. You did a bad thing."

Lily felt sorry and scared. She did not mean to do a bad thing. She just wanted to keep her beetle. She looked for it in the garden, but she could not find it. She lost her beetle and she made mom mad.

She started to cry. She said she was sorry. She said she would not do it again.

Mom hugged her and said she forgave her. She said she loved her. She said she understood. She said she would help her find another bug to play with in the garden. But she said she had to let it go when she was done.

Lily nodded. She learned her lesson. She said she would keep her promise. She said she would let the bug go. She said she loved mom too. She said she was happy.

Bob was playing in the park when he saw something shiny. It was a crocodile! He ran up to the little creature and thought it was so adorable.

Bob asked the crocodile, "What are you doing here?" The crocodile grinned and answered, "I'm here to shine!" It was so happy to see Bob and showed him how it liked to play in the sun.

Bob named the crocodile Sunny and they became the best of friends. Every morning, Sunny would go outside and soak up the sun's rays and shine. All the other animals in the park were amazed at how beautifully Sunny shone.

The other animals were so envious of Sunny and Bob's friendship. Bob was happy to show everyone how wonderful Sunny was and how adorable the little crocodile shone.

Mary and her dad were walking past a building. Mary said to her dad, "look at the red building, it's so bright!" Her dad smiled at her and said, "Let's go and paint it." Mary was so excited, so she ran over to get some paint.

When they started to paint, they could see a man watching them from far away. Suddenly, the man shouted, "Hey! You can't paint this building!" Mary was very scared and didn't want to stop painting.

Her dad quickly stood between Mary and the man. He said firmly, "It's ok sweetheart, we will finish what we started." The man was getting angry, so Mary's dad told him, "This building could use a nice red color, it will look beautiful!"

The man thought about it for a moment and then said, "I guess it will look nice. You can finish painting it." Mary was so glad that the man had changed his mind. She and her dad finished painting the red building and it looked beautiful!

Once there was a little boy named Jack. He was three years old and loved going on adventures.

Jack was sad, but then he noticed something on the left side of the door. It was a key! He picked up the key and tried it in the lock. It worked! The door opened and there was a garden inside.

Jack was so excited! He walked into the garden and saw many tasty fruits and vegetables. He picked some of the fruits and ate them. They were so tasty!

Jack smiled and enjoyed the rest of his adventure. He was so happy that he found the key and could explore the garden.

Sammy looked at the big building with wonder: it was so high, so tall and tight. Sammy had never seen something so big before. He kept staring up, amazed.

Then Sammy's father, Tom, said: "Let's go Sammy, help me push the door open"!

They both put all their strength onto the door and, finally, the door opened. Sammy was curious to see what was inside, so he entered with Tom.

When he looked inside the building he couldn't believe his eyes: all around Sammy there were big colorful blocks, some were so big they were almost as tall as Sammy himself! Sammy was so excited, he started having fun and playing with them. He pushed and piled them high, creating the tightest towers he had ever seen.

Tom was impressed by all the towers Sammy had created and said: "Well done Sammy, you should be an engineer when you grow up! With these words in his mind, Sammy continued to wonder and play with the blocks.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a sister named Rose who loved to make tasty treats. One day, Rose made some yummy cookies and left them on the table. Lily wanted to eat them all, but her mom said she could only have one. Lily tried to resist eating more, but she couldn't help herself. She ate all the cookies when her mom wasn't looking.

Later that day, Lily started to feel sick. Her tummy hurt and she felt very tired. Her mom took her to the hospital, but it was too late. Lily had eaten too many cookies and her body couldn't handle it. She passed away, leaving her sister Rose and her family very sad. From that day on, Rose never made tasty treats again because it reminded her of her sister.

Sam and Lily were best friends. They liked to play together every day. They had a lot of fun toys and games. One day, they decided to play on the swing. They pushed each other higher and higher. They laughed and shouted.

"Look at me, I'm flying!" Sam said.

"Me too, me too!" Lily said.

They swung faster and faster. They felt dizzy, but they did not stop. They wanted to see how high they could go. They did not see the big tree behind them.

CRASH!

Sam and Lily hit the tree with a loud noise. They fell off the swing and landed on the ground. They felt a lot of pain. They cried and screamed.

"Ow, ow, ow! My head hurts!" Sam said.

"Ow, ow, ow! My arm hurts!" Lily said.

They looked at each other. They saw blood and bruises. They were scared and sad. They wanted their moms and dads.

"Sam, I'm sorry, I'm sorry!" Lily said.

"Lily, I'm sorry, I'm sorry!" Sam said.

They hugged each other. They tried to comfort each other. But they did not feel better. They felt worse. They wished they had not played on the swing. They wished they had played something else. They wished they had listened to their moms and dads. They learned a hard lesson. They never played on the swing again.

Once upon a time, a big white bird lived in an ancient tree. The tree was very old and had many branches. The bird liked to sit on the branches and sing.

One day, a small yellow bird arrived at the tree. The yellow bird was tired and hungry. The white bird saw the yellow bird and wanted to help. The white bird shared its food with the yellow bird.

The yellow bird was happy and thanked the white bird. They became good friends and sang together every day. The ancient tree was filled with their sweet songs. And they lived happily ever after.

One day Sam was walking home from school. He felt so happy and he smiled as he looked at the ground. As he walked he saw a rabbit. He wanted to follow it, so he started to run. The rabbit hopped so quickly and Sam tried to keep up, but he couldn't. Suddenly, the rabbit disappeared into a bush. Sam stopped and he was worried. He looked around to see where the rabbit had gone. Then he heard a noise coming from the ground. He looked down and the rabbit was there. It had gone under the ground! Sam was so excited that he followed the rabbit. He started to dig and the rabbit hopped away. Sam followed it until the sun went down. He finally got home and he knew that he had had a great adventure that day!

Once upon a time there were two friends, Jack and Jill. They were playing in the garden when suddenly Jack said, "Let's go on a march!"
Jill was so excited and she said, "Yes, that would be fun!"

So the two friends set off together on their march. They started walking, and then running, but when they got to a lake, Jack said, "Let's stop and sort out the stones and pebbles around the edge". So the two of them did.

But after they had been sorting for a while, Jill started to get anxious. She said to Jack, "This is taking too long. Can we go now?" Jack looked up and said, "Just one more minute."

But time passed and they were still sorting, and Jill felt more and more anxious. She said, "Please, Jack, can we go now?" But still Jack said, "Just another few seconds".

But then suddenly it began to rain, and there was a thunder-storm. Jill and Jack had to run for cover, but Jill was too anxious and she slipped in the mud. Jack tried to help, but he flew onto the ground too. The two friends were both very sad and in the end they had to go home without having a march.

Once upon a time there was a little dinosaur. He was very cute and very small. He went on an adventure every day and he always asked his friends if they wanted to join him.

One day, the little dinosaur was walking around and he saw a big tree. He asked his friend, "What is that?" His friend said, "That's a tree." The little dinosaur asked, "Can I climb it?" His friend said, "Yes, you can try."

So the little dinosaur started to climb the tree. He was very slow, but he finally made it to the top. He was so excited that he shouted, "I did it!"

At the top of the tree, the little dinosaur saw a bird's nest. He asked his friend, "What's that?" His friend said, "That's a bird's nest. Let's see if we can see the little baby birds inside."

The little dinosaur carefully looked inside the nest, and sure enough, there were little baby birds. He was so happy and he waved to them. It was such a wonderful adventure!

Once upon a time, there was a green bird. She flew over the trees and down to the ground. The bird wanted to find a special person to help her.

She flew until she saw a building. She saw a sign that said "veterinarian". She had no idea what that meant, but she wanted to find out.

The bird passed through the door and there was a lady inside. The bird hop-hopped up to the lady, who smiled at her. The lady said she was the veterinarian and she could help her. The bird was happy, and the veterinarian took her to a special room.

The veterinarian took out a bright light and looked at the bird's feet. She said the bird was healthy and she passed the test. The bird thanked the veterinarian, and the veterinarian said she could go back and fly.

The bird flapped her wings, and it felt so good. She flew high into the sky, a happy green bird.

Ben and Lily were playing by the pond. They liked to watch the fish and the ducks swim in the water. They also liked to throw stones and sticks into the pond and see the ripples.

"Look, Lily, I found a big branch!" Ben said, holding a long and thick stick. "I can use it as a boat!"

He put the branch in the water and tried to push it with his hands. But the branch was too heavy and it sank.

"Oh no, Ben, your boat is gone!" Lily said, feeling sorry for her brother.

Ben was sad. He wanted to get his branch back. He took off his shoes and socks and rolled up his pants. He stepped into the pond and felt the cold water on his feet.

"I can swim and get it!" he said, bravely.

But Ben was not a good swimmer. He was clumsy and he splashed a lot. He could not reach the branch. He only got wet and muddy.

"Ben, come back, it's too deep!" Lily shouted, worried.

She ran to the edge of the pond and reached out her hand. Ben saw her and grabbed her hand. She pulled him out of the water and hugged him.

"Are you okay, Ben?" she asked, softly.

"I'm okay, Lily, thank you," he said, shivering.

They walked back to their mom, who was sitting on a bench. She saw them and smiled.

"What happened, my darlings?" she asked, kindly.

"We were playing with a branch, but it sank and Ben tried to swim and get it, but he couldn't and he got wet and Lily helped him and we're sorry, mom," they said, quickly.

Mom laughed and wrapped them in a towel.

"It's okay, my darlings, I'm glad you're safe and you helped each other. But next time, be careful and don't go into the pond without me, okay?" she said, gently.

"Okay, mom," they said, nodding.

They snuggled with mom and watched the fish and the ducks swim in the water. They forgot about the branch. They were happy.

Tim and Kim are twins. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a gray cat in a tree. The cat is scared and meows. Tim and Kim want to help the cat.

They look for a ladder, but they do not find one. They look for a rope, but they do not find one. They look for a big stick, but they do not find one. They are sad and do not know what to do.

Then, they see a big dog. The dog is tough and strong. He can run fast and jump high. Tim and Kim have an idea. They call the dog and show him the cat. The dog barks and runs to the tree. He jumps and catches the cat in his mouth. He brings the cat to Tim and Kim.

The cat is not hurt. He is happy and purrs. Tim and Kim are happy too. They pet the cat and the dog. They thank the dog for his rescue. The dog wags his tail and licks their faces. They are all friends. They play in the park until it is time to go home.

Tom and Mia were friends who liked to play with clay. They made many things with clay, like animals, cars, and houses. One day, they decided to make a volcano with clay. They used a big bottle as the base and covered it with brown clay. They made a hole at the top and put some red clay inside.

"Look, our volcano is ready!" Tom said. "Let's pretend it can erupt and make lava!"

"How do we make lava?" Mia asked.

"We need something that can melt and be hot and red," Tom said. He looked around and saw a box of crayons. He took some red crayons and broke them into pieces. He put them in a small pot and asked Mia to help him carry it to the kitchen.

"Can we use the stove?" Mia asked.

"Yes, but we have to be careful. It is very hot and can burn us," Tom said. He turned on the stove and put the pot on it. He watched as the crayons started to melt and become liquid.

"Wow, it looks like lava!" Mia said.

"Let's take it to our volcano and make it erupt!" Tom said. He used a big spoon to scoop some of the melted crayons and poured it into the hole of the volcano. The red liquid flowed down the sides of the volcano and made a mess on the floor.

"It looks so cool!" Mia said.

"It looks so rich!" Tom said.

They heard a loud voice from the living room. It was Tom's mom.

"Tom! Mia! What are you doing in the kitchen? Come here right now!"

Tom and Mia looked at each other and ran to the living room. They saw Tom's mom holding a newspaper. She looked very angry.

"Look at this! You ruined my newspaper with your crayons! It was a very important newspaper! It had a lot of rich people and their houses in it! How could you do this?"

Tom and Mia felt very sorry. They did not mean to ruin the newspaper. They only wanted to play with their volcano.

"We are sorry, mom. We did not know it was your newspaper. We were making lava for our volcano," Tom said.

"Your volcano? What volcano?" Tom's mom asked.

Tom and Mia took her to the kitchen and showed her their volcano. They explained how they made it with clay and crayons.

Tom's mom looked at their volcano and the mess on the floor. She was still angry, but she also saw how creative and clever they were.

"I see. You made a very nice volcano, but you also made a very big mess. You have to clean it up and apologize to me. And no more playing with crayons on the stove. It is very dangerous. Do you understand?"

Tom and Mia nodded. They said they were sorry and hugged Tom's mom. They cleaned up the mess and put away their volcano. They learned their lesson and promised to be more careful next time.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She liked to play on the beach with her yellow bucket and shovel. One day, she went to the ocean to collect shells. She found a big, shiny one and shouted, "Look, Mommy! Look what I found!"

Mommy came over and said, "Wow, that's a beautiful shell, Lily. It's as big as your hand!"

Lily was so happy and shouted, "I'm going to keep it forever!"

Mommy smiled and said, "That's a great idea. Let's put it in your bucket so we can take it home."

Lily and Mommy spent the rest of the day playing on the beach and collecting more shells. They had so much fun together by the ocean.

Once upon a time, there was a loud monkey. He lived in a big tree. He liked to bounce on the branches. His jumps were so high that he almost reached the sky. He enjoyed bouncing so much.

One day, a little girl decided to play with the monkey. She told him to bounce even higher. As he did this, his jumps got louder and louder. He could feel the wind in his fur.

The little girl and the monkey had so much fun together. They bounced up and down until they saw the sun setting. Then it was time to go home.

The monkey waved goodbye to the little girl. He thought to himself, that bouncing is the best way to have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a farmer who had a big farm. He had many animals like cows, horses, and chickens. One day, he bought a big ostrich. The ostrich was very tall and had long legs. The farmer thought the ostrich was very useful because it could run very fast.

The farmer decided to sell some of his animals to other farmers. He wanted to sell the cows and the horses, but he didn't want to sell the ostrich. He thought the ostrich was too special to sell. The other farmers came to the farm and looked at the animals. They liked the cows and the horses, but they also liked the ostrich. They asked the farmer if he would sell the ostrich too.

The farmer thought about it for a while and decided to sell the ostrich. He knew the other farmers would take good care of the ostrich. The other farmers were very happy to buy the ostrich. They thanked the farmer and took the ostrich to their farm. The farmer was happy too because he sold some of his animals and made some money.

Arya and her dad were very excited. They had all of the equipment they needed to make a tower. They had lots of blocks, colourful plastic tubes and several modest sized boxes. Arya and her dad carefully arranged the equipment and started to make the tower.

First they put all of the blocks on the floor and stacked them together. Then Arya's dad took two of the plastic tubes and put them on top of the blocks. They were very steady and strong.

Next Arya's dad lifted some of the boxes and placed them in-between the plastic tubes. He made sure they were balanced and secure.

Arya helped too. She added the last of the blocks to the top of the tower. They were on board with how tall the tower was and clapped in joy!

They stood back and admired their creation. The tower was very tall and it felt great to have made it!

Once upon a time, there was a small kitten called Smokey. Smokey was very curious and one day, Smokey found an odd looking tank. The tank was large and white, and Smokey had never seen one before. Smokey decided to examine the tank more closely.

Smokey hopped into the tank and was surprised to find a lot of tasty treats! Smokey felt very grateful and quickly gobbled up all the treats. After, Smokey examined the tank even more and noticed a button on one side. Smokey pushed the button, and all of a sudden a lot of fun bubbles came out of the tank! Smokey was so excited and had lots of fun playing with all the bubbles.

Smokey felt so happy and grateful that he had discovered the tank and all its treats and fun. From then on, Smokey and the tank were the best of friends!

Once upon a time there were two children, Max and Kat. They both wanted to play with the big drum in Grandma's house.

"Can we please play with the drum?" asked Max.

Grandma smiled. "Yes, but you must be polite and wait until I say it's ok to start," she said.

Max and Kat waited patiently for a few minutes. Then Grandma said it was ok to start.

They both ran to the drum and started to bang it. They had so much fun and the drum made lots of loud noise. They kept playing but Grandma said it was time to delay the fun.

Max and Kat did as they were told and stopped. They said thank you to Grandma for letting them play with the drum and said goodbye.

The two children were sad to go but they were also very polite. They remembered to delay their fun and thanked Grandma for letting them play with the drum.

When she looked more closely, she saw something special - a big, shiny yellow leaf. She was very happy and decided to give the leaf to her mommy.

Molly picked up the leaf and when she saw it in her hands, she smiled. She gave her mommy the new leaf and they both smiled.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they find a big box near the slide. The box is empty. Tom has an idea.

"Let's fly with the box!" he says to Mia.

"How can we fly with the box?" Mia asks.

"Look, we can put the box on the slide. Then we can sit in the box and slide down. It will be like flying!" Tom says.

Mia thinks it sounds fun. She nods and smiles. They lift the box and put it on the slide. They climb in the box and hold each other's hands.

"Ready, set, go!" Tom shouts.

They slide down the slide in the box. The box goes fast and makes a loud noise. Tom and Mia feel the wind in their hair and the sun on their faces. They laugh and scream.

"We are flying! We are flying!" they say.

The box reaches the end of the slide and falls on the grass. Tom and Mia tumble out of the box. They are not hurt. They are happy.

"That was awesome!" Tom says.

"Let's do it again!" Mia says.

They run back to the slide with the box. They fly with the box again and again. They have a lot of fun. They are the best flyers in the park.

Once upon a time there was a little boy called Jack. He was three years old and his favourite thing to do was play outside. One day Jack went out to play and he heard a big sneeze. He rushed over to see what had happened and he saw a wide dragon.

Jack was scared at first, but then he noticed the dragon was smiling at him.

"Hello there!" the dragon said.

"Hi!" Jack said back. He was feeling brave now.

"I'm so sorry to sneeze like that," the dragon said. "I just had the most amazing dream and it made me so happy that I couldn't help but sneeze!"

Jack smiled. He was glad the dragon was so cheerful.

"Thank you for being so nice," said the dragon. Then it flew away, leaving Jack feeling happy and wide awake.

Once upon a time there was a modest Missle. He remembered being with his family, but he had been gone for a very long time. Everywhere he went, he was reminded of his family, and it made him feel sad. One day, he decided to find his family again. He flew far away to the place where he had last seen them. When he arrived, he found them all waiting for him. Everyone was so happy to see each other. They hugged and cried together.

The modest missile was so glad he remembered to come back home and be with his family. They all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time there was a boy named Tom. Tom wanted to behave as best as he could. Every morning, he was given a strip of paper and told to behave until the end of the day. But Tom could never quite keep his promise and he often ended up being naughty.

One day, Tom found a fragile butterfly. He wanted to play with it so badly, but his mom warned him not to. He needed to be extra careful because the butterfly was very delicate. But Tom couldn't resist the temptation and he soon started to play with the butterfly.

Unfortunately, he didn't handle the butterfly very carefully and it eventually broke its fragile wings. Tom felt very sad and he knew he shouldn't have disobeyed his mom. He quickly put the broken butterfly back in its place, but he had lost the strip of paper his mom gave him in the morning. Tom was afraid of getting punished, so he ran away and hid somewhere.

At the end of the day, Tom finally returned home. His mom was so happy to see him, she didn't even mention the strip of paper.

Tom learned his lesson and from then on he tried to always behave correctly.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to cook with her mommy in the kitchen. One day, while they were making pancakes, Lily accidentally dropped the pan on the floor. It broke into tiny pieces because it was fragile.

Lily felt very sad because the pan was her mommy's favorite. She asked, "Mommy, can we fix the pan?" Her mommy said, "I don't think we can, but we can get a new one." Lily replied, "But mommy, we don't have enough money to buy a new one."

Suddenly, they heard a loud noise outside. They looked out the window and saw that their neighbor's cat was stuck in a tree. Lily said, "Mommy, we have to rescue the cat!" They quickly ran outside and helped the cat get down from the tree. The neighbor was so grateful that she gave them some money to buy a new pan. Lily was happy because they could make pancakes again, and the cat was happy because it was safe.

One day, the farmer asked the animals to help him. He wanted to dig up a root from the ground. They all started to stir and dig.

The animals dug for a long time until finally the root was revealed. It was big, but the animals were too tired from all their stirring to be excited.

The farmer thanked the animals for all their hard work. He said that he couldn't have done it without them. The animals were so happy that they could help their bossy farmer.

Lila and Tom are friends. They like to play outside. One day, they find a big tent in the yard. They want to go inside.

"Can we play in the tent?" Lila asks Tom.

"Yes, let's go!" Tom says. They run to the tent and open the flap.

Inside, they see a lot of pillows and blankets. They also see a basket with some food. They are hungry.

"Look, there are apples and cheese and bread!" Lila says. She takes an apple and bites it.

"Yuck!" she says. "This apple is bitter. It makes my mouth hurt."

She spits out the apple and makes a face.

"Let me try," Tom says. He takes another apple and bites it.

"Yuck!" he says. "This apple is bitter too. It is not good."

He spits out the apple and makes a face.

They look at the basket and see a note. It says:

"Dear Lila and Tom,

I am sorry, but these apples are not for you. They are for the birds. They like bitter apples. You can have the cheese and bread. They are yummy.

Love, Mom."

Lila and Tom laugh. They are silly. They sit in the tent and eat the cheese and bread. They are happy. They share with the birds. The birds are happy too. They all play in the tent.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, she went to a big hall with her mommy and daddy. The hall was very big and had many seats for people to sit on.

Lily saw a mysterious door at the end of the hall. She wanted to see what was behind the door, but her parents said it was not safe.

Lily sat on one of the seats and watched as people came and went. She wondered if anyone would go behind the mysterious door. But no one did.

Finally, it was time to go home. Lily was sad she didn't get to see what was behind the mysterious door. But she was happy she got to sit on a seat in the big hall.

Anna and Ben were playing in the garden. They liked to dig in the dirt and look for bugs. Sometimes they found worms, snails, or beetles. They put them in a jar and watched them move.

One day, they saw something shiny in the dirt. It was a coin. It was round and silver and had a picture on it. Anna and Ben were very happy. They thought it was a treasure.

"Look, look, we found a treasure!" they shouted to their mom, who was sitting on the porch.

Mom came to see what they had. She smiled and said, "That's a very nice coin, but it's not a treasure. It's a quarter. You can use it to buy something at the store."

Anna and Ben were confused. They didn't know what a quarter was. They thought it was more special than that.

"But it's so shiny and pretty," Anna said.

"And it has a picture of a man with a hat," Ben said.

Mom explained, "That's George Washington. He was a wise man who helped our country a long time ago. He was the first president. He's on the quarter because he's important."

Anna and Ben looked at the coin again. They felt a little disappointed. They thought they had found something more exciting.

Mom saw their faces and said, "Don't be sad. You still found something interesting. And I have a surprise for you. Do you want to see it?"

Anna and Ben nodded. They liked surprises.

Mom took them to the car and drove them to the store. She gave them each a quarter and said, "You can use these to buy something you like. Maybe a candy, a sticker, or a toy. What do you think?"

Anna and Ben smiled. They were happy again. They thanked Mom and ran to the store. They looked at all the things they could buy. They felt like they had a treasure after all.

Tim walked around the park with the kids and they found the parents. The parents were happy to see their kids and thanked Tim for his help. Tim felt proud that he could help the kids and their parents. From that day on, the kids knew they could always count on tall cop Tim to help them.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy man who lived in a big house. He had a pet lizard that he loved very much. One day, the lizard got lost and the man searched for him everywhere but could not find him. The man began to weep because he was so sad that his lizard was gone.

Days went by and the man continued to weep. Then, one day, the man heard a noise in his garden. He went outside and saw his lizard! The man was so happy that he picked up the lizard and hugged him tightly. From that day on, the man made sure to keep a close eye on his beloved lizard so he would never get lost again.

"What was that?" Maggie said.

A deep voice came from behind her. "It's me, the big scary bear!"

Maggie was so scared she almost fell out of the tree. She slowly looked around and sure enough there was a big bear standing right behind her.

The bear growled. "What are you doing in my tree?"

Maggie was scared, but she was brave. She slowly stood up and said, "I was looking for some nice flowers."

The bear nodded and said, "Well, be careful. I'm always around. Come on, I'll help you get down."

He helped Maggie climb back down safely. When she was finally on the ground, she thanked the bear for his help and ran off home. She was relieved and happy to know she was safe.

"This ink is bad!" said the little girl sadly.

"I'll bring back some better ink so you can draw with it and have fun," he said.

Gus loved to drive his reliable vehicle. Every day, he would hop in, start it up and drive off! But today, Gus was disappointed. When he went to turn on the vehicle, it wouldn't start. He resisted turning away and tried again and again, but it just wouldn't go. He had to find someone else to help him.

So Gus walked off to find help. He walked past a house and saw a mechanic. Waving his hands, he called out, "Please help me!" The mechanic smiled and said he'd help.

They both went back to the vehicle and the man opened the hood. Right away he saw the problem, but he knew Gus could fix it. The mechanic showed Gus what to do and said, "If you resist giving up, I promise your vehicle will be reliable again."

Gus followed the mechanic's instructions, and quickly he heard the engine roar to life! He jumped for joy and thanked the mechanic. He promised to stay reliable and never give up again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy was playing with his toys when he saw a butterfly. The butterfly was very pretty and had many colors. Timmy wanted to catch the butterfly, so he ran after it. But the butterfly disappeared! Timmy looked everywhere but he couldn't find it.

Later that day, Timmy's mom was cutting some paper with scissors. Timmy saw the scissors and wanted to play with them. But his mom told him that scissors were not toys and they were very sharp. Timmy understood and didn't touch the scissors.

At dinner time, Timmy's dad gave him a lemon to taste. Timmy made a face because the lemon was very sour. His dad laughed and said that some fruits are sour but they are still good for you. Timmy learned something new and finished his dinner happily.

Lily was sick and had to go to the hospital. She did not like the hospital. It was big and loud and scary. She wanted to go home and play with her toys.

A nurse came to see Lily. She had a white coat and a kind smile. She said, "Hello, Lily. I'm here to help you feel better. I need to check your temperature and give you some medicine. Can you be brave for me?"

Lily nodded, but she felt nervous. The nurse put a stick in her mouth and looked at a watch. Then she took a syringe from a tray. Lily saw the needle and started to cry. She did not want the needle. It would hurt.

The nurse said, "I know, Lily. It's not fun, but it's very important. The medicine will make you strong and fight the germs. It will be over soon, I promise. Do you want me to hold your hand?"

Lily nodded again, and the nurse held her hand. She felt a little better. The nurse said, "You're doing great, Lily. You're very brave. Do you know what this medicine reminds me of?"

Lily shook her head, still crying.

The nurse said, "It reminds me of strawberry jam. It's red and sweet and good for you. Do you like strawberry jam?"

Lily sniffed and said, "Yes, I do. I like it on toast."

The nurse said, "Me too. Maybe when you get better, you can have some toast with strawberry jam. How does that sound?"

Lily smiled a little and said, "That sounds nice."

The nurse said, "Good. Now, let's count to three and then I'll give you the medicine, okay? One, two, three!"

The nurse pushed the syringe and Lily felt a pinch. She whimpered, but it was over quickly. The nurse said, "You did it, Lily! You're amazing! I'm so proud of you!"

Lily felt proud too, but also embarrassed. She had cried in front of the nurse. She said, "I'm sorry, I was scared."

The nurse said, "That's okay, Lily. Everyone gets scared sometimes. Even me. But you were very brave and strong. You don't have to be sorry. You have to be happy. You're getting better every day."

Lily hugged the nurse and said, "Thank you, nurse. You're very nice. You make me happy."

The nurse hugged her back and said, "You're very welcome, Lily. You make me happy too."

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to eat yogurt for breakfast. Mom gives them each a cup of yogurt and a spoon. Sara likes strawberry yogurt. Ben likes blueberry yogurt.

Sara looks at Ben's yogurt. She thinks it is beautiful. It is purple and has little dots. She wants to try it. She says to Ben, "Can I have some of your yogurt?"

Ben says, "No, this is my yogurt. You have your own yogurt. It is pink and has seeds. You like it."

Sara says, "But I want to taste your yogurt. It looks yummy. Please, Ben, please."

Ben says, "No, Sara, no. Go away. Leave me alone."

Sara gets angry. She pulls Ben's yogurt cup from his hand. Ben pulls it back. They pull and pull. The yogurt cup breaks. The yogurt spills on the table and on their clothes. They both cry.

Mom hears them cry. She comes to the kitchen. She sees the mess. She is not happy. She says, "What happened here? Why did you pull the yogurt cup? Why did you spill the yogurt?"

Sara and Ben say, "We are sorry, Mom. We wanted to share the yogurt. But we pulled too hard. We made a mistake."

Mom says, "It's okay to share, but you have to ask nicely and wait for an answer. You don't pull things from each other. That is not nice. That is rude. You have to be gentle and respectful. Do you understand?"

Sara and Ben say, "Yes, Mom, we understand. We are sorry. We love you."

Mom says, "I love you too. But now you have to clean up the mess. And then you have to change your clothes. And then you can have some more yogurt. But this time, no pulling, okay?"

Sara and Ben say, "Okay, Mom, okay. No pulling. Thank you for the yogurt. It is delicious."

Tom and Lily are twins. They like to go on the subway with their mom. The subway is a big train that goes under the ground. It makes a loud noise and goes very fast.

One day, they go on the subway to visit their grandma. They sit on the seats and look out the window. They see lights and tunnels and other trains. They are very happy.

Tom says, "Mom, can we sing a song?" Mom says, "Sure, what song do you want to sing?" Tom says, "I want to sing the ABC song. It is my favorite song. I know all the letters." Lily says, "Me too. I can sing the ABC song. I am very intelligent."

Mom smiles and says, "You are both very intelligent. Let's sing the ABC song together." They start to sing, "A, B, C, D, E, F, G..." They sing very loud and clear. They have fun.

Some people on the subway hear them sing. They smile and clap. They say, "What a lovely song. You are very good singers. You are very cute." Tom and Lily feel proud. They say, "Thank you. We love to sing. We love the subway." Mom hugs them and says, "I love you. You make me happy." They reach their grandma's stop. They get off the subway and wave goodbye to the people. They go to see their grandma. She is very happy to see them. She gives them cookies and milk. They tell her about their subway adventure. They sing the ABC song for her. She says, "You are very intelligent. You are very good singers. You are very sweet." They hug her and say, "We love you, grandma. You make us happy." They have a wonderful day.

Once upon a time there was a little duck. It was a happy duck who liked to splash in the pond. Then one day, it saw something shiny on the water - it was a big, ugly print. The print was big and ugly and the duck was scared.

But it didn't stay scared for too long. It remembered how much fun it had splashing in the pond. So it took a deep breath and it bravely jumped into the water. As soon as it did, it made a big splash that made the ugly print go away. It couldn't believe it had been so brave.

The little duck was very happy now. It had been brave and the ugly print was gone. And it could splash whenever it wanted. So the little duck splashed and splashed and splashed in the pond, and it stayed happy forever.

Tom and Sam are brothers. They like to help their dad in the kitchen. Dad is a cook. He makes yummy food for people. He works hard every day.

One day, Tom and Sam want to work with dad. They ask him, "Dad, can we work with you? We want to learn how to cook."

Dad smiles and says, "Sure, you can work with me. But you have to be careful. Cooking is fun, but it can also be dangerous. You have to follow my rules."

Dad gives Tom and Sam aprons and hats. He shows them how to wash their hands and the vegetables. He tells them to cut the carrots with a knife. He says, "The knife is sharp. You have to hold it with two hands. And you have to cut slowly and gently. Don't cut your fingers. And don't touch the stove. It is hot."

Tom and Sam nod and say, "OK, dad. We will be careful." They take the knife and the carrots. They try to cut them like dad. But it is hard. The carrots are tough. They have to use a lot of strength. They make funny faces and noises. They say, "This is hard work. But we can do it."

Dad watches them and laughs. He says, "You are doing great, boys. You are good helpers. I am proud of you. Soon, you will be good cooks like me." He hugs them and kisses them. He says, "I love you, Tom and Sam. You make me happy."

Tom and Sam hug him back and say, "We love you, dad. You make us happy. And you make the best food ever." They smile and keep working. They have fun in the kitchen.

One day Mommy, Daddy and Sarah went out to the beach. Sarah was very excited to find shells. She started looking up and down the beach and found some very interesting ones. Mommy asked Sarah which shell she thought was the most special. Sarah picked up a shiny white shell and said, "This one! Look at it shine!"

Daddy was very proud of Sarah and said, "You found a beautiful shell! Good job!"

Sarah smiled and said, "I'm going to keep it forever."

Mommy smiled and said, "I'm sure it will make you very happy each time you look at it."

Sarah nodded and looked at the beautiful shell with a big grin on her face. She was so proud to have found such a special shell. She was sure it would always make her feel happy.

Once upon a time, there were three forest animals, named Fran, Mabel, and Dandy. Fran was a rabbit, Mabel was a rabbit and Dandy was a squirrel. They all loved to gather together in the forest and play.

One day, the animals decided to have a competitive race to see who was the fasted. They all lined up at the start line and Mabel took of first. She was so fast that she didn't even see the law of the forest that said no one must run faster than the rabbits.

Before she knew it, Mabel had broken the law. Fran and Dandy were so sad. They each gathered their courage and confronted Mabel. But Mabel refused to apologize or listen.

The moral of the story is that it is important to obey and respect the law. If you don't, you might break the law and end up in trouble.

Once upon a time, there was a brave little girl who wanted to find something special. She set off on an adventure to find it.

She travelled through the forest, over a hill and down a river. After a long time of searching, she finally found it!

She found an ancient fuel, which glowed very brightly. She was so excited, she couldn't believe her luck.

The little girl took the fuel home and planted it in her garden. It grew into a beautiful plant with different coloured flowers.

The brave little girl was very pleased with her find. She always remembered that special day when she found the ancient fuel.

Sam and Mia are playing in the garden. They like to run and jump and laugh. But it is very hot today. The sun is bright and the air is dry. Sam and Mia are thirsty and tired.

"Let's go inside and drink some water," Sam says to Mia. "We can play more later."

"OK," Mia says. "But we have to be alert. Mom said there are ants on the step. They can bite us if we touch them."

Sam and Mia nod. They know ants are small and fast and sometimes angry. They do not want to make them mad. They walk carefully to the door. They look at the step. They see many ants crawling on it. Some are red and some are black.

"Wow, look at all the ants!" Sam says. "They are busy. What are they doing?"

"I don't know," Mia says. "Maybe they are looking for food. Or water. Maybe they are thirsty too."

Sam and Mia feel sorry for the ants. They want to help them. They have an idea.

"Wait here," Sam says. He runs inside and gets a cup of water. He comes back and pours some water on the ground near the step. He makes a small puddle.

"Here, ants," he says. "You can drink this. It is cool and wet. It will make you happy."

Mia smiles. She thinks Sam is kind and smart. She watches the ants. Some of them stop and go to the puddle. They drink the water. They look happy.

"See, they like it!" Mia says. "You are a good friend, Sam."

Sam and Mia hug. They are happy too. They go inside and drink some water. They are not thirsty anymore. They are ready to play more. They are good friends.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Jack and Jill. Jack wanted to be the king of the land and always told Jill that he would be the one to rule. But Jill didn't like this and refused to surrender to Jack.

One day, when Jack was playing with his cushion, Jill took it away. Jack was so angry, he started to yell at her! He said "Give me back my cushion!"

But Jill refused, throwing it from one side of the room to the other! She said "No way! I'm not going to surrender!"

Jack wasn't about to give in. He grabbed the cushion and headed for the door. But Jill was quicker and managed to get there first, standing with her feet firmly on the ground.

Jack tried pulling her away from the door, but it was no use. For the third time, he said "Give me back my cushion!"

Finally, Jill agreed to give it back, but only on one condition: that Jack agreed to share the cushion with her, using it at regular intervals. Jack agreed, and they both ended up sharing the cushion and ruling the land together.
The end.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play together. Today, they go to the cinema with Lily's mum. They want to see a film about animals.

They sit in big chairs and eat popcorn. The film starts. They see lions, monkeys, birds and more. The animals do funny things. Lily and Tom laugh a lot. They think the film is jolly.

But then, a big snake comes on the screen. It is scary. It hisses and tries to bite a mouse. Lily and Tom do not like the snake. They hide behind their popcorn. They want the snake to go away.

"Shh, it's okay," Lily's mum says. "It's not real. It's just a film. The snake can't hurt you."

Lily and Tom peek at the screen. The mouse runs away from the snake. The snake slithers away. The film shows other animals again. They are happy and safe.

Lily and Tom feel better. They smile and hug each other. They watch the rest of the film. They seem to enjoy it. They clap when the film ends. They say thank you to Lily's mum. They had a good time.

Once upon a time there lived a boy named Jack. He was curious to explore the world around him. One day he decided to take a walk in the forest. While walking, he saw a large whip! Jack wondered what it was and was so curious that he decided to take it.

When Jack brought the whip back home, his mom saw him and asked him where he got it. Jack told his mom he found it in the forest. His mom said "I think it is better if you remain here with me, Jack!"

Jack was disappointed and started to cry. His mom hugged him and said, "Don't worry, Jack! We can go back to the forest tomorrow and leave it back where it was".

Jack was so excited and couldn't wait to go back to the forest the next day. He grabbed his mom's hand and said, "Let's go, Mom!". So the next day, Jack and his mom went back to the forest and found the same spot where Jack had found the whip. They left it there, and Jack smiled happily, knowing that he had done the right thing.

Once upon a time there lived a happy little boy named Jack. He loved to play outside and make new friends.

One day, Jack was playing in the park when he stumbled upon an old, rusty trap. It was a scary sight. He froze as he tried to remember where he had seen something like it before.

Jack called out to his friend, "Hey, do you know what this is?"

His friend replied, "I think it's an animal trap. People use it to trap animals but it looks dead now."

Jack remembered hearing a story about animal traps that his grandma had told him. He shivered. He was glad he was safe from the trap's danger.

Jack and his friend decided it was best to leave the trap and never come back. They ran away as fast as they could and never looked back.

The End.

Anna and Ben were twins who liked to visit their grandma every week. Grandma lived in a big house with a nice garden and many toys. She was wealthy and gave them gifts and treats. Anna and Ben loved their grandma very much.

One day, when they went to visit her, they saw smoke coming out of her house. They were scared and ran to the door. They knocked and shouted, "Grandma, grandma, are you okay?" But no one answered. They saw a fire truck and some firefighters coming to help. They hoped grandma was safe.

The firefighters put out the fire and brought grandma out. She was coughing and had some burns. She hugged Anna and Ben and said, "I'm so glad you're here. I'm sorry, my house is ruined. I was cooking and forgot to turn off the stove. The fire started and spread quickly. I couldn't get out."

Anna and Ben felt sorry for grandma. They said, "We're sorry too, grandma. We don't care about your house or your toys or your gifts. We care about you. You're the best grandma in the world. We love you."

Grandma smiled and said, "Thank you, my darlings. You're the best twins in the world. I love you too. You know, I learned a lesson today. Having a lot of money and things doesn't make you happy. Having a loving family and friends does. I'm lucky to have you."

The moral value of the story is: Family and love are more important than money and things.

Sam and Mia like to play at the park. They like to swing, climb and slide. One day, they see a big slide that they have never tried before. It is very high and very long.

"Let's go on the big slide!" Sam says. He runs to the ladder and climbs up.

"Wait for me!" Mia says. She follows him, but she is a little scared.

They reach the top of the slide and look down. It is very steep and very fast.

"Are you ready?" Sam asks.

"I don't know," Mia says. "It looks scary."

"Don't worry, I will go first and catch you," Sam says. He is brave and wants to be a hero. He sits on the slide and pushes himself down.

"Whee!" he shouts as he slides down. He feels the wind in his hair and the sun on his face. He reaches the bottom and lands on the grass. He is happy and proud.

"Come on, Mia, it's fun!" he calls to her.

Mia looks at Sam. He is smiling and waving at her. She wants to try the slide too, but she is still scared.

"Maybe I can do it," she says to herself. She sits on the slide and holds on to the sides. She closes her eyes and slides down.

"Ahh!" she screams as she slides down. She feels the slide go up and down and twist and turn. She opens her eyes and sees the ground coming closer. She is scared and excited.

She reaches the bottom and slides off the slide. She bumps into Sam and they both fall on the grass. They are both messy and dirty, but they are also happy and laughing.

"You did it, Mia, you did it!" Sam says. He hugs her and helps her up.

"You are my hero, Sam, you are my hero!" Mia says. She hugs him back and thanks him.

They look at each other and smile. They are friends and they like to slide. They run to the slide again and slide down together. They have fun at the park.

Sara was sad. She missed her mom and dad. They went to work and left her with Nana. Nana was nice, but Sara wanted her mom and dad.

Nana saw Sara crying on the couch. She came over and gave her a hug. "What's wrong, Sara?" she asked.

"I want mom and dad," Sara sobbed. "They are gone for a long time."

Nana wiped Sara's tears and smiled. "They will be back soon, Sara. They love you very much. They are working hard to make money for you and them. Do you know what money is?"

Sara nodded. "Money is what you use to buy things. Like toys and clothes and food."

"That's right, Sara. And sometimes, mom and dad have to work a lot to buy those things. But they always think of you and miss you too. They will call you later and tell you how much they love you. Do you want to hear their voices?"

Sara nodded again. She liked to hear mom and dad's voices. They made her feel happy and safe.

Nana took Sara to the phone and dialed a number. She handed the phone to Sara. "Here, Sara. Say hello to mom."

Sara heard mom's voice on the phone. "Hi, Sara. How are you, my sweetie?"

"Hi, mom. I miss you," Sara said.

"I miss you too, Sara. I love you so much. Are you having fun with Nana?"

Sara looked at Nana, who smiled and winked. "Yes, mom. Nana is nice. She comforted me when I was sad. She gave me a hug and a kiss."

"That's good, Sara. Nana is very kind and caring. She loves you too. You are lucky to have her. And you know what? I have a surprise for you when I come home."

"What is it, mom?" Sara asked, curious.

"It's a secret, Sara. But I'll give you a hint. It's something yummy that you like to eat. Can you guess what it is?"

Sara thought for a moment. She liked to eat many things. But there was one thing that she liked the most. It was sweet and soft and had many colors. "Is it cake, mom?" she guessed.

Mom laughed. "You are very smart, Sara. Yes, it is cake. I bought a cake for you and dad and me. We will have a special treat tonight. How does that sound?"

Sara clapped her hands and smiled. "That sounds great, mom. I love cake. Thank you, mom."

"You're welcome, Sara. I can't wait to see you and hug you and eat cake with you. But now I have to go back to work. I'll talk to you later, okay?"

"Okay, mom. I love you. Bye."

"I love you too, Sara. Bye."

Sara hung up the phone and hugged Nana. "Mom bought a cake for us, Nana. Isn't that nice?"

"That's very nice, Sara. Mom is very thoughtful and generous. She wants to make you happy. You are lucky to have her. And she is lucky to have you. You are a good girl, Sara. You are brave and patient and polite. You make mom and dad proud."

Sara felt warm and fuzzy inside. She was happy to have Nana and mom and dad. They all loved her and she loved them. She learned that sometimes, mom and dad had to work, but they always came back. And sometimes, Nana had to comfort her, but she always made her feel better. And sometimes, they had a yummy cake, but they always shared it. They had a good rhythm of life. They were a happy family.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and loved to explore the world around her. One day, her parents showed her a special tool called a microscope. It was very weird and Jane was curious.

When she looked through the microscope she saw so many things that she had never seen before! She saw tiny ants, tiny hairs and tiny pieces of dust. She felt like she had discovered a whole new world.

Suddenly, Jane noticed something strange moving around inside the microscope. It was a tiny creature, and it was resisting her attempts to grab it. Jane tried and tried but she just couldn't catch it!

Jane wanted to explore this new creature so she asked her parents if she could try and figure out what it was. They said yes and Jane was so excited.

She got out her magnifying glass and studied the tiny creature. After a few minutes, Jane realized it was a weird kind of bug. She had never seen one before and she felt proud that she had figured it out.

Jane was so happy that she was able to resist the mystery bug and find out what it was!
