Lily asked her mom to help her. Her mom picked her up and she was able to reach the jar. Lily was so happy! She opened the jar and took a cookie. It was so yummy!

From that time on, Lily knew that if she couldn't reach something, she could always ask for help. And if she was patient, she would be able to enjoy something yummy. The end.

Once there was a baby who loved to dream. The baby dreamt about lovely things everyday. One night in her dream, she saw a magnificent castle with a beautiful garden. It was so lovely that she wanted to explore it. As she approached the castle gates, she saw a bright light that shone from within.

The baby loved the light and wanted to get closer to it. She ran and ran until she reached the castle entrance. When she stepped inside, she found a magical world filled with wonderful colours and sounds. She saw animals running around and flowers blooming.

The baby smiled with delight as she explored the castle. Suddenly, she heard a deep comforting voice that said, "Come closer. I love you, my lovely baby". The baby now knew this place was a special place.

The baby no longer wanted to explore the castle. She wanted to stay in her dream and be with this loving voice forever. She smiled and closed her eyes, resting in the warmth and comfort of being so loved.

Once there was a little bunny. He was a very happy bunny, but he was also very messy! His fur was filthy and his home was filled with junk.

One day, spring came and the sun was shining brightly. The bunny was inspired to tidy up his home. He emptied out all the junk and swept the floors.

When he was finished, he was very proud of himself. To reward himself, the bunny decided to go on an adventure.

He hopped through the forest until he found a tree with lots of treats. He stepped up onto a branch and picked the best one he could find. Then he ran back home to enjoy his yummy treat.

The bunny was so happy to have found the treats and enjoyed his day of cleaning and adventure. From then on, he always made sure his home was neat and tidy, so he would always be ready for new spring adventures!

Steve the driver was driving down the road in his car. Everything was going smoothly until he blinked. Suddenly he saw something harmless up ahead in the distance. It was a small puppy!

Steve quickly pulled the car over to the side of the road. He stepped out of the car to take a closer look at the puppy. He knelt down, smiled and said hello.

The puppy wagged its tail at Steve, but then it started to bark.

"What's the matter? It looks like you're scared" said Steve.
The puppy just kept barking and wouldn't stop.

Steve was concerned and he worried that the puppy was lost or hurt. He then noticed a man with a concerned look on his face walking towards him. The man asked Steve what was happening.

Steve said: "This harmless puppy is scared. Do you know it?"

The man nodded and said "Yes, this is my puppy. He got lost and scared. I'm so glad he was found by someone who was so kind and caring."

The man thanked Steve and the two of them took the puppy back home. Now the puppy was safe and sound!

Once upon a time, there was a war. Many people were sad and scared. The purple sky was filled with smoke from the cannons and guns. The war was so long, it seemed like it would never end.

One day, a brave hero decided to take a stand. He climbed to the top of the hill and owned a big flag, purple like the sky. He shouted to the warriors, calling for a delay. He said that they should all stop their fighting, and that enough people had already been hurt.

The other warriors felt the brave hero's words in their hearts. They were a bit scared, but they agreed to the delay. The war stopped and the sky started to clear. Happy warriors hugged each other and thanked the brave hero.

The war was over and they all lived happily ever after.

Tommy was walking in the field nearby his house. He was enjoying being outside and was looking at the trees around him. Suddenly he stumbled over a rock in the grass. He sat up and looked around. The sun was bright and he could see all of the way to the horizon, it was a clear day. He noticed something strange, it was so quiet. He couldn't even hear a bird or a cricket. He was used to hearing his mom and dad talking but now there was only silence. He was a little bit scared but also a bit excited to explore. He looked around and noticed a small path next to him. He started to walk down it, feeling very brave. He soon happily ran off to find adventure.

Lily and Ben like to play in the kitchen. They find a big bowl and a spoon. They want to make a cake.

"What do we need to make a cake?" Lily asks.

"We need flour and eggs and milk and sugar," Ben says.

They look for the ingredients in the cupboards and the fridge. They put them in the bowl. They start to mix them with the spoon.

"Mix, mix, mix," they sing. "We are making a cake!"

But they mix too hard and too fast. The batter splashes out of the bowl. It makes a big mess on the table and the floor and their clothes.

"Oh no!" Lily says. "We are wet and sticky!"

They hear Mom coming. They are scared. They hide under the table.

"Who made this mess?" Mom says. She sees the bowl and the spoon and the batter. She sees Lily and Ben hiding under the table.

"Did you do this?" Mom asks.

Lily and Ben nod. They are sorry.

"We wanted to make a cake," Lily says.

Mom sighs. She is not happy. But she is not angry.

"Next time, you have to ask me first," Mom says. "And you have to be careful and gentle when you mix."

She helps them clean up the mess. She puts their wet clothes in the washing machine. She gives them new clothes.

She finds a cookie in her pocket. She breaks it in half. She gives one half to Lily and one half to Ben.

"Here, this is your cake for today," Mom says. "Maybe tomorrow we can make a real cake together."

Lily and Ben smile. They say thank you. They eat their cookie. They hug Mom.

They still like to play in the kitchen. But they always ask Mom first. And they always mix slowly and softly.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the sunshine. One day, she went on an adventure with her best friend, Teddy the bear. They went on a walk in the forest, and Lily asked Teddy if he wanted to search for treasure.

Teddy was very intelligent and knew just where to look. They walked through the trees and over the rocks until they found a shiny diamond. Lily was so happy, and she thanked Teddy for being so smart. But then, they heard a loud noise coming from behind them.

It was a big, scary dragon! Teddy knew they had to run away fast. They ran and ran until they were safe at home. Lily hugged Teddy and said, "You're my hero, Teddy. You're so intelligent and you saved us from the dragon." Teddy smiled and hugged her back, happy to have helped his best friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, she went to her grandma's house. Grandma was cooking yummy cookies in the kitchen.

Lily asked, "Can I help you cook, grandma?"

Grandma smiled and said, "Of course, you can help me mix the dough."

While they were cooking, grandma told Lily a story about history. She told her about how people used to live a long time ago and how things have changed since then.

After they finished cooking, grandma gave Lily a cookie. Lily took a big bite, but it didn't fit in her mouth.

Grandma laughed and said, "Remember, Lily, always take small bites and chew your food well."

Lily learned an important lesson that day - to always take small bites and chew her food well.

Timmy and his Dad were walking down the street when they saw a cop. The cop was very tall and he had a shiny shirt.

Timmy's Dad said to the cop, "Why are you here?"

The cop said, "I'm here to manage the traffic."

Timmy's Dad thought that was very brave of the cop.

But then, Timmy saw a man walking by and he was being very selfish. He was pushing other people out of the way to get to where he wanted to go.

The cop saw the man and said, "I'm sorry, Sir, but you can't be so selfish."

The man stopped what he was doing and said he was sorry.

The cop then gave the man a stern warning and said, "You need to be more considerate or I will have to give you a ticket."

The man said he understood and then he went on his way.

Timmy was so impressed with how the cop managed the situation. He felt better knowing there were people like the cop who would help look after everyone.

One day a fox was hungry so he went searching for food. He looked in the tall grass but there was nothing to eat. He looked in the nearby trees but there was still nothing to eat. He started to get bored.

Suddenly, he spotted a little girl nearby. She had a bucket of food and she was happy to feed the fox. The fox was so excited and he gobbled up all the food in the bucket. He was very happy and his tummy was full.

The fox thanked the girl and he scampered away into the forest. The girl was happy to help the fox. She enjoyed feeding the fox because it was not boring at all.

Lily and Ben were very happy to move to a new house. They had many boxes with their toys and clothes. They wanted to unpack them and play in their new rooms.

"Can we unpack now, Mom?" Lily asked.

"Not yet, sweetie. We have to wait for Dad to come home. He has the keys to the rooms," Mom said.

Lily and Ben were sad. They wanted to unpack now. They looked around the living room. They saw a big couch. It was red and blue and yellow. It had many shapes on it. It was a unique couch.

"Wow, look at this couch!" Ben said. "It looks like a puzzle. Let's play on it!"

Lily and Ben climbed on the couch. They jumped and bounced and slid. They had fun. They did not see the sign on the couch. It said: "Do not sit on the couch. It is very old and fragile. It will break."

The couch made a loud noise. It cracked and snapped and collapsed. Lily and Ben fell to the floor. They were hurt and scared. They cried.

Mom heard the noise. She ran to the living room. She saw the broken couch and the crying children. She was angry and worried.

"What have you done?" Mom yelled. "This couch was very special. It belonged to Grandma. She gave it to us as a gift. It was very expensive and rare. You have ruined it!"

Lily and Ben felt very bad. They said they were sorry. They did not mean to break the couch. They just wanted to play.

Mom did not listen. She said they were naughty and careless. She said they had to go to their rooms and stay there. She said they could not unpack or play until Dad came home. She said he would be very mad too.

Lily and Ben went to their rooms. They were sad and lonely. They wished they had not played on the couch. They wished they had waited to unpack. They wished they had a different new house. They wished they had a different mom.

Once upon a time, there was a happy little boy named Tim. Tim loved trains and would watch them go by his house every day. One day, Tim saw a train that looked different from the others. It was a bright red train with a big smiley face on the front. Tim was very excited and jumped up and down with joy.

Suddenly, Tim's mom called him for dinner. Tim was so excited about the train that he forgot to be alert and tripped on a toy. He fell down and started to frown. His mom quickly came over to help him up and asked him what was wrong. Tim told her about the red train with the smiley face and how he had tripped because he was so excited. His mom smiled and said, "Don't worry, Tim. We can go see the train together tomorrow. Tim's frown turned into a big smile and he was happy again.

Once upon a time there were two friends, a fox and a rabbit. The fox always was naughty and wanted to do whatever he wanted. The rabbit was always kindly and helped whenever he could. On day, they went to the forest to explore it together. The fox saw a twig and wanted to pick it up, but the rabbit warned him not to because twigs have thorns that could hurts them.

But the fox was naughty and didn't listen. He picked it up and got a thorn stuck in his paw. He started crying and the rabbit hurried over to help him. The rabbit carefully removed the thorn and then cursed himself for not protecting his friend.

The fox learned a valuable lesson that day about why it's important to listen to others. The rabbit also learned a lesson about protecting his friend. They grew closer and remained friends. They also vowed to always look out for each other and to trust each other. The moral of the story is that it's important to protect your friends and to always listen to their advice.

Toby was a careless young boy. He loved to explore the world around him. Today he decided to wear his old red jacket and explore the countryside. He skipped over the hills and ran through the fields.

He soon came across a tall oat field. He watched as the oat swayed softly in the wind. With a smile on his face he decided to explore further. He followed the oat field until he came across a small stream.

Toby found a patch of grass by the stream and sat down. He heard the sound of water running and the oat softly swaying in the wind. He felt so peaceful and content. It felt like he could stay there forever.

Suddenly he could feel something tickling his head. He looked up and saw that hundreds of oat straws had fallen on him. He had been so careless that he hadn't noticed the oat straws falling from the trees.

Toby laughed and brushed the oat straws off his head. Then he jumped up and ran back home, his red jacket billowing in the wind. He had a wonderful day and he now knew the feeling of being surrounded by nature.

Mum and Dad took Alex to the beach. Alex was very excited and skipped around the sand. All of a sudden, he spotted something amazing. It was a big rock that sparkled in the sun. He was so excited that he wanted to lift it.

Mum said, "That's an amazing rock. Can you lift it?". Alex tried with all his might and eventually he managed to lift the rock. He laughed with joy and ran around the beach with it.

Dad said, "That's an amazing rock, Alex. Can you carry it to me?". Alex tried to carry the rock but it was too heavy. He struggled and worked hard but eventually he managed to lift it again.

Mum said, "Well done, Alex. You are so strong. You did an amazing job lifting that rock". Alex was so proud of himself and felt so strong. He held up the rock and said to Mum and Dad, "I did it!".

Tommy was very nervous when he went to the library with his mum. He had never been to a library before and had never seen so many books! His mum was nice and told him it was ok to be a bit scared. She said they could take some books home to read.

At the library they quickly found lots of books they liked. Tommy picked out one that was full of pictures and his mum chose one with lots of words. They both took them to the front desk to pay.

At home, Tommy was so excited about his new book. He and his mum read it together and he loved looking at all the pictures. From then on, Tommy and his mum went to the library every week. They always took home two books and spent lots of time reading them.

Tommy felt much less nervous at the library, and his mum was so proud of him!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack. He was three years old and loved playing outside. One day, Jack went out to the woods to explore. The sun was shining, and the mist was light.

Jack felt brave and said to himself: "I will go deep into the woods and find something cool!" So he went, and soon the mist was getting thicker. Suddenly he heard a strange noise. As he got closer, a strange creature appeared.

It was a monster! Jack was so scared he couldn't move. The monster growled and started to come closer. Jack shouted, "Stop! You are being stupid!" But the monster kept coming.

Jack knew he had to do something. He closed his eyes and screamed, "I surrender!" Suddenly the monster stopped and said, "Good. Now go away and never come back."

Jack opened his eyes and couldn't believe it. He had surrendered and survived! That day Jack learned an important lesson that he would never forget - always be brave and surrender if need be!

John and Jane were walking in the park. John pointed to a pet across the street and said, "Look, Jane! Over there!" Jane turned to gaze at the pet. It was a big, fat dog. Its fur was thick and fluffy.

John said, "It looks like I want to pet it!" Jane shook her head and said, "No! It's too wild, you better stay away!" But John didn't listen. He walked across the street and started to pet the dog.

John and Jane looked in amazement as the dog licked John's face. The dog was no longer wild - it was very friendly! John and Jane laughed and decided to take the pet home with them.

John had foreshadowed their pet from the beginning - he knew it was friendly and he wanted to pet it. The thick fur was a sign of its warmth and kind heart, and so the pet found its new home with John and Jane.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to swing on the playground. One day, she was swinging so high that she felt like she was flying!

Suddenly, she heard her friend Tommy calling her. "Lily, come over here!" Tommy said. "What's up?" Lily asked. "Look at my new earrings!" Tommy said, pointing to his ears.

Lily looked closely and saw the shiny earrings. "Wow, they look fine!" she said. "Can I try them on?"

Tommy took off his earrings and let Lily put them on. She swung back and forth while wearing the earrings, feeling like a princess. "Thanks, Tommy!" Lily said with a big smile.

Her mom gave her a big plate of food. The little girl was so excited; she decided to try eating it her new way. She started by using her fork and knife to cut the food into pieces. Then she used her spoon to get the pieces onto her plate.

Now the little girl knows how to enjoy meals in a very wise way. Eating was never so much fun!

Wanda was a wise old lady who lived in a small village. Every day she would wake up early and go out for a walk. She enjoyed walking around and seeing the sights. One day, she woke up and saw a cart parked outside her house. It had a big wheel and a wooden box on the top. She was curious, so she went up to take a closer look. She noticed a little girl sitting in the cart.

The little girl said hello to Wanda and asked if she could take the cart for a ride. Wanda said no, but the little girl was determined and kept asking her. Wanda was starting to get annoyed, but then she suddenly had a wise thought. She said it was alright if the little girl wanted to take the cart but reminded her that she would have to be careful.

The little girl thanked Wanda, jumped into the cart, and disappeared into the distance. Wanda smiled and watched her go. She had used her wisdom to turn the conflict into something positive.

Once upon a time, there was an ancient man. He would come down to the river to bring bananas for his family.

One day, as he was picking up some bananas, he heard a little voice.

"Can you bring me a banana too?" asked the little voice.

The old man looked around and, to his surprise, he saw a small child. He was three years old and was looking up at the ancient man with big bright eyes.

The old man kindly smiled and said, "Yes, I can bring you a banana."

The old man brought some bananas home to his family and he also shared one with the young child.

The old man taught the young child that it was important to be kind and generous to everyone, no matter how big or small they are.

The moral of the story is to remember to be kind and generous to everyone.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk. He saw a mysterious igloo in the snow. He was very curious and wanted to see what was inside.

As Tim got closer, he could hear voices coming from the igloo. He was a little scared, but he wanted to know who was talking. Tim peeked inside the igloo and saw two animals talking to each other. One was a big bear and the other was a small rabbit. They were arguing about who should live in the igloo.

"I found it first," said the bear. "I should live here."

"But I need a home too," said the rabbit. "It's very cold outside."

Tim thought for a moment, and then he had an idea. "Why don't you both live in the igloo?" he asked. "You can be warm and safe together."

The bear and the rabbit agreed, and they all became friends. From that day on, they lived in the mysterious igloo and had many fun adventures together.

"Butterfly! Hello!" the little girl shouted.
The butterfly replied, "Hello little girl, why are you here?"

The little girl smiled and said, "I am here to write a story. Can you help me?"

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play together. One day, they go to the lake with their moms. The lake is big and blue. It has fish and ducks.

Anna and Ben see a boat. The boat is red and white. It has a sail and a wheel. They want to ride the boat. They ask their moms.

"Can we ride the boat, please?" Anna says.

"Can we, can we?" Ben says.

Their moms look at the boat. They look at Anna and Ben. They nod.

"Okay, you can ride the boat. But be careful and stay close," Anna's mom says.

"Listen to the man who drives the boat. He is dependable. He knows the lake well," Ben's mom says.

Anna and Ben are happy. They run to the boat. They put on life jackets. They climb on the boat. The man who drives the boat smiles. He starts the boat. The boat moves on the lake.

Anna and Ben sit on the boat. They look at the water. They look at the sky. They see clouds and birds. They feel the wind and the sun. They are having fun.

"Look, a duck!" Anna points.

"Quack, quack!" Ben says.

They laugh. They wave to the duck. The duck swims away.

"Let's play a game," Anna says.

"What game?" Ben asks.

"Who can see more things on the lake. The one who sees more things wins," Anna says.

"Okay, let's play. I go first. I see a fish," Ben says.

"I see a flower," Anna says.

"I see a rock," Ben says.

"I see a leaf," Anna says.

They take turns. They see many things on the lake. They count them. They are both good at the game. They are both smart.

"Who wins?" Ben asks.

"I don't know. We see the same things," Anna says.

"Maybe we both win," Ben says.

"Maybe we do," Anna says.

They smile. They hug. They are both happy. They are both winners. They are both friends.

Sophie started to cry but then she heard a voice. It was coming from the swamp. It said, "What's the matter?"
Startled, Sophie turned around to see that it was a friendly old frog.
"I'm so sorry," she said. "I just wanted to explore the swamp, but I got all muddy."

The frog smiled and said, "You don't have to be sorry, it happens to a lot of explorers. Here, let me help you clean off." With a wave of his hand, he used his magic to make Sophie clean again.

"Oh thank you!" said Sophie. She thanked the frog several times, but the frog said, "No need to thank me, I was just being nice."

Sophie realized that he was being very selfless, when some of her other adventures had been selfish. She wanted to stay and thank the frog, but he jumped away into the swamp. "Good luck Sophie!" he said as he left.

Jack nodded, excited to show his mother he knew how to do it. He stood up and walked over to the door and carefully shut it.

Once upon a time there was a small boy called Jack. He had a neighbor called Bill. Jack and Bill didn't always get along as Bill could be quite grumpy.

One day, Jack had a great idea. He was going to have a competition with Bill to see who could win! All the kids on the street were invited to join in the challenge.

Jack and Bill faced each other and each of them was given an orange. The challenge was to see who could squeeze the most juice out of their orange the fastest.

The kids all cheered as Jack started to squeeze his orange with all of his might. Jack was squeezing so hard that the orange couldn't take it anymore and his orange exploded!

"Hooray!" all of the kids cheered. Jack was declared the winner and Bill had to grumble and accept defeat.

"Look Timmy, I found a bone!" Spot barked.

Timmy smiled and said, "Wow, Spot, that's a big bone. Can you bring it to me?"

But then, a big dog came running from the street. It barked loudly and jumped over the fence. It wanted to play with the toys, too. It knocked over the house and the car with its paws. It grabbed the robot with its teeth and shook it hard.

Lila and Ben were scared. They ran to their mom, who was sitting on the porch. "Mom, mom, help us! The dog is ruining our toys!" they cried.

Mom saw the dog and got up. She grabbed a broom and walked towards the backyard. She shouted at the dog, "Go away, you bad dog! Leave the toys alone!"

The dog heard mom and dropped the robot. It looked at mom and growled. It did not want to go away. It was a tough dog.

But mom was tougher. She waved the broom and hit the dog on the nose. The dog yelped and ran away. It jumped over the fence and disappeared.

Mom put down the broom and hugged Lila and Ben. "Don't worry, my darlings. I'm here to protect you. Are you okay?" she asked.

Lila and Ben nodded. They were happy that mom saved them. They thanked her and gave her a kiss. Then they went to check on their toys. The house and the car were broken, but the robot was still okay. They fixed it with some more screws and planks. They decided to play with it inside, where the dog could not find them. They were very smart.

Once upon a time there was a wide river that cut through the forest. On one side of the river, a family of rabbits lived peacefully. They had plenty of food to eat and were very happy.

One day, the rabbits were out gathering food when they heard a terrible noise coming from the river. They looked over and saw a large bear struggling in the water. The bear had become stuck in the river, and was in danger of drowning. The rabbits quickly ran to help.

The rabbits tried to pull the bear out, but they were too small. They needed a plan!

Just then, one of the rabbits had an idea. "Let's get a big pill and put it in the river," said the rabbit, "We can place the pill in between the bear and the river's edge. That way, it will prevent the bear from drifting away with the current!"

The other rabbits liked the idea, so they quickly found a pill and put it in the river. Sure enough, it worked! The pill was wide and strong enough to keep the bear in place, until someone larger could come and rescue it.

The rabbits were glad that they could help, and they were also proud of their quick thinking. This story reminds us that with a bit of creativity and cooperation, even the smallest of us can make a big difference.

Jack said, "That's a skeleton! It doesn't belong here. It must have been put here by someone."

Lucy said, "You're right. We shouldn't touch it. Let's leave it here and tell an adult."

Once, there was a boy. He was very frustrated because he wanted to draw but he had no chalk. The boy looked around, but he couldn't find any chalk. He asked his dad for some, but his dad didn't have any either. "What should I do?" thought the boy.

Suddenly, he had an idea. He took the tiny pieces of chalk that his little sister had been using, and put them in a cup. Then he put the cup in a pot of hot water. He stirred it quickly, and after a few minutes, he poured the water out of the pot.

The boy was very surprised. Instead of tiny pieces of chalk, he found that the pieces had shrunk! Now, he had enough chalk to draw whatever he wanted.

He was so happy! He thanked his dad and quickly started to draw. He spent the whole day creating his very own masterpiece.

The boy was very proud of himself. He had used his brain and solved a problem, all by himself. He felt very proud and happy.

Sam and Mia are playing in the park. They see a big hole in the ground. Sam wants to go near it, but Mia is scared.

"Come on, Mia, let's see what's in there!" Sam urges. He runs to the hole and looks inside.

"No, Sam, don't! It could be a trap!" Mia shouts. She follows him and tries to pull him back.

But it is too late. Sam slips and falls into the hole. He lands on something soft and fluffy. He hears a loud roar.

"Help! Help! There's a bear in here!" Sam cries. He is very afraid.

Mia hears him and runs to the hole. She sees a big, brown bear with a broken leg. It is lying on a pile of leaves and sticks. It looks sad and hurt.

"Sam, are you okay?" Mia calls. She is very brave.

"I'm okay, but the bear is angry!" Sam says. He is very sorry.

The bear growls and tries to get up, but it can't. It is stuck in the trap. It is very hungry.

"Maybe we can help the bear," Mia says. She has an idea.

She runs to the bench and gets her sandwich. She throws it into the hole. The bear smells it and eats it. It is very thankful.

"Thank you, little girl," the bear says. It is very friendly.

"You're welcome, Mr. Bear," Mia says. She is very happy.

"Can you help me get out of here?" Sam asks. He is very hopeful.

"Of course, little boy," the bear says. It is very gentle.

It lifts Sam with its paw and puts him on the edge of the hole. Sam climbs out and hugs Mia. They are very relieved.

"Thank you, Mr. Bear," Sam says. He is very grateful.

"You're welcome, little boy," the bear says. It is very proud.

"Can we be your friends?" Mia asks. She is very curious.

"Sure, you can be my friends," the bear says. It is very lonely.

"Yay, we have a new friend!" Sam and Mia say. They are very excited.

They wave goodbye to the bear and run to tell their mom. They are very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a rich man who lived in a big house with his wife. He loved his wife very much and always wanted to make her happy.

One day, the rich man went to the store and bought a beautiful necklace for his wife. He knew she would love it and he couldn't wait to give it to her.

When he got home, he showed his wife the necklace and she was very happy. She put it on right away and they hugged each other.

But later that day, they went for a walk and the necklace fell off without them noticing. They searched and searched but couldn't find it anywhere. The rich man was sad because he wanted his wife to have the necklace forever.

But then, a kind stranger found the necklace and attached it to a note that said "Found this beautiful necklace, hope it brings happiness to whoever it belongs to." The rich man and his wife were so happy and grateful to the stranger for returning the necklace.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat apples because they were so yummy. One day, she found an amazing apple in the garden. It was so big and red!

Lily decided to pray and thank God for the amazing apple. She closed her eyes and said, "Thank you, God, for this yummy apple. It's amazing!" She took a big bite and it tasted so good.

From that day on, Lily always prayed before eating her apples. She knew that God made them grow and wanted to say thank you. And every time she found an amazing apple, she knew it was a special gift from God.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved eating grapes so much that she would eat them every day. One day, she found a grape that was as big as a ball. She was so happy that she ate it all up in one bite.

But then something strange happened. Lily started to shrink! She became as small as a grape herself. She looked around and saw that everything around her was now very big. She felt scared and didn't know what to do.

Suddenly, she saw a fake flower nearby. She remembered that her mom had told her that it wasn't real. But Lily had an idea. She climbed onto the fake flower and used it to climb up to the table. From there, she was able to call out for help.

Her mom came running and saw Lily on the table. She picked her up and hugged her tightly. Lily learned that sometimes things can be big or small, real or fake, but what matters most is the love and care of those around us.

It was a warm summer day and Becky wanted pizza for lunch. She asked for her mom to buy it for her, but her mom said no.

Becky had to figure out a way to get the pizza herself. She paused for a moment to think and then an idea came to her.

She walked up to the house of a wealthy man. She knocked on the door, and when he answered, she asked if he could buy her a pizza. He smiled and said, "Yes!"

Becky ran back to her house with the pizza in hand. Her mom was surprised, but pleased. She said, "Well done!"

Becky smiled. She had figured out a way to get the pizza she wanted. It was a great day!

Once upon a time there was an adorable bunny. Bunny loved to hop and bounce around the spring. Every day the little bunny would hop and bounce around like he was flying.

One day bunny was bouncing around the spring when he saw a pretty flower. The bunny stopped and said, "Oh, hello flower! You are so pretty and I love that you have many colors."

The flower replied, "Thank you! It's nice to meet you, too."

The bunny and flower then hopped around the spring together. They jumped, bounced, and had lots of fun. Soon they said goodbye, but promised they would meet again soon.

The bunny hopped back home with a big smile on his face. From then one, the bunny bounced around the spring every day, hoping to meet up with the pretty flower. He was so happy to have made a friend.

Once upon a time, there was a powerful elephant called Emma. Emma had a very big trunk and she was admired by the other animals in the forest.

One day, Emma was walking through the forest and she found a skull lying on the ground. She was curious and wanted to pick it up.

Suddenly, a voice said "Please leave the skull alone." It was a small mouse who was living in the forest.

The elephant smiled kindly and said "OK. I will leave the skull". She left it there and continued her walk through the forest.

The other animals in the forest were amazed by Emma's actions. She had shown them that it was always important to please others and to show respect.

The moral of the story is: Always be kind and consider the feelings of others before taking any action.

Tom and Sue are twins. They like to play in their room. They have many toys and books. But they also like to peek at Mom and Dad's things.

One day, they see a big radio on the shelf. It has many buttons and lights. They want to touch it and hear the sounds. They think Mom and Dad are not home. They climb on the chair and reach for the radio.

But they are wrong. Mom and Dad are in the kitchen. They hear a loud noise from the radio. They run to the room and see Tom and Sue with the radio. They are angry and scared.

"Tom! Sue! What are you doing?" Mom shouts. "That is our radio. It is not a toy. You could break it or hurt yourself. That is very stupid and naughty."

Tom and Sue feel bad. They drop the radio and say sorry. They hug Mom and Dad and promise not to peek at their things again. Mom and Dad forgive them and tell them to be careful and listen. They put the radio back on the shelf and take Tom and Sue to the living room. They play with their toys and books and have fun. They do not peek at the radio anymore.

It was a cold day outside. Mama and Jonny stepped out for a walk. Mama said "Let's go! Time to walk!" Jonny beamed with excitement.

As they took steps, Mama noticed a big puddle on the way. "Oh, look Jonny! There's a puddle!". Jonny smiled and jumped with joy.

Mama asked him "Do you want to pour water on the puddle?" Jonny was thrilled to do it. He filled his little pail with water from a nearby pond and started to pour it on the puddle.

When the puddle was full, he was surprised to see frogs jumping out of it. The little frogs hopped around, enjoying the cold water. Jonny was so thrilled with the sight that he kept playing with the frogs until it was time to go home.

One day, a little boy named Tom went to play outside. He saw a black cat sitting near a big tree. The cat was looking up at the tree. Tom asked, "What are you looking at, black cat?"

The cat replied, "My toy is stuck in the tree. Can you help me get it?" Tom looked up and saw a red ball stuck in the tree. He wanted to help the cat, so he tried to climb the tree. As Tom climbed higher, he got close to the red ball. He reached out to grab it.

Just as Tom got the red ball, he lost his grip on the tree. He started to fall. The black cat was scared for Tom. But luckily, Tom landed on a soft pile of leaves. He was okay!

Tom gave the red ball to the black cat. The cat was very happy and thanked Tom. They became good friends and played together all day.

George was playing in the park when a tall man in a black coat grabbed his arm. George started to cry and the man said, "It's okay, I'm here to help you". George was scared and said, "Where are you taking me?"

The man said, "I'm taking you to jail". George became more scared. He asked, "What is jail?" The man said, "It's a hot place where bad people go".

George was very scared but he didn't stop crying. He was sure he was a good boy and he didn't want to go to jail.

Just then, George's mom showed up and asked the man, "What do you think you are doing?"

The man said, "I'm just trying to help him".

George's mom said, "Thank you, but it's time for George to come home." She grabbed his hand and they both went home. George was so relieved to be home and away from that hot jail.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sally who was almost three years old. She was starting to feel more and more nervous as bedtime was coming close. All she wanted was to have a good sleep, but she was very anxious.

So, in order to settle down better, Sally's parents decided to get her a special bedroom. When she saw it, she was thrilled. They painted all the walls in her favorite color and filled the room with her favorite stuffed animals.

Sally's parents, who were so happy to observe their daughter's contentment, tucked her in with a hug and a kiss. The bedroom was so cozy that it immediately made Sally feel calmer.

The little girl drifted off to sleep, dreaming of wonderful adventures and feeling much more relaxed. Hopefully, every night she can settle down in her bedroom with ease.

John held the tire tightly as he thought of his next plan. He was very thoughtful. He wanted to make something special.

Suddenly, his friends came running up to him. They were excited to play with him and the tire.

John showed the tire around to all of his friends. They thought it was really cool. They began to make a big, tall tower with the tire. The tire was so big, it nearly touched the sky!

John was happy that he found such a big tire. He held it carefully as his friends laughed and played. They had so much fun!

John made sure to hold the tire until it was time to go home. He was very thoughtful and he wanted to make sure it was safe. As he gave the tire to his mom, he knew he had a really fun day, thanks to the tire.

One day, Jack went to the zoo. He was so excited to see animals. He saw elephants, giraffes, and even a rhinoceros. It was so big; Jack just had to pick it! But he couldn't quite reach that high yet.

He asked his mom, "Mom, can I pick the rhinoceros?"

"No, Jack. That's too high for you. Maybe when you're a little older" his mom said.

Jack was disappointed but he was still excited to see the rest of the animals. While Jack was looking at the other animals, his mom ran up to him with a big surprise.

"Look, Jack! I know you wanted to pick the rhinoceros, so I found someone who is generous enough to let you do it!"

It was true! Jack was so happy that he could finally pick the rhinoceros. Using all his strength, he put both hands on it and lifted it up. What a feeling!

Jack's mom smiled at him and said, "See, Jack, I told you that if you were patient and kept your hope up, you'd get to pick the rhinoceros one day."

Mia and Leo were eager to play hide and seek in the garden. Mia counted to ten while Leo ran to find a good spot. He saw a big bush with white flowers and thought it was a perfect place to hide. He crawled under the bush and waited for Mia to find him.

Mia looked around the garden and tried to guess where Leo was. She saw the swing, the slide, the sandbox, but no Leo. She wondered if he was behind the tree or under the table. She decided to check the tree first. She walked slowly and quietly, hoping to scare Leo.

But when she reached the tree, she saw something that scared her instead. It was a big, round, white thing with green leaves. It looked like a giant cauliflower, but it had eyes and a mouth. It was a cauliflower monster!

The cauliflower monster saw Mia and opened its mouth. It said in a loud and scary voice, "Hello, little girl. Do you want to be my snack?"

Mia screamed and ran away. She was very afraid of the cauliflower monster. She wanted to find Leo and go back to the house.

Leo heard Mia scream and came out of his hiding spot. He saw the cauliflower monster and realized it was not a real monster. It was a puppet that his dad had made for a joke. He felt bad for scaring Mia. He ran after her and called her name.

"Mia, Mia, wait! It's not a real monster, it's a puppet! I'm sorry, I didn't know it was there. Please don't be mad at me!"

Mia stopped and turned around. She saw Leo holding the puppet and smiling. She realized it was a joke and felt silly for being scared. She smiled back and said, "It's okay, Leo. You didn't scare me, you just surprised me. But next time, don't hide behind a cauliflower. It's not a good spot."

"Yes, you can," he said.

Then Daisy began to zip up the hill. She was so fast! When she got to the top she was so proud of herself. She felt very grateful for her pick that had helped her zip up the hill.

Daisy looked around the hill and saw a lovely view. She was so happy and grinned from ear to ear.

She waved goodbye to her dad, zipped down the hill and continued on her adventure.

Once upon a time there was a bunny named Benny. Benny loved to hop so one day he decided to play with a hoop. He found a big hoop, an even bigger hoop, and all different sizes of hoops. Benny hopped over the small, medium, and big hoops which was lots of fun!

Next Benny tried something different. He spun the hoop as he hopped through it. Benny was so happy because it was even better than he imagined. He spun and hopped until he was too tired to play anymore.

He left the hoop outside and said goodbye. The next day, he said hello again and brought another friend. They both spun and hopped through the hoop and laughed and the whole day. It was a different kind of fun!

So Benny would always go to the same spot and hop and spin with his friends. He liked that everyone could play a different way and it was always a fun game.

Once upon a time, there was a great mommy who loved her daughter very much. Every day, the mommy would pray for her daughter to be happy and healthy, and kiss her little angel goodnight.

One night, it started to rain outside. The mommy took her daughter to the window to see the rainbow in the sky. "See that rainbow?" asked the mommy. "That's God's promise that everything will be okay."

The daughter smiled and said, "I'm going to pray for the rainbow, so it never goes away."

The mommy was so proud, and gave her daughter a big hug. "That's a great prayer," she said. "God will always listen to you, no matter how small you are."

The daughter smiled, feeling glad she could make her mommy happy. And ever since that day, she always says a prayer for the rainbow.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play outside and explore the world around him. One day, he found a caterpillar crawling on the ground.

Tim said, "Hello little caterpillar, what are you doing?"

The caterpillar replied, "I am trying to be a butterfly. I need to eat lots of leaves and grow big and thick."

Tim watched as the caterpillar ate and ate. Soon, the caterpillar grew an inch longer and Tim exclaimed, "Wow, you're getting bigger!"

The caterpillar smiled and said, "Yes, I am growing so I can be a beautiful butterfly one day." Tim was so happy to have made a new friend and couldn't wait to see the caterpillar transform into a butterfly.

Mia went to find her friend, Tom. She told him, "I found a big box under a tree. Can you help me grab it?" Tom was curious too. They both went to the tree and tried to grab the box together. With Tom's help, they could lift it.

They opened the mystery box and found many colorful balls inside. Mia and Tom were very happy. They played with the balls all day long, laughing and having fun. The curious friends enjoyed their mystery surprise.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly girl named Lucy. She was very curious and loved learning new things. One day, Lucy went to the park by herself. As she ran around, she stumbled upon a wise owl. The owl asked Lucy, "Do you want to learn a lesson?" Lucy nodded her head happily and the owl began to speak.

"If you're ever feeling lost, remember that wisdom will always be there for you. Just be reliable and never give up. Even if you lose something, you can always find it again if you try hard enough."

Lucy thought about the owl's words for a moment and smiled. She thanked the owl for the wise advice. From that day forward, she always kept the owl's words in mind. Whenever Lucy lost something, she remembered the owl's message and searched until she found it.

The wise owl smiled and flew away, leaving Lucy with a valuable lesson that she would never forget.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little dog named Max. Max loved to swing on the big tree in his yard. One day, Max's friend, a cat named Luna, came for a visit. They played together and had lots of fun.

Max and Luna found a bigger tree for the bird to swing on. The bird was so happy and thanked Max and Luna for being so clever. They all played together and had a great time. And from that day on, Max, Luna, and the bird became best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat ice cream with a spoon. But one day, she accidentally dropped her favorite spoon and it broke into tiny pieces. Lily felt sad and cried a lot.

Her mother came to her and said, "Don't worry, Lily. We can get you a new spoon." Lily felt a little better but still sad.

The next day, Lily's mother surprised her with a new spoon. Lily was so happy and hugged her mother tightly. Her mother said, "See, Lily, when something bad happens, we should always hope for something good to come out of it. And now, we have a new spoon and you can enjoy your ice cream again."

From that day on, Lily learned that even when things don't go as planned, we should always hope for something good and be grateful for what we have. And whenever she used her new spoon, she remembered the lesson her mother taught her and felt quiet and content.

Lily liked to feed her dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was Anna. Anna had long hair and a pink dress. Lily gave Anna some water and a cookie every day.

One day, Lily saw her mom's perfume on the dresser. It smelled nice and had a pretty bottle. Lily wanted to make Anna smell nice too. She took the perfume and sprayed it on Anna's hair and dress.

But Anna did not like the perfume. It made her hair sticky and her dress wet. It also made her nose hurt. She started to cry.

Lily's mom heard the cry and came to see what was wrong. She saw Lily holding Anna and the perfume bottle.

"Lily, what did you do?" she asked.

"I wanted to feed Anna perfume, Mommy. I thought she would like it," Lily said.

"No, Lily, perfume is not for feeding. Perfume is for smelling. You can feed Anna water and cookies, but not perfume. Perfume can make her sick and ruin her clothes. Do you understand?" Mommy said.

Lily nodded. She felt sorry for Anna. She hugged her and said, "I'm sorry, Anna. I won't feed you perfume again. I love you."

Mommy smiled and said, "That's good, Lily. I love you too. Now let's go wash Anna's hair and dress. And next time, ask me before you use my perfume, okay?"

Lily agreed. She learned that perfume is not for feeding, and that she should be careful with other people's things. She also learned that Anna loved her even when she made a mistake.

Tim was a boy who liked to look at small things with his microscope. He had a microscope that his dad gave him for his birthday. He liked to look at bugs, leaves, and sand with his microscope. He learned many things from his microscope.

One day, Tim went to his friend Anna's house to play. Anna also had a microscope, but it was bigger and newer than Tim's. Anna showed Tim her microscope and said, "Look, my microscope is better than yours. It can see more things and has more lights. Do you want to try it?"

Tim felt hurt by Anna's words. He thought Anna was being mean and rude. He said, "No, I don't want to try your microscope. My microscope is good enough for me. I don't need a bigger or newer one. I like my microscope."

Anna was surprised by Tim's answer. She said, "But why? Don't you want to see more things and learn more things? Don't you want to have fun with my microscope? It's not nice to refuse a friend's offer."

Tim said, "It's not nice to make fun of a friend's gift. My dad gave me my microscope and I love it. It's special to me. You should be happy with what you have and not brag about what you have. That's not how friends behave."

Anna realized that Tim was right. She felt sorry for hurting Tim's feelings. She said, "You're right, Tim. I'm sorry for being mean and rude. I didn't mean to hurt you. I was just excited to share my microscope with you. I'm happy that you love your microscope. Can we still be friends and play with our microscopes together?"

Tim smiled and said, "Yes, we can still be friends and play with our microscopes together. Maybe we can learn from each other and see different things. Thank you for saying sorry. I'm sorry for refusing your offer. Let's have fun with our microscopes."

The moral of the story is: Be respectful of other people's feelings and belongings. Don't judge or boast about what you have or don't have. Be grateful for what you have and share it with others. Friendship is more important than things.

The customer asked the nosy staff member to close the store, so she could buy something in peace. But the nosy staff member refused. He said it was against the store rules.

The customer was annoyed, so she walked away. But the nosy staff member followed her and kept asking her questions. In the end, the customer was so mad that she had to close the store herself! She was very happy when the store was finally closed and she could buy what she needed in peace.

Mia went outside with the chalk. She dared herself to draw a big picture on the ground. She drew a big sun, a house, and a tree. Mia was very happy with her picture. She felt proud that she could do it all by herself.
