One day, a big thief came to a small town. He wanted to take things that were not his. The people in the town were frustrated. They did not like the thief.

A little girl named Lily saw the thief. She had an idea to make him go away. She started to laugh very loud. The thief did not know why she was laughing.

Lily's laugh made everyone else laugh too. The thief felt silly and did not want to take things anymore. He left the town and never came back. The people were happy and thanked Lily for her big laugh.

Once there was a fish who loved to swim. Every day he would splash around and enjoy the water. But one day he noticed something strange. In the pond there was a mop! He had never seen anything like it before.

The fish swam closer to inspect the mop, wondering what it was doing there. He swam around and around, inspecting the mop from all angles. It was so different from everything he was used to.

He decided to take a closer look and decided to take a swim inside the mop. Inside, he found a world of twirling fibers and soft bristles. He enjoyed exploring this strange new place. After a while, he swam back out again and watched the mop as it floated around the pond.

He swam off, happy that he had seen something so different. He vowed to tell all his fellow fish about this strange adventure, and they were all amazed by it.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a rabbit who needed some food. He searched around for something to eat but he couldn't find anything. He was getting very hungry and started to feel sad.

Suddenly, he saw a zipper on the ground! He hopped over and opened it with his fuzzy little paws. Inside the zipper was lots of yummy food!

The rabbit was very excited! He gobbled up the food and the zipper stretched and stretched as he ate. It was very flexible!

The rabbit was full. He thanked the zipper and scurried away. The zipper lay there on the ground, happy to have fed the rabbit.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. She was happy and loved to play. One day, Sue wanted to play a game called hide-and-seek. She asked her mom if she could, but Sue's mom said no. She said it was too dark outside and she was scared.

Sue was very scared too. She felt scared when it was dark. So she decided to play a game inside the house. To make her mother happy, Sue chose a game that didn't need darkness.

She chose to play tag with her mom! Although Sue was frightened to play in the dark, she knew she would be safe playing inside the house. So Sue and her mom started to play. They laughed and chased each other around the house. It was so much fun!

When they were finished, Sue and her mom hugged each other. They were both happy that Sue had decided to play a game that made them both feel safe.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sarah who loved to sleep. Every night she would snuggle up in her bed, close her eyes and dream wonderful dreams.

One night was very different, however. Sarah was very sleepy but she couldn't seem to go to bed. She just kept looking out of her bedroom window at the dark night sky.

Suddenly her mom appeared in the doorway with a calendar in her hand. She said to Sarah, "It's time for bed, sweetheart. Look, you can see the crescent moon on the calendar!"

Sarah gasped as she spotted the crescent moon in the bottom corner of the calendar. She said to her mom, "I'm so sleepy now. I'll go to sleep now mom."

Her mom smiled, kissed her on the forehead and said, "Goodnight sweetheart."

Sarah closed her eyes and went to sleep. She slept soundly throughout the night and dreamt wonderful, wonderful dreams.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who lived in a nest. The bird was very weak and couldn't fly very high. One day, a big bird came and said, "I will throw you out of your nest if you don't give me all your food."

The little bird was scared and gave the big bird all his food. But the big bird wasn't happy and said, "I want your nest too!" The little bird cried and said, "But I have nowhere else to go!"

The big bird didn't care and threw the little bird out of his nest. The little bird fell and hurt himself very badly. He couldn't fly anymore and had to live on the ground. The little bird was very sad and missed his nest. The end.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with toys and draw pictures. One day, Lily draws a picture of a cat. She uses a black crayon and makes a big circle for the head, two small triangles for the ears, and a long tail. She shows her picture to Tom.

"Look, Tom, this is my cat. His name is Max. Do you like him?" Lily asks.

Tom looks at the picture and smiles. He thinks the cat is cute. He likes cats too. He has a cat at home. His cat is white and fluffy.

"Yes, Lily, I like your cat. He is very cute. Can I draw a picture of my cat too?" Tom asks.

Lily nods and gives Tom a white crayon. She helps him find a blank paper. Tom draws a picture of his cat. He makes a big circle for the head, two small triangles for the ears, and a long tail. He adds some dots for the eyes and nose, and some lines for the whiskers. He shows his picture to Lily.

"Look, Lily, this is my cat. Her name is Snowy. Do you like her?" Tom asks.

Lily looks at the picture and smiles. She thinks the cat is cute. She likes white and fluffy cats too. She has a teddy bear that is white and fluffy.

"Yes, Tom, I like your cat. She is very cute. Can I hug your cat?" Lily asks.

Tom nods and gives Lily his picture. Lily hugs the picture and pretends it is a real cat. She makes a purring sound. Tom hugs Lily's picture and pretends it is a real cat. He makes a meowing sound. They laugh and play with their pictures. They are happy. They are friends.

Once upon a time there was a very playful cube. Every day it would bounce and roll around the room. Sometimes it would even float in the air!

The cube was so full of energy that it never wanted to rest. Until one day it found a cozy corner and decided to take a nap.

It was so peaceful in the corner that soon the cube was fast asleep. It slept and slept for a whole day!

When it woke up, the cube was happy and full of energy once again. So it bounced around the room, playful and joyful.

But then it remembered how peaceful it was when it was resting and from then on it made sure to take regular naps, so it could play even more energetically.

Ben and Mia went to a pizza place with Mom and Dad. They liked pizza very much. They sat at a big table and looked at the menu. The menu had pictures of different pizzas.

"I want cheese pizza," Ben said.

"I want pepperoni pizza," Mia said.

Mom and Dad smiled and nodded. They told the waiter what they wanted.

The waiter brought them some water and bread. Ben and Mia were hungry. They wanted their pizza soon.

They saw the waiter bring pizza to another table. Ben and Mia tried to see what kind of pizza it was. They stretched their necks and leaned forward.

"Be careful," Mom said. "Don't spill your water."

But Ben and Mia did not listen. They wanted to see the pizza. They reached for the menu to look at the pictures again.

But the menu was too far. Ben and Mia reached too much. They knocked over their water. The water spilled on the table and on their clothes.

"Oh no!" Ben and Mia said.

Mom and Dad were not happy. They got some napkins and tried to clean up the mess.

"I'm sorry," Ben said.

"Me too," Mia said.

They felt sad. They did not mean to spill the water. They just wanted to see the pizza.

The waiter came back with their pizza. He saw the wet table and the wet clothes.

"Are you okay?" he asked.

Ben and Mia nodded. They were still sad.

The waiter smiled. He said, "Don't worry. Accidents happen. Here is your pizza. Enjoy!"

He put the pizza on the table. He gave them some extra napkins and some cheese.

Ben and Mia looked at the pizza. It smelled good. It looked good. It had cheese and pepperoni.

They smiled. They said, "Thank you!"

They forgot about the water. They ate their pizza. They were happy.

Anna and Ben were twins who liked to observe everything. They had a big book with pictures of animals, plants, rocks and stars. They asked their mom and dad many questions about the book. They were eager to learn new things.

One day, they felt the floor shake and heard a loud noise. They were scared and ran to their mom and dad. Mom and dad hugged them and said, "Don't worry, it's just an earthquake. It happens sometimes when the ground moves. We are safe inside our house."

Anna and Ben looked out the window and saw some cracks on the road and some broken branches on the trees. They also saw some people helping each other to clean up the mess. They asked their mom and dad, "Why do earthquakes happen? Why do people help each other?"

Mom and dad explained, "Earthquakes happen because the earth is made of big pieces that move sometimes. We don't know when or where they will happen, but we can be prepared and stay calm. People help each other because we are all part of the same community. We care for each other and share what we have. That's what makes us strong and kind."

Anna and Ben nodded and smiled. They learned something new and important. They said, "Thank you, mom and dad, for teaching us and protecting us. We love you and we love our community. We want to help too when we grow up."

Mom and dad kissed them and said, "We love you too, Anna and Ben. You are smart and brave. We are proud of you and your curiosity. You can help by being good and respectful to everyone. That's the best way to show your love and gratitude."

Charlie wanted to go outside and play. He saw something funny hopping in the grass. It was a spot! He was so excited he wanted to catch it. He started jumping, trying to catch the spot.

But it was too fast and he kept missing it. Charlie tried over and over. He even asked his mom to help but she couldn't catch it either. Finally, Charlie got really close and did a big jump, and caught the spot!

He was so happy and ran inside to show his mom. His mom said it was very funny and gave him a hug. Charlie vowed never to forget the fun he had trying to catch the spot.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She wanted a red waffle for breakfast, but her mom said they didn't have any. Lily felt sad and started to cry.

Her mom tried to make her feel better by offering her pancakes, but Lily still wanted a red waffle. She stomped her feet and yelled, "I want a red waffle!"

Her mom thought for a moment and then said, "I have an idea! Let's make our own red waffles!" Lily's face lit up with excitement and they went to the kitchen to make the waffles together.

They mixed the batter, added red food coloring, and cooked the waffles until they were crispy and delicious. Lily was so happy that she got her red waffle and she hugged her mom tightly. From that day on, they made red waffles every weekend and had lots of fun together.

Once there was a gentle bear. His name was Bo. Bo was very curious and every day he wanted to explore the world around him. One afternoon, Bo wanted to go on an adventure. He asked his mom if he could go outside. His mom told him that he could, but only if he was very careful. She was worried he might get hurt.

Bo agreed and his mom permitted him to go on his adventure. With a skip and a jump, Bo hopped down the path. He wondered what he might find. He heard a sound and looked to the side and saw a frog.

The frog said "hello, bear." and they began to chat. The frog said he had a delightful story to tell if Bo would like to listen. Bo said yes and the frog began to tell the most wonderful story. It was a happy story, full of smiles and laughter.

When Bo heard the end of the story, he hugged the frog goodbye and started his journey home. Bo was so excited to tell his mom the story!

His mom was happy to hear the gentle story and was so pleased that Bo was safe.

Peter was walking in the forest one day, looking for something special. Suddenly, he heard a noise. "Hello?" he said. "Who is there?"

From the trees, a tall figure appeared. He had a box in his hands.

"I'm here to deliver something special," the tall figure said. "It is a safe."

Peter was very excited. He asked, "What is inside?"

The figure replied, "It is something unknown. You will find out soon."

Then the figure handed Peter the box and disappeared into the forest. Peter opened the safe, and inside he found something wonderful. It was a mysterious surprise that made him feel very happy indeed.

Once upon a time there was a mom, a dad and a little girl. They had a nice house and the little girl loved to play in her room.

One day, the little girl was playing with her toys in her room when the mom and the dad said to her: "Be alert! Don't forget to look around, so you don't get hurt." The girl nodded and listened to her parents. She promised them to stay alert.

The little girl obeyed her parents and looked around her room. Suddenly, she noticed there was a broken toy on the floor. If she hadn't been alert, she could have stepped on the toy and gotten hurt.

The little girl was very happy to have been alert because she understood it was important to listen to her mom and dad. She learned that it was important to be alert and have a look around every time before taking a step.


The moral of the story is: Always be alert and listen to your parents!

Once upon a time there was a kind turtle who lived in a pond. Every day he liked to take a swim and it was lots of fun. One day he swam and swam and swam until his neck got a bit sore. He saw a zigzag path that lead from the pond and he decided to explore.

He started walking, and every time his feet touched the ground he said 'Break, break, break'. He had so much fun on his journey, and he smiled and laughed at each new thing.

Soon he came to a big patch of grass and he thought it looked so nice. He started walking around, but he couldn't find the end of the zigzag path. While he was looking around, he accidentally stepped on a twig and it broke.

He was a bit frightenend but then he remembered that he was a kind turtle. He said softly "It's alright. Everything will be ok". He gave a happy smile and kept on walking along his zigzag path until he found his way back to the pond.

He was so pleased with his adventure and he made sure to always stay kind. He returned to the pond and kept on swimming, and he never forgot about the fun he had on his zigzag journey.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a village with her mommy and daddy. One day, Lily went to the park to play with her friends. But when she got there, she realized that she forgot her favorite toy at home. She felt nervous and sad.

Lily's friend, Tommy, saw that she was upset and asked her what was wrong. Lily told him that she forgot her toy at home and she missed it. Tommy said, "Don't worry, we can play together and have fun!"

Lily felt better and they played together. They ran, jumped, and laughed. Lily realized that she didn't need her toy to have fun with her friends. She was happy in the village with her friends and family.

Once upon a time, there was a big green swamp. It was full of slimy frogs and squishy bugs. One day, a little girl came to the swamp with a big bowl. She wanted to mix some yummy things in the bowl.

She looked around the swamp and found some juicy berries and sweet honey. She put them in the bowl and started to mix them together. The berries turned red and the honey got sticky.

Suddenly, a frog hopped over to her and said, "That smells yummy!" The little girl smiled and offered the frog a taste. He licked his lips and said, "Mmm, that's delicious!" From that day on, the little girl came back to the swamp to mix more yummy things and share them with her new frog friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to run and play all day long. But when it was time for bed, he would get very restless. He would toss and turn and couldn't fall asleep.

One night, Tim's mom came into his room and saw that he was still awake. She told him to try and rest and that she would be back soon. Tim tried to close his eyes and relax, but he couldn't stop thinking about all the fun things he did that day.

Suddenly, he heard a loud snap! He looked down and saw that his sock had come off. He reached down to put it back on and felt much more comfortable. He closed his eyes again and before he knew it, he was fast asleep. From that day on, Tim made sure his socks were snug before going to bed.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. He loved walking in the park with his mom. As they walked, they saw ducks swimming in the pond. The boy wanted to play with them. He whistled loudly to try to get their attention. He waved his arms and jumped up and down.

The ducks heard the boy whistling. They swam towards him. He was so excited! He loved watching them swim. Suddenly, he saw an acorn on the ground. It was a big, wide acorn. He picked it up and whistled. The ducks got closer and closer.

The boy threw the acorn in the pond. The ducks followed the acorn and quickly ate it up. The boy was so happy! He loved watching the ducks in the park. He and his mom walked away, whistling as they went.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Lucy. One day Lucy dared to go into her mum's office. When she saw it for the first time, it looked so big. But Lucy was brave and took a step inside. Everything in the office was so neat and tidy, it was like a palace.

On the desk Lucy noticed a gentle brown bear. She was so excited and carefully hugged it. She was so gentle that the bear never moved. Lucy looked around the office and tried to find something else exciting to do. She saw a big chair and dared herself to sit on it. She closed her eyes and imagined it was a giant throne.

She was feeling so brave that before she knew it, she had explored the whole office. Lucy was so proud of what she had done and so excited to tell everyone about her adventures in the office.

Lily and Sam were going to visit their grandma. They packed their bags and waited for the taxi. They were very excited to see grandma and her big garden.

"Taxi is here!" Sam shouted. He ran to the door and opened it. Lily followed him with her bag. The taxi driver helped them put their bags in the trunk.

"Hello, kids. Where are you going?" the taxi driver asked.

"We are going to grandma's house. She lives far away, in the country. She has a lot of flowers and animals. We love grandma!" Lily said.

"That sounds nice. I hope you have a good time. Do you want to listen to some music?" the taxi driver offered.

"Yes, please!" Sam said. He liked music. He hoped the taxi driver had some songs he knew.

The taxi driver turned on the radio. He found a station that played children's songs. Lily and Sam sang along. They had fun in the taxi.

The taxi driver smiled. He liked children. They were happy and loud. He was used to driving in the city, where it was noisy and busy. He enjoyed the quiet of the country. He saw green fields and blue sky.

"Look, there is grandma's house!" Lily pointed. She saw a red roof and a white fence. She saw grandma waving at them.

"Hello, grandma!" Lily and Sam shouted. They jumped out of the taxi and ran to hug her.

"Hello, my darlings. I'm so glad you're here. Come in, I have a surprise for you." Grandma said.

"What is it, grandma?" Lily and Sam asked.

"You'll see. It's something that includes all your favorite things." Grandma said. She winked at the taxi driver.

The taxi driver took out their bags and gave them to grandma. He thanked her for the tip and waved goodbye.

"Have a wonderful time, kids. And thank you for the music." He said.

He drove away, feeling happy. He liked his job. He met different people and saw different places. He wondered what surprise grandma had for Lily and Sam. He hoped it was something fun.

Bob liked to jog. He liked to jog in the park every day. He jogged around the big trees and around the lake. He liked to jog so much that he even jogged in his house.

One day, when he was jogging in his house, he saw a new TV. He ran over to look at it. It was a very good TV. It had lots of colors. He wanted it!

So Bob kept jogging. After a few days, he had jogged enough to earn the TV. He was so excited.

He ran to his mom and showed her the TV. She said he could keep it because he had jogged so much. Bob was very happy.

He went back to his house, put in the new TV and watched it. He was very happy with his good TV.

One morning, a boy woke up early. He looked around the room and saw a magazine on the floor. He picked it up and looked at all of the pictures. They were so colourful and interesting. He liked flipping through the pages.

The magazine was so useful. It had all different kinds of stories, pictures, and games. He loved looking through it. The boy decided to take the magazine with him finding it hard to part with it

He took the magazine to his bedroom and looked through it some more. He read and looked at the pictures and played some of the games. He really enjoyed it. The magazine was so much fun to use.

The boy spent all morning looking at the magazine. He was having so much fun learning and playing with it. He thought it was the most useful and fun thing ever!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mom asked her to help with the dishes. Lily was happy to help, but she was too weak to carry the big dish.

Lily's mom said, "Don't worry, you can rub the dish with this sponge."

Lily took the sponge and started to rub the dish. She rubbed and rubbed until the dish was clean.

Lily's mom was proud of her and said, "You did a great job, Lily! Now, let's put the dish away."

Lily smiled and said, "Okay, Mommy!" and they put the clean dish away together.

Tom and Lily were playing with their cardboard castle. They had made it with their dad, who was very good at cutting and sticking. They liked to pretend they were a king and a queen, and they had a lot of fun.

But one day, their dog Spot ran into the castle and knocked it over. He was very excited and wanted to play, but he did not know he was breaking their castle. Tom and Lily were very sad. They cried and shouted at Spot. "Bad dog! Go away! You ruined our castle!"

Spot felt sorry and wagged his tail. He did not mean to make them unhappy. He licked their faces and tried to cheer them up. But Tom and Lily were still angry. They did not want to play with him.

Their dad heard the noise and came to see what was wrong. He saw the broken castle and the sad children. He hugged them and said, "Don't worry, my loves. We can repair the castle. It will be as good as new. Do you want to help me?"

Tom and Lily nodded. They wiped their tears and smiled. They liked to help their dad. He gave them some more cardboard, some tape, and some scissors. They worked together to fix the castle. They made it even bigger and better than before. They added some windows, some flags, and some flowers.

When they were done, they were very proud of their castle. They thanked their dad and gave him a big hug. They invited Spot to join them. They said, "We are sorry, Spot. We know you did not mean to hurt our castle. You are our friend. Do you want to play with us?"

Spot barked happily. He was glad they were not mad at him. He ran into the castle and wagged his tail. He pretended he was a guard dog, and he protected the king and the queen. Tom and Lily laughed and played with him. They were a jolly family. They had a lot of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emma. She was three years old and she loved to imagine things. Every day she would dream of things far away.

One day, Emma imagined she saw a black bird in the sky. It was so pale, she thought it was sleeping.

Emma asked her mom, "Mommy, what is that pale black bird in the sky?"

Her mom smiled and said, "That is a hawk. It is a very special kind of bird.

Emma imagined that someday she could be as special as a hawk and fly away.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy liked to go for walks with his mommy. One day, Timmy and his mommy went for a walk in the park. It was warm outside, and the sun was shining. Timmy felt happy.

As they walked, Timmy saw a pretty flower. He wanted to take it home with him. "Mommy, can we take this flower?" he asked. His mommy said yes, and Timmy felt excited. He carefully picked the flower and held it in his hand.

After their walk, Timmy and his mommy went back home. Timmy put the flower in a vase and showed it to his daddy. "Look what we took from the park!" he said. His daddy smiled and said it was beautiful. Timmy felt proud of himself.

Once upon a time there was a small pup. It had four furry paws and its fur was a lovely golden color. It liked to explore the world around it.

Every morning the pup went for a walk in the park. It observed the birds chirping and the trees swaying. Sometimes, it even saw different kinds of animals.

On one of its walks, the pup spotted a strange creature. It was small, but had a long tail and bigger ears than normal. The pup liked it and decided to follow it.

It watched as the creature hopped from one side of the park to the other, using its different-looking paws. After a while, the pup decided to go back home.

It was happy to have observed something new and different that day. It trotted happily back, dreaming of more exciting discoveries it could make.

Once upon a time there was a dragon. He lived in a deep, dark cave. One day, he had an adventure. He flew out of the cave and into the sky. The dragon flew so high that he could see the world below.

The dragon had something special he wanted to bring back to his cave. He flew over the ocean and in the deep blue water he spotted a big, shiny treasure. He swooped down to get it and with all his might, he brought the treasure back to his deep cave.

The dragon was so happy with his new treasure. Day by day he showed it off to his dragon friends and they all thought it was so cool. From that day on, the dragon felt even more special. His deep cave was now full of happy memories and his very own shiny treasure.

Jimmy was playing outside with his friends when they found a big rock. They were excited and quickly put it in the middle of the lawn. They all tried to balance it up so it wouldn't wobble. Jimmy was very organized and tried to see if he could make the rock stay upright on just one spike. It took some trial and error, but he eventually figured it out and the rock was balanced! His friends cheered and celebrated, jumping around and giving Jimmy high-fives. The rock was balanced, and everyone was happy!

Once upon a time, there was a man named Jack. Jack had an axe to chop trees. One day, Jack's axe broke and he couldn't chop trees anymore. Jack was sad because he loved to chop trees. He went to his friend Jill and asked her to help him fix his axe. Jill was fair and kind, so she agreed to help.

Jill took the axe and looked at it carefully. She saw that the handle was broken. She got some wood and fixed the handle. Jack was happy because his axe was fixed. He thanked Jill and they went to chop some trees together. They had a fun day and Jack was happy to have his axe back. From that day on, Jack knew that he could always count on Jill to help him fix things.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved playing with his toys, but he always wanted more. One day, he saw a shiny toy in the store. It was very expensive, but Timmy wanted it so much that he begged his mom to buy it for him. His mom said no, but Timmy kept urging her to buy it.

Finally, Timmy's mom bought him the expensive toy. Timmy was so happy and he played with it all day long. But the next day, Timmy's little sister accidentally broke the toy. Timmy was very upset and cried a lot. He realized that victory doesn't always mean having the most expensive things.

The moral of the story is that it's important to be grateful for what you have and not always want more. Timmy learned his lesson and started to appreciate his other toys more. But he still wished he had listened to his mom and saved his money for something more special.

One day, Johnny took his case and went for a jog in the park. It was a hot day and he was getting tired so he looked for a shady spot to rest. He found a big, wide tree and put his case down beneath it. Johnny lay down to take a nap, but he was soon woken up by a loud noise. It was a grumpy old man who shouted, "This is my spot! You can't stay here!"

Confused, Johnny sat up and asked, "Why not?"

The old man said, "Because this is where I always sit, I don't want strangers here! Now go away and find somewhere else to rest."

Johnny felt angry. He wanted to stay in the cool shade of the big wide tree. He stood up and shouted, "No! I will not go. This is a free park and everyone can stay here."

Seeing how brave Johnny was, the old man softened. He chuckled and said, "Fine, you can stay. I'm too old to argue anymore."

Glad that he got to stay, Johnny smiled. He picked up his case and laid back down for a nice rest beneath the big, wide tree.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a man with a big shiny thing that made loud noises. He was playing jazz, he said.

"What is jazz?" Lily asked.

"It is a kind of music that makes you feel happy and free. You can play jazz with many different things, like this saxophone, or a piano, or a drum," the man said.

He showed them how he blew into the saxophone and moved his fingers to make different sounds. Lily and Ben clapped and danced to the music.

"Can we try?" Ben asked.

"Sure, but be careful. It is not easy to play jazz. You have to listen and improvise," the man said.

He gave them the saxophone and helped them hold it. Lily tried to blow into it, but only made a squeaky noise. Ben tried to press the buttons, but they were too hard. They both laughed.

"It is fun, but hard," Lily said.

"Yes, it is. But you can learn if you practice and love jazz," the man said.

He took back the saxophone and played a song for them. It was fast and smooth and had many colors. Lily and Ben wondered how he could do that. They liked jazz.

They thanked the man and said goodbye. They ran back to their mom, who was waiting for them on a bench. She had a big orange balloon for them.

"Look, mom, we learned about jazz today!" Lily said.

"Wow, that sounds exciting. What is jazz?" mom asked.

Lily and Ben tried to explain, but they could not find the right words. They decided to show her instead. They took the balloon and pretended it was a saxophone. They blew into it and pressed it and made funny noises. They smiled and danced and had fun. Mom smiled too.

They were playing jazz.

Once upon a time there was a gifted boy. His name was Jack and he was three years old. He had a very special problem to solve.

One day, Jack said "I'm in trouble, what can I do?". His mom knew just what to do. She said to him, "I know! Let's mail something to Daddy".

Jack was very excited and went to get his favorite toy. He took it to the post office and asked the postman to mail it to his dad.

The postman was very kind. He said, "I'm sure your dad will love it". So Jack thanked him and smiled.

When Daddy received his package, he was delighted! He called Jack to thank him. Jack was so happy that he had been able to help his dad, even from far away.

It was all thanks to the postman and Jack's quick thinking that the trouble was solved.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Savannah. She was three years old and loved to make things.

One day, Savannah was feeling brave. She grabbed a mixer from the kitchen and poured some water into it. She flipped on the switch, and the mixer started to mix.

"I bet this will shrink me," she said, hopping into the mixer.

Suddenly, the water started to bubble and splash about. Savannah began to shrink!

The bubbling water was wet and hot. Savannah quickly jumped out of the mixer and scampered to the living room.

"Why was I brave enough to hop into the mixer?" She asked herself.

The next day, Savannah took a big breath and stepped into the mixer again. This time, the bubbling water made her shrink even more!

"It's a good thing I kept my courage," Savannah said, smiling.

Savannah stepped out of the mixer and waved her hands in the air. She was tiny now, and the experiment worked!

She'd been brave enough to shrink herself with a mixer and wet water.

Once upon a time, there was a silver tiger. The tiger was very fancy and everyone was scared when it roared. One day, a little girl was walking past and heard the tiger's roar. She was very brave and decided to go and see the tiger. When she got there, the tiger was not scary at all. It was making funny noises and the little girl laughed. The tiger also seemed to like her, so she petted its shiny silver fur. From that day on, the tiger and the girl were friends and the tiger no longer roared. Instead, it purred and the little girl called it her special pet.

Amy saw a very unusual pond. It looked like it went on forever! She wanted to dive in and explore it. Amy ran over and jumped in. The water was so cold and felt amazing! She swam around, looking at all the fish and plants in the pond.

Suddenly, she saw something purple and shiny near the bottom of the pond. She swam down to get a closer look. It was a beautiful purple shell! She grabbed it and held it tight in her hand.

Amy was so excited that she decided to stay in the pond a little longer. She dove through the water for what felt like forever. Finally, she climbed out, exhausted but happy. She ran home with her special purple shell.

Once upon a time, there was a regular gem. It was very special and sparkled brightly. One day, a girl saw the gem and wanted to look at it more closely. She went to examine it.

The girl looked very carefully at the gem. She saw how amazing it was and how the light bounced off it. It was so pretty. The gem glowed and the girl felt very happy.

The girl kept the gem safe for a long time. She showed it to her friends and family, and everyone admired the gem. They thought it was beautiful and special.

Every day, the girl would examine the gem. She couldn't help but look at it and feel happy. The girl was so glad that she found the regular gem. She was going to keep it forever.

Timmy and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to swing and slide and run. They saw a big tree with a hole. They wanted to see what was inside.

"Maybe there is a squirrel or a mouse," Timmy said.

"Or a fairy or a treasure," Lily said.

They walked to the tree and looked in the hole. They saw two big eyes and a beak. It was an owl. The owl was not happy. It made a loud noise and flew out of the hole. It scratched Timmy's face with its claws. Timmy cried.

"Ow, ow, ow! It hurts! It hurts!" Timmy said.

Lily was scared. She hugged Timmy and tried to help him. She saw some blood on his face. She ran to get their mom.

"Mom, mom, come quick! An owl hurt Timmy!" Lily said.

Mom heard Lily and ran to them. She saw Timmy's face and took him in her arms. She cleaned his wound and put a band-aid on it. She kissed him and hugged him.

"It's okay, Timmy. You are brave. The owl was scared too. It didn't mean to hurt you," Mom said.

Timmy stopped crying. He looked at the owl. The owl was sitting on a branch. It looked at Timmy. Timmy was angry.

"I hate you, owl! You are mean and bad!" Timmy said.

The owl blinked and tilted its head. It made a soft noise and flew away. Timmy watched it go. He felt a little sorry.

"Maybe the owl was just protecting its home. Maybe it didn't hate me," Timmy said.

Mom smiled and nodded. She took Timmy and Lily by the hand and walked to the car.

"Let's go home and have some ice cream. That will make you feel better," Mom said.

Timmy and Lily agreed. They liked ice cream. They forgot about the owl. They were happy.

Jenny was out walking with her mom one day. She saw something ahead on the path - it was a big, furry animal! She was so excited! She asked her mom, "What is it?" Her mom said, "It's a bear!" Jenny was scared; she felt a bit fearful. But her mom said, "Don't worry - this bear will not hurt us. We just need to keep away from him. Keep walking on the trail and be careful not to get too close."

So Jenny and her mom kept walking on the trail. They looked back every now and again to make sure the bear wasn't following them. Soon, the bear disappeared from sight and Jenny was no longer fearful. She felt happy and excited as she continued down the trail with her mom.

Once upon a time there was a girl who wanted to build an object. She looked around and saw lots of things she could use, like blocks, stones and sticks. But the girl was too lazy to really make something. She was trying to think of a way to make something without having to work very hard.

Suddenly, she had an idea. She grabbed a big stick and some stones and got to work. She stuck the bigger stones on the stick, and then she added some smaller ones. She kept going until she had made a beautiful object.

When she finished, the object was so special and it looked so nice in the sun. The girl was so happy that she was able to make something all by herself. She had been lazy, but in the end, that didn't matter. She had done it!

Once there was a girl named Helena. She was dependable and kind, like a dragonfly. Her best friend was a lion from the local zoo.

One day, Helena and the lion decided to go for a walk. They had planned to visit a hotel, but the lion was too scared.

Helena tried to encourage the lion to come with her. She promised he would be fine if he stayed close to her. But the lion just wouldn't listen.

So Helena went to the hotel by herself. But the lion got mad and ran after her. When the lion caught up to Helena, he slapped her!

Helena was so sad. She knew the lion was just being scared, but she was still hurt. She wasn't sure what to do, until the hotel staff came running to help. They sent the lion back to the zoo and helped Helena back to her house.

Helena was grateful for the hotel staff. They were dependable and kind, like a dragonfly. She knew she would be safe with them.

One day, Timmy and his mommy were walking in the woods. It was a very gloomy day, but Timmy still wanted to explore. Mommy warned him to be careful, but he was too curious to listen. He ran ahead, climbing up and down the hills.

Suddenly, a creek appeared in front of him. But he noticed a hot, fast-moving steam floating on the surface. It was very hot, but Timmy had to cross the creek. He was scared, but he put his brave face on and did it. He carefully followed the stream of hot air, and he found his way to the other side.

When Timmy made it to safety, his mommy was there. She hugged him tightly and said, "You have been so brave today." She was so proud of him.

From then on, Timmy never stopped exploring the woods. Whenever he would come across a hot, gloomy day, he would always remember his brave journey and smile.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a special toy, a small doll named Daisy. Lily loved to play with Daisy all day long. One day, Lily found a magic thread. She was very excited.

Lily said to Daisy, "Let's play with this thread! I wonder what it can do." So, they started to play with the thread. Suddenly, Daisy began to shrink! Lily was scared and did not know what to do. She tried to pull the thread to make Daisy big again, but it did not work.

Lily was persistent. She did not give up. She asked her mom for help. Her mom told her, "Sometimes, we need to be patient and think before we play with new things." Lily thought about it and decided to be patient. She waited and waited. After a while, Daisy grew back to her normal size.

Lily was so happy to have her friend back. She learned the importance of being patient and thinking before playing with new things. From that day on, Lily always made sure to be careful with her toys and to ask for help when she needed it. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Cristy. She was three years old and loved to color. Every day, Cristy would take out her coloring desk and color all her favorite pictures. Her favorite color was orange, so she always looked for an orange crayon first.

Today was different, though, because Cristy felt very scared. She was about to start a new school and she didn't know anyone there. She sat at her coloring desk, feeling scared, when her mom walked in.

"It's OK, Cristy" her mom said. "I'm here to encourage you. You'll make lots of friends and have lots of fun at your new school."

Cristy smiled a bit. Her mom was so nice to always encourage her. She picked up an orange crayon and started to color. She knew she'd make lots of friends at her new school and soon she was already picking out her favorite dress to wear the next day.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Katrina. She was playing with her toys when she saw something unusual.

Right in the middle of her room, there was a switch. It looked really old and strange. Katrina was curious and wanted to play with it.

She walked over and asked her mom, "Mommy, what's this switch?
Her mom smiled and said, "It's an old switch. It will help us separate the lights in this room.

Katrina looked at the switch and said, "Why does it look so unusual?
Her mom replied, "It's special. Other switches don't look like this one.

Katrina got excited and put her hand on the switch. Her mom quickly said, "No darling, don't touch it. It's very special.

Katrina listened and said, "OK, mommy. I won't touch it.

And so, Katrina and her mom had a good time playing and talking about the strange switch.

Maggie wanted a monkey. Every day, she asked her mom for a monkey. Her mom said she couldn't have one so Maggie stayed sad.

One day, Maggie saw something in the sky. It was a white monkey! It had big wings and was so high up.

Maggie wanted the monkey so much. She waved her arms at it and shouted. But the monkey stayed high in the sky.

Then Maggie had an idea. She ran inside to get a colorful balloon. She tied the balloon to a long string, and waved it in the air. The white monkey saw it and came flying down.

Maggie was so happy! She ran to hug the monkey and it tickled her with its furry fur. Maggie and her white monkey played together all day, and they were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved playing with blocks. She had a big pile of blocks in her room that she would build with every day. One day, Lily's little brother came into her room and knocked down her block tower. Lily got very upset and said, "Bad brother! You made me suffer! My blocks are all messed up now."

Her brother looked sad and said, "Sorry Lily. Can I help you build a new tower?" Lily forgave her brother and they built a new tower together. They had so much fun playing with the blocks that they forgot all about the bad thing that happened before. From that day on, Lily and her brother played together with the blocks every day and never fought again.

There was a pretty barn in the countryside. Inside it was a little puppy and a scissor. The pup was napping when the scissor went tiptoeing around it. "Hey little pup," said the scissor. "Wake up! I want to cut something pretty."
The pup's eyes shot open. "What do you want to cut?" it said.
The scissor waved its blade. "I want to cut some of this hay in the barn. I'll make it look really pretty!"
The pup didn't know what to do. Cut the hay? The pup thought it was a funny idea. "All right," it said.
So the pup and the scissor played and cut the hay together. Little pieces of hay floated about the barn and it looked really pretty!
The pup and the scissor were both tired after their fun. They snuggled together and fell asleep. The pup and the scissor were happy in that pretty barn.

Once there was a little boy. He was playing in the garden when he saw a dove. He was so excited that he started to rush towards the dove. But before he could touch it, the dove flew away! The little boy was very confused. He ran to his mother to tell her what he saw. His mother told him that it was a dove and that it was in a hurry to get somewhere. He was still very confused, but he decided to rush around the garden until he found the dove again. After a few minutes, he spotted the dove in the sky. He was so happy he jumped up and shouted in delight. His mother was very proud of him for spotting the dove again.

Ben and Lily were going on a trip with their mom and dad. They were very excited to fly on a big plane and see a new place. They packed their bags and went to the airport.

When they got on the plane, Ben and Lily looked out the window and saw the clouds and the sky. They felt like they were flying too. They asked their mom and dad many questions about the trip. "Where are we going?" "How long will it take?" "What will we do there?"

Mom and dad smiled and answered their questions. They told them they were going to a city with many buildings and people. They said it would take a few hours to get there. They said they would stay in a hotel and visit some parks and museums.

Ben and Lily were happy and curious. They wanted to see everything. They played with their toys and read some books on the plane. They also ate some snacks and drank some juice.

But then, something bad happened. Lily felt sick in her tummy. She told her mom she needed to go to the bathroom. Mom took her by the hand and walked to the back of the plane. But before they got there, Lily threw up on the floor. She felt very bad and ashamed. She started to cry.

Mom hugged her and said, "It's okay, Lily. It's not your fault. Sometimes people get sick on planes. It's normal. Don't worry. We will clean it up and you will feel better soon."

Lily nodded and wiped her tears. She said, "I'm sorry, Mom. I'm sorry, Ben. I'm sorry, everyone."

Mom said, "It's okay, Lily. Everyone understands. Ben is not mad at you. He loves you. And so do I. And so does Dad. And so do the people on the plane. They are nice and kind. They will help us."

Lily smiled a little and said, "Thank you, Mom. I love you too. And I love Ben. And I love Dad. And I love the people on the plane. They are nice and kind."

Mom and Lily went to the bathroom and cleaned up. Then they went back to their seats. Ben gave Lily a hug and a kiss. He said, "I'm glad you're okay, Lily. You're my best sister. I can't wait to see the hotel with you. It will be fun."

Lily said, "Thank you, Ben. You're my best brother. I can't wait to see the hotel with you too. It will be fun."

Mom and Dad smiled and said, "We're proud of you, Ben and Lily. You're brave and sweet. We love you very much. We're almost there. Are you ready to see the city?"

Ben and Lily said, "Yes, we are. We're ready to see the city. We're ready to have fun. We're ready to fly."

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run, jump and slide on the grass and the swings. They saw a large tree with many leaves and branches. They wanted to discover what was behind the tree.

They ran to the tree and looked behind it. They saw a big box with a red bow on it. They wondered what was inside the box. They opened the box and found many toys and books. They were very happy and excited.

"Wow, look at all these things!" Lily said. "We can play with them and read them!"

"Yes, this is so fun!" Ben said. "But who left this box here? Maybe it belongs to someone else."

They looked around and saw a sign on the tree. It said: "This box is for the children of the park. Please share and enjoy. Happy day!"

Lily and Ben smiled. They realized that someone was very kind and generous. They decided to share the toys and books with the other children in the park. They also said thank you to the person who left the box.

They learned that it is good to share and be grateful. They had a wonderful day with their new friends and their new things. They also made a card with a bow and put it on the tree. They wrote: "Thank you for the gift. You are very nice. We love you."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Jane. Jane was very sad. She didn't know why but it made her feel very heavy in her heart. She wanted the sad feeling to go away.

One day, Jane decided to go for a walk in the park. It was a beautiful sunny day and she wanted to see the animals and play in the sunshine.

But when she got to the park, Jane saw that all the animals were scattering away in fear. The birds flew up over the trees, the squirrels hid in the leaves and the rabbits ran off into the bushes. Jane was very surprised and wondered why the animals were scared.

Then Jane saw what was happening. A little girl was crying in the park and her pain was making the animals scatter away. Jane felt so sad for her but she also knew that she could help.

Jane held out her hand and told the little girl not to cry. She said that she would help her and that together they would make the pain go away.

The little girl smiled, wiped away her tears and took Jane's hand. Together they walked away from the park. Soon the animal's stopped scattering and the sun was shining brighter than ever. Jane and the little girl were happy to have helped each other and they both knew that the sad feeling was gone.

Once upon a time, there was a big factory that made yummy candy. The candy was so good that everyone wanted to eat it. One day, the factory stopped making candy and the people were sad. They wanted their candy back!

So, a little girl named Lily decided to help. She went to the factory and rang the doorbell. But no one answered. She rang it again, but still no one came. Lily was determined to help, so she climbed over the fence and went inside.

Inside the factory, Lily saw that the machines had stopped working. She knew she had to fix them. She pressed some buttons and pulled some levers, and soon the machines started working again. The candy started coming out, and everyone was happy again. Lily was a hero!

Once upon a time there were two friends, Sam and Sarah. Sam found a red pocket in his room. Sarah asked what was in the pocket, but Sam said it was a secret. Sarah was jealous.

"Please let me see! asked Sarah. But Sam just shook his head.

"It's too special, said Sam. He put the pocket in his pocket and flew away.

Sarah was still jealous. She wanted to know what was in the pocket. She chased after him, shaking her fists at him.

But no matter how far she chased, Sam was too fast.

The end.

Mama was very competitive. When her friends asked her to do something, she always offered the best solution.

One day, Mama was playing outdoors with her friends. Her friends asked, "How can we play?" Mama offered to play tag. Everyone was excited and they started running around and having fun.

Mama kept playing with her friends until it was time to go home. When they went back home, Mama saw a surprise. Her mother had cooked dinner and was ready to serve it. Mama was so happy and thanked her mother for offering the yummy dinner.

Everyone enjoyed the dinner. Mama was happy they were able to have such a great time. After dinner, her friends offered to help her clean up.

Mama thanked them for their wonderful offer. With all the help, it was no time before the kitchen was sparkling clean.

Mama was proud of her mother for being so competitive.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia was very hungry. She wanted to find something yummy to eat. Mia walked outside to look for food.

As Mia walked, she saw a beautiful rainbow in the sky. The colors of the rainbow started to change. The colors became brighter and prettier. Mia was so happy to see the rainbow change.

Mia followed the rainbow and found a big tree with lots of fruit. She climbed the tree and picked the fruit. Mia ate the fruit and was not hungry anymore. She sat under the tree and watched the pretty rainbow in the sky.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to make houses with twigs and leaves. They found a long twig that was good for the roof. Lily said, "This is mine. I found it first." Ben said, "No, it is mine. I saw it first." They both pulled the long twig and it broke in half.

Lily and Ben were sad and angry. They did not want to share the long twig. They did not want to play together anymore. They sat on the grass and looked away from each other.

A squirrel came by and saw the broken twig. He needed a twig for his nest. He picked up one half and ran away. Lily and Ben did not notice. Then a bird came by and saw the other half. He needed a twig for his nest too. He picked up the other half and flew away. Lily and Ben did not notice.

Lily and Ben looked up and saw that the long twig was gone. They looked around and saw the squirrel and the bird with their halves. They realized that they had been selfish and silly. They said, "We are sorry. We should have shared the long twig. We should have played nicely together." They hugged and said, "Let's find another twig and replace the roof. Let's make a bigger house and invite the squirrel and the bird to join us."

Lily and Ben learned that sharing is caring and that friends are more important than things. They had fun playing with the twigs and the animals. They were happy and proud of their house.

Once upon a time, there was a tall giraffe named Jerry. Jerry was very happy because he had strong muscles that helped him run fast and reach high leaves to eat.

One day, Jerry's friend the monkey lost his banana. The monkey searched high and low but couldn't find it. Jerry offered to help and started to search too.

They looked in the trees, on the ground, and even in the river. After a while, they found the banana hiding behind a tall rock. The monkey was very happy and thanked Jerry for his help.

Jerry felt proud of his strong muscles that helped him search for the lost banana. From that day on, Jerry and the monkey were the best of friends and searched for lost things together.

One day, Mary was in the park playing with her friends. They were running and laughing and having a lot of fun. Suddenly, Mary saw something blinking across the park. It was at the end of a row of trees.

She walked over to it and saw it was a funny looking bird! It had a blue head, a yellow body and pink legs. Every time it blinked its eyes, it made the biggest, brightest light. It was amazing!

Mary reached out to touch it and it flew away. She watched it fly across the park, and it started blinking again at the end of the row of trees. She tried to follow, but it was too fast!

Mary had so much fun watching the funny blinking bird. She was sad it flew away, but maybe one day she'd see it again.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Jack. He was very excited that he had been given permission to fly his kite. Jack was feeling very happy.

He went to the park with his kite. Jack looked up at the sky and saw it was very blue. He looked at the trees around him and they were very tall.

He started to get his kite ready. But as he was winding the string, he saw a gust of wind coming. He held onto his kite tightly and waited.

When the wind had passed he began to fly his kite. It soared high in to the sky. Jack smiled as he watched it go higher and higher. It was a beautiful sight.

Suddenly, Jack heard a voice coming from nearby.

"That kite is going very high in the sky," the voice said.

Jack looked around and saw an old man standing next to him. The man asked if Jack would permit him to fly the kite too. Jack smiled and nodded yes.

He gave the old man the string and they both watched the kite soar higher and higher into the sky. Jack was happy he was able to share the joy of flying a kite with someone else.

Once upon a time, there was a big, bright and beautiful True. True was so nice and friendly and always happy, but one day True got very sad. True's best friend, who was very ignorant, had not been very kind to True. True felt alone and lost.

Then True saw Big Hug. Big Hug was a very kind and gentle friend. Big Hug snuggled close to True and hugged True very tight. True felt safe and loved. Big Hug taught True that even when someone is ignorant, True should stay kind and true to themselves.

Big Hug stayed with True, and they were very happy together. True and Big Hug hugged each other all the time, forever and always.

The end!

John was feeling very miserable. He had a big bruise on his arm and was really struggling to move it.

John saw a melon near him, so he tried to rub the bruise. It felt better - the melon was really cool and John could feel the bruise getting better.

As John kept rubbing the melon, his arm started to feel less sore. He felt so happy he decided to take the melon home with him.

John kept rubbing the melon until he got home. When he arrived, his arm was all better and he was no longer feeling miserable.

From that day on, John took the melon with him wherever he went. He found that rubbing it really helped his bruises.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lucy. She was three years old and loved to explore.
One day, she heard a loud van outside her house. She ran to the window and saw that the van was a bright blue color. She was very excited and wanted to explore the van.

Lucy ran outside and to her surprise, the driver was her uncle. Her uncle smiled and said, "Hi Lucy! Would you like to come with me for a ride?"
Lucy nodded and hopped into the van.

The van went fast and Lucy felt the wind blowing in her hair. Soon, they arrived at a park. Lucy could see a lot of people with big tools cutting the grass. She stared for a moment, mesmerized at all the loud noises.

Her uncle caught her attention and said, "Lucy why don't you help me cut some of this grass too?"

Lucy eagerly grabbed a toy tool and started to cut. She was very proud of all the work she had done and couldn't wait to show her parents.

At the end of the day, they drove back home and Lucy said goodbye to her uncle. She ran inside and showed her parents her toy tool and explained how she had helped cut the grass.

Her parents smiled, proud of the work she had done.

One day, a sleepy pigeon was walking around in the park. Suddenly, it saw a pile of rotting apples. The pigeon hurried over and pecked at the apples. "Yum!" it said.

At that moment, a little girl walking by noticed the pigeon. She was only three years old. She said "Hello, Pigeon. Those apples look yummy!"

The pigeon smiled. But it looked a bit down. "I'm so tired after eating all these apples," it groaned. "I wish I could take a nap."

The little girl smiled. She said, "Why don't you rest here in the park? I'll watch over you and make sure nothing bad happens."

The pigeon was delighted. It snuggled up against the girl's feet and fell fast asleep. The little girl stayed and watched over her sleepy friend until the sun went down.

Once upon a time, there was a small sun up in the sky. It smiled at everyone and made them feel happy. But the small sun was sad because it was not very bright.

One day a brave little boy named Joey wanted to make the small sun shine brighter. He tried to think of a way he could help it.

Then he had an idea. He grabbed a cloth and some special stuff called polish. He rubbed the cloth on the small sun until it sparkled and shone. Everyone in the land was so happy to see the sun shining so brightly.

From then on, Joey came back every day and polished the small sun with love. It would shine so brightly that it made everyone smile with joy.

And that is the story of how a brave little boy made the small sun shine.

At the pond, she could see all the ducks and geese swimming. She smiled and watched them gracefully swimming around. The ducks reminded her of the funny cartoons in her book.

So she took out her book and started to carry it around to show the ducks and geese. When they saw the pictures, the ducks and geese started to laugh and play. Jane smiled and felt so happy that she started to skip around the pond with her cartoon book.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the sun. One day, she found a big, juicy apple on a tree. She picked it and took a big bite. It was so delicious! She drank some water from a nearby stream to wash it down.

As she wandered through the forest, she saw a naughty monkey swinging from palm trees. The monkey was making a lot of noise and throwing coconuts around. Lily thought it was funny, but she knew she shouldn't be as naughty as the monkey.

Suddenly, she heard her mommy calling her for dinner. She ran back home and told her mommy about her adventure in the forest. She showed her the apple and told her about the naughty monkey. Her mommy was happy that Lily had a fun day, but reminded her to always be good and not to take things from strangers. They sat down at the table and enjoyed a delicious meal together.

Once upon a time, there was a rough jungle. It was full of tall trees and bright flowers. Animals all around made it come alive.

One day, a little girl named Julia went deep into the jungle. She was very brave. She wanted to explore.

As she went she had to watch out for thorns. She had to mind where she stepped. It was often very muddy.

As she went further and further the jungle was getting thicker. The trees were closer together and there were more bushes. It began to get really dark. Julia was starting to feel a bit scared.

Then, all of a sudden, she heard an animal sound. She looked up and saw a beautiful parrot. The parrot said, "Don't be afraid, mind your way and you'll be out soon."

Julia followed the parrot's advice. Sure enough, after some time, she found herself out of the rough jungle. She felt so happy and relieved. She had made it!
