"Look at my new vest!" Baby Bear said.

One of Baby Bear's friends said, "It looks so cosy!"

Another friend said, "Let me try it on!"

Baby Bear said, "No, that's my vest!"

His friend laughed and said, "I'm just playing, I won't bite it!"

Baby Bear laughed too and said, "Yes, you can try it on if you like."

So his friend slipped into the soft vest and said, "It's so warm and comfy!"

Baby Bear was very happy with his vest and said, "Let's go for a walk now!"

His friends agreed and all three bears went for a walk, wearing their soft vests.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. It was a hot day, and Tim wanted to play in the water. He heard other kids laughing and splashing in the water.

"Mom, can I play in the water too?" Tim asked. His mom looked around and saw a sign with the park's rules. "The law says no playing in the water, Tim. We have to follow the rules," she said.

Tim was sad, but he knew it was important to follow the law. Instead, he played on the swings and had fun with his mom. They had a great day at the park, even without playing in the water.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a flute. She loved to play her flute every day. One day, Sue wanted to find a box to keep her flute safe.

Sue looked for a box that her flute would fit in. She found a big box, but her flute was too small for it. Then she found a tiny box, but her flute was too big for it. Finally, she found a box that was just right. Her flute fit in it perfectly.

Sue's friend, Tom, came over to play. He saw the box and asked, "What's in the box, Sue?" Sue said, "My flute is in there. I found a box that fits it just right." Tom said, "That's great! Can I try to play your flute?" Sue said, "Yes, but you must be honest and careful with it."

Tom was honest and careful with Sue's flute. They took turns playing the flute and had lots of fun. Sue was happy that she found a box that fit her flute, and she was glad to have an honest friend like Tom.

Lily was a big girl who liked to do things by herself. She was independent and did not need help from anyone. She liked to climb on the sofa, the bed, and the chairs. She liked to reach for the things that were high up and see what they were.

One day, she saw a shiny bottle on her mommy's dresser. It was pink and had a flower on it. Lily wanted to see what it was. She climbed on the chair and then on the dresser. She grabbed the bottle and opened it. It smelled nice, like roses and candy. Lily liked the smell. She put some on her neck and her wrists. She felt pretty and happy.

"Look, mommy, I have perfume!" she said, running to her mommy in the living room.

Mommy looked at Lily and smiled. She smelled the perfume and recognized it. It was her special perfume that she only wore on special days.

"That's very nice, Lily, but that's mommy's perfume. You can't use it without asking. It's not for little girls. It's for grown-ups." she said gently.

Lily felt sad. She did not know that she did something wrong. She thought mommy would be proud of her for being independent and finding the perfume.

"I'm sorry, mommy. I just wanted to smell like you. You smell so good." she said, hugging mommy.

Mommy hugged Lily back and kissed her. She understood that Lily was curious and wanted to be like her.

"It's okay, Lily. I'm not mad at you. You are very sweet and smart. But you have to be careful when you climb on things. You could fall and hurt yourself. And you have to ask before you use something that is not yours. Do you understand?" she said.

Lily nodded. She understood. She said sorry again and gave mommy the bottle.

"Can I have some perfume when I'm a grown-up?" she asked.

"Of course, Lily. When you're a grown-up, you can have your own perfume. But for now, you don't need it. You already smell lovely, just like a flower." mommy said.

Lily smiled. She was happy again. She loved mommy and mommy loved her. She did not need perfume to be pretty and happy. She was already pretty and happy, just the way she was.

Dave and Sally were two best friends who loved to play. One day, they decided to go to the stable to play with the horses.

When they arrived, they were amazed by how incredible it looked. They couldn't believe their eyes. There were so many horses of all sizes! Dave and Sally ran and skipped their way to the horses.

When they reached the stables, the horses were so happy to see them. One brown horse even neighed in delight. Dave and Sally patted the horses and fed the horses carrots. They laughed and giggled as the horses nibbled away.

The two friends spent the day playing with the horses. They were so happy and had an incredible time. When it was time to go home, they couldn't believe the day was already over. But they promised to come back to the stable soon.

One day little Tom had an exam. He was very scared. He was afraid he would not do well. Then he had an idea! He decided he would try something original.

Tom took out a sack. He filled it with all kinds of things. He put rocks, feathers, sticks and shells in the sack.

The day of the exam came. Tom opened his sack and small things flew everywhere. Everyone in the room was surprised.

In the end, Tom passed his exam! His original idea worked. He was so happy! Everyone was impressed with his creativity.

From then on, Tom was not scared of exams anymore. He was ready to try something original every time.

Bobby was three years old and loved napping. His favorite thing to do was cuddle up with his mom and nap in the sunshine. One day, Bobby was napping in the garden when he saw a rainbow. He got up to go look at it and ran over to the rainbow. He wanted to get to the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow, but it was too high up.

Bobby thought of a plan. He asked his mom if he could have the ladder from the garage. She was hesitant, but let him have it. Bobby climbed up the ladder and tried to reach for the pot of gold but it was too far away.

Suddenly, Bobby heard a voice. It was his little brother who said, "Bobby, don't be selfish. Share the rainbow with me too. Please, let me up the ladder to see it." Bobby thought about it, and then decided to share with his brother. He let him up the ladder and they both marveled at the colors of the rainbow together.

Once upon a time, there was a dog named Spot. Spot had a pretty collar. One day, Spot saw a ball. The ball was fast. Spot wanted to catch the ball.

Spot ran and ran. He tried to catch the ball. "Woof, woof!" said Spot. But the ball was too fast for Spot. Spot tried to jump, but he could not catch the ball. Then, Spot began to fall.

As Spot fell, his collar got stuck on a tree. "Woof, woof!" Spot called for help. A girl named Sue heard Spot. Sue came to help. "Don't worry, Spot," said Sue. She helped Spot get his collar off the tree.

Spot was happy. He wagged his tail. "Thank you, Sue," said Spot. From that day on, Spot and Sue were best friends. They played with the fast ball together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a long mark on the ground. It was a nice sunny day and Mark decided to take a nap there. He laid down and closed his eyes, and he was soon fast asleep.

As Mark was napping, a bird flew down from the sky and perched on a nearby branch. It chirped and sang a sweet little song, and Mark even smiled in his sleep.

After a long nap, Mark finally opened his tired eyes. He stretched out his arms and legs, and rubbed his eyes with his fists. He knelt down and touched the long mark with his fingers. He smiled and remembered the nice nap he had taken there.

Lily and Ben liked to play at the pond. They liked to see the ducks and feed them bread. One day, they saw a big duck with a red hat. It looked funny and friendly.

"Hello, duck," Lily said. "Do you want some bread?"

The duck nodded and came closer. It took the bread from Lily's hand and ate it. Then it looked at Ben.

"Hello, duck," Ben said. "Do you want more bread?"

The duck nodded and came closer. It took the bread from Ben's hand and ate it. Then it opened its mouth wide and bit Ben's arm.

"Ow!" Ben cried. "Help! The duck is hurting me!"

Lily was scared. She ran to get help. She saw a man and a woman near the pond. They were Ben's mom and dad.

"Help!" Lily shouted. "The duck is hurting Ben!"

The man and the woman ran to the pond. They saw the duck with the red hat. It was not a duck. It was a crocodile. It had Ben in its mouth. It was dragging him into the water.

"Ben!" the man and the woman screamed. "Let him go!"

But the crocodile did not let go. It disappeared into the water with Ben. The pond was empty. There was no Ben. There was no duck. There was only a red hat floating on the surface.

Lily, the man and the woman cried. They were very sad. They had lost Ben. The crocodile had taken him away. The end.

One day, Mark and his mommy went to an airport. Mark was very curious and wanted to understand what an airport was. When they arrived, lots of people were busy running around catching their flights and hauling their suitcases. Mark was mesmerized.

His mommy smiled and said: "It's a big airport, Mark. Everyone here is busy getting ready for their trip. You see, an airport is a place where people come and go."

"But why are the people rushing around mommy?" asked Mark.

His mommy could tell he was curious and said: "Well, some people are very lazy and like to leave things to the last minute and that's why they are in a rush. Some people are just punctual and want to make sure they don't miss their flights."

Mark nodded as he understood what was going on and his mommy was proud that he grasped it so quickly. With that, Mark went on to explore the airport and soon, he was able to recognize the travelers who were punctual versus those who were lazy.

Once upon a time there was a bird who wanted to explore the world. She stood on the rooftop of her nest and looked around. Everywhere there was something new to see! She spread her wings and soared higher and higher in the sky.

She could see the tall mountains and green forests in the distance. She flew up, up, up until she reached the top of the tallest mountain. From here she could see as far as the eye could see. She even spotted a beautiful lake far away.

She started to fly slowly across the lake and soon she spotted a tray on the ground. She flew down to take a closer look and saw that it was filled with the most delicious food. She tucked in and enjoyed her lunch in the bright sunshine.

When the bird was full she could feel the wind on her wings. She spread them wide and soared higher and higher in the sky. Up and up she went until she was in the clouds. She flew around, enjoying the view until she was as high as she could go.

The bird flew back down towards her nest and as she neared she could see her family waiting for her. Finally home, she settled in her nest ready for another day of adventure!

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lisa. One day, Lisa was feeling very thirsty, so she went to get a cup of water. She filled her cup, and then poured it over the top of her head.

"Why are you doing that?" her mom asked.
Lisa smiled and said, "I'm making a reef!"

Her mom laughed and said, "That's a strange way of making a reef!"

Lisa went out looking for treasures. She found a shiny shell and a large rock. She lifted the rock and saw something disgusting underneath. "Eww!" she shouted in surprise.

Lisa put everything back and then poured more water on the ground to make an even bigger reef. After it was finished, she yelled, "It's perfect!"

The end.

Rose and her dad were in the garage. Rose was very upset because she had to leave soon.

"I don't want to leave, Daddy," Rose said.

"I know, but we have to go," her dad said. "Look, I'll bring you something special when I come back."

Rose smiled but tears still ran down her cheeks. Her dad hugged her tight and then he put her in the car. Rose waved goodbye as they drove away.

When her dad came back, he had a big surprise. He opened the garage door and a puppy was sitting there! Rose's eyes lit up and she hugged the puppy tight. She knew it was time to leave, but this time she was excited!

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved her loyal dog, Spot, very much. Spot always followed Lily wherever she went. One day, Lily put on her favorite dress and went to the park with Spot. They played and had fun, but then it was time to go home.

Lily told Spot to stay by the bench while she went to get her bike. But when she came back, Spot was gone! Lily was sad and scared. She looked all around the park, but Spot was nowhere to be found.

Just when Lily thought she would never see Spot again, she heard a bark. She followed the sound and saw Spot running towards her with a dress in his mouth! It was Lily's dress that she had left behind on the bench. Spot had been loyal and stayed with the dress until Lily came back. Lily was so happy to have her dress and her loyal dog back with her. From that day on, Lily made sure to never leave Spot behind again.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was a very happy dog, but he was also very filthy. He loved to play in the mud and roll around in the dirt. One day, Spot found a big box. He thought it would be fun to see if he could fit inside it.

Spot jumped into the box and found that he fit just right. He was so happy that he wagged his tail and barked with joy. But then, Spot noticed the weight of the box. It was very heavy! He thought it would be fun to push the box around the yard and see what was inside.

Spot pushed the box all around the yard, but the weight of the box made him very tired. He decided to take a break and lay down in the sun. As he lay there, Spot's owner came outside and saw the filthy dog and the heavy box. She laughed and said, "Spot, you are such a silly dog!" She opened the box and found a big bag of dog food inside. Spot wagged his tail, happy with his new discovery, and his owner gave him a big hug.

Once there was a little girl named Sue. She was only three years old and always liked to explore and find new things. One day when she was playing, she found some sunglasses in the grass. She picked them up and realize how useful they could be.

Sue tried on the sunglasses and they were the perfect size. She liked how they made everything look yellowish, like the sun was shining brighter. She was so excited that she went running around to show her parents her new discovery.

They recognized the sunglasses as an old pair that had been lost years ago. They were so surprised to see Sue with them, and even more impressed with how useful she thought they were.

From that day, Sue always wore her sunglasses when she went outside. They made her feel special, like she had discovered something amazing. She never stopped finding new things that made her day brighter.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty swan. The swan swam in the lake every day. One day, the swan saw something bad in the water. It was trying to prevent it from coming close. It flapped its wings very fast and splashed the water. The bad thing went away and all was safe in the lake again.

The swan was so proud of itself. It had used its mighty wings to protect the lake. It then flew away, leaving the lake safe and happy.

The end.

"Oh no!" cried Jack. He had just come out of the store, and realized he had forgotten something very important. He had to deliver it in time, but he could not remember what it was.

He tried to remember, but everything he thought of was wrong. Then he shouted, "MUSTACHE!"

Jack remembered he was supposed to deliver a strong mustache to his dad! He raced back inside the store and grabbed the item, then ran out quickly.

He managed to get back to his dad's house just in time. Jack quickly delivered the mustache and his dad thanked him.

"Thank goodness you remembered! You make a great delivery boy! I'm so happy you remembered," he said with a smile.

Jack felt proud and strong for delivering the mustache on time. He knew he had to be quick and remember what he was supposed to do. From now on, he was going to always remember in the future!

On Tuesday, Sam and Lily went to the park with Mom. They saw a big pond with many fish. The fish had different colors: red, blue, yellow, green. Sam and Lily liked to watch the fish swim and splash.

"Look, Lily, that fish is glowing!" Sam said, pointing to a fish that had a bright light on its head. "It is so pretty!"

Lily nodded. She wanted to touch the glowing fish. She leaned over the edge of the pond and reached out her hand. But the fish was too deep. Lily could not reach it. She slipped and fell into the water.

"Help, help!" Lily cried. She was scared and wet. The water was cold and dark.

Mom heard Lily's cry and ran to the pond. She grabbed Lily's hand and pulled her out of the water. She hugged Lily and wrapped her in a towel.

"Are you okay, Lily?" Mom asked, worried.

Lily nodded. She was okay, but she was still shivering. She looked at the glowing fish and said, "Sorry, fish. I just wanted to say hello."

The fish blinked its light and swam away. Sam and Lily smiled. They learned a lesson. They should not try to touch the fish. They should just watch them and enjoy their glow.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to climb trees. He climbed trees every day. One day, Timmy saw a lovely gun on the ground. He picked it up and played with it. Timmy didn't know that guns are dangerous.

Timmy's mom told him to never touch guns. But Timmy forgot. He climbed his favorite tree with the gun in his hand. Suddenly, the gun went off and Timmy fell out of the tree. Timmy's mom found him and took him to the hospital. Timmy learned a valuable lesson that day. Guns are not toys and can hurt people.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who was very lonely. She had no friends or family, so she would wander around a loop in the park by herself. As she was walking she would listen to the birds and admire the trees.

At the end of the loop, she saw a little puppy, playing on its own. She wanted to talk to the puppy so that she wouldn't be so lonely. The puppy saw her and walked over. She asked the puppy, "What's your name? In reply, the puppy barked. She asked again "Where do you live? The puppy barked again.

The little girl smiled. She knew that the puppy was her new friend. She bent down and said "Let's go on a walk. The puppy barked happily and they looped back around the park together.

The little girl was no longer lonely. She had found a friend in the puppy and was very happy. From then on, they would walk around the loop every day, together.

Once upon a time, there was a bad man. He carried a cross and hated to share. One day, he was mad and decided to burn the cross. The cross started to burn and it made the bad man sad. He realised that he had done a bad thing.

The bad man started to cry. He had made a mistake and now he felt bad. He was sorry for burning the cross.

Suddenly, a kind fairy appeared. She saw what had happened and said that it was okay. She waved her wand and suddenly the cross was whole again. The bad man was so happy. He thanked the kind fairy and promised he would never burn the cross again.

The bad man now shares and is always kind to everyone. He never forgets what the kind fairy did for him and is always grateful.

Max stared at the huge tree in front of him. He had never seen anything so tall and grand. He couldn't help but feel small and insignificant. Max began to explore the grounds in front of him. He spotted some eyes watching him. A squirrel had been staring at Max with inquisitive eyes, seemingly asking him why he had come to play in this unfamiliar place. Max smiled and waved, not wishing to be ignorant of the creatures around him.

The squirrel hopped away and Max continued to explore. He stumbled upon a group of children playing tag. Max's eyes lit up and he ran towards them. The children stopped running and stared at the new kid. Max looked back and forth between the children and the squirrel, not knowing what to do. After a moment of uncertainty, the kids invited Max to join the game and show them how to play.

Max enjoyed the game and soon forgot about the staring squirrel. He and the kids ran around the park, pretending to be super heroes and villains. They laughed and joked, having the time of their lives.

Max smiled as he wandered home later that evening and thought of the game he had played. He knew that, even when things seem strange and unfamiliar, it never hurts to give things a try.

Once upon a time, there was a happy otter who lived in a river. The otter loved to swim and dive in the water. One day, a deaf rabbit came to the river and asked the otter for help. The rabbit said, "I can't find my carrot. Can you help me find it?"

The otter replied, "Sure, I can help you find your carrot. Let's dive into the water and look for it together." So, the otter and the rabbit dove into the water and searched for the carrot. But, they couldn't find it anywhere.

Suddenly, a big bird swooped down and snatched the rabbit away. The otter was sad and couldn't believe what had happened. The otter realized that sometimes bad things happen and there's nothing we can do to stop them. From that day on, the otter swam alone and missed his friend, the rabbit.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. He was nervous because he had to go to the dentist, but his mom said it was important to take care of his teeth. When they got to the dentist's office, Timmy saw a picture of a cowboy on the wall. The cowboy had a big gun and Timmy said, "I want to shoot a gun like that!"

His mom said, "Guns are dangerous and we don't play with them. Let's focus on taking care of your teeth." Timmy sat in the dentist's chair and the dentist looked at his face. He said, "Timmy, you have a beautiful smile. Let's make sure it stays that way." Timmy felt better and he let the dentist clean his teeth. When they were done, the dentist gave Timmy a sticker that said "Great job!" and Timmy felt proud of himself.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved cookies more than anything in the world. One day, Timmy's mom baked a big batch of cookies and gave him one to eat. Timmy was so happy and took a big bite of the cookie.

After eating his cookie, Timmy started to feel tired. He went to his room and lay down on his bed. But he couldn't sleep because he still had some energy left. So, he decided to kick his ball around the room.

Timmy kicked the ball so hard that it hit the wall and bounced back to him. He kicked it again and again until he was too tired to play anymore. Then he snuggled up with his favorite teddy bear and fell asleep, dreaming of cookies and kicking his ball. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sarah. She loved to do lots of fun things. One day, Sarah heard a noise coming from the kitchen. She hurried to see what was happening and saw her mommy shaking a big calendar.

"Mommy, why are you shaking the calendar?" Sarah asked.

Mommy smiled and said, "I'm shaking the calendar because it's time to turn the page and start a new month."

Sarah was very excited. She helped her mommy flip the page to reveal a brand new month.

"It's very quiet in here," Sarah said.

Mommy nodded. "Yes, it is quiet. It's the beginning of a new month and time to start afresh. Let's go have some fun."

They quickly changed into their warm sweaters and snuck out of the house. As they walked, Sarah noticed all the trees shaking in the wind and she smiled.

Then he went outside and found a big open space. He kicked the rag around and ran after it. He felt happy and it made him forget that he was sad.

The little boy continued to play with his rag until it was time to go home. He put his rag in his pocket and went home with a smile on his face. He was no longer sad.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She loved playing with her toys and dressing up in her mum's shoes. One day Lucy decided to try on a pretty pair of leather shoes. She walked around in them, feeling very happy as they were so much nicer than her other shoes.

Just then, Lucy heard her mum's voice. "Lucy! Those are not your shoes. Where did you find them?"

"I found them in the cupboard," replied Lucy.

"Oh my! Those are mummy's special shoes! They are very hard and delicate - you must be careful!" her mum explained.

Lucy nodded and promised to take care of her mum's special shoes. She went outside and enjoyed walking around in them, happy to show off her new leather shoes.

Ben and Lily liked to go to the harbor with their dad. They saw big boats and small boats, and many fish and birds. Sometimes they helped dad with his fishing net, and sometimes they played with the sand and shells.

One day, they saw a very big boat with many flags and people. It looked like a castle on the water. Dad said it was a cruise ship, and it took people to faraway places.

"Wow, I want to go on that boat!" Ben said.

"Me too!" Lily said.

"Maybe one day you will," dad said. "But for now, you can dream about it."

That night, Ben and Lily dreamed about the cruise ship. They dreamed that they went on board and met the captain and the crew. They dreamed that they saw dolphins and whales, and islands and mountains. They dreamed that they ate ice cream and cake, and danced and sang. They dreamed that they had a lot of fun.

They woke up with big smiles on their faces. They told dad about their dreams.

"That sounds wonderful," dad said. "Maybe one day your dreams will come true. But for now, I'm glad you had a good night's sleep."

He hugged them and kissed them, and they got ready for another day at the harbor. The harbor was calm and bright, and they loved it.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily who loved to wear her purple hat. One day, she went to the park to play with her friends. She put her hat on the bench and started playing.

Suddenly, a gust of wind blew and her hat disappeared! Lily started to cry and her friends asked what was wrong. "My hat is gone," she said sadly.

"Don't worry, we'll help you find it," said her friend Jack. They searched high and low, but the hat was nowhere to be found. Just when they were about to give up, a little bird flew down and brought Lily her purple hat back. Lily was so happy and thanked the bird. From that day on, she never took her hat off again.

Once upon a time, there was a seal. He lived by the beach and loved playing in the ocean all day. One day, a big, selfish wave came and tried to sweep the seal away.

The Seal called out to the wave, "Please stay away from me!"
But the big, selfish wave ignored the Seal's plea and kept pushing him further away from the beach.

The Seal eventually managed to stay near the shore by swimming very hard. He was very tired, but he was happy he was back home.

The Seal warned the wave, "You must be more careful and less selfish when you play with others!"

The wave tried to listen, but it was hard because it was so big and strong. The wave promised to try its best to be more careful and finally the Seal was satisfied.

From then on, the Seal and the wave played together near the beach. The wave learnt to be less selfish and the Seal learnt that it was important to stand up for himself.

Lily liked to sing. She sang all the time. She sang in the morning, she sang at noon, she sang at night. She sang when she was happy, she sang when she was sad, she sang when she was bored. She sang to her toys, she sang to her cat, she sang to her mom and dad.

One day, Lily went to the park with her mom. She saw a big slide made of steel. It was shiny and cold. Lily wanted to slide on it. She ran to the slide and climbed up the steps. She sang a song as she slid down. "Whee, whee, whee, I love to slide, slide, slide!"

But when she reached the bottom, she saw a fat boy waiting for his turn. He was angry. He did not like Lily's song. He said, "Stop singing! You are too loud! You hurt my ears! Go away!"

Lily was scared. She did not want to make the boy mad. She did not want to hurt his ears. She did not want to go away. She liked to sing. She said, "I'm sorry, I did not mean to be loud. I just like to sing. Singing makes me happy. Do you want to sing with me?"

The boy looked at Lily. He saw her big eyes and her smile. He felt a little sorry. He did not have many friends. He did not know how to sing. He said, "I don't know how to sing. I don't have a good voice. I don't think I can make you happy."

Lily said, "That's okay. You don't have to have a good voice to sing. You just have to have fun. Singing is fun. I can teach you how to sing. Come on, let's try. It's easy. Just follow me."

Lily started to sing a simple song. She sang slowly and clearly. She sang with joy. She sang with love. She sang with hope. The boy listened to Lily. He felt a little curious. He felt a little brave. He felt a little happy. He opened his mouth and tried to sing along. He sang softly and shyly. He sang with fear. He sang with doubt. He sang with hope.

Lily and the boy sang together. They sang on the slide. They sang in the park. They sang to the sky. They sang to each other. They sang a new song. A song of friendship. A song of kindness. A song of happiness.

Tom and Sam are brothers. They like to play with their dad. Their dad is big and strong and hairy. He has a lot of hair on his chest and his arms and his face.

One day, they are playing in the living room. Their dad is lying on the couch and they are jumping on him. They are laughing and having fun.

"Tickle, tickle, tickle!" Tom says, as he tickles his dad's chest. His dad laughs and pretends to be ticklish.

"Stop, stop, stop!" his dad says, as he tickles Tom back. Tom laughs and squirms.

"Me too, me too, me too!" Sam says, as he joins the tickling. He tickles his dad's face. His dad laughs and pretends to be ticklish.

"Enough, enough, enough!" his dad says, as he tickles Sam back. Sam laughs and squirms.

They repeat this game for a while, until they are all tired and happy. They hug their dad and say they love him. Their dad hugs them back and says he loves them too.

They are a happy family. They like to play and laugh and repeat.

Ben and Mia were playing in the kitchen. They liked to pretend they were chefs and make yummy food. Ben found a big knife on the table. He wanted to cut some bread with it.

"Look, Mia, I have a knife!" he said. "I can cut anything!"

Mia was scared. She knew the knife was sharp and dangerous. She did not want Ben to hurt himself or her.

"Ben, no!" she cried. "Put the knife down! It is not for playing!"

Ben did not listen. He thought Mia was silly and scared. He waved the knife in the air.

"Ha ha, Mia, I am a great chef! I can cut rare meat with this knife!"

Mia ran to the door and called for Mom. She hoped Mom would come soon and stop Ben.

Mom heard Mia's voice and came to the kitchen. She saw Ben with the knife and was very angry and worried.

"Ben, what are you doing?" she shouted. "Drop the knife right now! You could hurt yourself or Mia!"

Ben was surprised and scared. He dropped the knife on the floor. It made a loud noise.

"I'm sorry, Mom," he said. "I was just playing."

Mom picked up the knife and put it away. She hugged Ben and Mia and checked them for cuts.

"Playing with knives is not fun, Ben," she said. "It is very dangerous. You could have cut yourself or Mia very badly. You need to relax and play with safe toys."

Ben felt sorry and ashamed. He nodded and said he understood.

"I'm sorry, Mia," he said to his sister. "I didn't mean to scare you."

Mia hugged him and said it was okay.

"Let's go to the living room and play with the blocks," she said. "That is more fun and safe."

Ben agreed. He and Mia went to the living room and played with the blocks. They built a big tower and a house. They forgot about the knife and had a good time. Mom smiled and watched them. She was glad they were safe and happy.

Mia tried to do a yoga pose, but she could not do it. She tried again and again, but it was hard. Mia started to frown. She did not like not being able to do the yoga pose. Her mom saw her frown and came over to help her.

Mia's mom showed her how to do the yoga pose. She held Mia's hand and helped her balance. Soon, Mia was able to do the pose. She was so happy and not frowning anymore. Mia and her mom did yoga together and had a great time.

Once there was a very fine rabbit. He was always looking for food in the garden. One day, he asked the other animals to borrow some smoke, because he was feeling very hungry.

The cow said, "Yes, I can give you some smoke, but you must promise to return it".

The rabbit agreed to this and borrowed smoke from the cow. He quickly ate the smoke and thanked the cow.

The next day, the rabbit wanted to borrow some more smoke. But the cow said, "No, you must return what you borrowed first".

So the rabbit said sorry to the cow and returned the smoke. The cow was happy that the rabbit kept his promise and he let the rabbit borrow more smoke.

The rabbit was very thankful to the cow for being so kind. From then on, the cow and the rabbit became very good friends.

Alex wanted to go to the park but Jenny wanted to stay at home and play with her clock. The clock was Jenny's favorite toy and it was purple.

Alex said, "Why don't we go to the park? We can have so much fun!

But Jenny shouted, "NO! I want to play with my clock!

Alex was surprised and said, "But the clock doesn't do much.

Jenny wrinkled her nose and nodded her head. "Yes, but I still like it better than the park.

Alex shrugged his shoulders and said, "Okay, let's play with your clock then!

So Jenny and Alex played with the purple clock until it was time to go home. Even though they disagreed, they still had a great time together.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly dog named Max. Max loved to play with his toys and run around outside. One day, Max's owner had to go to the office and couldn't take Max with him. Max didn't like being alone, so he decided to go with his owner.

When they got to the office, Max saw a big sack on the floor. He was curious and went over to sniff it. Suddenly, the sack moved and a cat jumped out! Max barked and chased the cat around the office. The owner was not happy and told Max to stop. Max felt sad and went to sit in a corner.

But then, the owner realized that Max was just trying to play. He picked up Max and gave him a big hug. From then on, Max was allowed to come to the office with his owner every day. And even better, the cat became Max's friend and they played together all the time. The end.

Once there was a bunny who lived in the forest. He was always very happy, hopping around and making new friends. Every day he hopped around the forest meeting new animals.

One day, he was hopping near a stream and heard a funny noise. He stopped and looked to see what it was. Suddenly he saw a big snake! He was very anxious but he bravely hopped closer. The snake was friendly and they talked for a while. They even played tag, hopping around the stream.

The bunny and the snake became best friends. Every day they would hop around the forest, having a lot of fun. They were always very happy together. It made the bunny less anxious.

The bunny and the snake would hop all around the forest, exploring new lands and having adventures. They were the best of friends and the forest was a much happier place with them hopping around.

Tom was a big boy who liked to play with his toys. He had a lot of cars, trains, and planes that he could zoom and crash. But his favorite toy was his red suit. It was a suit that made him look like a fireman. He had a hat, a coat, and a hose that he could spray water with.

One day, Tom's mom said they had to go to a party. She said he had to wear a nice suit, not his red suit. Tom did not like that. He wanted to wear his red suit and play fireman. He did not want to wear a blue suit that was rough and tight. He did not want to go to a party with boring people.

Tom's mom tried to make him happy. She said he could give his red suit to his cousin Ben, who was coming to the party. She said Ben was very small and did not have many toys. She said Ben would love to play fireman with Tom's red suit. Tom thought about it. He liked Ben, but he loved his red suit. He did not want to give it away.

Tom and his mom went to the party. Tom wore his blue suit and was very sad. He saw Ben, who was wearing a green shirt and jeans. Ben smiled and waved at Tom. Tom did not smile back. He wanted his red suit.

Then, something happened. Tom heard a loud noise and saw smoke. There was a fire in the kitchen. Everyone was scared and ran outside. Tom saw a real fireman come with a big truck and a long hose. He saw the fireman wear a red suit, just like his. He saw the fireman spray water and put out the fire. He saw the fireman save the day.

Tom felt something in his heart. He felt proud of the fireman. He felt sorry for Ben. He felt silly for being sad. He ran to Ben and hugged him. He said he was sorry. He said he had a gift for him. He said he wanted to give him his red suit. He said he wanted to share his favorite toy. He said he wanted to be his friend.

Ben was very happy. He thanked Tom and put on the red suit. He said he loved it. He said he wanted to play fireman with Tom. He said he wanted to be his friend too.

Tom and Ben played fireman together. They had a lot of fun. They zoomed and crashed and sprayed water. They pretended to save the day. They laughed and smiled. They wore their suits and were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a panda who lived in a deep forest. He was happy and content, and he loved to explore. Today, the panda was feeling curious, so he decided to go for a walk.

As the panda walked through the forest, he stumbled across something that made him gasp in surprise. It was a pile of rotten fruit. He looked around and noticed that the trees were covered in rotten fruit. He thought it was strange, so he decided to investigate.

"Hello?" he called out. Suddenly, a voice came from the shadows.

"Yes, who is there?"

The panda stepped closer to the voice. Suddenly, he felt a hand on his shoulder. It was a giant!

"You must leave my forest," the giant said. "My fruit is rotting, and if you stay, it will rot even more."

The panda was scared, so he quickly ran away. He couldn't believe what had happened and he was so sad. He never returned to the deep forest.

Peter was excited. It was the first day of school and he was ready to make new friends. He said goodbye to his mom and dad as he hopped out of the car. He was so eager to get inside and get started.

On the way, Peter's dad reminded him to be serious at school. He said to always listen to his teachers, respect others and do his best. Peter nodded to show he understood.

When he got to school, Peter found out that there were a few special things he had to do every day. He had to stand up every morning when the flag was raised, he had to bring his own lunch and he also had to shake his teacher's hand when he said hello.

Peter's dad reminded him of these things every day. He said to always remember because these things made him look serious and respectful. Peter was careful to follow all the rules, so that everyone would know how responsible he was.

After a few days of school, Peter got the hang of it. He felt so proud that he was able to show how serious he was about his school day.

Once upon a time, there was a blue monster. He was smelly and mean. He liked to destroy things. He ruined all the toys in the house and tore the furniture apart! He even destroyed the walls! The family was so mad! So they decided to get rid of the smelly blue monster. They took him to the park and told him never to come back. The blue monster never came back, and the family was so happy! They could finally play with their toys and live in a peaceful home. The end.

Sally was feeling very independent. She wanted to explore the world. So she left her home and started walking.

Soon she came to a busy road. She stopped, but she didn't know what to do.

Suddenly, from far away, she heard a voice. It was her mom. She was calling out, warning Sally to stay away from the road.

But Sally was very independent and couldn't listen to her mom. She crossed the road and kept exploring.

Eventually, she was tired and it started to get dark. She was scared and wanted to go home.

Just then, her mom appeared. She said, "I warned you about the road, but you didn't listen." She picked Sally up and carried her back home.

Sally had learned her lesson. From now on she would be careful, and listen to her mom's warnings.

Lily and Ben were best friends. They liked to play outside in the summer. They had a big yard with a swing, a slide and a sandbox. They also had a dog named Spot who was very friendly and crazy.

One day, Lily and Ben decided to make a cake in the sandbox. They used sand, water, leaves and flowers. They mixed everything in a bucket and poured it into a pan. They pretended it was a chocolate cake with frosting and sprinkles.

"Look at our cake!" Lily said. "It smells so good!"

"Can we eat it?" Ben asked.

"No, silly!" Lily said. "It's not a real cake. It's a sand cake. We can only pretend to eat it."

They cut the cake into slices and gave one to Spot. Spot licked the cake and wagged his tail. He liked the sand cake.

Suddenly, they heard a loud voice. It was Lily's mom.

"Lily! Ben! Come inside right now!" she shouted. "It's too hot outside. You need to drink some water and rest."

Lily and Ben looked at each other. They did not want to go inside. They wanted to play more.

"But mom, we're having fun!" Lily said.

"No buts!" her mom said. "You can play later. Now shut the door and come inside!"

Lily and Ben sighed. They picked up their bucket and pan and followed Spot to the door. They shut the door and went inside. They hoped they could play outside again soon. They loved the summer and their sand cake.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Lucy. She was very small and had lots of curly hair.

One day, Lucy went to visit her grandma in the kitchen. Her grandma was baking a cake for her birthday. Lucy watched her grandma for a long time. She saw her using lots of lovely tools to make the cake.

It wasn't easy for Lucy's grandma. She had to struggle to mix all the ingredients. She had to struggle to make the cake fluffy. But at last, the cake was ready, and it was so lovely!

Lucy and her grandma smiled and hugged. Lucy was so happy to spend the day in the kitchen with her grandma. She knew it was filled with love.

Tom and Lily were twins who loved to draw. They had many crayons and papers to make creative pictures. One day, they went to the zoo with their mom and dad. They saw many animals, like lions, monkeys, and elephants. But their favorite was the zebra. They admired its black and white stripes and its long tail.

"Can we draw the zebra, mom?" Tom asked.

"Sure, sweetie. Here are some papers and crayons. You can sit on this bench and draw what you see," mom said.

Tom and Lily were happy. They started to draw the zebra with their crayons. Tom used black and white crayons to make the stripes. Lily used pink and purple crayons to make the stripes. She also added some flowers and stars to make the zebra more pretty.

"Look, Tom. This is my zebra. Isn't it beautiful?" Lily said, showing her picture to Tom.

Tom looked at Lily's picture. He did not like it. He thought it was silly and wrong. He wanted his picture to be the best.

"No, Lily. Your zebra is ugly. Zebras are not pink and purple. They are black and white. You ruined the zebra. You are not creative. You are stupid," Tom said, tearing Lily's picture in half.

Lily was shocked and sad. She started to cry. She loved her zebra. She thought it was creative and fun. She did not understand why Tom was so mean.

"Tom, how could you do that? That was Lily's picture. She worked hard on it. She was creative and happy. You hurt her feelings. You are not nice. You are mean," mom said, scolding Tom.

Tom felt ashamed and sorry. He realized he was wrong. He did not want to make Lily cry. He wanted to make her smile.

"I'm sorry, Lily. I'm sorry, mom. I was mean. I did not like your zebra, but I should not have torn it. It was your picture. You can draw it however you want. You are creative and smart. Can you forgive me?" Tom said, apologizing to Lily and mom.

Lily looked at Tom. She saw he was sincere. She wiped her tears. She smiled.

"It's okay, Tom. I forgive you. You are my brother. I love you. Can we draw another zebra together?" Lily said, forgiving Tom and mom.

Tom smiled back. He hugged Lily. He loved her too. He nodded.

"Yes, Lily. We can draw another zebra together. We can use any colors we want. We can be creative and happy," Tom said, agreeing with Lily and mom.

Mom smiled too. She was proud of Tom and Lily. She gave them new papers and crayons. They sat on the bench and drew another zebra together. They admired their zebra and each other. They had fun at the zoo.

Once upon a time there was a brown bear. His name was Ted. One week Ted noticed that his house was all broken. He was so sad. He wanted to fix it all but he didn't know how.

So he started to ask his friends.

One day Ted asked his friend, "Do you know how to fix my house?

His friend said, "I sure do. I can help you fix it in just one week.

Ted was so happy! He said, "Thank you so much! Let's get started right away.

His friend brought all the tools they needed and they spent the whole week fixing the house. In the end, Ted's house was all shiny and new, just like it was before.

Ted was so happy! He gave his friend a big hug.

The End.

Once upon a time there was a mirror. The mirror was shiny and round. It was very pretty.

One day, a little boy came along. He was very polite. He said please and thank you. He liked the mirror and wanted to cover it.

The little boy carefully covered the mirror with a big blanket. It looked like a house. He was so happy.

The little boy play inside the blanket house all day. He had lots of fun. He even pretended to look in the mirror, but of course he couldn't see anything because it was covered up.

At the end of the day, the little boy thanked the mirror again for its fun day and uncovered it before he went to bed. The mirror was very happy.

Sam and Lily were twins who liked to play pretend. One day, they decided to play fancy restaurant. Sam put on a big hat and a fake mustache and said he was the chef. Lily put on a dress and a necklace and said she was the customer.

Sam went to the kitchen and looked for things to cook. He found some bread, cheese, eggs, and jam. He thought he could make a fancy sandwich with them. He put the bread in the toaster, the cheese in the microwave, and the eggs in a pan. He did not watch the time or the heat.

Lily waited at the table and pretended to read a menu. She heard some noises from the kitchen. She heard a beep, a pop, and a sizzle. She smelled something burning. She got up and ran to the kitchen. She saw Sam with a big mess. The bread was black, the cheese was bubbly, and the eggs were brown. The jam was on the floor.

"Sam, what did you do?" Lily asked. "You ruined the food!"

"I was trying to cook a fancy sandwich," Sam said. "But I don't know how to cook."

Lily felt sorry for Sam. She saw that he was sad and scared. She hugged him and said, "It's okay, Sam. Cooking is hard. You need to learn and practice. And you need to be careful and safe. Maybe we can ask Mom or Dad to help us next time."

Sam nodded and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I wanted to make you happy. I thought you would like a fancy sandwich."

Lily smiled and said, "I love you, Sam. You don't need to make me a fancy sandwich. You can make me a simple sandwich with just bread and jam. Or we can eat something else. As long as we play together, I'm happy."

Sam and Lily cleaned up the kitchen and threw away the bad food. They made a simple sandwich with bread and jam and shared it. They learned that cooking is not easy and that they should be careful and safe. They also learned that they don't need to be fancy to have fun and to make each other happy.

Tom and Jen are friends. They like to play with dogs. One day, they visit a big house with a big yard. The owner of the house has a big dog. His name is Max.

"Hello, Max!" Tom and Jen say. "Can we play with you?" Max wags his tail. He likes Tom and Jen. He runs to them and licks their faces. They laugh and pet him.

The owner of the house comes out. He is a nice man. He says, "Hello, Tom and Jen. Do you like Max? He is a good dog. But he is lonely. I have to go away for a long time. I need someone to take care of him. Do you want to keep him?"

Tom and Jen look at each other. They love Max. But they live in a small apartment. They have no yard. They have no room for a big dog. They feel sad.

"Sorry, sir," Tom says. "We can't keep Max. We have no space. He needs a big yard. He needs a big house. He needs a big owner."

The owner of the house nods. He says, "I understand. Thank you for being honest. But don't worry. I will find a good home for Max. He will be happy. He will have a new friend. He will not be lonely."

Tom and Jen hug Max. They say goodbye. They hope he will be happy. They walk away. They look back. They see Max in the big yard. He looks sad. He looks empty.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy was very curious and excited to explore the world around her.

One day, Daisy decided to set a little fire in her backyard. She was very careful, so she was able to build the fire in a modest size. The fire was warm and inviting.

Daisy and her friends played near the fire, telling stories and laughing. Soon, it was time to say goodbye to their fire. Daisy and her friends grabbed some buckets of water and filled them up, and then slowly poured them on the fire to put it out. Daisy made sure the fire was out before they left, and then she said goodbye to the fire.

Daisy and her friends had lots of fun playing near the fire and were sad to see it go. But Daisy knew that it was important to keep the fire modest and to always put fires out properly.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She wanted to make a birthday cake for her mom, but she didn't know how. Her mom said, "Don't worry, Lily. I will help you." Lily was happy to hear that.

Her mom brought out all the ingredients and they started to mix them together. Lily was having a hard time, but her mom encouraged her to keep trying. Finally, the cake batter was ready and they put it in the oven.

While the cake was baking, Lily's mom lit a candle and put it in the middle of the kitchen table. It was a helpful reminder that they were making a cake for a special occasion. When the cake was done, they took it out of the oven and decorated it with frosting and sprinkles.

Lily was so proud of the cake she made with her mom's help. She couldn't wait to give it to her mom for her birthday. Her mom said, "You did a great job, Lily. You are such a helpful and talented baker!" Lily smiled and felt very happy.

Lily had a bad fall from her bike. She hurt her arm and it was very sore. Mom took her to the hospital to see the doctor. The doctor was nice and gave her a sticker.

The doctor said he needed to take a picture of her arm. He called it an x-ray. He said it was important to see if her arm was broken or not. Lily was scared, but Mom held her hand.

The doctor took Lily to a big machine. He told her to stand still and not move. He put a big apron on her and a small one on her arm. He said it was to protect her from the x-ray. He went behind a window and pressed a button. Lily heard a beep and saw a flash.

The doctor came back and smiled. He showed Lily the x-ray on a screen. He pointed to her arm and said it was not broken, just bruised. He said she was very brave and lucky. He gave her a bandage and some ice. He said she could go home and rest.

Lily was happy and relieved. She thanked the doctor and hugged Mom. She said she was sorry for falling and promised to be careful. Mom said she was proud of her and kissed her. They went home and had some cookies. Lily felt better and showed her x-ray to her teddy bear.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who loved to explore. One day, he decided to go on a special adventure and explore a spooky forest. He was brave, but as he walked through the trees, he started to shiver.

The little boy kept walking and soon he came to a big, dark cave. There were strange noises coming from the cave, and the boy was so scared that he put his hands over his ears. Suddenly, he heard a gentle tapping coming from inside the cave.

The little boy was curious, so he walked inside and looked around. Suddenly, he saw a pale little creature in the corner of the cave. It was a tiny mouse, tapping away at the walls of the cave with its tiny feet.

The little boy smiled and slowly approached the mouse. He stretched out his hand and the mouse crawled onto his palm. Carefully, the little boy took the mouse outside the cave and set it free. They both thanked each other and waved goodbye.

The little boy was no longer scared. He'd made a new furry friend, and nothing in the spooky forest could scare him!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jimmy. He was three years old and loved to imagine. One morning he thought of something extra special. He imagined a magic spell!

Jimmy knew that spells were very powerful and he wanted to make one of his own. He started to think about what kind of spell he would make. Suddenly he had an idea!

He told his mom that he wanted to go outside and find some lovely things. She bundled him up and Jimmy went off. He searched around the garden and soon his bag was full of flowers, rocks, leaves and even a slimy worm.

Next, Jimmy found a corner of the garden that was extra filthy. It was full of mud and bugs. He found some crumbly dirt and scooped it up. He tucked it inside his bag and scurried home.

Back in his bedroom, Jimmy carefully poured the crumbly dirt onto the floor and began to form the letters of a spell with it. He imagined that someday this spell might make things better! Finally he said the magic words and the spell was cast.

Jimmy clapped his hands in joy. He had made a real, honest-to-goodness spell!

Once upon a time, there was a big purple dragon named Spike. Spike had a long tail that he liked to swing around when he was happy. One day, Spike was flying high in the sky when he accidentally crashed into a tree. Ouch! Spike hurt his tail and it started to bleed.

Spike went to his friend, a wise old owl, and asked for help. "My tail hurts, can you heal it?" Spike asked. The owl looked at Spike's tail and said, "Don't worry, I can heal it with some magic leaves." The owl gave Spike some purple leaves to put on his tail.

After a few days, Spike's tail was all better. "Thank you for healing my tail," Spike said to the owl. "No problem," the owl replied. "That's what friends are for." From that day on, Spike was more careful when he flew and never crashed into a tree again.

One day, a mom and her son were looking for things to do. Mom suggested that they go for a walk in the park. The son shouted "yes!" excitedly as he liked going for walks.

So the two of them headed off to the park. At the park, there were lots of lovely trees and colorful flowers. They soon came across a pond. It had some ducks swimming in it and they were so much fun to watch!

Mom pointed to a distant hill. She said that if they went to the top, there would be an amazing view. The son agreed and so they headed up the hill. It was difficult to climb at first, but eventually they made it.

From the top, they could see for miles! They saw the pond with the ducks, the trees, and so much more. They stayed there for some time and enjoyed the distant view. It was a wonderful day and they had fun together.

Ben and Lily were playing in their room. They had many toys and books and pictures. Lily liked to look at the pictures in the frames on the wall. She saw one of her and Ben when they were babies. They were smiling and hugging.

"Look, Ben, this is us!" Lily said, pointing at the frame.

Ben came over and looked. He did not like the picture. He thought he looked silly and small. He wanted to be big and strong.

"I don't like this picture. It is bad. Take it off the wall!" Ben said, grabbing the frame.

"No, Ben, don't! It is a nice picture. Mommy and Daddy gave it to us. They love us!" Lily said, holding the frame.

Ben and Lily pulled and pushed. The frame fell to the floor. It broke. The glass cracked. The picture tore. Ben and Lily gasped.

They heard Mommy coming up the stairs. They were scared. They knew Mommy would be angry. They did not want to get in trouble.

"Quick, hide the frame!" Ben whispered.

"Where?" Lily asked.

"Under the bed!" Ben said.

They pushed the frame under the bed. They hoped Mommy would not see it. They sat on the bed and pretended to read a book.

Mommy opened the door. She smiled at Ben and Lily.

"Hello, my sweeties. What are you doing?" Mommy asked.

"We are reading a book, Mommy. It is a good book. It has animals and colors and numbers." Lily said.

"That's wonderful, my darlings. I'm so proud of you. Can I see the book?" Mommy asked.

Ben and Lily showed Mommy the book. Mommy looked at the book. She saw something shiny under the bed. She frowned.

"What is that under the bed?" Mommy asked.

Ben and Lily looked at each other. They did not know what to say. They felt guilty and sorry.

"Mommy, we are sorry. We broke the frame. We had a fight. We did not mean to. Please don't be mad." Lily said.

Mommy sighed. She bent down and pulled out the frame. She saw the broken glass and the torn picture. She felt sad and angry.

"Ben, Lily, this is not okay. This is a very bad thing. You broke the frame. You ruined the picture. You lied to me. You hurt each other. Why did you do that?" Mommy asked.

Ben and Lily hung their heads. They did not have a good answer. They knew they were wrong.

"Mommy, we are sorry. We love you. We love each other. We will not do it again. Please forgive us." Ben said.

Mommy looked at Ben and Lily. She saw their tears and their remorse. She loved them very much. She hugged them and kissed them.

"I love you too, Ben and Lily. I forgive you. But you have to learn from this. You have to be careful with your things. You have to be kind to each other. You have to tell me the truth. Do you understand?" Mommy asked.

"Yes, Mommy, we understand. We will do better. Thank you, Mommy." Ben and Lily said.

Mommy smiled. She took the frame and the picture. She said she would try to fix them. She said they would talk more later. She said she was glad they were her children. She left the room.

Ben and Lily looked at each other. They felt relieved and grateful. They hugged and said sorry. They decided to play nicely. They did not want to break anything else. They did not want to slap each other. They did not want to hide anything from Mommy. They wanted to be good.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play sport with his friends. He liked to run and jump and kick the ball. Timmy's dad was always proud of him when he played sport.

One day, Timmy's dad gave him a new pair of shoes. They were red and shiny and Timmy loved them. But the shoelaces were too long and kept getting in the way when he played sport. Timmy's dad showed him how to tie his shoelaces so they wouldn't come undone.

From that day on, Timmy was even better at playing sport. He could run faster and jump higher because he didn't have to worry about his shoelaces. Timmy's dad was so proud of him for learning how to tie his shoelaces all by himself.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jo. Jo was very hungry, but there was nothing to eat in her kitchen! She looked in her fridge and there was zero! She looked in her cupboards and again found zero!

Jo was so hungry she began to cry. Then she had an idea. She went outside and started walking. She went to her friend's house and asked if she had something to eat. Her friend said no but she said she could help her. She gave Jo an apple and a banana.

Jo was so excited! She thanked her friend and went back home. When she got there, she frowned because her kitchen was still empty. But then she thought about the apple and banana. She smiled and shrugged. She knew she wouldn't be hungry anymore.

The End.

Once upon a time, a little girl went for a walk. She had a jug with her. It was a big, blue jug. She wanted to fill it with something healthy, so she went to the shop.

When she got there, she asked the shopkeeper for some healthy things. The shopkeeper showed her a bowl of apples and a jug of orange juice. She picked the apples, and the shopkeeper filled the jug with the orange juice.

The little girl thanked the shopkeeper and went on her way. She knew that she could eat some of the apples and drink some of the orange juice. She thought it was a healthy and delicious combination.

Before she knew it, she was back at home. She shared the healthy treats with her family and they all were very happy. They all knew that this was the perfect way to stay healthy.

Tom is a cat who likes tuna. He sees a can of tuna on the table. He wants to eat it. He jumps on the chair and then on the table. He tries to open the can with his paw. He cannot open it. He is sad.

Lily is a girl who likes Tom. She sees Tom on the table. She knows he wants tuna. She runs to the kitchen and gets a can opener. She rushes back to the table. She opens the can for Tom. She gives him a spoon of tuna. He is happy.

Tom licks the tuna and purrs. He thanks Lily with a nuzzle. Lily smiles and hugs Tom. She likes to make him happy. They are friends. They share the tuna. They are full and happy.

One day, Katie and her mommy went to the park. Katie saw a sack and she was so excited to get it. She asked mommy if she could have it and mommy said yes.

Katie wanted to jump with it, so she and her mommy filled it up with leaves and hopped around in it. That night, Katie and her mommy went to the beach.

Katie saw a lot of little things in the water. She asked what they were and her mommy said they were called fish. Katie thought it would be fun to swim with the little fish.

So, the next day, Katie and her mommy went to the store and bought her a swimsuit. She was so happy! Katie put on her swimsuit and, with her mommy, went to the beach.

Katie swam happily with the little fish. Then, when it was time to go home, Katie remembered the sack. She grabbed it, and they filled it up with shells they found on the beach.

Katie was so excited to show everyone the things she had found. She was so proud and happy she had the sack to bring it all home with her.

Once upon a time there lived a giant. He was very tall, with a kind face and gentle heart. He lived in a place with rolling hills and luscious trees. One day, the giant decided to go for a walk in the forest near his home.

As the giant was walking, he heard rustling from the bushes. He stopped and looked around, and noticed a small bunny peeking out. He was surprised to see such a small creature in the deep forest.

The giant said to the bunny, "Hello there! What are you doing here all by yourself?" The bunny squeaked in response, "I'm looking for someone to play with."

The giant was very thoughtful, so he offered to play with the bunny. They both began running around and laughing through the grassy meadow. The bunny taught the giant how to behave like a bunny, hopping and jumping, and they both had a lot of fun.

The giant and bunny played together until the sun set, and then they went their separate ways. The giant waved goodbye to his new friend, promising to come back and visit soon.

The giant went back to his home feeling happy. He was glad that he he had been so thoughtful towards others, and that he had made a new furry friend.

Once there was a little boy who wanted to sail. He went to the bookshelf and saw a boat. He was so excited! He quickly grabbed it and went to the water.

He put the boat in the water, got in, and began to sail. He was having so much fun! But then something happened. The wind suddenly got strong and the boat started to move faster. The boy got very fearful.

He called for help. Luckily, a friendly boat came to help him. He was safe. He thanked the boat and they sailed together back to shore.

When they got back, the boy put the boat back on the bookshelf. He was very happy that he got to sail, but he wasn't so sure he wanted to sail again.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby little bunny called Jack. He had big ears and a fluffy tail. He loved jumping around in the sunshine.
One day Jack was hopping around on a sunny day, when he saw a spring. He ran over and said "What's this?"
He saw that the spring was shiny, with colours of silver and gold. He blinked as the sun reflected off it. His little bunny eyes grew bigger.

Jack hopped around the spring and he touched it with his paw. It made a funny noise and he jumped back in surprise.

He heard a friendly voice. "Hello, Jack," it said. Jack looked around, but he couldn't see anyone.
The voice laughed and said, "I'm the friendly spring! I'm here to give you a present!"

Jack blinked again, surprised. He hopped closer to the spring. Suddenly a magical trinket popped out of the spring.
Jack blinked and clapped his hands. He held the trinket close and thanked the friendly spring.
From then on, Jack was the happiest bunny in the meadow!

One day, there was a delicate cat in the garden. She was always very friendly and curious when people would visit. One day, a little girl and her mom visited the garden and asked the cat if she wanted to come with them. The cat happily agreed and the girl's mom signed some papers to make it official.

The cat was very excited to explore the girl's home. She loved to smell all the delicious food and take a nap in cozy places. Whenever the girl and her mom had company over, the cat liked to say hello and get some scratches. She was so gentle and delicate, everyone was very fond of her.

The cat never left the girl's side, she was always by her side. Everywhere the girl went, the cat was sure to follow. Once in awhile, the girl would sign to the cat, as if she was trying to communicate something. It was so special to see a girl and her beloved cat be so close. They had formed an unspoken bond, which would never be broken.

Once upon a time there was a tall tree. One day, a little bird perched on the branch of the tree. The bird had a surprise for the tree. It had a hook!

The little bird said to the tree, "I have a surprise for you!"
The tree was excited and said, "What is it?"

The little bird smiled and said, "I brought you a hook. Now you can hold things like apples and nuts."
The tree was delighted and said, "Thank you, little bird!"

The bird smiled and flew away, happy to have surprised the tall tree.

Ben was very excited. Today was his uncle's birthday party. He got dressed up in his best outfit and went to the park. The sun was shining bright and the trees were green and tall.

When he arrived, he saw his uncle holding a big, red balloon. He was very happy and gave him a big hug.

"Help me stand it up!" his uncle said pointing to a big, white cake.

Ben carefully stood up the cake and then his uncle lit the candles. He was very careful with the fire.

He gave Uncle a big hug and asked, "Can I have some cake?"

"Of course you can, but make sure that it isn't too hot or you might burn your tongue!" Uncle said.

Ben waited a few minutes until the cake was not so hot and then carefully cut a piece for himself. It was delicious! He gave Uncle another big hug and they both enjoyed the delicious cake.

Molly was a cheerful three-year-old who loved to make snow angels in the winter. One day, she asked her mom if she could go outside and make a snow angel.

Her mom said, "Yes, but you must prepare. Put on your mittens, your boots and your hat."

Molly was so excited that she started to put on all of the things that her mom had suggested. She put on her blue mittens, her warm boots and her pink hat.

Once she was prepared, she ran outside. She found the perfect spot in the snow and began to spread her arms and legs to make a snow angel. After she finished, she was so proud of her masterpiece.

She saw her mom watching her and smiled. Her mom said, "How cheerful you are, Molly."

Molly beamed with pride and went back inside to warm up.

Once upon a time there was a fox who wanted to escape. He was very alert and always listening for danger. He carefully looked around for a safe place away from the hunters.

Suddenly, the fox heard the noise of hunters coming close. He knew he had to escape! Quickly he ran away, his heart beating fast. He ran and ran until he found a safe spot to hide.

The fox noticed a big hole in the ground. He jumped in, safe now from the hunters. The hole was dark and there was a strange smell coming from it. He was curious and moved forward.

When he got to the end of the hole, he discovered an open mouth! It was the mouth of a sleeping bear! The fox was very alert but he knew he had to escape. He quickly ran back out of the hole and ran away from the bear.

The fox ran and ran until he was safe again. He thanked his luck for helping him escape the hunters and from the sleep bear. He knew to always be alert and escape if danger is nearby!

Once upon a time there was a little duck. He was very sad because he had no home. He looked all around but could not find a place to settle.

One day he heard some news, and his eyes lit up. The news said that a pond was nearby and it was the perfect place for a duck to live. The little duck hopped with joy and quickly flew to the pond.

When he got there it was hard to see what was inside, because the pond was very muddy. But the hard work was worth it: in the pond he found the perfect place to settle. He was the happiest duck in the world!

He was now safe and had plenty of food. The little duck was so happy, he almost forgot that he was ever sad. And he lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a shy horse named Tim. Tim lived in a stable near a big tree. He loved to eat apples that fell from the tree. One day, a girl named Sue came to the stable. She wanted to play with Tim.

"Hi Tim, let's play!" said Sue. Tim was shy, so he did not come out of the stable. Sue had an idea. She picked up an apple from the ground. "Look, Tim! I have an apple for you!" Tim liked apples, so he came out of the stable.

Sue and Tim played all day. They ran and jumped in the sun. Then, Sue found a sharp stick on the ground. "Oh no, Tim! This stick is sharp. We must be careful not to step on it." Tim nodded, and they continued playing. They had a fun day together, and Tim was not shy anymore.

Once upon a time there was an ugly monkey. He knew he was ugly and it made him very sad.
One day he was feeling very lonely and ran away. He walked for a long time until he came to a big jungle.

The monkey went in and found a big tree. It was full of ripe bananas and he was very happy. He ate as many bananas as he could and then looked around.

Suddenly, he spotted another monkey. It was not ugly like him; it was very funny. The monkey was dancing and singing. The ugly monkey was so scared he didn't know what to do.

But then the funny monkey saw him and said, 'Hello! You look a bit lost. Do you want to play with me?' The ugly monkey was very happy and said yes.

They played together all day and the ugly monkey had lots of fun. He didn't know it then, but he had made a new friend. The two monkeys became best friends and they were never lonely again.

Tim and Lily were playing in the park with their mom. They saw a big statue of a man on a horse. The statue was very old and had a lot of birds on it.

"Who is that?" Tim asked his mom.

"That is a king. He was very important a long time ago. He helped people and fought bad guys," mom said.

"Can we introduce ourselves to him?" Lily asked.

Mom smiled. "Sure, you can say hello to him. But be careful, don't touch him. He is very fragile and might break."

Tim and Lily ran to the statue. They waved their hands and said, "Hello, Mr. King. We are Tim and Lily. We are playing in the park. You have a nice horse."

The statue did not say anything. He just looked at them with his stone eyes.

Tim and Lily got bored. They wanted to play something else. Tim saw a shiny button on the statue's coat. He wondered what it did. He reached out his hand and pressed it.

Suddenly, the statue made a loud noise. It sounded like a horn. It scared Tim and Lily and the birds. The birds flew away and pooped on the statue and on Tim and Lily.

Mom heard the noise and came running. She saw the mess and the button. She was angry.

"Tim, did you press the button?" she asked.

Tim felt guilty. He nodded his head and said, "Yes, mom. I'm sorry. I was curious. I didn't know it would make a noise."

Mom sighed. She took a tissue and wiped Tim and Lily's faces and clothes. She said, "Tim, you should not touch things that are not yours. You could have damaged the statue or hurt yourself or someone else. You have to be respectful and careful."

Tim said, "I'm sorry, mom. I won't do it again. I'm sorry, Mr. King. I didn't mean to scare you or make you dirty."

Mom said, "It's okay, Tim. I forgive you. But you have to learn from your mistake. Next time, ask before you touch something. And say thank you to the statue for letting us visit him."

Tim and Lily said, "Thank you, Mr. King. Goodbye, Mr. King."

They hugged mom and left the park. They learned a lesson that day. They learned to be polite and curious, but not naughty.
