The boy was so excited. He ran to show his mom the shiny penny. His mom said it was a lucky penny.

So, the boy decided to keep it in his pocket. Everyone who saw it was amazed by the shiny penny.

The boy loved his new penny. He liked how it sparkled in the sun. He also liked feeling how heavy it was in his pocket. The boy was very proud of his new dark copper penny.

Once there was a dad, a mom, and a little boy named Max. They all wanted to go on an adventure. So, they packed up their things and headed off.

They stood at the side of a river that flowed fast and clear. A nosy duck swam up to them and asked what they were doing. Dad said they were just on a little adventure.

Max was so excited! He wanted to play in the river. Dad and Mom said it was okay, as long as they watched him closely. So, they all stripped down and jumped in the water. It was so cool and wet!

Max loved standing in the river, jumping up and down and splashing the nosy duck. They played for a really long time, but eventually it was starting to get dark.

So, Dad and Mom got out and dried off. Max was sad but knew it was time to go home. They said their goodbyes to the nosy duck and headed home, dreaming of their next big adventure!

Lily loved to watch the veterinarian work. She liked to see how he helped the animals that were sick or hurt. He had many tools and medicines that he used to make them feel better. He was very kind and gentle with the animals, and they trusted him.

One day, Lily went to the veterinarian's office with her mom and her dog, Max. Max had a cut on his paw from playing in the park. He was limping and whining. Lily was worried about him. She hugged him and told him it would be okay.

The veterinarian came to see them and smiled. He said, "Hello, Lily. Hello, Max. What happened to your paw, buddy?" He looked at the cut and cleaned it with a soft cloth. He said, "This is not too bad. I just need to put a bandage on it and give you some medicine to prevent infection. You'll be fine in a few days."

He wrapped Max's paw with a white bandage and gave him a treat. He also gave Lily a sticker that said, "I'm a useful helper." He said, "Thank you, Lily, for being a useful helper. You helped Max by being calm and brave. You also helped me by watching and learning. Do you like to watch me work?"

Lily nodded and said, "Yes, I do. I want to be a veterinarian when I grow up. I want to help animals like you do."

The veterinarian smiled and said, "That's wonderful, Lily. You have a good heart and a curious mind. I'm sure you'll be a great veterinarian someday. But for now, you can keep watching and asking questions. And don't forget to take good care of Max. He needs your love and attention."

Lily smiled and said, "I will. Thank you, veterinarian. You're the best."

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a family who lived in a house near a forest. They had a big garden and they liked to spend time outside. One day, they decided to take a walk in the forest.

As they were walking, they saw something shiny between the trees. It seemed very valuable and expensive! They knew they had to find out what it was. So, they carefully picked it up and took it home.

When they arrived home, they opened up the shiny thing. It was a gold ring! Suddenly, the family felt very lucky. They knew that the ring was special and valuable. So, they decided to keep it safe and put it on a shelf.

From then on, the family always remembered their special discovery in the forest. The gold ring seemed very valuable and expensive.

Lily likes to help her mom in the kitchen. She likes to polish the apples with a cloth. She makes them shiny and red. She puts them in a big bowl on the table.

Mom says, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good helper. Do you want an apple for a snack?"

Lily nods and smiles. She picks an apple from the bowl. She takes a big bite. It is sweet and juicy. She says, "Mmm, this is a delicious apple. I like it."

Mom says, "I'm glad you like it. Apples are good for you. They make you strong and healthy."

Lily eats her apple and gives mom a hug. She says, "I love you, mom. And I love apples."

Lily was very happy today. She was going to visit her grandma and grandpa. They lived in a big house with a garden and a dog. Lily loved to play with the dog and help grandma with the flowers.

"Are you ready, Lily?" asked mom. "We have to go soon."

"Yes, mom!" said Lily. She put on her shoes and her coat. She grabbed her backpack. Inside, she had a drawing for grandma and grandpa. She had made it with crayons and stickers. It was a picture of a rainbow and a sun.

They got in the car and drove to grandma and grandpa's house. Lily looked out the window and saw the trees and the birds. She sang a song with mom. She was very excited.

When they arrived, grandma and grandpa were waiting for them. They hugged and kissed Lily and mom. They said they were happy to see them.

"Look what I made for you!" said Lily. She showed them her drawing. "It's a rainbow and a sun. Do you like it?"

"Oh, Lily, it's beautiful!" said grandma. "Thank you so much. You are very talented."

"We love it, Lily," said grandpa. "You are very kind. Can we hang it on the fridge?"

"Yes, please!" said Lily. She smiled. She was proud of her drawing.

They went inside and had some cookies and milk. They talked and laughed. Lily told them about her school and her friends. She asked them about their dog and their garden. She said she wanted to play with them.

"Of course, Lily," said grandma. "We have a lot of fun things to do. You can help me feed the birds and water the plants. You can also play fetch with the dog. He likes you a lot."

"And you can help me build a birdhouse," said grandpa. "I have some wood and some tools. You can paint it with your favorite colors. We can hang it on a tree."

"Wow, that sounds awesome!" said Lily. She clapped her hands. She was very happy. She loved to visit her grandma and grandpa. They were very nice and fun. They always had something new to show her. They always made her feel special.

She believed that they were the best grandma and grandpa in the world. And she was their regular visitor.

As she got closer, the dog appeared to be wagging his tail and seemed happy to see her. Lily's mom told her to be careful and not to touch the dog without asking the owner first. The dog's owner appeared and said it was okay for Lily to pet him.

Lily was very happy and pet the dog gently. She thought he was the nicest dog she had ever met. After a little while, it was time for Lily and her mom to go home. Lily waved goodbye to the dog and his owner, and they walked away, feeling happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play with her dolls and stuffed animals. One day, she asked her mommy if they could go to the museum. Her mommy said yes and they went to the museum.

When they got there, Lily saw a beautiful vase. She asked her mommy if she could touch it, but her mommy said no. Lily was sad and accidentally knocked the vase over. It broke into many pieces. Lily's mommy was upset and said, "Lily, you shouldn't have touched the vase. It's broken now."

Lily felt bad and said sorry. Her mommy forgave her and they went to the gift shop. Lily saw a pretty teapot and asked her mommy if they could buy it. Once upon a time, there was an old man and an old woman who lived in an ancient house. One day, the old man opened a jar he had found in a corner of their home. Suddenly, the jars lid blinked. The old man and woman were very scared, so the old woman said, "What should we do?!" The old man said, "It must be some kind of magic. We should put it back quickly!"

But before they could do anything, an ancient spirit appeared in the room. The old man and woman were so frightened that they could not move. The spirit spoke to them in a deep voice, "You found my jar. Now you must pay the price!"

The old man and woman had no choice but to obey the ancient spirit. The spirit commanded them to give all of their possessions to him. They reluctantly gave away everything but their clothes.

When the ancient spirit left, the old man and woman were left with nothing. They were so sad and unhappy. They had to work many hours every day just to survive. Whenever they looked at the jar, they would blink and remember their bad luck.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a girl named Anna. She had a brilliant red ball. She loved to play with her ball in the yard. One day, she saw a pear tree with big pears. Anna wanted to eat a pear, so she tried to hit the pear with her ball.

Anna threw her ball at the pear tree. She missed the first time. She missed the second time too. But she did not give up. Anna tried again and again. She wanted the pear very much.

On her fifth try, Anna hit the pear with her ball. The pear fell on the ground. Anna picked it up and took a big bite. It was sweet and yummy. Anna was very happy. She played with her ball and ate the pear under the tree.

His mom said yes, and they bought the orange squash. When they got home, Tim played with the squash like it was a ball. He rolled it on the floor and laughed as it wobbled around. His mom smiled and told him that they were going to cook the squash for dinner.

Tim helped his mom cut the squash into pieces. They put the pieces in a big pot and cooked them until they were soft. Then, they mashed the squash and put it on their plates. Tim tasted the orange squash and thought it was yummy. He was happy that he got to play with the squash and eat it too.

Once upon a time, there was a big lion. He lived in the jungle with his friends. One day, the lion went for a walk and saw a branch on the ground. He picked it up and played with it. The branch was long and brown.

As the sun went down, the lion realized he was lost. The jungle became dark and scary. Suddenly, he heard a loud roar. It was a tiger! The lion tried to run away, but he couldn't. The tiger was too fast.

The next day, the lion's friends found the branch on the ground. They knew something was wrong because the lion never left his toys behind. They searched for him and found him lying on the ground. He had been attacked by the tiger. The lion's friends were very sad and missed him very much.

Lily ran outside and started picking tomatoes. She picked a big, red tomato and held it up to her mommy. "Look, mommy! I found a tomato!" she said with a big smile.

Tim and Sam are friends. They like to play with toys and make noise. One day, they find a big drum in the garage. It is old and dusty, but it makes a loud sound when they hit it.

"Let's play with the drum!" Tim says. "We can make our own music!"

"OK!" Sam says. "But we need something to hit it with. What can we use?"

They look around and see some sticks, some spoons, and some stones. They each pick one and start to hit the drum with them. The drum makes different sounds with different things.

"Wow, this is fun!" Tim says. "Listen to our original music!"

They hit the drum faster and louder. They laugh and sing. They do not hear Mom coming into the garage.

"Boys, what are you doing?" Mom asks. "You are making too much noise!"

Tim and Sam stop and look at Mom. They see that she is not happy. They feel sorry.

"Sorry, Mom," Tim says. "We were just playing with the drum. We wanted to make our own music."

"Music?" Mom says. "That was music? It sounded like a lot of banging!"

She looks at the drum and sees that it is old and dusty. She remembers that it was her grandfather's drum. He used to play it in a band. He gave it to her when she was little. She feels sad.

"Boys, this drum is very special to me," Mom says. "It was my grandfather's drum. He loved music. He taught me how to play it. He said that music is a gift that you share with others. You do not just make noise with it."

She picks up the drum and wipes off the dust. She shows them how to hold it and how to hit it gently. She makes a soft and beautiful sound.

"Wow, Mom, you are good!" Tim says. "Can you teach us how to play it?"

"Sure, I can," Mom says. "But you have to be careful and respectful with the drum. It is not a toy. It is a musical instrument. You have to learn how to play it well and how to make music that others can enjoy."

"OK, Mom, we will," Sam says. "We want to learn how to make music like you and your grandfather."

Mom smiles and hugs them. She gives them each a stick and teaches them how to play the drum. They learn how to make different sounds and rhythms. They learn how to play together and listen to each other. They make their own original music, but this time, it is not too loud or too fast. It is sweet and harmonious.

They play the drum for a long time. They have fun and make Mom happy. They feel proud and grateful. They love the drum and the music. They love Mom and her grandfather. They love each other.

One day, Mark was playing outside in his yard. He heard a noise. He stopped and listened. It sounded like a farm animal. He quietly walked to the fence and peeked through. It was a dependable sheep. He heard the sheep start to baa. He thought it sounded so funny! He laughed and giggled.

Mark wanted to see the sheep up close, so he decided to go inside and tell his mom. He said, "Mom, can I go and see the dependable sheep?" His mom said, "Yes, but I want you to listen to me. Don't go too far away, okay?" Mark nodded and ran off to the fence.

He saw the sheep and was very excited. He watched the sheep for a few minutes and listened to it baa. Then he started to count the sheep's fluffy fur. He was having so much fun! He waved to the dependable sheep and ran back to his house.

Mark had a great time. He learned that when you listen carefully, you can have a really fun adventure!

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside in the springtime. He would run and jump and play with his friends. One day, Timmy's mom asked him to help her clean up the house. She gave him a big bin and asked him to put all his toys in it. Timmy was very organized and put all his toys in the bin.

After cleaning up, Timmy went outside to play again. He saw his friends playing with their toys, but Timmy couldn't find his. He looked all around but couldn't find it. Then he remembered that he put it in the bin. He ran inside and found the bin with all his toys in it. Timmy was happy that he was organized and found his toy.

From that day on, Timmy made sure to put all his toys in the bin after playing with them. He knew that being organized would help him find his toys and keep his room clean. And he continued to play outside in the beautiful springtime with his friends. The end.

Tom and Lily were playing with their toy cars. They liked to pretend they were racing on a big road. Tom had a blue car and Lily had an orange car. They made noises like vroom and beep.

"Let's see who is faster," Tom said. "We start here and finish there." He pointed to a line on the floor.

"OK," Lily said. "But we need fuel first. Our cars are hungry." She picked up a bottle of water and poured some on her car. "There, now you have fuel."

Tom laughed. "That's not fuel, that's water. Water is for drinking, not for cars. My car has real fuel." He showed Lily a can of juice. "This is fuel. It makes my car go fast."

Lily frowned. "But juice is for drinking too. And it's sticky. It will make your car dirty."

Tom shrugged. "I don't care. I want to win. Ready, set, go!" He pushed his car hard and zoomed ahead of Lily.

Lily followed him with her car. She saw Tom's car leave a trail of juice on the floor. She thought it looked funny. She smiled and kept playing.

Soon, Tom reached the finish line. He cheered. "I won, I won! I told you my car is faster. My fuel is better."

Lily reached the finish line too. She clapped. "Good job, Tom. You are fast. But look at your car. It's all sticky and wet. And look at the floor. It's all messy and slippery. You made a big mess."

Tom looked at his car. He touched it and felt the juice. He looked at the floor. He saw the juice. He felt bad. He realized he was wrong. He said sorry to Lily. "You're right, Lily. I made a big mess. Water is not fuel, and juice is not fuel either. They are for drinking. I was silly and rude. Can you forgive me?"

Lily nodded. She hugged Tom. "Of course I forgive you, Tom. You are my friend. And you know what? I like your car. It's blue and shiny. And I like my car too. It's orange and bright. They are both nice cars. They don't need fuel. They need love."

Tom smiled. He hugged Lily back. "You're right, Lily. They are both nice cars. And they both have love. And we have love too. We are friends. And friends don't fight over silly things. They share and care."

They cleaned up the mess together. They played with their cars some more. They had fun. They learned a lesson. The end.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Sarah. She was three years old. One day she wanted to do something fun, so she decided to climb. She went to the backyard and saw a big hole. Sarah thought it would be a great adventure, so she climbed in the hole.

Once inside, Sarah realized there was a big tree with a lot of apples. She was so excited! She started picking the apples from the tree and eating them. They were so delicious and sweet. Suddenly, Sarah heard someone calling for her. It was her mom. But Sarah didn't want to leave the hole, so she hid.

When her mom finally found her, she saw that Sarah was covered in dirt and her clothes were spoiled. She was very upset. But even after she scolded Sarah, she couldn't help but to enjoy the deliciousApple that she had eaten. Sarah's mom hugged her and they laughed together.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to play with dolls and cars. One day, they find a big cross in the park. It is shiny and has jewels on it.

"Wow, look at this cross!" Sara says. "It is so pretty. I want to keep it."

"No, I want to keep it!" Ben says. "It is so cool. I saw it first."

They both grab the cross and pull. They are very cross with each other. They do not want to share.

A man sees them and comes over. He is poor and has dirty clothes. He looks sad and angry.

"Hey, that is my cross!" he says. "I found it in the trash. It is the only thing I have. Give it back to me!"

Sara and Ben are scared. They do not know what to do. They look at the cross and then at the man.

They decide to discuss. They whisper to each other.

"Maybe we should give the cross to the man," Sara says. "He needs it more than we do. He has nothing else."

"But it is so pretty and cool," Ben says. "We found it in the park. It is ours."

"But he found it first," Sara says. "It is not nice to take things from people. He will be very sad if we keep it."

Ben thinks for a moment. He nods.

"Okay, you are right," he says. "We should be kind and share. Maybe he will let us play with it sometimes."

They let go of the cross and give it to the man. The man smiles and thanks them. He says they are very good and sweet. He says they can play with the cross whenever they want.

Sara and Ben are happy. They hug the man and the cross. They are not cross anymore. They are friends.

Luckily, a big bird saw her and wanted to protect her. The big bird flew over to the little birdie and said, "I will protect you from harm."

The little birdie was grateful and followed the big bird to safety. From that day on, the little birdie learned that it was okay to be independent, but it's always good to have someone protect you when needed.

Jim and Ella were walking through the park when they saw a big bush. Ella was a bit scared of it. Jim told her that she had nothing to be afraid of, but Ella was still scared of the bush.

Suddenly, there was a noise from behind the bush! Ella got even more scared, but Jim said it was probably just an animal. He took Ella's hand and started to walk towards the bush. Ella was shaking and very scared.

When they got closer to the bush, Jim saw two rabbits! Ella was so relieved and jumped up and down with happiness. She was very lucky that it was just two little rabbits and not something that could have really scared her! Her lucky day!

Jim and Ella both laughed and stayed to watch the rabbits hop around. It was so much fun!

Lily was excited to play hide and seek with her big brother Tom. She ran to the bedroom and looked for a good spot to hide. She saw a big box under the bed and crawled inside. She pulled a rag over her head and waited for Tom to find her.

Tom counted to ten and then shouted, "Ready or not, here I come!" He looked in the closet, under the desk, behind the curtains, but he could not find Lily. He wondered where she was hiding. He went to the bedroom and saw the box under the bed. He thought Lily might be in there.

He walked to the box and lifted the rag. He saw Lily's face and smiled. He said, "I found you, Lily!" But Lily did not smile back. She did not move or make a sound. She was very still and cold. Tom felt scared and sad. He screamed for Mom and Dad.

Mom and Dad came running to the bedroom. They saw Tom and Lily and the box. They saw the rag and the hole in the box. They saw the rat poison that had spilled from the hole. They realized what had happened. They cried and hugged Tom. They called for help, but it was too late. Lily was gone.

Ben and Lily were playing in the park. They liked to roll down the hill and feel the wind on their faces. They laughed and shouted as they rolled over and over.

"Look, Lily, a light!" Ben said, pointing to a shiny thing on the grass. He ran to pick it up. It was a small, round, metal thing with a button on it.

"What is it?" Lily asked, following him.

"I don't know. Maybe it makes a sound or a picture. Let's see." Ben pressed the button.

Suddenly, there was a loud bang and a flash of fire. Ben and Lily screamed and dropped the thing. It was a firework, and it was very dangerous.

"Run, run!" Lily cried, grabbing Ben's hand. They ran away from the firework as fast as they could.

A man who was walking his dog saw them and ran to help. He picked up the firework and threw it in a bin. He checked if Ben and Lily were hurt.

"Are you okay, kids? That was a very bad thing to play with. It could have hurt you very badly. Where are your parents?" he asked.

Ben and Lily were scared and sorry. They told the man where their mum was. She was sitting on a bench, reading a book. She did not see what happened.

The man took them to their mum and told her what happened. She was very angry and worried. She hugged them and thanked the man. She told them to never touch anything that looked like a light again. She said they were very lucky to be safe.

Ben and Lily said they were sorry and they learned their lesson. They never played with fireworks again. They only rolled down the hill and had fun.

Lily liked to paint. She had many colors and brushes. She painted flowers and trees and animals. She was happy when she painted.

One day, she saw a big painting in the school. It was very pretty. It had many shapes and lines and dots. Lily wanted to paint like that. She asked her teacher, "How can I paint like that?"

Her teacher smiled and said, "That is a special painting. It is by a famous artist. He worked very hard and learned a lot. He had a gift for painting. But you can also paint well, Lily. You just need to practice and raise your skills."

Lily nodded and went home. She tried to paint like the famous artist. She used many colors and brushes. She made shapes and lines and dots. But her painting did not look like the big painting. It looked messy and ugly. Lily was sad. She did not like her painting.

Her mom saw her painting and said, "What a beautiful painting, Lily! I love it. It is so colorful and creative. You have a gift for painting, too. You just need to be yourself and paint what you see and feel. You don't need to copy anyone else. You are not ordinary, Lily. You are special."

Lily smiled and hugged her mom. She looked at her painting again. She saw flowers and trees and animals. She saw her own style and expression. She was happy with her painting.

The moral of the story is: Be yourself and appreciate your own talents. Don't compare yourself to others or try to imitate them. You are special and unique.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play with toys and make noise. One day, they find a big box in the garage. Inside the box, there are many things. There are pots and pans, spoons and forks, bells and whistles, and a trumpet.

"Wow, look at all these things!" Lily says. "Let's make music!"

"OK, let's mix them up and see what happens!" Ben says. They take out the things and put them on the floor. They pick up some spoons and forks and bang them on the pots and pans. They shake the bells and whistles and blow the trumpet.

"Bang, bang, clang, clang, ding, ding, toot, toot!" They make a lot of noise. They are happy.

But then, they hear a voice. It is Lily's mom. She comes into the garage and sees the mess.

"What are you doing, Lily and Ben?" she asks. "You are making too much noise!"

"Sorry, mom, we are making music!" Lily says.

"Music? This is not music, this is noise!" mom says. "You need to learn how to play the trumpet properly. And you need to clean up this mess!"

"OK, mom, we will clean up," Ben says. "But can we keep the trumpet? We like it!"

"Only if you promise to practice and be quiet," mom says.

"OK, we promise," Lily and Ben say. They put the things back in the box and take the trumpet. They go to Lily's room and close the door. They try to play the trumpet softly. They are still happy.

Once, there was a little girl called Sophia. She was three years old and very excited to go outside. Her mum said she could go out to play.

So, Sophia put on her warm coat and ran outside. She wanted to go fast, so she ran and ran, speeding around the garden.

Sophia's mum saw her running quickly and called, "Sophia, slow down or you might fall over!

Sophia thought for a moment and then decided to slow down. She remembered what her mum had said.

When Sophia stopped running, she saw something delicious on the ground: a yummy snack! She quickly picked it up and gobbled it up. It was warm and tasted delicious - just what Sophia needed after all that running.

Sophia smiled and ran back inside to continue playing.

Once upon a time, there was a girl. She was very brave and wanted to explore the world. One day, she was walking in a forest when she came to a big, high sign. It said "Danger - do not go ahead!". But the brave girl decided to examine the sign and keep walking.

Soon she realised she had made a mistake, because the deeper she went into the forest, the higher the trees became. She started to feel very scared.

At last, the girl found an even bigger, higher sign. It said "Danger - do not go any further!" But this time the brave girl decided to obey the sign, and she stopped walking.

But unfortunately, it was too late. Just then, the highest tree in the forest came tumbling down! The brave girl was crushed beneath it, and the forest was full of sadness.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a girl who loved music. Every day she would pack up her music and take it with her.

It made her feel so happy and alive. She would sing and dance and forget everything else. But one day, something very strange began to happen.

The girl noticed that the music was getting quieter and quieter. She could still hear it, but it was very distant. She began to worry and tried to get closer to it.

She followed the music until she came to a big hill. The girl climbed up to the top and found that the music was coming from a nice bird that was sitting on a tree.

The girl laughed and clapped her hands. She thanked the bird for playing such nice music and they both enjoyed it until it was time to pack up and go home.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Lucy. She had lots of toys in her room, including a special magnet. One day, the magnet accidently split into two pieces.

The pieces were too small for Lucy to hold. She was very sad, and started to cry. She was so upset that she started to hurt herself.

Suddenly, something magical happened. The two pieces of the magnet magically bounced off the floor, and put themselves back together.

When the magnet was whole again, Lucy was so happy that she gave it a big cuddle. She would always be careful with it from then on!

Once upon a time, Mommy and Daddy were cleaning in their big house. They had been cleaning for a long time and were getting tired. Suddenly Mommy felt something soft under her foot. She looked down and saw a pair of scissors! She picked them up and was surprised. She did not know she had lost them!

Dad was not happy. He was worried that someone had disturbed their house. He looked around carefully, but did not see anyone. Then, he noticed a squirrel. It was peeking at them with big eyes. It was holding the scissors in its mouth!

Dad smiled and called to Mommy. She came over and saw the squirrel. He was so gentle and charming! Mommy took the scissors from the squirrel. Then, she gave it some food and watched it eat.

The squirrel finished its food and scampered away. Mommy and Daddy watched it go and smiled. They were glad that only a tiny animal had disturbed their house!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was very excited to take a bath in her tall bathtub. Her mommy filled it up with warm water and bubbles. Lily stepped in and splashed around.

Suddenly, she noticed her favorite rubber duck was missing. "Mommy, where is my ducky?" she asked.

Her mommy replied, "I think it's hiding behind the shampoo bottle." Lily reached over and found her ducky. "Yay!" she exclaimed.

After she was all clean, Lily stepped out of the bathtub and wrapped herself in a soft towel. "That was fun," she said to her mommy. "Can we do it again tomorrow?"

Tom and Lily are playing in the park. They like to pretend they are lions. They run and jump and roar. They make their hands into claws and chase each other.

"Roar! I am the king of the jungle!" Tom says.

"Roar! I am the queen of the jungle!" Lily says.

They have fun and laugh.

But then, Tom feels something on his leg. It is a bug. It bites him. It hurts. It makes him itch.

"Ow! Ow! Ow!" Tom says. He stops playing. He scratches his leg.

Lily sees him. She runs to him.

"What is wrong, Tom?" Lily asks.

"A bug bit me. It is bad. It makes me itch." Tom says.

Lily looks at his leg. She sees a red spot. She feels sorry for him.

"I have an idea. Come with me." Lily says.

She takes him to the water fountain. She turns it on. She puts his leg under the water. The water is cold. It makes him feel better.

"Does it still itch?" Lily asks.

"A little. But not so much. Thank you, Lily." Tom says.

They hug. They are friends.

They go back to playing. They are ready to roar again. They are happy.

Once upon a time there was a man who had a bell. His bell would ring very loudly! Every morning he would take his bell and stand in the street to sell his goods.

One day, a little girl came to the man's stand and asked what he was selling. The man said he had some spicy treats. The little girl told the man that she liked spicy foods, so she grabbed one and paid him.

The girl took a bite and realized it was too spicy. Tears began to well up in her eyes but the man said, "Don't cry. Take your bell and ring it. That will make the burning feeling go away."

The little girl took her bell and rang it. Sure enough, it worked! Her burning sensation slowly faded away. She thanked the man and smiled.

After that, the little girl came to the man's stand every day with her bell. And every day the man would sell her something spicy and tell her how to use her bell. All the other children in the street wanted bells, too, but the little girl kept her special secret.

Once upon a time there was a boy. His name was Tim. He was three years old. Every night, Tim's Mommy and Daddy would take him outside. They would look up and see the stars.

One night, Tim's Mommy pointed at a star. "That's a special star," she said to Tim. "Remember it." Tim did.

The next night, Tim looked for the special star. It wasn't there. He looked for it every night, but he could not find it.

One night, Tim's Daddy took him outside. They looked up at the sky. There it was - the special star! Tim was very happy.

From then on, every night Tim looked for the special star. He could find it easily - it was not like the other stars. It always stayed in the same place. It was like a regular star.

Tim always remembered the special star. He always looked for it, and it made him smile.

Once upon a time, there was a gray bear who loved to go on adventures. He ventured through the wood until he came across a deep cave. As he ventured further into the cave, he soon stumbled upon a strange tasting fruit. He did not know what it was, but he decided to try it. The bear could not believe his taste buds! He had never tasted anything like it before!

He couldn't wait to share the fruit with his friends. He scooped up a big basket of the mysterious fruit and quickly tied it closed with some twine. The bear was so excited for his friends to try the new mysterious fruit he had found.

The bear ran to his friends' home with the basket of fruit, and it wasn't long until they were all gathered around and trying different pieces. They were so happy to find out what the strange tasting fruit was. Each of them had their own unique opinion on the taste and found it absolutely delightful.

The bear was so happy that he was able to tie the basket and share his discovery with his friends. They all ate until the basket was empty, and the new taste was something they could all remember for years.

Once there was a boy named Joe. He was very excited because he was going to get to eat some spaghetti. He went to the kitchen and saw a big pot of warm spaghetti on the table. Joe was so happy to see the spaghetti that he smiled.

He slowly took the lid off the pot and revealed the most delicious smelling spaghetti. He couldn't believe his luck. He started to eat the spaghetti with a spoon, enjoying every bite as he ate.

When he finished, he felt very peaceful. He liked the way spaghetti made him feel so he decided to make some more. He went to the kitchen, grabbed the ingredients and made the most delicious spaghetti he had ever tasted.

Every day after that Joe would make and eat some spaghetti. Every day he would feel peaceful, just like he did that first day. It revealed to Joe that having spaghetti was a great way to make himself feel calm and happy.

Mandy asked Jack, "What are you doing with your toy?

Jack explained, "I'm trying to write a poem about my toy. I want to make it special. Mandy listened carefully as Jack shared his idea.

Mandy said, "I wish I had a toy too. I could try and write a poem about it!

Jack said, "You could use your imagination, Mandy. That's just as good!

Mandy smiled. She was so happy to have a friend like Jack. She closed her eyes and began dreaming of a special poem for her toy.

Once upon a time there was a brave little girl. She liked to swim. Every day she would go for a swim in the pond near her house. One day, when she was swimming, something caught her eye. She swam closer and saw it was a sheet. She swam over to it and carefully grabbed hold of it. She was so patient as she carefully swam back with it.

After she got out of the pond she carefully spread out the sheet. There were lots of beautiful colors painted on it. They were so pretty and the little girl was so happy. She used the sheet to make a big tent and inside she made a bed. She could now have her own special place to take naps and read books. She was so excited to have found the sheet!

The little girl went back to the pond to swim every day. She was so brave and patient. She was so happy she found the sheet that day. She would never forget her special find on her special swim.

Tommy and his mom were walking in the park, when they saw a chicken. Tommy's eyes lit up, and he asked his mom if he could pet the chicken. His mom smiled and said yes, then they both sat down next to the chicken. They discussed what color it was and what the chicken said. They could hear it saying 'cluck, cluck'.

Tommy happily pet the chicken, and it felt so soft and warm. The chicken seemed very mellow and sleepy. After a while, Tommy's mom said it was time to go home. Tommy said goodbye to the chicken and waved as they walked away.

Tommy and his mom discussed how much fun it had been to pet the chicken. Tommy was very excited about his visit to the park and asked his mom if he could visit the chicken again soon. Tommy's mom said yes, and Tommy smiled as they went home.

One day, a little girl named Emma was playing in her garden. Suddenly, she saw a mighty brick. She ran towards it and shouted to her mum: "Mum, look! A mighty brick!".

Mum smiled and said: "What luck! Let's hurry and make something with it!".

So the two of them worked hard. They put a lot of effort into making something with the brick. After some time, Emma's mum proudly said: "Look Emma, we made a really nice tower".

Emma's eyes sparkled. She hugged her mother and said: "Our work looks mighty!" They both laughed. Then they continued to build other things with the brick until it was time to go inside.

Once there was a boy named Joe. He was three years old. One day, Joe was playing in his backyard. He noticed some cardboard next to a tree. Joe was very glad to see the cardboard. He quickly picked it up and shook it.

Joe's father saw him. He said, "Hey Joe! What do you have there?"

Joe replied, "I found some cardboard! I'm shaking it."

Joe's father laughed and said, "That's fun! What will you do with it?"

Joe smiled and said, "I'm going to make a fort!" He was so glad that he got to use the cardboard. He quickly ran around gathering sticks and rocks. He carefully put the pieces together, and soon he had a perfect fort!

Joe was so proud of his cardboard fort. He shook it one last time, just to make sure it was sturdy. Then he waved to his dad and smiled. He was so glad he found the cardboard.

Lily liked to imagine things. She imagined she was a princess, a doctor, a teacher, or a superhero. She liked to draw her imaginations on paper and show them to her mom and dad.

One day, she saw her dad working on his computer. He was typing and clicking and looking at the screen. Lily was curious. She asked, "Dad, what are you doing?"

"I'm writing a story, Lily. A story for a book," her dad said.

"Can I see your story?" Lily asked.

"Not yet, Lily. It's not finished. I need to work on it some more," her dad said.

Lily was impatient. She wanted to see the story now. She thought it would be more fun than her drawings. She said, "Please, dad, can I see it? Just a little bit?"

Her dad sighed. He loved Lily, but he needed to concentrate. He said, "Okay, Lily, but only for a minute. Then you have to let me work, okay?"

He turned the computer so Lily could see the screen. Lily saw a lot of words. She could not read them. She was disappointed. She said, "This is not a story. This is boring. Where are the pictures?"

Her dad smiled. He said, "The pictures are in your head, Lily. You have to imagine them. That's what reading is. You use the words to imagine the story."

Lily frowned. She did not understand. She said, "But I like to see the pictures. I like to draw them. Can I draw on your computer?"

Her dad shook his head. He said, "No, Lily, you can't draw on my computer. That would ruin it. You can draw on your own paper. Here, I have some for you."

He gave Lily some paper and crayons. He said, "Why don't you draw your own story, Lily? You have a great imagination. You can make up anything you want."

Lily thought for a moment. She said, "Okay, dad, I will draw my own story. But can you read me your story when you are done?"

Her dad nodded. He said, "Of course, Lily. I would love to read you my story. But you have to be patient, okay? It might take a while."

Lily agreed. She said, "Okay, dad, I will be patient. But I will be excited too."

She took the paper and crayons and went to her room. She started to draw her own story. She imagined she was a princess, a doctor, a teacher, and a superhero. She had a lot of fun. She could not wait to show her dad her story. And she could not wait to hear his story too.

One day, a dog and a cat were playing in the park. The dog liked to chase the cat and bark loudly. The cat liked to hide and jump on the dog. They had a lot of fun together.

But then, a big bird came and landed on a tree. The bird had bright feathers and a long beak. The bird looked at the dog and the cat and said, "You are so ignorant! You don't know anything about the world. You only know how to play and make noise. You should be ashamed of yourselves!"

The dog and the cat were surprised and angry. They did not like the bird's words. They did not think they were ignorant. They thought they knew a lot of things. They decided to teach the bird a lesson.

The dog ran to the tree and barked louder than ever. The cat climbed to the branch and scratched the bird's feathers. The bird was scared and tried to fly away. But the dog and the cat were faster and stronger. They pulled the bird down and made it fall on the ground.

The bird was hurt and embarrassed. It had lost some of its feathers and its beak was bent. It wished it had not been so rude to the dog and the cat. It realized it had been ignorant too. It did not know everything about the dog and the cat. It did not know that they were smart and brave and kind.

The dog and the cat felt sorry for the bird. They did not want to hurt it anymore. They decided to forgive it and help it. They licked its wounds and gave it some of their fur to keep it warm. They told it some of the things they knew and asked it some of the things it knew. They learned from each other and became friends.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mom asked her to help set the table for dinner. Lily happily agreed and went to get the plates and forks from the cabinet.

As she was setting the table, Lily noticed that the cabinet was a pretty blue color. "Mommy, the cabinet is blue!" she exclaimed.

Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, it is. It's one of my favorite colors."

After they finished setting the table, Lily's dad came home from work. "Wow, the table looks great!" he said.

Lily beamed with pride and said, "I helped set it!"

Her parents were so proud of her and they all enjoyed a delicious dinner together. The end.

John was so excited. He was going to the beach with his family. He couldn't wait to dive into the ocean.

When they arrived at the beach, John put on his red swimsuit and his yellow life vest. He couldn't wait to jump in!

John's dad told him, "Be careful, John. Don't go too deep."

John waved at his dad and yelled, "Don't worry! I'm a great diver!"

John dove and splashed around in the waves. He was having so much fun. He dove one last time and when he came back up, he felt a sharp pain in his leg.

John screamed out in pain. His dad ran up to him and saw a big, jagged rock by where John had been diving. He had cut himself on the rock.

John's dad took him back to the house. He put some ointment on the cut and gave John a big steak to eat.

John had a lot of fun at the beach, but his hurt leg kept him from having any more fun that day.

Once upon a time, there was a bunny named Benny who loved to hop all around. One sunny day, Benny saw a funny-looking cane lying on the ground. He picked it up and started to play with it, pretending he was a wizard.

Suddenly, a wise old turtle named Tommy appeared and said, "Benny, be careful with that cane. It may look funny, but it's actually very powerful. You could hurt yourself or others if you're not careful."

Benny didn't listen and continued to play with the cane. But as he was hopping around, he accidentally hit his friend, a squirrel named Sammy, with the cane. Sammy was hurt and had to go to the doctor.

Benny felt very sorry and realized that Tommy was right. He promised to always be careful with things that may seem funny or harmless but could actually be dangerous. From that day on, Benny hopped around with caution and never played with strange objects again.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to read. They had many books in their white cabin in the woods. One day, they found a new book on the shelf. It had a picture of a big bear on the cover.

"Let's read this book," Lily said. "It looks fun."

"OK," Ben said. "But we have to be quiet. Mom and Dad are sleeping."

They opened the book and began to read. The book was about a boy and a girl who went to the forest and met a friendly bear. The bear showed them his cave and his honey. Lily and Ben liked the story. They wished they could meet a bear too.

But as they read, they did not hear a loud noise outside. It was a real bear, and he was hungry. He smelled the honey in the cabin and wanted some. He scratched the door with his claws and roared.

Lily and Ben heard the noise and stopped reading. They were scared. They ran to their parents' room and woke them up.

"Mom, Dad, there's a bear outside!" Lily cried.

"A bear?" Dad said. He got up and looked out the window. He saw the bear trying to break the door. He grabbed his rifle and ran outside.

"Stay here," he told Mom, Lily, and Ben. "I'll scare the bear away."

They heard a loud bang and then another roar. They waited for Dad to come back. They hoped he was OK. They hugged each other and prayed.

After a few minutes, they heard Dad's voice. He was safe. He had shot the bear in the leg and made him run away. He came back to the cabin and hugged his family.

"Are you OK, Dad?" Ben asked.

"Yes, I'm OK," Dad said. "But we have to be careful. There are wild animals in the woods. We can't leave honey outside. And we can't read books about bears. They might attract them."

Lily and Ben nodded. They were sorry they had read the book. They decided to read a different book next time. A book about something safe and nice. Like flowers. Or rainbows. Or puppies.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and climb trees. One day, she saw a big orange branch on the ground. She picked it up and showed it to her mom.

"Look, Mommy! I found an orange branch!" Lily said.

"That's very nice, dear. But be careful, branches can be dangerous," her mom warned.

Lily didn't listen and continued to play with the branch. She even took it to school to show her friends. But during recess, the branch broke and hurt Lily's foot. She had to go to the hospital and study in bed for a long time.

The moral of the story is to listen to your parents and be careful with things that can hurt you.

She saw Ben at the door. Ben was her best friend. He had a big box in his hands. He smiled and said, "Happy birthday, Lily! I have a present for you."

Lily was curious. She opened the box and saw a doll. The doll had long hair, a pink dress, and shoes. Lily loved the doll. She hugged Ben and said, "Thank you, Ben! She is so pretty. What is her name?"

Ben thought for a moment. He said, "Her name is Daisy. Do you like her?"

Lily nodded. She said, "I like her very much. Do you want to play with her?"

Ben nodded. He said, "Yes, I do. Can I offer her some cake?"

Lily giggled. She said, "Yes, you can. But be careful. She is clumsy. She might drop the cake."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to buy candies from the vendor who came to her street every day. One day, Lily did not have enough money to buy her favorite candy. She started to weep and cry.

The vendor saw Lily crying and he felt very sad. He patiently listened to her and told her that he had a special offer for her. He gave her the candy for free and said, "Always be patient and good things will happen".

Lily was very happy and thanked the vendor with a big smile. From that day on, she always saved her money and bought candies from the vendor. She learned that being patient is important and good things will come to those who wait.

Once upon a time, there was a kind little bird called Daisy. Daisy loved to sing her sweet little songs all around the forest. Every day she would fly high above the trees searching for a special chamber.

On this day, while Daisy was flying, she saw a beautiful chamber made of twigs and leaves. It seemed to be calling to her, so Daisy decided to fly closer.

As she flew inside the chamber, Daisy began to sing. Her melodic voice filled the air with a sweet sound.

Suddenly, Daisy heard another voice singing. A friendly voice replied to her Song. Daisy looked around and saw a rabbit hopping around the chamber. The rabbit smiled at her and said, "Greetings! My name is Sprinkles. I love to sing too! Would you like to sing together?

Daisy happily agreed and the two animals happily sang together for the rest of the day. Daisy was so thankful to have met Sprinkles and for the chance to sing in such a kind chamber.

Once upon a time, there was a sleepy cat named Love. Love liked to roll around in the grass. One sunny day, Love went outside to roll and play.

Love saw a big ball and wanted to play with it. Love rolled the ball down a hill. The ball rolled faster and faster. Love ran after the ball, feeling less sleepy now.

At the bottom of the hill, Love met a dog. The dog said, "I want to play too!" Love and the dog played with the ball. But then, the ball rolled into a big, dark hole. Love and the dog could not get the ball back. They both felt sad and went home.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. They run and jump and laugh. They see a big dog. The dog is brown and fluffy. The dog is friendly. He wags his tail.

"Hello, dog!" Lily says. "What is your name?"

The dog barks. He likes Lily and Tom. He wants to play with them.

"Let's play a joke on the dog," Tom says. "We can hide behind that tree and call him."

"OK," Lily says. "That sounds fun."

They run behind the tree. They wait for the dog to come. They say, "Here, dog! Come here!"

The dog hears them. He looks around. He does not see them. He is confused. He runs to the tree. He sniffs and sniffs.

"Surprise!" Lily and Tom say. They jump out from behind the tree. They giggle.

The dog is surprised. He barks. He is not angry. He is happy. He licks Lily and Tom. He thinks they are funny.

Lily and Tom are glad. They hug the dog. They say, "You are a good dog. We like you."

They play more with the dog. They have a lot of fun. They are happy. They are friends.

Sam and Ben were brothers who liked to cook. They cooked eggs, toast, soup and cake. They cooked for their mom and dad, who said they were good cooks. But Sam and Ben were also competitive. They wanted to be the best cook in the family.

One day, they decided to have a cook-off. They each chose a recipe from a book and went to the kitchen. They got the pots, pans, spoons and bowls. They got the flour, sugar, butter and eggs. They got the cheese, ham, bread and tomatoes. They got the milk, chocolate, cream and cherries. They got everything they needed to cook.

They started to cook. Sam made a cheese and ham pie. Ben made a chocolate and cherry cake. They worked hard and fast. They did not help each other. They did not share. They did not talk. They only cooked.

They finished cooking. They put their dishes on the table. They called their mom and dad. They said, "Look what we cooked! Who is the best cook?"

Their mom and dad came to the table. They looked at the dishes. They looked at Sam and Ben. They looked sad and angry. They said, "What did you do? You made a big mess! You wasted a lot of food! You did not clean up! You did not work together! You did not have fun! You are not good cooks!"

Sam and Ben felt bad. They looked at their dishes. They looked at the kitchen. They looked at the landscape outside. They saw the sun, the sky, the trees and the flowers. They saw the birds, the bees, the squirrels and the rabbits. They saw how beautiful and happy everything was. They wished they were like that.

They said, "We are sorry, mom and dad. We were silly and selfish. We wanted to be the best cook, but we forgot to be good brothers. We forgot to enjoy cooking. We forgot to love each other."

Their mom and dad hugged them. They said, "We forgive you, Sam and Ben. We love you, no matter what. But you have to learn from your mistake. You have to clean up the kitchen. You have to share the food. You have to help each other. You have to have fun. You have to be good cooks and good brothers."

Sam and Ben nodded. They said, "We will, mom and dad. We will." They started to clean up the kitchen. They started to share the food. They started to help each other. They started to have fun. They started to be good cooks and good brothers. But it was too late. The cheese and ham pie was cold and hard. The chocolate and cherry cake was burnt and bitter. They did not taste good. They did not make anyone happy. They had a bad ending.

Jim was very intelligent. He was walking in the park and noticed a big, colourful festival. Jim was excited. He decided to go see what all of the noise was about.

When Jim got to the festival, he saw a man stealing candy from a stand. He was amazed. He had never seen someone do something like this before.

Jim ran over to the man and said, "Hey! You can't do that! That's stealing!"

The man turned and looked surprised. He said, "What are you going to do about it, little one?"

Jim smiled and replied, "I'm going to get help! You'd better run!"

The man ran off and Jim laughed. He was proud of himself for being so intelligent and brave to stand up to the man. From that day on, Jim always made sure to look out for people stealing at festivals.

Once upon a time, there was a group of animals who lived in the forest. They were all friends and liked to play together. One day, they decided to have a race to see who was the fastest.

The rabbit was very fast and thought he would win, but the clever turtle had a plan. He asked the rabbit if he wanted to take a nap before the race. The rabbit agreed and fell asleep.

While the rabbit was sleeping, the turtle quietly snuck past him and won the race. The other animals were surprised and asked the turtle how he did it. The turtle said, "I may not be the fastest, but I am clever and found a way to win."

The moral of the story is that sometimes being clever can help you succeed, even if you are not the strongest or fastest.

Tim and Kim are twins. They like to play with their toys. But they do not like to share. One day, they have a big fight over a red car.

"Give me the car! It is mine!" Tim says.

"No, it is mine! I saw it first!" Kim says.

They pull and tug on the car. They are very rude to each other. They make a lot of noise.

Mom hears them and comes to their room. She is not happy.

"What are you doing? Why are you fighting?" Mom asks.

They both point at the car and say, "It is mine!"

Mom shakes her head. She has an idea.

"OK, if you both want the car so much, why don't you sell it?" Mom says.

"Sell it?" Tim and Kim say.

"Yes, sell it. You can use the money to buy something else. Maybe something you both like. Or something you can share." Mom says.

Tim and Kim think about it. They look at the car. They look at each other.

"OK, let's sell it." Tim says.

"OK, let's sell it." Kim says.

They hug and say sorry. They are not rude anymore.

They take the car to the yard. They make a sign that says, "Car for sale. Five cents."

Soon, a boy comes by. He sees the car. He likes it.

"Can I buy the car?" He asks.

"Yes, you can. It is five cents." Tim says.

The boy gives them a nickel. He takes the car. He is happy.

Tim and Kim are happy too. They have five cents. They go to the store. They buy a big bag of candy. They share it.

They also buy a band-aid. They put it on Kim's toe. She hurt it when she pulled the car. It feels better.

They go home. They thank Mom for her idea. They play with their other toys. They share them. They are nice to each other. They are happy.

Tim and Mia like to play in the park. They see many things in the park: trees, flowers, birds, dogs, and people. One day, they see something new in the park: a big crane. The crane is yellow and tall. It has a long arm and a hook. It can lift and move heavy things.

"Wow, look at the crane!" Tim says. "It is so big and strong. I want to see it closer."

"Me too," Mia says. "But be careful. The crane might be loud and scary."

They run to the crane. They see a man in a small house on the crane. He has a helmet and a radio. He can control the crane with buttons and levers.

"Hello, Mr. Crane Man!" Tim shouts. "Can you show us how the crane works?"

The man hears Tim and Mia. He smiles and waves. He says, "Hello, kids. Do you like the crane?"

"Yes, we do!" Tim and Mia say.

"Well, I can show you something. Watch this." The man pushes a button. The crane arm moves up and down. It makes a whirring sound. The hook swings in the air.

"Wow, that is cool!" Tim says.

The man pushes another button. The crane arm bends and straightens. It looks like it is bowing.

"Look, the crane is bowing!" Mia says.

The man says, "Yes, it is. The crane is saying hello to you. Can you bow back?"

Tim and Mia nod. They bend their knees and lower their heads. They bow to the crane.

The crane makes a beeping sound. It sounds like it is happy.

The man says, "Good job, kids. You are very polite. The crane likes you."

Tim and Mia smile. They feel proud and happy. They say, "Thank you, Mr. Crane Man. You are very nice. The crane is very nice too."

The man says, "You are welcome, kids. You are very brave and curious. Do you want to see more?"

Tim and Mia say, "Yes, please!"

The man says, "OK, then. Follow me. I will show you the other cranes and machines in the park. They are all working on a new playground for you and your friends."

Tim and Mia clap their hands. They are excited. They follow the man to see the other cranes and machines. They learn many new things. They have a lot of fun. They are not shy anymore. They make a new friend. His name is Mr. Crane Man.

Mum put a dessert on the kitchen table. It was a yummy-looking cake!

Dad came into the kitchen. He smiled and said, "Are you ready to wrap the dessert?"

Mum said, "Yes, let's be careful!".

The duo carefully started to wrap the dessert. They used a lot of nice coloured paper.

When they were finished, the dessert was all wrapped up and looked beautiful.

Mum said, "I can't wait to eat it!"

Dad laughed and said, "Me too!"

Once upon a time, there were two frogs. One of them was very weak, but the other frog chose to ignore it. The weak frog croaked and hopped up to the other frog and asked, "Why won't you talk to me?" But the other frog just ignored it.

The weak frog tried and tried to get the other frog's attention, but he kept on ignoring it. So, the weak frog hopped away sadly.

The next day, the weak frog came back and said to the other frog, "Please talk to me, I'm lonely." But again, the other frog ignored the weak frog.

The weak frog felt so upset and angry. It hopped around the other frog and shouted, "Why won't you talk to me?!" But still, the other frog ignored it.

The weak frog was so heartbroken. It had to find someone else to talk to, someone who would listen and care.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Sam. He was 3 years old and he liked to explore.

One sunny day, Sam was playing in the garden when he saw a big, green door. He had never seen a door like this before and he walked over to it. He pushed and pulled but the door wouldn't budge. He then leaned against the door with all his might and suddenly it opened.

Sam stepped inside and he saw a room filled with toys and books. All the things were so colorful and exciting. He couldn't believe his luck! There was something to explore in every corner of the room.

Then he saw a big, boring chair in the far corner. Sam was disappointed that it was so boring. He asked it, "Why are you so boring? I want to have fun!"

The chair chuckled and said, "Well, I have been leaning against this wall for so long that all the fun has been sucked out of me."

Sam understood, so he asked the chair to join in the fun. Sure enough, with a few minutes, the chair was laughing and having a great time.

Sam, the chair and the door - they all had a great day full of fun and laughter!

Jade and her mom were walking near the beach. The beach was filled with beautiful things, like little boats and pretty shells. Suddenly, Jade's mom started to get worried. She shouted, "Where is the dog? He disappeared!"

Jade was scared. She asked, "Mommy, did the dog go away?" Her mom said, "He is probably running around the beach. Let's go look for him."

They searched around the beach, but they couldn't find the dog. Then, Jade saw something moving near the sand. It was the dog! He had been hiding behind a tree the whole time. "Come here!" shouted Jade, and the dog ran to her and cuddled in her arms. Jade was happy.

Jade's mom said, "We were so scared, but now we found the dog. Let's go home now." As they started walking, Jade looked back towards the beach. She said goodbye to the beach and waved to it. Then, they started walking back home.

Steve was a leopard who was very nosy. He always wanted to peek into windows and see what was happening inside. One day he was feeling very sleepy and he decided to take a nap. He laid down under a shady tree and before he knew it, he was fast asleep.

Soon, Steve started to yawn. He opened his mouth wide and took a big, deep yawn. He felt much better now and decided to look around. All of a sudden, he spotted something from the corner of his eye. "What is that?" he thought.

He quietly snuck up to the window and peeked inside. There, he saw a little boy having his afternoon nap. He couldn't believe his eyes - the little boy was yawning just like him! He watched in amazement as the boy stretched out his arms and yawned again.

Steve smiled and thought to himself, "It looks like I'm not the only nosy leopard taking naps around here!"

Once upon a time, there were two special friends. One was named Ben and the other was named Bob. Together, they shared lots of wonderful things.

One day, Ben spotted something very incredible outside. "Bob, look! What is that?" asked Ben.

Bob looked outside and smiled. "That's a big, bright rainbow! It's so wondrous," said Bob excitedly.

Ben smiled too. "Let's share this amazing sight with everyone else," he said.

So the two friends brought their friends outside to share the incredible rainbow. Everyone gasped in wonder when they saw it. It was truly amazing.

The friends clapped and cheered. They were so happy to share the incredible wonder of the rainbow.

One day, Sam and Lily went to school. They liked school because they learned new things and played with their friends. But today, something was different. When they got to their classroom, the door was closed and the lights were off. It was very dark inside.

"Where is our teacher?" Sam asked.

"I don't know. Maybe she is late," Lily said.

They knocked on the door, but no one answered. They tried to open the door, but it was locked. They felt scared and confused.

"Maybe we should go to another classroom and ask for help," Sam suggested.

"OK, let's go," Lily agreed.

They walked down the hall, looking for another teacher or a friend. But they saw no one. All the other classrooms were also closed and dark. The school was very quiet and empty.

"Where is everyone?" Lily questioned.

"I don't know. Maybe there is a fire drill or something," Sam guessed.

They heard a loud noise from the end of the hall. It sounded like a bell or a siren. They ran towards the noise, hoping to find someone. But when they got there, they saw a big red sign that said "EXIT". They pushed the door open and went outside.

They saw a lot of people on the playground. They saw their teacher, their friends, and other teachers. They also saw some fire trucks and firefighters. They smelled smoke and saw some flames coming from a window.

"What is going on?" Sam and Lily asked.

Their teacher hugged them and said, "I'm so glad you are safe. There was a fire in the school. We had to evacuate quickly. I'm sorry I couldn't find you in the classroom. I thought you were with the others."

Sam and Lily felt relieved and happy. They were not alone anymore. They joined their friends and watched the firefighters put out the fire. They learned a new word: "evacuate". They also learned a new lesson: "always follow the teacher's instructions". They hoped the fire would not damage their classroom too much. They wanted to go back to school soon.

Once there were two friends, Ben and Sam. They both loved to laugh and play. Every day they would make each other laugh and that made them both very happy.

One day they were walking in the forest. Ben saw something shiny on the ground. It was a piece of money! He was so lucky! Ben and Sam laughed a lot and it made them even happier.

They decided to spend the money on ice cream. They both enjoyed it a lot and it made them even more happy.

After finishing their ice cream, Ben and Sam hugged each other and laughed until their stomachs hurt. They were so lucky that day! They laughed and hugged again and went home feeling very content.

Once there was a sleepy bear who lived in a deep forest.He saw a wrap in the forest and he wanted to explore it. He tried to walk forward but his feet got stuck. He didn't know what to do, so he tried to reverse his steps. This time he managed to move forward and he found himself inside the wrap.

The bear made his way inside the wrap, exploring all the nooks and crannies. At the end of the wrap there was a pile of leaves and he was determined to get to them. He kept on trying to move forward, but he couldn't. Then he tried reversing and it worked! He made it to the pile of leaves and he could smell something sweet. He was so happy and he felt so cozy in his wrap. He fell asleep in the pile of leaves, dreaming of all the new places he could explore.

Once there was a regular house with two people living in it. One day, something special appeared. A big box with a chessboard inside, with small figures made of wood. The two people were very excited and started playing the game together.

They moved the figures around the board, taking turns. The game was so much fun, they played it all day long. They smiled and laughed every time one of them put the other one in checkmate.

When it was time to go to bed, they put the chessboard and the figures away. But they knew they'd play again the next day, because they loved the game.

The chess appeared every day and it was always the same. The two people were regulars at the game now, and they remained happy playing it together.

Once upon a time, there was a silly cat named Tom. Tom loved to go to the mall with his friend, a little girl named Sue. They liked to look at the toys and eat ice cream together.

One day, Tom and Sue went to the mall to play. They saw a big ball and wanted to play with it. But the ball was too high for them to reach. They tried to jump, but they could not get it.

Tom had an idea. He asked Sue to stand on his back. Sue managed to reach the ball. They were very happy and played with the ball all day at the mall. They had a lot of fun and laughed a lot.

Once, there was a boy. He was three years old. He liked to hang out with his friends.

One day, he went to the library to find a story. He looked around but he couldn't find one. He was very sad.

Then he saw a big book. It had pretty pictures and it looked very interesting. He looked inside and saw a story. It was a story about a magical bird.

He read the story and found it very useful. He loved the story and he wanted to hang it on the wall so he could see it every day.

The boy was so happy. He had found a useful story that he could hang on the wall. The end.

One day, a little boy named Tim was very excited. His mom was going to prepare a big cake for his birthday party. Tim wanted to help his mom, so he went to the kitchen. He saw a long cable on the floor and thought it was a toy.

"Mom, can I play with this?" Tim asked, holding the cable. "No, Tim. That is not a toy. It is for the mixer. Now, let's prepare the cake," his mom said. Tim was sad he couldn't play with the cable, but he was happy to help with the cake.

They started to prepare the cake. Tim poured the flour and sugar into the big bowl. His mom added the eggs and milk. But Tim was still thinking about the cable. When his mom was not looking, he took the cable and started to play with it.

Suddenly, the cable got stuck in the mixer. The mixer turned on and made a big mess. The cake mix went everywhere! Tim's mom was very upset. "Oh no, Tim! I told you not to play with the cable. Now we can't have a cake for your birthday party."

Tim felt very bad. He learned that he should always listen to his mom.

Once upon a time there was a monster who was mean and wanted to wreck things. He wanted to make things bad. He found a bottle of poison and opened it. He poured the poison all over the place but it didn't do anything. He got angry and destroyed everything he could find. Soon, the poison made the ground and sky so bad that it hurt everyone. Everybody was scared and sad. They tried to fix the damage by cleaning up, but it was too late. The monster had destroyed everything.

Lila and Ben were playing in the park. They saw a big statue of a man with a spear. The spear was shiny and long.

"Look, a spear!" Ben said. "I want to touch it."

"No, Ben, don't touch it!" Lila said. "It is not for us. It is for the statue."

But Ben did not listen. He ran to the statue and reached for the spear. He touched it with his hand.

Suddenly, a loud voice shouted. "Hey, you! Get away from my spear!"

Lila and Ben looked up. The statue was angry. It was not a statue. It was a real man. He was dressed like a soldier. He had a helmet and a shield.

"Who are you?" the man asked. "Why are you touching my spear?"

Lila and Ben were scared. They did not know who the man was. They did not know why he had a spear.

"We are sorry, sir," Lila said. "We were just playing. We did not mean to touch your spear."

The man looked at them. He saw that they were just children. He saw that they were sorry.

He sighed. He put down his spear. He smiled.

"It's okay, kids," he said. "I am not angry. I am just a reenactor. I pretend to be a soldier from the past. This is not a real spear. It is a toy."

He showed them his spear. He let them touch it gently.

Lila and Ben were curious. They asked him questions. He told them stories. He taught them about history.

Lila and Ben were happy. They learned something new. They made a new friend. They thanked the man for his spear. They said goodbye.

They ran back to their mom. They told her what happened. She was surprised. She was glad.

She hugged them. She said, "You are very brave and polite. I am proud of you."

Mama was sad and miserable, she didn't like to leave home. But one day, Papa encouraged her to go with him. He said they would go find a special spear. Mama didn't understand why they needed a spear, but she trusted Papa.

So they set off. Mama and Papa walked a long way until they got to a big river. On the other side, Papa showed Mama that there was a big tree with a special spear hanging from its branches. Mama was very excited.

When the two of them crossed the river, Mama was scared, but Papa encouraged her to be brave. Eventually, Mama made it to the other side of the river. Papa handed her the spear, and it was so beautiful. They smiled at each other, and Mama was no longer feeling so miserable.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy lion. He was always curious about the world and often roamed around. One day, he stumbled upon a big broccoli! He was so excited and wanted to take a bite, so he opened his big mouth and roared with excitement.

Just then, a little rabbit hopped by. The nosy lion asked, "What's that? The little rabbit replied, "That's broccoli! It's yummy and crunchy! The nosy lion couldn't believe it. He exclaimed, "No way!

The nosy lion decided to take a bite of the broccoli. He took a crunchy bite and it tasted delicious! He was so happy. He roared in delight and thanked the little rabbit for showing him the yummy broccoli. From then on, he always knew to look out for tasty broccoli!

Once upon a time, there was a hot party. All of the friends were there and they were all wearing fun costumes. Everyone was so excited to dress up and show off their outfits!

The friends were having so much fun and then suddenly, the doorbell rang. Everyone was curious to see who was at the door.

When they opened the door, they saw a little girl. She was wearing a cute pink costume, with a sparkly wand in her hand and a sparkly crown on her head.

The friends at the party were amazed! They accepted the little girl into the party and she was so happy! She danced and sang and laughed with her new friends.

At the end of the night, everyone hugged the little girl goodbye and she went home with a big smile on her face. She had such a fun time and was so happy to be accepted.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved going to the beach. She loved playing in the sand and swimming in the ocean. One day, her friend Tommy recommended a new beach to her. He said it was incredible and had the best waves for surfing.

Excited to try out the new beach, Lily and her family packed their bags and headed out. When they arrived, Lily was amazed at how beautiful the beach was. She ran to the water and started swimming. But soon, she noticed something strange. The waves were getting bigger and bigger, and the water was pulling her out to sea.

Lily's parents tried to save her, but the waves were too strong. In the end, Lily was lost to the ocean. The moral of the story is that sometimes, even if something seems incredible, it can be dangerous. It's important to listen to warnings and be careful.

One day, a little girl called Jenny was playing in her garden. She saw her dad polishing his car and she wanted to help. She asked, "Dad, can I help you polish the car?

Her dad smiled and said, "Yes of course you can, Jenny.

Jenny found a soft cloth and began to polish the car. She sang a gentle tune as she worked.

Once she was done, Jenny felt very proud. She asked, "What else should I polish, Dad?

Her dad thought for a moment, then said, "How about those vegetables in the kitchen? Can you polish them?

Jenny went to the kitchen and found a mild vegetable, like a cucumber. She grabbed a cloth and began to polish it until it shone brightly.

Once she was finished, Jenny smiled. She had polished the vegetable and now it looked so delicious.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. One day, Timmy was eating some spicy chips and he accidentally spilled some on his shirt. His mom told him to come to the kitchen for a towel to clean it up.

Timmy was feeling lazy and didn't want to get up, so he ignored his mom's request. But the spicy chips made his shirt itchy, so he finally got up and went to the kitchen to get the towel.

His mom told him that it's important to listen and follow instructions, even if we don't feel like it. Timmy learned that it's better to take care of things right away instead of waiting and making things worse. From that day on, Timmy always listened to his mom and did what he was told.

Tom and Anna liked to play in their big wardrobe. It had many clothes and hats and shoes that they could try on. Sometimes they pretended to be kings and queens, or pirates and fairies, or doctors and teachers.

One day, they found a big red cape in the wardrobe. It was soft and shiny and had a golden clasp. Tom put it on and said, "Look, Anna, I am a superhero! I can soar in the sky and save the world!"

Anna laughed and said, "You are silly, Tom. You cannot soar in the sky. You need wings or a plane or a rocket to do that."

Tom said, "No, I don't. I just need to believe and run fast and jump high. Watch me!" He ran to the window and opened it. He felt the wind in his hair and the sun on his face. He said, "Here I go!" and jumped out of the window.

Anna screamed and ran to the window. She was scared and worried. She looked down and saw Tom lying on the grass. He was not moving. She cried and shouted, "Tom, Tom, are you okay? Please wake up!"

A wise old owl was sitting on a branch near the window. He saw what happened and flew down to Tom. He said, "Hoo, hoo, little boy, what were you thinking? You cannot soar in the sky with a cape. You are not a superhero. You are a human. Humans need to be careful and listen to their sisters. They are wise and love you."

Tom opened his eyes and groaned. He felt pain in his legs and arms and head. He saw the owl and said, "Who are you? Are you a superhero?"

The owl said, "No, I am not a superhero. I am an owl. I can soar in the sky with my wings. But I do not save the world. I just watch and learn and teach. You should do the same."

Tom said, "I am sorry, owl. I was silly and foolish. I wanted to soar in the sky and have fun. But I did not think and I did not listen. I hurt myself and I scared Anna. I love Anna. She is wise and kind."

The owl said, "Hoo, hoo, that is good, little boy. You learned a lesson today. You should always be careful and listen to your sister. She loves you too. Now, let me help you. I will call your mom and dad. They will come and take you to the doctor. He will make you feel better. Then you can play in the wardrobe again. But remember, no more jumping out of the window. Okay?"

Tom said, "Okay, owl. Thank you for helping me. You are wise and nice. Can I be your friend?"

The owl said, "Hoo, hoo, of course, little boy. You can be my friend. And I will be yours. But only if you promise to be careful and listen to your sister. Deal?"

Tom said, "Deal, owl. Deal." He smiled and hugged the owl. Anna ran down the stairs and hugged Tom too. She was happy and relieved. She said, "I love you, Tom. You are my brother. And you are my friend."

The owl said, "Hoo, hoo, what a happy ending. You are a lucky family. You have each other. And you have me. Now, let's go and find your mom and dad. They will be worried too. Come on, let's go." He flapped his wings and soared in the sky. Tom and Anna followed him. They held hands and looked up. They saw the owl and the cape and the sun. They felt the wind and the grass and the love. They were happy and grateful. They learned a lesson today. They learned to be careful and listen and love. And they learned to soar in their hearts.
