He started to tear off the grass and there it was. A shiny coin. He picked it up and showed it to his friend. They were so excited. They started to hug and jump around in joy. They had never seen anything like it before. They showed the coin to their parents and told them about the unusual discovery. That day, the children had found an unexpected treasure.

Once upon a time, there was a helpless pit. It was deep and dark, and no one ever came near it. One day, there was a soft noise and something started to happen. A tiny flower had begun to bloom, right in the darkest corner of the pit. It was the most beautiful thing the pit had ever seen. The flower shone and brought light and happiness to the helpless pit. Everyone was amazed and happy, but the flower didn't stay forever. Soon, the petals began to fall off and the flower disappeared. But the pit was still happy because the flower had made it bloom.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a smart robot named Rob. Rob belonged to a family who lived near a big metal factory. One day, Rob saw flames coming from the factory and he knew that it could be dangerous.

Rob said to his family, "Let's go and prevent the fire from spreading. His family was very scared, but they agreed.

They all went together to the factory. When they got there, they quickly took out a big metal bucket of water. Rob was very smart and knew what to do, so he used the water to put out the flames.

Everyone was so happy that they were able to prevent the fire from spreading. Rob was very proud of himself too. From that day on, everyone in the family always looked to Rob for help.

One day, a little boy named Tom went to the park with his mom. He saw some ducks by the pond and wanted to feed them. He had some bread in his bag and broke it into small pieces. He threw some bread to the ducks and they came closer.

Tom liked the ducks. They had soft feathers and funny beaks. He wanted to touch one. He reached out his hand and tried to pet a duck. But the duck did not like that. The duck bit Tom's finger. Tom cried out loud. It hurt a lot.

Mom heard Tom and ran to him. She saw the duck and the bread. She hugged Tom and said, "I'm sorry, Tom. The duck was hungry and scared. You should not try to touch the ducks. They might bite you. They are not like your toys. They are wild animals."

Tom nodded. He understood. He was sorry too. He said, "I'm sorry, duck. I did not mean to make you angry. You can have the bread. I will not bother you again."

Mom smiled. She said, "That's very kind of you, Tom. Let's go and watch the ducks from a distance. They are doing something interesting. They are marching."

Tom looked and saw the ducks. They were walking in a line, one behind the other. They followed a big duck with a green head. They quacked and flapped their wings. They looked like they were having fun.

Tom forgot his pain. He laughed and clapped his hands. He said, "Look, Mom. The ducks are marching. They are not ordinary ducks. They are special ducks. They are silly ducks. I like them."

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sarah. She was three years old. One day she asked her mom, "Mama, what is blue?"

Her mom smiled and bent down on one knee to explain. "Blue is a beautiful color," she said. "It's like a bright clear sky or the deep blue sea."

Sarah thought about this for a few seconds and asked her mom, "What else is blue?"

Her mom thought for a moment and said, "Your Daddy's eyes are blue, and so is your room at the apartment."

Sarah seemed satisfied with her mom's explanation, but she had one more question. "Mama, why is blue serious?"

Her mom looked serious and said, "When things are blue, Sarah, it means they are sad. It's like when someone you love is gone. That's why it's so important to show love and kindness to one another."

Sarah nodded, understanding what her mom was trying to explain to her. She smiled and said, "I love you, Mama!" Her mom smiled back, filled with love and warmth.

Once upon a time, there was a girl who loved to spin. Every day she would spin around and around in circles until she felt dizzy and fell down.

But one day, she found a jar of glue. She smiled when she saw how it looked. The glue was so attractive with its sparkly color.

So she decided to make something fun with her glue and spinning. She put the glue on the floor and started to spin around it. The glue made a beautiful pattern from her spinning.

The girl was very happy with the pattern. She wanted to make more and show it to her friends. So she gathered more glue and kept spinning and making pretty patterns on the floor.

Reckless she didn't stop until all the glue was finished. She made the most attractive patterns she had ever seen!

Once there was a happy little boy named James. He liked to paint. One day he went to an office. There were lots of colors and he was so excited. He got out a paintbrush and started to paint. James painted a healthy sun and a green tree. He also painted a blue fish and a yellow duck.

He smiled as he looked at his paintings. He had done a very good job. Everyone in the office was very proud of James. Everyone laughed and cheered as he showed them his pictures.

Then James put down his paintbrush and took a deep breath. He was so relieved that he had finished his painting. The office filled with love and joy. All the colors looked so bright and healthy against the white walls.

James had made a masterpiece that day. Everyone agreed that his painting was beautiful.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear. The bear was very sad because he had a broken toy. He wanted to play with it, but he couldn't because it was broken.

One day, a kind girl saw the bear and his broken toy. She wanted to help the bear, so she asked him if he wanted her to repair his toy. The bear was so happy and said yes.

The girl took the toy and went home. She used some glue to repair it. It was a mild day, so she left the toy outside to dry. When it was ready, she brought it back to the bear. The bear was overjoyed and gave the girl a big hug to say thank you. From that day on, the bear and the girl were good friends.

Once there was a little girl called Spike. She loved exploring the world and she was always so happy. She looked around everywhere to see all of the special things.

One day Spike was walking and she saw a gray bird. She wanted to find out more about it so she decided to follow it. The bird was so fast and it soon flew away. Spike was upset but then she had an idea.

Spike decided to make a look-out point. She built a tower with some sticks and put it in a tree with a view of the sky. She sat inside and waited to see the gray bird.

After a while, the gray bird returned and flew around the tower so Spike could see it better. She was so delighted and waved at it with a big smile. Spike had decided to make a new friend!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to bake with her mommy. They would mix flour, sugar, and eggs together to make yummy cakes. One day, they decided to bake a green cake. Lily was so excited!

But when they started baking, they ran into a troubled problem. They didn't have any green food coloring! Lily was sad because she really wanted a green cake. But her mommy had an idea. They decided to use spinach to make the cake green. And it worked! The cake turned out so yummy and green. Lily was so happy and proud of herself for baking a green cake.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a fragile, old book in his room. He ran to his mom and said, "Mom, look at this book! Can you read me the story inside?"

Mom smiled and said, "I remember this book! It was mine when I was a little girl. Let's read the story together."

The story was about a brave dog who saved a little girl from a big, scary monster. The dog barked loudly and chased the monster away. The little girl hugged the dog and said, "Thank you for saving me!"

Tim loved the story and asked his mom to read it again. They read the story many times that day, and Tim learned that even fragile, old things can be very special.

From that day on, Tim took good care of the fragile book and read the story every night before bed. He always remembered the brave dog and the little girl, and knew that he could be brave too.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Jessie. She had lots of toys and loved to play. One day, she wanted to do something different.

So Jessie decided to try a new style. She knit a scarf and put it on. It was so long it went all the way down to her toes.

Jessie spun around and around in circles and had lots of fun. But after a while, it started to feel a bit boring. She wanted something new.

So Jessie added some sparkles and a glittery bow to her scarf. It looked really cool. She ran up and down showing the world her new scarf.

Soon, the fun came to an end. Jessie said goodbye to her fun new style and went back to playing with her toys. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl who loved yarn. She wanted to use it to make something special, but her mom said she was too small. This made the girl very sad. She hated not being able to make something with her yarn.

One day, the girl found an empty box in the garage. She decided this would be a perfect place to keep her yarn. She gathered all the yarn up and put it in the box. She liked the box because it kept her yarn safe.

At night, the girl would take her yarn out of the box and sit in the bright moonlight. She would imagine what she could make with it. She was never too tired to create something. With her yarn, she could make anything!

The girl loved her box of yarn and she never let it get too empty. She made sure to buy more yarn when she needed it. She was so happy because she could always have her special box of yarn.

Once there was a girl named Anna who loved to wear silly clothes. One day she put on a fun green skirt with yellow stripes. She twirled and danced around in the skirt and it looked so silly!

Anna decided to go outside and show her skirt to her friends. When she got to the park she waved at her friends, "Look at my skirt!" she said.

All her friends started to laugh, but then one boy named Sam said, "Why are you wearing that silly skirt?"

Anna stood up tall and said, "I like my skirt and I want to show it off!"

Sam smiled and said, "I think your skirt is nice. You look very happy in it."

Anna smiled and twirled around again in her skirt, she knew she looked silly but she didn't care. She was proud of her skirt and happy to show it off.

Once upon a time, there was a big fun fair in the park. A little girl named Lily and her dog, Bo, went to the fair. They saw a game where you could win a prize. Lily wanted to play the game, but she didn't know how. Bo looked at the game and wagged his tail.

A kind man saw Lily and said, "Do you want to play? I can help you." Lily was happy and said, "Yes, please!" The man told Lily to throw the ball and try to hit the bottles. Lily threw the ball and knocked down all the bottles. The man smiled and said, "Great job! You win a prize."

Lily looked at the prizes and saw a big, fluffy bear. She wanted the bear, but she was too small to reach it. Bo saw the bear too and wanted to help Lily. He jumped up and grabbed the bear with his mouth. Lily was so happy and hugged Bo. They went home with their new friend, the big fluffy bear, and had a great day at the fun fair.

Tom was sick. He had a bad cough and a sore throat. His mom said he had to take medicine to feel better. But Tom did not like medicine. It was bitter and yucky.

"Please, Tom, drink your medicine. It will help you stop coughing," his mom said. She had a spoon with some syrup in it. She tried to put it in Tom's mouth, but he turned his head away.

"No, no, no! I don't want medicine! It's gross!" Tom said. He hid under his blanket.

His mom sighed. She had an idea. She went to the kitchen and got some juice and a bowl. She poured some juice and some medicine in the bowl. She took a whisk and started to mix them together.

"Tom, look what I have for you. It's a special drink. It's sweet and yummy. And it will make you feel better, too," she said. She came back to Tom's room with the bowl and a straw.

Tom peeked out from his blanket. He saw the bowl. It was orange and bubbly. He smelled the drink. It was fruity and nice. He was curious.

"What is it, mom?" he asked.

"It's a magic drink. It has juice and medicine in it. But you can't taste the medicine, because I mixed it very well. Do you want to try it?" she asked.

Tom nodded. He took the straw and sipped the drink. It was sweet and yummy. He did not taste the medicine at all. He drank some more. He felt his throat getting less sore. He smiled.

"Thank you, mom. This drink is good. I like it," he said.

His mom smiled, too. She hugged him and kissed his forehead.

"You're welcome, Tom. I'm glad you like it. You're a brave boy. Now, rest and get well soon," she said.

She tucked him in and turned off the light. She left the room, but not before she whispered, "I love you, Tom."

Tom closed his eyes and fell asleep. He dreamed of orange bubbles and magic drinks. He did not cough anymore. He felt better. He loved his mom.

Once there was a little girl who loved balloons. She wished everyday that she could have one of her own.

One night, the little girl went to sleep and had the most wonderful dream. She dreamt of floating away in a big, round balloon,smiling in the breeze. She felt so happy and free.

The little girl woke up with a smile on her face. She looked around the room and noticed something extraordinary - a balloon floating near the window! She jumped out of bed and walked over to take a closer look.

"Where did you come from?" she asked the balloon.

"Someone gave me to you as a gift," it replied. "I am here to help you make all your dreams come true!"

The little girl smiled, then thanked the balloon for being so useful. She hugged it tightly and felt excited to go explore the world in her new balloon!

One day, Little Jimmy wanted to play a game. He had a special map that was green. He looked at the map and knew he had to use it to find his way.

Jimmy followed the path on the map, but it led him to a strange place. He didn't know where he was and he began to feel scared. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise like a monster.

Jimmy was brave and shouted, "Who's there?" Out of the shadows came a big, green monster with four eyes. The monster was scary, but Jimmy didn't give up. He knew he had to use the map to get back home.

Jimmy looked at the map and used it to find his way back. So he followed the green map and made it safely back home.

He was so proud of himself for being brave and using the map to help him get back home. Even though he had been scared, he had used the map to make it back safe and sound.

Once upon a time there was a clever little boy named Paul. Every day, Paul and his mom went for walks in the forest. Paul loved to go on walks because he could collect lots of things and carry them around in his pocket.

One day while he was walking, Paul noticed an old blanket in a pile of leaves. He liked the blanket because it was so bright and colorful. Paul looked up at his mom and said, "Can I please collect the blanket?"

His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Paul. You are so intelligent to find such a nice blanket!"

Paul was so happy. He quickly put the blanket in his pocket and went home. From then on, Paul always carried his blanket with him wherever he went and he even used it as a special pillow to sleep with at night.

The end.

Tim and Mia like to play in the park. They see a big slide with a zip at the end. They want to try it. They climb up the steps and sit on the slide. They hold hands and say "ready, set, go!"

They slide down very fast. They feel the wind on their faces. They laugh and scream. They reach the end of the slide and the zip pulls them up in the air. They fly like birds for a moment. They feel happy and excited.

But then they see a fat cat on the zip. The cat is scared and angry. It hisses and scratches. It wants to get off the zip. It makes the zip shake and wobble. Tim and Mia lose their balance. They fall off the zip and land on the grass. They are not hurt, but they are surprised and sad.

The cat runs away from the zip. It finds a tree and climbs up. It looks down at Tim and Mia. It thinks they are silly and mean. It does not want to play with them.

Tim and Mia get up and brush off the grass. They look at the zip and the slide. They do not want to try them again. They decide to play with the swings and the sandbox. They think they are more fun and safe. They do not like the fat cat. They hope it stays in the tree.

Once upon a time, there was a patient bear who needed to organize a party for her friends.

The bear went to the park with her picnic basket and put down a blanket. She then took out her lunch and started to prepare the food.

Suddenly, a 3 year old girl called out to the bear, "What are you doing?"

The bear smiled, "I'm organizing a party for my friends!"

The girl was so excited and asked, "Can I come, too?"

The bear said "Of course, you can join us! You just need to be patient until we're all done setting up."

So the little girl waited, and when the bear was finally finished organizing the party, they all had a wonderful time eating and playing together.

Ben and Mia were playing in the garden. They liked to pretend they were spies on a secret mission. They had a big box that was their spy base. They had a supply of snacks, toys and books inside the box. They also had a key to lock the box and keep it safe.

One day, a new boy moved in next door. His name was Sam. He saw Ben and Mia playing and wanted to join them. He knocked on the box and said, "Hello, can I play with you?"

Ben and Mia looked at each other. They did not know Sam. They did not know if he was a good spy or a bad spy. They decided to test him. Mia said, "You can play with us, but you have to pass a spy challenge first. You have to find the key to our spy base. We hid it somewhere in the garden. You have ten minutes. Go!"

Sam was excited. He liked spy games. He ran around the garden, looking for the key. He looked under the flowers, behind the rocks, in the birdhouse. He could not find the key. He was running out of time. He started to feel sad and frustrated.

Ben and Mia watched him from the box. They felt sorry for him. They knew the key was very hard to find. They had buried it under the sandpit. They had been very patient and clever. They decided to give Sam a hint. Ben said, "Sam, the key is near something that you can dig with."

Sam heard Ben's voice. He looked around. He saw the sandpit. He ran to it. He grabbed a shovel and started to dig. He dug and dug until he felt something metal. He pulled it out. It was the key! He was so happy. He ran to the box and unlocked it. He opened it and saw Ben and Mia smiling at him. They said, "Well done, Sam! You passed the spy challenge. You are a good spy. You can play with us."

Sam smiled back. He said, "Thank you, Ben and Mia. You are good spies too. You are also very kind and patient. Can we be friends?"

Ben and Mia nodded. They said, "Yes, we can be friends. Welcome to our spy team."

They invited Sam to join them in the box. They shared their supply of snacks, toys and books. They had fun playing spy games together. They became good friends.

Mama and Zac were walking in the park. Zac wanted to have some fun.
Mama: "Let's go spray some water from the port, Zac!"

Zac: "Yah, Mama! That will be fun."

So Mama picked up Zac and brought him to the port. There was a big hosepipe attached to the port. Mama unscrewed the cap and water began to come out.

Mama: "It's going to get hot, Zac, so let's spray the water at each other."

Zac and Mama were laughing as they spent a lot of time spraying water at each other. The water felt really cool and refreshing on the hot day. Zac had so much fun with Mama.

Mama: "Come on, Zac, it's time to go home."

So they went home and Zac wasn't tired at all!

Once upon a time there was a chubby girl. She was sad because she was suffering. Everywhere she went, she felt like she didn't belong. She wanted to find someone to help her suffer less.

One day the girl was walking in the forest, when she heard a noise. It sounded like singing! She followed the sound and found a wee fairy. The girl was so excited to find the fairy.

The fairy was happy to meet the girl and asked what was wrong. The girl told the fairy that she was suffering. The fairy was sad and wanted to help. She waved her wand and gave the girl a hug.

The fairy had magic dust she sprinkled on the girl. Instantly, the girl felt much better! She wasn't suffering any more! The fairy smiled and said, "You are brave, sweet girl". The girl hugged the fairy one more time before saying goodbye.

The girl went back home and she was no longer sad. She will always be grateful to the fairy for making her feel better.

Once upon a time, there was a smart girl who liked to play in the garden with her number. Every day, she would go out and use her spade to dig and do wonderful things in the garden.

One day, the girl had an idea. She used her number and her spade to dig and prepare a special bed for a surprise. She worked hard and carefully, and after her hard work was done, she was pleased.

The next day, the girl was excited when she went outside, for she knew something special was going to happen. She was right! The girl looked in the special bed and saw that it had grown two bright red flowers! She was so pleased with her work and smiled happily. She knew that it was her hard work and her smart number that had made the special bed where the flower grew.

Once upon a time, there were two brothers. The older brother had a golden crown and he kept it locked in a special box. The younger brother wanted to play with the crown, so he asked his older brother if he could please unlock the box.

The older brother was very upset and said "No, you can't unlock the box! It's only for grownups".

The younger brother was sad but he asked again. "Please! I promise I'll be careful," he said.

The older brother thought for a moment before he said, "Okay. You can have the crown, but you have to give it back when you're done".

The younger brother happily unlocked the box and put on the golden crown. He laughed and jumped around with joy. He had so much fun wearing the crown that he forgot to give it back when he was done!

Luckily, his older brother was so kind that he didn't get upset. He just laughed and said, "Let's play a game. Now, see if you can unlock the box again!"

The younger brother was thrilled to try again. He worked and worked until he finally unlocked the box and put the golden crown back.

The two brothers laughed and hugged, happy to be together. They had so much fun playing and learning about unlocking things.

Mama and Sammy went to the store. Mama said they were going to buy a watch. Sammy was very worried. They went to the counter and bought the watch. It was pink and sparkly. Sammy was very happy and felt better.

Mama put the watch on Sammy's wrist. It was too big, but she said it would fit soon. Sammy was excited to wear the watch.

The next day, Sammy went to the playground. He loved playing, but he was worried he would lose the watch. He kept checking it to make sure it was still on.

Mama laughed and said Sammy didn't need to worry. She said the watch was his to keep and reminded him to be careful. Sammy smiled and hugged Mama. Now, he could keep having fun with his new watch!

Once upon a time there was a pirate. He was very tall and always wore a big black hat. One day the pirate decided to go on a journey. He sailed away in his big boat until he reached a big island.

The pirate saw something that made him very excited - it was a big treasure chest! He couldn't wait to open it. He opened it and found lots of gold inside. He tried to carry it back to his boat, but it was heavy.

Then the pirate had an idea. He stretched his arms and legs in all directions to make himself bigger. Now he was big enough to carry the treasure! He smiled and was happy.

On his way back, the pirate felt guilty. He thought he should share the treasure with everyone. So, he started taking some for himself and then giving some away.

Eventually, the pirate went back to his boat with a small pile of gold. He was happy that he had shared and his journey was over. He knew it was a good thing to be nice and share. The end!

One day, a little boy named Tim went to the park with his mom. They saw a big tree with a tip that touched the sky. Tim wanted to climb the tree, but his mom said it was too difficult for him.

While they were walking, Tim found an apple on the ground. It was red and shiny, but it had a spot that was rot. "Mom, can I eat this apple?" Tim asked. "No, Tim," said his mom, "it's not good to eat because it has rot."

Tim was sad, but then he saw a squirrel. The squirrel ran up the tree and came back down with a fresh apple. The squirrel gave the apple to Tim, and he was very happy. Tim thanked the squirrel and shared the apple with his mom. They had a great day at the park.

Once there was a little girl named Sarah. She was three years old and loved books. Every day she would go to the library to look at the books.

One day she noticed that the shelves were empty. She went to the librarian and asked why the shelves were empty.

The librarian smiled and said, "I will provide you with a special book."

When the librarian handed Sarah the book, Sarah was very happy. She opened the book and it was filled with pictures of animals and stories. Sarah was so excited, she stayed at the library all day looking at the book.

In the end, Sarah left the library with a big smile on her face. She had a special book that the librarian provided her with and she was very happy.

One day, a boy and a girl went to play near a big river. They saw a raft near the water. The boy and girl were very excited. They wanted to ride the raft and have fun.

They jumped on the raft and started to go down the river. The water was wild and fast. The boy and girl laughed and screamed as they went down the river. They were having a great time.

But then, the raft hit a big rock. The raft broke and the boy and girl fell into the water. They were scared and cried for help. Sadly, no one was there to help them, and they could not swim back to the land.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily and her best friend, a teddy bear named Teddy, went for a walk in the park. They played on the swings and climbed on the jungle gym. Suddenly, the sky turned dark and it started to rain.

Lily and Teddy ran to a nearby tree to take shelter from the rain. But the tree was rough and scratchy, and it hurt their skin. Lily said, "This tree is too rough. We need to find a better place to hide."

Teddy replied, "I know a place. Let's call my friend, Mr. Rabbit. He has a cozy burrow where we can stay dry."

Lily and Teddy called Mr. Rabbit on the phone and he invited them to his burrow. They went inside and had a warm cup of tea with Mr. Rabbit. They were safe and happy, even though it was dark and stormy outside.

The moral of the story is that when you have a problem, you should call a friend for help. And sometimes, the best solution is not the most obvious one.

One day, a little girl named Sarah was playing with her toys at the table. She had an original toy car and an airplane made of wood. She was having lots of fun pretending to fly the airplane around the room. Suddenly, she heard a noise coming from outside. She looked out the window and saw a bright red car zipping along the street! It was so shiny and fast that Sarah leapt up from the table and ran outside to get a better look. She could not believe how quickly it was moving! Sarah watched as the car zipped away in the distance. She wished that she could race around in a speedy car like that one. As she turned back to go inside, Sarah looked down and saw that her special original toy car was still there on the table. She smiled and picked it up to play with. Sarah could imagine her toy car zipping around town, just like the red car she had seen outside.

Ben and Lily are squirrels who live in a big tree. They like to play and find nuts to eat. One day, they see a shiny acorn on the ground. It is big and brown and looks very yummy.

"Look, Lily, an acorn!" Ben says. He runs to get it.

"No, Ben, it is mine!" Lily says. She runs faster and grabs the acorn.

They both hold the acorn and pull hard. They fight and shout and make a lot of noise.

"Let go, Lily, it is mine!"

"Let go, Ben, it is mine!"

They are so busy fighting that they do not see a big dog coming. The dog is hungry and angry. He sees the squirrels and the acorn. He barks and runs to catch them.

Ben and Lily hear the dog and get very scared. They are anxious and do not know what to do. They drop the acorn and run up the tree. They hide in a hole and hope the dog will go away.

The dog sniffs the acorn and eats it. He is happy and leaves. Ben and Lily peek out of the hole and see the acorn is gone. They feel sad and sorry.

"Ben, I am sorry I fought with you," Lily says.

"Lily, I am sorry I fought with you," Ben says.

They hug and say they are friends. They decide to share and find more acorns together. They are happy and safe.

Once upon a time, there was a tiny mouse who lived deep in the woods. Every day, he gathered tasty treats to eat, like nuts and berries.

One night, the mouse heard a loud noise outside his burrow. He looked outside and saw flashes of lightning in the sky! He was scared and tried to go back inside, but the lightning scared him too much.

The mouse's best friend was a bear who lived nearby. He saw how scared the mouse was and said, "It's okay, little mouse. That's just the sky doing somersaults. The lightning is weak and it can't get you.

The mouse felt much better after the bear talked to him. He gathered up his courage and looked up at the sky. Sure enough, the lightning was weak and very far away.

The mouse smiled at the bear and said, "Thanks for being my friend. Then they both went back to their burrows.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was very happy, and liked to play outside. One day the little girl wanted to go to the lake. She grabbed a special toy boat and ran to the lake.

When she got there she saw a row of pretty boats and she was very excited. She put her toy boat in the water and acted like it was a real boat. She moved it around, going up and down the row of boats, and it made her laugh.

The little girl had a great time, and when it was time to go home, she put her boat back in her bag with a big smile on her face. She was very happy.

Tom is a boy who likes animals. He goes to the zoo with his mom and dad. He sees many animals, like lions, monkeys, and birds. He wants to touch them, but his mom says no. She says some animals can bite or scratch.

Tom sees a big cage with a snake inside. The snake is long and green, with black spots. It looks at Tom with small eyes. Tom is not afraid. He thinks the snake is harmless. He says to his mom, "Can I rub the snake, mom? Please?"

His mom shakes her head. She says, "No, Tom. The snake is not harmless. It is venomous. That means it can make you sick with its teeth. You have to stay away from the cage."

Tom is sad. He likes the snake. He thinks it is pretty and soft. He wants to be its friend. He says, "But mom, the snake is lonely. It has no friends in the cage. Maybe it wants to rub me too."

His mom smiles. She says, "Tom, you are very kind. But the snake is not lonely. It likes to be alone. It does not want to rub you. It wants to sleep and eat. It is happy in its cage. You can look at it, but not touch it. OK?"

Tom nods. He understands. He says, "OK, mom. I will look at the snake, but not rub it. Maybe I can rub another animal. Like a rabbit or a cat. They are harmless, right?"

His mom says, "Yes, Tom. Some animals are harmless. They like to rub and play. But you have to ask first. And be gentle. OK?"

Tom says, "OK, mom. I will ask and be gentle. I love animals. And I love you, mom."

His mom says, "I love you too, Tom. Now let's go and see the other animals. Maybe we can find a rabbit or a cat for you to rub."

Once upon a time, there was a little flower who lived in a beautiful garden. Every day, the sun shone down on the flower and made it glow brightly.

The little flower's main goal was to keep growing and getting bigger, so it worked hard and was very persistent.

One day, the flower noticed a big, strong weed growing nearby. The weed was growing even faster than the flower and it was starting to block some of the sunshine from reaching the flower.

The flower said to the weed, "Please move so that I can keep glowing. Sunshine is what helps me grow."

But the weed refused to move and said, "No way! I like it here. I'm going to stay here and keep growing."

The flower was unhappy but it was determined to keep glowing, so it kept trying to get the weed to move. Even though the weed didn't listen, the flower kept being persistent and eventually, it won!

The weed moved away and the flower started to glow even brighter. It had won the battle and could keep growing.

Once upon a time there was a mild spider. The spider scurried all around the house. Everywhere it went, it found something fun to do.

But one day, the spider suddenly disappeared! No one knew where it went. Everyone was sad and confused.

The little boy in the house was especially sad. He had grown quite fond of the spider, so he wanted to find out where it had gone.

He searched high and low, in the attic and in the basement, but still he couldn't find it. He even asked his brother and sister, but they didn't know either.

Finally, the little boy went outside to look for the mild spider. He asked some nearby animals, but none of them had seen it.

Then, just when the little boy was about to give up, he saw a tiny, mild spider scurrying by. His eyes lit up, and he quickly chased after it. It had disappeared, but now it was back again!

Once there was a mommy and a daddy. Mommy and Daddy had a little girl. Her name was Lily.

One day, Lily saw a snake. She was scared! Then, Lily's Daddy saw the snake too. He said, "Don't worry, Lily. I will get rid of the snake."

Lily and Daddy talked and discussed what to do. Daddy said that the snake needed to go away. Lily agreed.

Then, Daddy got a shovel. He was really loud. The snake heard the noise and ran away. Lily and Daddy smiled.

The mommy came outside and asked, "What's going on?". Lily and Daddy discussed what had happened and then the mommy smiled, too. They were all happy that the snake was gone.

Lily said, "Let's celebrate. Let's have ice cream!" Mommy and Daddy smiled and agreed. They all had a wonderful time.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys all day long. One day, her mom asked her to help with the laundry. Lily was eager to help her mom, so she ran to the laundry room.

Her mom showed her how to put the clothes in the washing machine. Lily was so happy to help that she replied, "I love helping you, Mommy!" After the laundry was done, they hung the clothes out to dry. Lily felt proud of herself for helping her mom.

From that day on, Lily was always eager to help her mom with the laundry. She loved to reply, "Yes, Mommy!" whenever her mom asked for her help. And together they always got the laundry done in no time. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Peter. Peter wanted to reach the furniture in his bedroom. So, he got a chair to help him stand up higher.

At first, he was careful and stable as he got higher and higher. But when he tried to reach the furniture, the chair shifted and he began to fall.

He asked his mom for help. She said, "Be careful, Peter! That's not a good way to get the furniture!" But still, Peter kept trying until he eventually got the furniture.

But when Peter got the furniture, he noticed it was broken. He asked his mom, "Is it still good?"

His mom shook her head sadly and said, "No, Peter. The furniture is no longer good."

Peter was very sad. He had tried his best to reach the furniture, but it had not turned out the way he wanted.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to wear pretty dresses and twirl around. One day, Lily saw a boy named Max. Max was not very nice, he liked to tease Lily and make her feel bad.

Lily felt weak and sad when Max teased her. She didn't want to wear her pretty dress anymore because she thought Max would make fun of her.

But one day, Lily decided she didn't want to feel weak anymore. She put on her prettiest dress and twirled around in front of Max. Max tried to tease her again, but Lily didn't care. She knew she looked beautiful and that was all that mattered.

Max saw how confident Lily was and realized he was wrong to tease her. From then on, Max and Lily became friends and played together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a new crystal. It was so shiny and sparkly. Jack and Jill wanted to play with it.

Jack said, "Let me quit first!

But Jill said, "No, my turn!

So they had a big fight. They both started to cry.

Suddenly, they heard their mum call. She said, "It's okay. There's enough of the new crystal for both of you.

So they stopped crying and shared the new crystal. They both had fun playing with it together.

One day there was a big rain. Carla put on her boot and ran outside. She was so dizzy when the rain was coming down on her. She laughed and jumped around in the puddles. The rain felt so good on her face and she grinned from ear to ear.

Carla ran and splashed around for what felt like forever until the rain went away. She ran from her house and under the big tree. She smiled when the sun peeked out from behind the clouds and the rain stopped.

Carla looked down and saw that her boot was full of water. She carefully tipped it over and watched the water pour out. She looked up with a smile, happy that she had such a fun time jumping in puddles and getting wet.

Once upon a time, there was a mama and a baby. The mama was nice and the baby was so sweet. One day, the mama and the baby were walking outside when they saw a filthy emergency truck. The mama was so scared when she saw the truck. The baby, however, was so curious! He ran over to the truck and asked what it was. The mama said, "This is an emergency truck. It's there to help people in need.

The baby was excited and wanted to touch it. As the baby approached the truck, the mama said, "No, don't touch it, it's filthy. The baby was sad but the mama gave him a kiss to make him feel better.

The mama and the baby continued their walk, and when they passed the emergency truck again, the baby said, "Thanks, emergency truck, for helping people! The mama smiled and kissed the baby again. They went on their way, hand in hand.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly girl named Lily. She loved to collect things like rocks, leaves, and flowers. One day, she found a pretty notebook and decided to collect stickers to put inside. She went to the store and picked out lots of stickers with animals and stars.

Lily was so happy with her new stickers that she showed them to her friends at school. They were so impressed that they asked her to bring her notebook to show them every day. Lily loved showing off her collection and her friends loved seeing it.

Every day, Lily would add more stickers to her notebook and her collection grew bigger and bigger. She was so proud of her collection and loved sharing it with her friends. And they all lived happily ever after. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little frog named Freddy. Freddy was very brave, and he loved to hop around the pond with his friends. One day, he saw a big, green creature lurking in the bushes. Freddy knew he had to be careful, so he spoke to his friends and warned them about the creature.

The next day, Freddy and his friends were playing by the pond when the big, green creature appeared. Freddy remembered his warning and bravely stood in front of his friends. The creature tried to scare them, but Freddy spoke up and told him to go away.

The creature was surprised by Freddy's bravery and decided to leave them alone. From that day on, Freddy was known as the bravest frog in the pond, and all of his friends looked up to him. Freddy was happy that he could protect his friends and keep them safe from harm.

One day, a little girl was out walking in the park. She looked down and saw a caterpillar crawling along the ground. She was so excited that she squatted down to get a better look.

The little girl stared in fascination at the caterpillar. She examined its fine hairs and its delicate feelers. She was enchanted.

"Hello, Mr Caterpillar," said the little girl. "I think you're so fine!"

The caterpillar seemed to smile and continued to gaze up at the little girl. They were both captivated by the moment - until a gust of wind blew the caterpillar away.

The little girl watched as the caterpillar sailed away, feeling thankful to have shared the moment together. No matter how far away the caterpillar went, the little girl knew it would always have a special place in her heart.

John and his mom were walking through the park. They both saw a frog jumping in the pond. John was curious so he decided to jump in too.

John's mom said, "No, John, you can't jump in there! You might get wet and cold."

But John was determined and said, "Jump! Jump!"

John's mom said, "No, no. This is no game. This is a war and you must be careful."

John was very curious and kept looking around the pond. He saw a turtle swimming in the water. "Turtle!" he shouted.

John couldn't take his eyes off the turtle, so he decided to jump in the pond after all! His mom said, "Be careful, John! Come out of there before you get too wet!"

But John was already having fun and he kept jumping around and splashing in the water.

John's mom couldn't help but smile. She was happy to see her son having so much fun. When John was done jumping, she wrapped her warm coat around him.

John smiled and said, "Jump! Jump!" He was already thinking of his next adventure.

Once upon a time, there were two friends. One day, they were walking together and saw something very big. It was a giant! The giant was huge and both of the friends felt scared.

They decided to run and hide. But the giant saw them and started to chase them. So the two friends tried to run away but the giant was too fast. They were getting very frustrated.

Just then, a friendly bird flew down and had an idea. It said to them, "Jump in this barrel and roll away! The giant won't be able to see you!" So the two friends ran and jumped in it. They held on tight and rolled away. The giant couldn't find them.

At the end, the two friends were safe and they never saw the giant again.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Ellie. Ellie was three years old and she loved to play outside. Every day she asked her mom if she could go outside and play.

One day Ellie's mom said yes. She told Ellie that they were going to have some fun in the back yard. Ellie was excited.

When they got to the back yard, Ellies mom told her it was time to do an exercise. She said it was time to split. They split into two teams and played a game. Ellie loved it!

When both teams had their turn, Ellies mother said it was time to count up the points. It was fair because both teams had the same amount of points.

Ellie was so happy. She smiled and thanked her mom for a fun and fair exercise.

The End.

Once upon a time, there was a big elephant named Ellie. Ellie was enormous and loved to play with her friends. One day, Ellie and her friends were playing hide and seek in the jungle. Ellie was so good at hiding that nobody could find her.

After a while, Ellie's friends got worried and started looking for her. They searched high and low, but they couldn't find her anywhere. Suddenly, they heard Ellie's voice from a distance. She was stuck in a trap, and she couldn't get out.

Ellie's friends tried to help her, but they couldn't. They decided to go and get help from the other animals in the jungle. They went to the monkey and asked for his help. The monkey said, "I will help you, but we need to hurry. Ellie's condition will increase if we don't help her soon."

The monkey and Ellie's friends rushed back to the trap. When they got there, Ellie was in a lot of pain. The monkey tried to free her, but it was too late. Ellie's condition had increased too much, and she couldn't be saved. Ellie's friends were very sad, and they missed her very much. From that day on, they always made sure to point out any traps they saw in the jungle.

So, she jumped into the water and made a big splash! The fish were surprised but happy to see the new fairy. They swam around her and she laughed with joy. From that day on, the fairy loved to splash in the pond with her fish friends.

One day, her mommy and daddy took her to a big park. Quincy was so excited to see all the new sights and sounds. She loved looking at all the trees and animals.

As Quincy was exploring the park, she suddenly saw something sparkle. She ran over to it and found a shiny penny on the ground. She was so lucky to find it!

Quincy picked up the penny and put it in her pocket. She knew that she must have become very lucky to find a penny like that.

Quincy decided to take the penny home. She told her mommy and daddy all about the penny and they helped her keep it safe in a special jar.

Quincy now alway brings her lucky penny with her every time she goes for a walk or to the park. She knows that if she's ever feeling a bit down, she can look at the penny and it will make her feel better. It has certainly become a special pass for her!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to swing on her swing set in her backyard. One day, her mom urged her to clean up her toys and put them away in an organized way.

Lily didn't want to stop swinging, but she knew she had to listen to her mom. So she got off the swing and started picking up her toys. As she was putting them away, she talked to her teddy bear.

"I wish I could swing all day," Lily said.

"I know you do," Teddy replied. "But it's important to be organized too."

Lily smiled and finished putting away her toys. Then she ran back to her swing and swung higher than ever before, feeling proud of herself for being responsible and organized.

Once upon a time, there was a very mild melon. This melon was different from all the other melons, because it wasn't too sweet like the others.

One day, a small girl came upon this special melon. She put it in a basket and decided to take it home.

At home, the girl wanted to sort the melon out. She thought and thought until she had an idea. She thought of cutting and slicing the melon into small pieces.

When she finished, the girl was happy with her sorting. The melon pieces were spread all around, looking very tasty and mild.

The girl clapped her hands and smiled. Now she could enjoy her mild melon!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack who went out to the garden with his father. Jack's dad was a farmer and Jack wanted to help him.

"Look at all these tomatoes!" Jack's dad said. He pointed to a huge plant with lots of bright red tomatoes growing on it.

"Let's sell them," he said. The dad picked up the tomatoes, and Jack followed him happily.

On the way to the market they passed by a dark alley. "That's a strange place," thought Jack.

At the market, Jack watched his dad carefully sell the tomatoes. He was so proud of his dad.

When it was time to go back home, Jack gave his dad a huge hug.
"Thank you for helping me sell the tomatoes," his dad said, with a big smile.

And from then on, Jack and his dad went to the market every week to sell their tomatoes.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a wise old owl. The wise owl liked to sit on a tree and watch the animals play. One day, a small bunny was hopping around the forest. The bunny was happy and full of motion. He moved fast from one place to another.

As the bunny moved, he did not see a big rock on the ground. He stumble and fell down. The wise owl saw this and flew to help the bunny. The owl helped the bunny stand up and made sure he was okay.

The wise owl told the bunny, "Be careful when you move fast. Look where you are going." The bunny thanked the wise owl and learned to be more careful. From that day, the bunny always looked where he was going and they both played happily in the forest.

Jimmy was walking along the path, carrying a big box. The sun was starting to rise, so the grass was sparkly and golden. Jimmy was feeling a bit uncomfortable because the box was heavy.

Then Jimmy saw a rabbit hopping along the path. He stopped to admire the little creature with its soft fur. The rabbit stopped hopping and looked up at Jimmy with its big eyes.

Jimmy asked the rabbit: "Do you want to help me carry this box? It's very heavy."

The rabbit shook its head and said: "I'm sorry, it's too big for me!"

So Jimmy took a deep breath and kept on carrying the box. As the sun got brighter and brighter, Jimmy felt a bit stronger. Eventually, the box was heavy no more and Jimmy reached his destination.

The sunrise was beautiful, and Jimmy was happy he'd made it.

Once upon a time, there was a clever little mouse named Max. Max loved to play outside and explore. One day, Max was walking down the road and saw a big cat coming towards him. Max started to panic and didn't know what to do.

But Max was clever and quickly ran into a small hole in the side of the road. The cat tried to catch Max, but he was too quick and escaped. From that day on, Max was always careful when he went down the road and made sure to stay away from any cats.

Max learned that it's important to be clever and know what to do when you panic. And even though the road can be scary sometimes, if you're clever like Max, you can always find a way to stay safe.

Matt was very excited and asked what the pill did. His mom explained that the pill would help keep him healthy and strong. Matt was a bit unsure, but with a little encouragement he was ready to take the pill.

Matt put the pill in his little hand and filled his mouth with water. He swallowed the pill and smiled. He was proud that he was taking steps to become a healthy grownup.

Matt kept taking his pill each day and soon he saw the results! He was feeling healthy and strong and was happy he followed his mom's advice.

But Jenny was too smart for her own good. Because she had hidden her phone, when her mom called for her, there was no way for Jenny to answer. She knew she had made a mistake.

So Jenny was very sad. No matter how hard she looked, she couldn't find her phone. It was gone forever. Jenny was no longer cheerful. She had made a bad decision. She should have never dared to hide her phone.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy gear. It couldn't do much except share drinks.

One day, it clumsily tried to pour some juice. It spilt it everywhere, but that didn't stop it. It kept trying, splashing the drink all around.

Finally it got it right. It filled up two cups and then took them to two kids. One had big eyes and the other had little eyes. They both smiled and clapped.

The gear felt proud that it could give the children something nice to drink. The two kids thanked the gear with big hugs.

The gear was happy and so were the kids. And they all drank their drinks and had a great time. The End.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a tiny flea. This flea had a rough time finding a place to live. He wanted a warm and cozy home, but he was too small for anyone to see him.

One day, the flea saw a big, fluffy dog. The dog looked warm and cozy, so the flea jumped on the dog's back. The dog didn't notice the flea because he was so small. The flea was happy to have a new home.

But, the dog's owner saw the flea and wanted him to vanish. The owner tried to catch the flea, but the flea was too fast. The flea jumped and jumped until he vanished into the grass. The flea was sad, but he knew he had to find a new home. And so, the little flea kept looking for a warm and cozy place to live.

Once upon a time, there was a small bird who loved to fly up high in the sky. The bird would flap its wings and soar through the clouds. One day, the bird saw a beautiful butterfly and admired its colorful wings.

The bird wanted to be friends with the butterfly, so it flew closer to say hello. But as the bird got closer, it accidentally hit the butterfly with its wing. The butterfly fell to the ground and couldn't fly anymore.

The bird felt very sad and tried to help the butterfly, but it was too late. The butterfly had died. The bird realized that it should have admired the butterfly from afar and not gotten too close. From then on, the bird flew alone and always remembered the small butterfly it had accidentally hurt.

One day, Janet and Tom were playing a game in the garden. They were having so much fun running around and exploring. Suddenly, Janet said, "We should lie down in the grass and see what we can see in the sky". "OK!", said Tom. So they lay down and looked up at the clouds.

Meanwhile, the Nosy Crow flew over and saw them in the grass. "What are you two doing?", the crow asked. Janet and Tom both laughed. "We are playing a game!", said Tom. The crow was curious and wanted to join in. But Tom and Janet said, "No! This game is just for us".

So the Nosy Crow flew away and Tom and Janet went back to having fun in the garden. They laughed and ran around for hours. But one thing was for sure - they were happy to have their special game together!

Jacob was a three year old and he loved going on adventures. He wanted to go to the park and play, but his mom said no. She said that Jacob's body was too fragile and he needed to rest. So Jacob stayed at home, feeling disappointed and sad.

But Jacob found a way to have an adventure right at home! He filled a sack with all the pillows and blankets he could find. Then he laid down in the middle of the pile and pretended he was camping. His stomach was full of excitement and he imagined his camping adventure.

Suddenly, he noticed that the blanket underneath him was too thin and he could feel the ground below. Jacob knew that he needed to be extra careful because his body was fragile and he couldn't get hurt.

He carefully tucked himself into the pillow pile again and finally went to sleep, happy with his adventure.

Once, there was a fragile cow. He was lonely and wanted a friend. So, he decided to search for one.

He wandered for a long time, struggling to find a friend. He walked through the fields, through the towns and through the woods.

And then, he finally found someone! The cow was so happy and excited. But to his dismay, it was not a friend! It was a huge, steaming steak. All cooked and ready to eat.

The cow was so disappointed. He tried hard to struggle and run away. But the steak was so big and heavy, it was too much for him. He could not escape. In the end, he was eaten.

The cow had a bad ending. He never found his friend.

Once upon a time, there was a prince and a princess who lived in a castle on the east side of a big forest. Every morning, the prince and princess would ride their horses in the forest. But one day, something naughty happened.

While exploring the forest, the prince and princess noticed that a big stone was blocking their path.

"This stone is so big, how can we pass?" asked the princess.

The prince smiled and said, "Let's see if our horses can pass it!"

The prince and princess gave it a try and the horses passed the big stone!

The prince and princess were so proud of themselves and their horses, and they rode off happily into the east.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play with toys and books. One day, they find a folder on the table. The folder is red and has a sticker of a star.

"What is in the folder?" Lily asks.

"I don't know. Let's open it and see," Ben says.

They open the folder and see many papers. The papers have drawings and words on them. They look like homework.

"Wow, these are pretty. Can we take some?" Lily says.

"No, we can't. These are not ours. They belong to someone else. Maybe they are Mom's or Dad's. We should close the folder and put it back," Ben says.

But Lily does not listen. She takes a paper and puts it in her pocket. She thinks it is a gift for her.

"Come on, Ben. Let's go and play outside," she says.

They go outside and play with a ball. They have fun. But then, Lily feels something wet in her pocket. She takes out the paper and sees that it is covered with juice. The juice is sour and smells bad. It has spilled from her bottle.

"Yuck! What is this?" Lily says.

"Oh no! That is the paper you took from the folder. You should not have done that. Now it is ruined and you have made a mess," Ben says.

Lily feels sorry. She knows she did something wrong. She wants to fix it.

"I'm sorry, Ben. You were right. I should not have taken the paper. It was not mine. It was not a gift. It was homework. How can I make it better?" she says.

"Let's go back and tell the truth. Maybe we can help to make a new paper. Or maybe we can say sorry and give a hug. I'm sure they will forgive us," Ben says.

They go back and find the folder. They see that it is Mom's folder. Mom is a teacher. She has homework for her students.

"Mom, we have something to tell you," Lily says.

They tell Mom what they did. They show her the paper. They say sorry and give a hug.

Mom is not happy. She is sad and angry. She says they should not have opened the folder. They should not have taken the paper. They should have asked first. They should have respected her work.

But Mom is also kind. She sees that they are sorry. She sees that they want to help. She says they can make a new paper. They can draw and write something nice. They can say sorry to the student.

They make a new paper. They put it in the folder. They close the folder and put it back. They promise to never do it again.

Mom smiles. She says she loves them. She says they have learned a lesson. She says they have done a good thing.

Lily and Ben are happy. They have learned to be honest and respectful. They have learned to say sorry and to make things better. They have learned that taking things that are not yours is not a gift. It is a mistake.

When Lily came back, she saw that Emma had taken the crown and was playing with it by herself. Lily felt a bit jealous but she didn't say anything. She asked Emma to give the crown back and they continued to play together happily.

Once upon a Sunday, a little boy was sitting on a seat. He was looking out of the window and watched the clouds rolling by. It was a very peaceful and quiet day. He could hear the birds singing in the trees, and the bees buzzing around the flowers.

Suddenly, he saw a big, white balloon flying in the sky. He watched it float and twirl until it disappeared in the distance. The boy leaned back and noticed a big tree in the garden. The leaves on the tree were dancing in the wind and the sun was shining through them. It was so peaceful.

The little boy closed his eyes and imagined he was flying through the sky like the balloon. Then he opened them again, smiled and got up from the seat. Sunday was a wonderful day!

Red started to run, and the wolf chased her. She remembered her grandmas' words again and shouted, "I will shoot you, wolf!" Suddenly, a hunter appeared and shot the wolf with his gun. Will was excited to start looking. He looked all around, but didn't see any cats. He asked his mom where he could find one.

His mom said cats like to hang out in places like gardens and backyards. Will went to the backyard and looked around. He couldn't find a cat anywhere.

Will was about to give up when he finally saw a cat! He was so excited to meet the cat. He got closer and realized the cat was very scared. He knew it would be difficult, but he was determined to make friends with the cat.

He slowly approached the cat and spoke to it softly. He offered the cat a treat and after a few tries, the cat finally accepted. Will was so happy. He got to meet a cat!

The little girl was so excited that she ran all the way to the castle's gates. The gatekeeper said she couldn't go in without the key. She had to find the special key to enter the castle.

The little girl looked high and low but couldn't find the key. Then something magical happened. She saw a little gray mouse scurrying up the castle wall. It had something shiny in its paws. It was the key!

The little mouse dropped the key and the girl quickly grabbed it. She clapped her hands in glee and thanked the mouse. Now she could open the castle and applaud the king and queen!

Once there was a little boy called Paul. He was very competitive and loved to race. One day he saw a big bush and decided to have a race. He asked his best friend if he wanted to race, but his friend said no.

Paul was determined so he started running around the bush. He zigzagged and jumped over it. He was really fast, but he was concentrating so much that he didn't notice a big hole near the bush.

Sadly, Paul fell into the big hole and couldn't get out. He was stuck and shouted for help, but no one heard him.

Paul was in the big hole all day, until night-time came. He was unable to get out and had to stay there until morning.

It was a bad ending for Paul as he stayed in the big hole all night. But he learned an important lesson - don't be too competitive!

John thought he was in trouble. He tried to explain what he was doing but quickly realized he was in trouble. His teacher said, "This isn't a fair for you to play. You should be in school. Then she took him home.

John was so sad. He was sure he would never get to go to a fair again.

Once upon a time there was a stubborn faucet that was leaking everywhere. A brave 3 year old, Joe, was determined to solve the problem.

Joe's mom asked him, "Can you please help me with this leaking faucet? It's making a mess!" Joe said, "Yes, I will help you. I will solve the problem!"

Joe got to work. He tried turning the faucet handle. He tried tightening the pipes. He tried making adjustments of all kinds. But every time he thought he had solved the problem, the faucet leaks kept coming.

Joe kept on working and he kept on trying. But the faucet was stubborn and nothing seemed to work.

Luckily, Joe's mom came to the rescue. She said, "Joe, sweetheart, how about we call the plumber? They will know how to solve this problem."

Joe and his mom called the plumber who quickly fixed the leaky faucet. Joe was happy and proud of himself for helping to solve the problem.

And Joe and his mom lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside on her porch, even when it was icy outside. One day, her mom said, "Lily, it's too icy outside. You need to stay inside today."

Lily was sad because she wanted to play outside. She asked her mom, "Can't I just stay on the porch? It's not too icy there."

Her mom thought about it and said, "Okay, but be careful and don't slip on the ice."

Lily was happy and went outside to play on the porch. She had fun playing with her toys and watching the snow fall. When it was time to go inside, Lily's mom came to the porch and said, "Good job staying safe on the porch, Lily."

Lily smiled and said, "I love playing on the porch, even when it's icy outside."

Lily and Anna are sisters. They like to play with dolls and dress them up. Lily has a lovely pink dress for her doll. Anna likes the pink dress too. She asks Lily if she can have it.

Lily says no. She wants to keep the pink dress for her doll. Anna feels sad. She cries and tells Mommy. Mommy comes and talks to Lily and Anna. She tells them that sharing is good. Sharing makes others happy and makes you happy too.

Lily thinks about what Mommy says. She loves Anna and does not want her to be sad. She decides to share the pink dress with Anna. She gives it to her and says sorry. Anna smiles and says thank you. She hugs Lily and says she loves her.

Lily and Anna are happy. They play with their dolls and share the pink dress. They take turns and say nice things to each other. Mommy is proud of them. She tells them they are lovely sisters. She gives them a big hug and a kiss.

Once upon a time, there was an old man. He was walking through a dark alley late at night. Suddenly he heard a noise! He looked around and saw an old toilet. He started to tremble with fear. He thought something bad was going to happen.

He started to run away, but it was too late. A big, scary monster appeared and he chased after the old man. He ran and ran as fast as he could, but the monster was faster. He screamed for help, but no one came.

The old man could not escape and the monster grabbed him. He was so scared that he closed his eyes and waited for the monster to do something awful. He trembled with fear, waiting and waiting. Unfortunately, the monster ate him and the old man was never seen again.

Once there was a little girl who was three. She had a very special game that she liked to play: pretending. Every day, she would pretend to be a princess, a doctor, a farmer, and more.

One day, a stranger came to her house. The girl was a bit scared of the stranger, but she still gave him a big smile. The stranger smiled back and asked if she wanted to play pretend with him. The girl was so excited, and and of course she said yes.

They pretended to be pirates for awhile, and then pretend to have a tea party with some of the girl's dolls. The girl's mom watched them and smiled.

Suddenly, the stranger reached for a fragile antiques on the shelf. The girl got scared and ran into her mom's arms. The stranger said he was sorry and put the fragile item back. Afterwards, the girl thanked him, and the stranger said good bye and left. The little girl learned that strangers can be nice, too.

Once upon a time there was a child. She was small, but compassionate. She tried her best to be helpful and kind.

One day, the child saw an old man walking all alone. She noticed that he seemed sad and walked with a slow, steady pace. She decided to surprise him and cheer him up.

The child ran up to the old man and gave him a big smile. He looked surprised. She asked if he would like to take a walk with her. The man said yes and they started walking together.

The child talked and laughed with the old man. She smiled and asked him questions. It made him feel better and more hopeful. By the time they were done walking, he was much happier.

The child's compassion made the old man feel seen and loved. She had brightened his day and helped him to feel better.

Once upon a time, there was a small boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to have fun and play with his toys. One day, he went to the park with his mom and saw a big sandbox. He wanted to play in it, but it was empty.

Timmy's mom told him to wait a little bit, and soon enough, a big truck arrived. The truck had a big container full of sand, and it started to fill the sandbox. Timmy was so excited to play in the sand, and he had so much fun building castles and digging tunnels.

After a while, Timmy's mom told him it was time to go home. Timmy was sad to leave the sandbox, but he was happy because he had so much fun. As they walked away, Timmy looked back at the sandbox and saw that it was now filled with sand. He smiled, knowing that he would come back to play again soon.

Once upon a time, there was a bee. He was very weird. He liked to part with his friends and do his own thing.

So one day the bee went for a nice walk in the park. Suddenly, he saw an ant!

He thought to himself "that ant is weird!" He hopped over and he said "hello!" The ant was very confused.

The bee said "let's part ways and go explore the park together!" The ant said "okay!"

They had a lot of fun exploring and learning about the world around them. After a while, they said goodbye.

The bee and the ant were both very happy they got to part in such a fun adventure together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily went to the park to play with her friends. They all ran around and had lots of funny fun. They played on the swings and went down the slide.

But then, Lily's friends wanted to play a game that she didn't like. She didn't want to play, but she didn't know how to tell them. Finally, she said, "I don't like that game. Can we play something else?" Her friends accepted her idea and they played a new game that was lots of fun.

After a while, it was time for Lily to go home. She waved goodbye to her friends and started walking home. As she walked, she noticed the motion of the leaves on the trees and the birds flying in the sky. She thought about how much fun she had with her friends and how happy she was that she spoke up and told them what she wanted to do.

Bob remembered the day he got his first potato. It was a bright yellow one and it looked so delicious. He couldn't wait to try it! But first, he wanted to show it to his mom. He was surprised when she told him that he could make something with it.

That day, Bob learned how to make potato chips. He carefully peeled the skin of the yellow potato and used the flour to cut it into small pieces. Then he cooked the chips. When they were ready, Bob took a bite and it was so tasty!

He ate the whole plate and felt so happy. Bob was proud of himself for making something new. He couldn't wait to tell his mom about it. From that day on, he always remembered the yellow potato and the chips he made with it.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Sara. She saw something very exciting one day. She saw a cliff! It was so high that she thought if she jumped off she might fly like a bird.

But she knew that this was not safe. So, Sara decided to make a plan. She got a long piece of rope and began to tie a big knot at the top of the cliff. She made sure it was extra strong and tight so that it would lock the cliff and keep her safe.

Once she finished tying the knot, she stepped back and admired her work. It was the perfect place to explore and play. She jumped around and climbed up the cliff. It was so exciting that she was screaming with joy.

As she was playing, Sara kept the knot securely locked. That way nobody could get in or out. It kept her safe from harm and danger.

Sara had so much fun that day and created a special memory. She knew she could always go back to the little cliff for a big adventure.

Mrs. Brown had a very tidy bedroom. Every week, she would take some time to dress it up with new things. She put new pillows on the bed and some bright curtains on the window. Mrs. Brown loved to get dressed up too. Every Sunday she would put on her best dress. It was her favorite yellow one with the big bow. She would make her hair nice and tidy and then put on some jewellery. Mrs. Brown loved to make her bedroom and herself look neat and tidy every week.

Ben and Mia went to the big water place with Mom and Dad. They saw many fish and animals in the water. They liked the dolphins the most. The dolphins were gray and smooth and had a big smile. They jumped and played in the water.

"Look, Mom, the dolphins are happy!" Mia said.

"Yes, they are, sweetie. They are very healthy and smart animals. They can do tricks and talk to each other," Mom said.

"Can I talk to them, too?" Ben asked.

"Maybe you can try. But you have to be gentle and respectful. They are not toys, they are living beings," Dad said.

Ben and Mia went closer to the glass where the dolphins were. They waved and said hello. One of the dolphins came near them and made a funny sound. Ben and Mia laughed. They felt like the dolphin was their friend.

The dolphin moved its head up and down, as if it wanted them to do something. Ben and Mia copied the dolphin. The dolphin seemed happy. It splashed some water with its tail. Ben and Mia got wet and giggled.

Then, the dolphin did something else. It slapped its fin on the water, making a loud noise. Ben and Mia were surprised. They looked at the dolphin. The dolphin looked at them and slapped its fin again.

"What does it want?" Mia asked.

"I don't know. Maybe it wants us to slap, too," Ben said.

Ben and Mia raised their hands and slapped them together. The dolphin saw them and made another funny sound. It slapped its fin again. Ben and Mia slapped their hands again. They started a game of slap with the dolphin. They had fun and laughed a lot.

Mom and Dad watched them and smiled. They were happy to see their children have a good time with the dolphin. They thought the dolphin was very friendly and playful. They took a picture of Ben and Mia and the dolphin. They wanted to remember this special day.

Mommy was cooking dinner when suddenly her little girl, Lucy, started to cry. She was hungry. Mommy smiled and told her, "It's ok, I have a special snack for you." She put the snack on the counter, and Lucy was so excited. She wanted to reach it, but it was too high.

Mommy bent down and told Lucy, "Lean on me and I'll pick it up for you." So Lucy leaned on her mommy and Mommy picked up the snack.

Lucy was so happy! She gave her mommy a big hug and began to eat the snack. It was delicious. She felt safe and happy, knowing her mommy was there.

Mommy smiled and said, "See how safe it is to lean on me?" Lucy smiled and hugged her mommy, and enjoyed the rest of her snack.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to hop on the swings and slide down the slide. They saw a big red car in the parking lot. It had a shiny buckle on the door.

"Wow, look at that car!" Lily said. "It must be very fast."

"Let's go see it!" Ben said. "Maybe we can touch it."

They ran to the car and tried to open the door. But it was locked. They pushed and pulled, but the buckle did not move.

"Hey, what are you doing?" A loud voice said. It was the owner of the car. He was angry. He came out of the store and saw the kids.

"Get away from my car!" He shouted. "You could scratch it or break it!"

Lily and Ben were scared. They did not mean to make him mad. They just wanted to see the car.

"Sorry, mister," Lily said. "We just liked your car. It is very pretty."

"Yeah, sorry," Ben said. "We did not hurt it. We just wanted to touch it."

The owner of the car looked at them. He saw that they were just curious. He was not so angry anymore. He decided to be nice.

"Well, thank you for the compliment," he said. "But you should not touch other people's things without asking. It is not polite."

He opened the door and showed them the inside of the car. It had a big wheel and a lot of buttons.

"This is how I drive the car," he said. "I turn the wheel and push the buttons. It is very fun."

Lily and Ben smiled. They liked to learn new things. They thanked the owner of the car for showing them.

"Can we hop in the car?" Lily asked. "Just for a minute?"

The owner of the car thought for a moment. He saw that they were good kids. He decided to let them.

"Okay, but only for a minute," he said. "And be careful. Do not touch anything."

He helped them hop in the car. They sat on the seats and looked around. They felt very happy.

The owner of the car smiled. He remembered when he was a kid. He liked to hop in cars too. He felt very happy too.

Once upon a time, there was a little child named Timmy. Timmy was very little, but he was very smart too. One day, Timmy's mommy told him it was time to go to bed. Timmy didn't want to go to bed because he wanted to play more.

"Timmy, it's time for bed," said mommy.

"I don't want to go to bed," said Timmy.

Mommy reminded Timmy that he needed to sleep so he could grow big and strong. Timmy listened to his mommy and went to bed. He closed his eyes and fell asleep quickly.

The next morning, Timmy woke up feeling happy and refreshed. He remembered what mommy said and felt proud that he listened. From that day on, Timmy always went to bed on time so he could be a big and strong little boy.

Lily liked to play in the garden. She liked to smell the flowers and watch the bees. One day, she saw a red berry on a bush. It looked like a candy. She wanted to taste it.

She picked the berry and put it in her mouth. But it was not a candy. It was bitter. It made her tongue hurt. She spit it out and cried. Mommy heard her and came to see.

Mommy saw the berry and said, "Oh, no, Lily. That is a bad berry. It can make you sick. You should not eat it. You should only eat what I give you."

She hugged Lily and took her inside. She gave her some water and a cookie. The cookie was sweet. It made Lily feel better. She said, "Sorry, Mommy. I will not eat bad berries again."

Mommy said, "It's okay, Lily. You did not know. But now you do. Bad berries are bitter. Good berries are sweet. Next time, we can go to the market and buy some good berries. How does that sound?"

Lily smiled and said, "Yes, Mommy. I like good berries. And I love you."

Mommy said, "I love you too, Lily." They hugged and ate their cookies.

Then, Benny remembered what his mommy always said, "Don't judge a book by its cover." Benny decided to be brave and talk to the wolf. The wolf was actually very nice and just wanted to be friends.

Benny and the wolf played together all day until they got very sleepy. Benny learned that it's important to not be afraid of things just because they look different and to always give others a chance. From that day on, Benny and the wolf were the best of friends. The end.

Timmy was so happy that he caught the butterfly. He showed it to his mom and said, "Look, Mommy! I catched a butterfly!" His mom said, "Wow, Timmy! That's amazing! But remember, we have to be gentle with the butterfly. It's a living thing."

After a while, Timmy got tired of holding the butterfly. He wanted to play with his stick instead. He found a stick on the ground and started to play with it. He pretended that the stick was a sword and he was a brave knight. He swung the stick around and said, "I'm a knight! I'm going to save the princess!"

Timmy's mom said, "That's a great stick, Timmy! But be careful not to hurt anyone with it." Timmy nodded and continued to play with his stick.

After a while, Timmy got hungry. His mom gave him a yummy apple to eat. Timmy took a big bite and said, "Mmm, this apple is yummy! Thank you, Mommy!" Timmy and his mom sat on the grass and enjoyed the sunny day together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the garden. One day, she found a caterpillar on a leaf. It was brown and fuzzy.

Lily picked up the caterpillar and said, "Hello, little caterpillar. Do you want to be my friend?"

The caterpillar didn't say anything, but Lily believed that it was happy to have a friend. She brought it inside and put it in a jar with some leaves.

Every day, Lily would talk to the caterpillar and watch it grow bigger and bigger. She believed that one day it would turn into a beautiful butterfly.

And one day, that's exactly what happened! The caterpillar turned into a butterfly with wings as big as Lily's hands. Lily was so happy and she knew that her belief in her little friend had come true.
