So he ran outside to find the balloon and he looked up in the sky. But suddenly the wind blew and the balloon flew away!

Jack was silly and got very angry. He yelled, 'Mine! Mine!' But that didn't help, the balloon was gone.

Jack's mum was there to help and she said to him, 'No Jack, that doesn't belong to us. That looks a bit disgusting'. Jack didn't understand why his balloon had gone away, but he was okay and his mum was there to hug him.

John loved to observe. He liked to watch the animals in the backyard, the birds flying in the sky, and the leaves in the trees. One day, John was observing an old jug. He noticed it was made of plastic and was a light blue color. It was a normal jug, but he found it interesting.

John kept looking at the jug. He wondered if it could do something special. Suddenly, he heard a sound coming from it. He was amazed! He looked inside the jug, and he saw many small bugs. John smiled. It was special after all.

John kept looking at the jug for a long time. He was happy to see the bugs inside it. He was sad when it was time to go. But he was happy he was able to observe the jug.

Tim and Sue are friends. They like to play in the park. They run and jump and slide and swing. They have fun.

One day, they see something new in the park. It is a big mushroom. It is red and white and round. It looks like a hat.

"Wow, look at that!" Tim says. "Can we touch it?"

"I don't know," Sue says. "Maybe it is not good."

They go closer to the mushroom. They smell it. It smells nice.

"Maybe it is tasty," Tim says. "Can we eat it?"

"I don't know," Sue says. "Maybe it is not food."

They look at the mushroom. They are curious.

"Let's try a little bit," Tim says. He breaks off a small piece. He puts it in his mouth.

"Yuck!" he says. He spits it out. "It is not tasty. It is bad."

Sue laughs. She does not eat the mushroom. She is smart.

They run away from the mushroom. They find their mom and dad. They tell them about the mushroom.

"Never eat mushrooms in the park," mom and dad say. "They can be dangerous. Only eat mushrooms that we give you. They are safe and tasty."

Tim and Sue nod. They learn a lesson. They play in the park. They have fun.

Suddenly, a strange man came out of nowhere. He had a mean look in his face. He shouted at the little boy and said, "Take that staff off, now!"

The little boy was scared and quickly took the staff off. He started crying because he was frightened.

The strange man then said, "That's what you get for not asking permission before you wear something." And with that, he grabbed the staff and ran off.

The little boy felt sad and watched the man disappear. He sadly returned home empty handed.

One day, the farmer said to his little boy, "It's time to go deliver the Corn! The little boy was excited and ran to get the Corn.

The Corn was smooth and bright yellow. The little boy loaded the Corn onto the wagon and they trundled off to the village.

When they arrived at the village the little boy couldn't wait to start delivering the Corn. He quickly unloaded the Corn and went from door to door, asking the people if they'd like to buy smooth Corn from the farm.

Everyone was very pleased to see the little boy and bought the Corn. They were all very happy to have smooth Corn for their meals.

The little boy and the farmer were very happy too, and went back to the farm for more Corn to deliver.

Timmy went to the post office and sent the letter. On the way back, he saw a big tiger. The tiger looked very busy. Timmy thought the tiger was going to work.

Timmy said, "Hello Mr. Tiger, are you going to work?"

The tiger replied, "Yes, I'm going to work. I have to catch my lunch."

Timmy said, "Okay, bye Mr. Tiger. Have a good lunch."

The next day, Timmy heard some bad news. The tiger had eaten one of his friends. Timmy was very sad. He realized that the tiger was not busy, he was just hungry.

Jack wanted to go to the party too, but could not because he did not have an invitation. He was jealous of Jill and started to feel sad.

Jack's face lit up with surprise, and he was so happy! They both held their invitation pieces in the air and started to jog around the park together. They laughed and giggled, feeling so lively and excited.

Lily was excited to see what was inside the hole. She put her hand inside and felt something furry. Suddenly, a voice came from inside the hole and scared her. Lily and Max ran away as fast as they could.

Later that day, Lily's mom asked her what happened in the forest. Lily told her about the scary voice in the tree. Her mom explained that it was probably just an animal and not to be scared. From then on, Lily and Max always checked inside the tree before putting their hands in.

Sam and Mia are playing in the park. They like to imagine that they are superheroes. Sam wears a red cape and Mia wears a blue mask.

"Look, there is a big tree!" Sam says. "Let's climb it and save the birds!"

"OK, but be careful!" Mia says. "The tree is very high and the branches are thin."

They run to the tree and start to climb. Sam goes first and Mia follows him. They see many birds on the tree. They are singing and flying.

"Hello, birds!" Sam says. "We are here to help you. Do you have a goal?"

One bird looks at Sam and says, "Yes, we do. We want to find a new home. The old one is too small and noisy."

"Where do you want to go?" Mia asks.

"We want to go to the lake. There is more water and food there. But we don't know how to get there. It is far and we are afraid of the big animals."

"Don't worry, we can help you!" Sam says. "We are superheroes. We can fly and protect you. We have a useful idea. Follow us!"

Sam and Mia jump from the tree and spread their arms. They pretend to fly and lead the birds to the lake. The birds follow them and thank them.

"Thank you, superheroes!" the birds say. "You are very kind and brave. You helped us reach our goal. We are happy in our new home."

"You are welcome, birds!" Sam and Mia say. "We are happy to help you. We like to imagine and have fun. Goodbye, birds!"

Sam and Mia wave to the birds and go back to the park. They smile and hug each other. They are proud of their adventure. They imagine more stories and play until it is time to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in her backyard. One day, while she was playing, she found a small berry bush. She picked a few berries and ate them. They tasted sweet and juicy!

A few days later, Lily was playing in her backyard again. She saw a big rock near the berry bush. She got excited and picked up the rock. She saw something moving under it. It was a little bug! The bug was crawling on the ground. Lily watched it for a while, then put the rock back where she found it.

The next day, Lily went back to the berry bush. She saw the bug again! This time, she knew where to find it. She lifted the rock and saw the bug. She was so excited to see it again! From then on, every day, she would go to the berry bush and lift the rock to see the little bug. It was her secret friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved going to the park with her mommy, but today was different. Her mommy had to go to work, so Lily had to go to a new place called daycare.

When they arrived, Lily saw lots of new kids and toys. But when her mommy said goodbye, Lily started to cry. "I don't want to stay here, mommy," she said. "I want to go home with you."

Her mommy hugged her and said, "I know it's hard, but you have to stay here. This is a regular place where you can play and make new friends."

Lily still refused to stay, but then she saw a little girl with a doll. "I like your doll," Lily said. The little girl smiled and said, "You can play with her if you want." Lily smiled back and decided to stay. She played with the doll and made lots of new friends. When her mommy came to pick her up, Lily didn't want to leave!

Lily was a girl who loved to dance. She danced every day in her room, in the park, and at school. She liked to dance with her friends, her toys, and her dog. She was happy when she danced.

One day, Lily went to the zoo with her mom and dad. She saw many animals, like lions, elephants, and giraffes. She liked them all, but her favorite was the monkey. The monkey was cute and funny. He jumped and swung on the trees. He made funny faces and noises. Lily wanted to dance with the monkey.

She asked her mom and dad if she could go closer to the monkey. They said yes, but be careful. Lily ran to the fence and waved at the monkey. The monkey saw her and waved back. Lily started to dance. She moved her arms and legs and spun around. The monkey liked her dance. He copied her moves and danced with her. Lily and the monkey had fun.

But then, Lily got too excited. She wanted to touch the monkey. She climbed on the fence and reached out her hand. The monkey reached out his hand too. But he was not gentle. He grabbed Lily's hand and pulled her hard. Lily fell off the fence and hurt herself. She cried and screamed. Her mom and dad ran to her and hugged her. They took her to the nurse and bandaged her wound. Lily was sad and scared.

The monkey was sorry. He did not mean to hurt Lily. He just wanted to play. He looked at Lily and made a sad face. He tried to say sorry, but Lily did not hear him. She did not look at him. She did not want to dance with him anymore.

Lily learned a lesson. She learned that dancing is fun, but not with everyone. She learned that some animals are cute, but not friendly. She learned that she should listen to her mom and dad, and be careful. She still loved to dance, but only with her friends, her toys, and her dog. She was happy again.

Bob was a careful woodsman. He loved to spend his days in the forest, tending to the trees and wildlife. Each day, Bob would go out and work hard with his trusty axe.

One day, Bob stumbled upon something unusual. It was a magical boulder with an inscription that read, "Serve and be served . Bob was intrigued and cautiously approached the boulder.

Suddenly, the boulder began to move and shimmer. Out of it stepped an old man, who warned Bob to be careful. The old man said that the boulder was there to serve people who needed a favour and one had to serve in return. Bob was surprised and thanked the old man.

Bob decided to try the boulder's power. He wished for his axe to be sharper. Suddenly, the boulder started to move and it granted his wish. However, Bob had forgotten to mention that he needed to serve something in return.

Bob realized that he was in trouble and quickly promised to serve the boulder anytime it needed it. The boulder granted him a warning and said that one should always remember to be careful when making a wish. With that, the boulder disappeared.

Bob was grateful for the warning and thanked the magical boulder for teaching him an important lesson. From then on, Bob remembered to be careful when using his axe.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Annie. Annie loved jelly more than anything in the world. One day, Annie saw something very dangerous in her kitchen. It was a big box of jelly, but it was attached to the kitchen counter. Annie knew she couldn't get it on her own, so she asked for help.

Her mom came into the kitchen and saw the dangerous box of jelly. She knew that it must have been attached very tightly, but with a few tools, she was soon able to detach it from the counter.

Annie was so excited when her mom finally released the big box of jelly. She hugged her mom tightly and thanked her for the delicious treat.

Annie quickly unwrapped the box and couldn't wait to have a spoonful of the sweet jelly. She pretended it was a special adventure, as if she had found the jelly all on her own.

Annie enjoyed her special treat and was so happy that her mom was able to help her get the dangerous box of jelly off the counter.

Ben and Mia were playing in the park. They liked to swing, slide and run. They also had a big bear toy that they took turns to hug. The bear was soft and brown and had a red bow.

But then, a big wind came and blew the bear away. The bear flew over the fence and landed in a tree. Ben and Mia ran after the bear, but they could not reach it. They were very sad.

"Help, help!" they cried. "Our bear is stuck in the tree!"

A man heard them and came to see. He had a long ladder and a big bag. He climbed the ladder and got the bear. He put the bear in the bag and zipped it up. He came down the ladder and gave the bag to Ben and Mia.

"Here you go, kids," he said. "Your bear is safe and sound. Don't let him fly away again."

Ben and Mia thanked the man and opened the bag. They hugged the bear and smiled. They were very happy. They went back to the park and played some more. They also tied the bear to a swing, so he would not fly away again. They had a lot of fun.

Lily liked to watch TV after school. She liked to see the animals and the songs and the stories. But today, her dad was grumpy. He had a lot of work to do on his computer. He did not want to hear the TV.

"Turn off the TV, Lily," he said. "It is too loud. I need to work."

"But, Dad, I want to watch TV," Lily said. "It is fun. Please, can I watch a little more?"

"No, Lily, you can't," her dad said. "You have to play with your toys or read a book. The TV is not good for you."

Lily was sad. She did not like to play with her toys or read a book. She liked to watch TV. She turned off the TV and went to her room. She felt angry and bored.

She saw her teddy bear on her bed. She picked it up and hugged it. She remembered that her teddy bear knew how to sign. Her mom had taught her and her teddy bear some signs when she was a baby. She decided to sign with her teddy bear.

She signed, "Hello, teddy bear. How are you?"

Her teddy bear signed back, "Hello, Lily. I am fine. How are you?"

Lily signed, "I am not fine. I am sad. My dad is grumpy. He does not let me watch TV."

Her teddy bear signed, "I am sorry, Lily. That is not nice. Why don't you tell him how you feel?"

Lily thought for a moment. She signed, "Maybe I will. Maybe he will understand. Thank you, teddy bear. You are a good friend."

Her teddy bear signed, "You are welcome, Lily. You are a good friend, too. I love you."

Lily smiled. She felt a little better. She loved her teddy bear, too. She decided to go and talk to her dad. Maybe he would listen. Maybe he would let her watch TV for a little while. Maybe he would not be so grumpy. She hoped so. She took her teddy bear with her and went to find her dad.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty little girl who really wanted to be a fireman. She asked her mom, "Mommy, can I be a fireman?

Her mom smiled and said, "No, darling. Girls can't be firemen.

The girl thought for a moment. Then she said, "What can I put on to be like a fireman?

Her mom smiled and said, "You can put on your nice red dress and a firefighter hat. That way you can pretend to be a fireman!

And so the little girl put on her red dress and her firefighter hat, and she looked like a real fireman! She had so much fun pretending to be a fireman.

The moral of the story is that even though we may not always be able to do the things we want, with a bit of creativity, we can often find other ways to make our dreams come true.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Billy and Bea. Billy had a big head and lots of brown curls, while Bea was bald.

Everyday, Billy and Bea would go on an adventure. One day, they decided to go on an adventure in the rain.

Bea was so excited, but Billy was afraid. "What if I get wet?" he asked.

Bea smiled. She said, "That's the best part - the rhythm of the rain makes us feel alive!"

So, Billy and Bea danced in the rain. They laughed and splashed and spun.

When they were done, Billy said, "Thanks for showing me how wonderful the rain can be!"

Bea smiled and said, "It's ok to be different, like me being bald and you having curly hair. When we celebrate each other's differences, our world can be even more beautiful!"

The two friends hugged, then skipped home. They knew that no matter what they looked like - they could always have fun and be friends.

It was a beautiful day and Jack was excited for his first quiz. He was a bit scared but he knew he had studied really hard and he was ready. His friends were all excited too, and Jack felt a bit envious when he saw everyone high-fiving each other.

Jack sighed and took a deep breath. He wanted to relax so he closed his eyes and allowed his mind to clear. He sat in the sun, letting the warmth sink into him, until he felt calmer.

When it was time, Jack entered the room with nervousness in his stomach. He was handed a quiz and he suddenly felt happy and excited. He concentrated hard and finished the quiz before anyone else.

When the results were announced, Jack smiled when he heard his name. His friends congratulated him, and his heart was bursting with joy and pride. He had passed his first quiz, and he couldn't have been more happy.

After, Jack and his friends went to the playground where they played until it was dark. When it was time to go home Jack felt calm and relaxed. He was happy to have passed his quiz, and the feeling of accomplishment made him more relaxed than ever.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who lived in a tree. The bird was very lonely because it didn't have any friends to play with. One day, the bird decided to fly down to the ground to look for a friend.

As the bird was flying, it saw a rabbit hopping by. The bird asked the rabbit if it wanted to be friends, but the rabbit said no and hopped away. The bird felt even more lonely than before.

Suddenly, the wind started to blow really hard and the bird's feathers started to zip around. The bird was scared, but then it saw a little mouse hiding in a hole. The bird asked the mouse if it wanted to be friends and the mouse said yes! From that day on, the bird and the mouse played together every day and were never lonely again.

Grandma was deep in thought when she looked up to see a little girl.

"What is that, Grandma?" the girl asked.

Grandma smiled and said, "It's a lizard. It's cute and harmless."

The girl was intrigued and cautiously stepped closer to the lizard. But then something disgusting suddenly jumped out, scaring them both.

The girl started to weep. Grandma picked her up and let her snuggle into her arms.

The girl looked at the lizard and asked Grandma, "Will the bad thing come back?"

Grandma sadly shook her head and said, "Yes, with all disgusting things that stay for a while, it will come back."

The little girl started to cry again and Grandma hugged her tightly. They both wept as they thought of the bad thing coming back.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Sarah. She loved to explore and was always curious about the world.

One day, Sarah was playing in her backyard when she heard a strange noise. She stopped to listen, then looked around. She couldn't see anything, so Sarah asked her mom: "Mom, what was that noise?"

Her mom replied, "That's the police, sweetheart. They are looking for something."

Sarah was intrigued, and asked, "What are they looking for?"

Her mom said, "I don't know. We can go take a look. Come on!"

So, Sarah and her mom walked down the street to watch the police search. The police were being very persistent and searching everywhere - but they couldn't find what they were looking for.

Sarah stayed and watched until the police gave up, then headed home with her mom.

That night, Sarah dreamed of being a detective, searching the world and finding things that nobody else could. One day, she thought, she would be brave and persistent enough to be the one who could help the police find what they were looking for.

Once upon a time, there was an old tree. One day, a mysterious whistle was heard coming from it.

Two children playing nearby stopped in their tracks.

"What was that?" the first child said.

The second child shook their head. "I don't know, let's investigate!"

They both crept up to the tree, and peered around. A surprise awaited them - inside a small hole in the trunk was a wonderful gift.

It was a whistle, shining in the sunlight.

The two children gasped.
"It's beautiful," the first child said.

The second child carefully reached in and grabbed the mysterious find.

She held it in her hands and with a deep breath, blew it.
The sound that emerged revealed a secret - a flock of colourful birds flew out of the tree!

The children jumped and clapped, watching as the birds flew away.

The mysterious whistle will always be a reminder of the amazing mystery they revealed.

Lily and Ben are playing in the garden. They see a big box with a lock and a label. The label says "Do not open". Lily and Ben are curious. They want to know what is inside the box.

"Let's unlock the box and see what is inside," says Lily.

"But the label says no," says Ben.

"Maybe it is a surprise for us," says Lily. "Maybe it is a toy or a book or a cake."

Lily finds a key under a flower pot. She puts the key in the lock and turns it. The lock clicks and the box opens. Lily and Ben look inside.

They see many colorful things. They see paper, crayons, scissors, glue, stickers, feathers, and more. They are happy. They like to make things with these things. They are creative.

"Wow, look at all these things," says Lily. "We can make anything we want."

"Maybe this is a gift for us," says Ben. "Maybe someone wants us to have fun and be creative."

Lily and Ben take out the things and start to make things. They make hats, masks, cards, animals, and more. They have fun and laugh. They do not see the other label on the other side of the box. The other label says "Happy birthday, Lily and Ben. Love, Grandma and Grandpa."

Miko suggested they look for a movie they didn't own. Poppy was excited to find something new.

Miko said, "Yes, let's get it. We can watch it together."

Once upon a time, there was a pretty girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and her friends. One day, she went to the park with her mommy and daddy. She saw a butterfly and tried to catch it, but it flew away.

Suddenly, Lily started to feel sick and her tummy hurt. Her mommy and daddy took her to the hospital. The doctor said she had a fever and gave her medicine. Lily was scared but her mommy and daddy told her everything would be okay.

After taking the medicine, Lily started to feel better. She looked at herself in the mirror and noticed she had shrunk! "Mommy, daddy, look at me! I'm so small!" she said. Her mommy and daddy laughed and said it was just her imagination. From that day on, Lily always remembered her adventure at the hospital.

One day, Tim took his cool tap outside to play. He put the tap on the ground and turned it on. The water started to flow. Tim was so happy. He played and played with his cool tap.

But then, a big dog came. The dog saw Tim's cool tap and wanted to play too. The dog grabbed the tap in his mouth and ran away. Tim was very sad. He cried and cried. Tim's cool tap was gone, and he could not play with it anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the garden with her toys. One day, while she was playing with her dolls, she found a shiny knife. It looked very attractive, but her mommy always told her not to touch knives because they were dangerous.

Lily decided to show the knife to her friend, Timmy. "Look what I found!" she exclaimed. "It's so pretty!" Timmy looked at the knife and said, "Be careful, Lily. Knives can hurt you."

Suddenly, Lily's dolls started to shrink! "Oh no!" cried Lily. "My dolls are getting smaller!" Timmy looked closer and saw that the dolls were actually growing bigger. "Lily," he said, "you're just getting bigger! The knife must have made you grow!"

Lily was amazed. She had never grown before. She put the knife down and ran to tell her mommy what had happened. From that day on, she never touched knives again, but she was always curious about what other magical things might happen in her garden.

Once there was a mummy and her little girl, Rose. Rose was very small and loved everything pink.

On the day of Rose's third birthday, the mummy had to go to work, so she kissed Rose and said goodbye. She said she would miss her, very much.

Rose was feeling very helpless and alone. She looked everywhere around the house, but mummy wasn't there.

Suddenly Rose remembered! Mummy said she was going to buy her a new pink present. Rose knew that when mummy came back, she'd have something special for her.

So Rose waited and waited, until mummy finally came home. She gave Rose the biggest and brightest pink present Rose had ever seen. Rose was so happy and hugged her mummy tight.

Mummy said she missed Rose so much, and she was never going to leave her alone again. Rose smiled and said "I missed you too mummy."

Sam and Lily are playing with their toys in the living room. Sam has a big truck and Lily has a doll. They are having fun, but they are also very noisy.

"Vroom, vroom, beep, beep!" Sam makes loud sounds with his truck. He drives it around the couch and the table.

"Shh, shh, hush, hush!" Lily says to her doll. She pretends to feed it and rock it to sleep.

"Sam, you are too noisy!" Lily says to her brother. "You are waking up my baby!"

"Sorry, Lily, but I need to drive my truck!" Sam says. "It is a very important job!"

"Can you drive it somewhere else?" Lily asks. "Maybe in the kitchen or the hallway?"

"No, I like it here!" Sam says. "There is more space and more things to see!"

They start to argue and their voices get louder and louder. Their mom hears them from the other room. She comes to the living room and sees the mess and the noise.

"Sam, Lily, what is going on here?" she asks. "Why are you so noisy?"

They both try to explain, but they talk at the same time.

"He is too noisy!" Lily says.

"She is too bossy!" Sam says.

Mom shakes her head and sighs. She picks up a figure from the floor. It is a superhero with a cape and a mask.

"Look, kids, this is a superhero figure!" she says. "He has a special power. He can make people quiet and calm. Do you want to try?"

Sam and Lily nod. They are curious and they like superheroes.

"OK, then, listen carefully," mom says. "When I say the magic word, you have to stop talking and be quiet. The magic word is 'silence'. Ready?"

"Ready!" Sam and Lily say.

Mom holds the figure in front of them and says, "Silence!"

Sam and Lily close their mouths and look at the figure. They are quiet and calm.

Mom smiles and says, "Good job, kids! You are very good at being quiet. Now, do you want to share your toys and play nicely?"

Sam and Lily nod. They say sorry to each other and hug. They decide to play together with the truck and the doll. They are still having fun, but they are not noisy anymore.

Mom is happy and proud. She puts the figure on the shelf and says, "Thank you, superhero! You saved the day!"

Once upon a time, in a big green forest, there lived a small, hairy bear named Bobby. Bobby loved to take long walks in the forest. One sunny day, while on his walk, he saw a big tree with a small hole in it.

Bobby went close to the tree and wanted to see what was inside. He took a peek into the hole and saw a tiny, shy bird. The bird said, "Hello, I'm Timmy. Who are you?"

Bobby smiled and said, "I'm Bobby, the hairy bear. I like to walk in the forest. Do you want to be my friend?" Timmy the bird was very happy and said, "Yes, I want to be your friend!"

From that day on, Bobby and Timmy became best friends. They would go on walks together and play fun games. They were always there for each other, and the forest became an even happier place with their friendship.

One day, Chris's mum called him down for dinner. She had made his favourite - meat! But when Chris saw the meat, he started to laugh and call it names. His mum knew he was being very foolish and told him off.

Baby smiled and kept climbing.

Baby gasped and smiled.

Mommy said, "See Baby, books can be fun!"

The family smiled. They were glad they had taken the train. They knew they had found a kind and understanding friend.

Once upon a time there was a sleepy lion who lived in a jungle with many animals. Everyday he would lay in the sun and take a nap, but he was feeling very grumpy.

One day he decided to let out a big roar. He roared so loudly that all of his friends stopped and looked at him in surprise. The lion was feeling better already!

He told all of the animals in the jungle that he was hungry. Without delay a little orange tiger came running up to him. He gave the lion an orange-it was so sweet and juicy.

The lion was pleased and thanked the tiger. He took a bite and roared loud enough for all the animals in the jungle to hear. Everyone was happy and went back to playing again.

The lion was no longer grumpy or sleepy. He continued to roar with joy until the sun went down.

Sara and Ben like to play in the garden. They look for bugs and flowers and rocks. One day, they see a lizard on the wall. The lizard is green and has a long tail.

"Look, a lizard!" Sara says. "Can we catch it?"

Ben shakes his head. "No, lizards are independent. They do not like to be held. They run away fast."

Sara nods. She wants to be kind to the lizard. She picks up a leaf and holds it out. "Maybe the lizard is hungry. Maybe it likes leaves."

The lizard looks at the leaf. It flicks its tongue. It does not move.

"Pass me a stick," Ben says. "Maybe the lizard likes to play."

Sara passes him a stick. Ben gently touches the lizard's tail with the stick. The lizard jumps and runs along the wall.

"Wow, it is so quick!" Ben says.

Sara laughs. "It is a good game. Let's follow the lizard and see where it goes."

They run after the lizard, but they do not catch it. The lizard is too independent. It finds a hole in the wall and disappears.

Sara and Ben stop and smile. They had fun with the lizard. They say thank you and goodbye to the lizard. Then they look for more things to play with in the garden.

"What's that smell?" said the mouse.

The mouse followed the scent until it came across a bottle that said "POISON" on it.

"Oh no!" the mouse exclaimed, feeling scared.

Suddenly, it heard a voice. It was the mouse's mommy.

"Don't be scared," said Mommy Mouse. "It's just a bottle of poison, it won't hurt you."

The mouse felt safer, and it scurried away, relieved not to be scared anymore. It never forgot to stay away from the bottle of poison, and from then on, it was super-cautious!

Fred found a very unusual surprise on the roof. It was a butterfly! Fred tried to catch the butterfly but it kept flying away from him. When Fred tried to follow it, the butterfly flew even higher! Fred was so excited to find such an unusual creature on his roof.

Then, Fred noticed a jar. It had a very special lid with a hole in it. He realized that he could catch the butterfly by putting the jar over it and then closing the lid.

After a few tries, Fred managed to catch the butterfly! Fred took his butterfly to his garden and watched it flutter around. From that day on, Fred went up onto the roof whenever he wanted to find something unusual.

Once there was a careless little boy. He was always playing outside, running and jumping. One day he was playing in the garden when he saw a big tree. He couldn't resist it and decided to pull at the branches. He pulled hard, so hard that the tree wobbled and it's leaves started to rustle.

The little boy's mom was watching him from the window. She opened the window and yelled "What are you doing, you should be more careful!". The little boy replied "I'm sorry mom, I was just curious and wanted to see what would happen if I pull the tree". His mom, seeing that he was sorry, smiled and said "Well, next time be more careful and don't pull too hard". Then, the little boy promised that he would be more careful and his mom went back inside.

The little boy decided to stay outside and play near the tree. He played for a few hours but this time he made sure that he was careful and didn't pull at the branches of the tree again.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He was only three years old.
One day, Tim wanted to explore outside and went for a walk.
The sky looked strange - it was all foggy. The fog was so thick, he couldn't even see his feet!
Tim stirred the fog with his hands and something amazing happened. Suddenly, he could see for miles ahead! It was like looking through a window.
Tim was very excited. He ran fast through the fog, exploring everywhere until he could no longer see.
He had to find his way home, so he stirred the fog one last time, and eventually, he made it!
He had a wonderful adventure and would never forget the amazing fog and how he stirred it.

Lily started to feel sad and worried. She couldn't stand the thought of losing her toy forever. She decided to ask her mom for help.

Her mom asked Lily to describe her toy and asked her to think of the last time she played with it. Suddenly, Lily remembered that she had left her toy at the park. She was so happy to have found her toy and said the word "thank you" to her mom.

From that day on, Lily learned to be more careful with her toys and to always remember where she put them. She was happy to have her ordinary day turn into an extraordinary one because she found her missing toy.

Once there was a boy named Tom. Tom loved to play with his football. He would kick it around gently in the park every day. One day his mum and dad said to Tom, "It's time to go home now". But Tom didn't listen. He wanted to keep playing with the football. So his mum and dad said, "We'll remind you when it's time to go home!"

Tom continued to kick the football around gently. Then he heard his mum and dad calling to him. "Tom! Time to go home now, remember?" cried his mum. Tom was sad. He wanted to keep playing with the football. But he remembered what his parents had said. So Tom put the football away and went home.

At home, his mum said, "Remember what we said, Tom? We'll remind you when it's time to go home!" Tom smiled. He knew he could have more fun with his football tomorrow.

Ben and Mia were playing in the yard. They found a big sheet and wanted to make a tent. They looked for two sticks to hold up the sheet. Ben saw a stick near the fence. He ran to get it. But he did not see the dog next door. The dog barked and jumped at Ben. Ben was scared and dropped the stick. He ran back to Mia.

"Ben, what happened?" Mia asked. She saw Ben's face was red and his eyes were wet.

"The dog, the dog!" Ben said. "He was angry and loud. He wanted my stick. I was afraid and let go."

Mia hugged Ben. "It's okay, Ben. The dog can't hurt you. He is behind the fence. You are brave. You can try again."

Ben shook his head. "No, no. I don't want to. I am ashamed. I am not brave. I am a baby."

Mia looked at Ben. She had an idea. She smiled and said, "Ben, you are not a baby. You are my brother. And I have a plan. We can carry the sheet together. We can find another stick. We can make the tent. And we can hide from the dog. He will not see us. He will not bark. He will be quiet. Do you want to try?"

Ben looked at Mia. He saw her smile. He felt her hug. He heard her plan. He nodded and said, "Yes, yes. I want to try. We can carry the sheet together. We can make the tent. We can hide from the dog. We can have fun."

Mia and Ben carried the sheet together. They found another stick. They made the tent. They hid from the dog. They had fun. They were not ashamed. They were happy.

Once upon a time there was a gorilla. This gorilla liked to spin around and around. It spun so fast that it looked like a tornado! All of the other animals in the jungle watched the gorilla with amazement.

Then one day the gorilla found something tasty. It was a round piece of fruit! The gorilla took one bite, and it was so delicious! The gorilla started spinning even faster, this time with the tasty fruit in its hands.

The gorilla spun and spun until it felt dizzy. Finally the gorilla stopped spinning, and the juicy fruit was gone. The other animals laughed and cheered, it was so fun to watch the gorilla spin with the tasty fruit!

Once there was a puppy called True. He was a very lively pup with lots of energy. Every day he would run around the yard and bark with joy. But one day, True started to frown. His family was so sad. They had never seen him this way before.

True's family decided to take him for a walk. As soon as they got outside, his eyes lit up. True started to bark with excitement. He laughed and ran around, just as lively as before.

True's family was so happy to see him happy again. They knew that true was truly a lively pup. As they walked, they saw True smiling and they knew they'd made the right decision. True had reminded them that being happy should be kept true.

Once there was a little girl named Lila who was very tired. She had been so busy playing that she hadn't taken the time to rest. Lila wanted to keep playing but her body was telling her that she needed to stop and take a break.

One day, Lila's mommy noticed how tired she was. She hugged Lila and said, "Lila, you need time to rest and heal." Lila didn't understand what her mommy meant.

That night, Lila went to bed early and fell asleep. When she woke up the next day, she felt so much better. She realized that taking time to rest made her feel better.

Now, whenever Lila feels tired she takes time to rest and heal. And soon, she is full of energy again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lizzy. She was very restless and loved to rush around.

One day, Lizzy was in the kitchen when she noticed a brush laying on the counter. She became very excited and shouted, "A brush!

Mommy came into the kitchen and saw Lizzy playing with the brush. She said, "Lizzy, be careful with that brush. It's not a toy!

But Lizzy didn't want to listen. She was too excited and rushed around the kitchen with the brush in her hand.

Suddenly, Mommy grabbed the brush from Lizzy. "No, Lizzy. That's not for you. You need to be more careful and not rush around so much.

Lizzy sighed and said, "OK Mommy.

From that day on, Lizzy learned to be more careful when she rushed around, and was thankful that her mommy looked out for her.

Once upon a time there was a car. It drove up and down the street. The traffic passed it and went on its way. The car took a turn and went down a road. It was a good road and the car drove very fast.

The driver of the car saw something up ahead. It was a big truck! The truck had stopped and was blocking the road. The driver honked but the truck didn't move. He shouted out his window, "Why are you stopping here?"

The driver of the truck said, "There's an accident on this road. You can't pass." The driver of the car tried reversing but there was too much traffic behind him. He couldn't pass. He had to wait in the car until the traffic cleared.

The driver was very angry and had a bad ending. He was stuck in the same place for hours until the traffic moved. He learned a valuable lesson that day. It's always good to obey traffic rules!

Lily and Max are twins. They look the same, but they like different things. Lily likes to draw and Max likes to run. They are unique.

One day, they go to the park with their family. Mom and Dad sit on a bench and watch them play. Lily finds a big tree and leans on it. She takes out her crayons and paper and starts to draw the tree. She likes the green leaves and the brown bark.

Max sees a slide and runs to it. He climbs up the ladder and slides down fast. He likes the wind in his hair and the sun on his face. He slides again and again.

"Look, Mom, look, Dad!" Max shouts. "I'm sliding so fast!"

Mom and Dad smile and clap. They are proud of Max.

"Look, Mom, look, Dad!" Lily says. "I drew the tree!"

Mom and Dad walk to Lily and lean on the tree too. They look at her drawing and hug her. They are proud of Lily.

"You are both amazing!" Mom says. "You are unique and we love you!"

Lily and Max smile and hug their family. They are happy. They are unique and they are loved.

Once there was a little boy named Tom. He loved playing with all his toys and going outside to explore the world.

One day, Tom was playing in the garden when he heard a noise coming from the bushes. He slowly came closer and saw a little bunny trembling with fear. "Hi there," said Tom softly.

The bunny did not answer. It just stared at Tom with big eyes and kept shaking. Suddenly, it jumped up and ran away.

Tom felt sorry for the bunny and wanted to help. He decided to get it a treat. He went inside the house and got an apple, but when he came back, the bunny was gone. Instead, he saw a small, deaf cat.

Tom put the apple down and stepped back. The cat stopped trembling and slowly made its way towards the apple. Tom smiled as she ate her treat. He said goodbye and went back inside the house.

Tom and Sam are brothers. They like to play with their new toy rocket. The rocket is red and shiny. It can make sounds and lights.

One day, they decide to go to the park with their rocket. They want to launch it in the air. They pack some fuel in a bottle. The fuel is water mixed with soap. It makes bubbles.

At the park, they find a big hill. They put the rocket on the ground and fill it with fuel. They press a button and the rocket starts to beep.

"Ready, Sam?" Tom asks.

"Ready, Tom!" Sam says.

They count together. "One, two, three!"

They step back and watch the rocket. It zooms up in the sky. It leaves a trail of bubbles behind. It makes a loud whoosh.

"Wow!" Tom and Sam shout. "It's so high!"

They clap and cheer. They are very happy. They love their new toy rocket.

Once there was a little boy named Sam. He liked to clap his hands and laugh. One day, his mommy took him for a walk downtown. On their way, Sam saw a tall building with the word "Lawyer" written on it. He said, "Mommy, what is that building for?" His mommy said, "That's where lawyers work. They help people solve their problems." Sam clapped his hands and said, "Yay! That's great!"

They kept walking until they saw a man sitting on a bench. His clothes were all wrinkled and he had a tight frown on his face. Sam's mommy said, "Oh. That man must not be feeling so happy." Sam was curious and asked, "Why not?"

His mommy said, "Maybe he is having trouble with the law. That's why he needs a lawyer." Sam said, "Oh, I see." He started to clap his hands and said, "Mommy, I want to be a lawyer when I grow up."

So his mommy hugged him tight and said, "Yes, a lawyer is a great job if you want to help people." Sam smiled and said, "Yes! I want to help people when I grow up."

The moral of the story is: Law is important and it's important to help people.

Once upon a time there was a goose. He was thick with feathers and he could mark the sky with his wings. Every morning the goose would wake up and fly for miles and miles.

One day, the goose was flying high in the sky. Suddenly, the goose heard a voice. When he looked down, he saw a small 3 year old boy.

The boy looked up at the goose and yelled, "Hello, Goose! The goose replied, "Hello! What are you doing here?

The boy explained, "I came out to mark the sky and find clouds I can fly on! Would you like to join me?

The goose agreed and soon the two were flying together. They marked the sky with their wings and found clouds to sit and rest on. The boy and the goose had a wonderful time and they became the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a fridge that she loved to own. She liked to put her favorite foods in it, like apples and carrots.

One day, Lily's nosy little brother, Max, decided to look inside the fridge. He saw a piece of cake that he wanted to eat. But Lily told him that it was her special treat and he couldn't have it. Max got upset and started to cry.

Lily realized that it wasn't fair to keep all the treats to herself. She decided to share the cake with Max. Max was happy and they both enjoyed the cake together. Lily learned that sharing is important and it makes everyone happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big jungle. In the jungle, there lived many animals like lions, monkeys, and elephants. One day, a new animal came to the jungle. It was a funny snake. The snake was very long and had a lot of colors on its skin.

The other animals were scared of the snake because they had never seen anything like it before. But the snake was very friendly and wanted to make friends with everyone. So, it went around the jungle and introduced itself to all the animals.

The monkey was the first to talk to the snake. The monkey said, "Hello, Mr. Snake. You look very funny. Do you want to play with me?" The snake was happy to hear that and said, "Yes, I would love to play with you, Mr. Monkey." And from that day on, the snake and the monkey became very good friends.

Once upon a time there was a girl. She loved to whistle. She would whistle all the time. She was very fast.

One day, she went on a long walk. As she whistled, she saw a piece of gum. She knew she wanted to have it.

So she ran to the gum and picked it up. She smiled and said, "Yay! Now I can chew gum while I whistle!"

But then she heard someone say, "Hey, that's my gum!"

It was her friend, who had been looking for the gum. She smiled, gave her friend the gum, and said, "Let's both whistle while we chew this yummy gum!"

And so the two friends chewed gum and whistled a happy, fast song together. And they both had lots of fun.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Ducky. Ducky loved to swim in the pond every day. One day, Ducky met a frog named Froggy.

"Hi, Froggy! Do you want to swim with me in the pond?" asked Ducky.

"I can't swim, but I can show you something useful," replied Froggy.

Froggy showed Ducky a big leaf that he could use as a boat to float on the pond. Ducky was so happy and thanked Froggy for showing him something useful. From that day on, Ducky and Froggy became good friends and went on many adventures together on their leaf boat.

Once there was a little girl named Sarah. She wanted to wear her mama's shoes. She put on one shoe, but it did not fit. "It's too small," said Sarah. She felt ashamed.

Mama told her not to worry. "Sarah, let's try the other shoe," Mama said. Together, Sarah and Mama tried on the other shoe, but it didn't fit either. Mama gave her a hug. "It's okay, Sarah. Some things don't fit," said Mama.

Sarah was sad. She wanted the shoes to fit, but it was too late. She had already tried both shoes.

Suddenly, Mama had an idea. She had a great big bucket of water. "Sarah, try the shoe in the water," said Mama. Sarah dipped one of the shoes into the water and it fit perfectly! She was so happy!

"See, Sarah," said Mama with a smile, "Sometimes things fit in water." Sarah smiled with joy and hugged Mama. She was no longer ashamed.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. He was very proud because he had a perfect name. He would rub his name often, just to make sure it stayed perfect.

One day, the boy went to his favourite place to rub his name. But, when he got there, he was shocked. His perfect name had become all rubbed away!

The little boy was so sad. He was not proud anymore, because his name was gone. He tried to rub it back, but nothing worked.

The little boy never got his perfect name back. He stayed sad and even stopped rubbing his name.

The end.

Once there was a noisy march. Little Nathan was very curious to see what was going on. He ran outside and saw all the people marching together. It was very exciting and Nathan thought it was, too!

When the march got closer and closer to his house, the noise got louder and louder. All the screaming and shouting made Nathan scared. He tried to close the windows to block out the noise, but it was still very loud.

His mum saw he was upset. She went outside and saw the march. Then she went back inside and comforted Nathan. She told him that the march was peaceful and that the people were just being noisy.

Eventually, the march moved on and there was peace once again. Nathan was thankful that it didn't disturb his sleep too much. That evening he learned a valuable lesson: sometimes life can be noisy but that it's always best to stay calm and not get too worked up.

Once there was an enormous pumpkin. It was bigger than anything anyone had ever seen. It was so big that it took two grown-ups to move it.
Everyone was amazed at the gigantic pumpkin.
Then the pumpkin started to rot. It went brown and slimy and started to fall apart. Gross!
Everyone was so disappointed. The enormous pumpkin was now a slimy mess.
But then something magical happened! From the slimy mess of the pumpkin came a whole lot of little baby pumpkins. They weren't as big as the enormous one, but they were still special.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little kitten. The kitten was wet because it was raining outside. The wet kitten was sad and wanted to find a warm place to stay.

The kitten went on a walk to find a warm place. On the way, the kitten saw a big tree. The kitten tried to climb the tree to get away from the wet ground. But the kitten did not know how to climb very well, so it did stumble and fall back down.

A nice girl saw the kitten and picked it up. She took the wet kitten to her home. The girl gave the kitten a warm blanket and some food. The kitten was very happy and did not stumble anymore. The girl and the kitten became best friends and lived happily ever after.

Once there was an elderly man who enjoyed going for walks in the park. One day, as he was strolling, he saw an insect flying around. The insect was wrapped in a colourful piece of cloth. The elderly man was intrigued, so he followed it.

The insect led him to a pond, where it rested on a lily pad floating on the water. The cloth that was wrapped around the insect began to slowly unfold and it was then that the elderly man realized that the cloth was a beautiful butterfly! It fluttered its colourful wings and then flew away.

The elderly man was captivated and he knew where he would go for his daily walks from now on - the ponds in the park! He would be there, in the early mornings, watching the peaceful creatures wrap themselves in their pretty wings and take off in search of adventure.

Tim asked his mom, "Can I keep the drum?" His mom said, "Yes, but we need to store it in the garage when you are not playing with it." Tim was very happy and played with the drum all day. He made loud and fun sounds with it.

At night, Tim stored the drum in the garage like his mom said. The next day, he found another mysterious drum in his room. He played with both drums and had lots of fun. Tim loved his mysterious drums and took good care of them.

His mother smiled and replied, "Yes, Jack, but don't pick them all. You can pick one for your special collection."

Jack asked, "What kind of collection?"

His mother said, "A yellow flower collection. You can find more of them and it will be so beautiful."

Jack smiled. He carefully chose the most beautiful yellow flower and carefully put it in his pocket. He couldn't wait to look for more. He was so excited that he ran off to find more yellow flowers.

He would wonder if there were more yellow flowers out there. As he searched, his excitement only grew. He was sure he would find more by the end of the day.

Once upon a time, there was a player. He was an ordinary player, but he was good at the game. One day, he decided to practice extra hard. He grabbed a stick, and sharpened it. Then he started to practice.

He practiced and practiced until finally, he got very sharp. He hit the ball with the stick, and it flew far and fast!

His friend watched in awe. "That was amazing!" his friend said.

The player smiled. "Thanks," he replied. "But I think I can do even better."

He stepped back, and started to practice some more. He swung the stick, and again and again, the ball flew even further. He had become really sharp!

His friend was very impressed. "Wow!" his friend said. "Now you're the best player ever!"

The player smiled, and nodded in agreement. He was the sharpest player in town!

But her mom said, "You will be okay, Lily. You will make new friends. Look, there is an elephant toy in the corner. You like elephants, right?"

Lily nodded. She liked elephants a lot. They were big and gray and had long noses. She walked to the corner and picked up the elephant toy. It was soft and cuddly. She hugged it.

Then she saw a boy with curly hair and a big smile. He came to her and said, "Hi, I'm Ben. Do you like elephants too?"

Lily smiled back. She said, "Yes, I do. This is my new elephant. Do you want to play with me?"

Ben said, "Sure, I do. I have a new car. We can make them go vroom vroom together."

Lily and Ben played with the elephant and the car. They had fun. They laughed and talked. Lily did not feel worry anymore. She felt happy. She had a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved going to the zoo with his mommy and daddy. The zoo was famous for having lots of animals like lions, tigers, and monkeys.

One day, when Timmy and his family went to the zoo, they saw that the animals were all sad and lonely. They weren't moving or making any noise. Timmy asked his mommy why the animals were sad. His mommy told him that they missed their families and friends who lived in the wild.

Timmy didn't want the animals to be sad anymore, so he decided to gather some toys and snacks for them. He brought his favorite teddy bear, some bananas, and some cookies. When they got to the zoo, Timmy gave the toys and snacks to the animals. The animals were so happy and started playing with the toys and eating the snacks. Timmy felt happy too, knowing that he had made the animals feel better.

Sam and Ben are brothers. They like to play with their toy cars and trucks. One day, they hear a big noise outside. They run to the window and see a big truck arrive. The truck has many oats in the back. Oats are good for horses and cows.

"Wow, look at all the oats!" Sam says. "Can we go and see them?"

"OK, but we have to be careful," Ben says. "The truck driver might not like it."

They put on their shoes and jackets and go outside. They sneak behind the truck and climb on the back. They see many bags of oats. They touch them and smell them. They are soft and sweet.

"Let's take some oats home," Sam says. "We can feed them to our stuffed animals."

"Good idea," Ben says. "But we have to be quick."

They grab a bag of oats and try to lift it. But it is too heavy for them. They drop it on the ground. The bag breaks and oats spill everywhere.

"Oh no!" Sam says. "What do we do?"

"Run!" Ben says.

But it is too late. The truck driver hears the noise and comes to the back. He sees the boys and the oats. He is very angry.

"Hey, you little thieves!" he shouts. "What are you doing with my oats?"

He grabs Sam and Ben by their jackets and drags them to the front. He puts them in his truck and locks the door. He calls their mom and tells her what they did.

Sam and Ben are scared and sorry. They cry and hug each other. They wish they never touched the oats. They have to wait a long time for their mom to arrive. They know they are in big trouble. They have a bad ending.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who loved to soar high in the sky. She flew higher and higher, taking in the texture of the clouds and the peaceful blue sky.

The little bird had one wish - to soar even higher. So every day, she flapped her wings powerfully and flew higher than before, trying to reach her goal.

Then one day, the little bird got a surprise. As she flew through the clouds, she heard a voice say "Hello! I'm the sky bird. Would you like to come with me?"

The little bird was excited. Without hesitating, she said yes, and soon the sky bird was soaring higher with the little bird following close by.

The sky bird showed the little bird the beauty of the world from above. Together they flew in peace, exploring the world from higher and higher heights.

The little bird had never felt so happy before. She loved soaring with the sky bird and soon reached her goal of flying even higher.

She ran outside to thank them and hugged them all. They were so happy that the little girl was smiling again.

Bobbie watched her body as it splashed into the water. As she swam, she saw a big rock. She climbed it and looked around. Suddenly she spotted a large creature. Bobbie was scared, but she was curious. She watched as it swam towards her. Bobbie's Grandpa had told her that if you stay still and are reliable, animals will usually leave you alone. Bobbie stays still and watched the creature. Just then the creature stopped and went away. Bobbie was relieved. She got off the rock and was careful as she swam back. She was happy to be back on shore and hugged her reliable Grandpa. He was so proud that Bobbie was brave and watched her body.

Once upon a time there was a very nice boy called Jack. He liked to play and talk with the other kids in the park. One day, Jack was playing rough and made another child cry. His parents were very sad when they heard what had happened.

Jack's mom said to him, "Jack, why did you make the other child cry? That was not very nice."

Jack said, "I don't know, mom. I just wanted to play."

Jack's mom said, "Jack, when you talk to other people, you need to be kind. Not harsh. Harsh words hurt, just like pain. We never want to hurt anyone, OK?"

Jack nodded and said, "OK, mom. I'll try to be kinder when I talk to people."

Since then, Jack learned to be kind and gentle with his words. He never forgot the lesson his mom taught him and he always tried to be nice to others.

Moral of the story: Being kind and gentle with our words is important, as harsh words can hurt just like physical pain.

From that day onwards they were the best of friends. They would play and tease each other every day and the bear always felt so jolly.

Once there was a big fish. One day, the fish swam around looking for something. He swam up to an island and saw a seal.

The fish wanted to be friends with the seal, so he knelt down in the water. He was careful not to splash the seal.

The seal was a little scared at first, but then he smiled. He wagged his flippers, which was a sign that he liked the fish.

The fish and the seal were very happy to be together. They spent the day playing tag, swimming and eating together.

The fish and the seal were best friends from that day forward. They were careful to look out for each other, just like good friends do!

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She loved to spin around and around. Every night she would spin until the sun went down.

One night, the little girl was spinning when suddenly the sunset was the prettiest sight she had ever seen. She stopped spinning and watched the colors of the sky change from yellow to purple. It was so beautiful.

The little girl knew that sunsets were important, so she promised to always take time to stop spinning and watch the sunset together.

From then on, every evening she would spin until the sun started to go down, and then she would stop and watch the sunset. The little girl felt lucky to be able to enjoy such a beautiful sight.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to collect coins. One day, she found a shiny coin on the ground. It was very thin and she was very happy.

Lily went to her friend, Timmy, to show him her special coin. "Look, Timmy! I found a shiny coin!" she said. Timmy nodded his head and said, "Wow, that's so cool!"

Lily and Timmy decided to go to the store to buy some candy with the coin. They walked to the candy aisle and picked out their favorite sweets. When they got to the cashier, Lily handed the thin coin to the lady. The lady smiled and said, "Thank you, sweetie!"

Lily and Timmy walked out of the store, holding their candy. "That was fun, let's do it again sometime," said Timmy. Lily nodded her head and said, "Yes, let's find more coins!" And they walked away, happy and content with their treasure.

Mia was playing in the garden one sunny day when she saw a pepper. She picked it up and it spoke to her. The pepper said, "I'm a bossy pepper and I want you to take me home!" Mia was a bit surprised, but she agreed. She took the pepper home and put it on the kitchen table.

Mia's mom was busy cooking dinner when Mia showed her the pepper. Mom was surprised, too! She asked, "What do you want me to do with the pepper?" The bossy pepper spoke again, "I want to be in your dinner!"

Mia's mom smiled and looked around for things to add to the dinner. She put some onions, garlic, and other spices in the pan and then mixed in the pepper. Mia and her mom had a delicious dinner that night and the pepper was the best part!

Once upon a time there was a bear named Bob. Bob liked solving puzzles and he was always looking for something to do. One cold winter day, Bob was very bored and wanted something to do. He looked around and noticed a big puzzle on the floor. He was glad he found it and excited to try to solve it.

Bob started to put the puzzle pieces together and was soon able to solve it. As he looked at the completed puzzle he was so glad. The puzzle was beautiful, it was a picture of some trees in the snow.

Bob jumped up and ran out of the room. He wanted to look at the trees in real life, so he ran outside into the cold winter air. He looked around and noticed the trees just outside. He was so glad to see them in winter's beauty.

Bob smiled, happy that he had solved the puzzle and that he was able to see the trees in person. He would never forget this day and would always love winter for its beauty.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a big, wide forest. In the forest lived a little rabbit named Benny. Benny loved to hop and play all day long. One day, Benny met a friendly deer named Dolly.

"Hello, Benny!" said Dolly. "What are you doing today?"

"I'm going to brush my fur," replied Benny. "What about you?"

"I'm on my way to bow to the king of the forest," said Dolly.

Benny wanted to come along, but he was too small to bow to the king. He felt sad and left out. But Dolly had an idea. "Why don't you come with me and we can ask the king if there's anything you can do to help in the forest?"

Benny was excited and agreed to go with Dolly. When they arrived at the king's castle, they saw that the king was upset because the river had become too wide and was causing problems for the animals in the forest.

Dolly and Benny offered to help by using their brush and bow to clear the river. The king was pleased and thanked them for their help. Benny was happy to have made a difference in the forest and to have made a new friend in Dolly.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Emily and Sarah. They liked to have fun. One day Emily thought of a great idea. She said, "Let's have a party!"

Sarah smiled and said, "That sounds like fun, let's do it!"

So Emily and Sarah put together a lively party. They got decorations and yummy treats. They invited all their friends and soon the house was full of laughter and smiles.

Everyone had a great time. They played, sang, and laughed. Everyone said the party was the best ever!

At the end of the night, Emily and Sarah were so happy they had decided to have a party. They felt they had become closer friends and had lots of wonderful memories.

Mommy was baking a cake in a pan. She mixed together some ingredients and put the pan in the oven. She set the timer so she wouldn't forget. After a while, the timer beeped. Mommy took the pan out of the oven and saw that the cake was fair. It was light brown, and smelled very good!

Mommy smiled, and cut the cake into little pieces. Then, she put them on a plate and brought the plate over to the table. Mommy said, "Who wants cake?" Everyone smiled and said, "Me!" They all had some cake, and it was delicious!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy was very hungry and wanted to eat something yummy. His mommy said they were going to a hotel to eat food.

On the way to the hotel, Timmy saw a big truck with lots of dirt on it. His mommy said, "Look Timmy, that truck needs to wash the dirt off."

When they arrived at the hotel, Timmy saw a big sign that said "All You Can Eat". He was so happy and ate lots of food. After eating, Timmy went to wash his hands in the bathroom. He saw a man who was very dirty and needed to wash his clothes.

The man thanked Timmy for helping him and gave him a big hug. Timmy felt happy that he could help someone who needed to wash their clothes. From that day on, Timmy always remembered to wash his hands before eating and to help people who need to wash their things.

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to play outside. One day, they see white snow on the ground. They put on their warm hats, coats, and boots. They run outside to play.

"Look, Ben, snow!" Sara says. "We can make a snowman!"

"OK, Sara, let's do it!" Ben says. They roll big balls of snow and stack them up. They find a carrot, a hat, and a scarf for the snowman. They give him eyes, a nose, and a mouth.

"Hello, Mr. Snowman!" Sara says. "You are very cool and nice!"

"Hello, Sara and Ben!" Mr. Snowman says. "You are very kind and fun!"

Sara and Ben are surprised. They did not know that the snowman can talk.

"Can you play with us, Mr. Snowman?" Ben asks.

"Sure, I can play with you, Ben!" Mr. Snowman says. "But we have to be quick. The sun is coming out and I will melt soon."

Sara and Ben play with Mr. Snowman. They make snow angels, throw snowballs, and slide on the ice. They have a lot of fun.

But soon, the snow starts to melt. Mr. Snowman becomes smaller and smaller.

"Goodbye, Sara and Ben!" Mr. Snowman says. "Thank you for playing with me! Maybe I will see you again next winter!"

"Goodbye, Mr. Snowman!" Sara and Ben say. "We will miss you! You are our friend!"

They hug Mr. Snowman and go back inside. They are happy and sad. They hope to see Mr. Snowman again. They drink hot chocolate and tell their mom about their adventure.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play in their bedroom. They have a big slide that goes from their bed to the floor. They take turns to slide down and laugh.

One day, Anna slides down too fast and bumps her head on the wall. She cries and holds her head. Ben hears her and runs to her. He hugs her and says, "Are you okay, Anna? I'm sorry you hurt your head."

Anna stops crying and smiles at Ben. She says, "Thank you, Ben. You are very compassionate. You always help me when I'm sad or hurt."

Ben smiles back and says, "You're welcome, Anna. You are my sister and I love you. Do you want to slide again?"

Anna nods and says, "Yes, I do. But let's slide slower and be careful."

They hold hands and slide down together. They giggle and have fun. They are happy and compassionate twins.

When Julia got onto the swing she was so happy! She flew back and forth, higher and higher. Suddenly, Julia noticed something hidden behind the trees. She slowed down her swing and tried to see what it was. Suddenly something moved and Julia screamed, she wanted to get down from the swing very quickly.

Julia's parents heard her scream and ran over to see what was wrong. When they saw what it was, they all started to laugh. It was a baby rabbit, hiding behind the tree. Julia was so relieved and her parents said she could try to feed it a carrot if she was careful.

So it turned out that Julia had found a hidden surprise and ended up getting her swing ride anyway.

Jill said, "Let's keep walking. I don't mind if I get wet!"

So, they kept walking in the rain until it stopped. When the rain stopped, Jill and her mom were both a bit wet but they had had lots of fun.

While Timmy was taking a shower, he heard a loud noise. It was a bad guy! Timmy quickly got out of the shower and grabbed his toy gun. He ran to the living room and saw the bad guy. Timmy pointed his toy gun and said, "I'm going to shoot you!"

The bad guy laughed and said, "You can't shoot me with that toy gun." But Timmy didn't listen. He pulled the trigger and...nothing happened. The bad guy took Timmy's toy gun and ran away.

Timmy was sad and scared. He realized that his toy gun couldn't keep him safe. He promised to never play with guns again and to always listen to his mom. From that day on, Timmy took showers and went to bed feeling safe and happy.

John's dad was also smiling and said, "You sure did, John. You made something very impressive."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who really wanted to explore the world. So one day, she decided to take a walk in a nearby valley. As she walked down the path, she noticed something that made her stop and stare: old, bendy trees that were filling up the valley. She wanted to get closer and explore, but the trees looked so strong and tall.

The little girl soon realized she wasn't alone in the valley. A big, brown bear was standing right in front of her. The girl was a bit scared, but the bear looked at her with compassionate eyes. She knew the bear wouldn't hurt her. The bear seemed to understand that she wanted to explore the valley, so he slowly stepped aside and allowed her to pass.

The girl was amazed by the beauty of the valley. As she looked around, she noticed there were lots of flowers, tall grass, and colorful butterflies. She felt safe, but also excited and curious. She knew the bear had yielded to her, allowing her to explore the valley without any problems.

The little girl thanked the bear for his compassion, and for allowing her to explore the valley. She promised him she would always cherish the beauty of the valley and never forget the experience of being allowed to explore it. The bear smiled, and then slowly walked away, leaving the girl to explore and treasure the valley.

Johnny saw Timmy's toys and wanted to play with them too. Timmy didn't want Johnny to play with his toys because he was afraid Johnny would break them.

Johnny said, "Timmy, can I please play with your toys? I promise I won't break them."

Timmy replied, "No, I don't want you to poke my toys and make them itch."

Johnny felt sad because he really wanted to play with Timmy's toys. But then, Johnny had an idea. He said, "Timmy, I have my own toys at home. Let's go to my house and play with my toys instead!"

Timmy thought about it and agreed. They went to Johnny's house and had lots of fun playing with Johnny's toys together. Timmy realized that sharing his toys with his friend was much more fun than keeping them all to himself.

Once upon a time, there was a little fish. The fish was very hot and helpless in the bright sun. The fish wanted to find a way to cool down, so it took a deep breath and dove into the sea.

As the little fish dove down and down, it began to feel the lovely coolness of the sea. It was so nice and cool under the waves. The fish swam around, feeling more and more relaxed.

Soon the fish found an unfamiliar object. The object was made of metal and was getting warmer and warmer from the heat of the sun. It felt like a prison, trapping the little fish.

But the fish was brave and it decided to dive even deeper. And as it dove and dove, the little fish finally found a secret cave. In the cave, it was much cooler and the little fish was safe.

The little fish was very happy that it had found the secret cave. The fish stayed there until its scales had cooled off and it felt no more heat. For the first time, the little fish felt cool and free.

Tom and Mia are playing in a big maze. They like to find new ways and see new things. But they also want to find the exit. The exit is where they can go back to their mom and dad.

They see a sign that says "Exit this way". They follow the sign, but they find a big wall. The wall has a red door, but the door is locked. There is a puzzle on the door. The puzzle has four shapes: a circle, a square, a triangle and a star.

"Can you solve the puzzle?" Tom asks Mia.

"I don't know," Mia says. "Maybe we have to match the shapes."

They look around and see four holes in the wall. Each hole has a shape on it. They try to put the shapes in the holes, but they don't fit.

"Maybe we have to switch the shapes," Tom says.

They switch the shapes and try again. This time, they fit. The door makes a click sound and opens.

"Yay, we solved the puzzle!" Mia says.

They go through the door and see the exit. They run to their mom and dad and hug them.

"We found the exit!" Tom says.

"We solved the puzzle!" Mia says.

Mom and dad are very proud of them. They say they are very smart and brave. They give them a big kiss and a cookie. Tom and Mia are very happy. They like to play in the maze, but they also like to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved to bake cookies. She would mix the dough and cut them into different shapes like circles, squares, and triangles. Her cookies were always tasty and everyone in the neighborhood loved them.

One day, a big, mean wolf came to her house and said, "Give me all your cookies or I will blow your house down!" Lily was scared, but she didn't want to give away her cookies. So she said, "No, these are mine. You can't have them."

The wolf got angry and blew down her house. All her cookies were gone and she was left with nothing. The moral of the story is that sometimes it's better to share and be kind, even if it means giving up something you love.

Once upon a time, there lived a bear named Jack. He was a strong and brave bear and he liked to protect his friends. One day, Jack met a little duck who was very sad. The duck had lost her beautiful striped feathers and she was feeling miserable. So Jack decided to help the duck.

He took the duck to a special place with a pond and lots of trees. There were also many colourful feathers that were very soft and comfortable. Jack told the duck to stay by the pond and he went off to find some snacks for her.

Jack also gathered many colourful feathers to make a blanket for the duck. He worked very hard creating the beautiful blanket and soon he had made the most beautiful striped blanket ever!

The little duck was so happy when she saw the blanket Jack had made. She thanked Jack for protecting her and helping her to feel warm and safe. From that day on, the little duck had her own special striped blanket to keep her warm, and she never felt sad again.

Lila was very happy. It was her birthday. She had a big cake with candles. She had many balloons. She had many friends. They played games and sang songs.

Lila's mom gave her a present. It was a ring. It was shiny and pretty. Lila loved it. She put it on her finger. She said thank you to her mom.

Lila wanted to show her ring to her friends. She ran to them. She did not see the balloon on the floor. She stepped on it. It popped. It made a loud noise. Lila was scared. She dropped her ring. It rolled away.

Lila cried. She looked for her ring. She could not find it. She was very sad. She did not want to eat cake. She did not want to play games. She did not want to sing songs.

Lila's mom hugged her. She said, "Don't worry, Lila. We will find your ring. It is not lost. It is just hiding. Maybe it is under the table. Maybe it is behind the sofa. Maybe it is in the trash. We will look everywhere."

Lila and her mom looked everywhere. They found many things. They found a sock. They found a toy. They found a spoon. But they did not find the ring.

Lila was about to give up. She said, "Mom, I think my ring is gone. I think someone took it. I think it is not fair. It was my birthday. It was my present. It was my ring."

Lila's mom said, "Lila, I know you are upset. But you should not blame others. Maybe someone found your ring and did not know it was yours. Maybe they wanted to give it back to you. Maybe they are looking for you right now. You should be kind and honest. You should share and forgive. You should be happy and grateful. You have a cake. You have balloons. You have friends. You have me. You have many things to be happy about."

Lila thought about what her mom said. She realized she was being selfish and angry. She said, "Mom, you are right. I am sorry. I should not be mean. I should be nice. I should not be sad. I should be happy. I have many things to be happy about. Thank you, mom. I love you."

Lila and her mom hugged again. They went back to the party. They ate cake. It was delicious. They played games. They had fun. They sang songs. They were loud.

Then, Lila heard a voice. It was her friend Ben. He said, "Lila, I have something for you. I found it on the floor. I think it is yours. It is shiny and pretty. It is a ring."

Lila was surprised. She saw her ring. It was in Ben's hand. She said, "Ben, thank you. You are very nice. You are very honest. You are my friend. This is my ring. It is my birthday. It is my present. But I want to share it with you. You can wear it too. It is our ring."

Ben was happy. He said, "Lila, you are welcome. You are very kind. You are very generous. You are my friend. This is your ring. It is your birthday. It is your present. But I want to share it with you. You can wear it too. It is our ring."

Lila and Ben smiled. They put the ring on their fingers. They showed it to their friends. They said, "Look, we have a ring. It is shiny and pretty. It is our ring. We share it. We are friends. We are happy."

Lila and Ben learned a lesson. They learned to be kind and honest. They learned to share and forgive. They learned to be happy and grateful. They learned to love and be loved. They learned to be good.
