Once upon a time, there was a patient ground. He liked to mix things. Everywhere he went, he'd take a bag and mix the things he found there.

One day, he walked into a garden. He saw all sorts of flowers, fruits and vegetables. He was so excited, he started to mix them together! He found some oranges, so he tossed them in his bag. Then he added some purple flowers and some green leaves.

Once he was done, he took his bag to the kitchen and carefully mixed it together. In the end, he made a delicious juice! All the other vegetables and fruits were mixed together too and they made a tasty soup.

The patient ground kept on mixing things wherever he went and soon everyone wanted to taste what he made. So, each time he mixed something, the yummy dishes he created would be shared with all the people around.

Mama and Papa put their arms around the baby. A prize was waiting for him. Papa put his arm around the baby's shoulder and said, "I have a surprise prize for you, my sweet little one. You can have it if you are graceful and kind."

The baby smiled and looked up at the prize with wide eyes. It was a rocking chair made of wood and it shined in the sun. Mama said it was a very special prize and the baby should take care of it.

The baby gently touched the chair. Then he looked up at Mama and Papa and gave them a big hug. He was so happy.

Mama patted the baby on the back and said, "That's very graceful of you, my little one." Papa smiled and said, "You are a very special little boy and you deserve this prize. We love you very much."

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside and get dirty. His mom always told him to change his clothes when he came inside. But Timmy didn't like changing his clothes. He thought it was boring.

One day, Timmy went to play in the mud. He forgot to change his clothes when he came inside. His mom saw him and got mad. She told him to change his clothes right away. Timmy didn't understand why his mom was so upset.

Later that day, Timmy went to visit his friend Billy. Billy's mom said, "Wow, Timmy, your clothes are so dirty! Don't you feel ashamed?" Timmy didn't know what ashamed meant, but he could tell from Billy's mom's face that it wasn't good.

From that day on, Timmy made sure to change his clothes when he came inside. He didn't want anyone to think he was ashamed of being dirty. And he understood that it was important to listen to his mom.

Once upon a time, there was a girl. Her name was Sue. She had to take an exam. She felt really weird about it.

But Sue was smart and brave. She worked very hard to complete the exam. It took a long time, but she kept at it.

Finally, on the day of the exam, she was ready. She was very proud of what she had done.

The exam was very long and difficult. But Sue kept thinking of her goal and she used her big brain to answer every question.

When the exam was over, Sue felt really happy. She had done it! She had completed the exam.

Sue felt very proud of herself. Even though taking the exam was weird, she had done a great job!

Once upon a time there was an ugly market. It was a place where people went to buy and sell things. But the market wasn't nice. The stalls were old and boring with nothing exciting to see.

Then one day, something amazing happened. A kind lady came to the market and provided new things for people to buy and sell. She brought toys, games and sweets so it made the market much more fun. Everyone was very happy and enjoyed being at the market.

Now there are lots of different things to do and buy. Everyday more people come to the market as it has become so much nicer. The ugly market has changed and everyone loves visiting the market to have fun.

Once there was a little bear who was very cute. He wanted to try something new, so he decided to make a missile. He worked very hard and tried his best, but he was not sure if it was working. He kept trying and finally the missile flew off into the sky. He cheered with joy and ran around, excited to see how far his missile flew. The cute little bear was proud that he managed to make something fly and enjoy watching it soar. He hopped around with excitement for his achievement. The little bear wanted to keep trying and making new things. He was never afraid to try something new and kept on trying until he found success.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. He loved to play with his toys, especially his fire truck. It had a big red flame on the side that he thought was so cool.

One day, Timmy saw a toy at the store that he really wanted. It was a big, expensive robot that could walk and talk. But Timmy's mom said they couldn't afford it right now.

Timmy was sad, but he didn't give up hope. He decided to part with some of his old toys and sell them at a yard sale. He worked hard all week to get everything ready.

On the day of the yard sale, lots of people came to buy Timmy's toys. He sold almost everything, but there was one toy he just couldn't part with - his fire truck. As the day went on, Timmy started to feel better and better about his decision to sell his toys. And then, just as the yard sale was ending, a man came up to Timmy and offered him a lot of money for his fire truck. Timmy thought about it for a moment, but then he remembered how much he loved that toy. So he said no, and took his fire truck back inside. And he was happy, because he knew he had made the right choice.

Once upon a time there was a war in a distant land. It was fought using very intelligent machines. Every day, the machines would zoom around the battlefield. The machines were so intelligent that they could help the soldiers defeat their enemies.

One day, something strange happened. The machines suddenly stopped zooming. The soldiers were very confused and worried. They thought the war was over.

Then, one of the soldiers had an idea. He commanded his machine to zoom around the enemy's machines. His machine did as he said and the enemy's machines began to move again.

The soldiers cheered when they realized the war wasn't over and they continued to fight. They won the war because of their courageous and intelligent machines.

The machines flew back home after the war was over and everyone celebrated. The war was won because of their trusty machines that zoomed around the battlefield.

Sam screamed loudly as he raced around his house. He loved playing and was so excited to try his new game. His mom shouted from the kitchen, "Be careful, Sam!"

Sam darted out to the patio and found a big blue box with a funny number on it. He didn't know what it was but he could hear something inside. He quickly yelled, "What's in here!?"

He unlatched the lid of the blue box and spotted a yellow parakeet inside! The bird started to sing out loud and Sam yelled with joy. It was the perfect present for his birthday!

Sam held the little bird in his hands and ran inside to show his mom. He screamed, "Look what I found in the blue box!" His mom smiled as she hugged her son.

Once upon a time, in a big pond, there was a swan. The swan was very big and white. It liked to swim and play all day. One day, the swan saw something new near the pond. It was a small fire. The swan did not know what fire was, but it watched the fire from far away.

The next day, the swan saw a little boy playing near the fire. The boy was having fun, but the swan was worried. It remembered the fire from the day before. The swan thought the fire could burn the boy. So, the swan decided to watch the boy and the fire very closely.

As the boy played, he got closer and closer to the fire. The swan knew it was time to help. It swam very fast to the boy and used its big wings to push him away from the fire. The boy was safe, and he thanked the swan for saving him. From that day on, the swan and the boy became best friends, and they played together every day near the big pond.

One day, in the meadow, there was a zebra. She was very happy, eating her breakfast and running around in the sun. But suddenly, the zebra got a strange feeling. She started to sneeze! She sneezed and sneezed, until the unknown happened. It was raining! A rainbow appeared in the sky and everyone was so delighted. No one knew where the rain had come from, but the zebra started to laugh and said, "It must have been my sneezing that made it rain!" The other animals laughed, too. And they all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time there was a famous tower. It was big and tall and everyone wanted to see it. But nobody could enter the tower, and nobody knew what was inside.

One day, while everyone was walking around admiring the tower, a small bug appeared. The bug revealed a secret door right at the bottom of the tower. Everyone was amazed!

So the people all filed inside to see what was there. When they opened the door, they saw a huge surprise. Inside the tower were lots of delicious treats. Everyone was so happy. There were also lots of fun games and toys to play with.

The people had such a great time at the tower that it became even more famous. Everyone started talking about the amazing tower and all the fun things inside. They all spread the secret and the famous tower was loved by people for a long time.

Lila and Tom were playing in the park. They liked to pretend they were explorers. They saw a big hill with rocks and dirt. They wanted to climb it.

"Let's go to the top of the hill," Lila said. "Maybe we can see the whole park from there."

"OK, but be careful," Tom said. "The hill might be dangerous. It might be a volcano."

Lila and Tom started to climb the hill. They used their hands and feet to hold on to the rocks and dirt. They were almost at the top when they heard a loud noise.

"Boom!" The hill shook and smoke came out of it. Lila and Tom saw red and orange fire and lava. The hill was a volcano. It was glowing and spitting.

"Run!" Tom shouted. "The volcano is angry. It will hurt us."

Lila and Tom ran down the hill as fast as they could. They were scared and hot. They reached the bottom of the hill and ran to their mom and dad. They hugged them and told them what happened.

"Wow, you are very brave," Mom said. "But you should not play near volcanoes. They are very dangerous."

"We know, Mom," Lila said. "We are sorry. We will not do it again."

"Good," Dad said. "Now let's go home and have some ice cream. You need to cool down."

Lila and Tom smiled and nodded. They were happy to be safe and to have ice cream. They learned a lesson. They would not play near volcanoes anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy was very tired because he had been playing all day. He wanted to take a nap, but he had a secret. He had a toy that he loved very much, but it was broken.

Timmy didn't want anyone to know about his broken toy, so he hid it under his bed. But when his mom came in to check on him, she saw the broken toy and asked him about it. Timmy was scared to tell her the secret, but he knew he had to be honest.

He told his mom that he accidentally broke his toy and he was sorry. His mom hugged him and told him it was okay, and that they could try to fix it together. Timmy felt better knowing that he didn't have to keep the secret anymore and that his mom still loved him even though he broke his toy.

The sun was setting. Jack watched the last of its orange rays disappear over the horizon. He shivered as the wind blew around him, then made his way to his cozy little house. Inside, he found a warm blanket, some candy, and a cable. Jack smiled and grabbed the cable. He knew it would keep him busy.

Soon after, Jack was very sleepy. He curled up in his blanket and started to nod off. Suddenly, a wise old man appeared. He said to Jack, "You should not be playing with that cable. It is too dangerous!"

Jack ignored the man and continued to play. But before he knew it, he was fast asleep.

The next morning, the wise old man came back. He was upset to see the cable damaged. Jack was sad too. He realized that he should have listened to the wise man.

The wise old man warned Jack again before he left. "Don't forget-it is very important to always listen to people who are wise."

Sara and Ben were playing in the sandbox. They liked to imagine they were in a big desert, looking for an oasis. An oasis was a place with water and trees and animals, where they could rest and have fun.

"Look, I found an oasis!" Sara said, pointing to a pile of sand with some plastic flowers and a toy camel. "Let's go there and have a picnic!"

"OK, but hurry up!" Ben said, feeling impatient. He was thirsty and hungry, and he wanted to drink some juice and eat some crackers. He grabbed his backpack and followed Sara.

They reached the oasis and sat down on a towel. Sara opened her backpack and took out a bottle of juice and a box of crackers. She poured some juice into two cups and handed one to Ben.

"Here you go, Ben. Cheers!" Sara said, smiling.

"Cheers, Sara. Thank you!" Ben said, taking a sip of juice. He felt better and smiled too.

They ate some crackers and shared some stories. They pretended they saw lions and monkeys and birds in the oasis. They laughed and had fun.

They were happy they had each other and their imagination.

Once upon a time, there was a funny looking bird. He had long and flexible wings, and he loved to soar in the sky. One day, the bird was flying around when he noticed a hole in the ground. He thought it looked interesting, so he flew down to investigate it.

Once he got closer to the hole, the bird was shocked to find that an animal was stuck inside of it. The bird offered to help the animal out, but it was so scared that he just stayed still. The bird knew that he had to find a way to help the animal.

He flew up and looked down. He noticed that the hole was very deep and that it had curved walls. He thought for a moment, and then he had an idea. He flew down and started to peck at the walls of the hole, slowly shaping the edges until it was curved enough for the animal to turn around.

The bird was so proud of his work that he returned to the ground. The animal then saw the chance of escape and ran away from the hole. The bird was happy to have helped.

The moral of this story is that we should always be flexible when it comes to helping others. With a little thought and effort, we can often find a way to come to the aid of those in need.

Once upon a time there lived a boy. His name was Joe. He was three years old and he lived with his family in a small but cozy house.

One day something special happened. Joe's family got a puppy! They were very happy. Joe was so excited to have a new pet. They named the puppy Spot.

Spot had lots of fur that was so soft and cuddly. Joe would give Spot hugs every day. But at night, Spot had to stay downstairs in the kitchen.

Joe didn't like it. He didn't like staying away from Spot. But Dad said it was best for Spot - it was too cold for Spot to stay outside. So every night, Joe said goodbye to Spot and wished him a good night.

One night it was especially cold. Joe's family had to leave Spot outside. Joe was so sad and he cried. He thought it was too harsh to leave Spot outside in the cold.

But they had to do it. So Dad brought out some blankets and a basket to keep Spot warm. Spot stayed outside all night, but he was safe and warm.

When the morning came Spot was back inside the house with Joe. Joe was so happy to see Spot. From then on he would always make sure that Spot had a warm and cozy place to stay.

Once there was a little girl named Sarah. She hated new buildings because they were too big. Every day, Sarah would go to the park and see the new building that had just been built. It was so tall and big that it made her scared.

She asked her mom why the building was there, and her mom said, "It's a new apartment building for people to live in." Sarah still didn't like it. She thought it was too big and she was scared of it.

So every day, Sarah would go to the park and stand very far away from the building. She would look at it, but stay very far away. Whenever she saw the building, she remembered that she hated it.

Sarah's mom felt sad for her. She knew that the building wouldn't hurt her, she just wanted to help Sarah understand. So they went to the park together and Sarah's mom slowly talked her closer and closer to the building.

At first, Sarah was scared, but then she saw all the people in the building who were happy and living their lives. She finally understood that even though the building was big, it made people happy and it was a good thing. From then on, Sarah was happy to visit the building.

Sara loves to visit her grandma and grandpa on the farm. They have many animals that Sara likes to feed and pet. Sara's favorite animal is the horse, who is big and brown and has a long mane. Sara always asks her grandpa if she can ride the horse, but he says no, it is too dangerous.

One day, Sara sees her grandpa saddle the horse and get ready to ride. She runs to him and says, "Please, grandpa, can I ride with you? I will be very good and hold on tight. Please, please, please!" She insists and begs and does not listen to her grandpa.

Her grandpa sighs and says, "All right, Sara, but only for a little while. You have to wear this helmet and sit in front of me. And you have to do what I say, okay?" Sara nods and smiles and says, "Okay, grandpa, thank you, thank you, thank you!" She puts on the helmet and climbs on the horse with her grandpa's help.

They ride around the farm and Sara feels very happy. She sees the cows and the sheep and the chickens and the pigs. She waves to her grandma, who is working in the garden. She feels the wind in her hair and the horse's muscles under her. She thinks riding a horse is the best thing ever.

But riding a horse is also hard work. Sara soon gets tired and her legs and arms start to ache. She says to her grandpa, "Grandpa, can we go back now? I am sleepy and sore." Her grandpa says, "Sure, Sara, we can go back. You did very well, but riding a horse is hard, isn't it?" Sara says, "Yes, grandpa, it is hard, but it is also fun. Can we do it again tomorrow?" Her grandpa laughs and says, "Maybe, Sara, maybe. But now let's go and have some cookies and milk, okay?" Sara says, "Okay, grandpa, that sounds good." They go back to the barn and get off the horse. Sara hugs the horse and says, "Thank you, horse, for the ride. You are very nice." The horse neighs and nuzzles Sara. Sara and her grandpa walk to the house, holding hands. Sara feels very happy and loves her grandpa and the farm.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Joe. He was three years old and loved going to the beach. One day Joe asked his mom to take him to the beach. His mom agreed and they got in the car and drove off.

When they arrived at the beach, Joe ran out of the car and directed his attention to the sand. His mom smiled and began to explain what sand was. She said it was made from tiny pieces of rocks that were too small to pick up. She also said that the sand felt soft and nice between the toes. Joe thought that sounded fun and asked if he could try it.

His mom said yes and Joe started to play in the sand. He made sand castles, a tunnel, and lots of tiny mountains. He was so happy at the beach and didn't want to leave. Suddenly, his mom told him it was time to go home. Joe was disappointed but his mom promised him something sweet when they got home.

Joe jumped up and started to follow his mom to the car. When they got home, Joe's mom showed him some cupcakes. Joe's eyes lit up - they all looked so delicious. Joe's mom picked out the biggest cupcake and handed it to him. Joe thanked her, and the cupcake tasted sweet and even sweeter because his mom had promised it!

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lily. She was very excited for Christmas because it was only one day away.

Lily asked her mum, "What will Santa bring me?"

"That's a worry for another day, Lily!" said her mum.

Lily couldn't wait, so the next day she woke up very early. Suddenly, her room started to change. She now saw a Christmas tree with presents underneath it.

"Mummy! Mummy!" she yelled. "Santa came!"

Lily was so excited. She opened all her presents, finding lots of surprises inside.

When she was finished, Lily hugged her mum and said, "My Christmas is changed because of you."

Once upon a time, there was a green player who lived in a small house in the countryside. He was always looking for something new to do.

One day he was walking around the village when he saw a tall tree. It was so tall he couldn't even see the top!

He wanted to climb to the top and see what was there. He thought he could do it, but he was a bit worried. He went to ask his best friend for help.

He asked his friend if he wanted to come and help him. His friend looked around and then shrugged his shoulders and said, "Why not?

So together they began to climb the tall tree. It was harder than they thought and they had to take many breaks, but they eventually made it to the top. From there, they could see the whole village and the countryside beyond.

The green player was so happy. He had done something he didn't think he could do and he knew he could do even more in the future.

Anna and Ben were twins who loved Christmas. They wanted to get many presents from Santa Claus, who came at night and put them under the tree. But they also knew that Santa only gave presents to good children who behaved well.

One day, their mom told them that they had to help her clean the house, because it was very messy. Anna and Ben did not like to clean. They wanted to play with their toys and watch TV. They started to whine and complain.

"Mom, this is boring!" Anna said.

"Mom, this is hard!" Ben said.

"Anna and Ben, you have to help me," their mom said. "If you don't, Santa will not come to our house. He will see that you are naughty and skip our chimney. Do you want that?"

Anna and Ben looked at each other. They did not want Santa to skip their chimney. They wanted to get many presents. They decided to behave and help their mom.

"OK, mom, we will help you," Anna said.

"Sorry, mom, we will be good," Ben said.

Their mom smiled and hugged them. She gave them each a cloth and a spray bottle. They started to clean the windows, the tables, and the chairs. They worked hard and fast. They made the house very shiny and tidy.

When they finished, their mom praised them and gave them a cookie. She told them that they were very good children and that Santa would be very happy with them. She also told them that she had a surprise for them. She took them to the living room and showed them a big box wrapped in colorful paper.

"This is a special present for you, from me and dad," she said. "You can open it now, because you have been so helpful and kind. We are very proud of you."

Anna and Ben were very excited. They thanked their mom and dad and tore the paper. Inside the box, they found a big train set with many tracks, cars, and buildings. They loved it. They hugged their mom and dad again and started to play with their new toy.

They played until it was time for bed. Their mom and dad tucked them in and kissed them good night. They told them that they loved them and that Santa would come soon. They also told them that they had to sleep, because Santa only came when children were asleep.

Anna and Ben nodded and closed their eyes. They dreamed of Santa and his reindeer, flying in the sky and bringing presents to all the good children. They hoped that Santa would find their chimney and put many presents under their tree. They were very happy and grateful. They had behaved well and had a wonderful day.

Her work was difficult, and the girl's concentration was intense. She carefully drew lines, circles and shapes, and to make it even more special, she added some sparkles. Soon, enough her page was full of wonderful designs.

The girl was so proud of her work. Her mom was very curious about it and asked the girl, "What did you design?"

The little girl smiled and pointed out her drawings on the page. She said with a proud voice, "I designed a beautiful rainbow!"

From the top, she could lean over and look down. It made her feel dizzy. With a meow of pleasure, she hopped down the cliff and ran off to explore something else.

Once upon a time there was a pretty trumpet. Every day the trumpet would play a song to the birds in the sky. The birds were so happy when they heard the trumpet that they would sway and dance in the air.

One night, the trumpet was very tired and decided it needed to sleep. So it closed its eyes, took a deep breath, and went off to sleep.

In the morning, the trumpet woke up. It felt so refreshed! It played its song over and over and the birds sang along. It was the prettiest trumpet anyone had ever heard!

The trumpet kept playing its song every day and night until it was time for it to go to sleep again. And every time it did so, the birds would sing and dance. It was a great life for the trumpet, and it was very happy.

Tom and Lily were playing in the garden. They liked to smell the flowers and the herbs. Lily saw a big herb with purple leaves. She picked it and gave it to Tom.

"Here, smell this herb. It is nice," she said.

Tom smelled the herb. He liked it too. He said, "Thank you, Lily. This herb is nice. Can I pass it to Mom?"

Lily nodded. She said, "Yes, you can pass it to Mom. Maybe she can use it for cooking."

Tom and Lily ran to the kitchen. They saw Mom making soup. They said, "Mom, look what we found in the garden. It is a big herb with purple leaves. Can you use it for the soup?"

Mom smiled. She said, "Wow, what a big herb. It is called basil. It is very good for the soup. Thank you, Tom and Lily. You are very helpful."

She took the herb and chopped it. She added it to the soup. She said, "This soup will be very tasty. Do you want to try some?"

Tom and Lily nodded. They said, "Yes, please. We want to try some."

Mom gave them each a bowl of soup. They tasted it. They said, "Mmm, this soup is very tasty. We like the herb. It makes the soup better."

Mom hugged them. She said, "I'm glad you like the soup. And I'm glad you like the herb. You are very smart to find it in the garden. You are my big helpers."

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside with his friends. One day, Timmy's mom told him it was time for a haircut. Timmy didn't want a haircut because he liked his hair long. But his mom said it was important to look neat and clean.

Timmy went to the barber and he didn't like the way his hair looked after. He thought it didn't fit him anymore. But his mom told him that it was just a matter of getting used to the new look.

After a few days, Timmy realized that his new haircut made him look more grown up and neat. He felt proud of himself for trying something new. The moral of the story is that sometimes we have to try new things and give them a chance to see if they fit us.

One sunny day, a beautiful lady walked to the beach with a bag. Inside her bag, she had a long strip of ribbon. When she arrived, she found two small children playing in the sand.

The lady smiled and asked, "Would you like some ribbon?" The two children nodded excitedly. So, the lady opened her bag and supplied them with the ribbon.

The two children smiled with joy and began to wrap the beautiful strip around their waists. They laughed and played with it until the sun had gone down.

The lady was very happy to see the children enjoying the ribbon. She smiled to herself, and walked home.

Once upon a time there was a helpless truck. It was stuck at the bottom of a steep hill. The truck tried and tried to get back up the hill, but nothing it did made any difference.

Suddenly, the truck saw a big number leaning against the hill. It decided to lean on it too. Little by little, the truck was able to lean on the number and inch its way up the hill.

The truck was very relieved. Before long, it had made it to the top of the hill and drove away happily. From then on, the truck leaned on the number whenever it needed a helping hand.

One morning, Tim and his mom went to the park. They saw a big tree with a cat in it. The cat was very high up and needed help to get down. Tim wanted to help the cat, so he asked his mom if they could remove the cat from the tree and make it safe.

Tim's mom found a long stick. She used the stick to reach the cat in the tree. The cat was scared, but Tim's mom was very careful. She slowly removed the cat from the tree. The cat was now safe in her arms.

Tim and his mom took the cat home. They gave it some food and water. The cat was happy and safe with Tim and his mom. They played with the cat all day and had lots of fun. Tim was glad that they could help the cat and make it safe.

Tom is a lawyer. He works in a big building with many rooms. He helps people with problems. He likes his work, but he is very busy.

One day, he has a huge problem. He has to go to a place where people talk to a judge. The judge decides who is right and who is wrong. Tom has to talk to the judge and show him papers. He has to help his friend, who is in trouble.

Tom is worried. He does not want to make a mistake. He wants to help his friend. He calls his wife, Mary. He tells her about his problem.

"Mary, I have a huge problem. I have to go to the place where people talk to a judge. I have to help my friend, who is in trouble. I am scared. What if I say something wrong?"

Mary listens to Tom. She loves him. She wants to help him. She says, "Tom, you are a good lawyer. You work hard. You know what to do. You can help your friend. Do not be scared. I am proud of you. I will wait for you at home. I will make you a nice dinner. You can do it!"

Tom feels better. He thanks Mary. He says, "Mary, you are a good wife. You help me. You make me happy. I love you. I will see you soon. Goodbye!"

Tom hangs up the phone. He takes his papers. He goes to the place where people talk to a judge. He is ready to work. He is ready to help his friend. He is not scared anymore. He is brave. He is a good lawyer.

Tom and Lily like to play in the rain. They put on their boots and coats and run outside. They splash in the puddles and laugh. They see a big truck coming down the street. It is very slow and loud.

The truck has a hose on the back. It sprays water on the street. The water is dirty and smelly. Tom and Lily do not like it. They try to run away, but it is too late. The water hits them and they get very wet. They soak their boots and coats and hair.

Tom and Lily are not happy. They cry and shiver. They go back inside. Mom sees them and hugs them. She takes off their wet clothes and gives them dry ones. She makes them hot cocoa and cookies. She tells them a story. Tom and Lily feel better. They smile and thank Mom. They decide not to play in the rain anymore.

Once upon a time there was a sweet little girl called Lola. She was three years old and loved going on adventures with her brothers and sisters. One day her mommy said it was time for a surprise. They drove to the doctor's office and Lola was very excited.

When they arrived at the doctor's office, there were lots of interesting things to look at. Finally it was time for Lola's surprise - the doctor said she was going to have an operation. Lola didn't understand what an operation was, so she asked her mommy.

Mommy explained that it was nothing to worry about, and that it was just going to make her healthier and stronger. Lola was a bit scared but she trusted her mommy and nodded. The doctor was so sweet and kind that it made Lola less scared. Then she went to sleep for the operation.

When she woke up, Lola was surprised to hear that it was all over and that she was now healthy and strong. She was so happy she jumped out of bed, hugged her mommy and thanked the doctor.

Once there was a boy called Paul. Paul was three years old and had a very precious wallet. Every time Paul saw it he felt so grateful.

One day Paul decided to take his wallet to the store to buy an ice-cream. He was so excited but on arrival he realised he had left his wallet at home. He felt so disappointed and quickly realised he had to go home without his ice-cream.

When he arrived home he was very relieved to find his wallet on the kitchen table. He laughed and said out loud, "Phew, I was really missing my wallet!".

Paul was very happy and grateful to have his wallet back, and from then onwards he was very careful with it.

John was so excited and wanted to show his friends the frog. But to his surprise, the little creature hopped away the moment it saw him. He was so frustrated and could not understand why the frog was running away from him.

John started to follow the frog, but it was very fast and he was getting tired. Suddenly, he heard the frog talking to him. "Why are you following me? I thought you wanted to be my friend!"

John was surprised and surprised to hear the frog talking to him. He told the frog that he wanted to show his friends the frog and reveal its secret to them. The frog finally agreed, and they returned back to John's friends.

Once there was a little boy named Fred. He was only three years old and loved to explore. One day, he found a wood block in a field. It was a very special wood block that he wanted to solve.

He tried to twist and turn the block but he couldn't figure out how to do it. He asked his mom, "Mom, how do I solve the wood block?"

His mom said, "First, you need to look at it closely. Make sure you read each side carefully. Try to shift the block side to side until it clicks into place. That's how you solve the wood block."

Fred was very excited and tried again. He looked closely, then moved the little block side to side until it clicked.

Suddenly, the wood block was solved! Fred was so happy that he had solved the wood block that he screamed in delight.

From then on, Fred was determined to work on solving more and more little wood blocks.

Once upon a time, there was a big, slow turtle. The turtle liked to look at the sky at night. He loved to see the stars. One star was his best friend. The star would always shine bright for the turtle.

One day, the turtle found a box. The box had a lock on it. He wanted to open the box. He tried and tried, but he could not open the lock. The turtle was sad. He asked his star friend for help.

The star said, "I will help you, but you must promise to never lock the box again." The turtle promised. The star used its light to open the lock. Inside the box was a beautiful, shiny rock. The turtle was very happy.

But the turtle did not keep his promise. He locked the box again. The star was very sad. The star stopped shining for the turtle. The turtle was alone in the dark.

The moral of the story is: always keep your promises to your friends.

"Look, look!" Sam shouted, pointing at the sky. "Fireworks!"

Lily saw the bright colors and shapes exploding in the air. She clapped and cheered. She liked the fireworks. They were pretty and harmless. They did not hurt anyone or anything.

"Wow, wow!" Lily said, looking at Sam. "Do you like the fireworks?"

Sam nodded and smiled. He liked the fireworks too. They were loud and fun. They made him feel happy and brave.

"Boom, boom!" Sam said, making noises with his mouth. He pretended to be a firework too.

Lily laughed and joined him. She also made noises with her mouth. She pretended to be a firework too.

"Boom, boom!" Lily and Sam said, jumping and spinning. They were fireworks.

Mom and Dad watched them and smiled. They were proud of their children. They hugged them and kissed them.

"You are the best fireworks ever," Mom said.

"We love you," Dad said.

Lily and Sam hugged them back. They loved them too.

"Let's go, Lily," Tom said. "Maybe we can find something fun."

Lily nodded. She followed Tom to the fence. They saw a hole in the wire. They crawled through the hole and into the trash.

The trash smelled bad. It was full of old things that were gray and rot. Lily and Tom did not like it. They wanted to go back.

"Tom, this is yucky," Lily said. "Let's go home."

Tom agreed. He turned around and tried to find the hole in the wire. But he could not see it. The trash was too high and too dark.

"Where is the hole, Lily?" Tom asked. He was scared.

Lily looked around. She could not see the hole either. She started to cry.

"I don't know, Tom," Lily said. "We are lost."

They hugged each other and cried. They hoped someone would find them. But no one did. They stayed in the trash until they were gray and rot too.

Once upon a time, there was a brown bird who lived in a tree. One day, the bird heard a strange noise coming from far away. At first, the bird did not recognize the noise, but then it realized it was the sound of a telephone ringing!

The bird flew away to find the telephone. It flew over a lake, a flowery meadow and a dark forest before finally spotting the telephone in a nearby field. The bird landed and looked around, but couldn't find anyone around.

It tapped the telephone with its beak, wondering what to do next. Suddenly, the telephone stopped ringing and started to make different sounds. Puzzled, the bird stepped back and listened. After a few seconds, it recognized a voice speaking. The bird thought it was the most wonderful thing it had ever heard!

Anna and Ben were playing in the kitchen. They liked to pretend they were chefs and make yummy food. They had a big bowl of potatoes and some spoons and forks.

"Let's make potato soup!" Anna said. "We can mash the potatoes and add some water and salt."

Ben nodded. He liked potato soup. He picked up a potato and tried to mash it with his fork. But the potato was too hard and the fork was too fragile. The fork broke in half and Ben cried.

"Ow! Ow! Ow!" Ben said. "The fork hurt my hand! And it's broken!"

Anna ran to him and hugged him. She was sorry for Ben. She looked at the broken fork and the hard potato. She had an idea.

"I know!" Anna said. "Let's suggest to Mommy that we bake the potatoes instead. Baking will make them soft and yummy. And we can use a knife to cut them. A knife is not fragile like a fork."

Ben stopped crying. He liked Anna's idea. He smiled and nodded.

"Okay!" Ben said. "Let's suggest to Mommy that we bake the potatoes. And we can add some cheese and butter too. That will make them even yummier!"

Anna and Ben took the bowl of potatoes and the knife and went to find Mommy. They hoped Mommy would like their suggestion and help them bake the potatoes. They were hungry and excited. They loved playing in the kitchen.

The line kept growing and growing. It was incredible! Everyone paused to take a closer look.

Suddenly the line started to move and changed shape. It started to look like a big butterfly flying in the sky. Everyone was so excited.

The big butterfly flew up high and started to dance in the sky. It was so beautiful. Everyone was in awe and clapped and cheered.

The big butterfly flew away and the line disappeared. Everyone was sad, but also happy because they had seen something so incredible. They all said goodbye and went home.

Once there was a bear who wanted to celebrate. He was ready to do something special. He saw a cave nearby, so he decided to go inside.

He was excited that he found a cave. He went inside and found lots of happy animals celebrating. All the animals were laughing, dancing and singing.

The bear was happy to join the animals in their celebration. He smiled and sang with them. It was so much fun!

When the animals were done celebrating, the bear thanked them for a wonderful time. Then he went on his way, ready for the next adventure.

Jimmy was a small boy, who loved to whistle. Whenever he felt happy, a little whistle would come out of his mouth. His parents were so proud of him, they knew he was special. Every year Jimmy and his parents would go on a long, incredible journey.

One year, they decided to go to the beach. When they got there, Jimmy couldn't wait to get out of the car. He tossed off his sandals and began to whistle as he ran down to the beach. Suddenly, a gust of wind whipped past him and his whistle flew away! He was so upset.

Jimmy began to cry, but his parents were there to help. Together, the three of them searched for the lost whistle. After a while, Jimmy spotted it lying in the sand, and scooped it up with a smile.

The moral of the story is to never give up when something you love is lost. If Jimmy had stopped trying, he wouldn't have found his whistle.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play at the gym. One day, he went to the gym with his mom. Tim was very happy.

At the gym, he saw a naughty cat. The cat jumped and ran all around. Tim laughed and said, "Look, Mom! The cat is so funny!" The cat jumped on a ball and started to roll.

Suddenly, the naughty cat began to fall. Tim was scared for the cat. He ran to catch it. The cat landed in Tim's arms. Tim's mom smiled and said, "Good job, Tim! You saved the cat!"

From that day on, Tim and the naughty cat became best friends. They played together at the gym every day. And they all lived happily ever after.

One day there was a very adventurous little bear named Dave. Dave loved exploring the world around him. He was in search of a special treasure.

So Dave set out into the forest. He wanted to find some unique and beautiful things. As he was walking, it started to rain! Dave was not afraid though. He ran faster to find shelter.

He came across an old tree, it had a big cut in it. He wondered what he could do. He saw a hole in the cut, it was the perfect size for Dave to hide in. He crawled in, and the rain stopped.

Dave was excited, he had found a great hideout. But then he heard some voices. It was two other adventurous bears. They were looking for a treasure too!

''Do you want to join us on our adventure?'' one bear asked.

Dave smiled, ''Yes! I would love to join you!'' And so the three of them went on a wonderful treasure hunt.

The End

Once upon a time, there was an intelligent penguin named Pip. Pip lived in a cold place with lots of ice and snow. He had many friends who liked to play with him.

One day, Pip and his friends were playing a game of hide and seek. Pip was very good at finding places to hide. His friend, Sam the seal, said, "Pip, you are so smart! I can never find you!" Pip smiled and said, "Thank you, Sam! I like to find new places to hide."

As the day went on, the sun began to set, and it was time for the game to end. Pip and his friends were tired but happy. They all went home to their families, excited to play again the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a wealthy family of four. They had two children, a boy and a girl. One day, the boy and girl were playing in the garden and they stumbled upon a strange box. They wondered what was inside and so the boy reached out to take it.

Suddenly, his hand was squeezed tightly. A voice asked the boy to open the box. His sister was scared so she ran away. The boy opened the box, and inside he found a monster. It was a giant angry beast with sharp teeth.

The beast began to squeeze the boy, who started to cry. The family heard the cries and tried to help, but it was too late. The monster had squeezed the boy to death. The family was filled with sorrow.

They all realized how dangerous the box was and how lucky they were before it was opened. Unfortunately, it was too late to save the boy, and the family was heartbroken.

On Tuesday, Sam and Mia went to the park with their mom. They liked to play on the swings, the slide and the sandbox. They also liked to share their toys and snacks with each other.

But today, something weird happened. A big boy came to the park and took Sam's truck. He said it was his and he would not give it back. Sam was sad and angry. He tried to get his truck back, but the big boy pushed him away.

"Hey, that's not nice!" Mia said. She ran to help her brother. She grabbed the truck from the big boy and gave it to Sam. "This is Sam's truck. You can't take it. You have to share."

The big boy was mad. He yelled at Mia and tried to take the truck again. But Mia was brave and strong. She held on to the truck and said, "No, you can't have it. Go away!"

The big boy saw that Mia was not afraid of him. He decided to leave them alone. He ran to the other side of the park and played with his own toys. He did not bother Sam and Mia anymore.

Sam and Mia hugged each other. They were happy to have their truck back. They thanked Mia for being a good sister. They said they would always stick together and not let anyone separate them. They went back to playing and having fun. Their mom smiled and watched them. She was proud of them. She thought they were the best kids in the world.

Lily liked to write in her diary every day. She had a pink diary with a lock and a key. She wrote about her friends, her toys, her dreams and her feelings. She did not let anyone read her diary. It was her secret.

One day, her brother Max saw her diary on her bed. He was curious. He wanted to know what Lily wrote. He took the diary and tried to open it. But it was locked. He looked for the key. He found it under Lily's pillow. He smiled and opened the diary.

He started to read the diary out loud. He read about Lily's crush on Ben, her fear of spiders, her wish to have a pony and her anger at Max. He laughed and made fun of Lily. He said, "Lily likes Ben, Lily likes Ben, Lily is scared of spiders, Lily wants a pony, Lily is mad at me!"

Lily heard Max's loud voice from the living room. She ran to her bedroom and saw Max with her diary. She was very upset. She shouted, "Max, give me back my diary! That is not nice! That is not fair! You are not allowed to read my diary!"

Max saw Lily and tried to hide the diary behind his back. He said, "Sorry, Lily, I was just joking. I did not mean to hurt you. Please don't tell mom and dad."

Lily snatched the diary from Max's hand. She locked it and put it in her drawer. She said, "Max, you are very mean. You broke my trust. You should not read other people's diaries. They are private. How would you feel if I read your diary?"

Max looked down and felt sorry. He said, "I don't have a diary. But I understand. I would feel bad if you read my diary. I apologize, Lily. I won't do it again. Can you forgive me?"

Lily looked at Max and saw that he was sincere. She said, "OK, Max, I forgive you. But you have to promise me that you will never touch my diary again. And you have to be nice to me and Ben."

Max nodded and said, "I promise, Lily. I will never touch your diary again. And I will be nice to you and Ben. Can we hug and make up?"

Lily smiled and said, "Yes, Max, we can hug and make up. You are still my brother and I love you."

They hugged and said sorry to each other. They decided to play together and share their toys. They were happy and forgot about the diary.

Once upon a time there was a little boy who had a big seat. It was thick and made of wood. The little boy was happy to have this seat and he always took it everywhere he went.

One day, while he was sitting on the seat, something happened that made him scared. A loud noise came out of nowhere and the little boy screamed.

He was so scared that he ran and hid behind the seat. He knew he was safe, because the seat was thick. He stayed there until he stopped feeling afraid.

After a while, the little boy came out from behind the seat. He was happy because he knew he was safe. He hugged the seat, thanked it for keeping him safe, and jumped off it, ready to enjoy his day again.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful dog named Spot. Spot had a toy gun that he loved to play with. He would run around the yard and pretend to be a hero. Spot was very happy.

One day, Spot met a new friend named Fuzzy. Fuzzy asked, "Can I play with your gun?" Spot thought about it and decided to trust Fuzzy. They played with the gun together and had lots of fun.

Later, Spot and Fuzzy saw a big ball stuck in a tree. Spot had an idea. He used his toy gun to knock the ball down. Both friends were so happy. They knew that trusting each other was good, and they played together every day after that.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Livy. She was three years old, and she was always full of fun and adventure. One day, Livy wanted to go exploring. She went outside and she saw a harmless alarm beside a tree. She thought it was a great thing to bring home.

Livy went back inside and showed her parent the alarm. Her mom asked her, "Livy, where did you get that?" Livy smiled and said, "I found it outside, can I keep it?" Her mom looked at the alarm and said, "No, it's not safe. We'd better take it back outside."

Livy didn't want to, so she decided to argue. She said, "But it's harmless!" Her mom said, "I know, but it's not for us to keep. Let's put it back where it belongs."

Livy reluctantly agreed and together, her and her mom took the alarm back outside. As they walked, Livy said, "Mommy, can I bring something else home?" Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, anything that is safe and harmless." Livy was happy and with a big smile on her face, she led the way home.

Once upon a time there lived a brave little girl called Mark. She loved to explore, and every day she would adventure around looking for new and exciting things.

Today she seemed to have stumbled upon something strange. As she looked around she could see an unknown ghost hovering in the air. She was a bit scared, but she decided to be brave and walked closer to take a better look.

"What an odd looking ghost" she thought to herself as she cautiously crept up closer to get a better look.

Suddenly the ghost spoke to her. "Mark go home!", it said in a creepy voice. Startled, she quickly scrambled to her feet and ran away as fast as she could.

Although scared, that night she still remembered the strange ghost and it stayed with her in her thoughts. She returned to the same spot the next day, but this time she brought a few brave friends.

When they saw the ghost, they were scared but too curious to leave. With a bit of encouragement, Mark stepped forward and timidly asked who the ghost was.

The ghost replied "I am an unknown spirit who is here to warn you about a great danger that is coming your way. Make sure you listen closely and heed my warning."

With that, the ghost disappeared, leaving a scared but thankful Mark and her friends behind. Thinking of the ghost's warning, they were more prepared to face the danger that was yet

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Joe. He was a very stubborn boy. Joe wanted to go and play by the big pine tree near his house.

So, he asked his mother if he could go. But his mom said no because it was getting dark outside. Joe was very upset and he asked her why.

Then his mom said, "Because it's not safe. It's too dark." Joe didn't want to listen, so he went outside anyway.

When Joe got to the pine tree, he saw something funny and started to laugh. It was a little squirrel with a green and yellow hat. The squirrel started to laugh too. Joe laughed so hard that he could hardly stop.

The little squirrel was just as stubborn as Joe. They laughed and chased each other for a long time until it was dark. Then Joe said goodbye to the squirrel and went back home. As Joe walked home, he couldn't stop smiling. He had a great time playing with the squirrel and his new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside in the hot sun. She ran and ran until she was very thirsty. She went to her mom and said, "Mommy, please can I have some water?" Her mommy gave her a big glass of water and Lily drank it all up.

But then, Lily wanted to play some more. She didn't want to go inside to use the bathroom. So, she went behind a tree and peed on the ground. Suddenly, a loud noise scared her. It was a bee! The bee stung her on her bottom and it hurt very much.

Lily learned a lesson that day. She should always use the bathroom and not pee outside. She also learned that bees don't like when you pee on their flowers. So, she said sorry to the bee and went inside to put some ice on her bottom.

Lily and Max were playing in the sandbox. They both wanted the same toy truck. It was red and shiny and had big wheels. They both reached for it at the same time.

"Mine!" Lily shouted.

"No, mine!" Max yelled.

They pulled and tugged on the truck. They did not want to share. They started to fight. They pushed and kicked and scratched each other. They made a lot of noise and got filthy with sand and dirt.

"Stop it, stop it!" their mom said. She came over and took the truck away. She was angry and sad. She looked at them with a frown.

"Look at you two. You are filthy and mean. Fighting is not nice. Fighting is not fun. You have to learn to play together and share. The truck is not a prize. It is a toy. Toys are for everyone. Now you have to say sorry and hug each other."

Lily and Max felt bad. They did not like to fight. They did not like to make their mom unhappy. They looked at each other and said sorry. They hugged each other and smiled.

"Can we have the truck back, please?" they asked.

"Only if you promise to take turns and be kind," their mom said.

They nodded and said yes. Their mom gave them the truck back. They wiped off the sand and dirt. They played with the truck together. They had fun. They were happy.

One day, two kids wanted to go on an adventure. So, they found an ostrich and hopped on its back. The ostrich stood up and started to run. The kids clung on tight, screaming with joy.

Soon, they arrived at the beach. The kids were amazed. There was so much to explore! The kids rushed to the edge of the shore, wading into the gushing water.

"Let's dive! shouted one of the kids.

But the other kid was hesitant. He was scared of the deep, dark water.

"It's ok. It will be fun, said the other kid.

So, the scared kid closed his eyes and dove in. The water was cool and refreshing. He stayed underwater for a few seconds before coming back up.

He smiled and exclaimed, "That was so fun!

The kids spent the rest of the day exploring the beach. The kids collected shells and built sandcastles. Even the ostrich had fun, waddling around in the sand.

Eventually, it was time to go home. The kids waved goodbye to the beach and went back to the ostrich. The ride back was boring, so the kids sang songs to pass the time. When they reached home, they were happy. They had had a wonderful day.

Once upon a time, there were two friends - a cat and a mouse. They both loved to eat pasta. Every day, the mouse would fill his bowl with lots of deep, yummy pasta. But he was too small to reach it.

The cat saw the mouse's problem and had an idea. He grabbed the bowl and stepped into a deep bowl of water. He placed the mouse's bowl on top of the water, so that the mouse could eat the pasta.

The mouse thanked the cat and started to eat the yummy pasta. The cat watched as the mouse enjoyed his meal. Later, they both laughed and played together. They were very happy!

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lucy. She was three years old, and she loved going to the river. Every day she would put on her red dress and skip along to find her friends.

When she got to the river there was a beautiful big tree with brilliant leaves. She loved to sit beneath it and watch the river flow. Every day she would explore the riverbank, looking for treasures and picking flowers.

One day she found a brilliant purple flower and she decided to pick it. She wanted to bring it back and show her friends, but then she heard someone calling her name. It was her friends, telling her they had something special to show her!

She ran over to them and they gave Lucy a present. It was a lovely bracelet that belonged to them all. It was a special symbol of friendship, and they all decided to wear it together.

Now every day they go to the river and they wear their friendship bracelet. They have lots of fun exploring and picking flowers, and they belong to each other like the river belongs to the tree.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a special style. She liked to wear big, colorful bows in her hair. One day, she went to the park to play with her friends.

At the park, there was a boy named Tom. Tom saw Lily's big bow and started to tease her. He said, "Your bow is too big! It looks funny!" Lily felt nervous and sad because of Tom's words.

Lily's friends saw her looking sad and came to help. They told her, "Don't worry, Lily. We like your style. Your bow is pretty!" Lily smiled and felt happy again. Together, they played and had fun at the park.

One day, a girl named Lucy found a big clock in her house. The clock was in a distant room that she never went to before. The clock was very old and did not work. Lucy wanted to fix the clock, so she tried to open it.

Lucy's friend, Tom, came over to play. He saw Lucy with the clock and said, "Don't break the clock, Lucy! It looks very old." But Lucy did not listen. She tried to open the clock and it broke into pieces. Lucy was sad and Tom was upset.

Lucy and Tom decided to tell Lucy's mom about the broken clock. Her mom said, "It's okay. The clock was old and we can fix it together." They all worked together to fix the clock. In the end, Lucy and Tom learned to be careful with old things and to listen to their friends.

Anna and Ben were playing in the park. They saw a big dog with a long tail and a red collar. The dog looked friendly and harmless. Anna wanted to pet the dog, but Ben was afraid.

"Can I touch the dog?" Anna asked Ben.

"No, the dog might bite you!" Ben said.

"But the dog is not angry. He is wagging his tail. He likes us," Anna said.

Anna walked slowly to the dog. The dog sniffed her hand and licked it. Anna smiled and stroked the dog's fur. The dog barked happily.

"See, the dog is nice. He is not scary," Anna said.

Ben watched from a distance. He felt cold and lonely. He wished he was brave like Anna. He wanted to be friends with the dog too.

The dog noticed Ben and ran to him. He offered him his paw and tilted his head. He wanted to play with Ben.

Ben was nervous. He did not know what to do. He looked at Anna. Anna nodded and smiled.

"Go on, Ben. The dog is harmless. He just wants to have fun," Anna said.

Ben took a deep breath and reached out his hand. He touched the dog's paw and felt its warmth. The dog wagged his tail and licked Ben's face. Ben laughed and hugged the dog.

"Wow, the dog is so soft and kind. I like him," Ben said.

Anna and Ben played with the dog for a long time. They threw a ball and ran with him. They had a lot of fun.

The dog's owner came and thanked Anna and Ben for being nice to his dog. He said his dog's name was Max and he was very friendly. He invited them to visit Max again.

Anna and Ben said goodbye to Max and his owner. They walked back to their parents, who were waiting for them.

"Did you have fun?" their parents asked.

"Yes, we did. We made a new friend. His name is Max and he is a dog," Anna and Ben said.

They told their parents about their adventure. Their parents were proud of them.

They learned that sometimes things that look scary are not. They learned that being brave and kind can lead to new friendships. They learned that dogs are not cold, but warm and harmless.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a kind girl named Amy. She had a big heart and was very compassionate. One day, she found a little lost bird in her yard. The bird had a hurt wing and could not fly.

Amy said, "Oh no! Little bird, I will help you!" She took the bird home and put a pin on its wing to make it better. Every day, she fed the bird and took care of it. The bird started to feel better and better.

One sunny day, the bird said, "Thank you, Amy, for helping me. Now, I can fly again!" The bird was so happy that it could fly. It was time for the bird to go back to its family. Amy and the bird said goodbye, and the bird flew away. In the end, Amy felt happy that she could help her new friend.

The sun shone down from above, glinting off the river below. A small duck was swimming and splashing in the water. Suddenly, the duck saw something else - a little fish swimming along in a zigzag motion. It looked so happy, the duck couldn't help but think it was fun.

The duck wanted to join in so it started to swim with the fish, zigzagging this way and that. It was the best game ever! Suddenly, the fish ducked down into a deep part of the river, and the duck followed it.

Together they plunged down into the river and went for a bath. The duck and fish swam in circles, playing and splashing around, making the water go sparkly. The water felt cool and soft on the duck's feathers, so it stuck around and kept swimming until it was too tired to keep going.

Finally, they finished and swam to the shallows. The duck was happy to be out of the water, as it had been a bit boring at times. But the duck was also sad to have said goodbye to its new friend. Still, the duck felt refreshed and happy and it was ready for its next adventure!

Lily and Max were playing in the garden. They liked to act like animals and make noises. Lily was a cat and Max was a dog. They chased each other around the sunflowers. The sunflowers were big and yellow and smiled at them.

"Meow, meow, I am a cat. I can climb the sunflowers," Lily said. She jumped on a sunflower and tried to reach the top.

"Woof, woof, I am a dog. I can dig under the sunflowers," Max said. He scratched the dirt and made a hole.

But the sunflowers did not like to be climbed or dug. They shook their heads and dropped their seeds. The seeds fell on Lily and Max and made them messy. They had seeds in their hair, on their clothes and on their faces.

"Hey, stop that! You are hurting the sunflowers," a voice said. It was Mom. She came out of the house and saw the mess.

"Lily, Max, what are you doing? You are making a mess of the garden and yourselves. You need to be gentle with the sunflowers. They are living things, not toys," Mom said.

Lily and Max felt sorry. They did not mean to hurt the sunflowers. They got down from the sunflower and came out of the hole. They said sorry to the sunflowers and to Mom.

"We are sorry, Mom. We were just acting like animals. We did not know the sunflowers would drop their seeds," Lily said.

"We are sorry, sunflowers. We did not mean to make you sad. You are very pretty and nice," Max said.

Mom smiled and hugged them. She said it was okay, but they had to be careful next time. She took them inside and helped them clean up. She gave them some cookies and milk and read them a story. Lily and Max learned to respect the sunflowers and to have fun without making a mess.

So Maria and her mommy took out a special device and started to record. They captured every single thing that was special and rare.

Little One was very curious about the lesson. He asked Mommy, "What is obey?

Mommy replied, "It means to listen and follow Mommy and Daddy's rules.

Little One asked, "What is believe?

Mommy looked into his eyes and said, "It means to know something deep down in your heart is true, like trusting that your thumb will always stay in the same place when you look at it.

Little One looked at his thumb. He kept it up and looked at it. His little thumb was still there! Little One smiled and said, "I understand. I can always believe my thumb will stay the same.

Mommy was so proud of Little One. He had learned a very important lesson that he would remember always. He was a very obedient child.

Once there was a boy and his pillow. The boy was very rude and he did not allow his pillow to do anything. The pillow was very unhappy because it could not play or speak.

One day the pillow had had enough of being treated badly and it decided to run away. It hopped off the bed and scurried away, leaving the boy behind.

The boy felt sad and realised he had treated the pillow badly. He called out for the pillow and begged it to come back.

The pillow thought about it for a moment and decided to give the boy another chance. It hopped back onto the bed and allowed the boy to give it a big hug.

From then on the boy was always kind to his pillow and never disallowed it from doing anything. He was very sorry for how he had acted before and was very thankful to have his best friend back.

Daddy smiled. "Of course!"

He watched as Daddy showed him how to make different letters. After a few minutes of teaching, Sam put the pen to the paper and wrote the letter M. Daddy said, "You did it! Now you know how to write. Let's do some more!"

Daddy and Sam kept practicing writing for half an hour. Then Daddy said to Sam, "Let's take a break." But, before they stopped, Sam noticed something disgusting in his glass of juice.

He pointed and asked, "Daddy what is this?"

Daddy said, "It looks like a fly. That's disgusting." He quickly poured the juice out and put a new glass of juice in front of Sam.

Sam took a sip. "Yummy! Daddy, you are the best!"

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jean. She was three years old and loved to explore the world around her. One day, while playing in the backyard, she stumbled upon a pile of ash. She was curious and decided to take a closer look.

She bent down and touched the ash. It was gritty and slightly cold. Suddenly, a big gust of wind blew, making some of the ash swirl into the air. Jean was surprised but not scared. She nodded and watched as the ash cloud slowly floated away.

Jean thought about what she had just seen. She didn't understand why the ash had flown away like that. She knew it was safe, but she wanted to know more.

So, Jean ran inside and told her mom all about it. After hearing Jean's story, her mom smiled and said, "I know what happened. The wind was blowing the ash away so that it wouldn't make a mess." Jean nodded and smiled. Now she knew why the ash had flown away and she felt safe.

Once upon a time there was a special place where everyone went to rest. Everyone loved it there because it was a very inviting place.

They would lay down in the soft grass and eat delicious fruits and vegetables. The sunshine beamed down on them which felt so nice and warm.

Then, it was time for everyone to gather around for storytime. All the children would snuggle close together as the grown-ups read them stories.

After listening to a few stories, it was time for a snack. The grown-ups made yummy food like cookies and cakes. Everyone feasted on the delicious snacks and then it was time to take a nap.

Everyone rested in the cozy grass beds until the sun went down. Then, they sang songs and played games until it was time to go home.

The special place was a wonderful place to go to rest. Everyone felt happy and relaxed after spending the day there. It was a delicious and delightful place.

Tom and Lily were playing with their toys in the living room. They had a lot of fun making noises and pretending to be animals. Tom was a lion and Lily was a zebra. They chased each other around the couch and the table.

But then they saw a big plate of cookies on the kitchen counter. They both wanted a cookie very much. They ran to the kitchen and tried to reach the plate. But it was too high for them. They jumped and stretched, but they could not get a cookie.

They looked at each other and had an idea. They decided to work together. Tom climbed on a chair and Lily climbed on his back. They were very careful not to fall. Tom reached the plate and grabbed a cookie. He gave it to Lily and she smiled. Then he took another cookie for himself.

They climbed down and ran back to the living room. They sat on the floor and ate their cookies. They were very happy and proud. They shared their cookies with their toys and pretended they were having a party.

But then they heard their mom coming. She saw the plate on the counter and the crumbs on the floor. She knew what they had done. She was not happy. She asked them who took the cookies.

Tom and Lily looked at each other and felt scared. They did not want to get in trouble. They did not know what to say. They thought mom would be angry and take away their toys.

But then they had another idea. They decided to be honest. They said they were sorry and they took the cookies. They said they were hungry and they worked together. They said they would not do it again.

Mom looked at them and smiled. She was not angry. She was glad they told the truth. She said they were good kids and she loved them. She said they could have one more cookie each, but only after dinner. She said they had to clean up the mess and wash their hands.

Tom and Lily said thank you and hugged mom. They were happy and relieved. They cleaned up the mess and washed their hands. They went back to the living room and played with their toys. They said they would not take cookies without asking again. They said they would always tell the truth. They said they were the best team ever. They said they had a funny day.

One day, a hungry bear went searching for food. He looked high and low, but found nothing. Eventually he came across a mineral. Excited, he quickly bit into it expecting to taste something tasty. But it was too hard to chew! The bear roared in frustration and kept searching. He soon stumbled across a bee rolling in its own honey. His tummy began to grumble and without thinking, he quickly bit the bee. Delicious honey spread out all around, and the bear licked it up greedily. Finally he was no longer hungry! And he never wanted to forget this delicious moment.

Tom and his Mom went on an adventure. They went to a museum. Tom had never been to one before.

When they arrived, he saw so many interesting things. Tom was amazed. He saw a big dinosaur and a big ship. It was so fun.

Tom and his Mom looked carefully around the museum. His Mom was very reliable and they explored everything carefully. Tom was so happy.

When they were done, they knew they had a great day. He was so excited to come back!

Mum was cleaning the house when she noticed a red ashtray on the kitchen table. She walked over to the table and asked her three-year-old daughter, "What's this?"

The little girl went up to the ashtray, picked it up and said, "It's to raise ashes."

Mum was amused and said, "But why is it red?"

The little girl gave it a big smile and said, "It's my red ashtray. I like it! It makes me happy."

Mum couldn't help but laugh. She said, "Well, if it makes you happy, it's okay to have it here."

The little girl kept playing with the red ashtray and smiled. Mum wiped the table, put away the cleaning tools and they both went out to the playground.

Once upon a time there was a boy called Bob. Bob was three years old and liked to explore. One day, Bob went into his mum's bedroom and saw a needle. He was a bit fearful and said to himself, "I need that needle". So, he picked it up and put it in his pocket.

After a while, Bob got so excited about the needle that he forgot to be cautious. Suddenly, he tripped and the needle dropped. It landed on the carpet and made a tiny hole. Bob started to cry and said, "I will get in trouble".

Bob's mum came in and said, "What happened here?". Bob was very scared and said, "I just wanted the needle". His mum saw that he was fearful and said, "It's OK, Bob. I can fix this. You don't need the needle".

Bob's mum fixed the tiny hole with her special needle and some thread. She said, "It's all better now. You don't have to be scared". Bob smiled and thanked his mum for fixing the hole.

The moral of the story is that it is important to always be careful when exploring and new things.

Once upon a time, a family went searching in the deep forest. The trees were tall and the sky was blue. Suddenly, they found a delicate sheet lying on the ground. The sheet was made from a very soft material.

The family did not know what to do with the sheet. So, they decided to take it with them and search for something else. They searched for some time but did not find anything else. Finally, they took the sheet home with them.

When they got home, the family laid out the sheet in the middle of the room. All of a sudden, the sheet started to move. Something was inside it! Slowly, the sheet opened and out came a tiny dragon.

The family was shocked! It seemed like the delicate sheet was the perfect hiding place for the dragon. It had been sleeping inside the sheet warm and safe. The family was so happy to give the dragon a home.

They all lived happily ever after!

Once upon a time, there was a green frog who loved waffles. Every day, he hopped around looking for new waffles to eat. One day, he saw the most delicious looking waffle he had ever seen. But it was too far away for him to get to. So the frog said, "What can I do now?".

Just then, he noticed a tiny puddle of water nearby. He hopped into the water, and he started to soak up the water with his big, green feet. Slowly, the puddle started to get bigger and bigger, until it was big enough for the frog to reach the waffle.

The frog was so happy when he was able to finally get the waffle. Then he hopped away and ate the delicious waffle. From then on, he made sure to always look for puddles to help him reach any waffles that he might find.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys and explore outside. One day, Timmy went for a walk and found an unusual rock. It was very pretty and shiny, so Timmy decided to keep it.

As Timmy was walking home, he saw a little bird that had fallen out of its nest. Timmy wanted to help the bird, so he picked it up and brought it home with him. Timmy's mom told him that they needed to let the bird stay in a box with some food and water until it was strong enough to fly again.

Timmy went to bed that night, feeling happy that he had helped the little bird. But the next morning, when Timmy woke up, he found that the bird had passed away. Timmy was very sad and cried because he wanted the bird to stay with him forever. Timmy's mom hugged him and told him that sometimes things don't go the way we want them to, but we can still be proud of ourselves for trying to help. Timmy kept the unusual rock as a reminder of the little bird he tried to save.

Once upon a time there was an old fox who had a fat tail. The fox was very proud of her tail and every day she would swish it from side to side. She wanted everyone to be able to see it and to appreciate its beauty.

One day, the fox came across a match and she thought it looked like the tip of her tail. She approached the match and tried to match her tail to the match. But no matter how hard she tried, her tail was not the same size as the match.

The fox got very frustrated and yelled, "Why can't my tail be like the match?"

An old rabbit hopped by and said, "It's ok to be different. Everyone is unique and that's what makes us special."

The fox thought about it and soon realized that the rabbit was right. She smiled and went on her way, happy with her fat tail.

The moral of the story is that it's OK to be different. We all have our own strengths and weaknesses, and that's what makes us special.

Lily and Ben love to play pretend. They have a big box full of costumes and props. They like to make up stories and act them out on their stage. Their stage is a rug in the living room with a curtain made of a sheet.

One day, they decide to play a story about a princess and a dragon. Lily puts on a pink dress and a crown. Ben puts on a green shirt and a mask with horns and teeth. He also finds a toy sword and a shield.

They go to their stage and start their story. Lily says, "I am the princess of this castle. I am very kind and brave. But there is a dragon who wants to take my castle and my treasure. He is very mean and scary."

Ben says, "I am the dragon. I am very strong and hungry. I want to take your castle and your treasure. And I want to eat you. Roar!"

He runs to the stage and pretends to breathe fire. Lily pretends to be scared and runs away. Ben chases her around the stage. He tries to catch her with his claws and teeth.

Lily says, "Help! Help! Is there anyone who can save me from the dragon?"

Ben says, "No one can save you. You are mine. Roar!"

He grabs her by the arm and pulls her to the stage. He shakes her gently and says, "Now I will eat you. Yum!"

Lily says, "No, please, don't eat me. You don't have to be mean and scary. You can be nice and friendly. You can share your castle and your treasure with me. And we can be friends."

Ben says, "Really? You want to be friends with me? But I am a dragon. And you are a princess."

Lily says, "Yes, I want to be friends with you. You are a dragon. And I am a princess. But we are also Lily and Ben. And we like to play pretend. And we are very creative."

Ben says, "You are right. We are Lily and Ben. And we like to play pretend. And we are very creative. And we can be friends. I'm sorry I tried to eat you. Can you forgive me?"

Lily says, "Of course I can forgive you. You are my friend. And friends forgive each other. Can you hug me?"

Ben says, "Yes, I can hug you. You are my friend. And friends hug each other."

They hug each other and smile. They end their story and bow to their audience. Their audience is their mom and dad, who are watching from the couch. They clap and cheer for Lily and Ben. They say, "That was a wonderful story. You are very creative and talented. We are very proud of you."

Lily and Ben say, "Thank you. We love you."

They run to their mom and dad and hug them. They are very happy. They love to play pretend. They love to be creative. And they love each other. The end.

The sun was shining through the window and Susie lay in her bed. She was excited to go to the subway with her mom and dad. Suddenly, she heard her mom say, "Susie, it's time to get up!"

Susie quickly washed up and got dressed. She skipped to the front door. Her dad smiled as he put on his coat, "Let's go!"

The family walked to the subway station. Susie looked around, it was different than she had imagined. She looked at her mom and dad, their faces had become sad. Susie saw a small tear drop from her mom's eye and wondered why she was so sorry.

The family stood in line until it was their turn. Susie stepped up to the ticket machine and placed her hand over the buttons. Before she could press them, her dad grabbed her hand and said, "Susie, we can't go on the subway today. I'm sorry, sweetheart."

Susie was confused and asked why. Her dad sighed and said, "We don't have enough money." Susie started to cry, she was so disappointed. All of her excitement had quickly faded away.

Susie's mom put her arms around her and said, "Don't worry, Susie. We'll go to the subway another day." But deep down Susie knew that "another day" was never going to come.
