Once upon a time, there was a little boy. He was only three. He was playing alone outside when he saw a cooler by the side of the road. He was very excited and ran over to open it. Inside was a big surprise! He found a big turtle. It was so cool. He wanted to take it home.

The turtle was troubled. It had been there for a long time and was very scared. The little boy tried to calm him, but it wouldn't snap out of it. He couldn't understand why the turtle was so scared.

Finally, the little boy came up with a plan. He took the cooler with the turtle inside and brought it home. He put it near the lake in his backyard. The lake was cool and peaceful.

The little boy watched over the cooler all day. By night, he noticed the turtle slowly coming out from the cooler. He was finally happy and safe. The turtle snapped around the lake and they became friends.

The little boy and the turtle were very happy and the turtle never had to be troubled again.

Once upon a time there was a bright, sunny day outside. The sun was shining, the birds were chirping and it was the perfect day to have fun.

She looked around for something to help stop it. Then she remembered her bucket! She quickly grabbed the bucket and filled it with hard rocks. Then she spread the rocks around the leak and watched as it slowly stopped growing.

The little girl was very proud of herself. She had saved the day and made the leak stop. She smiled and went back inside the house to enjoy the bright, sunny day.

Anna liked to play with blocks. She had many blocks of different shapes and colors. She could make towers, houses, cars and animals with them. She was very good at playing with blocks.

One day, she wanted to make a big square with her blocks. She looked for all the blocks that had four sides and were the same size. She found many blocks like that, but they were not all the same color. She wanted her square to be all blue, because blue was her favorite color.

She asked her brother Ben, who was playing with cars, if he had any blue blocks. He said no, he only had red and yellow blocks. He did not like blue. Anna was frustrated. She asked her sister Cara, who was drawing with crayons, if she had any blue blocks. She said yes, she had some, but she was using them to make a boat. She did not want to give them to Anna. Anna was more frustrated.

She saw her mom, who was reading a book, and ran to her. She said, "Mom, mom, please, I need blue blocks. I want to make a big square. Please, can you help me?" Her mom smiled and put down her book. She said, "Of course, Anna, I can help you. Let's go and look for some blue blocks together. Maybe we can find some in your toy box, or in the closet, or in the basement. And if we don't find enough, maybe we can ask Ben and Cara to share some with us. What do you think?"

Anna nodded and hugged her mom. She said, "Thank you, mom, you are the best. I love you." Her mom said, "I love you too, Anna. Now, let's go and make that big square." They went to look for blue blocks, and Anna was happy. She was not frustrated anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a kind little girl named Jane. Jane liked to play alone in the park. One day, while she was playing by herself in the park, she heard an unusual sound. It was ringing from far away, like a bell.

"What is that sound?" said Jane.

Just then a friendly voice came from behind her. "It's the ice cream truck," said the voice. "It follows a special line around the park and stops at the stops, to sell ice cream."

Jane's eyes lit up. She ran over to the ice cream truck, where she spotted the kind driver, who smiled at her. Jane bought the yummiest ice cream she ever tasted and the ringing sound filled the park. It was a magical day.

"I have a spot for you to hide," he said.

"Stay here," said Ben. "It's hard for the cats to find you in here."

Patch hopped into the thick patch of grass and stayed there until she was sure it was safe to come out.

They go to the cat. They say, "Hello, cat. Can we pet you?" The cat looks at them. The cat is anxious. The cat does not know them. The cat does not like rain. The cat does not want to be wet.

The cat hisses. The cat runs away. The cat swings its tail. The tail hits Anna and Ben. Anna and Ben are wet. They are wet from the rain and the tail. They are not happy. They cry.

They go back to their house. They see their mom. Their mom is kind. Their mom gives them a hug. Their mom gives them a towel. Their mom gives them a cookie. Their mom says, "Don't be sad. "Look, my car can go very fast!" Lily says. She pushes her car with her hand and it zooms across the floor.

"My car can go fast too!" Ben says. He pushes his car with his hand and it also zooms across the floor.

They have fun racing their cars and making them crash and beep.

But then, Ben's car stops moving. He pushes it harder, but it does not go. He looks at his car and sees a small hole on the bottom.

"Oh no, my car is broken!" Ben says. He feels sad and angry.

Lily sees Ben's car and the hole. She remembers something.

"Maybe your car needs a new battery," she says. She runs to the kitchen and comes back with a small, round thing. "This is a battery. It makes your car go."

She shows Ben how to open the hole and take out the old battery. It is dry and dirty. She puts in the new battery and closes the hole.

"Try it now," she says.

Ben pushes his car with his hand and it zooms across the floor again. He smiles and hugs Lily.

"Thank you, Lily! You fixed my car!" he says.

"You're welcome, Ben! I'm happy to help you," she says.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was very brave and one day she decided to go for an adventure in the dark woods. But it was getting late and the sky went black.

The brave little girl was afraid and started to stumble as she walked. Soon enough, she noticed a strange yellow light. She knew she had to explore it no matter how scared she felt.

She kept walking towards the light and suddenly she could smell something strange. It was a very bitter smell. The brave girl was brave and decided to keep exploring.

When she got closer, she realized that the yellow light was coming from a house in the middle of the dark woods. But before she could knock on the door, the door opened and out came a huge, ugly monster!

The brave girl was terrified, but she remembered how brave she was and decided she had to take on the monster. She bravely ran around the monster, making it stumble and fall on the ground.

Afterwards, she quickly ran away from the dark woods and back home to safety. The brave little girl never forgot how brave she was and lived happily ever after!

Sam and Lily were playing in the park. They saw a big tree with a hole in it. They wanted to see what was inside.

"Can we go look, please?" Sam asked Lily.

"OK, but be careful. Maybe there is a bug or a snake in there," Lily said.

They walked to the tree and peeked into the hole. It was dark and quiet. They heard a soft voice.

"Hello, little ones. Do you want to play with me?" the voice said.

Sam and Lily were curious. They could not see who was talking.

"Who are you?" Sam asked.

"I am a spirit. I live in this tree. I am lonely and bored. Come inside and I will show you some fun things," the voice said.

Sam and Lily thought it sounded nice. They wanted to have fun.

"OK, we will come in. But we have to go back soon. Our mom is waiting for us," Lily said.

They crawled into the hole and followed the voice. They did not know that the voice was lying. It was not a friendly spirit. It was a hungry spirit. It wanted to eat them.

The spirit led them to a dark and cold place. It had sharp teeth and claws. It smiled and said, "Thank you for coming, little ones. You are my dinner today."

Sam and Lily were scared. They tried to run away, but it was too late. The spirit grabbed them and bit them. They screamed and cried, but no one heard them.

The spirit ate them and felt happy. It had not eaten for a long time. It said, "That was delicious. I hope more children come to play with me."

The end.

One day, a little girl was talking to her mommy. She said, "Mommy, why do I have to worry? What is worry?" Her mommy said, "Worry is when you feel sad and scared about something." The little girl said, "Oh, ok. What can I do if I worry?"

Her mommy said, "Well, one thing you can do is look at a picture. A picture can make you feel better if you're worried, because it helps you to feel calm. So just look at a picture that you like, and it will make you feel better."

The little girl said, "Ok!" She went to get a picture from her bedroom. She picked out a dirty, old picture that she liked. She looked at it and smiled. She was no longer worried.

The little girl stuck the picture on her wall. She looked at it every day when she was feeling scared or worried. The picture helped her to feel brave, strong, and happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing in the garden with her spade. One day, her friend Jack came to play with her.

"Hi Lily, what are you doing?" asked Jack.

"I'm digging in the dirt with my spade," replied Lily.

"What are you looking for?" asked Jack.

"I'm looking for worms," said Lily. "I like to count how many I find."

After a while, Lily and Jack decided to have a picnic. They found a clear spot in the garden and laid out a blanket. They counted how many sandwiches they had and how many apples they could share.

"I love counting," said Lily. "It's so much fun!"

Jack agreed and they continued to count everything they could find in the garden. They had so much fun together and couldn't wait to play again.

Ben and Lily were bored. It was a gloomy day and they could not go outside. They looked at their toys, but they did not want to play with them. They wanted to do something fun and new.

"Let's spray water!" Ben said. He had an idea. He ran to the bathroom and got a spray bottle. He filled it with water and came back to the living room. "Look, Lily, I have a spray bottle. We can spray water on the plants, or the couch, or the wall!"

Lily smiled. She liked Ben's idea. She got another spray bottle and filled it with water too. They both ran around the living room, spraying water on everything they saw. They laughed and giggled as they made wet spots on the carpet, the curtains, and the pictures.

"Stop! Stop!" Mom said. She came into the living room and saw the mess. She was not happy. She took the spray bottles from Ben and Lily and put them away. "What are you doing? You can't spray water on the furniture and the walls! You will ruin them! You have to clean up this mess right now!"

Ben and Lily felt sorry. They did not mean to make Mom angry. They just wanted to have fun. They got some towels and wiped the water off the things they sprayed. They said sorry to Mom and gave her a hug.

Mom hugged them back. She was not angry anymore. She understood that they were bored and tried to have fun. She said, "It's okay, Ben and Lily. I know you were just playing. But next time, you have to ask me before you spray water on anything. And you have to be careful not to damage anything. Do you understand?"

Ben and Lily nodded. They understood. They said, "Yes, Mom. We understand. We will ask you next time. And we will be careful."

Mom smiled. She said, "Good. I love you, Ben and Lily. Now, why don't we do something else? How about we read a book, or play a game, or bake some cookies?"

Ben and Lily liked Mom's suggestions. They said, "Yes, Mom. That sounds fun. We love you too, Mom."

They followed Mom to the kitchen, where they found some books, some games, and some cookie dough. They forgot about the gloomy day and the spray bottles. They had a good time with Mom.

Once upon a time, a mummy, daddy and their little girl went to a park. The little girl was excited. She ran around, played with the other children and had lots of fun. Suddenly, she noticed something weird. It was a weapon that someone had left behind in the sandpit.

The little girl got curious. She picked it up, as she hadn't seen anything like it before. It felt cold and heavy in her hands. She looked around to see if anyone noticed her, but no-one was paying attention.

Suddenly, she realized what it was. She was holding a weapon. It made her feel scared and she quickly put it back where she found it. After that, the little girl went back to playing with her friends. She had learnt an important lesson that day. We must all be careful and never touch weird things!

Ben and Sam are friends. They like to play with cars and blocks. One day, they have a test at school. The test is about the nation. The nation is where they live. It has a flag and a song.

Ben is scared of the test. He does not know the flag and the song. He did not study. He says to Sam, "I do not want to do the test. Can we hide?"

Sam is thoughtful. He studied for the test. He knows the flag and the song. He says to Ben, "No, we cannot hide. The test is not hard. I can help you. Look, this is the flag. It has red and white stripes and blue stars. And this is the song. It goes like this: Oh, say can you see..."

Ben listens to Sam. He looks at the flag and the song. He tries to remember them. He says to Sam, "Thank you, Sam. You are a good friend. You are very thoughtful. Maybe the test is not so scary."

They go to the classroom. The teacher gives them the test. Ben and Sam do their best. They write the flag and the song. They are happy. They pass the test. They hug each other. They say, "We did it! We know the nation!"

It was something called yoga. She had never seen anything like it before. She wanted to learn more about it.

Jill decided to try it and so she joined a warm room with lots of people. They all smiled and made her feel welcome. Everyone was doing the same poses and breathing in a focused way.

Jill felt scares at first but with the help of her friends, she started to feel more relaxed. It was a warm experience and she felt the energy of the group.

Jill came away from the experience with a smile on her face. She had found something special and new. She would always remember this day and the amazing thing she had found.

One day there was a lonely spark in the gloomy sky. It was a magical spark and brought lots of fun wherever it went. The spark was so sparkly that everyone wanted to play with it. Everywhere the spark flew, children laughed and sang.

But then one day the spark got lost. No one could find it, even though they looked high and low. People were worried and sad. Everyone wondered where the spark had gone.

Then one day, little Tommy was walking in the park, when he saw the spark. He ran and grabbed it, but the spark started to disappear. Tommy held on tight, but the spark kept getting smaller and smaller, until it was too small for Tommy to hold.

The spark had disappeared, and little Tommy was very sad. But just then, a tiny speck of light shone from the sky, and it was the spark! It had gone up to the sky, but every so often it shone down from above and reminded little Tommy that it was still around, like a magical star of hope.

A little girl wanted to explore the big building. She said, "Let's look!" and she walked inside.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who loved to explore the world around her. She found a big and messy room filled with lots of exciting things. Everywhere she looked, she could find something new, like a bright red toy car. She was so excited! But then she saw the price tag--it said it was too expensive for her.

The little girl was sad, but then she remembered her mommy's words. Her mommy said, if you want something, you have to keep trying. So, the little girl started working hard to save up enough money to buy the bright red toy car.

She religiously kept her pocket money in a jar and added to it whenever she could. After a few months, the little girl had saved up enough money to buy the toy car!

The little girl was so excited that she kept the toy car with her everywhere she went. But, she stopped herself from making a mess by playing with it in places she wasn't supposed to. Even after all these years, the little girl still loves her bright red toy car and it is her most prized possession.

Ben and Lily were playing in the garden. They liked to look for insects and see what they did. They saw ants, bees, ladybugs and spiders. They were careful not to touch them or hurt them.

"Look, Ben, I found a caterpillar!" Lily said, holding a green and fuzzy insect in her hand. "It is so soft and cute!"

"Let me see!" Ben said, reaching for the caterpillar. "Wow, it is! Can we keep it as a pet?"

Lily nodded. "Yes, let's. We can make a home for it in a jar. Maybe it will turn into a butterfly!"

They ran inside the house and found an empty jar. They put some leaves and grass in it and made a hole in the lid. They gently placed the caterpillar inside and closed the jar.

They put the jar on the windowsill and watched the caterpillar. They gave it a name: Fluffy.

The next day, they went to check on Fluffy. But they were shocked by what they saw. Fluffy was not green and fuzzy anymore. He was brown and hard. He looked like a stick.

"What happened to Fluffy?" Lily cried. "He is not Fluffy anymore!"

Ben frowned. "Maybe he is sick. Or maybe he is sleeping. Maybe he will wake up and be Fluffy again."

They decided to wait and see. They hoped Fluffy would be okay.

The day after, they went to check on Fluffy again. But they were even more shocked by what they saw. Fluffy was not brown and hard anymore. He was not Fluffy at all. He was a butterfly. A beautiful butterfly with orange and black wings.

But the butterfly was not happy. He was trapped in the jar. He wanted to fly and be free. He tried to push the lid, but it was too tight. He tried to flap his wings, but it was too hot. He felt his wings start to melt.

"Help me! Help me!" the butterfly cried. But Ben and Lily could not hear him. They only saw the butterfly moving in the jar.

"Look, Lily, Fluffy turned into a butterfly!" Ben said, smiling. "He is so pretty!"

Lily smiled too. "He is! But he looks sad. Maybe he wants to go outside."

Ben nodded. "Maybe you are right. Let's open the jar and let him go."

They opened the jar and lifted the lid. The butterfly flew out of the jar and landed on Lily's hand. He looked at them with his big eyes.

"Thank you, thank you!" the butterfly said. But Ben and Lily could not understand him. They only heard a buzz.

"You are welcome, Fluffy!" Lily said, stroking his wings. "We are sorry we kept you in the jar. We did not know you would change."

The butterfly nodded. He was glad they were sorry. He was glad they let him go. He was not mad at them. He knew they were just curious.

He flew away from Lily's hand and into the air. He felt the breeze and the sun. He felt happy and free.

He waved his wings at Ben and Lily. "Goodbye, goodbye!" he said. But Ben and Lily could not see him. They only saw a flash of orange and black.

"Goodbye, Fluffy!" Ben and Lily said, waving back. "We hope you have a good life!"

They watched the butterfly fly away and disappear. They felt sad and happy at the same time. They learned something new. They learned that caterpillars turn into butterflies. They learned that insects can change. They learned that sometimes it is better to let things go.

John was so excited he could hardly keep still. But his mama warned him not to press too many of the buttons at once.

"It might make you dizzy," she said.

John promised to follow her advice and started pressing the buttons. Then something amazing happened. Suddenly the model started printing a beautiful picture. It was a picture of John and his mama together, holding hands and smiling.

John was so surprised that he was dizzy for a moment. He couldn't believe it was true. But it was - the model had printed the special picture he wanted so badly.

Lily and Ben are playing in the park. They see a big dog with brown fur and a long tail. The dog is running and barking. Lily and Ben want to touch the dog.

"Can we touch the dog?" Lily asks Ben.

"I don't know. Maybe he is tough and he will bite us," Ben says.

They run after the dog. The dog sees them and stops. He wags his tail and licks his nose. He looks friendly.

"Hi, dog. You are nice. Can we touch you?" Lily says.

She reaches out her hand to touch the dog's fur. The dog sniffs her hand and licks it. Lily giggles.

"See, he is not tough. He is soft and warm. You can touch him too, Ben," Lily says.

Ben is still scared. He stays behind Lily. He does not want to touch the dog.

"No, thank you. I don't want to touch him. He is too big and loud," Ben says.

Lily and the dog play together. They run and jump and roll on the grass. Ben watches them. He wishes he was brave like Lily. He wants to touch the dog too.

"Come on, Ben. Don't be afraid. The dog is nice. He will not hurt you. He wants to play with you too," Lily says.

She holds the dog's collar and brings him to Ben. The dog wags his tail and looks at Ben with his big eyes.

"Hello, Ben. I am Rex. I like you. Can you touch me?" the dog says.

Ben is surprised. He did not know that dogs can talk. He looks at Lily. She smiles and nods.

"Go ahead, Ben. He is talking to you. He wants to be your friend," Lily says.

Ben takes a deep breath. He reaches out his hand to touch the dog's fur. The dog sniffs his hand and licks it. Ben smiles.

"Hi, Rex. You are nice. I like you too. You are not tough. You are soft and warm. Can I play with you?" Ben says.

The dog barks happily. He nods his head.

"Yes, Ben. You can play with me. You are brave. You are my friend," the dog says.

Lily and Ben and the dog play together. They have fun. They are happy. They are friends.

Once there was a dog and his owner. The dog was big and the owner was fair. One day, they went to play in the park. The sun was shining and the sky was blue.

While they were playing, a little bug came by. The bug said, "I can make things shrink!" The owner and the dog looked at the bug. The owner said, "Please don't shrink us!"

The bug laughed and said, "I won't shrink you, but I will shrink your ball." The bug touched the ball and it shrank. The dog and the owner were sad, but they still had fun playing with the small ball.

At the end of the day, the bug said, "I will make your ball big again." The bug touched the ball and it grew back to its normal size. The owner and the dog were happy and thanked the bug. They all became friends and played together every day.

Tom is a boy who likes to play outside. He has a big yard with many trees and flowers. He also has a small coop with four chickens. He likes to feed them and watch them peck and cluck.

One day, Tom decides to explore the yard by himself. He is an independent boy who likes to discover new things. He puts on his boots and his hat and takes a basket. He says to his mom, "I am going to look for eggs, Mom. Can I?"

Mom smiles and says, "Yes, you can, Tom. But be careful and don't go too far. And come back soon, OK?"

Tom nods and says, "OK, Mom. Bye!" He runs to the coop and opens the door. He sees the four chickens sitting on their nests. He says, "Hello, chickens. Do you have any eggs for me?" He looks under each chicken and finds one egg. He puts them in his basket and says, "Thank you, chickens. You are very good."

Tom is happy with his eggs. He decides to keep exploring the yard. He walks around the trees and the flowers. He sees a butterfly and a bee. He sees a worm and a snail. He says, "Hello, butterfly. Hello, bee. Hello, worm. Hello, snail. You are very pretty."

Tom is having fun. He does not notice that he is going far from the house. He sees a fence and a gate. He says, "I wonder what is behind the fence. Maybe there are more chickens. Maybe there are more eggs. I want to see." He opens the gate and goes through.

Tom is surprised by what he sees. He sees a big field with many cows and sheep. He sees a barn and a tractor. He sees a man and a dog. He says, "Wow, this is a farm. This is not my yard. Where am I?"

The man sees Tom and says, "Hey, kid. What are you doing here? This is not your place. This is my place. Who are you?"

Tom is scared. He does not know the man. He does not know the dog. He says, "I am Tom. I live over there. I was looking for eggs. I have four chickens. See?" He shows his basket.

The man says, "Oh, I see. You have four chickens. That's nice. But you should not come here without asking. This is not safe. There are big animals here. They can hurt you. And you can get lost. Where is your mom?"

Tom says, "My mom is at home. She said I can look for eggs. But she said I should not go too far. And I should come back soon. I am sorry. I did not mean to come here. I was curious. I like to discover new things."

The man says, "I understand. You are an independent boy who likes to discover new things. That's good. But you should also listen to your mom. She knows what is best for you. She loves you. And she must be worried about you. You should go back now. And next time, if you want to see my farm, you can ask me. I can show you around. And maybe I can give you some eggs. I have many chickens. See?" He points to a large coop with dozens of chickens.

Tom says, "Thank you, sir. You are very kind. I would like to see your farm. And I would like some eggs. But not today. Today I have to go back. My mom is waiting for me. And I miss her. Goodbye, sir. Goodbye, dog. Goodbye, cows. Goodbye, sheep." He waves and runs back to the gate.

Tom goes back to his yard. He sees his house and his mom. He says, "Mom, Mom, I am back. I have eggs. And I have a story. I went to a farm. I saw a man and a dog. And many cows and sheep. And many chickens. It was amazing. But I am sorry. I went too far. I did not listen to you. I was wrong. Are you mad at me?"

Mom hugs Tom and says, "No, I am not mad at you, Tom. I am glad you are back. I was worried about you. You should not go too far. You should listen to me. I am happy you have eggs. And I am happy you have a story. You are an independent boy who likes to discover new things. That's wonderful. But you are also my boy who I love very much. And I want you to be safe. OK?"

Tom nods and says, "OK, Mom. I love you too. And I will be safe. And I will listen

Once upon a time, in a big green forest, there was a small rabbit named Remy. Remy was a nervous rabbit. He was always scared of big animals and loud noises. One day, Remy saw a sign that said, "Race Today! The winner gets a big prize!" Remy wanted to join the race and win.

Remy went to the race and saw many big animals. He felt even more nervous. But Remy's best friend, a tiny bird named Bree, said, "Don't worry, Remy! You can do it!" Remy took a deep breath and got ready to run.

The race started, and Remy ran as fast as he could. He passed a big bear who was too busy chewing on a big piece of honeycomb. Remy kept running and soon, he saw the finish line. With one last jump, Remy crossed the finish line and won the race!

All the animals cheered for Remy. He was so happy, and he wasn't nervous anymore. Remy and Bree shared the big prize, a huge pile of carrots, and they lived happily ever after.

Nina had so much fun, she couldn't believe when it was time to go home. She smiled and waved goodbye before skating back inside. What a fun day it had been!

Once upon a time there was an old man. He was walking in the woods. Suddenly he heard a voice. It said, "Hello there!"

The old man looked around and saw a young girl. He asked her, "Who are you?"

The girl said, "My name is Jane. I was talking to you!"

Jane and the old man started to talk. They talked about everything from the weather to the beef the old man was cooking for dinner. Jane was very interested because she had never tried beef before.

They became friends and the old man shared a piece of his beef with Jane. Jane enjoyed it and asked for more.

The old man smiled and said, "I'm glad you're enjoying it, Jane. I have plenty of beef for you. You can come back here anytime you want!"

And so, Jane and the old man were the best of friends. They talked and ate beef together often.

Once upon a time, there was a group of little bunnies. One of the bunnies was different from the rest, for he was much tougher than the rest! He was brave, strong and daring.

Every day, the bunnies would hop around in the meadow, playing and having fun. All the other bunnies were scared to try any daring jumps, but the tough bunny could hop higher than the rest.

The other bunnies began to get jealous of how tough the bunny was and they started pushing him around. The tough bunny tried to stay brave and hop around but the group kept bullying him.

One day, the tough bunny decided enough was enough and he loudly declared, "I may be different, but I will still be brave and keep hopping!"

The other bunnies realised their mistake, apologising for their meanness and accepting the tough bunny for who he was.

From then on, each bunny was accepted for who they were and the group hopped happily together!

The moral of this story is that it is important to accept everyone for who they are, no matter how different. Everyone should be accepted and celebrated for their unique qualities!

Tom and Lily smile. They say, "Thank you, Mom. We love you. We will be quiet. And we will clean up our toys."

Mom smiles too. She says, "I love you too, Tom and Lily. You are good twins. Now, let me finish my work."

Tom and Lily play quietly. They are happy. Mom finishes her work. She is happy too. They hug and kiss. They are a happy family.

When they were done, the island looked so nice and clean. There were no more leaves or sticks, and everywhere was sparkly and bright. The animals were so happy. Now they could enjoy their clean and happy island.

Tom and Mia went to the big water with Mom and Dad. They saw many fish and animals in the water. They liked to watch them swim and jump. One animal was very big and gray. It had a long nose and a big fin. It was a dolphin.

The dolphin came close to Tom and Mia. It made a funny sound. "Eee, eee, eee!" It seemed to say hello. Tom and Mia smiled and waved. They wanted to touch the dolphin. It looked very soft and smooth.

Mom and Dad said they could touch the dolphin. They helped Tom and Mia go into the water. The water was cold and wet. The dolphin was warm and soft. It let Tom and Mia pet its nose and fin. It seemed to like them. It gave them a gentle push with its nose. It seemed to want them to play.

Tom and Mia laughed and splashed. They played with the dolphin. They had a lot of fun. They thanked the dolphin for being their friend. The dolphin made another sound. "Eee, eee, eee!" It seemed to say goodbye. It swam away with a big splash.

Tom and Mia went back to Mom and Dad. They were happy and tired. They told Mom and Dad about the dolphin. They said it was the best day ever. They hoped to see the dolphin again.

The friends ate the fruit and their faces were covered in sticky juice. They laughed and played for the rest of the day until it was time for them to go home.

Once upon a time, there was a wise old bird. The bird lived in a big tree. One day, the bird found a screw on the ground. The bird did not know what it was.

The wise bird went to a squirrel friend and asked, "What is this?" The squirrel friend looked at the screw and said, "I don't know. Let's ask the rabbit." So they went to the rabbit's home.

The rabbit looked at the screw and said, "Oh, I know! That is a screw. It helps hold things together." The wise bird managed to find the right animal to help. The bird, the squirrel, and the rabbit all became good friends. They played and had fun together every day.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to go on adventures with her family. One day, they went camping in the mountains. They woke up early in the morning to see the sunrise. It was so pretty and pink!

After breakfast, Lily's mom asked her to help pack up their tent and sleeping bags. Lily was happy to help. She made sure everything was in the right bag and the tent was folded up nicely.

As they drove home, Lily thought about how perfect the trip was. She couldn't wait to go on another adventure with her family.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was happy and had many paint boxes in her bedroom. Every day, she enjoyed raising her paintbrushes and delighting in all the colors that the paint could make.

One day, the little girl felt nervous. She was scared of heights and wanted to get down from the wall, where she had been painting. But before she could climb down, there was a loud boom. The wall suddenly collapsed and the girl was trapped.

The little girl was sad and scared. But then, something amazing happened. Helpers quickly arrived to rescue her. They were able to set her free from the rubble.

After it was all over, the little girl was safe and sound. She never forgot the lesson she learned that day. No matter how much fun it is to raise paints and colors, one must always be careful and not take risks around heights.

Once there was a girl named Sarah. She was three years old and she liked to explore. One day, she asked her mom to explain what style meant. Her mom smiled and said, "Style is the special way you do things. It's like your own special way of doing things that's different from other people."

Sarah thought about that for a while and then asked, "What's normal?" Her mom answered, "Normal means what most people do or what's typical or common. For example, most people wear clothes, so wearing clothes is normal."

Sarah was very happy with her mom's explanation. She already had her own special style, but now she knew special didn't have to mean not normal. She decided she was happy to be both special and normal!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved horses and always dreamed of riding one. One day, she saw a horse race and wanted to watch it. She asked her mom, "Can we go see the horses run fast?"

Her mom said, "Sure, let's go!" They went to the race and watched the horses run. One horse was very fast and won the race. Lily was happy and said, "Wow, that horse is so fast! I want to ride a fast horse too!"

Her mom said, "Maybe one day you will ride a fast horse and win a race too." Lily smiled and said, "That would be so cool! But I don't want a dull horse, I want a fast one!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys, especially her dolls. One day, Lily's mom asked her to turn off the light in her room. Lily didn't want to do it because she was scared of the dark.

"Lily, can you please turn off the light?" her mom requested.

"No, I don't want to. It's too dark," Lily replied.

Her mom explained that the light wasn't needed during the day and that it was selfish to waste electricity. Lily understood and agreed to turn off the light. From that day on, she made sure to turn off the light when she left a room to save energy.

One day, a kid was playing with his pet in the backyard when his eyes caught a glimmer from the tree. He saw that there was a shiny gem stuck in the trunk. The kid said to his pet, "Look there's a glittery gem in that tree." His pet didn't believe him, but the kid was sure.

The kid took the shiny gem out and tried to put it in his pocket, but the gem was too big. He tried and tried, but it wouldn't fit. The gem was too tight. The kid's pet watched him in confusion.

The kid didn't want to leave the gem behind, so he came up with a plan. He looked around and found a stick. He stuck the stick in the crack of the trunk, and then he started to tear.

The kid worked hard until suddenly, the sound of cracking loudly filled the air. The kid had managed to tear the trunk in two. The gem fit perfectly in his pocket, and the kid felt happy that he could bring it home. He felt relieved as he held the gem tight in his pocket.

Once upon a time, there was a happy girl named Mia. She loved to dance and go outside to play. Every day, when Mia walked in the park she would dance around in circles with the birds.

One day, when Mia was in the park, she saw something shiny and sparkly in the trees. It was a box that said 'blue chocolate' on it. Mia was so excited to open the box.

She asked her mom, "Can I open the box please?" Her mother smiled and said, "Yes, you can open it, but only after you dance for 10 minutes!" Mia was so happy that she started to jerk and twirl around like a monkey.

When Mia finished her dance, her mom opened the box to reveal a big plate of blue chocolates. Mia ate all of the chocolates and smiled a very big smile. She learned that if you dance and have patience, you can get what you wish!

Sam and Mia are playing in the sand. They make a big castle with a tower and a flag. They are happy and proud of their castle.

But then a big wind comes and blows the flag away. The tower falls down and the sand is messy. Sam and Mia are sad and angry.

"Who did this?" Sam asks.

"The wind did this," Mia says. "It is not nice."

"Can we fix it?" Sam asks.

Mia thinks for a moment. She sees the sun shining in the sky. She has an idea.

"Maybe we can," she says. "We need the sun to help us."

She runs to the water and fills a bucket. She brings it back to the sand. She pours some water on the sand and makes it smooth and sticky.

"Look, Sam," she says. "Now we can fix the castle. The water makes the sand strong."

Sam smiles and nods. He helps Mia to fix the tower and the flag. They use the water and the sand to make the castle smooth and pretty again.

They are happy and proud of their castle again.

They look at the sun and say, "Thank you, sun, for helping us fix the castle. You are nice and bright."

Once upon a time, there was a small girl named Jenny. She was three years old. One day, Jenny found an interesting tube of toys. She was so excited!

She grabbed the tube and opened it. Inside, she could see lots of colourful shapes. There were stars, circles, squares and more. She was awe-struck!

Jenny said, "What shall I do?"

Her mommy smiled and said, "Let's count them, Jenny!"

So, Jenny and her mommy counted together. When they finished, they had counted 32 shapes.

Jenny was very happy. She smiled and said, "This is so interesting!"

Then, they put the toys back in the tube and put it away. Jenny and her mommy had a great time counting together.

Once upon a time, there was a popular hotel. Many people liked to stay there. One day, a girl named Lucy came to the hotel with her mom and dad. They were looking for a fun place to stay.

In the hotel, Lucy met a boy named Tim. Tim said, "Hi, I am Tim. Do you want to play with me?" Lucy said, "Yes, let's find a fun game to play!" They looked around the hotel and found a big room with toys.

They played with the toys and had lots of fun. When it was time to go, Lucy said, "Bye, Tim! I had fun playing with you." Tim said, "Bye, Lucy! I hope we can play again soon." Lucy and her family left the hotel with big smiles on their faces.

Once upon a time, there was a gray bird that was very, very fast! Every morning it would lift off the ground and soar through the blue sky. People in the park would watch the bird with amazement!

One day, the gray bird saw some kids playing with a kite. They were running, trying to make the kite go up in the sky. So the gray bird flew over and tried to help. It flew around and around, lifting higher and higher!

The kids were so excited! They cheered as the kite flew higher and higher with the help of the gray bird. When the kite finally landed back on the ground, the bird happily flew away to its nest.

The kids could never forget the fast, gray bird that helped them lift the kite in the sky. They hoped it would come back soon!

Once upon a time, there was a very sad alligator. He had no one to care for him. Each day was very bitter and he had no friends.

One day, he saw a little girl playing in the park. He wanted to be friends with her, to have someone to care for him. He slowly crawled up to her and said, "Hello". The little girl was surprised to see an alligator and she slowly backed away.

The alligator was very sad. He wanted to be friends and show the little girl how much he cared. He backed away slowly, understanding that the little girl was scared.

The next day, the alligator saw the same little girl in the park. He decided to try again. He slowly walked up to her and calmly said, "Please, I just want to be friends". The little girl smiled, understanding that the alligator only wanted to be friends and care for someone.

From then on, the alligator and the little girl were best friends. They played together in the park every day and the little girl cared for the alligator. The alligator was no longer sad or bitter - he was happy and joyful!

Lily and Tom are playing in the park. They see a big slide. They want to go on the slide. They run to the slide. But there is a problem. The slide is too high. They need to climb a ladder to reach the slide.

Lily is brave. She climbs the ladder. She holds on to the bars. She balances on the steps. She reaches the top of the slide. She looks down. She sees Tom. He is still on the ground. He is scared.

"Come on, Tom!" Lily says. "You can do it! It is fun!"

Tom shakes his head. He does not want to climb the ladder. He does not like heights. He thinks he will fall.

"No, Lily," Tom says. "I can't do it. It is too high. I want to go back."

Lily is sad. She wants to slide with Tom. She does not want to slide alone. She thinks of an idea.

"Look, Tom," Lily says. "There is another exit. You don't have to climb the ladder. You can go around the slide. There is a tunnel. You can crawl through the tunnel. You can meet me at the bottom of the slide."

Tom looks. He sees the tunnel. He likes tunnels. He thinks they are fun. He decides to try.

"Okay, Lily," Tom says. "I will go through the tunnel. You go on the slide. I will see you soon."

Lily smiles. She is happy. She slides down the slide. She feels the wind in her hair. She laughs. She reaches the bottom of the slide. She sees Tom. He is crawling out of the tunnel. He is smiling too.

"Hi, Lily," Tom says. "That was fun!"

"Hi, Tom," Lily says. "You did it! You are good!"

They hug. They are happy. They are friends. They play some more. They have a good time.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside, especially on windy days. He would run around and laugh with his friends. One day, Timmy's dad brought him a kite. The kite was big and colorful, and Timmy was so happy.

Timmy and his dad went to the park to fly the kite. Timmy ran and ran, and the kite went up and up. It soared high in the sky, and Timmy felt like he was flying too. The sun was shining, and the breeze was blowing, and Timmy had never felt so free.

But then, the wind started to get stronger. The kite was pulling harder and harder, and Timmy couldn't hold on. Suddenly, the kite broke free from his hands and flew away. Timmy was so sad. He looked up at the dirty sky, hoping to see his kite again. But it was gone.

Days passed, and Timmy had almost forgotten about his kite. But then, one day, he saw something in the sky. It was his kite! It had flown all the way back to him. Timmy was so happy, and he knew he would never let it go again.

Lila liked to play with her dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was a big doll with long hair and a velvet dress. The dress was colorful, with red and blue and yellow flowers. Lila liked to brush the doll's hair and make her dance and sing.

One day, Lila's mom said they had to go to grandma's house for a visit. Lila wanted to bring her doll, but mom said no. She said the doll was too big and too old and might get dirty or lost. Lila was sad, but she did what mom said. She left her doll on her bed and gave her a kiss goodbye.

At grandma's house, Lila had fun. She played with grandma's cat and ate cookies and read stories. She forgot about her doll for a while. But then, it started to rain. The rain was heavy and loud and made the windows wet. Lila looked outside and saw a big puddle in the yard. She wondered if her doll was okay.

When they came back home, Lila ran to her room. She wanted to see her doll and hug her and tell her about her day. But when she got to her bed, she saw something terrible. Her doll was not there. Instead, there was a hole in the roof and a pile of wet leaves and dirt. The rain had come in and ruined her bed and her doll.

Lila cried and cried. She picked up the leaves and dirt and looked for her doll. She found her under the mess, but she did not look like her doll anymore. Her hair was wet and tangled and full of bugs. Her velvet dress was torn and muddy and rotten. Her face was faded and cracked and sad.

Lila sobbed and held her doll. She said, "I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I love you." Mom came in and saw what happened. She hugged Lila and said, "I'm sorry, too, honey. It was an accident. We'll get you a new doll, okay?" But Lila shook her head. She said, "No, I want this doll. She's my favorite. She's my friend." Mom sighed and said, "Okay, okay. We'll try to fix her, okay? But she might not be the same as before. Do you understand?" Lila nodded and said, "I understand. But I don't care. I love her anyway." Mom smiled and said, "You're a good girl, Lila. And a good friend." She took Lila and her doll to the bathroom and tried to wash them and dry them and make them better. Lila hoped her doll would be happy again. She hoped the rain would stop. She hoped the hole in the roof would be fixed. She hoped her doll would still like her colorful velvet dress.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and go to the park. One day, she saw a big bird in the sky and she called out to it, "Hello birdie!"

The bird didn't answer, so Lily asked her mommy, "Why won't the birdie talk to me?"

Her mommy said, "Maybe the birdie is too far away to hear you. But did you know that when you grow up, you can go to a big school called a university and learn how to talk to birds and other animals?"

Lily's eyes widened in amazement. "Wow, a powerful school like that exists?" she asked.

"Yes, it does," her mommy replied. "And who knows, maybe one day you'll be a powerful animal communicator!"

Once upon a time there were two mice that lived in a farm. Their names were Daisy and Tom. Tom was very brave but Daisy was a bit fearful.
One day, Tom sailed away in a cage. Daisy was very sad and cried for many hours. She was so fearful that something bad may have happened to him.
Suddenly, Daisy heard a voice. It was Tom! Tom explained that he had floated away in his cage and the wind had brought him back to the farm. He had learnt an important lesson - that the greatest adventures are close to home.

So, Tom and Daisy stayed home, but still often went exploring together. They were no longer fearful, as they knew that even the most amazing adventures were right in their backyard. In the end, they both learnt an important life lesson - that home is a safe and special place.

Once upon a time, there was a little chicken named Penny. Penny loved to play with her friends and run around the farm. One day, Penny found a big egg in the barn. She picked it up and gave it a shake. It made a loud noise! Penny was so surprised that she dropped the egg.

Suddenly, the egg cracked open and out popped a baby chick. The baby chick was so small and fluffy. Penny was happy to have a new friend to play with. They ran around the farm together and had so much fun.

From that day on, Penny and the baby chick were always together. They played and explored the farm together. And every time they found an egg, they would give it a shake to see if it made a loud noise too.

Once upon a time, a dinosaur arrived. She was a big and friendly dinosaur, and she was very hungry. She looked around and saw a tasty apple. She picked it up and ate it. Then, she looked around and saw a big pile of tasty tomatoes. She ate them all up. Finally, she saw a big mound of yummy carrots. She munched on them until they were all gone. Then, she gave a big dinosaur roar of happiness because she had been so full and happy. Then, she curled up and went to sleep, content to have found such delicious food. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Joe. He had a nice coat that was very warm and cosy. One day Joe asked his dad to lend him his high boots. His dad said yes and Joe was so excited to wear them.

So Joe put on his coat and the boots and went outside. He felt so tall in his high boots! Joe enjoyed jumping and running around in them. After a while, he decided to lend them to his friend.

Joe's friend was also excited to wear the boots and he thanked Joe for lending them. Joe was happy that he could share his coat and boots with his friend. He enjoyed being outside with his friends and having lots of fun.

Once upon a time, a little frog named Tim went to a festival. The festival was full of fun games and yummy treats. Tim saw his friends and they all played together.

At the festival, there was a big jump game. Tim and his friends took turns to jump. The ground was a bit rough, but they didn't care. They laughed and jumped as high as they could.

In the end, Tim won the jump game. He was so happy! His friends clapped for him and they all had more fun at the festival. They ate sweet treats and played more games. It was a great day for Tim and his friends.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. They have lunch in a bag. They sit on a bench and eat.

"Look, Ben, I have a sandwich with cheese and ham. Do you want some?" Anna says.

"No, thank you, Anna. I have a banana and a cookie. Do you want some?" Ben says.

"Yes, please, Ben. I like bananas and cookies. They are sweet and soft." Anna says.

They share their lunch and talk. They see a bird on a tree. They see a dog on a leash. They see a flower on the grass.

"Anna, can I hold your hand?" Ben says.

"Sure, Ben. Why do you want to hold my hand?" Anna says.

"Because you are my friend. And because you are pale. You need some sun." Ben says.

Anna laughs. She is pale because she has blond hair and blue eyes. Ben is tan because he has brown hair and brown eyes.

"I don't need sun, Ben. I like being pale. But I like holding your hand, too. Because you are my friend." Anna says.

They hold hands and smile. They finish their lunch and play some more. They are happy.

Once upon a time there was a lady who had a big house. The house had a door with a special key. One day the lady started to go out for a long walk and she put the key in her pocket. Unfortunately the rude wind came and blew the key away. The lady searched high and low, but she could not find the key anywhere. She was stuck outside her house and could not get in.

A little girl who was three years old saw the lady and felt sad for her. The girl asked the lady if she needed help. The lady said yes and the little girl started looking for the key. She looked in the grass, behind the trees and even up in the sky, but she could not find the key.

The little girl thought of a clever plan. She suggested that they go look in the garden and see if the key had gotten stuck in the soil. They searched and searched, and finally the little girl found the key! The lady was so happy and thanked the little girl. She opened the door and went inside her house.

The little girl had done an amazing thing! She was so kind and helpful, even though the wind had been very rude. From then on, the lady and the little girl were best friends.

Lily liked to play with her doll in the living room. She had a tea party with her doll and some cups and plates. She put the cups and plates on the table. The table was near the window. On the window sill, there was a vase. The vase was big and blue and had flowers in it.

Lily wanted to smell the flowers. She stood on a chair and reached for the vase. But the vase was too far and too heavy. Lily could not hold the vase. The vase slipped from her hand and fell. It fell on the floor and broke. The water and the flowers spilled everywhere.

Lily was scared. She heard a loud noise. It was her mom. Her mom was fat and angry. She came into the living room and saw the mess. She saw the broken vase and the wet floor. She saw Lily and her doll and the tea party. She was very mad.

"Lily, what did you do?" she yelled. "You broke the vase! The vase was very old and very pretty. It was a gift from grandma. How could you be so naughty?"

Lily felt bad. She did not mean to break the vase. She only wanted to smell the flowers. She started to cry. She hugged her doll and said sorry.

"I'm sorry, mom. I'm sorry, grandma. I didn't mean to break the vase. I love you. Please don't be mad at me."

Mom sighed. She was still mad, but she also loved Lily. She picked up Lily and hugged her back.

"I love you too, Lily. But you have to be careful. You can't touch things that are not yours. You can't climb on chairs and tables. You can hurt yourself or break things. Do you understand?"

Lily nodded. She understood. She said sorry again. Mom kissed her and said it was okay. She helped Lily clean up the mess. They threw away the broken vase and the flowers. They dried the floor and the table. They put away the cups and plates. They moved the chair away from the window.

Mom said Lily could still play with her doll, but not in the living room. She said Lily could play in her bedroom or in the garden. Lily agreed. She took her doll and went to her bedroom. She was glad mom was not mad anymore. She hoped grandma would not be mad either. She decided to make a card for grandma and say sorry for the vase. She hoped grandma would like it. She hoped grandma would still love her. She was sure grandma would still love her. Grandma always loved her.

Lily and Ben were friends who liked to play together. One day, they found a big bag of popcorn in the kitchen. They wanted to eat some, but they also had an idea.

"Let's attach the popcorn to a string and make a necklace!" Lily said.

"That sounds fun!" Ben agreed.

They took some popcorn and a string and started to attach them. They made a long and colorful necklace that looked very pretty. They were very happy with their work.

But then, they heard a loud noise. It was their dog, Spot, who smelled the popcorn and came running. He saw the necklace and thought it was a toy. He jumped and grabbed it with his mouth. He pulled and pulled, and the popcorn started to pop off the string.

"Hey, stop that!" Lily shouted.

"Give it back!" Ben cried.

But Spot did not listen. He ran away with the necklace, leaving a trail of popcorn behind him. Lily and Ben chased him, but they could not catch him. They were very sad and angry.

They learned a lesson that day. They learned that popcorn is not a good thing to attach to a string. They learned that popcorn is better to eat than to play with. They learned that they should not leave their things where their dog can reach them. They learned that normal things can sometimes cause trouble.

Once upon a time, there were two twins. One twin was called Joe and one was called Jack. Joe was always working hard and always wanted to be successful. He wanted to make his dad proud. Jack was not as hardworking.

One day, Joe's dad said, "Joe, you are working so hard, soon you will be successful. I'm so proud of you." Joe smiled and kept on working.

A few days later, Joe was feeling so proud when he saw his dad smile. Jack had been lazy and was not successful like Joe. Joe cheered, because he was happy to be the successful twin.

Joe worked harder and harder, which meant he became more and more successful. Jack started working hard too and soon Jack was a successful twin too. They were both proud of each other and their dad was very happy.

The end.

Once there was a young little girl named Molly. She was only 3 years old and she loved to explore. One day she went outside to play and saw a tug boat in the harbor. She was very excited and wanted to explore it. She ran to the boat and started to pull the rope that was attached to it. Molly had so much fun and was soon able to move the boat around the harbor. She kept pulling the rope until she was all tired out. Molly was having so much fun that she barely noticed when her aunt called her back home for dinner. Molly was so excited about her adventure that she couldn't stop talking about it all night. Even though Molly was only 3 years old, she had pulled a boat all by herself and was very proud of her accomplishment.

Ben and Mia went to the big house with Mom and Dad. The big house had many people and many noises. Ben and Mia did not like the noises. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad said they had to stay and be nice. They said they had to watch a show. The show was on a big stage. The stage had lights and curtains and people in costumes. Ben and Mia did not know what the show was about.

The show started. The people on the stage sang and danced and talked. Ben and Mia did not understand the words. They did not like the songs. They did not like the dances. They were scared of the costumes. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad said they had to watch and clap. They said the show was fun and good. Ben and Mia did not think so. They wanted to go home.

The show ended. The people on the stage bowed. They said thank you and goodbye. The people in the seats clapped. They said bravo and hooray. Ben and Mia did not clap. They did not say anything. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad said they had to go and say hello. They said they had to be polite and friendly. Ben and Mia did not want to. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad took them to the stage. They met the people in costumes. The people smiled and hugged them. They said they were happy and proud. Ben and Mia did not smile. They did not hug. They were scared and shy. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad said they had to bow. They said it was a nice thing to do. Ben and Mia did not want to. They wanted to go home.

But they did it. They bowed. They said thank you and goodbye. They did not mean it. They wanted to go home.

Mom and Dad said they were good and brave. They said they were proud and happy. Ben and Mia did not care. They wanted to go home.

They went home. They took off their clothes. They put on their pajamas. They went to bed. They were glad and relieved. They were home.

One day Tim and his mum were walking in the park. Tim was excited because it was so cool outside.

"Look mummy, a tap!" exclaimed Tim, pointing at a shiny silver tap.

"Yes Tim," said his mum. "Let's watch the water come out of the tap!"

Tim was fascinated. He watched as clear, cool water came gushing out.

"Do you want to touch it Tim?" asked mummy.

Tim nodded and, very carefully, he touched the tap with his finger.

"It's cold mummy!" said Tim excitedly.

Mummy laughed. "Yes, it is cold! Let's watch the tap for a little longer, ok?"

They watched for ages, until the sun started to go down.

"Time to go home Tim," sighed mummy.

But the happy day in the park had made Tim smile from ear to ear. "This is so cool!" he said as they walked home.

Anna liked to play dress-up with her mom's clothes and jewelry. She would put on a big hat, a long dress, and a shiny necklace. Then she would look at herself in the mirror and pretend to be a princess.

One day, she saw a new ring on her mom's dresser. It was very pretty and sparkly. Anna wanted to try it on. She took the ring and put it on her finger. It was too big, but she didn't care. She felt very fancy.

She went to the mirror and admired the ring. She made funny faces and waved her hand. She was having so much fun that she didn't hear her mom coming into the room.

"Anna, what are you doing?" her mom asked, sounding angry. "That's my new ring. It's very expensive. You can't play with it. Give it back to me right now."

Anna was scared. She didn't want to make her mom mad. She tried to take off the ring, but it was stuck. She pulled and twisted, but it wouldn't come off. She started to cry.

Her mom saw that she was upset and sorry. She calmed down and hugged her. She said, "It's okay, sweetie. I know you didn't mean to be naughty. But you have to be careful with things that don't belong to you. Some things are very special and can break or get lost. Let's go to the kitchen and get some butter. Maybe that will help the ring slide off."

Anna nodded and followed her mom to the kitchen. She hoped the ring would come off soon. She learned her lesson. She decided to play dress-up with her own toys from now on.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play together. Today, they want to make a cake with Mom. Mom says they can help her stir the batter.

"Can I stir first?" Anna asks.

"No, I want to stir first!" Ben says.

They both grab the spoon and pull. The batter spills on the table and on their clothes.

"Oh no!" Mom says. "Look at the mess you made!"

Anna and Ben feel sorry. They say sorry to Mom. Mom is not angry. She says they can help her clean up.

After they clean up, Mom says they can watch a video. She puts a white disc in the machine. The video is about animals. Anna and Ben like animals. They sit on the couch and watch the video. They see lions, elephants, monkeys, and more. They learn their names and sounds.

Anna and Ben are happy. They hug Mom and thank her for the cake and the video. Mom hugs them back and says she loves them. They eat the cake and enjoy the video. They have a good day.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to listen to his mom as she read him stories every night. Tim liked to learn new words, and he was always excited to hear the fun language in the stories.

One day, Tim met a naughty cat named Max. Max liked to play tricks on Tim, like hiding his toys and making a mess in his room. Tim didn't like Max's naughty games, but he still wanted to be friends with the cat.

Tim decided to teach Max how to be nice. He would read stories to Max and show him the fun language he had learned. Max started to listen to Tim and soon, he stopped playing naughty tricks. Tim and Max became the best of friends, and they loved to listen to stories together.

Jack was very tired. He had played all day with his friends and wanted to go to sleep. He was in the living room in front of the fan, so it would be cool when he went to bed. He sat down and looked at the fan, his eyes drooping. Suddenly, he let out a big yawn.

The fan continued to spin, and Jack watched it. He was very curious, as he had never seen a fan before. He wondered how fast it moved and if it liked being in the room.

As he watched, Jack's brother came in the room. He had a big smile on his face. His brother was very competitive and he loved to win. He said that he wanted to have a race with the fan.

Jack thought this was a great idea! He was so excited that he almost forgot to yawn again. He watched as they both prepared to race. When they were ready, his brother said, "Go!" and they chased each other around the living room.

The fan was very fast, but Jack's brother was able to beat it. When he was finished, Jack was very proud of him. They both laughed and agreed to race again tomorrow. Jack yawned one last time before finally going to sleep.

One day, he hopped in the hedge and became very sleepy. He closed his eyes and went to sleep. He slept for hours and hours. When he woke up, he felt so refreshed.

The little bunny was so happy and energetic after his nap in the hedge. He hopped and jumped around the garden again. It was the best sleep he ever had!

"Look, Lily, ice cream!" Sam says. He points to the truck. "Can we have some, please?"

Lily nods. She likes ice cream too. She points to the truck and says, "Me too, me too!"

Their moms smile. They take them to the truck. They give them some money. The man in the truck gives them ice cream cones. Lily and Sam say, "Thank you!"

They lick their ice cream. It is cold and sweet. They hear the music. It is loud and happy. They dance and laugh.

But the sun is hot. It makes their ice cream melt. It drips on their hands and faces. They feel sticky and messy.

"Oh no, Lily, our ice cream!" Sam says. He points to his cone. "It is all gone!"

Lily looks at her cone. It is empty too. She feels sad. She says, "Me too, Sam, me too!"

Their moms see them. They take them to a fountain. They wash their hands and faces. They dry them with a towel. They hug them and say, "It's okay, Lily and Sam. You can have more ice cream another day."

Lily and Sam smile. They feel better. They say, "Okay, mom. We love you!"

They hold hands. They go back to the park. They play and have fun. They are happy.

Once upon a time there was a boy. The boy liked to sort colorful things. One day, the boy went outside and saw a tree. It had a lot of prunes on it! The boy was so excited. He wanted to sort the prunes. So the boy started picking them off the tree.

He sorted them into piles. He put all the yellow ones together, all the green ones together and all the red ones together. He gathered them all in a basket. He smiled. This was even better than sorting colorful blocks.

When the boy got home, he put the prunes in the kitchen. He was so proud of all the colorful prunes he had found. He decided he would make a yummy prune pie with all the prunes.

The boy and his family all ate the delicious, colorful prune pie, and everyone loved it. It was the best pie ever!

The boy was so glad he had sorted all the colorful prunes. He was glad he had found so many and put them together to make something special.

Robbie was a little boy and he loved his toy train. He always wanted to take it out and play with it.
One day, Robbie was taking his train outside. As he stepped out of the door, he saw a big ground. He was so excited! He thought it would be the perfect place to play with his train.
But as he started running towards the ground, he heard someone shout, "Hey, you can't play here!"
Robbie stopped and turned around. He saw a big, angry man. The man said, "This ground is for the grown-ups." Robbie was disappointed, so he started to cry.
But then the man said, "Don't worry. I'll tell you what: if you promise to be careful, I'll let you play here."
Robbie's eyes lit up. He smiled and said, "Yes!"
The man smiled back and said, "Okay, but remember to be careful. All right?"
Robbie nodded and received the big ground to play with his train. He said thank you and started running towards his train.
The man watched as Robbie played and smiled to himself.

He found a small spider in the middle of the cobweb. It was moving very slowly and carefully. Jim said 'hello' to the spider. The spider looked up and smiled. Jim knew it was the original maker of the cobweb. Jim thanked the spider for the beautiful cobweb, and then he continued his walk.

Jim was so happy to have found the original maker of the cobweb. He continued to notice things around the park. He noticed some colourful birds on a tree and some red flowers on the ground. He noticed the sound of the breeze blowing around him. It was a wonderful day in the park for Jim.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Lucy and Paul. They liked to play together and went everywhere together.

One day they decided to go to the park together. Lucy saw a huge train. She wanted to get on it, but Paul said no.

Lucy kept on asking, until Paul gave in and said yes. But soon after they got on the train, they realized it was too big and they were not allowed to stay.

An officer came and ordered them to get off. Even though the kids were sad, they had to obey the order. They realized that if they had obeyed the rule, they would have been safe.

They learned their lesson and never disobeyed rules again. They agreed that it's best to listen to others when they give orders.

Anna and Ben were going to the theater with their mom and dad. They were very excited to see the show with the animals and the songs. They put on their coats and hats and got in the car.

"Are we there yet?" Anna asked.

"Soon, honey. We have to hurry, or we will miss the start of the show," mom said.

Ben looked out the window and saw the big sign with the word theater. He saw many people walking and talking. He saw some lights and some balloons. He felt happy and curious.

They parked the car and got out. They followed mom and dad to the big door of the theater. They showed their tickets to a lady who smiled and said, "Enjoy the show!"

They walked inside and found their seats. They saw a big stage with a curtain. They heard some music and some noise.

"Look, Anna, there are the animals!" Ben said, pointing to the stage.

Anna looked and saw a lion, a monkey, a bear, and a bird. They were wearing costumes and hats. They were making funny sounds and movements.

Anna felt fearful. She did not like the lion. It looked big and scary. It roared and showed its teeth.

"Mom, I'm scared of the lion. Can we go home?" Anna said, holding mom's hand.

Mom hugged Anna and said, "It's okay, sweetie. The lion is not real. It's just a person wearing a lion suit. He is pretending to be a lion for the show. He won't hurt you or anyone. He is here to make us laugh and have fun."

Anna looked at mom and then at the lion. She saw that the lion had a zipper on its back and a tag on its ear. She saw that the lion was waving and winking at the people. She saw that the people were clapping and cheering.

Anna felt less fearful. She smiled and said, "Oh, I see. The lion is not real. He is just a person. He is funny and silly."

Mom nodded and said, "That's right. He is funny and silly. Do you want to watch the show with me and dad and Ben?"

Anna nodded and said, "Yes, please. I want to watch the show. Maybe the lion will do something funny."

Mom kissed Anna and said, "I'm glad you want to watch the show. I love you, Anna."

"I love you, mom," Anna said.

They watched the show together. They saw the animals sing and dance and tell jokes. They laughed and clapped and had a good time. Anna was not fearful of the lion anymore. She was happy and proud. She liked the theater.

He admired it for a moment and then decided to take it home. He tucked it safely under his arm and raced back to his den.

When he got there, he carefully placed the crystal in the centre of his den. He was so excited to have his very own crystal.

The fox was curious. He asked, "How do I keep it safe?

The owl said, "Be sure to never let it rot. Always keep it away from the damp and darkness, then you will be able to go on the most incredible journeys. The fox nodded and thanked the wise owl.

He set off on his first adventure that very day, with his crystal in tow. He discovered so many wonderful things and made lots of friends along the way.

The fox had the most amazing time with his crystal and was so grateful for his new and adventurous life.

When Lily came back inside, she saw the mess and started to cry. Her mom tried to comfort her, but Lily was so upset that she didn't want to listen. She ran to her room and didn't come out for a long time.

The bad ending was that Lily didn't get to eat the cake she helped make, and she was sad for the rest of the day. But her mom promised they would make another cake together soon, and Lily felt a little bit better.

Lila and Tom were playing in the woods. They liked to make huts with sticks and leaves. They found a big stick that looked like a sword. Tom said, "I am the king of the woods. You are my queen, Lila." Lila smiled and said, "Yes, you are the king, Tom. And I am your queen."

They heard a loud howl. They saw a big black wolf. The wolf had sharp teeth and red eyes. The wolf was hungry and angry. He wanted to eat Lila and Tom. He ran towards them, growling and snarling.

Tom and Lila were scared. They ran to their hut. They hoped the wolf would not find them. But the wolf smelled them. He followed them to their hut. He scratched and bit the sticks and leaves. He made a hole in the hut.

Tom and Lila saw the wolf's head. They screamed. They looked for something to fight the wolf. They saw the big stick that looked like a sword. Tom grabbed it and said, "Lila, help me. We have to burn the wolf."

Lila saw a small fire. Someone had left it in the woods. Lila ran to the fire. She picked up a stick that was on fire. She brought it to Tom. Tom touched the big stick with the fire stick. The big stick caught fire. It looked like a flaming sword.

Tom swung the big stick at the wolf. The wolf was afraid of the fire. He yelped and backed away. He ran into the woods. He did not want to eat Lila and Tom anymore.

Lila and Tom hugged each other. They were happy and brave. They said, "We burned the wolf. We are the king and queen of the woods." They laughed and played with their flaming sword. They were safe and free.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Alice. One day, she was at home when she heard a loud noise coming from the kitchen. She was brave, so she decided to go see what was happening.

When she got to the kitchen, she found her brother sitting on the floor, surrounded by broken dishes. The pieces of glass were all around him and he was crying. Alice wanted to know what had happened, so she asked him, "What happened?"

He answered, "I was playing with the dishes, but it was dangerous. I was trying to make a tower but I didn't succeed. Now, look at all the mess that I made!"

Alice could see that her brother was very sad, so she comforted him and said, "It's okay. You shouldn't play with dangerous things. Next time, don't mess with the kitchen. Let's clean up this mess together, alright?"

Her brother nodded and they cleaned up the mess together.

The moral of the story is that it is dangerous to play with dangerous things, and it's important to listen to your older siblings, who will often be able to advise you on how to behave safely.

Once upon a time in a small forest there lived a little rabbit. Each day the rabbit went for a walk, hopping around the forest and looking at all the animals and plants. One day, the rabbit noticed a strange mushroom growing on a tree. It was a tall mushroom with a long stem and a rounded top.

The rabbit was a bit troubled. He had never seen such a mushroom before. He wondered what kind of mushroom it was, why it was growing on a tree instead of the ground, and why it was so tall.

The rabbit decided to look around the mushroom and see if he could find some clues. He noticed some small beetles crawling around the base of the mushroom, and he realized that the mushroom must be providing food for the beetles. He saw some of the beetles climb up the stem of the mushroom and disappear inside the top.

The rabbit was a bit more troubled now. He had never seen anything like this before! Then suddenly, the top of the mushroom opened and out came a beautiful white butterfly. The rabbit smiled as he watched the butterfly fly from the tree and flutter away into the sky.

The rabbit noticed that the mushroom had been providing a home for the little beetles and a place for the beautiful white butterfly to hatch from its cocoon. He was so happy that he had taken the time to notice the mushroom and learn more about it.
