Anna was an adventurous girl who liked to wander around the house and find new things. One day, she saw a shiny screw on the floor. She picked it up and wondered what it was for. She decided to look for more screws and see if she could make something with them.

She wandered into the kitchen, where her mom was cooking. She saw a drawer with a lot of screws and tools. She opened the drawer and took out some screws and a screwdriver. She thought they looked like fun toys.

"Anna, what are you doing?" her mom asked, when she saw her with the screws and the screwdriver.

"I'm playing with screws, mom. They are shiny and fun," Anna said.

"Anna, those are not toys. They are tools. They are for fixing things, not playing. You could hurt yourself or break something with them. Put them back in the drawer, please," her mom said.

"But mom, I want to make something with them. I'm adventurous," Anna said.

"Being adventurous is good, but you also need to be careful and listen to your mom. Screws are not for making things, they are for holding things together. If you take them out, things might fall apart. Like this chair, for example. It has screws in it. If you unscrew them, the chair will break. Do you want that?" her mom said.

Anna looked at the chair and shook her head. She did not want to break the chair. She realized that screws were important and not toys. She said sorry to her mom and put the screws and the screwdriver back in the drawer.

"Good girl, Anna. I'm glad you understand. You can be adventurous, but you also need to be respectful and responsible. There are many other things you can play with and make things with, like blocks, crayons, or clay. Why don't you go to your room and find something else to do?" her mom said.

Anna nodded and hugged her mom. She said thank you and went to her room. She found her blocks and crayons and started to make a castle. She was still adventurous, but she also learned to be careful and listen to her mom. She was happy and proud of her castle.

Anna and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run, jump and slide on the grass and the swings. They were very happy and laughed a lot.

"Look, Anna, there is a pond with ducks!" Ben said, pointing to the water. "Let's go and see them!"

"Okay, Ben, let's go!" Anna said, following him. They ran to the pond and saw the ducks swimming and quacking. They were brown, white and black, and had shiny feathers.

"Hello, ducks!" Anna said, waving to them. "Do you want some bread?"

The ducks heard Anna and came closer to the edge of the pond. They looked at Anna and Ben with their small eyes and nodded their heads.

"Yes, please, we are hungry!" one of the ducks said, speaking to Anna and Ben. "Thank you for being thoughtful and kind!"

Anna and Ben were very surprised and happy. They had never heard a duck speak before. They smiled and broke some bread into pieces. They threw the bread into the water and watched the ducks eat it.

"Thank you, Anna and Ben!" the ducks said, after they finished the bread. "You are very nice and friendly!"

"You are welcome, ducks!" Anna and Ben said, feeling glad. "We like you too!"

They stayed with the ducks for a while, talking and playing. They learned the ducks' names and stories. They had a lot of fun and made new friends. They were very happy and laughed a lot.

One day, Tom and his family got a new puppy. Tom was so excited to take his new puppy for a walk. He went to get his new small leash to take the puppy outside. Tom's Mom said, "Wait! You can't take the puppy out without asking me. Tom asked, "Please Mom, can I take the puppy outside? But his Mom replied, "No, I don't permit it.

Tom was so sad. He wanted to take his puppy out so badly! He thought of a plan. He grabbed the small leash and tried to take his puppy outside when his Mom was not looking. But his Mom noticed him and said, "Tom! You are not allowed to take the puppy outside without my permission.

Tom kept trying to convince his Mom, but she stayed firm and said, "No, Tom. I will not permit it. Tom had no choice but to leave the puppy inside. He sadly walked away, leaving the small leash and the puppy inside.

Once upon a time there was a shop. It was a modern shop.

The shop had apples, oranges and bananas. It had lots of things to eat.

Every day the shop grew bigger and bigger. At first it was small, but it soon grew very big.

The shop kept getting bigger and bigger until one day it was so big it almost touched the sky. People came from far and wide to buy things at the shop.

Everyone was happy that the shop grew so big. They hoped it would stay big forever.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a clever girl named Sarah. She was always looking for ways to learn and explore. One day, Sarah wanted to take a test. She had heard that the test could show her how clever she was.

When the test started, Sarah realized that the questions were hard and she knew she had to be very careful. She worked hard and did her best. When the test was over Sarah was excited to see the results.

She went back to the place where she had taken the test. When the results were out, Sarah realized that she had done really well. She was so happy to find out that she was very clever and got an excellent score.

Sarah was very proud of herself for doing so well on the clever test. She knew that she had worked hard and that it had paid off. From then on, she always looked for new tests to take so she could challenge herself.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and dolls. One day, Lily's mom asked her to clean up her toys before dinner. Lily promised to do it, but she was playing slow.

Her mom reminded her, "Lily, remember your promise. Please clean up your toys before dinner."

Lily replied, "Okay, Mommy. I will do it now." She started to clean up, but she got distracted by her favorite doll. Her mom repeated, "Lily, please clean up your toys now."

Lily finally finished cleaning up her toys and went to dinner with her family. Her mom was proud of her for keeping her promise. From that day on, Lily learned to keep her promises and not to play slow when it was time to clean up.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Sarah. She liked to jog outside in the sunshine. Sarah enjoyed jogging slowly and gently.

One day, it was cloudy and looked like it might rain. Sarah didn't want to get wet, so she decided to stay inside. Suddenly, there was a big flash of lightning. It was so bright that it lit up the sky!

Sarah was scared, but then she heard a gentle sound coming from outside. She looked out of the window and saw something miraculous.

It was a giant cloud that was jogging around the sky! It was very gentle and seemed to be jogging at a slow and peaceful pace. Sarah smiled and waved at the cloud as it jogged away.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park with his uncle. They played with a ball and had lots of fun. Tim's uncle was not very good at playing, but Tim loved him anyway.

At the park, Tim saw his friend, Sam, and called him to play. Sam looked at Tim's uncle and said, "Your uncle is terrible at playing ball!" Tim felt embarrassed, but he still wanted to play with his uncle.

Tim said to Sam, "It's okay if my uncle is not good. We still have fun together." Sam understood and started playing with them. In the end, they all had a great time at the park.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Jack and Jill. They were always playing together in the park. But one day something terrible happened.

A flood came and it was so huge that all the water from the river got into the park. Jack and Jill were very scared.

"We have to use something! said Jack.

"What are we going to use? asked Jill.

"We can use a boat! We just have to find one, said Jack.

So Jack and Jill started looking for a boat. Sure enough, they found one and used it to keep themselves safe from the terrible flood. They made it out of the park safely and promised to never forget the lesson they had learned.

Once upon a time there was a very stubborn little girl. Every day she would have lots of fun playing with her toys and her friends. But this day the little girl had something extra special in her sack. She hugged it tightly as she walked around her garden, looking around for a special place to keep it.

"I want to keep my mystery package safe and sound," she said.

Just then, her mum came around the corner. "What do you have in there?" she asked.

The little girl held on tightly to the sack and said, "It's a surprise, but I want to keep it here because it's special".

Mum smiled and said, "Well if you're sure, I suppose it'll be OK to keep it here".

The little girl's face lit up. She found the perfect spot and put her sack down. "I won't let anyone take it," she said very firmly.

Mum laughed. "You can be very stubborn when you want to be, can't you?"

The little girl smiled proudly and said, "Yes I can. I'm going to keep my mystery package safe and sound".

And they all lived happily ever after.

Mama and Papa looked out of the window. "Oh no," they said. They could see a mighty storm coming.

Mama and Papa had wondered what to do. Suddenly, Little Jane came running down the stairs. She had a great idea.

Mama and Papa listened carefully. "Let's play a game!" said Little Jane. She jumped and clapped.

Mama and Papa lit some candles. They gathered around in a circle and held hands. Little Jane showed them how to touch their fingers together.

Then, Mama and Papa taught Little Jane a song about the night. The song was about a mighty wind and a soft moonlight. Little Jane loved it.

Mama, Papa, and Little Jane sang the song together until night had arrived and the storm had passed.

Afterwards, they all hugged tight. Mama whispered in Little Jane's ear: "Good job. You were so brave." Little Jane smiled and snuggled in Mama's arms.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to explore the forest behind her house. One day, while she was walking, she discovered a beautiful bow lying on the ground. She was so happy and picked it up.

Suddenly, she heard a voice coming from behind a tree. It was a little bunny who was upset because he lost his bow. Lily showed him the bow she found and the bunny was very happy.

"Thank you so much! You're my hero!" said the bunny. Lily smiled and they became friends. From that day on, they explored the forest together and had many more adventures.

The dog was very ignorant and just sat around. They took videos of it and laughed. After a while, the dog got up and started following Tom and Toby. Tom said, "What is this dog doing? Toby replied, "He looks like he wants to be our friend!

Tom and Toby then took the dog to the park and played with it. They laughed, chased it, and recorded all the funny moments. At the end of the day, they all went home happy. From that day on, Tom, Toby, and the dog were best friends.

Once upon a time, there was a furry bear who set off on an adventure. He hiked and hiked, until he came to an old, wrecked boat near a lake. He climbed inside and lay down, admiring the beauty of the lake.

Just then, he heard a loud noise and saw a giant monster come out of the lake. The furry bear knew he was in trouble and he quickly set a plan to escape. He worked quickly and he used the pieces of the wrecked boat to make a bridge over the lake.

The monster was chasing him and the furry bear had to hurry. But he was brave, and he made it across the bridge and to safety.

The furry bear was exhausted, but he smiled because he had set a plan and he'd made it out of danger!

The End

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. He loved to play with his toys and share them with his friends. One day, Timmy's friend asked to trade toys with him. Timmy thought about it for a moment and decided to trade.

After the trade, Timmy realized that he didn't like the new toy as much as he thought he would. He felt sad and wished he could undo the trade. But his friend was already playing with Timmy's old toy and was very proud of it.

Timmy learned that sometimes trades can happen, but it's important to think carefully about what you're giving away. He also learned that it's not nice to take back something you already traded, because it can make someone else unhappy. From that day on, Timmy was more thoughtful about trading and always made sure he was happy with the trade before it happened.

Once there was a little girl who had to do a lot of work. Every day was very long and the work kept coming. She worked hard and tried her best but it seemed like it was never enough.

One day she was so tired and could not keep going. She tried to stay awake but she fell asleep. She slept for a long time and when she woke up she found that her work was not done.

The little girl was very sad as she knew she had to stay up late to finish it. She worked long and hard to get it done. Finally, when it was done she felt relieved.

But then, she looked around and noticed that her room was still very dirty. She had forgotten to clean it when she was doing her work. And the mess was too big to clean in one night. She cried and cried and her Mom had to comfort her.

The little girl learned an important lesson that night. No matter how much work there is, she needs to leave time to clean so she can be happy.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily was playing outside. She saw a red apple on a tree and wanted to eat it. She tried to reach the apple with her hand, but it was too high up.

Lily's mom came outside and saw her trying to get the apple. "What are you doing, Lily?" she asked.

"I want the red apple, but I can't reach it," Lily replied.

"Don't worry, I'll help you," her mom said. She picked Lily up and lifted her high enough to reach the apple. Lily picked the apple with her finger and took a big bite. It was delicious!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside with her friends. One day, they decided to build a sandcastle at the beach.

Lily used her little shovel to make a big pile of sand. Her friends helped her pat it down to make it strong. They built tall towers and a big wall around their castle.

As they were playing, a fat dove flew down and landed on top of their castle. It looked at them with its shiny black eyes. Lily and her friends giggled and watched the dove as it sat there for a while.

When it was time to go home, Lily and her friends were sad to leave their sandcastle behind. But they knew they could come back and build another one soon. And maybe the fat dove would visit them again. The end.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Sally was searching for something. "What could it be?" she asked herself out loud.

Sally looked high and low throughout her home but she couldn't find it. Suddenly, she saw her favorite armchair in the corner. She ran over and decided to search there.

Sally searched everywhere on the armchair. She was about to give up, when she finally felt something yummy! She felt a smile on her face and opened her hands, and there it was. A tasty, sweet treat!

"Yay!" she yelled with excitement. Sally quickly ate the treat and thanked her armchair for the surprise.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Valentine. Valentine was feeling scared because she was all alone in the dark forest. She stepped cautiously, not sure which way to go. Suddenly, she saw a pick lying on the ground. She bent down and picked it up. Valentine felt a bit better now she had something to help her.

Valentine started to step slowly through the forest. All of a sudden she heard a noise. It sounded like thunder. Valentine felt scared again and she started to run. Behind her, she heard a strange noise. When Valentine looked back, she saw a big, scary monster!

Valentine didn't know what to do. She knew she had to get away from the monster, but she was too scared to run. Then she remembered that she had the pick in her hand. She stepped forward, holding the pick out in front of her. The monster looked scared and ran away.

Valentine was so happy she had been brave and that the pick had helped her. She stepped onwards and onwards until she reached the end of the forest. And she never looked back.

Sam and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a big stand with many toys. The stand is filthy. It has dirt, dust and spider webs.

"Look, Ben! Toys!" Sam says.

"Let's go and see!" Ben says.

They run to the stand. They see cars, dolls, balls and books. They want to play with them.

"Hello, boys," a voice says. "Do you like my toys?"

They turn around. They see an old man. He is the owner of the stand. He is also filthy. He has a long beard, a dirty hat and a torn coat.

"Who are you?" Sam asks.

"I am Tom. I collect toys. I invite you to play with them. But you have to be careful. Some of them are broken or sharp," Tom says.

"Thank you, Tom. Can we play with the cars?" Ben asks.

"Sure, you can. But only for a little while. Then you have to go home. It is getting dark," Tom says.

Sam and Ben nod. They pick up some cars. They make noises and pretend to drive. They have fun.

Tom smiles. He is happy to have visitors. He likes to share his toys. He watches them play. He remembers when he was a boy. He had many toys. He had many friends. He was not filthy. He was happy.

There was a mild day with the sun shining brightly. Two friends were playing a game of tag. One of them was named Jack and the other was named Jim. Jack had a plan to catch Jim but Jim was too fast. He ran out of sight and when Jack looked for him he couldn't find him.

Jack started to worry. He looked all around and couldn't find Jim. Jack started to lose steam and sat down to think. He thought and thought but he was still worried. Where was Jim? Then finally, Jack saw a puff of steam coming from behind a bush. Jack quickly ran over and found Jim, who had been hiding from him.

Jack was so relieved and gave Jim a big hug. Jim was laughing and so was Jack. Together, they enjoyed the mild day basking in the sun.

Dane was a three year old boy who loved to eat pears. His mom always bought a variety. Some were green, some were red and some were yellow.

One day, Dane wanted a sweet pear but his mom said no. "If you eat too many sweet pears, it will prevent you from going to sleep tonight," his mom said.

So Dane said, "Ok, I will just have one small sweet pear."

His mom smiled, and agreed.

Dane ate the sweet pear, and it tasted delicious. He was so happy, he wanted to have another sweet pear.

But his mom said no. "You had enough pears for now," she said with a smile. "You don't want to prevent yourself from going to sleep tonight!"

So Dane reluctantly agreed, and went off to play.

Inside the chamber, Max heard some rustling. He wondered what was making the noise. Suddenly, out of the shadows came a furry creature! Max jumped back and asked who the creature was.

"I am Lulu and I live here," said the furry creature.

Max was so happy to meet Lulu! The two friends chatted for a while and eventually Max had to go home. Max said goodbye and vowed to come back soon to visit.

She was so happy she started dancing and spinning. Everywhere she went, the pink rub would follow her. It was like her little friend. Everywhere she went, she felt like she had her own companion. Everywhere she went she tasted the sweet pink rub. Then she noticed that the rub was starting to disappear. She was eating it up! She looked around, but the rub was all gone. But it had given her a lot of fun. Even though the rub was gone, the memories of it were still in her heart.

Once there was a rich man who lived in a very big house. He had many friends who came to visit him often and they would always sit around his big network of tables and chairs.

One day he had a special visitor, a little 3 year old girl. She was amazed at how nice his house was.

"Wow, what a beautiful house!" she said.

The rich man smiled. "Thank you!" he replied.

The girl looked around and noticed something moving under the tables. She pointed to it and asked "What is that?"

The rich man said "That is a big puppy, he loves to lie down under the tables".

The girl was so excited and ran to pet the puppy.

The rich man looked at her with a big smile and said "I am so glad you like my big network of friends and family here in the house".

The little girl was so happy and gave the rich man a big hug. From that day on, the little girl and the rich man were the best of friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved ponies and always wanted one to call her own. One day, her mom took her to a farm where she saw a dark brown pony. She fell in love with it and said, "Mommy, I need that pony!"

Her mom said, "We can't just take the pony. We need to ask the farmer first."

So, Lily and her mom went to the farmer and asked if they could have the pony. The farmer said they could have it if they took good care of it.

Lily was so happy to have her own pony. She brushed its hair and fed it carrots every day. One night, when it was dark outside, Lily went to the pony and said, "Don't be scared, pony. I am here for you." The pony nuzzled her hand and they fell asleep together. From that day on, the pony was Lily's best friend.

Once upon a time, there was a big table in a room. On the table, there was a pretty picture. The picture was not on the wall, but Mom wanted to hang it up. She asked Dad, "Can you hang the pretty picture on the wall?"

Dad said, "Yes, I can hang it up. But first, I need to find a nail." He looked and looked, but he could not find a nail. He saw a big box near the table. The box was heavy. Dad thought, "Maybe there is a nail inside the box."

Dad tried to move the big box, but it was too heavy. He pushed and pushed, but the box did not move. Suddenly, the box fell on the big table. The pretty picture fell off the table and broke. Dad was sad, and Mom was sad too. They could not hang the pretty picture on the wall anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a mommy and her daughter. The daughter loved the color purple. She had a purple dress, purple shoes, and a purple hat. One day, they went to the park and played on the swings. The daughter accidentally ripped her purple dress. She was sad and cried.

The mommy asked, "What's wrong, my dear?" The daughter said, "My purple dress is broken." The mommy said, "Don't worry, we can restore it." They went home and the mommy sewed the dress back together. The daughter was happy again because her purple dress was fixed. They hugged and the daughter said, "Thank you, mommy."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She loved to play with her best friend, Tom. One day, they found a pile of ash near their playhouse. Amy was upset because the ash was dirty and made their playhouse look bad.

Tom said, "Let's clean it up!" But Amy insisted, "No, I don't want to touch it." Tom tried to make Amy feel better by saying, "It's okay, we can use a broom." But Amy still didn't want to help.

Tom started to clean up the ash by himself. He was not careful and the ash went into his eyes. Tom cried and his eyes hurt. Amy felt bad that she did not help Tom. They both learned that they should always work together and be careful when cleaning up messes.

Once upon a time there was a black driver. He had a very important job. He would drive his car around and keep people safe when they needed help. Every day he would get into his car and start his journey around the town. He would drive from place to place making sure all the people were ok.

One day, the driver was going for a special drive. There were very important people in the back of the car. He was careful to keep them safe as he weaved his way through the busy streets. He managed to get them to where they needed to go without any trouble.

At the end of the day, the driver looked very tired, but he had done a great job. He had kept the important people safe and had stayed alert during his drive. The people were very thankful and gave the driver a big hug when they thanked him before they left.

The driver smiled and waved goodbye as he drove off. He was already thinking about his next journey, but this time he was convinced that it would be even better.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy. He had a spear. He liked to pretend the spear was a magical wand. But one day the boy stumbled upon a soft creature. It was a furry friend. The little boy smiled. He wanted to pet the creature. He moved closer and made a soft noise to greet it. But the creature started to run away!

The little boy was sad. He followed the creature. He paused a few times, but the creature kept running. The little boy got tired. He stopped to catch his breath, but when he looked up, the creature had disappeared.

The little boy was sad. He wished he had been more careful. He wished he had paused and not followed the creature. He knew he wouldn't see it again.

The little boy went home sadly, waving his spear in the air. He would never see his soft friend again.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird. He had two wings and he liked to fly in the sky. One day, he was scared because he saw a big animal. He wanted to escape! The little bird flapped his wings and flew away so fast that the animal couldn't follow him. He quickly reached a tree and he hid there, feeling very safe. He was glad that he had escaped from the scary animal. That was his best escape ever!

Once upon a time, there was a mommy and a daughter. The mommy loved her daughter so much. But sometimes the daughter was bossy and told the mommy what to do.

One day, the mommy said, "I don't like it when you are bossy, it makes me sad."

The daughter said, "I'm sorry mommy, I don't mean to be bossy. I just want to play with you."

The mommy smiled and said, "I love playing with you too, but we need to take turns and be kind to each other."

From that day on, the daughter was not bossy anymore and they played together happily ever after. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved going out to eat with her family at the restaurant. One day, her parents took her to a very noisy restaurant. Lily didn't like the loud sounds, but she loved the yummy food.

While they were waiting for their food, Lily saw a boy at the next table. He had a toy that she wanted to play with. She asked him nicely, but he teased her and said no. This made Lily feel sad.

Finally, their food arrived and Lily forgot all about the boy. But when they were leaving, the boy's toy fell from his pocket. Lily picked it up and gave it back to him. The boy was surprised and said sorry for teasing her earlier. Lily felt happy and they both became friends.

One day, Jenny, who was three years old, saw a drain. She thought it would be fun to perform in it. So, she grabbed her regular toy drum and started drumming.

Mommy heard Jenny from the living room. She walked over to the drain and said, "What are you doing, Jenny?"

Jenny answered, "I'm performing! I'm the best drum player in the world!"

Mommy smiled. She said, "That's very nice, Jenny. It's time for your regular bath now."

Jenny held her drum close. "Ok mommy, she said.

So Jenny put her drum away, and went to take her regular bath. She was excited to perform later that day!

One day, a polite earth was going for a walk. He kept walking and walking until it started to get dark. He wanted to keep going but it was getting late, so he had to go home.

The next day, he woke up early and went for another walk. He kept walking and looking at the nice trees and flowers on the path. He came to a pond with some ducks and he said hello to them. They were polite and said hello back.

He kept walking and he came to a big field. He sat down for a rest, and he saw some clouds in the sky. He watched them for a while and he felt happy.

He kept walking and eventually he got home. He said thank you to the earth and the clouds and the ducks. He was thankful for their kind and polite company.

One day, Elliot wanted to go outside and play. He put on his rain boots and put on his raincoat. His mommy asked him to be careful because it was raining.

Elliot nodded and went out the door. He stepped into the big puddle outside and giggled. He loved the splash that the rain made.

Suddenly, a big giant appeared at the end of the street. Elliot had never seen anything like it before. He was so nervous that he wanted to run away, but he couldn't move.

The giant said, "Surrender or I'll get you!" Elliot was too scared to do anything, so he decided to surrender. He slowly put his hands in the air and said "I surrender."

The giant was very surprised. He said, "Are you sure?" Elliot nodded nervously, and the giant smiled. He said, " I really wanted a hug, not a surrender. Can I have a hug?"

Elliot smiled and ran forward to hug the giant. The giant laughed and gave Elliot a big hug. Then he gave Elliot a high-five and left.

Elliot went back inside feeling empowered and excited. He had faced his fear, and it only ended up in a hug!

Once there lived a little girl named Callie. She was very gifted; she could already count up to 20 at only three years old!

One day, Callie heard her Mommy and Daddy talking about a big surprise. Callie didn't know what it would be, but she was very excited.

The next day, Callie and her parents went on a special picnic. Callie couldn't wait to see what the surprise was!

When the family arrived at the beach, her Daddy showed Callie an oyster. He opened it for her and inside was the most beautiful shell! Callie was so happy that she jumped for joy!

Callie's family enjoyed their picnic and Callie proudly displayed her shell for everyone to see. From then on, Callie never forgot the wonderful surprise her Daddy showed her that day.

Once upon a time there was a man. He was so hungry. He looked high and low for something to eat but he could not find any food.

He was walking on the street and suddenly he saw a big window. He peeked inside and he saw a kitchen. There was a big glass filled with food! He felt so happy and excited.

He knocked on the window and said, "Hello! Can I have some food? I am so hungry!

Suddenly, an old lady opened the door. She said, "Come in, my child. You can have some of this food.

The man entered the kitchen, sat at the table, and began to eat. The food was delicious, and he ate until he was full.

After he finished eating, the old lady said, "Come back anytime you're hungry.

The man smiled and thanked her before he left. He kept looking for food, but he was never so lucky as he was when he peeked through the window glass.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to look up at the stars and dream about what was out there in the distant sky.

One night, she saw a shooting star and made a wish. "I wish everyone on Earth could unite and be friends," she said.

The next day, Lily went to the park and saw a boy playing by himself. She went up to him and asked if he wanted to play together. They became friends and played all day.

That night, Lily saw another shooting star and made another wish. "I wish that more people could unite and be friends like me and the boy at the park," she said.

The next day, Lily and her new friend went to the playground and saw another boy sitting alone. They went up to him and asked if he wanted to play. Soon, they were all playing together and having fun.

From then on, Lily made a wish on every shooting star she saw, and more and more people on Earth began to unite and be friends. And it all started with one little girl's wish.

Once upon a time, in a big green field, there was a little rabbit. The rabbit had a big friend, an elephant. They liked to play together every day.

One day, the rabbit saw a big, red ball stuck in a tree. The rabbit wanted to get the ball, but it was too high for him to reach. The rabbit asked his friend, the elephant, for help. The elephant was very tall and could reach the ball.

The elephant reached up with his long nose and got the ball from the tree. But then, a rude bird came and tried to take the ball away. The rabbit and the elephant did not like the rude bird. They worked together to keep the ball safe and played with it all day long.

Once upon a time there was a mommy and her little girl. The mommy was very excited. She said, "Let's go do an operation! It'll be so fun!"

The little girl didn't know what an operation was, but she was very excited too. She said, "What is an operation? Is it fun?"

The mommy laughed and said, "Yes, it's very fun. Let's go!"

The mommy and her little girl went to the doctor's office. They opened the door and went inside. The little girl was so excited! She saw toys in the doctor's office and said, "This looks so funny!" The mommy smiled and said, "That it does! Now let's get ready for our operation."

The doctor explained what the operation was and what it would do. Then he opened the tools and the little girl watched. When the operation was finished, the little girl felt very brave. She was happy she was able to help her mommy!

The mommy and her little girl left the doctor's office. The little girl said, "That was so fun! Can we do it again?"

The mommy smiled and said, "Maybe someday. For now, let's go get some ice cream!"

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Tweety. Tweety loved to fly and play with his friends. One day, Tweety saw a shiny object on top of a tall chimney. He wanted to get it, but he was too small to climb up the chimney.

Tweety asked his friends for help, but they were too scared. So, Tweety decided to try and fly up to the top of the chimney himself. He flew and flew, but he couldn't reach the shiny object. Suddenly, a gust of wind blew him off course and he fell to the ground.

Tweety was sad and hurt, but he learned an important lesson. He realized that sometimes it's better to ask for help and return the favor when someone needs it. From then on, Tweety and his friends always helped each other and everything was perfect.

Mimi was a very small girl, but she was brave. One day, Mimi had an idea. She wanted to try and manage a wave. She thought it would be fun to see if she could make the wave reach the shore.

Mimi was excited. She got ready and headed down to the beach. There, she saw the delicate waves rolling in. She waited and watched, hoping her plan would work.

Finally, Mimi took a deep breath and jumped in. She kicked and paddled, trying to manage the wave. At first it didn't seem to work, but then Mimi finally felt the wave start to carry her. She smiled and laughed as the wave brought her all the way to the shore.

Mimi had done it! She had managed a wave! She was so proud that she'd managed to do something that seemed so big and hard. She would never forget this moment and the joy she felt when she succeeded.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park with her friends. One day, Lily and her friends were playing tag when she saw a man walking towards them. He had a big bag with him.

Lily's mommy had told her to be careful around strangers, so she ran to her mommy to tell her about the man. Her mommy thanked her for being so smart and told her to stay close to her.

Later that day, Lily and her mommy went to the store to buy some oil for cooking. The bottle was very rough and bumpy, but Lily liked how it felt in her hands. When they got home, Lily helped her mommy cook dinner. She had to kneel on a chair to reach the stove, but she was very careful not to spill the oil.

As they sat down to eat, Lily's mommy told her how proud she was of her for being so smart and careful earlier. Lily smiled and felt happy. She knew she had done the right thing.

Once upon a time, there was a curious three year old. He was playing in his garden when he noticed a big, wide street. He had never seen anything like this before, so he walked closer. As he got closer, he heard funny noises coming from the street. He was so curious that he decided to go and take a look.

When he arrived, he saw so many new things. All kinds of people, cars, and animals were there. He had never seen so many different things before. He was so excited to explore and learn more.

He walked around the street, taking it all in. He gained lots of knowledge and made lots of new friends. Everyone was so kind and welcoming. He always came back home happy and full of new stories.

The street was special; it was like exploring a new world. Every time he went down the street he would always gain something new.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy who loved to fish. He would go to the pond every day with his fishing rod and try to catch a fish. One day, he caught a fish that was very big and very special. It was the most original fish he had ever seen.

Timmy was so excited to show his fish to his friend, Johnny. He ran to Johnny's house and said, "Look at my fish! It's so big and original!" Johnny was not impressed and said, "That fish is not original. I caught one just like it yesterday."

Timmy was very sad and didn't know what to do. He went back to the pond and threw the fish back into the water. As he was walking home, he saw a big bird swoop down and grab the fish out of the water. Timmy started to cry because he knew his special fish was gone forever.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Jane. She was very curious and enjoyed new adventures. She wanted to explore the world and all the different places she could visit.

One day she decided to go down to the river. When she arrived, something unusual happened. There was an old man sitting by the river. He was wearing a big hat and he smiled at her.

Jane sat down beside him and he asked her if she wanted to go on an adventure. Jane smiled and nodded her head. She was so excited.

The old man took her on a boat ride down the river. Along the way, they saw lots of animals and plants. They even saw some fish jumping in and out of the water. It was like nothing Jane had ever seen before.

When they reached the end of the river, the old man looked at Jane and smiled. She knew it was time to say goodbye. With one last nod of her head, she said goodbye and headed back home.

It had been a very unusual adventure, but Jane loved every minute of it.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max loved to play in the park with his ball. One day, Max's owner took him to the park, but they were delayed because of traffic. Max was sad because he wanted to play with his ball.

When they finally arrived, Max was so happy! He ran to get his ball, but there was a lot of noise from the other dogs barking. Max didn't like the noise, so he went to a quiet spot to play with his ball.

Max was an honest dog. When he accidentally knocked over a lady's drink, he went to tell her he was sorry. The lady was happy that Max was honest, and she gave him a treat. Max was so happy that he wagged his tail and ran back to play with his ball.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was very sad. One day she decided to go for a walk.

As the little girl walked, she noticed a door in front of her. She wanted to see what was inside so she put her hand on the handle and opened it. Inside, she saw a big open room.

At the far end of the room there was a huge head made of stone. The little girl was curious so she walked closer to take a look. Then her head started to move!

The little girl was very scared, but the head spoke. It said it was sorry for being so sad. It said that it had been alone for a long time and just wanted a friend.

The little girl smiled and said she would be happy to be the head's friend. From that day onwards, the little girl and the head were the best of friends!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim who loved to play with his toys. One day, Tim's mom asked him to clean up his room. But Tim didn't want to, he wanted to keep playing.

"Tim, you need to behave and clean up your toys," said his mom.

Tim didn't listen and continued to play. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise outside. He went to the window and saw a big truck with steel bars on it. The truck stopped in front of Tim's house and some men got out.

"Excuse me, little boy. We need to take some steel from your backyard," said one of the men.

Tim didn't know what to do. His backyard was not tidy and he was afraid the men would be angry. He quickly cleaned up his toys and went outside to show the men where the steel was.

"Wow, your backyard looks very tidy now. Thank you for showing us where the steel is," said the man.

Tim felt happy that he behaved and helped the men. He went back inside to continue playing with his toys, but this time he knew he had to clean up after himself.

Once upon a time, there was a huge bus. Inside, there were two people who were very much in love. One day, they decided to marry. Everyone was so excited. Everyone got on the big bus and they rode to the wedding.

At the wedding, there were flowers and pretty decorations. The bride wore a beautiful white dress and the groom looked handsome in his suit. Everyone came to celebrate their love and cheer the happy couple.

It was a beautiful day and the bus ride home was the best part of all. All the friends and family waved and cheered and wished the couple a happy married life. The bus was huge and very crowded, but everyone was having a good time.

The newly wedded couple finally arrived home and everyone said goodbye. They were now married forever.

The end.

One day, a little girl named Lily went to the park with her mom. She saw a human sitting on a bench and went to say hello. The human had a very interesting hat on.

"Hi there!" said Lily.

"Hello little one," said the human with a smile.

"I like your hat, it's very interesting," said Lily.

"Thank you, I got it from a faraway place," said the human.

Lily and the human talked for a while and then said goodbye. Lily felt happy that she met someone new and interesting.

Once upon a time there lived two very good friends: Joe and Bob. Joe loved zip so much, it was his favourite thing in the world.

One day Joe was playing with his zip when Bob came up to him. Bob had a generous idea. "Let's play a game!" he said enthusiastically.

Joe was excited to play. Bob pointed to the top of the hill and said "Let's race to the top and whoever gets there first is the winner!"

Joe was worried that Bob would win because he was so fast. Joe thought and thought and came up with an idea.
He said to Bob "Let's do something different. Let's throw our zip as far as we can and whoever gets it to land furthest away is the winner".

Bob liked Joe's idea and they both threw their zips. In the end Bob's zip went furthest and he was declared the winner!

Joe was very happy and proud of Bob, even though he lost. Joe and Bob went for a hug and enjoyed the rest of the day together.

Once upon a time, there was a small girl named Lily. She wanted to go to the park with her mom but her mom said they needed to go to the store first. So they went to the store and got a cart to put their things in.

Lily saw a toy she wanted but her mom said they didn't have enough money to buy it. Lily was sad and started to cry.

A kind man who worked at the store saw Lily crying and asked her what was wrong. Lily told him about the toy she wanted but couldn't have. The man raised his eyebrows and said, "I have an idea."

He took Lily and her mom to the toy aisle and showed them a small toy that was on sale. Lily was happy and her mom thanked the man. They put the toy in the cart and went to the park.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Bob and Nora. They loved to play together and had so much fun!

One day they decided to play a nice game of jazz. They danced, hopped and split around the room. They were so cute and adorable.

Suddenly, Nora shouted "I want to split again!". Bob decided to join in too, and together they split about the room.

Bob said "You are the best splitter ever Nora!" and Nora said "Thanks Bob, you too!" Then they laughed and hugged.

The two friends had such a fun day, splitting around and playing jazz. They had the best time ever!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved boats and wanted to sail on one. One day, he saw a big boat and asked the captain if he could go for a ride. The captain said yes and Timmy was so happy.

While they were sailing, Timmy got hungry and asked the captain if he had any food. The captain gave him some beef jerky and Timmy loved it.

As they continued to sail, Timmy saw a big building and asked the captain what it was. The captain said it was a modern skyscraper. Timmy thought it looked cool and said he wanted to work there when he grew up.

The captain smiled and said, "You can be anything you want to be, Timmy." Timmy smiled back and said, "I want to be a sailor like you!"

Jen likes to speed on her bike. She likes to go fast and feel the wind on her face. She likes to be independent and do things by herself. She does not like to wait for her mom or dad.

One day, Jen wants to go to the park. She does not want to ask her mom or dad. She thinks she can go by herself. She puts on her helmet and gets on her bike. She speeds down the street and turns the corner.

But Jen does not see the big rock on the road. She hits the rock and falls off her bike. She hurts her knee and her elbow. She starts to cry. She wishes she had asked her mom or dad.

Soon, a nice lady sees Jen and helps her. She calls Jen's mom and dad. They come quickly and hug Jen. They are not angry, but they are worried. They tell Jen that she should not go to the park by herself. They tell her that they love her and want her to be safe.

Jen says she is sorry. She says she will not speed on her bike again. She says she will ask her mom or dad before she goes anywhere. She says she loves them too. She learns that being independent is good, but being careful is better.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play at the park with her friends. One day, Lily and her friend Timmy were playing on the swings when they saw a funny looking pole.

Lily asked Timmy, "What is that silly pole doing here?" Timmy replied, "I don't know, maybe it's for the birds to sit on." Lily thought that was a foolish idea, but she didn't say anything.

As they continued to play, Lily's mom called out to her, "Lily, it's time to go home now." Lily said goodbye to Timmy and ran over to her mom. "Mommy, did you see the funny pole at the park?" she asked.

Her mom smiled and said, "Yes, I saw it. It's called a flagpole and it's used to hold up a flag." Lily was amazed and asked, "Can we come back to the park tomorrow and see the flag on the pole?" Her mom nodded and they walked home together, happy and excited for their next trip to the park.

Ben and his dad went to the office. Dad had to work on his computer. Ben wanted to play with dad's toys. Dad had a lot of toys on his desk. He had a car, a plane, a ball and a phone.

"Can I play with your toys, dad?" Ben asked.

"Sure, you can play with some of them. But be careful, okay? Don't break them or lose them," dad said.

Ben picked up the car and made vroom-vroom noises. He drove the car on the desk and on the floor. He had fun with the car. Then he saw the plane. He wanted to fly the plane. He put the car down and grabbed the plane.

He threw the plane in the air and watched it soar. He clapped his hands and laughed. He ran after the plane and caught it. He threw it again and again. He did not see where the plane was going.

The plane flew out of the window and landed on the street. Ben gasped and ran to the window. He saw the plane on the ground. He saw a big truck coming. He saw the truck run over the plane and smash it. He saw the plane break into many parts. He felt sad and scared.

He started to cry. He ran to dad and hugged him. He told him what happened.

"I'm sorry, dad. I broke your plane. It flew out of the window and the truck smashed it. It has many parts now. I'm sorry, I'm sorry," Ben sobbed.

Dad looked at the window and saw the broken plane. He felt angry and sad. He loved his plane. It was a gift from his friend. He wanted to yell at Ben. But he saw Ben's tears and heard his sorry. He felt sorry for Ben too.

He hugged Ben and stroked his hair. He said, "It's okay, Ben. I know you didn't mean to break it. It was an accident. Accidents happen sometimes. I'm not mad at you. I still love you. But you have to be more careful next time, okay? You have to watch where you throw things. You have to respect other people's things. Do you understand?"

Ben nodded and said, "Yes, dad. I understand. I'm sorry. I love you too."

Dad smiled and kissed Ben's cheek. He said, "Let's go home and have some ice cream. How does that sound?"

Ben smiled and said, "That sounds good, dad. Can we get chocolate?"

"Sure, we can get chocolate. Come on, let's go," dad said.

He picked up Ben and his bag. He said goodbye to his bald boss, who was watching them from his office. He said, "Sorry, boss. I have to go. My son had a little trouble with my plane."

His boss nodded and said, "No problem, dad. I understand. Kids will be kids. Have a good day."

Dad and Ben left the office and went to the car. They drove to the ice cream shop and got chocolate ice cream. They ate their ice cream and talked about their day. They forgot about the broken plane and the parts. They were happy.

Once upon a time, there was a purple bird. The bird had a whistle. It was a nice whistle. The bird liked to fly and play with its whistle. It would whistle songs all day long.

One day, the bird met a new friend. The friend was a little cat. The cat said, "Welcome, bird!" The bird said, "Thank you, cat!" They were happy to meet each other. They played together all day long.

The purple bird showed the cat its whistle. The cat liked the whistle too. They took turns playing with the whistle. They made up songs together. The bird and the cat became best friends. They played and whistled every day.

Once upon a time there was a mommy and her toddler. One day the mommy set a big pot of sauce on the stove. But soon the toddler got curious and came to see what the mommy was doing. He wanted to help.

The mommy let him stir the sauce, thinking he wouldn't do any harm. Soon the toddler had stirred the sauce so hard that it was getting all over the kitchen. It made the kitchen very messy!

The mommy was mad. She told the toddler not to touch the sauce again. But then she saw the toddler had tears in his eyes. She knew he was sorry for being naughty, so she hugged him and promised to help him clean up the mess.

Together they worked quickly to wipe upthe sauce before it ruined the kitchen. The mommy and the toddler were both happy and the kitchen was clean again. They promised never to leave the sauce unattended again.

Once there was a very young rabbit, who loved hopping and eating yummy carrots. One day, the young rabbit felt very brave and hopped outside of his home. He saw a big fierce animal walking in the forest. Instinctively, he ran and hid behind a big tree.

The big animal saw the rabbit and yelled, "Come here little one! I can give you lots of delicious things." The rabbit was scared but he was also curious and stepped out from behind the tree. The animal then said in a gentle voice, "I want to order you to do something for me. Will you do it?" The rabbit hesitated but eventually agreed.

The animal told the rabbit to go back home and bring all of his carrot reserves to his den in the forest. Despite feeling afraid, the rabbit stayed obedient and went back home. Sadly, once the bunny arrived at the animal's den, it became clear that the animal had tricked him. The big animal changed its voice to sound nasty and said, "I don't need carrots. I need you!"

The moral of this story is: always trust your instincts. Don't let other talk you into doing something that doesn't feel right.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys, especially his blocks. He would stack them up high and make different shapes. One day, Timmy's mommy asked him to help her measure the length of a piece of string. Timmy was so excited to help!

He grabbed his ruler and carefully measured the string. "Wow, mommy! This string is incredible! It's so long!" exclaimed Timmy. His mommy smiled and praised him for doing such a good job.

Later that day, Timmy went back to playing with his blocks. He decided to make a tower in the shape of a triangle. He stacked them up and measured how tall it was. "Look, mommy! I made a triangle tower!" Timmy said proudly. His mommy was amazed at how smart and creative her little boy was.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly little girl named Lily. One day, she decided to go outside to play. She saw a ball and decided to pick it up. She liked to throw the ball around and have lots of fun.

Lily noticed a butterfly flitting around nearby. She pointed at the butterfly and shouted, "Look, it's a lily!" She watched the butterfly fly away and she thought it was so pretty.

Then, Lily had an idea. She decided to throw the ball to the flying insect. She aimed carefully and threw the ball as hard as she could.

Unfortunately, the ball missed the butterfly and made a loud thump on the ground. But Lily was still happy - she had lots of fun throwing the ball and watching the lily fly away. What a jolly game it was!

Once there was a little girl named Rachel who liked to wear her favorite skirt. It was white, with tiny flowers stitched on it. Rachel would twirl and spin around in her skirt, feeling very happy.

One day, Rachel wore her favorite skirt to the park. She was feeling very careless, running and laughing without paying attention to where she was going. When she got to the swings, Rachel accidentally stepped on a big mud puddle. Her skirt was now covered in mud!

Rachel was very upset and started to cry. She didn't want to get in trouble for ruining her skirt. So she decided to lie to her mom and tell her the mud was already on the skirt when she found it.

Rachel was filled with guilt, but she still kept lying. Every time her mom asked about the mud, she just said: "It was already there when I found it."

Rachel was relieved when her mom believed her story. She knew she was wrong to tell such a careless lie. From then on, Rachel was more careful and tried not to tell any lies.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. One day, Timmy wanted to go on a bus ride. He asked his mommy, "Can we go on the bus, please?"

Mommy said, "Yes, we can go on the bus. But be careful and hold my hand."

Timmy and his mommy got on the bus. Timmy was so excited! He could hear the bus engine and the people talking. Suddenly, the bus made a loud noise and Timmy became frightened.

"It's okay, Timmy," said his mommy. "It's just the bus making a noise."

Timmy felt better and they continued their bus ride. When they got off the bus, Timmy said, "That was fun! Can we go on the bus again?"

Once upon a time, there was a baby named Lily. Lily was very cute and always happy. One day, Lily's mommy asked her, "Are you ready to go for a walk?" Lily replied, "Yes, I am ready!"

Lily's mommy picked her up and they went outside. They saw a bird in a tree and Lily said, "Look mommy, birdie!" Her mommy said, "Yes, that's a bird. What does a bird do?" Lily replied, "Birdie flies!"

As they were walking, they saw their neighbor and Lily's mommy said, "Let's say hi to our neighbor." The neighbor said, "Hello Lily, how are you?" Lily smiled and said, "I good! What you do?" The neighbor replied, "I am gardening. I like to grow flowers." Lily said, "Pretty flowers!" and they continued on their walk.

Once upon a time there was a clever triangle. He lived in a big house with lots of room to play. Every day he had fun running around inside and outside. One day he wanted to fly, so he tried to flap his sides really hard. But he couldn't get off the ground!

Then he noticed a big bird flying in the sky. The bird was very kind, and noticed the triangle trying to fly. The bird swooped down to help him out. He told the triangle about the power of support.

The triangle remembered what the bird said and decided to try it out. He asked his friends to help him and when they said yes, they all got behind the triangle and began to push. Suddenly the clever triangle was in the air!

They were all so happy and cheered loudly as the triangle soared across the sky. From then on, whenever the triangle wanted to fly, he knew he could always get some support from his friends.

Once upon a time, there was a man who had a very special wife. She was the most original person in the world. The man knew the most important thing was to value his wife. He gave her lots of compliments and took care of her every day.

One day, the man decided to throw his wife a surprise party to show her just how much he valued her. He bought a big cake and invited all their friends and family. Everyone had a great time.

The man gave his wife a special gift during the party. It was a necklace with a big sparkly diamond in the center. His wife was so happy and hugged him tight. She felt loved and valued by her husband.

The man knew that any day he spent with his wife was a day filled with lots of love and happiness. Having a wife that he valued was the best gift of all. And the original woman that she was just made it even better!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys, but one day, he accidentally broke his favorite toy car. Timmy was very sad and wanted to fix his car.

He went to his mommy and said, "Mommy, my car is broken. Can you fix it?"

Mommy said, "Of course, Timmy. Let's see what we can do."

They looked at the broken thing and mommy said, "I think we need some glue to fix it."

After they fixed the car, Timmy was very happy and said, "Thank you, mommy. Now my car is healthy again!"

Jack walked down the street. He could hear the police marching. The officers looked very serious, but their faces were mild.

"What are the police doing? Jack asked his mom.

"Well, they are protecting us, Mom told him. "They are marching to make sure we all stay safe.

Jack smiled. He knew the police were there to keep him safe. He watched them march until he could no longer see them.

Jack saw the police marching every day. He knew they were there even if he couldn't always see them. That made him feel happy and safe.

Once there was a big, successful car. John wanted to ride it like all the other kids. "Mom, can I ride the car?" He asked. "Yes, honey." said his mom, "But first it needs a few changes."

John waited and waited but the car didn't look any different. He asked his mom what was taking so long. "Tonight when you go to bed," she said, "the car will be ready for you to ride.

That evening, John couldn't wait to go to bed. Finally, it was time. His mom drove him out to the car. It looked just like a normal car. "Go ahead," she said.

Surprisingly, the car started up and off John went, riding the big and successful car. He drove around the neighborhood and all of the other kids saw him and cheered. John had a big smile on his face. He had his first successful ride!

Once upon a time, there was a wise old dog. His name was Spot. He lived in a very nice house with some kind people.

Every morning, the people would get out of bed and give Spot some breakfast. He usually got a big bowl of food, and then he'd move around and play with the people.

One morning, the people had something special for Spot. It was a big, yummy cake. Spot loved cake, and he wanted to move closer to the people so he could get closer to the cake. But the people said, "No, Spot. This cake is for us. You can't have any!"

Spot was very sad. He looked up at the people and whined. The people saw how sad Spot was, and then they realised that Spot was a wise old dog and he deserved something special, too. So they moved the cake a little closer to Spot and said, "Ok, you can have just one piece."

Spot was so happy! He moved closer to the cake, wagged his tail, and slowly ate the cake. When he was done, Spot gave the people lots of licks and thanked them with a big, happy bark!

John was walking along the beach at the harbor when he saw a delicate boat bobbing in the distance. He was excited and ran to it. When he got to the boat, he found three men onboard. When he asked them what they were doing, one of the men nodded and John knew it was something bad. He tried to run away but one of the men caught him. The men put John in the boat and sailed away.

John was scared but he didn't know what to do. He tried to call for help but the men wouldn't let him. After a while, they reached a dark and secret harbor. The men took John out of the boat and he was never seen again.

John was never seen again, but his memory lives on in the delicate boat that still floats in the harbor today.

Once upon a time, there was a little squirrel named Nutty. Nutty loved to play in the big oak tree in the forest. One day, Nutty found a shiny acorn on the ground. "Look, Mama!" Nutty said excitedly. "I found an acorn!"

Mama squirrel smiled and said, "That acorn looks ready to hang on the tree. Why don't you try to hang it up?"

Nutty climbed up the tree and found a good spot to hang the acorn. "I did it, Mama!" Nutty shouted down. "The acorn is hanging up high!"

Mama squirrel was proud of Nutty. "You did a great job, Nutty," she said. "Now let's go find some more acorns to hang." And Nutty and Mama squirrel spent the rest of the day collecting acorns and hanging them up in the oak tree.

Once upon a time, there was an adorable little cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to play in the mud and get dirty. One day, Kitty heard that it was her friend Doggy's birthday. She was very excited to go to the party.

But when Kitty got to Doggy's birthday party, everyone looked at her. She was too dirty from playing in the mud! Doggy's mom said, "Kitty, you need to clean yourself before you can come inside." Kitty felt sad and left the party.

Kitty went home and asked her mom to help her clean. They used soap and water to wash away all the mud. Now, Kitty was clean and shiny! She went back to Doggy's birthday party, and everyone was happy to see her. Kitty and her friends had a great time at the party.

Timmy was so happy that he started to wave his hands in the air. Suddenly, he accidentally spilled the juice all over the floor. Timmy's mom wasn't mad, she just helped him clean it up. From that day on, Timmy learned to be more careful when he waved his hands around.

Once upon a time there was a happy kite. She loved to dance in the wind. One day she slept too long and the wind grew strong. It carried her high, high in the sky. She felt scared and kept trying to find her way back home. But the wind kept pushing her further away. Eventually she was so high that she couldn't find her way back. She was so sad, and the wind carried her away and she never returned. The end.

Once upon a time, there lived a little girl named Suzy. One day, Suzy wanted to comb her hair. She looked for her comb but it was no where to be found. She asked her Mom, "Mom, where's my comb? Her Mom replied, "I don't know. Let's look for it. So they looked around the house but it was still nowhere to be found.

They finally found the comb in an empty drawer. Suzy's Mom said, "It looks like we have to work to find things sometimes! Suzy was happy and took her comb. She combed her hair and looked so pretty.

The end.

Once upon a time, there were two good friends, a boy and a girl. They were best friends and were always supporting each other.

One day, the girl wanted to climb the high tree in the garden. She was scared to climb on her own so she asked her friend to support her. The boy was happy to help her. He held her hands and said, "Don't be scared. I will support you."

The girl slowly began to climb the tree. But as she was climbing, she slipped and started to cry. Her friend said, "Don't worry, we will find a way to get you down."

The boy then had an idea. He went inside and brought some vanilla ice cream. He put the ice cream on a plate with a spoon and showed it to the girl. She smiled, because it was her favorite flavor.

The boy then asked the girl if she wanted him to help her descend the tree. She said yes, and he carefully held her and helped her get down from the tree. When she was safe on the ground, she hugged her friend and said, "Thank you for supporting me."

They both smiled and enjoyed the delicious ice cream together.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, named Jenny and Joe. They were very brave and helpful, and they loved playing together.

One day, Jenny and Joe saw a flock of sheep in a nearby field. They wanted to get closer to the animals, so they decided to build a fence around them.

Jenny and Joe worked very hard and they built a great fence. When they were done, they looked at the sheep and Joe said to Jenny, "Let's encourage the sheep .

Jenny and Joe both waved their hands at the sheep. Then they started calling out to them in a friendly way. The sheep were a bit scared at first, but after a few minutes they started getting close to Jenny and Joe.

The sheep were so friendly and curious, it was wonderful! Jenny and Joe continued to encourage the sheep and soon they were all close together.

Jenny and Joe laughed and thanked the sheep for being so helpful. They were happy to have made some new animal friends. Jenny and Joe were so proud of their work, and they continue to encourage the sheep every day.

Once upon a time there was a nice man who lived in a small house. He wanted to help his friends, so he went to look for stones.

It was a hot sunny day, and he was feeling very tired, yet he kept looking. Suddenly, he found a nice big stone. "This will help my friends!", he thought.

He lifted up the stone and started carrying it home, but it was too heavy. He tried his best to carry it, but it was just too big!

Just then, a kind lady saw him struggling. She asked, "Can I help you?"

The man was so happy and said, "Yes, please help! I'm trying to carry this stone home so I can help my friends."

The kind lady smiled and said, "Come on, let's try together. I'm very patient and I think we can do it!"

They worked together and managed to carry the stone all the way home.

When they arrived, the man thanked the kind lady. "You have been so patient. Thank you so much for helping me!"

The kind lady smiled and said, "You're welcome! Friends always help each other."

Once upon a time, there was a kind and generous boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys and share them with his friends. One day, Timmy's friend Sarah came over to play. Timmy showed her his toys and shared them with her.

But then, Sarah saw a toy that she really wanted. She asked Timmy if she could have it, but Timmy didn't want to give it away. Sarah got upset and said, "That's not fair! I want that toy!"

Timmy didn't know what to do. He wanted to make Sarah happy, but he also didn't want to waste his favorite toy. So, he thought for a moment and then said, "Sarah, I have an idea. How about we stand the toy up on the shelf and we can both look at it whenever we want?"

Sarah thought that was a great idea and they both smiled. Timmy was happy that he could share his toy with his friend and not waste it. They continued to play and have fun together.

When the box was open, Bob saw a nice, shiny ball inside. Bob smiled and threw the ball up in the air, and then caught it. He played with the ball, throwing it up and catching it, until it was dark and he had to go home.

Eric asked, "What can we do with it?"

Susie said, "We can push the buttons and see what happens!"

So they began pushing the buttons. When they pushed the red one, the mixer sang songs. When they pushed the blue one, it spun around. Every time they pushed a button, something new and exciting happened.

Eric laughed and said, "This is so much fun!"

Susie agreed and they kept pushing the buttons, discovering all the different things the mixer could do. They had an adventurous and exciting day together!

One day, a three-year-old boy saw the youth while he was driving. The boy was excited and asked when he could drive too.

The youth stopped to talk to the boy and promised that one day he could drive too. The three-year-old was thrilled and he waved as the youth began to drive away.

The youth drove away feeling happy that he had been able to give the three-year-old boy something to look forward to.

Once there was a boy, who loved to play in the backyard. Every day was the same; he would run around and giggle, trying to loop around his mom. But one day, something was different. He noticed that his mom was ignoring him. He didn't understand why she wasn't playing with him anymore. He asked her what was wrong, but she stayed quiet and looked away.

The next day he tried to act just like normal, but it was hard. His mom still ignored him, and every time he came close she seemed to move away. It made him feel very sad. He knew this wasn't the normal way for his mom to act.

Finally, after days and days of ignoring, his mom came outside to the backyard and hugged him tight. She said she was sorry and that she had just needed a little break. The boy was so relieved; things were back to normal now. They played together in the yard, looping around each other like they always did.

The boy learned that it's okay to take a break and that it doesn't mean that someone doesn't love you. He was glad that things were back to normal.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and look for pretty rocks. One day, she found a shiny diamond on the ground. It sparkled in the sun and made her so happy.

Lily ran home to show her mommy the diamond. "Look what I found!" she said, holding up the diamond.

Mommy looked at the diamond and said, "That's very pretty, but we need to give it back to whoever lost it."

Lily didn't want to give the diamond back. She refused to let it go. But mommy explained that it was the right thing to do.

Lily felt sad, but she knew mommy was right. She took a deep breath and felt calm. She gave the diamond to the police station so they could find the owner. And even though she didn't get to keep the diamond, she felt proud of herself for doing the right thing.

Soon, the furry foot came to a new place. It was a magical forest with wonderful creatures of all shapes and sizes. The furry foot was delighted and couldn't wait to explore.

The furry foot ran up to a friendly fox and said, "Let's have some fun!" So, they ran around and played lots of games. They jumped, laughed and skipped until the sun went down.

The furry foot was so happy that it decided to stay. It made many new friends and enjoyed all the wonderful wonders of the magical forest. The furry foot knew it would always be welcome and found a place to call home.

Lily felt sad and started to sneeze. She sneezed so much that her nose turned red and she couldn't stop. She tried to play with her toys, but she couldn't focus because of her sneezes. Lily's mom came back from the store and saw her daughter sneezing. She knew something was wrong and asked Lily what happened.

Lily told her mom that her favorite snack wasn't available at the store. Her mom hugged her and said they could make something else for snack time. Lily stopped sneezing and felt happy again. From that day on, Lily learned that sometimes things aren't available, but there are always other options to make us happy.

Suddenly, a big dog came and started to bark at her. Lily was scared and didn't know what to do. But then, a nice man came and picked up the tire. He carried it over to the dog and the dog started to play with it. Lily felt happy that the man helped her and that the dog found a new toy to play with.

From that day on, Lily made sure to always ask for help when she needed it. She never felt helpless again. And whenever she saw a tire, she remembered the kind man who helped her and the happy dog who played with it.

One day the dishwasher became tired, so it stopped working. Everyone was so sad. They tried to turn it back on, but nothing changed.

Then one bright morning, a 3 year old had an idea. He knew he could help the dishwasher. He ran over and gave it a big hug, and sure enough, it sprang back to life! Everyone cheered and thanked the toddler for all his hard work.

From that day forward, the dishwasher and the 3 year old were best friends. They worked together to separate the dishes, and the dishwasher never became tired again. Everyone was so happy.

They went to the ice-cream shop and Baby chose the gray cone. He took a lick and smiled. "This is so yummy," he said.

Mama smiled. "Be careful and don't get it on your fingers," she warned.

Baby tried to remain still and keep the ice-cream in the cone. But it was so delicious, he couldn't help himself from licking it quickly. Soon, the ice-cream was gone.

Mama laughed. She gave Baby a hug and said, "Let's get you another one." Baby smiled and clapped again.

The boy was so happy when he finally got the jewel, but not everything turned out perfect. Unfortunately, the jewel was not reliable and it broke soon after he bought it.

The little boy was very sad but he realized an important thing. He learned that if he wanted something valuable and reliable, it was better to work hard and save money, rather than depend on luck.

They took a soft and clean cloth and wiped the rabbit's fur. Then they brushed it very softly. Finally, they rubbed the rabbit's fur with the cloth until it was spotless.

Then the doctors put the rabbit back in bed to take a nap. After a few hours, the rabbit felt much better and even more playful than before.

The doctors smiled and said it was a successful operation. The rabbit was so happy that it started hopping around around the hospital room as if nothing had happened.

After what seemed like forever, the butterfly flew away. Willow was sad to see it go, but also happy that she had made such a wonderful friend. She continued to stare at its beauty as it flew off into the sky.

Janie didn't listen to Mama and she kept holding the frog. All of a sudden, the frog jumped out of Janie's hands and hopped away into the woods.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily and her mom went outside to walk in the rain. It was very wet outside, so they brought an umbrella with them. The wind was blowing the raindrops hard, so Lily's mom held the umbrella rough to keep them dry.

Lily saw some kids playing with their toy cars, so she asked her mom if she could race with them. Her mom said yes, but they had to be careful not to slip on the wet ground. Lily picked up her toy car and started to race with the other kids. They had so much fun!

Suddenly, the wind blew harder and it started to rain even harder. Lily's mom opened the umbrella wider to cover them all. "We have to go home now," said Lily's mom. "It's getting too wet outside." Lily was sad to leave, but she was happy that she got to race with her friends and stay dry under the umbrella.

Once upon a time there was a fat cat called Strip. Strip liked to speak a lot and often made conversation with everyone around him. One day, Strip went for a walk in the park and started to speak to a little boy.

The little boy's name was Tom and Tom was only three years old. Strip and Tom became good friends and soon, Tom started to teach Strip a few games. They played tag and hide and seek, and Strip even chased a few birds.

After playing, Strip and Tom decided to take a rest. Strip lay down, while Tom sat next to him. Then, Tom started to speak to Strip, asking him lots of questions. Strip answered each one carefully, as Tom was only a young child. Because Tom was so young, he did not understand big or difficult words - so Strip used simple words that Tom could understand.

Finally, they both decided it was time to go home. As they said goodbye, Strip promised to come back and play with Tom soon. Tom smiled and waved goodbye, as Strip trotted off down the path, content that he had a new friend.

Lila and Max were twins who liked to play outside. But today, it was raining hard and they had to stay inside. They felt bored and grumpy.

"Mom, can we go out?" Lila asked.

"No, sweetie, it's too wet and cold. You might get sick. Why don't you relax and read a book or draw a picture?" Mom said.

Lila and Max did not want to read or draw. They wanted to run and jump and splash in the puddles. They looked out the window and saw the rain falling and falling.

"Rain, rain, go away, come again another day!" they sang.

But the rain did not go away. It kept falling and falling.

Lila and Max sighed and lay down on the couch. They closed their eyes and tried to relax. But they still felt bored and grumpy.

Then, they heard a knock on the door. They opened their eyes and saw their friend Sam standing outside. He was holding a big umbrella and a bag.

"Hi, Lila and Max! Do you want to play with me?" Sam asked.

Lila and Max jumped up and ran to the door. They were happy to see Sam. He was their best friend and he was always thoughtful and fun.

"What do you have in your bag, Sam?" Lila asked.

Sam smiled and opened his bag. He took out some books, some crayons, some stickers, and some cookies.

"I have some things to make this rainy day more fun. We can read, draw, decorate, and snack. Do you want to join me?" Sam said.

Lila and Max nodded and smiled. They thanked Sam and invited him in. They followed him to the living room and sat on the floor. They picked a book and started to read. They picked some crayons and started to draw. They picked some stickers and started to decorate. They picked some cookies and started to snack.

They laughed and talked and shared. They forgot about the rain and their boredom. They felt happy and relaxed.

Mom watched them from the kitchen and smiled. She was glad that Sam came to visit. He was a thoughtful friend and he made this rainy day more fun.

One day, Max and his mom were walking home from the store. Max saw something sparkly on the ground. He crouched down to get a better look and gasped when he saw it was a screw. Max picked it up and started to gaze at it as they continued strolling on.

"That's very organized, Max," said his mom. "Yes, I'm very proud of you."

Max smiled and kept on gazing at his screw. Suddenly, he stopped in his tracks. His mom asked him what was wrong.

"I want to keep it, Mommy," he said, holding out the screw for her to see.

She laughed and said, "Oh, Max! That's not a very organized thing to do. Let's leave it where it was, OK?"

Max sighed and reluctantly put the screw back where he had found it. He kept his eyes on it until his mom said it was time to go. As they walked away, he sighed again and kept glancing back at the screw until they turned the corner.

Once upon a time, there was a smart bird named Bob. Bob loved to eat prunes. One day, Bob went to find some prunes to eat.

On his way, he met a big cat. The cat said, "Bob, I want to strike you!" Bob was scared, but he was intelligent. He thought of a plan. He told the cat, "If you let me go, I will give you some prunes."

The big cat liked prunes too, so he agreed. Bob gave the cat some prunes and flew away. The cat ate the prunes and smiled. But then, the cat felt sick. He ate too many prunes.

In the end, the big cat was not happy. He learned that eating too many prunes can make you sick. Bob was safe, but the cat had a bad day.
