Anna and Ben like to play in the garden. They have a big bucket and a small shovel. They collect soil from different places. They put the soil in the bucket and mix it with water. They make mud pies and mud cakes.

"Look, Anna, I made a mud pie with a flower on top!" Ben says. He shows Anna his mud pie. It has a yellow flower in the middle.

"That is very pretty, Ben. I made a mud cake with a worm on top!" Anna says. She shows Ben her mud cake. It has a brown worm in the middle.

"Yuck, Anna, that is crazy! Why did you put a worm on your mud cake?" Ben says. He makes a face. He does not like worms.

"I like worms. They are funny and wiggly. They help the soil grow plants. Do you want to try a bite of my mud cake?" Anna says. She smiles. She likes worms.

"No, thank you, Anna. I do not want to eat a worm. I want to eat a real cake. Let's go inside and ask Mom for a snack." Ben says. He puts down his mud pie. He takes Anna's hand. They run to the house.

"Okay, Ben. Maybe Mom has some real cake for us. But I still like my mud cake. And I still like worms." Anna says. She carries her mud cake. She does not want to leave it behind. She thinks it is the best mud cake ever.

Tim asked his mom, "Can I feed the jellyfish?" His mom said, "I don't know, let's try." They found some small fish to feed the jellyfish. The jellyfish ate the fish and seemed happy.

After a while, Tim thought it was boring to just watch the jellyfish. He wanted to play with other kids and build sand castles. So, Tim and his mom said goodbye to the jellyfish and went to have more fun at the beach.

Max was a very careless boy. He liked to play all day, but his mum kept telling him to help around the house. One day Max decided he would clean up the garden.

He started to pull out the weeds and pick up sticks, when suddenly he saw a tip in the garden! He was so excited - he had never found money before! Max was about to pick it up when his mum said, "Stop! Don't delay, put it back in its place."

Confused, Max asked, "Why? I found it - it's mine!" But his mum shook her head and replied, "No, it must have been dropped by someone and it's not polite to keep it. Quickly return it to its rightful owner."

Max had a light bulb moment and quickly put the tip back in the garden. He had been careless to not think about this before. Now he was sure to remember to return things to their rightful owners.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys, but he always wished he had more. One day, his mom took him to the toy store. Timmy was so happy! He saw many toys, but he wanted a plastic truck.

Timmy's mom bought him the truck and they went home. Timmy played with his new toy all day. He drove it around the house and made it jump over pillows. But then, the truck got stuck under the couch. Timmy tried to reach it, but he couldn't.

He asked his mom for help. She said, "I have an idea! Let's use an ordinary ruler!" Timmy didn't know what that meant, but he watched as his mom used the ruler to reach the truck. She got it out and gave it back to Timmy. He was so happy and thanked his mom. From that day on, Timmy loved his plastic truck even more.

Once upon a time, there lived a little girl named Nadia. She was three years old and loved to explore the world. One day, when Nadia was playing in the garden, she spotted a silly shrimp. She couldn't take her eyes off it and so she ran to call her mom.

"Mommy, Mommy, come look! I found a silly shrimp!" Nadia said.

Her mom smiled. "It is a very special shrimp. Let's put it in a bowl so it won't disappear," she said.

So they put the shrimp in a bowl and watched it. They decided to feed it and soon they saw it was having a lot of fun.

Nadia was so happy that the shrimp didn't disappear. The next day, mum told Nadia that the shrimp had disappeared.

"Where did it go, Mommy?" Nadia asked.

"I am not sure. Maybe someone took it or it just decided to disappear," her mom replied.

Nadia was sad, but she knew the shrimp would always be a part of her memories.

Once upon a time there was a special cricket. His name was Jack. He was incredible. He could jump super high and run very fast.

One day Jack wanted to go explore a new place near his home. But when he got there, he saw that his special wings were broken. He was sad.

Luckily, Jack found a friendly dragonfly to help him. Her name was Milly. Milly said she could repair Jack's wings.

Milly flew away and returned a few minutes later with a special tool. She used the tool to fix Jack's wings.

When Jack saw that his wings had been repaired he couldn't believe it. He thanked Milly and hopped away on his new, incredible wings!

Once upon a time, there was a prince and a princess. The prince loved the princess very much and wanted to marry her. All of the people in the kingdom were very excited for them.

The day of their wedding arrived, and the princess was feeling very nervous. Suddenly, there were loud noises in the sky. It was a missile! Everyone was scared and started to run.

The prince grabbed the princess and said: "Don't be nervous, we are going to marry because I love you." They looked into each other's eyes and both felt so brave. The prince asked: "Do you want to marry me today?"

The princess smiled and said: "Yes, I want to marry you."

They hugged each other and celebrated as the missile flew away. Everyone rejoiced and the prince and princess lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big bag of candy, and she loved to chew them all day long. One day, Lily's mommy told her that candy didn't have any power to make her strong and healthy. Lily felt ashamed because she didn't know that before.

Lily's mommy told her that fruits and veggies had the power to make her strong and healthy. Lily decided to try some fruits and veggies, and she found out that they were really yummy too! She didn't feel ashamed anymore because she knew that she was making a good choice for her body. From that day on, Lily ate lots of fruits and veggies, and she only had candy as a special treat.

Once upon a time, there was a light bunny who lived in a green forest. The bunny liked to hop and play all day. But one day, the green forest started to lose its color.

The light bunny wanted to restore the green color to the forest. So, the bunny asked his friends, the birds and the squirrels, to help him. They all tried to find a way to make the forest green again.

But no matter what they did, they could not restore the green to the forest. The light bunny and his friends were very sad. The forest stayed not green, and they could not play in it anymore.

Lila loves to play with her figures. She has many figures of animals, people and cars. She likes to make them talk and move. She has a big box where she keeps them all.

One day, Lila wants to play with her figures, but she cannot find her box. She looks everywhere in her room, but she cannot see it. She feels sad and angry. She thinks maybe her brother Ben took it. She goes to his room and knocks on his door.

Ben opens the door and says, "What do you want, Lila?" Lila sees her box on his bed. She says, "That is my box! Give it back to me!" Ben says, "No, it is mine now. I like your figures. They are cool." He holds a figure of a lion in his hand.

Lila tries to grab her box, but Ben is stronger. They pull and push and shout. The box falls on the floor and opens. The figures spill out and roll everywhere. Lila and Ben stop fighting and look at the mess. They see that some of the figures are broken. The lion's tail is snapped, the car's wheel is bent, the person's arm is cracked. They feel sorry and scared.

They hear their mom coming up the stairs. She says, "What is going on here? Why are you making so much noise?" Lila and Ben look at each other. They know they are in trouble. They say, "We are sorry, mom. We broke our figures. We were not fit to share." Mom says, "You need to learn to be kind and careful with your toys and with each other. You need to fix your figures and clean up this mess. And you need to say sorry to each other and hug." Lila and Ben nod and do what mom says. They hope their figures will be okay. They hug and say, "We are sorry. We love you." Mom smiles and says, "I love you too."

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play games. Today they play a game with medals. Medals are shiny things that you get when you do something good.

Anna and Ben have three medals each. They put them on a table. They take turns to pick one medal and say why they like it. Anna picks a medal with a star. She says, "I like this medal because it is pretty and it makes me happy." Ben picks a medal with a lion. He says, "I like this medal because it is strong and it makes me brave."

They keep picking medals and saying why they like them. But soon they run out of medals. They look at each other. They are bored. They want to play something else.

"Let's play squeeze," Anna says. "Squeeze is a fun game. We squeeze each other and see who laughs first."

"OK, let's play squeeze," Ben says. "But I will win. I can squeeze harder than you."

They stand up and face each other. They put their arms around each other. They start to squeeze. They squeeze and squeeze and squeeze. They try not to laugh. But they can't help it. They laugh and laugh and laugh. They fall on the floor. They hug each other. They are happy.

They forget about the medals. They don't need them. They have each other. They have fun. They love squeeze.

Terry and David were two best friends. They were always together playing in the park. One hot day, they were both very bored.

Suddenly, Terry had an idea. She said, "Let's go and play hockey. It will be fun!" David smiled and said, "Ok, let's go."

The two friends took their hockey sticks and ran to the hockey field. It was so hot outside, but they didn't care. They were excited to play hockey.

Terry and David played hockey together. They had a lot of fun chasing the hockey ball and running around the field. After playing, they both felt very tired but happy.

Terry and David valued their friendship and recognized that hockey was something that they both truly enjoyed. Playing hockey together had become a special bonding experience for them both.

Once upon a time there was a family of animals. There was a dog, a cat and a bird. They were all very naughty.

One day, the animals were playing together. They were running and jumping and having lots of fun.

Suddenly, the animals heard a noise. It was the sound of a lock. They had found a locked door!

The animals were curious and wanted to open the door. They tried pushing and pulling the door, but it wouldn't open.

Then the bird had an idea. He said to the animals, "Let's all spring together!"

The animals tried the bird's idea. They all jumped together. Sure enough, the lock opened.

The animals cheered as the door sprang open. They were so excited to see what was on the other side!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park. One day, Lily was playing with her toys when she heard a loud noise. She stopped to listen and heard a bird singing in the tree.

As Lily played, she noticed that the leaves on the trees were changing colors. Her mommy told her that it was because the season was changing from summer to fall. Lily thought the orange and yellow leaves were very pretty.

Suddenly, Lily saw an ugly bug crawling on her toy. She didn't like the bug because it was so ugly. She picked up her toy and went to play somewhere else. From then on, Lily always made sure to listen for any bugs before she played with her toys.

Molly was a three-year-old girl who loved playing dress up and looking at herself in the mirror. One day she found a mop in mommy's closet. It didn't belong there, but she thought it was fun to try. She put the mop on her head like a hat and laughed. Molly liked pretending that it was her beautiful princess crown.

Mommy came into the room and saw Molly wearing the mop. "Molly, put that back! It doesn't belong on your head. It's too disgusting!"

Molly was disappointed and sad. She put the mop back in the closet and went to find something else to do. She found her princess dress and put it on, finding happiness in the beauty of her dress.

Mommy smiled seeing Molly twirl in her dress. She hugged her and said, "That's much better! Let's go play." Molly giggled and they played together all afternoon.

Mama and Daddy took little Daniel to a place far away. Daniel had never seen anything like it before!

The place was so icy, it made Daniel shiver. Everywhere he looked, he saw mineral rocks of different shapes and sizes.

"What is this place, Mama?" Daniel asked, standing close to his parents.

"This is a mine, Daniel," Mama said, bending down and brushing the snow off one of the mineral rocks.

"A mine?" Daniel asked.

"Yes, sweetheart," Daddy said. "Miners work hard to design this mineral into useful things. They also dig for other useful minerals which are found here."

Daniel looked amazed. He had never seen something like this before. All the icy mineral rocks were so fascinating!

"Come, let's explore," Daddy said, taking Daniel's hand in his. Together, Daniel and his family explored the mine. They had a great time!

Lily liked to play with her toys, but she was bored of the same ones every day. She wanted something new and fun. One day, she saw a shiny wire on the floor. It looked like a snake, but it did not move. She picked it up and twisted it into different shapes. She made a circle, a star, a heart, and a flower.

She was having fun, but she wanted to show her mom. She ran to the kitchen, where her mom was making lunch. She held up the wire and said, "Look, mom, I made this!"

But her mom did not look. She was busy with the food. She said, "That's nice, Lily, but put that wire down. It's not a toy. It could hurt you."

Lily felt sad. She did not want to put the wire down. She liked it. She wanted her mom to like it too. She said, "But mom, it's not a toy. It's a magic wire. Watch!"

She waved the wire in the air and said, "Abracadabra!" She hoped something would happen. She hoped the wire would do something amazing.

But nothing happened. The wire was still a wire. It was not magic. It was boring. Lily felt even sadder. She dropped the wire on the floor and walked away.

She did not see the wire disappear. She did not see the wire turn into a snake. She did not see the snake slither out of the door. She did not see the snake find a new home in the garden. She did not see the snake make new friends with the birds and the bugs.

She missed the wire. She missed the fun. She missed the magic. She wished she had not dropped the wire. She wished she had kept it. She wished she had seen it disappear.

One day, a child named Alex was playing with their toy. They wanted to show it to their parents, so they ran to them, saying: "See Mom! See Dad! I have a toy!"

The parents smiled, and revealed a surprise - there was another toy, just like the one Alex had!

"Mom and Dad! How?" Alex asked.

"This one is the original," the parents replied. "We made it for you!"

Alex was so delighted. They hugged their parents and thanked them for making such a wonderful present. The parents smiled and hugged back, happy to share the special moment.

Alex carried the original toy with them everywhere, taking special care to keep it safe and sound. They showed it to all their friends, who thought it was the coolest thing they had ever seen!

Alex treasured the original toy - it was a reminder of a special moment spent with their parents, and it revealed a love that could never be broken.

Once upon a time, there was a little pink chair. It was in a big room with many other chairs. The pink chair felt sad because no one would ever seat on it. All the other chairs had friends who would sit and talk, but the pink chair was always alone.

One day, a kind girl named Lily walked into the room. She saw the pink chair and thought it looked lonely. Lily went to the pink chair and said, "Hi, pink chair! Can I seat on you?" The pink chair was so happy and said, "Yes, please! I have been waiting for a friend for a whole year!"

Lily sat on the pink chair and they became the best of friends. But the other chairs in the room became jealous. They did not like that the pink chair was getting all the attention. They started to argue with the pink chair. "Why do you get to have a friend? We want Lily to sit on us too!"

The pink chair did not want to fight. It wanted all the chairs to be friends. So, the pink chair said, "Let's all share! Lily can sit on each of us and we can all be friends!" The other chairs agreed, and from that day on, they all took turns having Lily sit on them. The room was full of happy chairs and they all became friends. And the pink chair was never lonely again.

Then she traveled back home, happy to have gone on such a fun adventure. Everywhere she went, she remembered the big mirror she saw in the forest.

She kept using her blanket to cover her scarf when it got cold, and everyone enjoyed seeing her cute outfit every day. She was so happy that she had found a way to stay warm and look so smart!

One morning, when the sun started to rise, Jim and Joe went for a walk. As they walked, Jim looked up and saw the beautiful sunrise. He could feel the warmth of the sun on his face. It was delicious.

Just then, Jim noticed a delivery truck driving by. A smile appeared on his face. He knew that the truck was delivering something special - it was Jim and Joe's favorite food!

They quickly ran back home, and sure enough - the delivery truck was there. Jim and Joe were so excited they could hardly wait! The driver gave them their food, and they thanked him.

Jim and Joe enjoyed their delicious lunch together in the bright sunshine. They were so happy to be together, and they celebrated the beautiful sunrise in their own special way.

One day, Sarah was feeling a bit adventurous and wanted to make something special. She grabbed some blue markers and started to decorate her coffee cup. She added a few stars, a big blue moon, and lots of swirls. She was very proud of her art when she was finished.

The next morning, Sarah set a pot of coffee on the stove and waited eagerly for it to boil. When the coffee was ready, she carefully poured the steaming liquid into her blue decorated cup. She smiled and wrapped her hands around the warm cup.

Sarah took a sip and was amazed. It was the best cup of coffee she had ever tasted! She smiled and knew that somehow, all the effort she put into decorating the cup had made the coffee extra special.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy old man. Every day he would ignore the people around him and be angry with everyone. One day he went to the market, and saw a big and delicious potato. He couldn't resist it and took the potato without asking.

The potato seller saw it and was very angry. He ran to the man and shouted at him. The man refused to admit that he had taken the potato and acted like he didn't know what was going on.

The potato seller was so mad that he decided the man had to be punished. He took the man to the police station, where the police said that the man had to pay a fine.

The man was so sad because he didn't have enough money to pay the fine. But he was also very wise and knew that if he learned his lesson, he would never do something like this again. The potato seller saw this, and decided to forgive him and let him go.

The man was very happy and thanked the potato seller. From then on the man was nice and friendly to other people, and never acted grumpy again. He learned his lesson, and never took anything without asking first.

Mum was busy teaching Emily the art of baking cakes. Emily was very excited, as baking cakes was her favourite activity. As Mum prepared all the ingredients, Emily paid close attention, wanting to learn all that she could.

"Mum, can I visit the bakery one day?" Emily asked with a broad smile on her face as she stirred the mixture.

Mum stopped what she was doing and looked Emily in the eyes. She replied, "Yes, you may visit the bakery, but for now let us continue teaching you how to make cakes at home."

Emily was very excited and continued mixing the ingredients, but after a while she began to feel a little uncomfortable. She stopped mixing and said, "Mum, I feel a bit uncomfortable can we take a break?"

Mum smiled and gave Emily a hug. "Yes, it's time for us to take a break. Let's go and get some fresh air."

Emily and Mum put on their coats, grabbed a book, and went out to visit the park. At the park they both enjoyed the fresh air, read the book and played some games, until they both forgot all about the feeling of being uncomfortable.

Lily and Sam were at the beach with their mom and dad. They liked to play in the sand and splash in the water. But they were afraid of sharks. They had seen a big shark on TV and it looked scary.

"Mom, are there sharks in the sea?" Lily asked.

"No, honey, there are no sharks here. This is a safe beach. You can swim and have fun," Mom said.

"Are you sure, Mom?" Sam said. "What if a shark comes and bites us?"

"Don't worry, Sam. Sharks don't like noisy places. They like quiet and deep water. And we are very noisy, right?" Mom said.

"Yes, we are!" Lily and Sam said. They laughed and made loud noises. They sang and shouted and clapped their hands.

The shark that was swimming nearby heard the noises. He did not like them. He thought they were annoying and rude. He wanted to swim away and find a peaceful spot. But he also wanted to improve his skills. He wanted to be a fast and strong shark. He wanted to catch more fish and impress his friends.

He decided to swim closer to the beach and see what was making the noises. Maybe he could scare them and make them stop. Maybe he could show them who was the boss of the sea.

He swam faster and faster. He saw the waves and the sand. He saw the people and the toys. He saw Lily and Sam. They were still making noises. They did not see him.

He opened his mouth and showed his teeth. He was ready to attack. He was ready to roar.

But then he felt something. Something hard and sharp. Something that hurt his nose. He stopped and looked. It was a shovel. A metal shovel. Lily had thrown it at him. She had seen him and she was brave.

"Go away, shark! Go away!" Lily yelled. "You are not welcome here! This is our beach!"

The shark was surprised and scared. He had never been hit by a shovel before. He had never been yelled at by a little girl before. He felt pain and shame. He turned around and swam away. He did not want to improve his skills anymore. He just wanted to be alone.

Lily and Sam hugged each other. They were happy and proud. They had chased away the shark. They had saved the beach.

"Wow, Lily, you are amazing!" Sam said.

"Thank you, Sam. You are amazing too!" Lily said.

They ran to their mom and dad and told them what had happened. Mom and dad were shocked and proud. They hugged and kissed them. They said they were very brave and smart.

They decided to leave the beach and go to the ice cream shop. They wanted to celebrate and have a treat. They did not want to see any more sharks. They had had enough of them.

They walked away from the water and the sand. They did not hear the shark. He was still swimming away. He was still feeling sorry for himself. He did not like noisy places. He did not like shovels. He did not like Lily and Sam. He did not like the beach.

Once upon a time, there was a happy dog. His name was Max. Max liked to play with his ball and chew on bones. One day, Max saw a big envelope on the ground. It was yellow and had a smiley face on it. Max was curious, so he sniffed it. Then he picked it up in his mouth and ran to his owner.

Max's owner was very cheerful when she saw the envelope. She opened it and saw a letter from her friend. Her friend invited her to a parade. Max's owner was so excited, she started to march around the room. Max barked and wagged his tail. He was happy too. They went to the parade and saw lots of people and animals. Max loved the music and the colorful floats. They had a great time together. The end.

The shark was very scary and Nemo was afraid. Suddenly, a hero fish came to rescue Nemo from the shark. The hero fish was very brave and strong. Once upon a time, there was a fluffy sheep named Fred. He was kind and gentle and always wearing his warm wool coat. One day, Fred was taking his afternoon nap in the sunny meadow when all of a sudden he heard someone calling his name.

"Fred, you must hurry!" It was Farmer Brown. He was standing at the edge of the meadow with a basket in his hands.

"Something rather exciting has happened," said Farmer Brown with a twinkle in his eyes. "I found a graceful butterfly and I need your wool coat so that I can carry it without harming it."

Fred was hesitant. He had never been without his wool coat before and he was scared that he would be cold. But Farmer Brown reassured him that he wouldn't be cold and that his coat would help the butterfly.

So Fred took a deep breath and removed his wool coat. He handed it to Farmer Brown and watched as he gently wrapped the butterfly in it. Fred smiled as he watched Farmer Brown hurry off with the butterfly safely tucked in his coat.

One day, they see a squirrel on the lumber. It has a big, bushy tail and shiny, black eyes. It looks at them and makes a funny noise. Tom and Mia want to pet the squirrel. They run to the lumber and try to catch it.

But the squirrel is very fast and smart. It jumps from one piece of lumber to another, and then to a tree. Tom and Mia follow it, but they are not careful. They knock down some of the lumber and make a big, messy pile. They also get splinters and scratches on their hands and legs.

Dad hears the noise and comes to the yard. He sees the mess and the kids. He is not happy. He tells them to stop chasing the squirrel and to clean up the lumber. He also gives them band-aids and hugs. He says they can play with the lumber, but they have to be gentle and safe.

Tom and Mia say they are sorry and they help Dad. They also say goodbye to the squirrel, who is still in the tree. They hope it will come back another day, but they will not run after it. They will just watch it and smile.

Once upon a time there was an owl. He was hidden up in a tree, playing a game of hide and seek with himself. He was so small that no one could see him.

Then one day he heard a noise. He looked around and saw a big thing coming towards him. He was scared but then he realised that it was just the wind.

The wind was so strong that it started to cover the owl. He was scared and tried to fly away, but the wind was too strong and it kept covering him. He was so scared that he closed his eyes tight.

After a few minutes the wind stopped and the owl opened his eyes. He found that the wind had covered him with thin leaves. He was safe, happy and hidden again.

Then one day, he saw a plate full of delicious cakes on the table in the middle of the room. He had never seen cakes before, so he was even shyer than before. He wanted to try one, but he was too scared.

His mummy saw him looking at the cakes and said, "Go on Mark, why don't you try one?" But Mark was still too scared. He didn't think he would fit in if he ate one.

So Mark's mummy said, "It's okay Mark, you can have one if you want." But Mark still didn't move. He looked so sad and shy that his mummy just gave him a kiss and said, "Maybe next time."

Unfortunately, that next time never came. Mark never tasted the cakes and he never managed to fit in. He stayed in the corner all his life, watching the others and wishing he could be brave enough to join in.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and look at the stars. One day, she saw a reindeer in her backyard. The reindeer had a red nose and was very friendly. Lily was so happy to see the reindeer and they became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a small dog named Lola. Every day she would go to the park and play fetch with her owner.

One day, it was very hot outside. Lola was so hot, she started to pant and lose her energy. Then she saw a nice patch of dust near the tree.

"Oh, this dust looks so cool, Lola thought.

She ran into the dust and started to play. She jumped and rolled, feeling so happy.

But soon she realised that she had lost her favourite ball in the dust. She looked everywhere, but couldn't find it.

"Lola, what's wrong? her owner said.

"I lost my ball, Lola said sadly.

"Don't worry. Look, here it is! said Lola's owner, picking up her ball from the dust.

Lola was so happy. She wagged her tail and said, "Thanks for finding my ball!

And that's how Lola didn't lose her ball in the hot dust.

"I stumbled," whimpered Little Billy, "on the stones."

Once upon a time, there was a wide bear. The bear liked to spray water all the time. He liked to do it with his trunk and his tail. One day, the bear decided to spray water everywhere, even in the sky. He sprayed it so high that it made it rain. The rain made the bear very happy and he danced and played around all day in the rain. When the rain stopped, the bear was still happy because all he needed to do was spray some more water. He sprayed and sprayed until he was very tired and went to sleep. The end.

Sara and Ben are friends. They like to walk in the park with their moms. The park is big and green. There are many trees and flowers. The park is peaceful.

One day, Sara and Ben see a big apple tree. The apples are red and shiny. Sara and Ben want to pick some apples. They ask their moms.

"Mom, can we pick some apples?" Sara says.

"Mom, can we pick some apples?" Ben says.

Their moms nod and smile. They say, "Yes, you can pick some apples. But be careful. Don't climb too high. And don't eat too many."

Sara and Ben are happy. They run to the apple tree. They pick some apples. They put them in a basket. They share some apples with their moms. The apples are sweet and juicy.

Sara and Ben say, "Thank you, apple tree. Thank you, mom."

They hug their moms. They hug each other. They are happy and peaceful. They walk back home with their moms. They have a good day.

John and his mommy were walking through the store. He saw something he wanted and asked, "Mommy, can I have that yogurt?" His mommy said, "Let's discuss it". They went over to the refrigerator and looked at all the yogurt. Finally, John spotted a big strawberry yogurt. It looked so good and it was hot and gooey. He knew he had to try it. His mommy said, "Ok, since it's hot outside and this yogurt looks yummy, you can have one". He was so excited! He had the yogurt and it was the best snack ever. He thanked his mommy and they went home.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a bossy big sister named Sarah who always told her what to do. One night, Sarah told Lily it was time to get into bed.

Lily didn't want to go to bed yet, but Sarah insisted. "You need to get some sleep so you can grow big and strong," she said.

Lily reluctantly climbed into bed and Sarah tucked her in. "Goodnight, Lily," she said. "Sweet dreams."

But Lily couldn't sleep. She tossed and turned, thinking about all the fun things she wanted to do instead of going to bed. Finally, she called out to Sarah, "I can't get to sleep!"

Sarah came back into the room and sat on the edge of Lily's bed. "What's wrong?" she asked.

"I don't know," Lily said. "I just can't get sleepy."

Sarah smiled. "I know something that might help," she said. "Let's sing a lullaby together." And so they sang a soft, sweet song until Lily's eyes grew heavy and she drifted off to sleep.

Lily and Ben like to play with their toys in the living room. They have many toys: cars, dolls, blocks, books and more. But sometimes they make a big mess and Mom is not happy.

One day, Mom says, "Lily and Ben, you need to organize your toys. Put them in the boxes and on the shelves. Then you can have a snack."

Lily and Ben nod and start to organize their toys. Lily puts the dolls in a pink box and the books on a shelf. Ben puts the cars in a blue box and the blocks on another shelf. They work hard and fast.

But then they see an orange chair in the corner. It is a new chair that Mom bought for them. It is soft and cozy and has a picture of a lion on it.

"Wow, look at the orange chair!" Lily says. "It is so pretty and fun!"

"Can we sit on it?" Ben asks.

"Sure, let's try it!" Lily says.

They run to the orange chair and climb on it. They hug the lion and giggle. They forget about organizing their toys.

Mom comes back and sees the orange chair. She also sees the toys on the floor.

"Lily and Ben, what are you doing?" Mom asks. "You did not organize your toys. You only played on the orange chair."

Lily and Ben look at Mom. They feel sorry.

"We are sorry, Mom," Lily says. "We like the orange chair so much. It is our favorite toy."

Mom smiles. She understands. She likes the orange chair too.

"It is okay, Lily and Ben," Mom says. "The orange chair is very nice. But you still need to organize your toys. How about we do it together? Then we can have a snack and sit on the orange chair."

Lily and Ben agree. They get off the orange chair and help Mom organize their toys. They put them in the boxes and on the shelves. They make the living room neat and tidy.

Then they have a snack and sit on the orange chair. They hug the lion and Mom. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Julie. She was three years old. One day, Julie was in school, playing with her friends. She was using her eraser to draw a picture. She needed more space, so she asked her friend for help.

She said to her friend, "Can I borrow your eraser? Mine is too little." Her friend smiled and said, "Yes, you can borrow my eraser." Julie took her friend's eraser and started to draw.

She had lots of fun drawing and coloring with the eraser. She thanked her friend for letting her borrow it. Then, when she was finished, she gave the eraser back to her friend. Julie's friend smiled and thanked her for taking care of it.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a brave boy named Timmy. Timmy lived in a little hut in the forest with his mommy and daddy. One day, Timmy went for a walk in the woods. He saw a big bird in the sky and observed it flying.

As Timmy was walking, he heard a loud noise. He went to investigate and saw a squirrel stuck in a tree. Timmy was brave and climbed up the tree to rescue the squirrel. The squirrel was very happy and thanked Timmy by giving him a nut.

After the adventure, Timmy went back to his hut and told his mommy and daddy about what happened. They were proud of him for being so brave and kind to the squirrel. From that day on, Timmy made sure to observe everything around him and help anyone who needed it.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to collect pennies. One day, she saw a man who looked sad. She wanted to make him happy, so she gave him a penny. The man smiled and said thank you.

Later that day, Lily went to a candy store. She saw a sweet candy that she really wanted. She asked her mom to buy it for her, but her mom said no. Lily remembered the man she gave the penny to and thought that maybe he needed it more than she needed the candy.

Lily went back to find the man, but he was gone. She felt sad and disappointed. On her way home, she saw a boy who was crying. She asked him what was wrong, and he said he lost his penny. Lily remembered the penny she gave to the man and thought maybe it was the boy's penny.

She gave the boy the penny and he stopped crying. But when Lily got home, she realized that she didn't have any pennies left to add to her collection. She felt sad and wished she didn't give away her pennies. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a chubby bunny. She was so chubby she couldn't wear regular socks. So she found some chubby socks and put them on.

"I wonder where I can find more chubby socks?" the bunny asked her friend.

Her friend smiled. "Let's examine all of these socks," he said, pointing to a pile of socks.

The bunnies sifted through the pile of socks and found a few more chubby socks.

"Yes!" the bunny said, hopping up and down. "Now I have enough chubby socks!"

The bunny grabbed all of her chubby socks and hopped away, happy and content. The end!

The bird flew around the tree and the little girl followed it. The bird's wings were strong and it landed on one of the tree's branches. The little girl was delighted and wanted to continue exploring. She wanted to say hello to the bird, but it flew away.

The little girl was sad but she had an idea. She asked her dad for help and he gave her a ladder. She carried the ladder to the modern tree and tried to reach the bird's nest. She was so close. She climbed just a bit higher and there it was - the bird's nest! She was so happy that she had solved the mystery.

The little girl thanked her dad for helping her and went to bed with a smile on her face. The modern tree was her new best friend.

Once upon a time, there was a dependable doctor named Dr. Brown. He was very kind and loved to help people feel better. One day, a little girl named Lily came to see Dr. Brown. She had a big boo-boo on her knee.

"Ouch!" cried Lily. "Can you heal my boo-boo, Dr. Brown?"

"Of course, I can help you," said Dr. Brown with a big smile. He gently put a soft bandage on Lily's knee. "There you go, all better now!"

Lily felt so much better and smiled back at Dr. Brown. She thanked him and gave him a big hug. From that day on, everyone knew that Dr. Brown was the best and most dependable doctor in the town. And they all lived happily ever after.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tommy. Tommy was three years old and he loved playing with his toys.
One day, Tommy was playing with his toys when he came across a strange looking grape. It was smooth and purple and Tommy had never seen one like it before. He was about to pick it up, when he heard a voice.
"Please pause," it said. "That's my grape!".
Tommy looked up and saw a smiling old lady.
"This is my special grape," she said. "It tastes delicious and I want to share with you".
Tommy smiled and paused. He reached out and took the grape from the lady's hand. As Tommy took a bite, he felt its sweetness and smoothness. He loved it!

The old lady smiled and watched Tommy happily eat the grape. "Thank you," she said.

Tommy smiled and then waved goodbye to the old lady as he skipped away to play with his toys. From then on, Tommy always paused and enjoyed a special grape when the old lady was around.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and loved to explore the forest near her house. It was a beautiful place full of singing birds and buzzing bees.

One day, Jane went into the forest but it felt different. The birds were quiet and the air was still. Jane grew fearful, so she started to walk back home. Suddenly, she heard a deep, loud growl coming from behind her.

She stopped in her tracks and turned around. There stood a big, mean bear. Jane froze with fear. She was so scared that she couldn't move.

The bear stared her menacingly. Jane wanted to escape, but she was too scared to move. Then she heard a voice in her head. It said, "Rest, Jane. You don't need to be afraid."

Jane cautiously closed her eyes and took a few deep breaths. When she opened them again, the bear was gone. She had used her courage and found a way to escape!

Rose was scared, but she was determined. She slowly started to climb the slide, clinging to each step until she reached the top. At the top, she felt a sense of victory.

She had done what she set out to do. She had changed and achieved a victory! Rose felt proud of herself and jumped down with a big smile on her face.

Once upon a time, there were two friends: John and Mary. They were always playing together and having lots of fun. One day, John thought of a game to play. He said: "Let's play hide and seek behind the blanket!".

Mary smiled and said "Ok!" But, then she shook her head and said "No, I don't want to".

John asked: "Why not?".

Mary frowned and said: "I think it's a dull game. I don't want to play".

John disagreed. He said: "No, it's a great game. Let's try it. I'm sure it will be fun!".

Mary thought for a moment and then said: "I don't know. I'm feeling a bit calm now. I'd rather just sit with my blanket and read a book".

John smiled and said: "Ok! We can do that! Let's just sit together and read".

Mary smiled and said "Yes, that sounds wonderful to me".

So, John and Mary agreed to enjoy a calm afternoon together, reading and snuggling under Mary's blanket.

"Are you ready, Lara?" asked her mom.

Lara nodded.
"Let's go!" said her mom.

So they drove and drove, until they reached their destination. There was a big ocean. Lara looked around and saw all the boats.

"Let's go on a boat, Mom!" said Lara, excited.

They climbed on board and they went out on the sea. The boat went faster and faster until it suddenly stopped.

"Lara, look up," said her mom.

Lara looked up and saw a big castle on the beach.

"That's where we're going," said her mom.

They got off the boat and ran across the beach. The sand was dry and the sun was setting.

Lara's mom put her hand on her shoulder.

"We made it, Lara," she said. "Welcome to your special adventure!"

Ann said, "Let's take turns pushing it around the yard!"

So Johnny and Ann took turns pushing the wheel around the yard. Johnny was careful not to bump into anything, but Ann was a bit careless.

After a while, Ann bumped into a tree and lost her balance. She fell over and started to cry. Johnny felt really bad and wanted to help.

He ran over to Ann and gave her a big hug. While they were hugging, Johnny gently pushed the wheel away so it wouldn't hurt her anymore.

Ann smiled at Johnny, and they went back to playing, taking turns pushing the big wheel all around the yard.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Ellie. She loved to explore the forest near her house. One day she went out to explore, and soon she found an acorn on the ground. She liked the way the acorn felt when she rubbed it, so she started to rub it harder and harder.

"Why do you keep rubbing that acorn?" an old man asked.

Ellie looked up. "I like how it feels," she said.

"That's very persistent of you," the old man said.

The old man smiled and nodded, then he took the acorn from her and put it in his pocket. "Maybe you'll see it again one day," he said mysteriously, before turning and walking away.

When Ellie got home, she asked her mom about the old man. Her mom smiled and said that one day, as she was about to give up, she had found a big oak tree in that very same spot where Ellie found the acorn. Ellie couldn't believe what she was hearing! The old man knew that if she kept rubbing the acorn, something special would happen.

From that day on, Ellie never gave up when she encountered something difficult; she knew that putting in a little bit of effort could have an amazing payoff.

Lila was a girl who liked to play with her toys. She had many toys, but her favorite was a doll house. The doll house was very big and had many rooms and things. Lila liked to pretend that she lived in the doll house with her dolls. She wished that she could shrink and go inside the doll house.

One day, she found a magic ring in her room. The ring had a button that said "shrink". Lila was curious and put the ring on her finger. She pressed the button and felt herself getting smaller and smaller. She was so happy that she could go inside the doll house. She ran to the doll house and opened the door.

She saw that the doll house was like a country. There were many dolls and animals and cars and trees. Lila thought that it was perfect. She played with the dolls and animals and cars and trees. She had a lot of fun. She forgot about the ring and the time.

After a while, she heard her mom calling her name. She realized that she had to go back to her room. She looked for the ring, but she could not find it. She had lost it somewhere in the doll house. She was scared and sad. She did not know how to get big again. She cried and cried.

She heard a voice saying "Don't cry, Lila. I can help you." She looked up and saw a fairy. The fairy had the ring in her hand. She said "I found your ring in the garden. You were very careless. You should not play with things that you don't understand. You could have been stuck in the doll house forever. You should be happy with what you have and not wish for things that are not real. The doll house is not perfect. It is only a toy. Your real home and family are perfect for you. They love you and take care of you. You should love them and take care of them too. Here, take your ring and press the button. You will get big again and go back to your room."

Lila took the ring and thanked the fairy. She pressed the button and felt herself getting bigger and bigger. She was back in her room. She saw her mom standing at the door. She ran to her mom and hugged her. She said "I'm sorry, mom. I love you. I don't want to shrink and go inside the doll house anymore. I want to stay with you and play with my toys." Her mom smiled and hugged her back. She said "I love you too, Lila. I'm glad you are back. You learned a valuable lesson today. You should always be careful and grateful. Now, let's go and have some cookies and milk."

Lila nodded and followed her mom. She looked at the doll house and said "Goodbye, doll house. You are a nice toy, but you are not my home. My home is here with my mom and dad and brother and sister. They are my perfect country." She smiled and went to have some cookies and milk. She was happy and wise.

Once upon a time there was a man. He was a fisherman. Every morning he woke up early. He went to the lake and he put on his fishing hat. Then he went fishing. He was always so restless!

One day the wind blew stronger than normal. He cast his line in the water but the wind was too strong and he couldn't keep the line in place.

He looked up. Above the lake was a big grey cloud. Suddenly, a bright light shone out from the cloud and flew down to the lake. It made the fisherman feel very calm and relaxed.

The fisherman was becoming something new. He was becoming a magical fisherman! He was still restless but he used his magic to help catch lots of fish. He was happy and had lots of fun.

They start to wrap the green yarn around a stick. They make a long and thin snake. They give it eyes and a tongue with other colors of yarn. They are happy with their snake. They hold it up and make it move.

They go to show their snake to Lily's mom. They say, "Look, mom, we made a snake with the green yarn. Do you like it?" Lily's mom looks at the snake. She smiles and says, "It is a very nice snake. But it is not very scary. It is very cute."

Lily and Ben look at each other. They think their snake is scary. They say, "No, mom, it is scary. Look, it can hiss and bite." They make the snake hiss and bite in the air. They try to scare Lily's mom.

Then, the bird flew back up into the sky and saw a rainbow. It was so pretty! The bird flew closer and closer until it was right by the rainbow. Once there was a bunny who had been walking for hours. He was tired and needed shelter from the bad weather. He tried and tried to find a place to rest, but he didn't see anything.

Then, he saw a big tree with lots of leaves. He thought it might make a good shelter. He hopped to the tree and nestled himself in between its branches. He felt so cozy and warm and was very thankful.

The bunny tried to be thoughtful and made the tree a special home. He bundled up leaves to make a comfortable bed. He decorated the tree with berries, twigs and stones that he found. He made a nice little door on the side.

The bunny was very happy to have a special home for himself. He was very thankful for his thoughtfulness in creating the shelter. And he was glad he was able to finally rest from his long journey.

Lily was feeling miserable that day. She was so sad that her brows were scrunched together and her mouth made a big, unhappy frown. She walked into her kitchen to find her mom, who asked, "Lily, what's the matter?"

Lily sighed, "I'm so sad. I want something yummy to eat." Lily's mom smiled. She reached down into the cupboard and pulled out a tin of tuna. She opened it and scooped some onto a plate for Lily.

Lily's frown slowly turned upside-down. "Tuna!" she exclaimed. She started to eat, and before long, her smile was back. "Thank you so much, Mom!" she said, and gave her mom a big hug.

Lily was no longer feeling miserable. She was happy because her mom had given her a tasty, snack.
