Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy had a big, hairy dog named Max. Max loved to run and play with Timmy, but he also loved to chew on things he shouldn't. One day, Timmy's mom came home with a new toy for Timmy. It was a shiny red car with wheels that spun round and round. Timmy was so happy to have the new toy, but Max was curious too. Max sniffed and licked the car until he accidentally cut it with his sharp teeth. Timmy was so sad and angry that Max had ruined his new toy. He cried and yelled at Max, but Max just looked at him with his big, sad eyes. Timmy's mom saw what had happened and she knew what to do. She took Max to the well in the backyard and gave him a big drink of water. Max was so happy and grateful that he licked Timmy's face and wagged his tail. From that day on, Timmy and Max were best friends again, and Timmy learned that sometimes accidents happen and it's important to forgive and forget.

Lily and Tom were friends. They liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big dog. The dog was crazy. It ran and jumped and barked.

"Wow, look at that dog!" Tom said. "It is so fast and loud!"

Lily was scared. She hid behind Tom. "I don't like that dog," she said. "It is too crazy. It might bite us."

The dog saw Lily and Tom. It ran to them. It wagged its tail and licked their faces.

"Hi, hi, hi!" the dog said. "You are my new friends! I am happy to see you! Welcome to my park!"

Lily and Tom were surprised. The dog was not mean. It was friendly. It just wanted to play.

"Hi, dog," Tom said. "What is your name?"

"My name is Spot," the dog said. "I live with my family. They are nice, but they are busy. They don't have time to play with me. So I come to the park to find friends."

Lily and Tom felt sorry for Spot. They decided to play with him. They threw a ball and Spot fetched it. They ran and Spot chased them. They laughed and Spot barked.

They had fun. They forgot that Spot was crazy. They liked him. He liked them.

They found a new way to play in the park. They found a new friend. They were happy.

Anna and Ben were playing in the park. They saw a big kangaroo. The kangaroo had a baby in her pouch. Anna and Ben wanted to see the baby.

"Can we touch the baby?" Anna asked the kangaroo.

The kangaroo shook her head. She did not want the kids to touch her baby. She was scared they would hurt him.

"Please, please, please!" Ben begged. He did not listen to the kangaroo. He ran to her and tried to pull the baby out of the pouch.

The kangaroo was angry. She slapped Ben with her paw. Ben fell to the ground. He cried and cried.

"Ow, ow, ow!" Ben said. "The kangaroo is mean! She slapped me!"

Anna ran to Ben. She hugged him and said sorry.

"You are stupid, Ben!" Anna said. "You should not touch the baby. The kangaroo is not mean. She is a good mom. She loves her baby. She wants to keep him safe. You should say sorry to the kangaroo."

Ben looked at the kangaroo. She was still shaking her head. She was still scared. Ben felt bad. He said sorry to the kangaroo.

"I am sorry, kangaroo," Ben said. "I did not mean to hurt your baby. You are a good mom. You are not mean. You are nice."

The kangaroo nodded. She smiled a little. She was happy that Ben said sorry. She let Anna and Ben see her baby. The baby was cute. He had big ears and a long tail. Anna and Ben smiled too. They said thank you to the kangaroo.

The kangaroo hopped away with her baby. Anna and Ben waved goodbye. They learned a lesson. They learned to be kind and respectful to animals. They learned not to be stupid.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Jack. He wanted a taxi all to himself! So one day, Jack decided to design his own taxi. He felt so proud of what he had created. He could go wherever he wanted-whenever he wanted!

His younger brother, Tom, was very jealous. Tom wanted a taxi too, but he just couldn't find the time to build one. Every day, he watched Jack get into his own taxi and roll right off into the city.

Finally, Tom got an idea. He decided to design a taxi just like Jack's! He worked hard and after several days, Tom had his own taxi that was just as nice as Jack's.

Tom was so proud of his accomplishment. Now both he and Jack could take their own taxis around town together. Jack was very happy to share his taxi with Tom, but deep down he was still a bit jealous.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Tom and Lucy.

One day, they decided to go on a search for some fruit. Tom and Lucy went to the garden and began to look around.

Tom said, "Let's go and search for some fruit!"

Lucy replied, "Good idea, let's go!"

So, the two friends searched around for some fruit.

After a while, Lucy found an apple tree with some beautiful red apples! She called to Tom, "Hey, look! I found some fruit!"

Tom heard Lucy and dashed over, but when he got there, all the apples were gone!

Disappointed, Tom said, "Why did the apples all disappear?"

Lucy replied, "Maybe a bird was hungry and ate them all. Let's try to find something else."

Tom and Lucy continued their search, but all the fruit they could find was too hard. It was impossible to eat.

Finally, they got back to the apple tree and Tom noticed something. There were some small green apples still hanging in the branches.

Tom smiled. He said, "Look! These ones are soft, we can eat them!"

Tom and Lucy both eagerly plucked the soft green apples off the tree and enjoyed them together.

It was a happy ending to their search for fruit!

Once upon a time there was a little girl who dreamed of visiting a mysterious land. One day, her wish came true and she flew away in a magic balloon. The sky was beautiful and the girl felt so dizzy from the excitement.

When she arrived in the mysterious land, the first thing she saw was a castle. She had no idea that the castle belonged to a wicked witch! The witch was not very pleased with the girl coming to her land, so she warned her not to behave badly.

The little girl was a bit scared, but she was also very curious. She started exploring the land and she saw lots of wonderful things. Suddenly, the ground started shaking and the witch came out of the castle. The girl felt dizzy and her heart was racing - she did not know what to do!

The witch told her that she had been a good guest and she was allowed to stay if she promised to continue behaving. The little girl was relieved and she said she would be ever so careful. She thanked the witch and from that day on, she had many wonderful adventures in the magical land.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing with her rubber ball. One day, Lily was playing with her ball when something bad happened. She accidentally threw the ball too hard and it hit her friend's face. Her friend started to cry because it hurt a lot.

Lily felt very sad and apologized to her friend. She knew it was her fault that her friend got hurt. Lily's friend forgave her and they hugged. From that day on, Lily learned to be more careful when playing with her rubber ball. She never wanted to hurt anyone again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside and explore the world around her. One day, she was walking in the park when she met a friendly dog named Max.

Max said, "Hi there! Can I recommend a fun way to play with me?"

Lily replied, "Sure! What's the helpful way to play?"

Max wagged his tail and said, "We can play fetch with this ball! You throw it and I'll bring it back to you."

Lily giggled with delight and said, "That sounds like a great way to play! Let's do it!" And so, they played together until the sun went down. From that day on, Lily and Max were the best of friends and always found helpful ways to play together.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing with her dollhouse and her toy animals. One day, she went outside to play with her friends. They played hide-and-seek and had a great time.

After a while, it was time for Lily to go back inside. She was so tired that she forgot to bring her favorite doll with her. She only realized this when she wanted to play with her again later that day.

Lily searched everywhere, but she couldn't find her doll. She was very sad and didn't know what to do. Her mom saw her crying and asked her what was wrong. Lily told her that she had forgotten her doll outside and couldn't find her.

Her mom went outside and looked around. After a while, she found the doll lying under a bush. Lily was so happy and hugged her mom. She said, "Thank you, Mommy! You are great at finding things!"

Once upon a time, there was a clever fox. He liked to listen to the birds singing on the branch of a big tree. One day, the fox saw a juicy fruit on the branch. He wanted to eat it, but he couldn't reach it. So, he thought of a clever plan. He listened carefully to the sound of the wind and jumped high to catch the branch. The fruit fell down and the fox happily ate it. From that day on, he always listened to the wind before jumping. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat pepper on her food. One day, she was eating her dinner when her pepper suddenly vanished from her plate. She looked around but couldn't find it anywhere.

Lily asked her mom, "Where did my pepper go?" Her mom looked serious and said, "I don't know, Lily. Maybe it vanished into thin air." Lily didn't understand what that meant but she knew her pepper was gone.

The next day, Lily was playing with her toys when her missing pepper suddenly appeared in front of her. She was so happy to have it back and ran to her mom to tell her the good news. Her mom was surprised but happy that Lily had found her pepper. From that day on, Lily made sure to keep a close eye on her pepper at all times.

One day, a little bird named Tim wanted to rise high in the sky. He wanted to see how high he could go. So, he flapped his wings and began to rise up, up, up.

As Tim rose higher, he met a big, comfortable cloud named Fluffy. Fluffy was soft and white. "Hello, little bird," Fluffy said. "Do you want to rest on me?" Tim was tired, so he said, "Yes, thank you!" Tim landed on Fluffy and felt very comfortable.

After resting, Tim was ready to rise again. He said goodbye to Fluffy and flew higher in the sky. Tim was so happy to rise and see the world below him. And he knew that whenever he needed a rest, his friend Fluffy would be there to help him.

Once, there was a famous quick rabbit named Jack. He had a grey and white fur, and long ears so he could hear everything. He lived in a big garden with a lot of other animals.

One day, Jack was hopping around the garden when he noticed he was very alone. He couldn't find any of his friends.

He called out "Where is everybody?"

Then, Jack heard a voice coming from the nearby tree. It was a wise old owl perched on a branch.

"My dear friend, something special is happening. All of your friends have gone to the fair. You must have missed it when you were hopping around."

Jack was sad he missed the fair, but was excited to know there would be more in the future. He promised himself he would never miss another!

Once upon a time, there was a bossy wife named Sally. Sally always told her husband what to do. One day, Sally and her husband were laying in bed.

Sally said, "Honey, can you please get me some water?"

Her husband replied, "Sure, my love."

Sally said, "And can you also get me my favorite book?"

Her husband said, "Of course, dear."

Sally was happy that her husband did what she asked, but she realized she was being too bossy. She said, "I'm sorry for being bossy. Thank you for being so nice to me."

Her husband smiled and said, "I love you, Sally."

Once upon a time, there was a big lion in the jungle. He was very strong and had a loud roar. All the animals were scared of him. One day, a little bird named Bluey flew by and saw the lion. Bluey was not scared and said, "Hello, Mr. Lion! What are you doing?"

The lion replied, "I am waiting for my lunch. I want to score a big meal today." Bluey thought for a moment and said, "I know where there are some colorful fruits that you might like. Follow me!" The lion was surprised but curious, so he followed Bluey.

As they walked, the lion saw a big tree with lots of colorful fruits. He was so happy and thanked Bluey. They became friends and the lion learned that not all animals are scary. From that day on, he never roared at anyone again.

Anna had a big box of toys. She liked to play with them, but she had too many. She wanted to sell some of them and buy a new book. She asked her mom to help her.

They put a sign on the lawn that said "Toy Sale". They put the toys on a table. Some people came and looked at the toys. They gave Anna coins for the toys they liked. Anna put the coins in a jar.

One of the toys was a teddy bear. It was brown and soft. Anna loved the teddy bear. It was her loyal friend. She hugged it every night. She did not want to sell it.

A boy came and saw the teddy bear. He wanted it. He gave Anna a coin. Anna took the coin, but she felt sad. She gave the boy the teddy bear. The boy smiled and ran away.

Anna started to cry. She missed her teddy bear. She wanted it back. She told her mom. Her mom hugged her and said, "I'm sorry, Anna. You can't get it back. You sold it. But you have many other toys and a new book. And you have me. I'm your loyal friend too."

Anna nodded. She wiped her tears. She looked at the jar of coins. She had enough to buy the book. She smiled. She said, "Thank you, mom. You're right. I have many things to be happy about. And maybe the boy will love the teddy bear as much as I did."

Anna and Ben were playing in the sand. They liked to make castles and dig holes. They had a big bucket of water to fill their holes and make the sand wet.

"Look, I made an oasis!" Ben said, pointing to a hole with water and some leaves. "It is a place where animals can drink and rest in the desert."

"That is nice, Ben," Anna said. "But I want to make a bigger oasis. Can I have some of your water?"

"No, Anna, this is my water. You have your own bucket. Go away!" Ben said, holding his bucket tightly.

Anna was angry. She wanted to share with Ben, but he was being mean. She tried to grab his bucket, but he pushed her away. They started to fight over the water.

"Stop it, you two!" Mom said, coming over. "Why are you fighting? There is enough water for both of you."

"Ben won't let me use his water, Mom. He is selfish!" Anna said, crying.

"Anna tried to take my water, Mom. She is greedy!" Ben said, pouting.

Mom sighed. She looked at the sand and saw their holes and castles. She had an idea.

"Why don't you work together and make a big oasis for both of you?" she said. "You can fill your holes with water and put some plants and rocks and toys. Then you can pretend you are brave explorers who found a secret oasis in the desert. Wouldn't that be fun?"

Anna and Ben looked at each other. They felt sorry for fighting. They nodded and smiled.

"OK, Mom, we will try that," Anna said.

"Sorry, Anna, I will share with you," Ben said.

They hugged and said sorry. Then they took their buckets and started to fill their holes with water. They added some plants and rocks and toys. They made a big oasis that looked beautiful.

"Wow, this is the best oasis ever!" Anna said.

"Yes, it is. And we made it together!" Ben said.

They played happily in their oasis. They pretended they were brave explorers who found a secret oasis in the desert. They had fun and forgot their fight. Mom watched them and smiled. She was proud of them.

Once upon a time, there was a nosy little boy called Ryan. He loved to explore the beach and collect shells.

One day, Ryan met a kind surfer. He showed Ryan how to ride the waves. Ryan was so excited and wanted to do it again.

But then the surfer warned Ryan to look out for the tide. He said it would come in and release the surfers back to shore.

So Ryan watched the tide come in. He saw it carry the surfers up the beach and away. He thought to himself, "I must remember to watch out for the tide next time".

And so the nosy little boy played happily at the beach, while always keeping a careful eye out for the tide.

Tom and Sue were playing in the park. They saw a big bird with brown feathers and a red thing on its head. It was a turkey.

"Hello, turkey!" Tom said. "Do you want to play with us?"

The turkey did not say anything. It just looked at them with its small eyes. It was mysterious.

Sue was curious. She wanted to touch the turkey. She walked slowly to the bird. She reached out her hand.

"Be careful, Sue!" Tom said. "The turkey might bite you!"

But the turkey did not bite Sue. It let her pet its feathers. It made a funny sound. It was friendly.

Tom and Sue smiled. They liked the turkey. They gave it some bread and water. They played with it for a while.

Then they heard their mom calling them. It was time to go home.

"Bye, turkey!" Tom and Sue said. "We will come back tomorrow!"

The turkey nodded. It watched them leave. It was happy.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a little puppy. She was a very persistent pup, but she wasn't sure what to do. Then one day, she saw a spot on the ground. It looked like a little pile of dirt and stones.

The pup started to mix them together with her nose. Once done, the pup started to play and was so happy.

But then the pup started to hear her mommy's voice in the distance saying, "Let's go, puppy."

The pup was sad and started to cry, but her mommy said, "You were so persistent with that spot. Look what you made!"

The pup looked and saw a beautiful little flower garden! She was so happy that she had mixed the dirt and stones together.

From that day on, the little puppy mixed up dirt and stones every day to make something beautiful.

Once upon a time, there was a shy little bunny named Benny. One day, Benny was hopping around in the grass when he saw a big carrot. He wanted to take it home to his mom, but it was too heavy for him to carry.

Just then, he saw a friendly mouse named Mickey. Benny asked, "Can you help me carry this carrot to my mom?" Mickey replied, "Of course, I can carry it for you!"

As they were walking, they saw a big, scary cat. The cat wanted to eat the carrot and Benny and Mickey too! Benny was so scared, he hid behind Mickey. But Mickey was brave and said, "You can't have the carrot or hurt my friends!"

The cat was so surprised by Mickey's bravery that he ran away. Benny was so happy and said, "Thank you for helping me carry the carrot and protecting me from the cat!" Mickey replied, "Anytime, my friend!" And they both hopped away happily.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, a girl and a boy. They were playing together in the park. Suddenly an unexpected surprise appeared! It was a big white swan. The swan had a wide wingspan and it wiggled its wings and waved at the two friends. The girl and the boy were so excited that they ran to give the swan a hug.

The swan happily accepted and gave them a ride, while they laughed and waved at all the other people in the park. After a while the swan had to go and so the two friends said goodbye sadly.

The girl and the boy learnt an important lesson that day. They learnt that surprises are good and that it is important to take the time to be kind to everyone and to wave hello.

The moral of this story? Always be friendly and never miss any opportunity to wave a friendly hello.

Lily was a little girl who loved dogs. She wanted to have a dog of her own, but her mom said no. She said dogs were too much work and too messy. Lily was sad, but she hoped that one day she would get a dog.

One day, Lily went to the park with her mom. She saw a big dog with brown fur and a wagging tail. The dog was playing with a ball. Lily wanted to play with the dog, too. She asked her mom if she could pet the dog. Her mom said yes, but she had to ask the owner first.

Lily walked up to the owner, a nice lady with a smile. She said, "Hello, can I pet your dog?" The lady said, "Sure, his name is Max. He is very friendly. He likes to give his paw." Lily reached out her hand and said, "Hi, Max. Can you give me your paw?" Max looked at Lily and lifted his paw. Lily took his paw and shook it. She felt happy.

Lily and Max played for a while. They threw the ball, ran around, and cuddled. Lily felt like Max was her friend. She wished she could take him home. She asked the lady, "Can I have Max? He is so nice and fun." The lady looked at Lily and said, "I'm sorry, sweetie, but Max is my dog. I can't give him to you. He is my best friend."

Lily felt sad. She understood that Max belonged to the lady, but she still wanted him. She hugged Max and said, "Bye, Max. I will miss you." Max licked her face and wagged his tail. He seemed to say, "Bye, Lily. I will miss you, too."

Lily and her mom left the park. Lily was quiet on the way home. She thought about Max and how much she liked him. She wondered if she would ever see him again.

When they got home, Lily saw a big surprise. There was a box on the porch. It had a bow and a card. The card said, "To Lily, from Mom. I love you." Lily opened the box and gasped. Inside was a puppy. A small dog with black fur and a pink nose. The puppy looked at Lily and wagged his tail. He jumped out of the box and ran to Lily. He licked her face and gave her his paw. Lily felt happy.

Lily hugged the puppy and said, "Thank you, Mom. Thank you for giving me a dog. He is so cute and sweet. What is his name?" Her mom smiled and said, "His name is Rex. He is your dog. He was at the shelter. He had no home and no family. He was lonely and sad. He needed someone to love him and take care of him. I thought you would be perfect for him."

Lily looked at Rex and said, "Hi, Rex. I love you. You are my dog. You are my best friend." Rex looked at Lily and said, "Woof. I love you. You are my girl. You are my best friend." They played and cuddled. They were happy.

Lily remembered Max and the lady. She hoped they were happy, too. She thought maybe they would meet again at the park. She thought maybe they could all be friends. She thought maybe Rex and Max could give each other their paws. She thought maybe the lady could give her mom a hug. She thought maybe they could all share the ball. She thought maybe they could all have fun.

Lily smiled. She had a dog. She had a mom. She had a home. She had a family. She had everything she wanted. She felt happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She went to a park with her mom and dad. When they arrived, Lily looked around and saw lots of trees, flowers, and kids playing.

Lily saw a staff member and she got excited. She ran up to him and asked, "Can I have a balloon, please?" The staff member smiled and said, "Of course, which color do you want?"

Lily picked a pink balloon and held it tightly. She was so excited that she ran to her parents and said, "Look, I got a balloon!" They smiled and said, "That's great, Lily. Let's go play on the swings." They all had a fun day at the park and Lily was happy with her new balloon.

One day a little girl named Grace was hungry for candy. As she was walking to the store, she saw a man come out with a bundle of candy beneath his arm. She couldn't believe what she was seeing and turned towards him with happy eyes.

Grace ran over to the man and asked him kindly if he had any candy. The man smiled and said yes he did. He flipped over his arm, and there were two big bags of delicious candy.

Grace was so happy, but then the man said he was only giving her one bag. Before she could say anything, the man bent down and put his gentle hand on her leg. He said to her, "If you want the other bag, you'll have to turn around three times and then wave to me."

Grace was confused but decided to give it a try. She turned around three times, and when she looked back the man was gone. But then, as she waved goodbye, the man popped his head from behind a tree. In his hands he had the other bag of candy, which he threw over to Grace with a big smile.

Grace thanked him and ran home with two big bags of candy in her hands. She never saw the man again, but every once in a while she remembers that magic day when she whispered a simple wish and it was granted.

The sun shone brightly in the sky. Mary was an excited 3 year old and she skipped along the path, although she did not have a particular purpose. She stopped under a big tree and noticed a grape that had fallen from its branches. She thought to herself, "Maybe I should take care of the grape and take it home with me".

But as she reached out to it, she was startled to hear a voice saying, "No, don't take it. You won't understand why, but you must leave it here". She looked around to see who had spoken, but she saw no one. Perplexed, she just stood there with her hand poised in midair when finally a bird landed on a branch next to her.

The bird cocked its head and looked at Mary. It said to her, "You must be ignorant of the consequences of taking care of this grape. It is not yours to take." Mary stared wide-eyed at the bird, but knew that it was right. She slowly lowered her hand and walked back home, feeling wiser for the experience.

Once upon a time there was a King. He always wanted people to agree with him. If they didn't, he got hot and angry.

One day he called everyone to his palace. Everyone was so excited and couldn't wait to see their King.

The King said, "Do you all agree I am the best King?" Everyone nodded and said, "Yes we do!"

The King smiled and said, "Thank you for agreeing with me".

But one brave girl stepped forwards and said, "I don't agree with you! You are not the best King".

This made the King really hot and angry. He shouted, "Who said that? Who disagrees with me?"

The girl stepped forwards and said, "It was me! I disagree with you!".

The King smiled and said, "I respect your opinion, even if you don't agree with me. You have a brave heart and I will make you my advisor".

And from then on, the King listened to her opinion and the people respected her bravery.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a modest room with a big window that let in lots of light. One day, Lily's friend came over to play with her. They decided to play dress-up and put on silly hats and dresses.

Lily's friend said, "I like your room. It's so pretty."

Lily smiled and said, "Thank you. I like it too."

They played for a while and then it was time for Lily's friend to go home. As she was leaving, she accidentally knocked over a vase and it broke into many pieces. Lily was sad because it was her favorite vase.

Lily's friend said, "I'm sorry. I didn't mean to break it."

Lily forgave her friend, but she was still sad about the vase. She learned that accidents can happen when you play with friends, but it's important to be careful.

Sara was a small dancer. She liked to dance with her friends at school. They danced to music and made shapes with their arms and legs.

One day, Sara saw a new girl in her class. Her name was Lila. Lila was shy and did not talk to anyone. She sat in a corner and looked at her shoes.

Sara wanted to be Lila's friend. She went to Lila and said, "Hi, I am Sara. Do you like to dance?"

Lila looked up and nodded. She liked to dance, but she was afraid to join the others.

Sara smiled and said, "Come with me. I will show you how we dance. It is fun and easy. You can do it."

Sara took Lila's hand and led her to the dance floor. She showed her how to move to the music and follow the teacher. Lila felt happy and brave. She danced with Sara and the other kids.

Sara and Lila became good friends. They danced together every day. They cared for each other and helped each other. They were small dancers, but they had big hearts.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park. One day, she saw a big knot on a tree. She wanted to grab it, but it was too high for her.

Just then, her friend Jack came over. Jack was very popular in the park because he was good at playing games. Lily asked Jack if he could help her grab the knot. Jack said, "Sure, I can grab it for you!"

Jack stood on his tiptoes and reached up to grab the knot. He gave it to Lily and she was so happy. Lily said, "Thank you, Jack! You're my best friend!" And they played together in the park all day. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to eat delicious food. One day, she found a big red apple in her kitchen.

Lily took a big bite with her mouth. The apple was very delicious. She was very happy to find such a tasty treat. She wanted to share the apple with her friends.

Lily went outside to find her friends. They were playing in the park. Lily showed them the delicious apple. Her friends took turns taking bites with their mouths. They all agreed that the apple was the most delicious thing they had ever eaten. They were all very happy and continued to play together in the park.

Tom and Sue wanted to build a big tower with their blocks. They had many blocks of different colors and shapes. They put some blocks on the floor and stacked them up. They wanted to make the tower as high as they could.

But Tom and Sue did not agree on how to build the tower. Tom wanted to use only blue and green blocks, because they were his favorite colors. Sue wanted to use all the colors, because they were more fun. They argued and fought over the blocks. They pulled and pushed and knocked down the tower.

"Stop it, Tom! You are stubborn and mean!" Sue said. "You are wasting the blocks. We can use all the colors and make a beautiful tower."

"No, Sue! You are stubborn and silly!" Tom said. "You are wasting the blocks. We can use only blue and green and make a strong tower."

They did not listen to each other. They did not share the blocks. They did not work together. They did not succeed in building the tower.

Mom came and saw the mess. She was not happy. She picked up the blocks and put them in a box. She said to Tom and Sue, "You are both stubborn and naughty. You wasted the blocks and your time. You did not succeed in building the tower. You did not have fun. You need to learn to cooperate and compromise. Now go to your rooms and think about what you did."

Tom and Sue felt sad and sorry. They realized they were wrong. They wished they had been nicer and smarter. They wished they had succeeded in building the tower. They wished they had had fun.

Once there was a beetle. She was very small and liked to find hidden things. Every day she looked for something new to find. One day she went on a long journey to find a special hidden prize.

On her way, she didn't know what she would see. She looked high and low, but she couldn't see anything. She was getting very tired until finally she found it! It was a hidden treat.

But when she got there, she was so surprised. She knew it was something amazing, because it was so special. She couldn't believe it was all for her. She was so happy, she wanted to share it with everybody.

Now her friends all know about her special hidden treat. She is so pleased to have found it, and she is happy to share it with everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She loved to play outside. One day, she found a big, fancy leaf. It was green and shiny. She wanted to show it to her friend, Tom.

Mia went to Tom's house and said, "Look at my fancy leaf! We can include it in our play time." Tom looked at the leaf and smiled. He said, "Wow, it's so pretty! Let's play with it."

They played with the leaf all day. They used it as a fan, a hat, and even a boat for their toys. As they played, they saw a tiny bug on the leaf. Mia wanted to squish it, but Tom said, "No, Mia! We should be kind to all living things, even bugs."

Mia listened to Tom and let the bug go. They continued to play with the leaf and had lots of fun. At the end of the day, Mia and Tom learned that it is important to be kind to all living things, big or small. And they also learned that even a simple leaf can bring so much joy and fun when shared with a friend.

Once there was a girl who was very happy. She was three years old and loved to jump. One day she was playing in her living room when she heard something loud. She looked over to the bookshelf and saw a frog jumping all around! She was so excited and jumped up and down too.

The frog leapt from the bookshelf and landed on the rug next to the girl. She laughed and the frog smiled. She started to jump around the room, first around the frog and then up on the couch. The frog watched, hopping around in excitement.

After a while the girl and her frog friend were both quite tired. She spoke to the frog and said, "Let's jump one more time!" The frog agreed and the two of them jumped up high. They landed and laughed and hugged.

The frog said goodbye and hopped away. The girl watched until he was out of sight. She filled with happy feelings, knowing that she had made a new friend.

Lily was a pupil in a big school. She liked to learn new things and play with her friends. But she was worried about the big test that her teacher gave her. She did not know many of the words and numbers on the paper.

"Can you help me, Sam?" she asked her friend, who sat next to her. "I don't know how to do this test."

Sam looked at her paper and shook his head. "I can't help you, Lily. The teacher said we have to do it by ourselves. And I don't know how to do it either. I'm scared."

Lily felt sad and scared too. She wished she could go home and hug her mom. She looked at the clock and saw that there was not much time left. She tried to write something on the paper, but she did not believe that it was right.

The teacher came and collected the papers. She looked at Lily's paper and frowned. She did not say anything, but Lily knew that she was not happy. She felt a tear roll down her cheek.

The next day, the teacher gave back the papers. She called Lily to the front of the class. She showed Lily's paper to everyone. It had a big red X on it.

"Lily, this is the worst paper I have ever seen. You did not answer any of the questions correctly. You did not even write your name. You are a very bad pupil. You have to stay after school and do extra work. And you have to tell your mom that you failed the test."

Lily felt like her heart was breaking. She heard the other pupils laugh and whisper. She wished she could disappear. She did not want to stay after school. She did not want to tell her mom. She did not want to be a bad pupil. She did not want to be in this school. She did not want to be alive.

Once upon a time, there was a little frog named Fred. Fred loved to jump and play in the pond all day. One day, Fred saw a big bird in the sky. The bird was a sight to see! Fred tried to jump high to see the bird better, but he couldn't jump high enough.

Suddenly, Fred heard a loud noise. He looked around and saw his friend, a deaf mole, coming towards him. Fred tried to jump to get the mole's attention, but the mole couldn't hear him. So, Fred decided to hop over to the mole and tap him on the head. The mole was happy to see his friend and they played together all day.

From that day on, Fred realized that even though he couldn't jump high enough to see everything, he still had his friends to play with and have fun. And that was all that mattered to him.

Once upon a time there was a harmless staff. It was so nice that everyone wanted to keep it. One day, a boy said he wanted the staff. The others said no, so the boy decided to take it anyway.

He started running away with the staff, but then he paused to take a breath. The other children were mad and started chasing him. The boy was scared, so he kept running. He paused again when he reached a big hill.

The boy looked back and saw the other children were still chasing him. He was scared and started running even faster. He looked back one more time and saw that the children had stopped chasing him.

The boy was so relieved and he paused for a moment to catch his breath. Then, he started running even faster to get away. When he was far away, he looked back and saw that the children had gone.

The boy was safe and he decided to keep the staff. From then on, the boy and staff were happy and stayed safe. They never had to worry about being chased again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She lived in a comfortable house with her mom, dad, and her cat, Whiskers. Lily loved to play with her mom's jewelry. She would put on the shiny necklaces and bracelets, and pretend she was a princess.

One day, Whiskers saw the shiny jewelry and thought they were toys. He started to play with them too. But, Whiskers made a mistake and tried to eat the jewelry! Lily saw Whiskers and said, "No, Whiskers! Don't eat the jewelry! That's not good for you!"

Lily told her mom and dad what happened. They were happy that Lily stopped Whiskers from eating the jewelry. They talked to Lily and said, "It's important to take care of our things and keep them safe. And it's good to help others when they make a mistake." Lily learned that it's nice to help others and to be careful with special things.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside, but one day it started to rain. Lily's mommy told her to take her purple umbrella with her, but Lily didn't want to. She said, "I don't need it. I won't get wet."

Lily's mommy said, "Don't lie, Lily. You will get wet without your umbrella." But Lily didn't listen and went outside without it. Soon, she was very wet and cold. She ran back inside and said, "Mommy, I'm sorry. I should have listened to you. I won't lie again."

Lily learned that it's important to listen to her mommy and to not lie. She also learned that her purple umbrella was very important to keep her dry in the rain. From that day on, Lily always took her umbrella with her when it rained.

Once upon a time, there was a lily growing in a field. Every day, a foolish farmer would come to the field and try to fold the lily like a piece of paper. But no matter how hard he tried, the lily would not fold.

One day, the farmer was feeling particularly foolish and yelled, "If I can't fold the lily, what is the point of planting it?"

Just then, a gentle voice came from behind him. It was the lily's grandmother. She said, "My dearest, you mustn't be so foolish. The point of planting a lily is to let it be."

The farmer thought about what the grandmother had said for a moment and realized she was right. From that moment on, he stopped trying to fold the lily and just let it be.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to climb things. One day, he saw a big shelf in his room. It was very tall and had lots of toys on it. Timmy wanted to climb the shelf to get his favorite toy, a little red car.

Timmy asked his mom if he could climb the shelf. His mom said no because it was too dangerous. Timmy was sad but he knew his mom was right. He decided to wait until his dad came home to ask him.

When his dad came home, Timmy asked him if he could climb the shelf. His dad said he could help him climb the shelf safely. Timmy was so happy! His dad lifted him up and Timmy reached for his little red car. He was so happy to have it back in his hands.

From that day on, Timmy knew that he should always ask for help when he wanted to climb something tall. He was glad he waited for his dad to come home.

Once upon a time there was a girl who was curious. She saw a mysterious candle and had to know what it was. She touched it and it turned on. Suddenly, the room got very dark and strange shadows appeared. The little girl was very scared!

The shadows moved around the room, getting closer and closer. The girl wanted to turn the candle off, but she didn't know how. No matter how hard she tried, nothing worked.

As the shadows got closer, the little girl started to cry. Then, out of the shadows came a scary monster and the little girl screamed. She ran away as fast as she could, leaving the mysterious candle behind.

The little girl was never seen again. She was never able to turn off the candle, and the mysterious monsters she saw still haunt her dreams.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She liked to go outside and play in the park. One day, she saw a bird with a thin beak gathering some seeds. Lily thought it was very interesting to watch.

As she was walking, she saw a hook on the ground. It was shiny and looked like it belonged to a fisherman. She picked it up and wondered what she could do with it.

Suddenly, she had an idea. She decided to gather some worms and use the hook to go fishing in the pond. She sat by the pond and waited patiently. After a while, she caught a fish! She was very happy and proud of herself.

From that day on, Lily loved to gather things and try new things with them. She learned that even a thin hook can be very useful. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a man who loved to load his journal with stories. Every day he wrote in his journal, but one day it was gloomy outside and the man didn't want to write. He felt sad and walked around feeling sadder and sadder.

The man thought maybe he should take a walk to feel happier. So he loaded his journal in a bag and started walking. He walked through the gloomy trees and looked at the gray sky. He felt a bit better.

Then he noticed a colorful bird flying above him. The bird was singing a happy song and the man smiled for the first time since he began walking. He watched the bird fly around and chirp its happy song. The man suddenly felt much happier and started to laugh.

The man was so happy that he started to skip. He skipped all the way back home, loaded his journal and wrote down all his happy thoughts. He was so glad that he had gone for a walk, even though it was a gloomy day. The man smiled and knew that tomorrow would be a better day.

Once upon a time, there was a big ranch. On this ranch, there were many animals. One of the animals was a loyal dog named Spot. Spot loved to help his friends on the ranch.

One day, Spot and his friends were talking. "Let's decorate the ranch for a party!" said Spot. All his friends agreed. They all worked together to make the ranch look nice. They put up colorful things and made the ranch look pretty.

After they finished decorating, they had a big party. All the animals had fun and danced. At the end of the day, they were all very happy. They learned that working together and helping each other is important. And that was the best day on the ranch.

Lily and Sam are pupils in the same class. They like to play together at break time. One day, they find a big ball in the playground. They want to kick it and throw it and roll it.

"Can I have the ball first?" Lily asks Sam.

"OK, but only for a little bit. Then it is my turn," Sam says.

Lily takes the ball and runs with it. She kicks it hard and it flies in the air. She laughs and claps her hands. She likes the ball.

Sam waits and waits. He wants to play with the ball too. He sees Lily having fun and he feels angry. He walks up to Lily and tries to grab the ball.

"Give me the ball! It is my turn!" Sam shouts at Lily.

"No, it is not! I am still playing!" Lily shouts back at Sam.

They pull and tug and push. The ball slips from their hands and rolls away. They chase after it, still shouting.

Miss Lee, their teacher, hears the noise. She comes out of the classroom and sees Lily and Sam. She is not happy. She blows her whistle and calls them.

"Lily and Sam, come here right now!" Miss Lee shouts.

Lily and Sam stop and look at Miss Lee. They feel scared. They know they are in trouble. They walk slowly to Miss Lee.

"Why are you shouting and fighting?" Miss Lee asks them.

They do not answer. They hang their heads and stare at the ground. They are quiet.

Miss Lee sighs. She takes the ball and puts it away. She tells Lily and Sam to sit on the bench and think about what they did. She says they cannot play with the ball or with each other until they say sorry.

Lily and Sam sit on the bench. They are sad and bored. They wish they had shared the ball and played nicely. They look at each other and whisper.

"I am sorry, Sam. I should have given you the ball," Lily says.

"I am sorry, Lily. I should not have shouted at you," Sam says.

They smile and hug. They are friends again. They tell Miss Lee they are sorry. Miss Lee smiles and nods. She says they can play with the ball and with each other again, but only if they are kind and fair.

Lily and Sam agree. They take the ball and run to the playground. They kick it and throw it and roll it. They take turns and say please and thank you. They laugh and have fun. They are happy.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Linda. Linda loved to read and she always had a book in her hands. One day Linda decided to take a holiday and enjoy the sun.

The holiday was fun but soon Linda noticed something strange. Everywhere she went, harmless little animals followed her. She wasn't sure why, but suddenly she felt a chill run down her spine. Was there something sinister lurking in the shadows?

Linda quickly lost interest in her holiday and decided to pick up a book and read instead. As she read, she noticed that the harmless animals had grown in number. They were now following her everywhere.

Just when Linda thought things couldn't get worse, the animals suddenly attacked her and took her away. No one ever saw Linda again. She was gone forever. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He liked playing with his toys and his family.

On a sunny day, Tim and his family went to the store to buy some food. After they came back, they began to make lunch.

Tim watched his mom turn on the tap at the sink to wash the fruits and vegetables. Suddenly, water started to come out of the cupboard below the sink.

Tim's mom looked upset. She said, "Oh no! What happened?"

Tim's dad came over and said, "Don't worry. We'll just have to turn the valve off and call a plumber to fix it."

So his dad turned the valve off and made a phone call. The plumber came and fixed the sink.

Everyone was happy! And Tim learned an important lesson that day - always be careful around water!

Once there was a naughty little boy. His name was Joe. He loved to test things and see what happened.

One day, Joe decided to test his wisdom and see how much he could learn. He asked his mom so many questions and tried to figure out the answers.

Mom was so proud of Joe and all the questions he asked. She even said "Wow, what wise questions Joe! You are so smart!"

Joe was so pleased and he felt like he had just passed a big test! He smiled from ear to ear and knew that his wisdom had made mom proud.

Joe was a naughty little boy, but his wisdom was helping him learn new things every day. He was so proud of how quickly he was learning and growing!

Once upon a time there was a boy named Joe. He was just three years old and he loved to explore his house. One room was his favorite: the living room.

The living room had a big, comfy couch that Joe liked to jump on. But one day Joe noticed something was wrong with the couch - it had a rip in it. Joe was very sad and he didn't know what to do.

Then his mommy came in and asked, "What's the matter, Joe?" "My couch is broken," Joe said, showing her the rip. His mommy smiled and said, "I can fix it, Joe. Don't worry."

So his mommy got out clean cloths, tools, and other things to repair the couch. Joe asked, "Is it going to be clean and new again?" His mommy replied, "Yes, it will be!"

Joe was so happy. After his mommy finished repairing the couch, it looked just like new - and it was nice and clean! Joe was very pleased. From then on, he loved hopping on his comfy couch.

Once upon a Wednesday, there was a little girl called Sarah. She was very confused. She was walking in the park when she saw something shiny on the ground. She held it close and thought it was very pretty.

Sarah was very happy and held the shiny thing tight. But then she heard a voice calling her name. It was her mummy! She was very confused as to why her mummy was there. She held out the shiny thing and showed it to mummy.

Mummy was very surprised and explained to Sarah that the shiny thing was a coin. She asked what Sarah was going to do with it. Sarah smiled and told her she was going to hold it until Wednesday.

Mummy smiled and told Sarah it was a good idea. Then she gave Sarah a hug, and they went home together. From then on, Sarah held on to the coin every Wednesday in case she ever got lost or confused again.

Once upon a time, there was a strong dog named Max. Max loved to play and rush around the big yard. He liked to play with his ball and run very fast.

One day, Max saw a cat in the yard. He wanted to play with the cat, but the cat was scared. Max rushed after the cat, but the cat ran away. Max was sad, but he kept trying to play with the cat.

Max's parent told him to stop, but Max did not listen. He kept rushing after the cat. The cat climbed a tall tree to hide from Max. Max tried to climb, but he fell down. Max got hurt and cried. His parent came to help him, but they were not happy. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a very long factory. Everywhere the factory went, it made lots of smoke. It made so much smoke, it made people cough. One day an old man came to the factory. He rubbed his hands together and said, "We need to make something to stop all this smoke". He worked for a long time and soon everyone was happy because the smoke was gone. The old man said, "Rubbing my hands together made me think of an idea. It worked and now everyone can breathe again". Everyone smiled and clapped. The factory was happy again and it was long and smoke free.

Once there was a girl who was three years old. She loved plants and she had an idea to make something special with them.

So one day she got all the plants that were in her garden and she took them inside. She sat them on the floor in a big circle and made sure they were all tidy.

Then she got some chairs and she sat one chair in the middle of the circle. She filled the chairs with her softest cushions and put a blanket on them too.

The girl was happy with what she had made. It was a special seat for her to sit in and watch the plants.

Every day she would sit in her special seat and admire the plants. She was very happy in her tidy and special seat surrounded by all the plants.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big factory. Many people worked in the factory. One day, a little boy named Tim went to the factory with his mom. Tim was very impatient. He did not want to wait for his mom to finish her work.

Tim saw a big machine and went close to it. He wanted to touch it but his mom said, "No, Tim! It is not safe." Tim was sad and began to weep. His mom felt bad and said, "Just wait a little more, we will go home soon."

But Tim did not listen. He went to the machine again when his mom was not looking. He touched the machine and it started to make a loud noise. People came running to see what happened. Tim's mom was very worried. She knew that Tim did not listen to her.

The big machine broke because of Tim. The factory had to stop working. Many people lost their jobs. Tim and his mom had to leave the town. They were very sad and wept as they said goodbye to their friends.

Molly was so excited. She had just gotten a card in the mail. She opened it and saw it had a dead bug inside! She was so happy to come home from school and find it. She had never seen a dead bug before. She asked her mom to show her how to put it in a container.

Next, Molly and her mom went to the store. Molly wanted to buy something special with her card. She picked out a brightly-colored toy.

When they got home, Molly was so excited to show her toy to her dad. She asked him to come outside and see it. He was just as excited as Molly. Together, they played with it for the rest of the day.

Molly always remembered the day she got her card with the dead bug inside. It was her first time ever seeing a dead bug and buying something with her very own card. She was so happy!

Tom and Lily are friends. They like to play with their scooters in the park. One day, it is very cloudy and windy. Tom and Lily do not care. They put on their coats and hats and go to the park.

They ride their scooters on the path. They go fast and slow. They make turns and jumps. They have fun. They see a big puddle. It is wet and shiny. Tom has an idea.

"Let's go through the puddle!" he says to Lily. "It will be fun!"

Lily is not sure. She thinks the puddle is too big and too wet. She does not want to get dirty and cold.

"I don't know, Tom," she says. "The puddle is very wet. Maybe we should go around it."

Tom laughs. He is not afraid of the puddle. He wants to try something new.

"Come on, Lily," he says. "Don't be scared. It will be fun. I will go first. You can watch me. Then you can guess how wet I will be."

Tom rides his scooter to the puddle. He goes very fast. He splashes into the puddle. The water goes up and down. It covers Tom and his scooter. He comes out of the puddle. He is very wet. He smiles.

"Wow, that was fun!" he says. "Look at me, Lily. I am very wet. Can you guess how wet I am?"

Lily looks at Tom. She sees the water on his coat, his hat, his face, his hair, his shoes, his socks, his scooter. She shakes her head.

"I can't guess, Tom," she says. "You are too wet. You are the wettest thing I ever saw. You need a towel."

Tom laughs. He does not mind being wet. He likes the puddle. He wants to go again.

"Come on, Lily," he says. "It is your turn. Don't you want to try? It is fun. You will like it."

Lily thinks. She looks at the puddle. She looks at Tom. She looks at her scooter. She makes a decision.

"OK, Tom," she says. "I will try. But only once. And then we go home. And we get dry. And we have hot chocolate."

Tom claps. He is happy. He likes to share his fun with Lily. He waits for Lily. Lily rides her scooter to the puddle. She goes slower than Tom. She splashes into the puddle. The water goes up and down. It covers Lily and her scooter. She comes out of the puddle. She is wet. She smiles.

"That was fun, Tom," she says. "You were right. I liked it. But I am wet. Can you guess how wet I am?"

Tom looks at Lily. He sees the water on her coat, her hat, her face, her hair, her shoes, her socks, her scooter. He nods.

"I can guess, Lily," he says. "You are wet. But not as wet as me. You are a little wet. You need a towel, too."

Lily laughs. She agrees with Tom. She is wet, but not as wet as him. She likes the puddle, but not as much as him. She is glad they are friends. They ride their scooters to their homes. They get towels. They get dry. They have hot chocolate. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a classroom. In this classroom, there were many kids who liked to play and learn. One day, a new teacher came. She was incredible. She had a big smile and loved to teach the kids new things.

The incredible teacher had an idea. She told the kids to put their toys away. Then, she put a big box in the middle of the classroom. The kids were very excited. They wondered what was inside the box.

The incredible teacher opened the box. Inside, there were many colorful balls. The kids were so happy. They played with the balls all day. They laughed and had lots of fun. The classroom was a very happy place.

Once there was a bear who was very lonely. Every day, he walked through the forest, looking for someone to play with. One day, he came across a little frog sitting on a leaf. The bear was very excited, and he asked the frog if he wanted to play.

The frog was very fearful. He didn't want to play with the big, scary bear. But the bear kept insisting, so the frog slowly agreed. The two of them had so much fun playing together.

The bear was so happy that he now had a friend. Every day, they went on an adventure. They explored the forest and had many wild adventures.

The bear and the frog were always there for each other. Whenever either one was frightened, they were both there to comfort and encourage each other. They showed each other how nothing was too scary if you have a friend.

They were such good friends that they lived happily ever after. They were always seen together, playing around a leaf on sunny days.
