Suddenly, a bird came flying down from the sky and perched on the wall. With one flap of its wings, the wall was gone! On the other side there was a beautiful garden.

Mary went inside the garden and looked around. Everywhere she looked there were flowers of different shapes and colors. She felt so happy and excited!

But soon it was time to go home and Mary had to leave the garden. As she said goodbye, she felt a bit ashamed. She knew it wasn't fair that she got to visit the special place while others couldn't.

But Mary promised that she'd remain kind and share her stories with her friends and family so they too could bask in the beauty of the garden.

Ben and Lily liked to play in the dark. They had special equipment that could glow. They had a flashlight, a lantern, and some glow sticks. They pretended they were explorers in a cave.

One night, they went to the backyard with their equipment. They turned off the flashlight and the lantern and only used the glow sticks. They waved them in the air and made shapes and letters. They had fun.

"Look, Ben, I can make a star!" Lily said, holding a glow stick in each hand.

"Wow, that's cool, Lily! Can you make a heart?" Ben asked, trying to bend his glow stick.

Lily tried to make a heart, but she was not careful. She broke her glow stick and the liquid spilled on her hand. It was sticky and cold. She cried.

"Ow, ow, ow! It hurts, Ben! Help me!" Lily said, dropping the glow stick.

Ben turned on the flashlight and saw Lily's hand. It was glowing green. He was scared.

"Don't worry, Lily, I'll help you. Come on, let's go to Mom and Dad. They'll know what to do." Ben said, grabbing Lily's other hand.

They ran to the house and knocked on the door. Mom and Dad opened the door and saw their children. They were surprised.

"What happened? Why are you glowing?" Mom asked, hugging Lily.

"We were playing with the glow sticks and Lily broke one and it spilled on her hand. It hurts, Mom. Make it stop." Ben said, showing Mom the glow stick.

"Oh, you poor thing. It's okay, Lily. It's not dangerous. It's just a chemical that makes light. We'll wash it off and put some cream on it. You'll be fine." Dad said, taking Lily to the bathroom.

Ben followed them and felt sorry for Lily. He decided to be more careful with the glow sticks next time. He also decided to share his flashlight and lantern with Lily. He loved his sister and wanted her to be happy.

Once upon a time, there lived a kangaroo. Every day, he would go out and examine different places. One day, he decided to go to the boring old forest. He slowly made his way through the trees and soon he heard a strange noise. He looked around and noticed a little purple bird. He slowly crept closer and he could see that the bird had a hurt wing. He knew he needed to help the little bird. He carefully examined the bird's wing and eventually knew what to do. He gently put some leaves on the wing and soon the bird was all better. The kangaroo felt very happy that he could help the little bird so he hopped away.

It was a beautiful day and Maria was playing with her friends. They were having so much fun playing together. Suddenly, Maria saw an ugly cut in her arm. She cried and asked "What happened?" Her friends said that it was dark outside and they should go inside.

Maria was scared and she wanted to go home. Her friends said that if she stayed the cut will just disappear. Maria agreed but she was still afraid.

After a while, Maria's friends had to leave. Maria put a bandage on her cut and tried hard to ignore it. Soon it became dark and the cut started to hurt a lot. Maria wanted to go home, but her friends were gone and she didn't know what to do. All of a sudden, the cut started to disappear. Maria was relieved and decided to try to find her way home.

When Maria made it home, she quickly ran to the bathroom. But instead of the cut being gone, it had become bigger and uglier. Maria shouted for help but no one came. Her cut just kept getting bigger and bigger until it had completely taken over her arm. In the end, Maria disappeared forever and nobody ever saw her again.

Once upon a time, there was a hard clock. The clock had lots of interesting parts - a big bell and lots of numbers.
The clock was very old and didn't keep time very well. The man wanted to get a new clock, so he took a sack and put the old clock into it.
Then he went to the market, and bought a new, shiny clock.
At home he put the new clock on the wall, and the old clock in the sack.
He went outside and put the sack in the garden, so the old clock could be part of the garden for all time.
The end.

Once upon a time, there was a jolly little doll. Her name was Sue. Sue enjoyed exploring the world around her. One day, she decided to go for a walk. She walked and walked and soon found herself in a big park.

Sue saw a prancing little rabbit and she wanted to follow it. She ran after it, but it was too fast for her. Suddenly, something made her stop. It was her friend, Joe, who had come to find her.

Joe put his arm around Sue and they talked and laughed together. They played a game of hide and seek and had lots of fun. Then it was time to go home. Joe held Sue's hand tightly as they walked back together.

Once they reached home, Joe was sad to say goodbye. But Sue smiled and waved, happy to know she had such a jolly friend.

Once upon a time, there were two people who were married. They were very happy together and they wanted to have a daughter. One day, their wish came true! They had a baby girl.

The baby girl grew and grew and she became a big girl. Everyday she grew smarter and smarter. But one day she did something very stupid. Her parents were mad. They thought their daughter had become too silly.

But no matter what, her parents still loved her. They hugged her and told her that she was still their beautiful daughter.

With time, the little girl became smarter and smarter. Eventually she became the smartest daughter in the whole world! The parents were very proud of her, and their love for her only grew.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy goose walking in the woods. He was looking for friends when he opened the door of a little cottage in the forest. Inside he found a little girl.

"Where have you come from?" asked the little girl.

"I am looking for friends," said the clumsy goose. "Will you be my friend?"

The little girl smiled and said, "Yes! I will be your friend."

The clumsy goose opened his wings and gave a big hug.

"Now let's go and explore the woods together," said the little girl.

So the little girl and the clumsy goose went exploring. They found a river and had lots of fun together.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Amy. She picked up a pen and paper and went outside to play in the field.

It was a big, soft field, full of green grass and trees. Suddenly she saw something in the grass - it was a shiny pen!

Amy was so excited, she took it and started to write in the dirt with it. She wrote her name, and then she quickly wrote a few more words. She was getting so good at writing, it made her feel really happy.

Soon it was time to go home, so Amy carefully put the pen and paper into her pocket and ran home. She had a lot of fun in the soft field and she was so proud of all the writing she did.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play with his toys, especially his toy cars. One day, Timmy's mom took him to a store to buy a new toy car. Timmy was so excited to see all the different cars on the shelves.

He found a red car that he really liked, but it was in a frame. Timmy asked his mom, "Can we take it out of the frame?" His mom said, "No, that's an original car and it needs to stay in the frame."

Suddenly, another little boy came up and tried to pinch Timmy's new car. Timmy said, "Hey, that's not nice! That's my car!" The other little boy said sorry and walked away. Timmy was happy to have his new car and he put it on his shelf at home, still in its original frame.

One day, Timmy went outside to play. He looked around and saw a gray sky with dark clouds. He was sad because he wanted to play in the sun. Suddenly, Timmy heard a loud rip and saw a tree tear in two! All around him, there were other trees tearing up. Timmy was scared and started to cry.

Suddenly, Timmy saw a giant, gray grill. It was huge and covering the whole sky! Timmy was stopped in his tracks. The grill was sending down drops of rain. Timmy was so happy- he could play in the rain and forget about the trees tearing! He ran around, playing and jumping in the rain.

Eventually, the rain stopped and the sky cleared up. Timmy was happy he got to play in the rain, and he waved goodbye to the big, gray grill. He never forgot the day the trees tore up, but he was glad the wet, gray grill came to save the day.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot liked to play and run all day. One day, Spot found a big ball. He wanted to play with the ball, but it was difficult for him to push it. Spot was in trouble.

Spot wanted to improve and be strong, so he could play with the big ball. He asked his friend, a wise old cat, what to do. The cat told Spot to eat good food and run every day. Spot listened to the cat and did what he said.

After some days, Spot felt stronger. He went back to the big ball and tried to push it again. This time, Spot could push the ball and play with it. Spot was so happy that he improved and solved his trouble.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play at the beach and look for crabs. One day, she found a small crab walking on the sand. She wanted to play with it, so she poked it gently with her finger. The crab didn't like it and pinched her finger with its claws.

Lily learned that she shouldn't poke the crab because it might hurt her. Instead, she decided to be obedient and watch the crab from a distance. She saw how it walked sideways and dug holes in the sand. It was very interesting to watch the crab go about its day.

From that day on, Lily became friends with the crab. She would visit it every time she went to the beach and watch it from a safe distance. The crab became used to her presence and didn't pinch her anymore. Lily was happy to have a new friend and learned that being obedient and respectful to animals was important. The end.

Once there was a bossy little boy called Peter. Peter wanted to go camping. He got his mum to take him and they set off to the woods. They arrived at the camp and Peter was so excited that he started pointing at all the things he wanted to do.

First, they went to the lake and Peter pointed at the ducks. He laughed when they made so much noise. Next, he pointed at the trees and said he wanted to climb them. His mum said no, so she took him to the tents instead.

Peter was so excited and he wanted to explore. He went over to the other tents and pointed at the people's stuff. He waved at them and said hello. Everyone thought Peter was so cute, even though he was bossy.

Later, Peter and his mum cooked some yummy food on the campfire. They shared it with the other people and then spent the night chatting and making s'mores. It was the perfect camping trip, thanks to Peter and his bossy finger-pointing!

Once upon a time, there was a little bird who loved to sing. Every day, he would sit on a branch and sing his heart out. One day, a big wind blew and the little bird lost his balance and fell from the tree. He was very scared and hurt his wing.

A nice lady who lived nearby saw the little bird and came to help. She gently picked him up and took him inside her house. She put a tap of water in a bowl and gave it to the little bird to drink. She also put a soft blanket over him to keep him warm.

The little bird was so happy that someone was there to help him. He learned that it's important to be kind to others and to help them when they fall. From that day on, he sang even more beautifully and always remembered the nice lady who helped him when he needed it most.

One day, a little boy named Tim saw a mighty figure in his room. The figure was big and strong. Tim was scared, but he wanted to be brave. So, he said, "Who are you?"

The figure smiled and said, "I am Mr. Mighty, and I am here to help you. I will show you how to manage your toys." Tim was happy to have a friend to help him. They started to clean up his room together.

As they worked, Tim felt stronger and braver. Mr. Mighty showed him how to pick up his toys and put them away. Soon, Tim's room was clean and neat. Tim said, "Thank you, Mr. Mighty! I can manage my toys now." Mr. Mighty smiled and said, "You did a great job, Tim!" And then, he was gone. Tim was proud of himself, and he knew that he could be mighty, too.

Tom and Lily were playing in their room. They had many toys, but they liked to pretend they were in a castle. They used a big box as a throne, and a blanket as a cape. They also had a candle that their mom gave them. It was not a real candle, but a toy one that could light up.

"Look, I am the king of the castle!" Tom said. He put the candle on the box and pressed a button. The candle glowed with a yellow light. "You are my queen, Lily. You have to do what I say."

Lily did not like that. She wanted to be the king too. She did not want to do what Tom said. She grabbed the candle from the box and ran away.

"Hey, give it back!" Tom shouted. He chased Lily around the room. "That is my candle! You have to share!"

"No, it is mine!" Lily said. She held the candle tight. "I want to be the king! You have to listen to me!"

They ran and ran, until they bumped into a table. The candle fell to the floor and broke. The light went out. The candle was empty.

Tom and Lily looked at the candle. They felt sad. They had broken their mom's gift. They had also broken their game.

"I'm sorry, Tom," Lily said. "I should not have taken your candle. I should have shared."

"I'm sorry, too, Lily," Tom said. "I should not have demanded you to do what I say. I should have let you be the king sometimes."

They hugged each other and said they were sorry. Then they went to find their mom. They told her what happened. They asked her if she could fix the candle.

Mom smiled and hugged them. She said she was glad they were sorry and that they learned to share. She said she could not fix the candle, but she had a surprise for them. She took them to the kitchen, where there was a big cake with two real candles on it.

"Happy birthday, Tom and Lily!" Mom said. "You are both four years old today! You can each make a wish and blow out a candle."

Tom and Lily smiled and clapped. They forgot about the broken toy candle. They had a real candle each. They made a wish and blew out the candles. They thanked Mom and cut the cake. They shared it with each other and with Mom. They had a happy birthday.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to travel with her parents in their car. One day, they went on a long journey and Lily fell asleep in her comfortable car seat.

When she woke up, her dad told her they were going to drive through a tunnel. Lily was scared because she had never been in a tunnel before. But her mom said, "Don't worry, it's just a dark road under the ground. We will be out soon."

As they drove through the tunnel, it got very dark and Lily held her mom's hand tightly. But when they came out the other side, she was so happy and said, "That was fun! Can we go through the tunnel again?" Her parents laughed and said, "Maybe next time, Lily."

Once there was a little girl named Lina. She loved to play in the garden. One day, Lina was playing with her toy train in the garden when she saw something strange. She leaned in closer to take a closer look and saw a rabbit.

Lina said, "Hello Mr Rabbit, what are you doing here?" But the rabbit just stared at her and said nothing.

Lina kept talking to the rabbit, telling it stories until it started to move closer. Eventually, the rabbit leaned against her leg!

Lina was so excited and started to stroke the rabbit's back. But then it suddenly jumped up and ran away!

Lina shouted after it, "Wait silly rabbit! Don't be so stupid!"

But the rabbit was already gone. So, Lina just returned to playing in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the park with her friends. One day, Lily saw a picture of an otter in a book and she wanted to see one in real life. So, she asked her mommy if they could go on an adventure to search for an otter.

Lily and her mommy packed a picnic and drove to a distant lake. When they arrived, Lily saw lots of ducks and fish, but no otters. She was sad, but her mommy told her to keep looking. Suddenly, Lily saw a cute little otter swimming in the lake! She was so happy and excited that she jumped up and down.

Lily and her mommy watched the otter play in the water for a while, and then they had their picnic. Lily felt so lucky to have seen an otter, and she couldn't wait to tell all her friends about her adventure. From that day on, Lily loved searching for new animals and exploring the world around her.

Once there was a small girl called Suzie who lived in a house with her mom. One day, Suzie was playing in the living room when she saw something on the floor.

'Mom, what is this?' asked Suzie.

'It's a letter,' replied her mom.

'What does it say?' said Suzie.

Her mom smiled and opened the letter. She read the letter very quietly.

'Mom, can I keep it?' said Suzie.

'Yes my darling, but you must keep it very quiet,' she replied.

So Suzie put the letter in an old box and made sure nobody else saw it. She wanted to keep it safe.

The next day, Suzie wanted to show her mom the letter again. When she opened the box, it was gone.

'Mom, where is the letter?' said Suzie.

'Oh, I had to remove it,' replied her mom.

Suzie was a little sad, but she knew it was the best thing to do. She promised herself that she would always keep her letters safe and quiet.

Then, Molly and her mom went to the supermarket and bought a thick, juicy grape. Molly was very happy and wanted to eat the grape right away. Her mom laughed and said that she must first sign an 'X' to show she agreed to buy the delicious grape.

Molly made a big 'X' on the paper. The grape was so juicy and good to eat. Molly hugged her mom and told her it was the most yummy grape she had ever tasted!

Anna and Ben are playing outside with their ball. They like to kick the ball to each other and run after it. They are having fun and feeling healthy.

But then they hear a loud noise in the sky. It is thunder. They look up and see dark clouds. They are scared of the thunder. It makes them jump and cry.

"Let's go inside, Ben!" Anna says. "We don't like thunder. It is too noisy and scary."

"OK, Anna," Ben says. "But where is our ball? We need to gather our ball. We don't want to lose it."

They look around and see their ball near a big tree. They run to the ball and pick it up. They hold it tight and run to the house. They are happy to have their ball.

They go inside and find their mom. She hugs them and tells them not to worry. She says the thunder will go away soon and the sun will come out again. She gives them some cookies and milk. They eat and drink and feel better.

They sit on the couch and watch some cartoons. They laugh and smile. They forget about the thunder. They are glad to be inside with their mom and their ball. They are safe and warm. They are happy.

The teacher thought it was a great idea and found some chalk for her. The girl drew lots of pictures on the pavement, from flowers to ships and animals.

Everyone loved the art. They all said it was the most amazing thing they had ever seen. The girl felt so proud, believing she could do anything she wanted.

Once upon a time there was a young girl named Sophia. She was sad because she hadn't seen her Mommy in a long time. Every day she would wear her favorite apron and go to the park to look for her.

One day, while sitting on a bench, she saw a woman who looked just like Mommy! She ran to the woman and said "Mommy, Mommy, it's me!". But the woman had a serious look on her face and shook her head. She said to Sophia that she was not her Mommy.

Sophia began to weep. She was so disappointed that Mommy wasn't there. She wiped tears from her face with her apron and sadly said, "I just want my Mommy".

At that moment, a man appeared out of the trees and said "Sophia, I'm your Dad!" He carried her home and she finally got to see her Mommy again that day. From then on, she was never sad for too long. She knew that her Mommy and Dad would always be there for her.

The End.

Jimmy loved to chew things. He would chew the arms of his couch, the legs of the table, and even his mom's biscuits. One day, Jimmy was chewing his mom's favorite high heel shoe when she came home. His mom was furious and scolded him harshly.

"Jimmy!" She shouted, "No more chewing shoes. From now on, you must chew your gum!"

Jimmy frowned and walked over to the cabinet and grabbed a piece of gum. He shoved it in his mouth and began to chew. However, it was too tough and tasted very harsh. He tried to chew it, but it was too difficult.

He then walked back to his mom and looked up at her. He frowned and asked, "Mommy, why can't I just chew the shoe?"

His mom smiled and said, "It's not good to chew things that don't belong to you. You must respect other people's things."

Jimmy nodded and promised to never chew any of his mom's things again.

One day, Timmy wanted to take a nap. He grabbed his port and headed to the park. He found a nice spot and laid down. He felt guilty because he was supposed to be helping his mom. But before he knew it, he was fast asleep.

As he slept, Timmy dreamed of being in a magical forest. He was running and playing games with his friends. He smiled as they laughed and had fun.

But soon enough the sun started peeking through the clouds. Timmy knew it was time to wake up. As he opened his eyes, he still felt a little guilty. He was supposed to help his mom.

He grabbed his port and made his way back home. He decided that the next time he took a nap, he would make sure to help his mom first.

Bear and Mouse were best friends. They liked to do everything together. One day, they decided to go for a walk. As they walked, they saw a mule. The mule was standing in a big field with not much else around him.

Bear asked Mouse, "Do you want to spend some time with the mule?

Mouse thought for a moment and then said, "Yes, let's go.

So, the two friends walked up to the mule. They were careful not to make too much noise, because it was a very hot day and the mule looked tired. When they got close enough, they could tell the mule had a harsh look on its face.

Mouse bravely stepped up and asked the mule, "Can we spend some time with you?

The mule didn't answer, but it seemed to be happy that Bear and Mouse were spending time with him. They stayed with the mule until the sun set and then said goodbye. They were sad to leave but glad they got to spend time with such a special creature!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who wanted to find treasure. He asked his mom and she said, "Yes, but it won't be easy. It might be difficult."

So, the little boy set off on his adventure. He looked everywhere, in the mountains, in the river, even in the fields, but he couldn't find any treasure.

He was beginning to get very frustrated, so he went even farther
until he stumbled across a cave. He went inside and saw a sparkle deep inside. He went closer and he saw it was treasure!

But as he grabbed it, he heard a loud noise. He panicked and he ran away as fast as he could. He was so happy and excited that he'd found the treasure!

Lily was a young girl who liked to play with her toys. She had many dolls and cars and blocks, but her favorite toy was a fork. She liked to pretend it was a sword or a wand or a comb. She did not like to share her fork with anyone, not even her brother Tom.

One day, Tom wanted to play with Lily's fork. He asked her nicely, but she said no. He tried to grab it from her, but she pulled it away. They started to fight over the fork, and Lily got angry. She shouted at Tom and hit him with the fork. Tom cried and ran to their mom.

Mom was very upset. She saw the fork and the mark on Tom's arm. She scolded Lily and took away her fork. She said Lily had to say sorry to Tom and be nice to him. She said Lily could not play with the fork anymore, because it was not a toy. She said Lily had to play with something else.

Lily felt sad and sorry. She did not mean to hurt Tom. She loved her brother and her fork. She wished she had shared her fork with him. She wished she had not fought with him. She wished she had not hit him. She regretted being mean and selfish. She wanted her fork back, but it was too late. She had lost her favorite toy and made her brother and mom unhappy. She had a bad day.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim had a toy plane. He loved his plane very much. It was red and went very fast. Tim played with his plane every day.

One day, Tim met a fierce dog named Max. Max liked to bark and run. Max saw Tim's plane and wanted to play too. So, Tim threw the plane and Max ran after it. They had fun playing together.

Tim asked Max a question. He said, "Do you like my plane, Max?" Max barked and wagged his tail. That meant Max liked the plane. Tim and Max became best friends and played with the plane every day.

Anna and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys and books. But sometimes they get tired and need a nap. Mommy tells them to go to their room and sleep for a little while.

"Can we take our round ball with us?" Anna asks. She likes to hug the ball when she sleeps.

"Sure, you can take your ball. But don't play with it in bed. Just cuddle it and close your eyes," Mommy says.

Anna and Ben go to their room. They put their ball on the bed and lie down next to it. They try to sleep, but they are not very sleepy. They look at the ball and smile.

"Let's roll the ball to each other," Ben whispers. He thinks it is a fun game.

"Okay, but be quiet. Mommy will hear us," Anna whispers back. She thinks it is a fun game too.

They start to roll the ball gently to each other. They giggle softly when the ball bounces on the bed. They have fun for a few minutes, but then the ball rolls too fast and falls off the bed. It makes a loud noise when it hits the floor.

Mommy hears the noise and comes to their room. She sees the ball on the floor and the twins on the bed. They look guilty and scared.

"What are you doing? I thought you were napping," Mommy says. She is not happy.

"We were napping, Mommy. But the ball wanted to play with us," Anna says. She tries to make an excuse.

"The ball wanted to play with you? That's silly. The ball is not alive. It can't talk or think. It's just a toy," Mommy says. She is wise.

"But the ball is round. Round things like to roll and bounce. They are happy when they move," Ben says. He tries to explain.

"Round things like to roll and bounce, but they don't have feelings. They don't know if they are happy or sad. They are just things. You have feelings. You know when you are happy or sad. You also know when you are tired and need a nap," Mommy says. She is kind.

Anna and Ben look at each other. They realize that Mommy is right. They are tired and need a nap. They say sorry to Mommy and ask her to put the ball back on the bed.

Mommy smiles and hugs them. She puts the ball back on the bed and tucks them in. She kisses them and says good night.

"Sleep well, my sweet twins. I love you," Mommy says.

Anna and Ben cuddle the ball and close their eyes. They feel warm and cozy. They fall asleep and have nice dreams. They are happy.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Lily went for a walk in the park with her mom. Lily loved to run and jump, but her mom told her to be careful because the ground was wet from the rain. Suddenly, Lily saw a big puddle in front of her and she wanted to jump in it.

Her mom said, "Lily, be careful! The water is very heavy and you might get wet."

Lily replied, "Mommy, I want to jump in the puddle! Can I step in it just one time?"

Her mom smiled and said, "Okay, but just one step."

Lily took one big step and felt the spring in the water. She giggled and said, "That was fun! Can we do it again?"

Her mom laughed and said, "Maybe next time, Lily." And they continued their walk in the park.

Peggy was a cheerful three year old. She loved to play in her garage. But one day, when she was playing in the garage, something strange happened. She asked her mom for help, but her mom ignored her.

Peggy felt sad and alone. She wondered why her mom was ignoring her. She tried again, but still no response. After a few more tries, Peggy decided to go outside and try to find someone to help her.

She looked and looked until she spotted her friend, Timmy. He was playing in the nearby park. She ran up to him and asked for help. Timmy happily agreed and the two friends went back to the garage.

When they arrived, Timmy fixed the problem in no time. Peggy was happy and grateful. She thanked him and gave him a big hug.

From then on, Peggy was determined never to be ignored again! She made sure to ask for help whenever she needed it. She was always cheerful, even when things didn't go her way.

Once upon a time there was a frog who needed a new home. He was looking everywhere, but he couldn't find the perfect spot. Then he remembered the old lime tree in the forest. He hopped along and soon arrived at the old tree.

The frog was so excited, he decided to make it his new home. But then something strange happened. The lime tree started to move, it was alive!

The frog was scared and jumped away, the lime tree had become a monster! It growled loudly and said "I will provide you a new home". The frog was still scared, but he was very brave and followed the monster.

The monster took the frog to a special treehouse in the middle of the forest. It was an amazing place and the frog was very happy.

The monster then said goodbye and hopped away. The frog was so relieved, he knew he was safe in his new home. He thanked the scary monster for providing him with a new home.

Sara and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to slide, swing and run. But they did not like the big dog that sometimes came. The dog was black and loud. It had sharp teeth and a long tail.

One day, the dog came when Sara and Ben were on the slide. It barked and ran towards them. Sara and Ben were fearful. They did not know what to do. They wanted to go to their mom, but she was far away.

"Help!" Sara cried. "The dog is coming!"

"Stay calm!" Ben said. "Maybe it is friendly. Maybe it just wants to play."

Ben tried to be brave. He got off the slide and stood in front of Sara. He held out his palm to the dog. He hoped the dog would sniff it and be nice.

The dog came closer and closer. It sniffed Ben's palm. Then it licked it. Ben felt the dog's wet tongue. He smiled. He rubbed the dog's head. The dog wagged its tail. It was friendly. It did want to play.

"See?" Ben said to Sara. "It is not a bad dog. It is a good dog. You can pet it too."

Sara got off the slide and joined Ben. She was still fearful, but she trusted Ben. She rubbed the dog's back. The dog licked her face. She giggled. She was not fearful anymore.

"Good dog!" Sara and Ben said. "We like you. What is your name?"

The dog barked. It sounded like "Rex". Sara and Ben repeated the name. "Rex, Rex, Rex!"

Rex was happy. He had made new friends. He played with Sara and Ben until their mom came. Their mom was surprised to see them with the dog. She asked them where they met Rex. Sara and Ben told her the story. Their mom was proud of them. She said they were brave and kind. She said they could play with Rex again, if his owner agreed.

Sara and Ben hugged Rex goodbye. They said they would see him soon. Rex barked and licked them. He was glad he met Sara and Ben. They were his best friends.

One day, the first friend said, "I want to show you something special". So the friends started painting the universe. One painted stars and planets, the other created boats and birds.

Suddenly, the second friend shouted, "Wow, let's make something else!" The first friend replied, "Let's make a big lucky rainbow!"

So the pair mixed their paints and inventions to make a bright, lucky rainbow. As they finished, they both knew they had created something special.

The friends shared a hug and promised to create something even bigger next time. They knew that if they worked together, they could paint the most wonderful universe.

"Lily, don't be scared," said her mommy. "Getting a haircut will make your hair healthy and pretty."

Lily still felt scared, but she trusted her mommy. They went to the hair salon, and the hairdresser cut Lily's hair just a little bit. When they were done, Lily looked in the mirror and saw that her hair still looked pretty. She wasn't scared anymore!

When Lily went back to school, her friends were jealous of her new haircut. "Your hair looks so pretty," they said. Lily felt happy and proud of her new haircut.

He took a long walk in the park and enjoyed the peaceful sunshine. As he walked, he passed by a tree with a swing hanging from one of its branches. Near the swing was a young girl, crying.

The old man stopped to look at the girl. He noticed her eyes were red and her face was sad. He asked the girl why she cried.

Gently, the man encouraged the girl to tell him what was wrong. The girl began to tell him about how she was afraid to go down the swing. She was scared that it would go too high or too fast.

So, the old man calmly put on his blue hat and hopped on the swing. He said, "See, I'm not afraid! Let's try it together.

The girl smiled, and she slowly climbed onto the swing beside him. Together they laughed and flew up in the air.

The old man encouraged the girl to keep trying. She did, and soon the girl was going higher and higher on the swing every time.

Lola was getting ready to go to the beach. She put on her floppy hat and summer dress. She grabbed her bucket and spade and skipped off to the sandy shore.

When she got there, the sea looked so vast and blue, like an endless ocean. She quickly ran over to it, ready to explore.

"Lola! Don't go too near. The water is heavy, and it can be dangerous," her mum called out.

Lola sighed. "But I want to dip my toes in the water," she said.

Mum shook her head. "No, Lola. You can go closer, but only with me. The sea can be very strong and deep."

Lola nodded, understanding her mum's words. She put her bucket down and felt the warm sand beneath her feet. She was so excited to explore!

Together, the two of them looked for shells, built sand castles and splashed in the waves. Lola had so much fun that before she knew it, the sun had set.

She quickly got dressed and hugged her mum. "Can I come back to the beach tomorrow?" she asked.

Mum smiled. "Yes, if you promise to stay close to me."

Lola nodded and smiled. She was already looking forward to her next beach adventure!

John was a very curious little boy. Everywhere he went, he liked to observe the things around him. One particular day, John noticed two children playing in the park. One of the children was wearing an attractive hat.

John went up to the children and asked, "What's that on your head?"

The child with the hat smiled and said, "It's my hat."

John wanted to see it, so the child let him try it on. It was warm and the rim felt nice on John's forehead.

"Can I keep it?" John asked excitedly.

The child shook her head and said, "No, I'm sorry. But I hope you will remember this hat and always observe the things around you."

John smiled and thanked the child, and as he went home he thought about the hat and the words of the older child.

John learned that day to always pay attention to his surroundings, and each day he tried to observe something new. Soon enough, he found his own hat-- it was attractive and he loved it!

One day, a little rabbit was hopping around the garden. Everywhere he looked he saw amazing things - bright flowers, tall trees, and yummy food. His favourite was sausages!

The little rabbit saw a wealthy man walking through the garden. He was wearing fancy clothes and had a big wallet in his pocket. The rabbit hopped over, hoping to get a sausage.

The man stopped, looked down at the rabbit and said, "Hello there! What would you like today?"

The rabbit hopped up and down and said, "Sausages please!"

The wealthy man smiled and took a sausage from his pocket. He gave it to the little rabbit, who hopped away happily. He hopped all the way home, where he enjoyed the tasty sausage.

The dog saw something shiny on the ground. It was a spike from a bike. The dog thought it was a toy. It picked up the spike with its mouth and ran away. Lily was angry and sad. She wanted her ball back. She ran after the dog and shouted.

The dog did not listen to Lily. It kept running with the spike. The spike hurt its mouth and made it bleed. The dog did not like the pain. It tried to spit out the spike, but it was stuck. The dog whimpered and slowed down. Lily caught up with the dog and grabbed its tail.

The dog was afraid and angry. It turned around and snapped at Lily. It bit her hand and made a wound. Lily screamed and let go of the dog. The dog ran away with the spike. Lily was hurt and bleeding. She fell to the ground and cried. No one came to help her. She was alone and in pain. The end.

After the fire was out, Tim's dad came back home. Tim said, "Dad, you are a hero! You stopped the fire at the store!" Tim's dad smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim. I am just doing my job to keep everyone safe." Tim was so proud of his dad, and they all had a big hug.

"I'm too nervous," replied the shy fish.

The shrimp smiled and replied, "That's ok, you don't have to do anything if you don't want to. I can allow you to just watch us if you like!"

The shy fish felt better, and decided to join. With that, he hopped and swam around with all the other fish. Everyone had lots of fun together, and the playful shrimp was very happy to have her friend join in on all the fun.

"John, is that you?" his father called out.

John nervously ran inside. He thought his father would be cross that he had taken the apron without asking.

"I found this apron in the garden," he said, feeling a bit scared.

But his father only smiled. "That's okay, you can keep it! It's available for you," he said.

John was so happy. He had found the perfect apron and his father had said he could keep it! He ran back outside, apron and all, ready to explore and have more adventures.

Once upon a time, there were two friends: a tiger and a bear. The tiger was always very jealous of the bear. The bear could climb up a tall ladder to reach the banana tree, but the tiger couldn't because he was too big and too clumsy.

One day, the bear came to the tiger's cave and said: "Friend, I'm tired from climbing the ladder and getting the bananas. Could you please come and help me?"

The tiger felt a little jealous but he said: "OK, I will come." Then he followed the bear to the banana tree. When they got there, the bear asked the tiger to hold the ladder while he climbed up. The tiger felt so jealous, watching the bear climb the ladder, but he held the ladder steady and soon enough, the bear got all the bananas.

The moral of this story is that it's important to help your friends and be unselfish. Even if we feel jealous sometimes, we should not let it stop us from being kind and generous to the ones we love.

Once upon a time, there was a big building. It was very tall and had lots of windows. One day, a monster came and wanted to destroy the building. The monster was very mean and had big teeth. He hit the building with his big hands, but it was very strong and didn't break.

Then, the monster got an idea. He went to get some tools to destroy the building. He got a hammer and a saw. He hit the building with the hammer and cut it with the saw. But the building was very thick and didn't break.

Finally, the monster gave up and went away. The building was still there, standing tall and strong. The people who lived inside were very happy that their home was safe. They knew that their building was very strong and could not be destroyed.

Tim was a boy who liked to play outside at night. He was not afraid of the dark or the noises he heard. He liked to look at the stars and the moon and listen to the crickets and the frogs. But he did not like the owl that lived in the big tree near his house. The owl was big and loud and had yellow eyes that glowed in the dark. Tim thought the owl was mean and scary and wanted to chase it away.

One night, Tim decided to throw rocks at the owl to make it leave. He sneaked out of his bed and went to the tree. He picked up some rocks and threw them at the owl. But the owl did not fly away. It flew down and landed on Tim's head. It scratched Tim's face and hair with its sharp claws and pecked him with its hard beak. Tim screamed and ran back to his house. He was hurt and scared and sorry.

His mom and dad heard him and came to help him. They cleaned his wounds and gave him a hug. They asked him what happened and why he was outside. Tim told them the truth. He said he was sorry for throwing rocks at the owl and that he was wrong. His mom and dad said they were glad he was honest and that they loved him. But they also said he should not hurt animals or play outside at night without permission. They said he should be kind and respectful to all living things and follow the rules. They said he should pray and ask God to forgive him and to help him be a good boy.

Tim felt bad for what he did and agreed with his mom and dad. He prayed and asked God to forgive him and to help him be a good boy. He also prayed and asked God to bless the owl and to heal its wounds. He hoped the owl would not be angry or afraid of him anymore. He learned his lesson and never threw rocks at the owl again. He also stayed in his bed at night and listened to the owl's hoots as a friend, not a foe. He realized the owl was not mean or scary, but beautiful and wise. He was not crazy, but curious. He was not a bad boy, but a growing boy. He thanked God for his mom and dad and for the owl and for the night. He closed his eyes and fell asleep.

One day, Lily was playing with Ted in her room. She pretended that Ted was a doctor and she was a patient. She put a bandage on his arm and gave him a spoon of honey. "There, Ted, you will feel better soon," she said.

But then she heard a loud noise from the kitchen. She ran to see what it was and left Ted on the floor. In the kitchen, she saw her mom and dad. They were holding a big cake with candles on it. "Surprise, Lily!" they said. "Happy birthday!"

Lily was so happy. She hugged her mom and dad and thanked them for the cake. They sang a song and cut the cake. Lily got a big piece with a lot of frosting. She ate it with a smile.

But then she remembered Ted. She ran back to her room and saw him on the floor. He looked sad and lonely. Lily felt sorry for him. She picked him up and hugged him. She rubbed his nose and said, "I'm sorry, Ted. I didn't forget you. You are my best friend. Do you want some cake?"

Ted nodded his head. Lily smiled and took him to the kitchen. She gave him a small piece of cake and a sip of milk. Ted smiled and licked his lips. He was happy again.

Lily and Ted ate their cake and milk together. They shared their birthday. They were both happy. They went back to their room and put their toys in the cupboard. They snuggled in bed and said good night. They dreamed of more adventures.

Jack said, "Yes, I can protect you. I am very strong and fair."

So, Jack and Lily walked together in the fields. Suddenly, they saw the scary wolf. Jack stood in front of Lily to protect her.

The wolf said, "I want to eat you, little girl!"

Jack said, "No, you cannot eat her. I will protect her."

The wolf was scared of Jack and ran away. Lily was very happy and said, "Thank you, Jack. You are my hero!"

From that day on, Jack and Lily were best friends and walked together in the fields every day.

Jack said, "What do you do?

The hedgehog smiled and said, "I'm a dependable hedgie. I keep you safe by living in your garden and keeping away harm.

Tom and Lily were friends who liked to play together. One day, they found a big ruler in the garage. It was a measure that could show how long things were. They decided to use it to measure themselves and see who was taller.

"Look, I am taller than you!" Tom said, standing next to the measure. He was very proud of his height.

"No, you are not!" Lily said, standing on her toes. She wanted to be as tall as Tom.

They both tried to stretch themselves as much as they could, but they were still the same height. They got angry and started to push each other.

"Stop it, you two!" Mom said, coming into the garage. She saw them fighting and took the measure away. "This is not a toy, it is a tool. You could hurt yourselves or break something. And it does not matter who is taller, you are both my children and I love you the same."

Tom and Lily felt ashamed of their behavior. They said sorry to each other and hugged. They realized that being friends was more important than being taller. They learned to be happy with who they were and not to compare themselves to others.

Mom smiled and gave them a cookie each. She was glad that they made up. She told them to go and play outside, but to be careful and not to be clumsy. She did not want them to fall or scrape their knees.

Tom and Lily nodded and ran outside. They found a ball and a hoop and had fun playing together. They forgot about the measure and their height. They were happy to have each other as friends.

Once upon a time, there was a old man who lived near the sea. His name was John. Every day, John would go out of his home to watch the sunset.

One night, something magical happened. As the sun slowly set, something familiar appeared to John. He couldn't believe his eyes. It was an old giant that looked just like him.

The giant waved and smiled at John. John smiled back, unsure what to do next. The giant then said "Hello, my name is Tom. Nice to meet you".

John was astonished. Then he said "Good day, my name is John. Nice to meet you too". He was so excited to introduce himself to someone else.

He was even more excited when the giant asked him to go for a walk. They walked under the beautiful orange sunset and talked about everything.

John and Tom became friends and the two of them watched sunsets together every day afterwards.

Lily was a chubby girl who liked to play with her dolls. She had many dolls, but her favorite was Anna, who had long yellow hair and a blue dress. Lily liked to pretend that Anna was a princess and she was her friend.

One day, Lily took Anna to the park with her mom. She saw a big black bird on a tree. She pointed at it and asked her mom, "What is that?"

"That is a raven, Lily. It is a kind of bird that can make loud noises and fly very fast," her mom said.

Lily wanted to see the raven closer. She left Anna on a bench and ran to the tree. She tried to climb it, but she was too chubby and slipped. She fell on the grass and hurt her knee. She started to cry.

The raven saw Lily and flew to the bench. It picked up Anna with its beak and flew away. Lily saw this and cried louder. She felt very sad and embarrassed. She wanted her doll back.

"Mommy, mommy, help me! The raven took Anna!" she shouted.

Her mom ran to her and hugged her. She said, "Don't worry, Lily. We will get Anna back. Let's go and find the raven."

They followed the raven to a nearby roof. They saw Anna on the edge of the roof. The raven was playing with her hair and dress. Lily shouted, "Give me Anna, you bad bird! She is mine!"

The raven looked at Lily and made a loud noise. It sounded like "Never, never!"

Lily felt angry and scared. She said, "Please, please, give me Anna. I love her. She is my friend."

The raven looked at Anna and then at Lily. It saw that Lily was crying and that she really loved her doll. It felt sorry for Lily. It flew to the roof and gently dropped Anna on the ground. It made a soft noise. It sounded like "Sorry, sorry."

Lily ran to Anna and hugged her. She said, "Thank you, raven. You are not so bad. You are a good bird."

The raven nodded and flew away. Lily smiled and kissed Anna. She said, "I'm so happy, Anna. You are safe. You are my princess."

Her mom smiled and said, "I'm proud of you, Lily. You were brave and kind. You made a new friend."

Lily and her mom went home. They played with Anna and had a nice tea party. Lily was not embarrassed anymore. She was happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Mandy who was very eager to learn how to tie her own shoes. Her mom had a ball of thread and she showed it to Mandy and said, "Look, this is thread. We can use it to tie your shoes."

Mandy was very excited and she said, "Yes! Let's do it!"

Mom grabbed the thread and started tying it around Mandy's shoelace. "Okay, see you have to pull the thread like this," she said, demonstrating for her.

Mandy watched intently and tried to follow what her mom was doing. After a few minutes, she got the hang of it and soon she was able to tie her own shoes! She was so proud and eager to show her mom what she had learned.

Mom was so proud of her and said, "That's wonderful, Mandy! Now you can tie your own shoes!" Mandy happily thanked her mom and showed her how she could tie her shoe.

After that, Mandy practiced tying her shoes every day until it became second-nature. Soon enough, she was able to tie her shoes with ease.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was walking along by the beach and she saw something shiny in the sand. She bent down to pick it up and it was a long rope! She thought it would be fun to play with it, so she started to twist it around her hands.

Suddenly, she heard a loud noise and when she looked up she saw that a big boat had crashed into the shore! Water was spreading everywhere and it was causing a wreck. The little girl was scared and started to cry, but then she realized that the rope she was twisting was actually attached to the boat and it was this rope that had stopped it from crashing more and causing even more harm.

The little girl was very brave and she used her rope to help the people from the boat get back to shore safely. She had relaxed the rope and they were able to climb to safety. In the end, the little girl's harmless twist saved the day! Everyone was very happy.

One day, the King and Queen had a surprise for the Princess. The King said: "My dear, we are taking you on a secret adventure. We will have fun and you can release your serious side for a little while." The Princess jumped for joy!

So Daisy went to find a special present. She wanted to give it to her sister, so that she would be happy again. With her big, green eyes, Daisy looked around the house until she found a pencil. It was special because it had a big feather on top.

Daisy was very excited! She went back to her sister and presented her with the pencil. Daisy's sister was delighted! One day, a playful dog named Spot saw a new path in the park. He wanted to explore it and see what was there. He ran to the path and barked at his friend, a shy cat named Fluffy.

"Come on, Fluffy, let's go to the new path. It looks fun and exciting. Don't you want to see what's there?" Spot said.

Fluffy looked at the path and felt scared. He saw that it was dark and narrow and had many thorns and rocks. He did not think it was fun or exciting. He thought it was dangerous and scary.

"No, Spot, I don't want to go to the new path. It looks bad and scary. I want to stay here where it's safe and sunny. You should stay here too, Spot. The new path might hurt you or get you lost." Fluffy said.

Spot laughed and wagged his tail. He did not listen to Fluffy. He thought Fluffy was silly and boring. He wanted to have an adventure and show Fluffy how brave he was.

"Fluffy, you are silly and boring. You are afraid of everything. I am not afraid of anything. I am brave and curious. I dare you to come with me to the new path. If you don't, I will call you a chicken and a coward." Spot said.

Fluffy felt sad and angry. He did not like being called a chicken and a coward. He wanted to be Spot's friend and have fun with him. But he also did not want to go to the new path. He did not know what to do.

What do you think Fluffy should do? Should he go with Spot to the new path or stay where he is? What do you think will happen to Spot and Fluffy? What is the moral of the story?

Once upon a time there was a kind, gentle king who ruled over a small kingdom. He was very beloved by his people and was known for always making wise decisions.

One morning, the king was taking a walk in his garden, when he noticed a delicate cloth on the ground. It had beautiful gold embroidery and must have belonged to a very rich person.

The king picked up the cloth and wondered how it had gotten there. At that moment, one of his servants hurried over to him.

The servant announced, "Your Majesty! You must lead us to find out who the cloth belongs to. It looks like a priceless heirloom and we should return it to the rightful owner."

The king agreed and set off with his servants on a journey to find the owner of the cloth. After a long search, the king and his servants found the true owner of the cloth - a poor family living in a nearby village.

The kind king took the cloth to the family and thanked them for their hard work and care. He also taught them a valuable lesson : always look after your possessions and take care of them as if they are as delicate as the cloth.

The family thanked the king and his servants for their kindness. From that day on, the king and his people led by example and taught others the same valuable lesson.

One day, a little girl named Mia and a little boy named Tim were playing outside. They saw a small flame in the grass. They were uncomfortable because they knew fire can be dangerous. They wanted to solve the problem.

Mia said, "We need water to put out the flame!" Tim agreed, "Yes, let's get water from the house." They ran to the house and filled a big cup with water. They were careful not to spill any water.

Mia and Tim went back to the flame and poured the water on it. The flame went away and they were happy. They solved the problem and kept everyone safe. They went back to playing, proud of their teamwork.

Lily liked to play dress-up with her dolls. She had many skirts and hats and shoes for them. She liked to make them look pretty and have tea parties. But her brother Max did not like dolls. He liked to play with cars and trucks and robots. He thought dolls were boring and silly.

One day, Lily and Max had to share the playroom. Lily wanted to play dress-up with her dolls, but Max wanted to play race with his cars. They both said, "This is my space. Go away!" They did not want to share. They started to fight and pull each other's toys. Lily grabbed Max's robot and Max grabbed Lily's skirt. They both tried to take them back.

"Give me my robot!" Max shouted.

"Give me my skirt!" Lily shouted.

They pulled and pulled, but neither of them would let go. The robot and the skirt started to rip and tear. Lily and Max both heard a loud snap. They looked at their hands and saw what they had done. The robot's arm was broken and the skirt was ripped in half. They both felt sad and sorry. They had ruined their toys.

They looked at each other and said, "I'm sorry. I didn't mean to break your toy."

"I'm sorry, too. I didn't mean to rip your skirt."

They hugged and said, "I love you. You are my brother/sister."

They decided to share the playroom and play together. They fixed the robot's arm with some tape and made a new skirt for the doll with some cloth. They played dress-up and race and had fun. They both learned to be kind and share. They both felt happy and proud. They both won.

Tommy was playing with his truck. It was his favorite toy. He watched it excitedly as it raced around the floor. Suddenly he saw something. It was fierce. He was scared.

It was an angry dog. It was snarling and barking loudly. It was clearly very mad. Tommy was scared and backed away. But the dog was faster, it was coming closer and closer.

Tommy tried to run but the dog was too fast. It pounced on Tommy and bit him. Tommy screamed in pain. His truck lay forgotten on the floor as the dog ran away.

Tommy's skin was burning and throbbing. He had been bitten and it hurt a lot. Tommy's parents rushed to his side. They saw the teeth marks and they knew that the dog had pinched him. Tommy was crying. His parents were worried and sad.

Sadly the dog was never found and Tommy was scarred for life. The fierce dog had caused Tommy a great deal of pain and sorrow.

Lila and Tom were playing in the garden. They had a big bucket of water and some cups. They liked to pour water from one cup to another and make splashes.

"Look, Tom, I can change the water!" Lila said. She poured some water into a red cup and then into a blue cup. The water looked purple.

"Wow, that's cool, Lila!" Tom said. He wanted to try too. He poured some water into a yellow cup and then into a green cup. The water looked brown.

They laughed and clapped their hands. They changed the water again and again, making different colors.

But then, Mom came out of the house. She looked serious. She saw the water on the grass and on their clothes.

"Lila and Tom, what are you doing?" Mom asked. "You are wasting water and making a mess. Water is important. You should not play with it like that."

Lila and Tom felt sorry. They did not know water was important. They thought it was just fun.

"Sorry, Mom," they said. "We did not mean to waste water. We were just changing the water."

Mom sighed. She was not angry, but she wanted them to learn. She took them inside and showed them a book. The book had pictures of water in different places: in the sky, in the sea, in the river, in the tap.

"Water is not just for playing," Mom said. "Water is for drinking, for washing, for growing plants, for living. Water can change too, but not by pouring it into cups. Water can change by heat and cold, by sun and rain, by wind and waves. Water can be solid, liquid, or gas. Water can be clear, cloudy, or colorful. Water is amazing. Do you want to learn more about water?"

Lila and Tom nodded. They were curious. They wanted to learn more about water. They said sorry again and hugged Mom.

"Thank you, Mom," they said. "You are amazing too."

Anna and Ben are playing with a ball in the park. They like to pass the ball to each other and catch it. Sometimes they miss the ball and it rolls away. Then they run after it and laugh.

One time, Ben passes the ball too hard and it flies over Anna's head. It hits a man who is walking his dog. The man says "Ow!" and the dog barks. Anna and Ben are scared. They run to the man and say "Sorry, sorry, sorry!"

The man is not angry. He rubs his head and smiles. He says "It's okay, kids. It was an accident. But be careful next time, okay?" He picks up the ball and gives it back to Anna and Ben. He says "Have fun, but watch where you throw the ball."

Anna and Ben say "Thank you, sir. We will be careful." They take the ball and go back to their spot. They pass the ball more gently and say "Sorry" to each other when they miss. They are happy to play with the ball and hear each other's voice.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily had a cozy bed with a soft mattress. One day, Lily's friend Tom came to visit her. Tom said, "Lily, can I join you in your bed?" Lily replied, "Yes, you can join me."

Tom climbed into the bed and accidentally spilled his juice on the mattress. Lily looked at Tom and said, "Tom, you made a mess. You should feel guilty." Tom felt bad and said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I didn't mean to spill my juice."

Lily forgave Tom and said, "It's okay, Tom. We can clean it up together." They grabbed some towels and cleaned up the juice. Tom felt happy that Lily wasn't mad at him anymore. They snuggled up in the bed together and fell asleep.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play on his rug with his toys. One day, Timmy's sister accidentally hurt his foot while they were playing. Timmy cried and cried because his foot hurt so much.

Timmy's mom came to see what happened and she saw that Timmy was hurt. She picked him up and put him on the couch. Timmy's sister felt really bad and wanted to make him feel better. She asked Timmy if he wanted to marry her favorite toy, a teddy bear.

Timmy thought that was a great idea and he stopped crying. He hugged the teddy bear and smiled. Timmy's sister was happy that he wasn't sad anymore and they continued playing on the rug together.

Once upon a time, in a small garden, there was a white bunny named Fluffy. Fluffy loved to play with his friends, the birds and the bugs. One sunny day, Fluffy saw a big, round squash. It was too high for him to reach. He wanted to play with it, so he asked his friend, the bird, for help.

"Please, bird, can you help me reach the big squash?" Fluffy asked. The bird nodded and flew up to the squash. The bird pushed the squash, and it fell down to the ground. Fluffy was very happy and thanked the bird.

Together, Fluffy and the bird rolled the squash around the garden. They played with it all day long. When they were tired, they sat down under a tree and shared some yummy leaves. They were happy to have each other as friends and to have such a fun day.

Once upon a time, there was a silly clown. He had a big red nose and wore a colorful hat. One day, he went to a party and brought his pen with him. He wanted to draw funny pictures for the kids.

When the clown started to draw, the kids clapped their hands. They loved the silly pictures he made. The clown felt happy and continued to draw more pictures. The kids clapped louder and louder each time he finished a drawing.

At the end of the party, the clown thanked the kids for clapping and gave each of them a silly drawing as a gift. The kids were so happy and thanked the clown for making their day so much fun. From that day on, the clown knew that drawing with his pen and making silly pictures was his favorite thing to do.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was very nice, and she liked to help her mommy. One day, her mommy asked her to help with the cooking.

The little girl was very excited! She helped mix all the ingredients together, so that they could make a fancy cauliflower dish. Then, they put the cauliflower in the oven to bake.

The little girl was anxious to see how it would taste, but she had to wait until it was finished. After a long wait, the little girl ran to the oven and opened the door.

The cauliflower looked so fancy and delicious! The little girl was so happy that she and her mommy had made something so yummy. She decided that the dish must've tasted delicious, because it looked so fancy.

The little girl was so proud that she had helped her mommy. She was glad to know that her help had made such a yummy dish!

Once upon a time there was a small orange caterpillar. He was very wide and even wider when he stretched his body out. Everyday he would explore around his garden, looking for something new.

One day he came across a very strange plant. He had never seen anything like it before.

The caterpillar crawled up to it and asked, "What do you do?"

The plant replied, "I can help you to understand the world around you."

The caterpillar was surprised. He asked, "How can you do that?"

The plant said, "By showing you what is wide and what is narrow."

The caterpillar thought for a moment and then understood what the plant meant. From then on, the caterpillar used the plant's advice to explore the world around him.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to climb trees in her backyard. One day, she climbed up a tall tree and saw her friend Max walking by.

"Hi Max!" Lily shouted from the tree.

Max looked up and saw Lily. "Wow, you're really high up there!" he said.

Lily giggled. "I love to climb. Do you want to climb with me?"

Max shook his head. "No thanks, I don't like climbing. I came to see if you wanted to have cereal with me. My mom made some regular cereal and it's really yummy."

Lily thought for a moment. "Okay, I'll come down and have cereal with you. But then we have to climb another tree!"

Max smiled. "Deal!"

So, Lily climbed down the tree and they both went inside to have cereal. After they finished, they went outside and climbed another tree together. They had so much fun that they decided to do it again the next day!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside in the wild woods. He would run around and measure how far he could go. One day, Timmy met a little girl named Lily. Lily had a big smile on her face and Timmy thought she was nice.

"Hi, Lily! Do you want to play with me?" Timmy asked.

"Yes, I would love to play with you!" Lily replied with a smile.

Timmy and Lily played together for a while, but then they started to argue about who was better at measuring. They both wanted to be the best. The argument got bigger and bigger until they were both very angry.

In the end, Timmy and Lily stopped being friends because of their argument. Timmy felt very sad and wished he had never argued with Lily. He realized that being friends was more important than being the best at measuring.

Once upon a time there was a small body. The body lived alone and felt very lonely. One day the body was walking on a path when it came to a wide river. It wanted to go to the other side, but didn't know how. Suddenly, it heard a voice.

The body jumped and looked around. It saw a small creature standing on a rock. "I can help you win," the creature said, pointing to the river.

The body looked around and saw a small boat. It started to get in the boat, but then realized it hadn't paid the creature. "What do I owe you?" the body asked.

The creature smiled. "You don't owe me anything. You can learn to help others without expecting anything in return. That is the way to win," the creature said.

The body thanked the creature and began rowing the boat. And so, the lonely body not only crossed the river, but also learned the value of kindness.

Once upon a time, there was a little mouse named Tim. Tim was a very reliable mouse. Every day, he would help his mom get food for the family. Tim's mom was very proud of him.

One day, while Tim was out getting food, he found a toy car. Tim thought, "I can replace my old toy car with this one!" He picked up the toy car and ran to show his mom.

When he got home, Tim said, "Look, Mom! I found a new toy car to replace my old one!" His mom smiled and said, "Good job, Tim! You are such a reliable mouse. I'm so proud of you!" Tim and his mom played with the toy car all day, and they were very happy.

Joseph found a battery on the ground in the park. He picked it up and brought it home.

When he got home, he showed his mom and dad what he had found. "What can I do with this battery?" Joseph asked.

"We can use it to power something," said mom.

Joseph thought for a moment but he couldn't stay what he wanted to power. His parents smiled at each other.

"Let's put it in this toy car," said dad. "Then your car can stay moving all day!"

They put the battery in the toy car.
Joseph and his parents stepped outside and the car started moving. It was hot outside, but the car kept going!

Joseph was so excited that he jumped up and down in the park. His parents laughed and joined in.

The battery kept going and going until it was almost dark outside. Then they took the battery out and brought it back inside.

Joseph put the battery away and went to bed. He couldn't wait until tomorrow to put the battery back in the toy car and play with it again.

Once there was a little frog. He hopped around the pond, trying to find something tasty and fun to do. Then one day he spotted it! A and new, shiny piece of gum. He wanted to grab it, but he was too scared. He had never seen something like it before. So he hopped a bit closer, and a little closer again. Then with one final big hop he sprang towards the gum and nibbled it.

What a surprise! It was so delicious and had such great flavour! He hopped around in joy, tasting the delicious gum. Then he hopped away and left the new gum behind.

The little frog had a great day, and now he knew that new and delicious things can be found if you just hop around.

Once there was a little bird called John. He was always happy and whistling. One day, his friend Bob asked him what he wanted to do today. John said he wanted to go and explore the world.

So they flew off together and soon came across a big dark forest. Bob was a bit worried and asked John if they should turn back, but John thought they should explore a bit further.

John flew over the trees and saw something that caught his eye. As he flew closer, he realized it was a nosy war. Bob knew what was coming next and he was worried since he was scared of wars. Still, he wanted to help John and followed him.

When they got inside the war, everything was loud, John got scared and started to whistle. As he was doing it, his whistling stopped the war. Everybody was surprised. They thought John was a magical bird! John and Bob soon flew away, happy and proud of their adventure.

Molly decided to get one. It was filled with cheese and tomatoes. It smelled so sweet. She opened it up and started to chew.

Molly chewed and chewed and chewed until the sandwich was all gone. She was so happy and felt so full.

Then she got a special treat. The subway worker gave her a cookie. It was really sweet. She smiled and started to chew on it.

Molly chewed and chewed until the cookie was all gone. She was so happy and felt so content. She thanked the subway worker and went on her way, ready to take on the day!

Once upon a time, there lived a bee in a hive. It was a clean hive and all the other bees had a happy life there. One day, the bee got sick and started to suffer. Soon, all the other bees noticed it and started to worry.

The Queen Bee gathered all the other bees and asked: "What can we do for our fellow bee who is suffering?"

The other bees thought and thought and finally, one of them proposed that they should try to make the hive even cleaner for the sick bee. So all the bees went to work and cleaned the hive from top to bottom.

When they were done, the sick bee felt better and the other bees were very happy. From then on, they took care to keep the hive always very clean and the sick bee had a happy and healthy life!

Once upon a time, there was a little birdie named Tweetie. Tweetie was very eager to fly and explore the world outside her nest. One day, she saw a twig on the ground and wondered what it would feel like to pick it up with her tiny beak.

Tweetie hopped down from her nest and tried to pick up the twig, but it was too heavy for her. She wondered if she could fly with it in her beak. So, she flapped her wings and tried to lift the twig off the ground. After a few tries, she finally lifted it up and flew back to her nest.

Her mama birdie was so proud of her and said, "You are a strong little birdie, Tweetie!" From that day on, Tweetie continued to explore the world around her, always eager to learn and discover new things. And she always remembered the day when she lifted the twig with her beak.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing outside in the garden. One day, she found a daisy and she was very happy. She picked it up and showed it to her mom. "Look mommy, I found a pretty daisy!" she said, feeling proud.

Her mom smiled and said, "That's lovely, Lily. Did you know that daisies can reveal a secret?" Lily's eyes widened with curiosity. "Really? What secret?" she asked.

Her mom replied, "If you pull the petals off one by one and say 'he loves me, he loves me not', the last petal will reveal if your crush loves you or not." Lily was amazed and decided to try it with her friend Jack. She pulled the petals off the daisy and the last one revealed that Jack did indeed love her. Lily was very happy and proud of her daisy's secret revealing power.

Bobby was very enthusiastic about the tide. He wanted to explore the shore and see all of the wonderful things that the tide was bringing.

So one day, Bobby invited his friends to come to the beach with him. They all had so much fun playing in the sand and collecting treasured from the beach.

Afterwards, they all went to a nearby pond and invited the ducks living there to join them. The ducks were so eager to join in on the fun and they followed them back to the beach.

At the beach, Bobby and his friends built castles in the sand and watched the ducks swim in the tide. The tide was so beautiful as it glistened in the sunshine.

They all had an amazing time at the beach, and when it was time to go, they all said goodbye to the ducks and thanked them for coming to join the fun. Bobby was so proud that he was able to invite his friends and the ducks to share in the wonderful experience that the tide had to offer.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Beatrice. She was walking in the woods and was getting very tired. She saw a tree and thought she should take a rest.

Beatrice sat down and looked up at the tree. She noticed something strange. There was a knot in the bark of the tree, and it was a bit different than the other knots.

Beatrice asked the tree, "What can I learn from you, wise tree?".

The tree replied, "If you look closely children, you can learn something from everything, even something as small as a knot on a tree. Each knot is different and has something to teach you."

Beatrice smiled and thought about what the tree had said. She thanked the tree and went on her journey, feeling a bit wiser.

Moral: You can learn something from everyone and everything you encounter, so don't take even the smallest detail for granted.

Once upon a time, there was a big lion who was very hungry. He had not eaten anything for many days. He walked around in the jungle, looking for food. Suddenly, he saw a group of animals marching towards him. They were happy and had big smiles on their faces.

The lion was scared at first, but then he realized that they were not afraid of him. They were marching towards him to give him food. They gave him a big piece of meat and the lion was very happy. He ate the meat and his hunger went away.

The lion was so happy that he started to march with the animals. They all had big smiles on their faces and they were happy to have helped the lion. From that day on, the lion was never hungry again and he became friends with the animals who had helped him.

Once upon a time, there was a big dog named Max. Max was always grumpy because he didn't like his dog food. One day, Max's owner gave him a big bone to chew on. Max was so happy and started to chew the bone with his big teeth. His mouth was wide open as he chewed and chewed.

Suddenly, Max heard a noise and looked up. He saw a little girl with a ball. The ball rolled towards Max and he stopped chewing. The little girl smiled and Max's grumpy face turned into a happy one. The little girl played with Max and they had so much fun together. From that day on, Max wasn't grumpy anymore because he had a new friend to play with and a bone to chew on.

Once upon a time, there was a little bear named Teddy. Teddy loved to play with his toys, but one day his favorite toy broke. He was sad and didn't know what to do.

His mommy said, "Yes, I can repair it. But first, let's have some honey to make you feel better."

Teddy's mommy gave him some honey, and he felt a little better. Then, she took his toy and repaired it. Teddy was so happy and hugged his mommy.
