Mother and Suzie were walking through the park. Suzie had on her red coat, and she was smiling. It was cold out, but Suzie didn't mind.

Suddenly, Mommy said, "Oh look Suzie! Strawberries!" Suzie saw a bush full of big, juicy strawberries. She wanted to pick one, so she did. It was so sweet and juicy!

Next, Mommy said, "Let's have an icy treat!" So they went to a shop and got icy ice cream. Suzie got strawberry ice cream. It was so cool and yummy!

When they finished their treat, Suzie and Mommy went back home. Suzie was so happy because she got to eat a strawberry and wear her red coat!

Tom and Sue are friends. They like to play with blocks. They build towers and houses and bridges. They have fun.

One day, they find a big block. It is red and shiny. It looks like a jewel. They want to play with it. They take it to their room.

"Look at this block!" Tom says. "It is so pretty. I want to do something with it."

"Me too," Sue says. "But what can we do?"

They think and think. They have an idea. They put the block on the floor. They jump on it.

"Crack!" The block breaks. It is not a block. It is a glass. It has sharp pieces. The pieces cut Tom and Sue. They cry.

Mom and Dad hear them. They run to the room. They see the glass and the blood. They are angry and sad.

"Who did this?" Mom asks. "Who broke the glass?"

Tom and Sue are scared. They do not want to tell. They lie.

"It was not us," Tom says. "It was the cat."

"It was not the cat," Sue says. "It was the wind."

Mom and Dad do not believe them. They know they are lying. They take them to the doctor. The doctor fixes their cuts. He gives them a bandage.

"You are lucky," the doctor says. "The glass could have hurt you more. You should not lie. You should tell the truth. Lying is bad. Truth is good."

Tom and Sue feel sorry. They know they did wrong. They say sorry to Mom and Dad. They say sorry to the cat. They say sorry to the wind. They learn a lesson. They do not lie again.

The end.

Moral: Lying is bad. Truth is good.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mom asked her to bring her a spoon from the kitchen drawer. But when Lily opened the drawer, she saw that it was rough and hard to open.

Lily said, "Mommy, the drawer is rough. I can't open it."

Her mom replied, "That's okay, Lily. Sometimes things are hard to do, but we have to keep trying."

Lily tried again and finally opened the drawer. She brought her mom the spoon and felt proud of herself. Her mom hugged her and said, "See, Lily? You did it! Remember, if something is hard, don't give up. Keep trying and you'll succeed."

From that day on, Lily remembered to always try her best and never give up, even if things were rough.

John and his dad went to the park. As they walked, they saw many animals. John saw a rabbit and wanted to get close to it, so he went to a small hole.

"Dad, can I talk to the rabbit?" asked John.

His dad had to explain that the rabbit was probably scared and would run away.

John thought for a moment and then asked, "Can we discuss the hole?"

His dad was happy with the idea and said, "Yes, let's discuss the hole".

John and his dad knelt down to take a closer look at the hole. They could see that it was small, but there was room for the rabbit to get in and out.

John was very pleased, and said, "The rabbit can live in the hole!"

His dad smiled and said, "That's a great idea, John. Now we just need to make sure the hole is safe for the rabbit to use."

John and his dad kept discussing the hole and discussing how they could make it safer. They talked until they knew it was safe for the rabbit to use the hole as his home.

John and his dad were very happy that they had helped the rabbit. They waved goodbye and went home.

Sara and Ben went to the doctor with Mom. They had to wait in a big room with many chairs and toys. Sara and Ben played with the blocks and the books. They were lively and happy.

"Next, please," a lady said. She had a badge with her name and a picture of a bone. Mom took Sara and Ben to a small room. There was a big machine and a screen.

"This is an x-ray machine," the lady said. "It can take a picture of your bones. We need to number your fingers and toes, so we can see them better. Can you help me?"

Sara and Ben nodded. They liked to number things. The lady gave them each a sticker with a number on it. She asked them to put the stickers on their fingers and toes.

"One, two, three, four, five," Sara said, as she stuck the stickers on her left hand.

"Six, seven, eight, nine, ten," Ben said, as he stuck the stickers on his right foot.

"Very good," the lady said. "Now, let's take the x-ray. You need to stand still and smile. Mom will be right here with you."

The lady pressed a button. The machine made a beep. The screen showed a picture of Sara and Ben's bones. They could see the numbers on their fingers and toes.

"Wow, look at that," Mom said. "You have beautiful bones. Can you see them?"

Sara and Ben looked at the screen. They saw their bones and the numbers. They smiled and clapped.

"Yes, we can see them," they said. "They are cool."

The lady printed the picture and gave it to Mom. She also gave Sara and Ben a sticker with a star on it.

"You did a great job," the lady said. "You are very brave and smart. Thank you for helping me with the x-ray."

Sara and Ben thanked the lady. They hugged Mom. They were proud and happy. They showed their x-ray picture and their star stickers to everyone. They liked to number their bones.

Once upon a time, there was a girl called Sally. She loved to play games, especially guessing games. Every day, Sally and her family would play a guessing game at mealtime. Today for lunch, Sally's mom made a very tidy meal.

Sally's dad began the game by asking, "I wonder what we are having for lunch today?" Sally tried hard to guess what the meal might be. She thought of all her favorite foods, but the meal didn't look like any of them.

Then, Sally's Mom smiled and said, "Let's see if you can guess our lunch today. Take a bite and guess!" Sally took a big bite of the meal. It was delicious! She quickly guessed it was her Mom's special pasta dish.

Everyone was so happy that Sally was able to guess the meal. Sally cheered happily and her family clapped for her. Sally loved these guessing games at mealtime!

Once upon a time there was a little boy, named John, who was very adventurous. He was always looking for an exciting new adventure. One day, while he was playing in the park, he met a pelican.

John and the pelican started to become good friends and they decided to go on an adventurous journey together. But when it came time to decide where they would go, they started to quarrel over the price of their trip.

John said: "It should be free!"

But the pelican replied: "No, we must have a price for this journey!"

John was getting frustrated and said: "I don't want to!"

But the pelican said gently: "You must pay something for the pleasure of exploring new places."

John understood what the pelican was trying to tell him and decided to continue their adventure by paying with something special - something from his heart. So, on their journey, they shared their experiences and their joys, always paying with the best things - their love and friendship.

At the end of the journey, John and the pelican had learned a valuable lesson - a good adventure is not worth the price of giving up something special.

Tim and Sam were playing in the park. They liked to pretend they were knights and fight with sticks. They found some big sticks that looked like weapons. Tim had a long stick that was impressive. Sam had a short stick that was not.

They started to fight with their sticks. Tim swung his stick at Sam. Sam blocked with his stick. Sam swung his stick at Tim. Tim blocked with his stick. They fought for a long time. They had fun.

But then Tim got angry. He wanted to win. He swung his stick harder and harder. Sam got scared. He tried to run away. But Tim chased him. He hit Sam with his stick. Sam fell down. He cried.

Tim stood over Sam. He felt proud. He had won. He did not see the big dog that was running towards him. The dog did not like Tim. The dog bit Tim. Tim screamed. He dropped his stick. He fell down. He cried.

Sam got up. He saw Tim and the dog. He felt sorry for Tim. He picked up his stick. He ran to help Tim. He hit the dog with his stick. The dog ran away. Sam helped Tim. They were both hurt. They were both sad. They did not have fun.

Molly was excited because today was the day she fed the fish! She put on her favorite bright blue dress and leapt out of bed. Molly couldn't wait to see the cool fish in their tank.

She was nearly to the bathroom where the tank was stored when she saw it - a huge pile of fish food! It was scattered on the ground and she wondered who fed the fish without cleaning up.

"Mommy, look!" Molly shouted.

Mommy smiled. "Yes, Molly, but we have to pick it up and feed the fish like we do every day."

Molly smiled, quickly picking up all the food. She opened the tank and sprinkled it on the water. Then she moved around quickly to see the cool fish swimming around.

Molly then ran off to the bathroom to get ready for the day. She couldn't wait to do it all again tomorrow!

Tom and Ann are friends. They like to play outside. They run, jump, and laugh. They have fun.

One day, Tom and Ann go to Tom's house. Tom's mom says, "You can watch TV for a little bit. Then you have to do your homework."

Tom and Ann say, "OK, thank you." They sit on the couch and turn on the TV. They see a show with animals. They like animals.

The show has a dog and a cat. The dog is big and brown. The cat is small and white. The dog and the cat do not like each other. They fight and chase. They make noise.

Tom and Ann watch the show. They think it is funny. They giggle and point.

"What is the dog's name?" Ann asks.

"I don't know," Tom says. "Maybe it is Spot."

"What is the cat's name?" Tom asks.

"I don't know," Ann says. "Maybe it is Fluffy."

They watch some more. The dog and the cat are lazy. They lie on the floor and sleep. They do not fight and chase. They do not make noise.

Tom and Ann are bored. They do not think it is funny. They yawn and look away.

"Let's turn off the TV," Tom says.

"OK, let's do that," Ann says. "Let's play something else."

They turn off the TV and get up. They go to Tom's room. They find some toys and books. They play and read. They have fun.

Once upon a time, there was a small rat. He was very smart. He knew the trick to reverse things. One day he was walking in the garden and saw a dog chasing a cat.

The rat ran to the cat and said, "Quick! I know how we can help you!" The cat was confused and asked, "How?"

The rat said, "Let's reverse this! Climb on my back, and I will take you to safety!" The cat was scared, but the rat was so smart, she believed him.

The rat quickly ran the other way and outsmarted the dog. The cat thanked the rat and said, "You are so smart!" The rat smiled and said, "It was no problem. Let's make sure this never happens again." The cat was so happy and together they made sure the dog would never scare her again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Daisy. Daisy was three years old and loved to play. She loved to play in the park and make new friends.

One day, Daisy was playing in the park when she noticed a big tree. The tree was very tall but also bent in one spot. Daisy decided to take a closer look and noticed that the tree was leaning. She was curious, so she decided to lean against the tree too.

When Daisy leaned against the tree, she felt something soft. It felt like a cloth! Daisy was happy to find a soft cloth in the park and decided to take it home. As she carried the cloth home, she was careful to keep it away from the mud and puddles.

When Daisy got home, she showed her mom the cloth. Her mom said it was made of very mild, soft material and must have been special. Daisy smiled and hugged her mom. Daisy would keep the cloth forever because of its special, mild feeling.

This is the story of a small bird who was very lonely. Every day the bird flew around looking for someone to talk to, but no one was around.

One day, the bird was so tired after its long flight that it decided to rest on a branch and close its eyes. As it dozed, the bird heard a voice below. 'What a beautiful day!' said the voice.

The bird opened its eyes and looked down. It saw a little girl looking up at the sky. 'Do you like the weather?' the bird said.

The little girl smiled. 'Yes! I like to spend time outside when the weather is nice,' she said.

The bird was happy to have found a friend. From then on, whenever it was lonely it flew down to the branch to rest and the little girl and the bird talked about the weather.

Once upon a time, there was an ugly ground. The ground was very sad. Nobody came to play on the ground.

One day, it started to rain. The ground was even more sad, but then it heard a voice.

"Let's relax!" the voice said. It was a small boy. The boy started to move around and relax on the ground. He rolled around in the mud and laughed. The ground started to feel happy.

Every day, the small boy came to the ugly ground and relaxed. He laughed and sang. The ground was so happy to have new friends. The ground was no longer ugly. Now, everybody came to relax and play on the ground.

And they all lived happily ever after!

Once there was a large tree in a meadow. It was so tall, it touched the clouds. Every day, the tree would lean against the wind. One day, the wind was especially strong and the tree had to lean very hard. To keep itself from falling, it spread its roots even deeper into the ground. In the end, the result of the tree's efforts was that it was even sturdier and taller than before. The meadow was now filled with its large branches. Everyone who saw the tree was amazed and they knew that it would never fall down.

Once upon a time there was a boy named Luke. He was three years old. He was playing in his backyard when he looked up and saw something he had never seen before. He saw something strange on the ceiling of the sky. It appeared to be a big, shiny star.

Luke was very excited and he ran inside to tell his parents. "Mommy! Daddy! Come outside and see! I see a star on the ceiling!" he said excitedly.

His parents followed him outdoors and sure enough, there was a big, shiny star on the ceiling of the sky. They were very surprised and couldn't believe their eyes!

"That star must be from a wealthy family," said his father. "Yes," said his mother, "because it is so big and shiny."

Luke was amazed that something so beautiful and sparkly could hang from the ceiling of the sky. He smiled with joy and ran back outside to play. From that day, Luke always looked up at the star when he was playing in the backyard, and it made him feel very special.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was taking a boat ride on the sea. Everything was so calm and peaceful. Suddenly, the boat started to move up and down and it felt strange. The little girl was scared because she knew something wasn't right. She started to feel terrible.

Suddenly, the boat started to sink! The little girl was so scared that she started to cry. Everything looked so dark and the water was so cold. She tried to stay calm even though she was feeling terrible.

Suddenly, the little girl saw something. It was a boat! She screamed and waved her hands to get the boat's attention. The boat came towards her and the people inside helped her get in the boat. She was so happy that she was safe. She smiled and hugged everyone who helped her.

The little girl was so grateful for their help and she promised to never forget how she felt when the boat started to sink.

Tom and Lily were playing with blocks. They wanted to build a big temple like the one they saw in a book. They had many blocks of different colors, but they liked the yellow ones best.

"Let's add more yellow blocks to the top," Tom said. "It will look like the sun."

"No, I want to add more blue blocks," Lily said. "It will look like the sky."

They started to argue and pull the blocks from each other. The temple began to shake and fall apart.

"Stop it, you two!" Mom said. She came into the room and saw the mess. "You need to share and work together, or you will ruin your temple."

Tom and Lily felt sorry. They did not want to fight or break their temple. They said sorry to each other and hugged.

"Can we try again, Mom?" Tom asked.

"Of course, you can," Mom said. "But this time, why don't you mix the colors and make a rainbow temple?"

Tom and Lily liked that idea. They smiled and started to add blocks of all colors to their temple. They made it bigger and prettier than before. They were happy and proud of their temple.

Once there was a girl. She was only three. She had a bad chair in her room.

One day, the girl sat in her chair and started to talk to it. She told the chair all sorts of things - how her day was, what she liked to do, and even the secrets she had been keeping.

The chair seemed to listen to her. It just sat there quietly and gave her a safe place to talk. The girl felt better after talking to her chair. She knew it was a bad chair, but it had given her a place to feel safe.

The girl talked to her chair every single day. She told it all sorts of stories and secrets. The chair never talked back, but it was nice to have something to talk to. That bad chair was the only friend she had.

Once upon a time, there was a tall clown. He was very funny and made everyone laugh. One day, he went to a big circus and saw lots of people. He wanted to sign his name on the wall, so he took a big pen and wrote his name. But the circus owner saw him and got very angry. He said the clown was a bad clown and kicked him out of the circus. The clown was very sad and went home. He never went back to the circus again.

Once upon a time, there was a mighty fisherman. He had a big fishing rod and lots of nets. Every day he would go out on his boat to cover the sea. He would go in the morning and come back in the evening with lots of fish. All the people in the village would come to buy his fish and admired his mighty fishing skills. He was always very happy to share his fish with the people of the village. One day, the fisherman was in a hurry and did not want to waste too much time covering the sea. He made a special wish and sure enough, the wish came true. From that day onwards, he caught so many fish it was almost unbelievable! They said it was a magical wish that granted the fisherman an abundance of fish! The people of the village clapped and thanked the fisherman for his fish. He was so happy to share his mighty fishing skills with everyone.

Once upon a time, there was a big fat cat named Fluffy. Fluffy lived in a cozy house with her owner, a kind lady named Sarah. Every day, Fluffy would look out the window and dream of exploring the valley beyond the house.

One day, Sarah decided to take Fluffy on a walk in the valley. Fluffy was so excited to finally see what was beyond her window. As they walked, Fluffy noticed a big, scary dog in the distance. Sarah tried to protect Fluffy, but the dog attacked and Fluffy suffered a big bite.

Fluffy was rushed to the vet, but unfortunately, the bite was too severe and Fluffy did not survive. Sarah was heartbroken and wished she had never taken Fluffy into the valley. From that day on, Sarah made sure to keep all her furry friends safe and sound inside the cozy house.

Lily loved to draw with her pastels. She had many colors and liked to make pictures of flowers, animals and people. One day, she wanted to draw her grandma, who was very kind and elderly. She asked her grandma to sit on the sofa and smile for her.

"Can I draw you, grandma?" Lily said.

"Of course, my dear. I would love to see your art," grandma said.

Lily took her pastels and a big paper and started to draw. She used yellow for grandma's hair, blue for her eyes, pink for her cheeks and brown for her dress. She tried to make her look happy and pretty.

But then, something bad happened. Lily's brother Tom came into the room and saw her drawing. He was naughty and wanted to make trouble. He grabbed a pastel and ran to the paper. He struck the paper with the pastel and made a big black mark on grandma's face.

"Ha ha, look at your grandma now!" Tom said.

Lily was very angry and sad. She started to cry and shouted at Tom.

"Tom, you are mean! You ruined my drawing! Go away!"

Grandma heard the noise and came to see what was wrong. She saw Lily crying and Tom laughing. She saw the paper with the black mark. She was not happy.

"Tom, what did you do? That was not nice. You should apologize to Lily and help her fix her drawing," grandma said.

Tom felt ashamed and sorry. He realized he had hurt Lily's feelings and spoiled her art. He said sorry to Lily and gave her a hug. He took another paper and a pastel and tried to draw grandma again. He gave it to Lily and said he hoped she liked it.

Lily looked at the new drawing. It was not very good, but she saw that Tom had tried. She smiled and thanked him. She said she liked it and that he could draw with her sometimes. She gave him a pastel and they started to draw together.

Grandma smiled and hugged them both. She said she was proud of them and that she loved their drawings. She put them on the wall and said they were beautiful. Lily and Tom felt happy and said they loved grandma too. They continued to draw with their pastels and had fun.

Once upon a time, there was a boy who loved to go outside and look at the stars. One night he was looking at the sky and he saw something sparkly. It was a comet! He was so excited and he watched it as it flew across the night sky.

The next night, the boy was so excited to see the comet again but unfortunately he couldn't. That night it was very cold. All the stars were frozen and the comet was gone. The boy was so sad, but he knew this was a normal part of winter.

As the cold winter passed, the boy kept looking up at the night sky. As weeks passed and the days were longer and brighter, he saw something sparkly in the sky again. It was the comet! He was so happy to see it again and he watched it until it flew out of sight.

The boy was happy and he knew that the cold winter had passed and everything was back to normal. He was now able to go outside and enjoy the stars, comets and all the other night sky wonders!

Once there was a frog called Fred. He loved to hop all day long! Every day, he hopped around the pond, playing and having fun.

One day, Fred decided to do some exercise. He started to hop up and down and round and round, as quickly as he could. He soon felt very healthy and strong.

Fred kept doing this exercise every day, hopping faster and further than ever before. Soon, all of his frog friends wanted to join in!

They all hopped together, jumping higher and higher. They felt so happy and healthy working out together. Fred was so proud of himself and his friends!

At the end of each day, they all lay down by the pond, feeling strong and healthy. Fred hoped they would keep doing their exercise forever!

Once upon a time, there was an elephant who lived in a beautiful jungle. He was always happy and loved to spin around and around in circles. One day, he decided to spin even faster, but it was too hard.

The elephant could not spin any faster and felt very sad. He tried very hard but just couldn't do it.

Then, his friend the monkey came along and said, "Don't worry, Elephant! You can do it!"

The elephant was filled with hope and tried one more time. This time, it worked! He spun so fast that he became a blur!

The other animals in the jungle were amazed. They had never seen an elephant spin so fast!

The elephant beamed with pride and joy. He was so proud of himself for spinning faster than ever before!

Anna and Ben were playing in the sand. They liked to make castles and hills and dig holes. They had a big bucket of water to wet the sand. They pretended they were in a desert and the bucket was an oasis.

But then, the sun got very hot and the water got very low. Anna and Ben were thirsty and tired. They wanted to go back to their mom and dad, who were sitting under a big umbrella. But they did not want to leave their sand creations.

"Let's remain here a little longer," Anna said. "Maybe we can find more water in the oasis."

Ben nodded. He took a small cup and scooped some water from the bucket. He poured it on a hill he had made. He hoped it would grow into a mountain.

But the water did not make the hill bigger. It only made it smaller and softer. The hill collapsed and the sand spilled everywhere.

"Oh no!" Ben cried. "My hill is gone!"

Anna laughed. She thought Ben was silly. She said, "That's not how water works, Ben. Water does not make sand bigger. It makes it smaller and wetter. You wasted our water!"

Ben felt sad. He did not mean to waste the water. He wanted to make a mountain. He said, "I'm sorry, Anna. I did not know. Can we share your water?"

Anna looked at her bucket. There was only a little water left. She felt sorry for Ben. She said, "OK, Ben. We can share my water. But we have to be careful. We have to drink some and save some for the sand."

Anna and Ben drank some water. They felt better. They wet some sand and made a new hill. They did not try to make it a mountain. They were happy with their hill.

They played for a little while longer. Then they decided to go back to their mom and dad. They left their bucket and their hill in the sand. They hoped someone else would find them and enjoy them.

They ran to their mom and dad. They hugged them and told them about their desert adventure. Their mom and dad smiled and gave them juice and cookies. They said, "You are very creative, Anna and Ben. But you are also very silly. Next time, don't forget to drink enough water and wear a hat."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jenny. She was only three years old but loved to explore the world around her.

One day, Jenny went on a walk around her garden. While she was walking, she saw something yellow lying in the grass. She picked it up and discovered it was a rock. She wondered why it was so yellow.

Jenny kept exploring and soon noticed a funny smell in the air. She couldn't work out where it was coming from so she kept looking around. Eventually, she found bubbles coming up from the water in the pond. She discovered these bubbles were oxygen!

Jenny was so excited by her discoveries and she couldn't wait to tell her mum and dad. She ran in, feeling very proud of herself. She showed them all the yellow rocks, the bubbles of oxygen and the special smell. Her parents were so happy and told Jenny that she was a brilliant explorer!

Lily and Tom were playing with their dolls in the garden. They had a tea party with cups and plates and cookies. Lily was very careful with her doll, because it was delicate. She had a pink dress and a hat with flowers.

"Can I hold your doll, Lily?" Tom asked. He liked the hat with flowers.

"Okay, but be gentle. She is delicate," Lily said. She gave her doll to Tom.

Tom took the doll and looked at her. He tried to put the hat on his head, but it was too small. He pulled it hard and it ripped.

"Oh no! You broke my hat!" Lily cried. She took her doll back and saw the rip. She was very sad and angry.

"I'm sorry, Lily. I didn't mean to," Tom said. He felt bad for making Lily cry.

"You have to pay for my hat. It was my favorite," Lily said.

"How can I pay for it? I don't have money," Tom said.

"You can pay with your cookies. Give me all your cookies," Lily said.

"But I'm hungry. Can I keep one cookie?" Tom asked.

"No. You have to give me all your cookies. That's the deal," Lily said.

Tom sighed. He liked his cookies, but he wanted to make Lily happy. He gave her all his cookies and said sorry again.

Lily took the cookies and smiled. She was still sad about her hat, but she liked the cookies. She ate one and gave one to her doll.

"Thank you, Tom. You are a good friend," Lily said.

"You're welcome, Lily. Can we still play?" Tom asked.

"Okay. But next time, be careful with my doll. She is delicate," Lily said.

They hugged and played some more. They had fun in the garden.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside in her garden. One day, she saw a small tomato plant. She said, "Hello little plant, how are you doing?" The plant said, "I am doing well, thank you for asking."

Lily took care of the plant every day. She watered it and gave it some food. The plant started to grow bigger and bigger. One day, Lily saw that the tomato on the plant had become fat and juicy. She was so happy and said, "Wow, you have grown so big and fat! You look delicious!"

Lily picked the tomato and took it inside to show her mom. Her mom was so proud of Lily and said, "You did a great job taking care of the tomato plant. It looks so yummy!" Lily smiled and said, "I love watching things grow!"

Ben and Mia like to play with their robot. Their robot is flexible. It can bend its arms and legs and twist its head. It can also make funny noises and lights.

One day, Ben and Mia take their robot to the park. They want to show it to their friends. They put their robot on the grass and turn it on. The robot starts to move and beep.

"Look at our robot!" Ben says. "It can do many things!"

"Wow, it is cool!" Mia says. "Can I touch it?"

"Sure, you can!" Ben says. "But be gentle. It is not a toy."

Mia reaches out her hand to touch the robot. But the robot does not like it. It thinks Mia is a stranger. It gets scared and runs away.

"Hey, come back!" Ben shouts. "That is our robot!"

Ben and Mia chase after the robot. But the robot is fast. It runs across the park, dodging people and dogs and bikes. It looks for a safe place to hide.

"Where is it going?" Mia asks. "We have to catch it!"

"Maybe it is looking for its home!" Ben says. "We have to follow it!"

Ben and Mia run after the robot. They hope they can find it and bring it back. They love their robot and do not want to lose it.

Max loved maps. He was clever and enjoyed looking at them and finding his way. One day, he found a very special map. It was old and yellow and had lots of secret pictures. He couldn't wait to go exploring with it.

He went to the local jungle, following the map. Max was very excited. As he was looking at the map, something bit him on the ankle. It was a snake! Max was scared and ran away.

Max's dad was clever and knew just what to do. He took Max back to the jungle, but this time he brought a long stick. Max was brave and used the stick to look at the map. He kept away from anything that could bite him.

Sammy was feeling embarrassed. He had been invited to his friend's birthday party and it was the first party he was attending. When he walked in, Sammy noticed everybody was dressed up nicely. But Sammy wasn't ready for the party; he was wearing his old, shabby shirt. All the other kids were looking at him. Sammy felt so embarrassed and his cheeks went red.

At the party, Sammy saw everybody was eating yummy shrimp. Sammy wanted to try some too but he was too embarrassed to ask. He sat alone and watched.

Sammy was feeling very restless. He wanted to hide away from the party. Suddenly he had an idea. He grabbed a bowl of shrimp, put it in his pocket and snuck out.

He ran away and ate the shrimp. Finally, he felt better. He didn't have to be embarrassed in front of all the other kids. But next time Sammy will be sure to be ready for the party.

Once upon a time, there was a cat named Tom. Tom had a job. His job was to serve food to his animal friends. Tom loved his job because he could help his friends.

One day, Tom found a filthy plate. He did not want to serve food on the filthy plate. Tom knew that he had to clean it first. So, Tom washed the filthy plate until it was shiny and clean.

After the plate was clean, Tom served yummy food to his friends. His friends were happy and thanked Tom for his help. Tom felt proud of his job and was glad he could make his friends happy.

Once upon a time, there was a big brown bear. He had a sharp nose and big paws. One day, the bear went for a walk in the forest. He saw a little rabbit who had a carrot in his paw. The bear wanted the carrot, but the rabbit was stubborn and didn't want to give it to him.

The bear tried to take the carrot, but the rabbit held on tight. The bear's paw was too big to fit in the rabbit's hand, so he couldn't take the carrot. The bear thought and thought, and then he had an idea. He asked the rabbit to trade the carrot for some honey from his honey pot. The rabbit loved honey, so he agreed.

The bear went back to his home and got some honey, and then he gave it to the rabbit. The rabbit was happy with the honey, and the bear was happy with the carrot. From that day on, the bear and the rabbit were friends, and they shared their food with each other. The end.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play with toys and eat snacks. One day, they find a big box of peanuts in the kitchen. They want to make something with the peanuts.

"Look, Mia, we can attach the peanuts with glue!" Tom says. He takes a bottle of glue and a peanut. He puts some glue on the peanut and sticks it to another peanut.

"Wow, Tom, that is fun!" Mia says. She takes two peanuts and glues them together. Then she glues them to a third peanut. "I made a triangle!"

Tom and Mia keep attaching peanuts until they have a lot of shapes. They have circles, squares, stars, and hearts. They are very proud of their peanut art.

But then, they hear a loud noise. It is Tom's dog, Spot. Spot smells the peanuts and runs to the box. He knocks over the box and spills the peanuts everywhere. He starts to eat the peanuts.

"Spot, no!" Tom shouts. He tries to stop Spot, but Spot is too fast. He eats some of the glued peanuts. They are hard and sticky in his mouth.

"Bad dog, Spot!" Mia cries. She is sad that Spot ruined their peanut art. She helps Tom pick up the peanuts that are left.

Tom and Mia decide to put the peanuts away. They do not want Spot to eat more glued peanuts. They hope Spot will be okay. They hug each other and say sorry.

"Maybe we can make something else with the peanuts another day," Tom says.

"Or maybe we can just eat them," Mia says.

They laugh and go to play with other toys. They are still friends.

He asked Lily to help him carry it to their living room. Lily tried to pick it up, but it was too heavy for her. She started to worry that they wouldn't be able to watch her favorite show anymore. But then, her daddy picked her up and carried her to the living room. They watched the animals on the new television together and Lily was very happy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved going to visit her uncle who lived in a big house. But today, something was difficult for her. She had a big bag of toys, but it was too heavy for her to carry. She tried and tried, but she couldn't do it.

Suddenly, Lily heard a scream coming from inside the house. She got scared and didn't know what to do. But then her uncle came out and saw her struggling with the bag. He smiled and said, "Don't worry, I'll help you."

Lily was happy that her uncle was there to help her. She gave him the bag and they went inside. They played with the toys and had lots of fun. Lily was happy that she got to spend time with her uncle and that he was there to help her when things were difficult.

Lily and Ben were playing in the backyard. They had a big tent, some toys and a picnic. They were having fun, but they also saw some mosquitoes flying around. They did not like mosquitoes. They made them itch and hurt.

"Go away, mosquitoes!" Lily said. "You are not nice. You are not our friends."

Ben nodded. He had a spray bottle with water. He tried to squirt the mosquitoes, but they were too fast. He was frustrated.

"Maybe we can include them in our game," he said. "Maybe they are lonely and want to play."

Lily thought about it. She was compassionate. She did not want to hurt anyone, even mosquitoes. But she was also scared.

"How can we play with them?" she asked. "They will bite us."

Ben had an idea. He took a toy net and a toy jar. He ran after a mosquito and caught it in the net. He put it in the jar and closed the lid. He showed it to Lily.

"Look, we have a pet mosquito!" he said. "We can name it and feed it and talk to it."

Lily looked at the mosquito. It was buzzing and trying to get out. She felt sorry for it.

"Ben, I don't think it likes being in the jar," she said. "It wants to be free. It is not happy."

Ben looked at the mosquito. He realized Lily was right. He was compassionate too. He did not want to make anyone sad, even mosquitoes.

"You are right, Lily," he said. "Let's let it go. Maybe it will find some other mosquitoes to play with."

He opened the jar and let the mosquito fly away. He and Lily waved goodbye to it.

"Bye, mosquito!" they said. "Have fun. Be nice. Don't bite."

They went back to their tent and their picnic. They were happy. They had learned something new. They had been compassionate.

Tom and Lily liked to play in the snow. They made a big snowman with a hat and a scarf. They gave him a carrot nose and two coal eyes. They were very happy.

One day, they saw a big hole in the snow. It was near the snowman. They wanted to see what was inside. They walked to the hole and looked down. It was very deep and dark. They could not see the bottom.

"Let's go in," said Tom. "Maybe there is something fun inside."

"No, Tom, it is too scary," said Lily. "We might get lost or hurt. Let's go back to the snowman."

But Tom did not listen. He jumped into the hole. He thought he would land on something soft. But he did not. He fell and fell and fell. He screamed, but Lily could not hear him. He disappeared.

Lily was very scared. She ran to the snowman and hugged him. She cried and cried. She wanted Tom to come back. But he did not. He was gone. The hole was too deep. Lily was alone. She was very sad. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who loved to pretend. Every day, she would close her eyes and imagine all kinds of wonderful things. One day, she pretended to be a snake, winding through the grass and slithering away.

The little girl's mom saw her, and decided to join in the fun. She grabbed a fragile stick from the ground and draped it across her back.

"Look Mommy!" the little girl said. "I'm pretending to be a snake now!"

Mommy laughed and said, "That's great! Are you going to be a scary snake or a friendly one?"

The little girl thought for a moment before responding. "I'm going to be the friendliest snake ever. That way I won't frighten anyone!"

Mommy was proud of her little girl and gave her a hug.

"That's very wise," she said. "You can still have fun pretending to be a snake, but remember to be gentle and kind."

The little girl smiled and nodded her head, then went back to pretending to be a snake.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her dolls and have tea parties with her friends. One day, Lily's mom asked her to clean her room before dinner. Lily said she would, but she forgot.

At dinner time, Lily's mom reminded her about cleaning her room. Lily felt bad that she forgot. After dinner, Lily went to her room to clean up. As she was cleaning, she looked in the mirror and saw that her room was messy, just like her hair. She decided to tidy up her hair and her room.

Lily's mom was happy when she saw that Lily cleaned her room. She gave Lily a big hug and said, "I'm proud of you for remembering to clean your room. And now your room looks so nice and tidy!" Lily smiled and felt happy that she made her mom proud. The moral of the story is that it's important to listen to your parents and to keep your things tidy.

Once upon a time, there was a dirty dog named Max. Max liked to dig in the mud and play with his friends. One day, Max found a big, shiny thing in the dirt. It was an axe. Max did not know what it was, but he thought it was fun to play with.

Max showed the axe to his friend, a little bird named Tim. Tim looked at the axe and said, "Be careful, Max! The axe is very sharp!" But Max did not understand what "sharp" meant. He just wanted to play with his new toy.

Max and Tim decided to play a game with the axe. They would take turns trying to hit a small ball with the axe. Max swung the axe and hit the ball very far. Tim tried to hit the ball too, but the axe was too heavy for him. They laughed and played until it was time for dinner. At the end of the day, Max and Tim learned that the sharp axe was a fun toy, but they should always be careful when playing with it.

Once upon a time, there was a happy little girl. She loved to laugh and make other people laugh too. Every day, she would make funny faces and tell funny jokes. Her mom and dad would laugh with her, she thought it was so fun!

One day, the little girl wanted to go play outside. But she had to remember to be careful, she knew the rules. Her mom told her to be nice to the other kids she saw.

So the little girl went outside and saw some other kids playing. They were all so nice, they said hello and let her play too. The little girl was so happy and they had so much fun!

Then, while they were playing, one of the kids said something rude. He said it so loudly that everyone could hear it. All the kids stopped playing and looked at him.

The little girl was so mad, she touched the rude boy's shoulder and said, "That isn't nice. We should all be kind to each other".

The rude boy hung his head and said, "Sorry". Then he smile and all the kids started to laugh. They all hugged each other and laughed and had fun for the rest of the day.
The little girl was so glad that she taught the rude boy a lesson. And she was even more glad that everyone was kind to each other.

Tommy and his dad were outside. His dad was wearing his shoes and he had a backpack on. He said "Let's go for a jog!"

Tommy smiled and ran to the wagon. It was big and red and he put it behind him.

They started jogging around the park. They passed trees and flowers. They waved to some birds in the sky.

The air smelled sweet and the sun was warm on Tommy's face. But after a while, Tommy started to get tired. His dad smiled and said "Let's go home in the wagon!"

The ride home was fun. But Tommy's stomach started to feel bitter. He drank some water and it tasted good.

Finally they arrived home. Tommy jumped out of the wagon and hugged his dad. He said "Thank you for the nice jog!"

Once upon a time there was a strong girl. She loved to explore, and one day she decided to go on an adventure. She went to the garage and found a vehicle. It looked different than the other ones around, and she was curious.

The girl asked her mom, "What is that vehicle, mommy?" Her mom smiled and said, "That's a special vehicle, sweetie. It's strong and it will take you anywhere you want to go." The girl was excited, and she said, "Let's try it out!"

The girl and her mom got in the vehicle, and it drove off with a loud whirring noise. They stopped in the most beautiful place the little girl had ever seen. There were tall trees and flowers all around, and the sun was shining brightly. She said, "Mommy, I love it here. I want to stay here forever."

Her mom smiled, and said, "I knew you would, sweetie. That's why I let you try the special vehicle. I'm glad you found a place you love."
The little girl felt a strong sense of love and security, and she knew that no matter where she went in life, she could always come back here. From then on, this place was her special place, and the girl's and her mom's vehicle took her there often.

Once upon a time, there was a huge pipe. One day, a little girl saw the pipe and became very excited. She ran to the pipe and said, "Oh wow! What a huge pipe!"

The girl decided to explore the pipe and started walking inside it. As she walked, she saw that the walls of the pipe were dirty and needed a good wipe.

The girl was determined to clean the pipe. She found a cloth and started to wipe the walls. She worked hard and soon the walls were clean.

The girl was very happy and looked around the clean pipe. Suddenly a voice said, "Good job! You've done a great job with the cleaning!

The girl was surprised and looked around to find who had said that. She saw a small fairy who was smiling at her.

"You have given this huge pipe a nice clean look, said the fairy. The girl felt very proud of her work.

The fairy then said, "You should come and visit the pipe again soon! The girl thanked the fairy and waved goodbye as she walked out of the huge pipe.

Once upon a time there were two young friends who loved each other very much. They spent every day together, playing and laughing.

One day, the two friends got into a fight. One friend was angry and sad and the other was scared.

Then, a judge came along. The judge asked the two friends to tell their side of the story. The judge listened to both of their stories and tried to support both friends.

But then, the judge made a decision. The judge said the angry friend was right and the scared friend was wrong.

The scared friend was very sad, because they felt like they had been judged unfairly. The two friends never spoke to each other again. The end.

One day, a little girl named Lucy decided to be very careful when she went to play outside. She had a calendar to remind her of when she needed to leave. She went outside and had so much fun. She played hide and seek, jumped rope and blew bubbles.

When the sun started to go down, Lucy remembered her calendar and it was time to leave. Lucy said goodbye to her friends and went home.

When Lucy got home, she smiled and looked at her calendar. She was very proud of herself because she was very careful and followed her calendar.

The next day, Lucy wanted to be careful again. She looked at her calendar and made sure the day was planned out and everything was ready to go.

Lucy was so happy she learned to be careful, use her calendar and leave on time.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Jake. Jake was three years old and wanted something very special. So he asked his parents, "Can I have a book?"

His parents smiled and said, "Sure, let's go to the library!"

When they got to the library, Jake was amazed by the amount of books! He saw books about lions, trees, and even a book about the unknown. Jake stopped and said, "I want that one!"

His parents looked at the book and said, "It looks very old, it must have been here for a long time."

The librarian said, "Yes, it's been here so long it's starting to rot."

Jake was scared, but he still wanted the book. So he asked his parents, "Can I have it?"

His parents said, "Yes, take good care of it and it won't rot anymore."

So Jake took the book home, and he looked after it every day. Now the book is as good as new, and Jake loves reading it!

Once upon a time, there was an elderly lady. She loved to teach children all about the world. One day, she was teaching a three-year-old boy. She showed him a faucet and explained to him that it was used to get water. The little boy was fascinated and asked lots of questions.

The elderly lady then told him all sorts of stories about how water is used every day. She explained that you can take a shower and wash your hands at the faucet. She also showed him how to turn the faucet off and on.

The little boy was amazed by all these new things he was learning. He watched intently as the elderly lady demonstrated how to turn the faucet handle and the water started coming out of the spout.

At the end, the little boy thanked the elderly lady for teaching him about the faucet. He was so excited to tell his mom and dad all about it when he got home. The elderly lady smiled and said she was glad to have helped.

Lily liked to watch her dad work on cars. He was a mechanic. He fixed cars that were broken or old. He had many tools and parts in his garage. Lily wanted to help him, but he said some things were not easy or safe for her.

One day, he was working on a big red car. He opened the hood and looked inside. He saw a problem with the engine. He needed a new part. He told Lily to stay in the garage while he went to the store.

Lily was curious. She wanted to see the engine. She climbed on a stool and peeked inside the hood. She saw many wires and pipes and metal things. She touched one of them. It was hot. She burned her finger. She cried.

Her dad heard her cry. He ran back to the garage. He saw Lily on the stool and the hood open. He was angry and worried. He lifted her down and checked her finger. He put some ice on it and gave her a hug.

He said, "Lily, you should not touch the engine. It is hot and dangerous. You could get hurt. You have to listen to me. I know you want to help, but this is not easy. You are too little. Maybe when you are older, you can learn more about cars. But for now, you can watch and ask questions. OK?"

Lily nodded. She was sorry. She said, "I'm sorry, dad. I just wanted to see. It looked interesting. Can you tell me what you are doing?"

Her dad smiled. He said, "Of course, I can. I'm glad you are interested. But you have to be careful. Come, I'll show you. This is the engine. It makes the car go. It needs gas and air and spark. But sometimes, it breaks or wears out. Then I have to fix it or replace it. Like this part here. It is called a spark plug. It makes a spark to ignite the gas and air. But this one is old and dirty. It does not work well. So I have to put a new one. Do you want to see?"

Lily said, "Yes, please. Can I watch?"

Her dad said, "Sure, you can watch. But don't touch. Just watch and learn. Maybe one day, you will be a mechanic too. But for now, you are my helper. And I love you."

Lily said, "I love you too, dad. And I love cars. Can you teach me more?"

Her dad said, "Yes, I can. But first, let's finish this car. Then we can have some cookies and milk. How does that sound?"

Lily said, "That sounds great. Let's do it."

Ben was a soldier. He had a big gun and a green hat. He liked to help people and stop bad guys. But sometimes he was nervous. He did not like loud noises and scary things.

One day, he had to go to a big city with his friends. They had to find a bad guy who had a bomb. The bad guy wanted to hurt many people. Ben and his friends had to stop him.

They walked in the streets and looked for clues. They saw a big truck with a sign. The sign said "BOMB". Ben and his friends ran to the truck. They wanted to see if the bad guy was there.

But when they got close, the truck made a loud noise. It was a trap. The bad guy had set a bomb in the truck. It exploded and made fire and smoke. Ben and his friends fell to the ground. They were hurt and scared.

Ben felt pain in his leg. He saw blood. He was very nervous. He wanted to go home. He wanted his mom. He cried.

"Ben, Ben, are you okay?" one of his friends asked. He was next to him. He had a bandage on his arm. He tried to help Ben.

"I'm scared. I want to go home. I don't want to be a soldier anymore," Ben said.

"Me too, Ben. Me too. But we have to be brave. We have to finish our job. We have to stop the bad guy. We have to save the people. Can you do that, Ben?" his friend said.

Ben looked at his friend. He saw his eyes. They were brave and kind. He felt a little better. He nodded.

"Okay. I can do that. I can be brave. I can be a soldier. Let's go," Ben said.

They got up and limped to their other friends. They had a new plan. They had to increase their speed and find the bad guy. They had to work together and be smart. They had to be soldiers.

Once upon a time, there was a smart duck who loved to explore. One day, the duck decided to go on an adventure and look for something new.

The duck looked around and noticed a big zip. It had never seen a zip before and wanted to see what was inside. The duck was very curious, so it opened the zip.

Suddenly, the duck heard a loud noise and got scared. "What was that?" asked the duck. Then, a big friendly bear came out of the zip.


"Don't be scared," said the bear. "I'm here to be your friend."

The duck was relieved and realized that the bear was very smart. The two of them became the best of friends and went on many adventures together.

One day, when Sally woke up, she was excited to open her bedroom door - she had heard an unexpected noise which made her curious. When she opened the door she found an envelope which was yellow on the outside.

Sally was thrilled and straight away she opened the envelope. Inside, she found a record which was bad.

Sally was upset and sad. She was feeling very confused. Why had someone given her a bad record? She ran to tell her mum who was able to explain that it was actually a good record, just recorded in a bad way.

From that day on, Sally made sure she always recorded her adventures - the good and the bad!

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who loved to ride her bike. Every day, she would jump on her bike and ride around the neighborhood, always taking the same path.

One day, something extraordinary happened. As she took her route, an angel appeared in front of her! She was so excited to see it. The angel spoke to the little girl, saying "You must spread love and kindness in the world. Are you willing to do that?"

The girl nodded her head, understanding the task ahead of her. The angel smiled and said, "Ignorance is not the answer - open your eyes to all creatures on earth". With that, the angel flew away, leaving the little girl to ponder this message.

From that day forward, the little girl dedicated her life to being kind and helping those around her - because of her, the world became a better place!

Once upon a time, two friendly birds were looking for a place to build their nest. They noticed a tall tree with lots of branches, so they flew over and asked the tree if they could make their home there.

The tree said, "I permit you to stay, but you must not make too much noise or hurt anyone." The birds happily agreed and settled into their new home.

Pretty soon, other birds found out about the new nest and wanted to join the friendly birds. The birds decided to invite the newcomers and start a network of friends.

As more and more birds joined, their new network soon became too noisy and upset some of the trees nearby. The trees were so upset, they decided to forbid the birds from staying any longer.

The birds had no choice but to find a new home. They had forgotten the importance of following the rules to ensure peace and harmony.

Moral: It is important to follow the rules in order to live peacefully.

Once upon a time there was a woolly lamb called Sam. Sam had a special hat with a big pom pom on top. He wore it everywhere and he was very proud of it.

One day, the weather was very hot and Sam was very tired. He wanted to rest, so he found a shady spot under a big tree and lay down.

Sam was soon fast asleep and he dreamed of running around in meadows and eating lots of yummy grass.

When Sam woke up, he felt very happy and refreshed. He gently put his hat back on, ready for his next adventure.

Suddenly, a voice called, "Sam! Is that you?". Sam looked up, and saw the biggest, hairiest bear he had ever seen! The bear said, "I was looking for someone to play with, do you want to join me?"

Sam smiled and said, "yes please!". He quickly ran off with the big, hairy bear, leaving his hat beneath the tree.

The two friends played all day and when evening came, Sam decided he wanted to rest. The friendly bear carried him all the way back home, where Sam finally put his special hat back on.

From that day on, they were the best of friends and they still play together whenever Sam needs to rest.

Finally Bob became frustrated. He yelled, "If you don't stop trying to join me, I'm not going to attach the tire!"

The other kids stopped and looked at him. Bob went over to the tire, and attached it to the bicycle.

Bob was very proud of himself. His bicycle now had two tires and was ready to ride!

Tom was very sad and his parents asked, "Why did you measure the puzzle, Tom? Tom replied, "I just wanted to have fun! His parents sighed and said, "No more puzzles for you, Tom.

Suddenly, the big field started to fill with people. Everyone was running around and jumping over the furry cones. The little girl's family laughed and explained to her that it was a game called 'cone race'.

The little girl was so excited that she joined in and raced around the furry cones. She even climbed up each one. Soon, the little girl increased her speed and she won the game. She was very proud of herself.

But it was time to go home. Sam was very sad and asked his mom if he could stay a bit longer. His mom smiled and said, "We can come back another time, Sam. It takes a lot of persistence to see a place you love twice!" Sam smiled, and they drove back home.

Once upon a time, there was a baby. The baby was very small and new. That means the baby was an infant. The baby lived in a big house with its family. The baby's mother was very happy that the baby saw the bird. She said, "What a smart baby we have!" The baby felt very proud. Even though the baby was small and new, it could see things too! One day, a little girl named Emily was playing in her garden. Suddenly, she heard a noise coming from her pond. She looked in and saw a small frog swimming around. She lifted it out of the water and it hopped away.

Emily felt sad that the frog had to leave, so she started to fill the pond with water. She filled it until the pond was full. After that, she planted some flowers around it, so that the frog could come back and find a nice place to stay.

Every year, Emily made sure the pond was full and that the flowers were alive and healthy. Then, one year, Emily noticed that the pond was empty and the flowers were dead. She was very sad and sat in her garden for a long time.

Finally, Emily decided she was going to do something. She filled the pond with water and planted new flowers. Then, she waited, and soon enough, a frog appeared. Emily was so happy that she jumped up and down and clapped her hands.

The little frog seemed to be happy too. Emily liked to watch it swim and play in the pond. She knew she had done the right thing, and she was proud of herself. From that day on, Emily made sure the pond was full and the flowers were alive, so that the little frog was happy and safe.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Sue and Paul. They were always playing together, but today Sue was feeling very sad.

"Why are you so sad, Sue?" Paul asked.

"I lost my boot," Sue said, looking down at her bare foot.

Paul thought and thought until finally he had an idea. He jumped up and shouted, "I know! We can sack the places we usually play and look for it!"

"Really?" Sue brightened.

So together they sacked all their favourite places, hopping from one spot to the next looking for Sue's lost boot. After an hour of searching, Sue found it!

"Hooray!" shouted Sue, holding up her boot with joy.

"I'm so happy for you," Paul said, giving her a big hug.

Sue and Paul were very happy and never gave up looking for Sue's lost boot. The end.

Once upon a time there was a girl called Lola. She had a wonderful garden with lots of trees. Lola and her family were so happy they always gathered around the trees in the garden.

One day Lola noticed that the trees were all empty. Not a single leaf was on the branches. Where had all the leaves gone?

Lola asked her daddy what had happened. Daddy said, "A strong wind came along and blew all the leaves away. Now we need to gather them back."

So, Lola and her daddy went out into the garden. They picked up all the leaves and put them back onto the trees. When they were finished, the garden was full and green again.

Lola and her daddy smiled. "Thank you for helping me," said Daddy. Lola smiled back, happy that the trees were full of leaves again.

Once upon a time there was a Queen. She was brave and fit and lived in a big castle. One day, the Queen heard a loud noise outside. She looked out the window and saw a big lion! The lion was roaring really loudly! The Queen was scared but bravely opened the window. She called out to the lion and asked why it was roaring. The lion said it was looking for something to eat. The Queen gave the lion some food. The lion was very happy and stopped roaring. The Queen was glad that she was fit and brave enough to help the lion. She waved goodbye to the lion as it walked away. The End.

Once upon a time, there was a friendly otter who lived by the river. He loved to swim and play all day. One day, he decided to prepare a fine dinner for his friends. He went to the market and bought some fish and vegetables.

The otter cooked the food and set the table. His friends arrived and they all sat down to eat. But then, the otter suddenly felt sick. He had accidentally eaten some bad food and he became very ill.

His friends tried to help him, but it was too late. The otter passed away, leaving his friends very sad. They remembered the fun times they had with him and how he always tried to prepare the best meals. From then on, they always made sure to check their food before eating it.

"I was curious," her sister replied.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl who was very selfish. She wanted to keep things all to herself and never share with anyone else.

One day, it was hot outside so she decided to get some ice. She went to the store and bought a big bag of ice. She offered to carry the bag for her mom, but she secretly wanted to keep it all to herself.

When she got home, she wanted to put the ice in the freezer so it stayed cold. But the bag was too heavy to lift by herself. She asked her mom for help, and she was able to lift it up and put it in the freezer.

The little girl was happy she could get her ice cold, but she was still very selfish and never offered to help her mom lift the bag of ice.

Suddenly, a kind man appeared and said, "I have a cheap tomato for you." Lily was so happy and said, "Thank you!" The man pulled out a small, ripe tomato from his basket and gave it to her.
