Once there was a little girl named Emma who was three years old. She was very small and she liked to wear a gray bracelet.

One day, Emma wanted to go to the park to play. She asked her mom if she could, but her mom said no. She was very sad and started to pray. She asked God for his help.

The next day, the sun was shining, and things seemed brighter. Emma was happy because mom said she could go to the park. She put on her gray bracelet and ran outside to play.

At the park, she saw lots of other kids playing and they were having so much fun. Emma wanted to join in, so she went over and asked if she could play with them. They said yes and welcomed her to their game.

When Emma came home later that day, she thanked God for listening to her prayers. She knew that she was lucky to have been able to go to the park and have so much fun. She smiled as she looked at her gray bracelet and said a prayer of thanks.

Mia and Leo were adventurous twins who liked to explore new things. One day, they found a big box in the garage. It had many tools inside, like hammers, nails, and screwdrivers.

"Wow, look at all these tools!" Mia said. "I wonder what they do."

"Let's try them out!" Leo said. He picked up a screwdriver and a nail. He saw a wooden board on the floor. He tried to push the nail into the board, but it was too hard.

"Maybe you need to twist it," Mia suggested. She took the screwdriver from Leo and put it on the nail. She turned it around and around, but nothing happened.

"It's not working," Mia said. "Maybe we need a different tool."

She looked inside the box and saw a shiny metal thing. It had a round handle and two long arms. She grabbed it and showed it to Leo.

"What is this?" she asked.

"I don't know," Leo said. "But it looks cool. Let's see what it does."

They walked to the board and put the metal thing on the nail. Mia squeezed the handle and the arms closed around the nail. She pulled hard and the nail came out of the board.

"Wow, it worked!" Mia said. "This tool is amazing. It can take out nails."

"Let's find more nails and take them out," Leo said. "This is fun."

They spent the next hour using the metal thing to take out nails from different boards. They made a pile of nails and boards on the floor. They felt proud of their work.

They did not know that the metal thing was a pliers and that the boards were part of a shelf that their dad was building. They did not know that their dad would be very angry when he saw what they had done. They only knew that they had an adventurous day with a new tool.

Once there was a kid who wanted to dive. He ran to the pool, but it was closed. He was so sad. He sat and watched the water, wishing he could swim.

Then he noticed a bad man. The man was walking towards the pool. The kid was scared and ran away. He hid behind a tree and watched.

The bad man opened the pool and jumped in. Then he started swimming and diving. The kid was so excited to see the bad man dive. He wanted to do it too.

The bad man swam away, and the kid ran to the pool. He jumped in and started to dive. He dived up and down, over and under the water. He was having so much fun! Finally, the kid smiled and said, "I can dive!

Once there was a little boy called Bob. Bob loved to explore and play, and one day he decided to go on an adventure. He packed his bag and ran out the door.

Bob travelled through the forest and across the meadows until he came to a space. He peeked inside and saw a big machine. Bob was brave, so he went right inside and started to play with it.

But it was a foolish thing to do! As Bob touched the machine, he accidentally hit a button. The machine began to whirr and rumble and then it exploded! It destroyed everything around it.

Bob was very scared, but thankfully he was unharmed. He quickly ran away and vowed never to be so foolish again!

Once there was a mummy and she wanted to teach her toddler something new. She said to the little one, "Let me teach you how to brush your hair!" The toddler was excited and couldn't wait to learn.

Mummy picked up a brush and comb, then sat the toddler down in front of the mirror. She showed him how to brush his hair on one side, how to move the brush carefully so he wouldn't pull it too hard. Then she showed him how to do the other side.

Mummy finished brushing his hair, and then she put on a fake hair tie. It was blue and it looked very nice in his hair. The toddler was excited and he gave mummy a big hug. He was so proud that he'd learnt something new.

Mummy smiled and said "Now you know how to brush your hair. You can do it by yourself now!". The little one's face lit up and he was very happy. He ran off to do it again.

Once upon a time, there was a yellow goat. The goat was very happy and loved to play in the sunshine all day.

One day, the goat felt sad and lonely because she had no friends. So she went looking for someone to play with. But she couldn't find anybody.

Just then, the wind started to blow. It blew and blew until it sent the goat flying. The little yellow goat flew over hills and rivers until she finally saw a group of goats down below.

The yellow goat waved her arms and shouted hello. The other goats waved back. The little goat was so excited that she flew down and joined them.

From that day on, the yellow goat and the other goats were best friends. Whenever the wind blew, they would hug each other and send each other off with a big smile.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Rose. She was very kind and compassionate. She loved to spend time outside, sitting under the big, beautiful clouds.

One day, Rose decided to climb a tree. As she was sitting in the branches, she noticed something below her. It was an old man who was sad and crying.

Rose climbed down the tree and approached the old man. She asked him why he was crying. The old man replied, "I am alone, and I have nobody to talk to.

Rose felt really sad for the old man. She wanted to help, so she offered to sit with him and keep him company.

The old man was very grateful and smiled. He appreciated Rose's compassionate nature and thanked her for being his friend.

They sat together beneath the big, beautiful clouds and talked for a long time. Rose made the old man feel happier than he had in a long time, and she was glad that she could bring some joy into his life.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl called Sarah who liked to peek at things. She liked to explore new places but sometimes it was hard, just like a puzzle. One day, Sarah peeked into a room she hadn't been in before and what she saw amazed her! The room was so clean, without any mess or rubbish anywhere and it was even bigger than she expected.

Sarah started to investigate the room, exploring behind doors and opening drawers. She soon realized the room was full of fun activities and things to do! She found a puzzle, some books and even an invention box full of fun gadgets and gizmos.

Sarah spent the rest of the day playing in her clean new room. She puzzles together an animal and read lots of stories. When it was time to go home, Sarah was so happy she had peeked around and discovered a room full of surprises!

Once upon a time, there was a chubby little boy. His name was Joe, and he liked to drink orange juice. One day, he was drinking his juice when he started to feel an itch on his arm. Joe scratched it and it felt better, so he kept drinking his juice.

After he finished, he went outside to play with his toys. He ran around, climbing and sliding. Then, he started to feel an itch all over his body. Joe tried to scratch it but he couldn't reach all the places that itched.

He decided to go to the bathroom and take a bath. After a nice warm soak, he felt much better. The itch was gone and all he wanted to do was relax and drink some more orange juice! So he did and soon fell asleep in his warm bed, happy and content.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and create things with her crayons. One day, she drew a picture of her bathroom. It was an ordinary bathroom with a sink, a toilet, and a bathtub.

Lily's mom saw the picture and was so proud of her. She said, "Lily, you are so creative! You made a beautiful picture of our bathroom." Lily smiled and felt happy.

Later that day, Lily went to the bathroom and looked at it in real life. She realized that her drawing wasn't exactly the same as the real bathroom. But that was okay, because she had created her own special version of it with her imagination and her crayons.

Once upon a time there was a girl named Chloe. She loved to play outside. She brought her toy and went to the park.

Today, the sky was very clear. The sun was shining bright, but there was a tall tree that gave Chloe shade.

Chloe liked the shade. It was cool and cozy. She found a big blanket and wrapped herself in it. She giggled and smiled. She was happy and comfortable.

Soon, she fell asleep in the shade. She dreamed of things she wanted to do in the park. When Chloe woke up, she was ready to play!

Once upon a time, there was a big gorilla named George. George was very slow and liked to eat bananas. One day, George was very hungry and went to the banana tree to find some food. But there were no bananas left!

George was sad and hungry. He looked around and saw a little boy with a basket of bananas. George wanted to eat the bananas, but the boy was scared of him. George tried to tell the boy that he was friendly, but the boy didn't understand.

Finally, George had an idea. He slowly walked towards the boy and gently took a banana from the basket. Then he gave the boy a big smile and slowly walked away. The boy was surprised and happy that George was not mean. From that day on, the boy would come to the banana tree and feed George bananas. They became good friends and George was never hungry again. The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat spaghetti with sauce. One day, her mom made spaghetti for dinner. Lily was so excited to eat it.

But when she took a bite, the sauce was too hot and it hurt her tongue. She started to cry. Her mom asked, "What's wrong, Lily?"

Lily said, "It hurts, Mommy. I don't like the sauce."

Her mom promised her that she would make the sauce cooler next time. Lily felt better and finished her dinner. From that day on, her mom always made sure the sauce wasn't too hot for her.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to run and play outside. One day, she went for a jog with her mommy. They held hands and ran together.

The weather was perfect, with the sun shining and a light breeze blowing. Lily felt happy and free as she ran with her mommy.

After their jog, they went to the park and played on the swings. Lily's mommy pushed her higher and higher, and Lily felt like she was flying. She held on tight to the chains and laughed with joy.

As the sun began to set, Lily and her mommy went home. Lily was tired but happy, and she fell asleep dreaming of the perfect day she had with her mommy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily went to the park with her mommy and daddy. At the park, there was a big slide. Lily loved to slide down the slide. She went up and down the slide many times.

After a while, Lily got tired of the regular slide. She wanted to choose a different slide. So, she went to the other side of the park and found a new slide. This slide was very tall and had a lot of twists and turns. Lily was scared at first, but she decided to try it anyway.

Lily climbed up the stairs and slid down the new slide. It was so much fun! She went up and down the new slide many times. When it was time to go home, Lily was happy that she chose the new slide. She couldn't wait to come back to the park and slide down it again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mary. Mary was only three years old. One day, Mary decided to go for a walk with her dad. She noticed an onion in the garden and she wanted to pick it up. Her dad said "No, it might scare you." Mary kept asking her dad about the onion, but he said the same thing.

Finally, Mary agreed and decided to leave the onion alone. But then, back at home, she saw a smooth onion in the kitchen. She was curious and asked her dad why this onion was so smooth. Her dad said, "This is a special onion. I peeled it so you can eat it."

Mary smiled and ate the onion. It was so smooth and yummy! From that day on, she knew onions weren't always scary.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to wear her sunglasses even when it was not sunny outside. One day, she went to the park and saw her friend Max playing on the swings.

"Hi Max," said Lily. "What are you doing?"

"I'm trying to ring the bell," replied Max. "But it's too high for me."

Lily saw that Max was serious about ringing the bell. She thought for a moment and then had an idea.

"Wait here," said Lily. "I'll be right back."

She ran to her house and grabbed a long stick. She brought it back to Max and showed him how to use it to ring the bell. Max was so happy and thanked Lily for her help. They played together for the rest of the day, wearing their sunglasses and having fun in the sun.

Once upon a time, there was a life full of fun and adventure. One day, a special invitation arrived for everyone in the house. It said to hang it up on the kitchen wall so everyone could see it.

When everyone saw the invitation, they were so excited! They gathered around and looked at it with curiosity. But there was one person who didn't understand. He was a little toddler, who was so young and ignorant that he couldn't figure out what it said.

The rest of the family explained that the invitation was for a very special party. They also teaches him how to hang it up on the wall. Finally, the family was ready to start the fun! Everyone left to get ready and the toddler was so excited he jumped up and down with joy. He was so happy that he had learned something new and that he was invited to the party.

Lily and Tom were friends who liked to play together. One day, they decided to make a cake for their moms. They found some flour, sugar, eggs, and milk in the kitchen. They mixed them in a big bowl and poured them in a pan. Then they put the pan in the oven and waited for the cake to bake.

While they were waiting, they saw a door in the wall that they had never noticed before. They were curious and wanted to see what was behind it. They opened the door and saw a dark and scary room. There were spiders, rats, and bats inside. They heard a loud growl and saw a big monster with sharp teeth and claws. They were very afraid and ran back to the kitchen.

They slammed the door behind them and locked it. They hugged each other and cried. They wished they had never opened the door. They learned that they should not go into places that they do not know. They should listen to their moms and stay safe.

Soon, the oven beeped and the cake was ready. It smelled yummy and looked delicious. They took it out and decorated it with frosting and sprinkles. They were happy and proud of their cake. They cut two slices and gave them to their moms. Their moms hugged them and thanked them. They said they were good and brave children. They ate the cake together and enjoyed it. They learned that they should make nice things for their moms and share them. They should be kind and grateful.

One day, a man had a big sack of apples. He wanted to sell them at the market and get some money. He put the sack on his back and walked to the market. He was very careful not to drop the sack or hurt his back.

On the way, he met a boy who was hungry and sad. The boy asked the man, "Can I have an apple, please? I have no food and no money."

The man looked at the boy and felt sorry for him. He said, "Of course, you can have an apple. Here, take one from my sack. It is good and sweet."

The boy thanked the man and took an apple. He bit into it and smiled. He said, "This is the best apple I ever had. You are very kind and generous."

The man smiled back and said, "You are welcome. I am glad you like it. Sharing is good. It makes us happy and helps others."

The man and the boy hugged and said goodbye. The man continued to the market and sold his apples. He got some money and bought some bread and cheese. He was happy and proud. He had done a good deed and earned a fair reward.

The moral of the story is: Be careful with your things, but also be generous with others. Sharing is good. It makes us happy and helps others.

Sarah stood gazing at the sky, her little face was full of wonder. The clouds were moving so quickly, and the sun was so bright.

In the corner of her eye, Sarah saw something sparkle. She walked over and saw it was a memory. It was golden in colour and it shone in the sun. Sarah thought it must be special and she wanted to take it home.

But then, when Sarah tried to pick it up, she heard something crack. The memory had broken into pieces. Sadness filled Sarah's face, but she remembered something her mama had said.

Mama had said that broken memories are just pieces of beautiful stories. So Sarah gathered all the broken pieces in her hands and looked back at the sky.

The clouds were still moving, the sun still shone and Sarah was sure that her broken memory would still be just as special. She took the pieces home and put them somewhere safe, so that she could always remember that special story.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play outside and run around. One day, Timmy was playing with his friends when he tripped and fell. He hurt his waist and started to cry.

His friends were sorry and tried to help him up, but Timmy said he couldn't move because his waist hurt too much. Timmy's mom came out and saw that he was hurt. She picked him up and took him inside to rest.

Timmy's mom put a bandage on his waist and gave him a hug. She told him that the pain would end soon and he would feel better. Timmy felt better knowing that his mom was there to take care of him.

Once upon a time there was a tall man called Paul. He liked to keep fit, so every day he did an exercise. He stood in a room and did deep breaths in and out. He made sure they were really tight.

Every day at five o'clock, Paul would put on his trainers, and go outside. He started running and he chased the birds away. He ran so fast, his trainers bounced on the ground. After a few minutes he stopped and did some stretching to make his muscles feel tight.

Then he ran again and this time he went straight ahead. He ran slowly at first, but then he started to go faster and faster. He ran all the way to the end of the street. When he got there he was so tired! He felt a bit dizzy and he had to sit down on the grass and take a rest.

Then after a few minutes he started to feel better. He did one more exercise before he started walking back home. As he walked home, Paul felt really proud of himself. Every day he managed to do a little bit more and his muscles felt really tight.

The End

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Lucy. Lucy wanted to find something special for dinner. Her family always had the same dinner and Lucy wanted something different this time.

So she went out for a walk and saw a hidden path. She decided to follow it to see where it would lead. After a while she came to a special place. On the ground there was a basket with food inside.

Lucy was so happy, she opened the basket and ate the dinner with big bites. It tasted so good! She wanted to keep it a secret, so she remained very quiet.

That night Lucy had the best dinner she had ever had. Every night afterwards she kept looking for the hidden basket in the same place, but it never came back. Lucy still remembers that wonderful dinner and she loves to tell the story to her friends.

Ben and Sam are brothers. They like to play with their toys in their room. But they are careless. They do not organize their toys. They leave them on the floor, on the bed, on the desk. Mom is not happy.

One day, Mom says, "Boys, you need to organize your toys. They are everywhere. You can lose them or break them. And they can hurt you or me if we step on them. Please put them in the boxes and the shelves."

Ben and Sam nod. They say, "Okay, Mom. We will organize our toys." But they do not. They keep playing with their toys. They do not listen to Mom.

Mom goes to the kitchen to make lunch. She does not see what Ben and Sam do. Ben and Sam find a leather bag in the closet. It is Dad's bag. He uses it for work. It has papers and pens and a laptop inside. Ben and Sam think it is a toy. They take it out. They open it. They spill the papers and pens and the laptop on the floor. They play with them. They are careless.

Mom comes back to the room. She sees the mess. She sees the leather bag. She sees the papers and pens and the laptop. She is angry. She says, "Boys, what have you done? This is not a toy. This is Dad's bag. You have ruined his things. You are careless. You did not organize your toys. You made more mess. You are in trouble."

Ben and Sam are scared. They say, "Sorry, Mom. We did not know. We thought it was a toy. We wanted to play." But Mom does not listen. She says, "No, you did not think. You did not listen. You did not care. You need to learn. You need to organize your toys. And you need to help me fix Dad's bag and his things. Now."

Ben and Sam are sad. They say, "Okay, Mom. We will help you. We will organize our toys. We will be careful. We will not do it again." They hope Mom and Dad will forgive them. They hope they can still play with their toys. But they know they have to be more careful. And they have to organize their toys.

Once there was a little girl who wanted to have a great time. She went to the doctor to test if she was okay. She felt excited as the doctor examined her. The doctor checked her head and asked her questions. She answered them all.

The doctor said she was doing great. The little girl smiled with happiness. The doctor gave her a hug and said she was a very brave girl. She thanked the doctor and waved goodbye as she left.

The little girl was so pleased that she was doing great. She was so glad she visited the doctor and was happy she was healthy. From that day forward she always took care of herself so she could keep feeling great.

Once upon a time, there was a lovely little girl named Lily. She was very young, only three years old. Lily liked to play with her toys and her friends.

One day, Lily was playing with her toys when she saw a big door. The door was close to her house. Lily wanted to know what was behind the door. She went to the door and opened it.

Behind the door, Lily found a beautiful garden. The garden had pretty flowers and butterflies. Lily played in the garden all day. She was very happy. At the end of the day, Lily went back home and closed the door. She couldn't wait to play in the garden again tomorrow.

Once upon a time, there was a little bunny named Pinky. Pinky loved to dance and sing. Every day, Pinky would perform for her friends in the forest. One day, while hopping around, Pinky saw a root sticking out of the ground. Pinky stopped to look at it and realized it was blocking the path. Pinky decided to dig it up and move it aside so her friends could get through. After a lot of hard work, Pinky finally got the root out of the way. Her friends were so proud of her for performing such a big task! From that day on, Pinky was known as the best dancer in the forest who could also move roots with ease.

Anna and Ben are friends. They like to play in the park. There is a big tree in the park. Anna and Ben want to climb the tree.

"Can we climb the tree?" Anna asks Ben.

"Yes, we can. But we have to be careful. The tree is very high." Ben says.

They hold hands and walk to the tree. They find a low branch and pull themselves up. They climb higher and higher. They see birds and squirrels and leaves. They feel happy and brave.

"Look, Ben, I have a bow!" Anna says. She shows Ben a bow made of grass and flowers. She found it on the tree.

"Wow, Anna, your bow is very pretty. You are good at making bows." Ben says. He smiles at Anna.

"Thank you, Ben. You are a good friend. Do you want to wear my bow?" Anna says. She puts the bow on Ben's head.

"Yes, thank you, Anna. I like your bow. You are a good friend, too." Ben says. He laughs.

They hug and look at the sky. They see clouds and sun and rainbows. They feel happy and loved. They are good friends. They like to climb the tree.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was very excited because it was almost time for her favorite holiday, Halloween. She wanted to dress up as a princess and go trick-or-treating with her friends.

One day, Lily's mom took her to the store to buy her princess costume. But when they got there, Lily saw so many costumes that she got confused and didn't know which one to pick. "Mommy, I don't know which one to choose!" Lily said.

"It's okay, sweetie," her mom said. "Let's cover our eyes and point to one." So they covered their eyes and pointed to a beautiful princess costume. Lily was so happy that she hugged her mom and said, "Thank you, mommy! I can't wait for Halloween!"

Once upon a time, there was a little boy, named Joe. He loved to explore the wild outdoors. One day, Joe was running around in the meadow, when he stumbled across a shiny coin. He immediately stopped and picked it up. "A coin!" he exclaimed, looking at it in amazement.

Joe carefully balanced the coin on his finger, marveling at its beauty. "Hmm," he thought. "How can I use this coin?" Joe decided to put it in his pocket and save it for later.

The next day, Joe was in the backyard playing. He remembered the coin and decided to pull it out of his pocket. He tried to balance it on his nose while he walked, but it kept falling off. He laughed out loud and tried again. After several wobbly attempts, he finally managed to balance the coin on his nose and walk around the garden with it.

Joe loved the feeling of wildness that he felt while walking around with a coin on his nose. He kept practicing and eventually became quite good at balancing the coin. Joe was so excited that he had learned how to balance something so wild.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Anne. She was three and very excited for today's adventure. She was going out to play in the park with her best friend.

Anne and her friend walked down the road. Suddenly, Anne saw a treat. It looked yummy! She asked her friend to lead her to the treat.

Anne's friend did not want Anne to get the treat. She told Anne that it was best to leave it alone. But Anne would not listen, she made her friend lead her to it.

The treat was in a box and Anne was so excited! She asked her friend to open the box, but her friend said no. Anne opened it anyway. Her friend warned her that the treat was not for her. But Anne didn't care, she wanted the treat.

Inside the box was a big bee! The bee was very upset. It stung Anne on her nose. Anne started to cry and felt very sore.

Anne learnt a valuable lesson that day - not to take treats that don't belong to her. Unfortunately, it ended badly for Anne today but in the future she would be more careful.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jane. She was three years old and liked to explore the world. One day, she was so excited to try out her new shoes. She put them on and tied them up nice and tight. The shoes were blue and sparkly, so she could not wait to wear them out.

She walked outside and went to the store. The store was full of neat things to look at. Jane noticed a basket of candy and her eyes went wide. It was full of different kinds of sweets! She wanted to try them all. Fortunately, the storekeeper was very nice and let her have a new piece of candy. She quickly ate it and put it in her pocket.

On the way home, Jane felt so happy. She could not believe her luck! She loved her new shoes and the yummy candy. She knew she was so lucky to have such nice things. Jane wore her shoes and smiled, feeling proud of herself for having such a fun adventure.

Once there was a little girl named Sally. She was very adorable, and she loved to go outside and play in the sunshine.

One day she saw something that made her eyes go wide and her heart filled up with joy. It was a bouncy yellow ball! Sally loved balls and she wanted to play with it, so she ran over and took it in her arms.

She started to bounce the ball around the yard, higher and higher each time, until suddenly it flew away into a nearby pond! The ball was soaked and it wouldn't bounce anymore.

Sally was sad, so she ran to get her father for help. He came back with some oil and carefully rubbed it into the ball. When it was done the ball looked good as new.

Sally was so happy she started to bounce the ball all over again. She forgot all about her sadness.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Amy. She was just three years old and very adventurous.

One day, she ran to the shore to explore. She could feel the sand under her toes, and she smiled.

Suddenly, she saw a shiny object in the sand. She bent down and picked it up. "It's a shell," she said. She wanted to show it to her mom, so she got ready to twist her body and run back home.

Suddenly, a voice called out from behind her. "Wait!" It was her dad. He had gone after her and was now ready with a bucket and some new shells. "Let's go look for more shells together," he said. Amy nodded and together they began to twist and turn, looking for more treasures at the shore.

At the end of the day, Amy was still smiling. After all, with her dad by her side, nothing could be better.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy who wanted to find the best toy to play with. He asked his friends to recommend some good toys, and they said they would help him.

The boy's friends listed some toys they had played with, and they gave each toy a score. A high score meant that the toy was really fun and a low score meant the toy wasn't as fun.

After a lot of hard work, the boy finally chose one of the toys that his friends had recommended. They all agreed that it was a very reliable toy and it earned the highest score!

The boy had a lot of fun with his toy and he was very happy that his friends had been able to recommend such a reliable toy for him to play with.

Once upon a time, there was a quiet little girl named Janey. She was only three years old and liked to play outside.

One day, Janey was playing in a nearby park when it started to rain. She became very wet and dirty, so she ran home.

When she got home, Janey's mom gave her a big, warm hug and wrapped her in a cozy blanket. She said "Janey, you need a bath".

Janey's mom gave her some soap, and Janey began to wash. She was happy to be getting clean and the soap smelled nice.

When she was done, Janey was nice and dry. She smiled and felt very good. Janey's mom said "Now you're as clean as can be!"

Janey laughed and ran off to play. She was so happy that her name was clean again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside in the grass and look at all the bugs. One day, she saw a little white beetle crawling on a leaf. "Wow, he's so pretty!" she said to herself.

Just then, her friend Jack came over. "What are you looking at, Lily?" he asked. "Look at this beetle I found! He's white and so cute," she replied. Jack looked at the beetle and said, "I don't like bugs. They're creepy."

Lily thought about what Jack said and then decided to let the beetle go. "I don't want him to feel scared," she said. Jack watched as Lily gently placed the beetle back onto the leaf. "You're right, Lily. He's not so bad after all," Jack said. And they both smiled and continued to play in the grass.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. He was very lonely and always wished for a friend. He had a bow that he was very fond of, it was his favorite toy.

One day, he was walking in his garden and noticed a strange light coming from a big tree. He walked closer and saw a beautiful fairy hovering in the air. The boy smiled and said "Hello!"

"Oh hello there!" said the fairy. "I'm here to restore your bow; it's a very special bow filled with magical powers."

The little boy was so excited! He thanked the fairy and the fairy waved her wand. Suddenly, his bow was transformed into a beautiful ribbon wrapped around his wrist.

The little boy was no longer lonely. He happily played with his bow all day long; he even got a new friend, the fairy! From then on, he was never lonely again.

Once upon a time, there was a delicate girl named Lucy. She loved playing with her toys and singing songs. One day, Lucy felt a tickle in her nose. She sneezed and said, "Ah-choo!"

That night, Lucy was scared of something scary in her dreams. She had a nightmare. It was so frightening she wanted to cry. But then something strange happened. Lucy sneezed and she felt much better.

The nightmare was gone and Lucy woke up feeling relieved. She said, "Ah-choo!" and snuggled up all warm and safe in her bed. That was the last time she ever had a nightmare again! From then on, sneezing always brought her comfort and protection.

Once upon a time, there was a man who lived in a big house on a hill. He was very mysterious and nobody knew what he did all day. One day, a little girl named Lily was walking by the man's house and saw him sitting outside. She said, "Hi, Mister! What are you doing?" The man replied, "I am just thinking."

Lily looked at the man and said, "You seem very mysterious. What do you recommend I do today?" The man thought for a moment and said, "I recommend you go to the park and play with your friends." Lily smiled and said, "That's a great idea! Thank you, Mister."

From that day on, Lily would often visit the man on the hill and they would have fun conversations. The man wasn't so mysterious anymore and Lily realized that he was just a kind old man who liked to think and give recommendations.

Minnie was very excited to see her daddy today. Every time he took her out she had lots of fun. She put her coat on, took her toys and waited at the door.

Minnie saw daddy and ran to him. She loved how friendly he was. He always smiled and gave her big hugs.

Today, daddy wanted to take her to the park. She had never seen it before and was looking forward to it. It was a sunny day and there were lots of other children playing.

All the children were playing together, having fun. Minnie was so happy that she had taken her favourite toy with her. She ran around with the other children, showing them her toy.

Eventually daddy called her over. He had brought some refreshments with him and wanted the whole family to have a picnic together. Minnie was so delighted to be spending time with her family and kept giving her daddy big hugs and kisses.

There was a girl who was feeling very uncomfortable. She was walking near a church and she saw some people who looked very happy. She wanted to feel happy too so she went inside the church.

An old man saw her and said hello. He came over to her and he asked her why she was feeling so uncomfortable. She quietly told him about her troubles.

The old man offered her a hug and told her that everything was going to be okay. She felt very comforted and, soon, she was smiling and enjoying being inside the church.

The old man was very kind and he stayed with her until she was feeling much better. The girl thanked him and said goodbye.

The old man told her that she was always welcome in the church if she ever felt uncomfortable again. The girl was so grateful and she was happy to know she had somewhere to go if she needed comfort.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to perform for her family and friends. She would sing and dance and make everyone happy.

One day, Lily's mom asked her to go to the mailbox and get the mail. Lily thought it sounded dull, but she went anyway. She found a letter that said her family was moving away.

Lily was sad and didn't want to leave her friends. She tried to perform a happy dance to make herself feel better, but it didn't work. She cried and cried until her mom came to comfort her.

From that day on, Lily learned that sometimes even performing can't make a bad situation better.

Ben and Mia like to run. They run in the park, they run in the yard, they run in the hall. They are fast. They want to be the fastest.

One day, they see a big sign. It says: "Race for kids. Win a trophy." Ben and Mia want to win the trophy. They think it is shiny and pretty. They sign up for the race.

The day of the race comes. Ben and Mia wear their best shoes. They go to the start line. They see many other kids. Some are big, some are small, some are fast, some are slow. Ben and Mia look at each other. They smile. They are ready.

The man with the whistle says: "Ready, set, go!" Ben and Mia run as fast as they can. They pass many kids. They are in front. They see the finish line. They see the trophy. They run faster.

But then, something happens. Ben trips on a rock. He falls. He hurts his knee. He cries. Mia stops. She sees Ben. She runs back to him. She helps him up. She hugs him. She says: "Are you OK, Ben? Don't cry. I'm here."

The other kids run past them. They finish the race. They cheer. They get the trophy. Ben and Mia don't get the trophy. They are sad.

The man with the whistle comes to them. He says: "You two are very brave. You helped each other. You are good friends. You don't need a trophy to be winners. You have something better. You have each other."

He gives them a medal. It says: "Best friends." Ben and Mia smile. They thank the man. They hug each other. They hang the medal on their wall. They are happy.

Once upon a time, there was a poor man who lived in a small house with his little daughter. He wanted to give her the best things, but he had very little money.

One day, the dad made a plan. He went looking for something special. He found a nice lock and took it home.

The daughter saw the lock and asked her dad: "What's that dad?"

The dad said: "It's a lock. It will keep us safe and our house will be secure."

The daughter smiled and said: "I like it, dad!"

The poor man and his daughter put the lock on the door, and it made them feel secure.

The moral of the story is that no matter how poor you are, if you have a clever plan, you can find ways to make your life better.

Lily and Tom are friends. They like to play with paper and scissors. They cut out shapes and stick them on the wall. One day, Lily has a new idea. She wants to cut out a heart.

"Look, Tom, I can cut a heart," she says. She folds a red paper in half and cuts along the edge. She opens the paper and shows Tom her heart.

"Wow, Lily, that is a pretty heart," Tom says. He likes Lily's heart. He wants to make one too. He folds a blue paper in half and cuts along the edge. He opens the paper and shows Lily his heart.

"Look, Lily, I can cut a heart too," he says. He smiles at Lily. Lily smiles back. She likes Tom's heart. She thinks they are both very good at cutting hearts.

"Let's stick our hearts together," Lily says. She takes some glue and puts it on the back of her heart. She presses it on Tom's heart. They make a big heart with two colors.

"Look, Tom, we made a new heart," she says. She is happy. Tom is happy too. They hug each other. They are happy friends with a new heart.

"Yes, it is famous!" Lily replied.

A man saw the truck and he knew that he had to save it. He ran over to the truck, and opened the door. Inside, he saw boxes and boxes of food! He smiled, because he knew he was going to save the food.

The man worked hard. He pushed and pulled the truck until it started moving again. Little by little, he moved the truck away from the smoke. Finally, he reached a safe place. The truck was saved!

The man was happy. He knew he had helped to save the food, and the truck too. The truck, heavy with the food it was carrying, trundled off down the street.

Lily was excited and asked, "What can I cook, mommy?" Her mommy replied, "Let's start with something simple. We can make pancakes together. First, we need to gather all the ingredients."

Lily helped her mommy step by step, mixing the flour, eggs, and milk together. She was very happy when the pancakes turned out to be delicious. "Mommy, can we name our pancakes?" she asked. "Of course, we can name them 'Lily's Easy Pancakes'," her mommy said proudly.

From that day on, Lily loved to cook and make easy pancakes with her mommy. She even taught her little brother how to make them too. They all enjoyed eating Lily's Easy Pancakes together as a family.

Once upon a time there was an avocado tree. It was so big that it could weigh a tonne! Every day the tree would produce delicious avocados. But one day, the tree suddenly stopped producing.

The farmer was puzzled. He went over to the tree and said, "Why are you so dead?"

The tree replied, "I miss weighing the avocados. I used to do it for the farmer every day."

The farmer understood and said "Don't worry, I will help you weigh them!"

So every day, the farmer would help the tree weigh the avocados. This made the tree happy again and back to life. The tree was now producing avocados again and everyone was happy!

One day the girl had a special task. She was told to reverse the room. She was excited to try and soon began her work. She moved the couch to the other side, put the rug in the opposite direction and switched around all the furniture.

When the work was finally done she felt so proud of herself. She had reversed the whole room and everything was in its new place. She smiled and skipped around the room. Her parents were so happy to see her so proud of her work.

One day, a brave knight was riding through a dark forest. He was on a journey to warn the villagers of a terrible danger. Suddenly, he heard a loud noise. It was coming from a nearby village. He quickly rode on his horse to the village.

When he arrived, the village was empty. He searched for people, but there was no one in sight. He saw a big castle, so he went inside. There he saw an old man who was trembling and scared.

The knight asked him what was wrong. The old man told him that the villagers had been warned of an evil monster coming to destroy the village. Everyone had left in a hurry, in fear of the monster.

The knight was brave and decided to stay and defend the village. He wanted to protect it from the monster. He made a plan to warn it away. He made a signal fire in the village center and used its smoke to warn the monster to stay away. The knight stayed in the dark village, ready to fight.

Luckily, the signal worked, and the monster never came. The brave knight had saved the village!

Lily and Ben were brother and sister. They liked to play outside in the sun. One day, they found a big box in the yard. They decided to make it their shelter. They put some blankets and pillows inside. They also drew some pictures on the box with crayons.

But their dog, Spot, did not like the box. He wanted to play with Lily and Ben. He barked and scratched at the box. He made it filthy with his paws. He tore some of the blankets and pillows. He also ate some of the crayons.

Lily and Ben were sad and angry. They told Spot to go away. They tried to fix their shelter. But it was too hard. They needed some help. They asked their mom to support them. Their mom was kind and smart. She gave them some tape and scissors. She helped them cut and stick the box. She also gave them some new blankets and pillows. She told them to share their shelter with Spot. She said he was lonely and wanted to be their friend.

Lily and Ben listened to their mom. They made some space for Spot in their shelter. They also gave him some toys and treats. They invited him to join their play. Spot was happy and wagged his tail. He licked their faces and cuddled with them. He stopped barking and scratching. He was a good dog.

Lily and Ben were happy too. They had a nice shelter and a nice dog. They thanked their mom for her support. They hugged and kissed her. They also hugged and kissed Spot. They had a lot of fun in their shelter. They were a happy family.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily who loved to play in her garden. She loved to run around and play hide and seek with her friends. One day, while playing, she noticed that the hedge in her garden was looking very tall and bushy. She thought to herself, "I wish the hedge could change and become shorter so that I can see my friends on the other side."

The next day, Lily's dad came to the garden with a pair of scissors. He started to cut the hedge and make it smaller. Lily was so happy! She could now see her friends on the other side. She ran to them and they played together. They played until the sun went down and it was time to go home.

When Lily came back to her garden the next day, she noticed that the hedge had changed again! This time, it was not tall and bushy, but it was in the shape of a heart. Lily was so surprised and happy. She thought to herself, "This is not the original hedge, but it's even better!" From that day on, Lily loved her garden even more and was always excited to see what surprises it had in store for her.

It was morning time and Max was feeling very lazy. He was laying on the bed and not feeling like getting up at all. He looked outside the window and saw his friend Sam walking in the garden. Max thought about joining her for some fun.

Max put on some clothes and slowly made his way outside. He smiled when he saw Sam and waved. Sam smiled back at him and asked if he wanted to play. Max happily agreed and asked what they were going to play.

Sam laughed and said, "I was thinking we could play a game where I try to tease you a little bit and you try not to laugh!" Max thought it sounded like fun and the game began. Sam told some jokes and poked fun at Max, but Max managed not to laugh.

After a few minutes, Sam said, "This game is so much fun, let's play again tomorrow morning!" Max smiled and nodded his head. He had a feeling that the next morning would be filled with lots of laughter.

Once upon a time, there lived a cat and a dog who were the very best of friends. They would have lots of fun together; running and playing.

One day, the friends decided to go explore a new park. As they approached a large lively gate, the cat wanted to get through quickly, but the dog stopped and said to the cat, "Don't forget to mind the gate! We want to be sure to be safe."

The cat, not wanting to wait, proceeded to climb over the gate. As soon as the cat touched it, an alarm went off and the friends quickly ran away.

The two friends made their way back home and discussed what had happened at the park. The dog explained how important it is to mind the rules and to always stay safe.

From that moment on, the cat and the dog were always sure to mind the rules and stay safe; remembering the lesson they learnt at the lively gate.

Tarzan knew what it was. He shouted "it's a red bird! Jane was very excited. The bird was so red and it was collecting things to make a nest.

Then the bird flew away. Tarzan and Jane watched it go until it was a small red speck in the sky. The next day Tarzan and Jane decided to search for the red bird. They looked all day but they couldn't find it.

The days and weeks went by and still they couldn't find it. But then, one day in the red season, Tarzan and Jane saw the red bird zipping around in the sky.

They watched it for a long time and were very happy that the bird had returned. The red bird reminded them of the beauty of the red season and being friends.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Stella was walking in the forest. She could hear the birds chirping and the leaves rustling in the breeze. Suddenly, Stella saw something very big under a tree. It seemed to be an ostrich!

Stella said in surprise, "Ostrich! Hi, Ostrich!"

The ostrich seemed shy and didn't say a word. Stella smiled and waved at the ostrich. The ostrich seemed interested in Stella and hesitantly stepped closer.

Stella said, "Do you want to play? I'll show you my favourite games!"

The ostrich nodded, so Stella picked up a stick and showed the ostrich how to play hopscotch. The ostrich seemed to enjoy hopping along the stones that Stella had drawn. Joyfully, Stella and the ostrich played the game together.

The ostrich was not so shy anymore and Stella and the ostrich became friends. They played in the forest until the sun was setting. Then the ostrich said goodbye to Stella and ran off into the forest.

Mom is not happy. She says, "Ben and Lily, you are both intelligent. You can share and take turns. Fighting is not nice. How about we watch something else? Something that you both like?"

Once upon a time in a big forest there lived a tiny caterpillar. He was so small and fragile that he could barely move around. He had never seen another caterpillar like him.

One day while he was crawling along, he heard a voice. It was a magical voice that said, "Hello, little caterpillar!" The caterpillar was so happy and he spoke back to the voice. The voice told him, "I'm a fairy and I can help you find other caterpillars like you!"

The fairy helped the tiny caterpillar find a big group of caterpillars. There were so many of them! The little caterpillar was so excited he couldn't speak. He made a lot of new friends that day! Every day, the caterpillar and the other caterpillars would gather together to eat and play.

The little caterpillar was so happy to have found his friends. He knew he had to be careful and delicate as he was still so fragile, so he moved around very gently. He made sure to take good care of himself and he had the best time with his new friends.

Sara likes to swing at the park. She has a red jacket that keeps her warm. She swings high and low, and feels happy.

One day, she sees a large dog at the park. The dog is brown and furry, and has a long tail. Sara thinks the dog is cute, and wants to pet it. She gets off the swing and walks to the dog.

But the dog does not like Sara. The dog barks and growls, and shows its teeth. Sara is scared, and runs back to the swing. She forgets her jacket on the ground.

The dog sees the jacket, and thinks it is a toy. The dog grabs the jacket with its mouth, and shakes it hard. The jacket rips and tears, and the stuffing comes out.

Sara cries, and calls for her mom. Her mom comes and hugs her, and tells her it is okay. Her mom is angry at the dog, and tells the owner to control it. The owner says sorry, and takes the dog away.

Sara is sad, because her jacket is ruined. Her mom says they will get a new jacket, and a new toy. Sara smiles, and hugs her mom. She still likes to swing, but she does not like large dogs.

Once upon a time there was a very elderly man with a very important name. He lived in a village far away and he was the best at scoring points in the village. Every day he played a game with the other villagers, and he always scored the most points.

One day, the elderly man's name was called out aloud. Everyone became very quiet and listened. He had been chosen to receive a special medal for his skill in scoring points. Everyone cheered as he stepped forward and the mayor put the medal around his neck. The elderly man smiled proudly as he looked at the shiny medal.

The villagers all started to chant the elderly man's name and the sound echoed through the village. He was so happy to have his name remembered and celebrated by all.

The elderly man went home that day feeling very proud. He kept the medal around his neck and even slept with it on. The next morning he woke up and put it on again, ready for the day. And from then on, he was known as the one with the best points-scoring skills in the village.

Tom and Lily were at the post office with their mom. They had to wait in a long line to buy some stamps. Tom and Lily were very bored. They did not like to wait and they did not care about stamps.

"Mom, can we go now?" Tom asked.

"Not yet, honey. We need these stamps to send some letters to grandma and grandpa. They will be happy to hear from us," mom said.

Tom and Lily sighed. They looked around the post office. They saw some pictures on the wall. They saw some boxes and envelopes. They saw some people working and some people waiting.

Lily saw something that caught her eye. She saw a big book on a table. The book had many colorful pages. The pages had many different stamps on them. Lily liked the stamps. They had animals and flowers and flags and cars and more.

"Look, Tom!" Lily said. She pointed to the book. "Look at the stamps! They are so pretty!"

Tom looked at the book. He did not think the stamps were pretty. He thought they were boring. He did not like stamps. He liked trucks and trains and planes.

"Those are not stamps, Lily. Those are stickers. Stamps are small and white and have numbers on them. Stickers are big and bright and have pictures on them. Stickers are for kids. Stamps are for grown-ups," Tom said.

Lily did not agree with Tom. She thought the stamps were stamps. She thought they were for everyone. She wanted to see more of them.

"Can we look at the book, mom?" Lily asked.

Mom smiled. She saw that Lily was interested in the stamps. She thought it was good for Lily to learn about different things.

"Sure, sweetie. You can look at the book. But be careful. Don't touch the stamps. They are very old and rare. They belong to the post office. They are not for sale," mom said.

Lily nodded. She was careful. She did not touch the stamps. She only looked at them. She turned the pages slowly. She saw stamps from many countries and many times. She saw stamps with birds and fish and stars and moons. She saw stamps with kings and queens and heroes and villains. She saw stamps with letters and words and shapes and colors. She was amazed.

Tom was not amazed. He was still bored. He did not look at the stamps. He looked at his watch. He wondered how long they had to wait. He wished they could go home and play with his toys. He thought stamps were boring. He did not understand why Lily liked them. He shrugged. He guessed everyone liked different things.

Lily and Ben were playing in the pool. They liked to splash and swim and have fun. Lily saw a big slide and wanted to try it. She said to Ben, "Let's go on the slide. It looks fun."

Ben was scared of the slide. He said, "No, Lily. The slide is too high and too fast. I don't want to go."

Lily said, "Don't be scared, Ben. Be brave. I will go first and show you. It will be easy and fun. Come on, Ben."

Lily got out of the pool and ran to the slide. She climbed up the ladder and sat on the top. She waved to Ben and said, "Look, Ben. I'm on the slide. Watch me go."

Lily pushed herself down the slide. She went very fast and very high. She screamed and laughed. She felt happy and brave.

But then something bad happened. Lily did not see a big hole in the slide. She fell through the hole and hit the ground. She hurt her head and her arm and her leg. She cried and cried. She was not happy and brave anymore. She was sad and hurt.

Ben saw what happened to Lily. He was very scared and very sorry. He ran to Lily and tried to help her. He said, "Lily, Lily. Are you okay? I'm sorry, Lily. I should have gone with you. I should have been brave."

Lily said, "Ben, Ben. It hurts. It hurts a lot. I need help. I need a towel. I need a hug."

Ben looked for a towel. He did not see any towel. He did not see any grown-up. He did not know what to do. He felt alone and helpless. He hugged Lily and cried with her. He wished he could make it better. He wished he could go back in time. He wished he never went to the pool.

Timmy had a great idea. He wanted to use the rope to swim! He dribbled over to the rope and attached it around his waist. Timmy was very brave and he wanted to see how far he could swim with the rope.

So Timmy jumped into the water with the rope and began to swim. He swam as fast as he could for a few minutes, until he reached the other side of the pond.

Timmy was so proud! He had swam across a whole pond, with the rope attached to him. The rope helped him to stay brave and adventurous. From that day onwards, Timmy always remembered how courageous and adventurous he had been, and he even go swimming with a rope sometimes.

Ben's eyes grew wide as he looked at the gray laser in his dad's hand. "It's so shiny!" he exclaimed.

His dad smiled. "Would you like to try it?" he asked.

"Thank you, dad," said Ben. His dad smiled. "You're welcome, Ben," he said. "It was my pleasure to offer the laser to you."

Anna likes cereal. She eats it every morning with milk. She likes to make noises with her spoon and mouth. She thinks it is fun.

But Mom does not like noisy cereal. She says, "Anna, please eat quietly. You are too loud. You will wake up your baby brother."

Anna does not want to wake up her baby brother. She loves him. She wants him to sleep well. She says, "Sorry, Mom. I will eat quietly."

She tries to eat quietly. But she still makes some noises. She likes the crunchy sound of the cereal. She likes the slurpy sound of the milk.

Mom hears the noises. She says, "Anna, you are still too loud. If you cannot eat quietly, you have to clean your bowl and spoon. You have to put them in the sink."

Anna does not want to clean her bowl and spoon. She wants to eat her cereal. She says, "But Mom, I am hungry. I want to finish my cereal."

Mom says, "You can finish your cereal, but you have to eat quietly. Or you have to clean your bowl and spoon. You choose."

Anna thinks. She does not like to clean. She likes to eat. She says, "OK, Mom. I choose to eat quietly."

She eats her cereal very quietly. She does not make any noises. She finishes her cereal. She is happy.

Mom is happy too. She says, "Good job, Anna. You ate quietly. You did not wake up your baby brother. You can put your bowl and spoon in the sink now."

Anna puts her bowl and spoon in the sink. She says, "Thank you, Mom. I love you."

Mom says, "I love you too, Anna. You are a good girl."

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Emily. Emily was very happy when she was at school. Her teacher was very nice and there were lots of fun things to do.

One day, Emily's teacher had a surprise present for her. It was a folder with lots of pictures of clouds and stars. Emily was so excited to have it and she clapped her hands and applauded.

But when Emily opened the folder, she was confused. There were so many pictures, but not a single cloud or star. She felt a bit guilty and asked her teacher why all the pictures were gone.

Her teacher smiled and said that all the pictures were now in Emily's heart, so now Emily could look at them anytime she wanted. Emily felt much better and clapped again for all the happy memories. The End.

Once upon a time, there was a mama bear. She was very big and strong. Mama Bear liked to go outside and play, so she put on her sunglasses and went for a walk in the woods.

But soon, the sun was gone and the sky turned dark. Mama Bear didn't like the darkness and it made her feel very bitter.

Mama Bear wanted to go home, but she was lost. That's when she saw something shiny in the corner of her eye. What was it?

Mama Bear took a closer look and she saw it was a little cub! Mama Bear knew he was lost too, so she smiled and said, "Hello there little cub. Can you tell me your name?"

The cub replied, "My name is Timmy." Mama Bear smiled even bigger and replied, "Well okay Timmy, let's find our way home together!"

Mama Bear and Timmy walked back to the forest together, and when they arrived home, Mama Bear gave Timmy a big hug and said, "You will always be my friend!"

And they both lived happily ever after.

She loved the sight of the trees and the birds. She was so happy that she asked her dad to weigh the binoculars so she could carry them with her everywhere she went.

The dog went back to the shelter every day. He would do nice things, like sleep and eat a yummy bone. He also made friends with some other dogs who liked the shelter too.

One day it was raining, and the little dog was scared. He wondered what to do. Then he thought about the shelter. He ran over and quickly went inside. He was glad he had a shelter to keep him safe and warm from the rain.

He stayed in the shelter until the rain stopped. After that, he went back to the park. He had lots of fun, and he was glad he had a shelter to go to when he needed one.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved her teddy bear named Teddy. One day, Lily went to the park with Teddy. She was playing with him when she accidentally dropped him in the pond. Teddy was wet and dirty. Lily was so sad and helpless. She didn't know what to do.

Suddenly, a kind old man came by and saw that Lily was upset. He asked her what happened. Lily told him about Teddy and how he was now dirty and wet. The old man said, "Don't worry, I can help you. We can replace Teddy with a new one." Lily was happy and thanked the old man.

But the old man took Lily to a store and bought her a new teddy bear. Lily was happy to have a new teddy bear, but she felt bad for her old teddy bear that was still in the pond. She learned that it's important to take care of the things we love and not replace them so easily.

Tom and Anna are friends. They live in a big city with many cars and buildings. They like to watch the city from their window. They see people, dogs, birds and buses.

One day, Tom and Anna go to the park with their mom. They play on the swings and the slide. They have fun. Then they see a big balloon in the sky. It is red and yellow and blue. It is very pretty.

"Look, Anna, a balloon!" Tom says.

"I see it, Tom. It is so big and bright!" Anna says.

They watch the balloon fly over the city. They wonder who is in the balloon. They wish they could go in the balloon too.

"Mom, can we go in the balloon?" Tom asks.

"Maybe one day, Tom. But not today. The balloon is very expensive and far away." Mom says.

Tom and Anna are sad. They want to go in the balloon. They watch the balloon until it is very small and disappears.

"Maybe we can make our own balloon, Anna." Tom says.

"How, Tom?" Anna asks.

"We can use a paper bag and some string and some air. And we can draw on it with crayons." Tom says.

"That sounds fun, Tom. Let's do it!" Anna says.

They run to their mom and ask for a paper bag and some string and some crayons. Mom gives them what they need. They go to a bench and make their own balloon. They blow air into the bag and tie it with the string. They draw on it with crayons. They make a face and a flower and a star.

"Look, mom, our balloon!" Tom and Anna say.

"It is very nice, Tom and Anna. You are very creative." Mom says.

They hold their balloon and run around the park. They pretend they are flying over the city. They laugh and smile. They are happy.

They watch their balloon and think they are lucky. They have each other and their mom and their city. They do not need a big balloon. They have a small balloon and a big imagination.

As they cleaned up the crumbs, Lily felt the rug and noticed it was rough. "Mommy, why is the rug so rough?" asked Lily. "It's because it's made of a rough material, like burlap," explained her mommy.

Lily and her mommy sat down on the rug and enjoyed their cookies together. "These cookies are so yummy and we had fun cooking them together," said Lily. "Yes, we did, sweetie. I love cooking with you," replied her mommy.

So Mama said, "I have a special treat here. It's fragile, though. You have to be careful with it."

Mama gave the little girl a plate with sugar on it. The sugar sparkled and glowed. It made the little girl very happy.

The little girl held the plate carefully in her hands. She sprinkled a bit of sugar on top of all her food. It was delicious.

The little girl was very careful with her plate of sugar. She knew it was fragile and she didn't want to break it.

Every day, Mama added a bit more sugar, so the little girl's plate never ran out. The little girl loved the special treat.

One day, a grumpy bear was walking in the woods. He was looking for something to eat when he heard a loud roar. He looked up and saw a helicopter flying in the sky. The roar was coming from its engine!

The grumpy bear was scared at first, but then he became curious. He wanted to see what the helicopter was up to. He followed the helicopter and watched it land in a big open field.

Soon a group of people came out of the helicopter. They all looked very happy and were laughing and talking. The grumpy bear thought that they looked like they were having a lot of fun! He wanted to join in the fun too. The bear crept closer and closer until he was right next to the group of people. He roared as loud as he could to get their attention.

The group of people turned and stared. The bear was a bit embarrassed and surprised to see that the people were actually scared of him. He quickly scampered away, back into the woods.

The grumpy bear never saw the people again, but he never forgot the roar of the helicopter. Every time he heard a helicopter over the trees, it made him smile and remember that special day.

"I'm so excited!" said one.

"Me too!" said the other.

The two friends both lined up and sat down, waiting for the race to start. Suddenly, one of the friends got a bit too excited and jumped up before they had to start running.

"That's not fair!", shouted the other.

The race referee came over, and warned the excited friend.

"You can't move until the race officially starts, otherwise you won't be able to win the medal."

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily went to the park with her mommy. It was a sunny day, and the park was full of kids playing. Lily saw a big slide and wanted to go on it. But her mommy said, "Not now, Lily. Let's go get some ice cream first."

They walked to the ice cream truck, and Lily had to choose which flavor she wanted. She picked strawberry. As they walked away, Lily accidentally dropped her ice cream on the ground. It was filthy! Her mommy said, "Don't worry, Lily. We can get you a new one."

After they got a new ice cream, Lily finally got to go on the big slide. She climbed up and slid down, laughing all the way. But when she got to the bottom, she felt very cold. She didn't realize that the slide was in the shade, and it was getting chilly. Her mommy wrapped her up in a warm blanket and said, "Next time, we'll bring a jacket."

Billy was walking through the woods, carrying a big bag of sugar. He had found the bag when he was looking for something to eat. Suddenly he saw a foolish rabbit hopping around.

Billy called out to the rabbit, "Hi there! Have you seen any food around here?"

The rabbit shook its head and said, "No, I'm sorry. But there's sure a lot of sugar!"

Billy smiled and said, "Yes, I know. I'm carrying it in my bag!"

The rabbit looked up at the bag and said, "Wow, that's a lot! What are you going to do with all that sugar?"

Billy laughed and said, "I'm going to make lots of sweet treats for everyone!"

The rabbit smiled and said, "That sounds like fun! You're very clever with all that sugar!"

Billy thanked the rabbit and said goodbye. He then continued on his journey, carrying the big bag of sugar. He was going to make some foolish treats, but they were sure to be delicious!

Ben and Lily were twins. They liked to play in the park. One day, they saw a big slide. Ben wanted to go fast on the slide. Lily was lazy and scared.

"Come on, Lily, let's go on the slide!" Ben said.

"No, Ben, it's too high and too fast. I don't want to go," Lily said.

Ben was angry. He wanted to have fun. He did not want to wait for Lily. He ran to the slide and climbed up the ladder. He looked down and saw Lily sitting on a bench. He shouted, "You are a baby, Lily! You are too lazy to play!"

Lily felt sad. She wanted to play with Ben, but she did not like the slide. She saw a man with a cart. He had ice cream and cookies. She liked ice cream and cookies. She walked to the man and asked, "Can I have some ice cream and cookies, please?"

The man smiled and said, "Sure, little girl. You can have as much as you want. But you have to pay me."

Lily did not have any money. She did not know what to do. She saw Ben sliding down the slide. He was going very fast. He looked happy. She wanted to be happy too. She said, "I don't have any money, but I can give you something else. I can give you my brother. He is fast and strong. You can eat him."

The man was surprised. He did not want to eat Ben. He wanted to sell ice cream and cookies. He said, "No, no, I don't want your brother. I don't eat people. That's very bad. Go away, little girl. You are crazy."

Lily did not listen. She grabbed Ben's hand and pulled him to the man. She said, "Here, take him. He is tasty. He is not lazy like me. He likes to go fast. You can have speed and eat him."

Ben was scared. He did not want to be eaten. He wanted to play on the slide. He screamed, "Help, help! Lily, what are you doing? Let me go! I don't want to be eaten!"

But it was too late. The man was angry and hungry. He opened his mouth and bit Ben. He ate him in one gulp. He said, "Mmm, that was good. Thank you, little girl. You are very kind. Do you want some ice cream and cookies now?"

Lily nodded. She did not care about Ben. She only cared about ice cream and cookies. She said, "Yes, please. I want some ice cream and cookies. They are yummy."

The man gave her some ice cream and cookies. She ate them and smiled. She did not know that she had done something very bad. She did not know that she would never see Ben again. She did not know that she was a bad sister. She did not know that she was a bad person. She only knew that she was happy.

One day, the noise-loving brother decided to take things to the next level. He brought a special toy - a wild, noisy, monster truck! It roared like a real monster.

This monster truck was too noisy for the peace-loving brother. He hated the loud noise of the truck and wanted it gone. But the noise-loving brother refused to spoil his fun.

So every day, the brothers argued and fought. The noise was so bad that it was spoiling their time together. That is until they came up with a brilliant idea.

The brothers agreed to take turns playing with the wild monster truck. This way, they could both still have fun and not spoil the peace and quiet for each other.

After a few days, the seed started to grow into a beautiful flower. The ant was very happy and proud of herself for being able to make something grow. She danced around the flower with joy.

From that day on, the ant took care of the flower every day. She made sure it had enough water and sunlight. The flower continued to bloom and grow bigger and bigger.

The other ants in the colony were amazed at the clever ant's gardening skills. They asked her how she did it, and she told them all about planting seeds and taking care of them. From then on, the ants all worked together to create a beautiful garden full of blooming flowers.

Once upon a time there was a boy called Joe. He loved eating blueberries. One day, he went to the woods to pick some blueberries. He found the biggest and most delicate blueberries he had ever seen.

Suddenly, he heard a voice. It was an old lady. "Those blueberries don't belong to you! She shouted.

Joe replied, "No, they are free. I want to pick them.

The old lady said "No! These blueberries are special. You must leave them. But Joe didn't listen. He started picking the delicate blueberries.

The old lady got mad. She stamped her foot and suddenly the ground started shaking. Joe fell to the ground. The old lady said "That is what happens if you don't listen. Now you must leave these blueberries alone!

Joe was sad but he understood. He said sorry to the old lady and then he went home. He never picked the blueberries again.

Next, they got a spoon and put in some butter and some vanilla. The father stirred everything around and said, "Let's put it in the oven so it will cook."

When the cake was done, it was white and soft. The parents said, "Look at the cake!" The daughter was so excited and said, "Can I have a bite?"

The parents gave her a piece and she loved it. As she finished her piece, she saw a fly. She pointed to the fly and said, "Look! A fly!" The mother and the father laughed and said, "Yes, that is a fly."

"Can I play with your doll, Mia?" Ben asked.

"OK, but be careful. She is very pretty and soft," Mia said.

"Can I play with your truck, Ben?" Mia asked.

"OK, but be careful. It is very loud and fast," Ben said.

They both felt frustrated and unhappy. They wanted their own toys back.

"Ben, I don't like your truck. It is too noisy and hard. I want my doll back," Mia said.

"Mia, I don't like your doll. It is too quiet and soft. I want my truck back," Ben said.

They gave each other their toys back and hugged. They felt happy and sorry.

"Sorry, Mia. I should have been more careful with your doll. She is lovely," Ben said.

"Sorry, Ben. I should have been more respectful of your truck. It is cool," Mia said.

"Mom, we were playing with our toys. We learned to fit and be creative," Ben said.

Their mom smiled and hugged them. She was proud and happy.

"That's wonderful, Mia and Ben. You are such good children. Now, let's eat," she said.

Tim ran to Sue's house and said, "Sue, look at my new gem! It's so big and shiny!" Sue looked at the gem and said, "Wow! That's a pretty gem, Tim. Can I keep it for a while?" But Tim was stubborn and said, "No, it's my gem. I want to keep it."

Sue was sad, but she understood. Tim and Sue played with other toys and had a fun day together. When it was time for Tim to go home, he looked at his gem and thought about Sue. He didn't want her to be sad, so he decided to give her the gem.

Tim said, "Sue, I want you to have this gem. We can share it and both keep it safe." Sue smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim! We'll be the best gem keepers ever!" From that day on, Tim and Sue took turns keeping the gem, and they were the best of friends.
