You are a good dog and a loyal friend". Max wagged his tail and smiled. He was happy that he could help his new friend. From that day on, Max and the bird became best friends and played together every day.

Lily liked to mail stories to her grandma. She wrote stories with crayons and stickers. She put them in envelopes and gave them to mom. Mom put stamps on them and took them to the big box.

One day, Lily was very tired. She had played a lot with her toys and her dog. She did not want to write a story. She wanted to sleep. But she did not want to make grandma sad.

She had an idea. She took a picture book from her shelf. She opened it and looked at the pictures. She made up a story with the pictures. She told the story to mom. Mom wrote the story on a paper. Lily put the paper and the book in a big envelope. She gave it to mom.

Mom smiled and said, "What a nice story, Lily! Grandma will love it!" She put a stamp on the big envelope and took it to the big box.

Lily was happy. She hugged mom and said, "I love you, mom. I love grandma, too." She went to bed and dreamed of grandma reading her story.

Lily and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run and slide and swing. It was a hot day, so they looked for some shade under a big tree. They found a nice spot and sat down on the grass.

"Look, a bug!" Ben said, pointing to a small black thing on a leaf. He reached out to touch it.

"Careful, Ben. It might bite you," Lily warned. She had learned that some bugs can hurt you with their mouths.

Ben did not listen. He wanted to see the bug up close. He picked up the leaf and brought it to his face. The bug did not like that. It bit Ben on his nose.

"Ow! Ow! Ow!" Ben cried, dropping the leaf and rubbing his nose. It was red and sore. He felt tears in his eyes.

Lily felt sorry for Ben. She hugged him and said, "I'm sorry, Ben. That was not fair. The bug was mean to you."

She gave him her water bottle and said, "Here, drink some water. It will make you feel better."

Ben drank some water and sniffed. He felt a little better. He smiled at Lily and said, "Thank you, Lily. You are a good friend."

They hugged again and decided to play something else. They left the bug alone and went to the sandbox. They had fun making castles and tunnels. They forgot about the bite and enjoyed the shade.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived two friends, Tom and Tim. They always played together. One day, they decided to race. The one who would win the race would get a big clap from everyone.

The race started, and both friends ran as fast as they could. Tom was ahead, and Tim felt bad. He wanted to win, too. So, Tim thought of a plan. He picked up a rock and threw it at Tom's leg. Tom fell down and felt hurt.

Tim won the race, but no one clapped for him. They saw what he did to Tom and felt ashamed of Tim. Tim realized that cheating was wrong. He said sorry to Tom and promised never to cheat again. From that day on, they played fair and had even more fun. The moral of the story is: "winning is not important, but playing fair is."

One day, a little girl called Sarah went to an old lab. It had fancy decorations. She looked around and noticed a big table. On this table was a sharp knife. She picked it up and noticed how pointy it was. She wanted to know what it was used for so she asked the lab worker.

The worker explained that the knife was very sharp and could cut through anything. She explained that if you needed to cut something very thin, this knife was the best for the job. Sarah was amazed. She had never seen anything so sharp!

She thanked the worker and took a good look around the lab. She noticed the fancy computers and gadgets. She was impressed by everything she saw. Sarah went home with a smile, full of excitement about the sharp knife she had seen in the lab.

Once there was a small boy. He was only 3 years old. He had a red shirt that he liked to wear. He loved to hear the sound of birds whistling in the trees.

One day the boy decided to make his own whistling sound. He put his fingers to his lips and blew out a soft whistle. The sound made him smile and he whistled louder and longer.

The boy laughed and ran around the yard, whistling as he went. He liked the sound of the birds and their cheerful whistles. He felt like he was part of the fun, too.

When he was tired of whistling, the boy put on his red shirt and ran inside. As he passed the kitchen window, he heard the birds singing and the breeze whistling softly through the trees.

The boy smiled and kept whistling until it was time for bed.

Once upon a time, there was a stable. Inside the stable were two ponies. The ponies were happy and enjoyed jumping around their home.

One day, one of the ponies made a mistake and knocked over a bucket. The other pony got mad and started to yell.

The yelling frightened the other pony. He ran outside and didn't come back. The pony inside the stable knew he had made the other one angry and he felt very sorry.

For days and days, the pony was alone. He missed his friend and hoped that he would come back. Then one day, he heard a familiar voice. It was his friend!

It turned out his friend had been looking for new places to play. The two ponies were so happy to be together again and they hugged each other and jumped around the stable. From then on, the two ponies stayed together and were never scared or mad at each other again.

Once there was a little boy who loved playing in the park. Every day he would look around for new things to explore. One day, he heard a strange voice coming from a tall tree nearby. He looked up and saw a bird singing from a branch. He listened carefully to the bird's melodic song. The little boy stood there for a long time, listening to the voice of the bird.

The little boy couldn't help but smile. He thought the voice was so beautiful. Finally, he waved goodbye to the bird and said, "Thank you for bringing me so much joy!"

The bird replied, "Anytime, my young friend. Just remember: always take time to appreciate the beauty of nature. Don't take it for granted."

The little boy nodded in understanding and said, "I won't forget." Then he ran off to the playground, happy and full of joy.

The moral of the story is that we should always take time to appreciate the beauty of nature. From listening to the song of a bird to admiring the skyline, looking at the natural world can bring us so much joy. When we appreciate the world around us, everything will seem a little more beautiful.

Ben and Lily liked to play in the garden. They had a big box of toys. They had balls, cars, dolls, and books. But their favorite toy was a big iron star. It was shiny and heavy. They liked to hold it and pretend it was magic.

One day, they saw a new sign on the fence. It said "No trespassing. Danger. Keep out." Ben and Lily were curious. They wanted to see what was behind the fence. They took their iron star and climbed over the fence. They saw a big factory. It had smoke and noise and machines. They were scared, but they wanted to explore.

They walked around the factory. They saw pipes and wires and buttons. They saw workers in helmets and gloves. They saw a big furnace. It was hot and red. Ben and Lily went closer. They saw something shiny inside the furnace. It looked like their iron star, but bigger and brighter. They wanted to touch it.

They threw their iron star into the furnace. They hoped it would make their star glow too. But they made a big mistake. The iron star was not magic. It was metal. And metal melts in the furnace. The iron star melted and made a loud bang. It made sparks and flames. It made the furnace explode.

The workers heard the explosion. They ran to the furnace. They saw Ben and Lily. They were dirty and scared, but not hurt. They were lucky. The workers were angry and worried. They asked Ben and Lily why they did that. Ben and Lily said they were sorry. They said they wanted to make their star glow.

The workers were not happy. They said Ben and Lily were naughty and foolish. They said they could have caused a big fire. They said they had to pay for the damage. They said they had to give them their iron star. But Ben and Lily said no. They said their iron star was their favorite toy. They said they loved it.

The workers saw how sad Ben and Lily were. They felt sorry for them. They remembered how they liked to play when they were little. They decided to be generous. They said Ben and Lily could keep their iron star. But they had to promise to never come back to the factory. And they had to promise to be careful with their toys.

Ben and Lily were happy. They said thank you. They said they were sorry. They said they would keep their promise. They took their iron star and climbed over the fence. They went back to the garden. They hugged their iron star. It was still shiny and heavy. But it did not glow. And they did not mind.

Once upon a time there was a cupboard. Every day it filled up with new things for the girl. One day it was filled with pretty, sparkly things in all sorts of colors. They were safe inside the cupboard.

The girl always loved opening the cupboard and seeing what was inside. She liked to take things out and look at them.

One day the girl opened the cupboard and had an idea. She would scatter the things inside all around the room. She had lots of fun with her idea. She moved quickly, taking out all of the things in the cupboard and scattering them around the room.

The girl took her time putting back the things in the cupboard. Then she said, "I will keep all my pretty things safe in here forever." And she did.

Mum was sitting in her chair and her son sat on the floor in front of her. Mum was looking into her son's eyes and said "Do you understand what truth is darling?"

The little boy smiled and nodded.

Mum said "The truth is that I love you very much, even when I am busy and cannot spend time with you. Do you accept this truth?"

The little boy smiled and said "Yes mum. Your love is sweet and I accept it".

Mum smiled and said "Good! That's my boy!" and she hugged her son.

The little boy felt happy and knew that the truth would always be accepted by his mum. The sweet truth of love was shared and accepted by both mum and son.

Lily and Tom were friends who liked to play together. One day, they went to the park with their moms. They saw a big truck that sold pastries. Lily and Tom both wanted a pastry, but their moms said they had to share one.

Lily and Tom picked a pastry that had chocolate and cream. It looked yummy. They took turns to bite it. Lily bit a big piece. Tom bit a small piece. Lily bit another big piece. Tom got jealous. He wanted more pastry.

Tom waited for Lily to look away. Then he grabbed the pastry and ran. Lily saw him and ran after him. She was angry. She shouted, "Give me back my pastry, Tom!"

Tom ran faster. He did not want to share. He ran across the street. He did not look for cars. A car came and hit Tom. Tom dropped the pastry. He fell down and cried. Lily stopped and gasped. She was scared.

The moms heard the noise and ran to Tom. They called for help. The car driver was sorry. He said he did not see Tom. Lily felt sad. She wished Tom did not run. She wished they shared the pastry. She wished they were still friends.

Once upon a time, there was a cute little rabbit. He had big ears and a big, fluffy tail. His name was Bounce.

Every day Bounce would hop through the forest, looking for something special. He was searching for something called "butter". He'd heard that it was really yummy.

One day, as Bounce hopped along looking for the butter, he came across a charming little stream. The water was crystal clear and sparkled in the sunshine. Bounce just had to take a closer look.

Bounce hopped closer and closer to the stream but then, something went wrong. He slipped on a rock and went flying into the water. The water was cold and deep, so Bounce started to panic. He wasn't sure he'd be able to get out.

Just then, he spotted a beam of bright light. It was the butter! He had finally found it. Using all his strength, Bounce bounced out of the water and grabbed the butter. It was the most delicious thing he had ever tasted.

Bounce happily hopped away, with the delicious butter in his paws. He had solved his own problem and was feeling very pleased with himself.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in her backyard, but one day she saw a butterfly flying high in the sky. She wanted to catch it, but it was too far away. She stretched her arms as far as she could, but she couldn't reach it.

Lily felt sad and went inside her house. Her mom saw her and asked what was wrong. Lily told her mom about the butterfly and how she couldn't reach it. Her mom told her to not be sad and that she would help her catch it.

They went outside together and Lily's mom held her up so she could reach the butterfly. Lily was so happy when she caught it and they both watched it fly away. Lily felt peace in her heart and she thanked her mom for helping her.

Afterwards, they went inside and had some tea. Lily's mom made it a little bitter, but Lily didn't mind. She was happy to have caught the butterfly and spend time with her mom.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy called Tom. Tom was a very curious child, and always wanted to explore. One day, Tom wanted to see what was inside his house. He decided to print a map of his home. As he explored, he became amazed with the amount of oxygen in each room.

Unfortunately, Tom went too far and found himself stuck in the basement. No matter how loud he called, no one heard. He felt so miserable.

Suddenly, Tom realized he was surrounded by oxygen tanks. Without wasting any time, Tom started to print a sign that said "help". After a few minutes, his mum heard the printing and came to rescue him. She hugged him tightly and said "Oh my boy, thank goodness you are safe".

Tom was so happy and learnt a valuable lesson that day. He realised that being brave is great, but being safe is essential.

Jane smiled and gave it a try. She found that the hug made her feel better.

Once upon a time, there was a smart doggy. He loved going on adventures and was always alert. Every morning, he would go outside and look for something new to explore.

On one bright day, he found something exciting! He found an open window and decided to go inside. He saw a room full of work and he quickly started helping. He thought it was so much fun to move the pen and paper around.

He was so busy that he lost track of time. By the time he realized it was getting late, his owners were coming home. He heard them so he quickly ran out the way he came.

That night, he couldn't stop thinking about the room full of work. So the next day he decided to return. He had a happy surprise waiting for him at the window. His owners had left a toy for him with a big hug. He was so delighted and cleverly brought the toy back to his home.

To this day, the doggy never stops being alert and always looks forward to his next adventure!

"Mommy, can I go for a swim?" she asked.

"Yes, you can Suzie. But remember to be careful and don't go too far out!" her mom said.

Suzie nodded and smiled. She quickly ran to the lake and sat down on the grass. She looked out over the lake and saw some ducks swimming in the water. She watched them for a few minutes and then decided to take the plunge. She waded into the lake and soon was swimming with the ducks. She had so much fun splashing and laughing in the water.

When she got tired, she got out of the lake and sat on the grass to dry off. While she sat there, she watched the ducks swim around happily. She felt happy and grateful for her special swim.

When they got to the park, they saw a big boy with a weapon. Timmy got scared and ran away. But his truck was still there. The big boy picked it up and broke it. Timmy was sad and angry.

He went home and told his mom what happened. She hugged him and said she would buy him a new truck. Timmy felt better and went to bed. The next day, he went back to the park with his new truck. He saw the big boy again, but this time he didn't bring his weapon. They played together and had fun. The end.

Sam and Mia love to play at the beach. They like to build sand castles, dig holes and look for shells. Sometimes they also go into the water and splash each other.

One day, they see some big waves coming. They see some people riding on the waves with boards. They look like they are having fun.

"Wow, look at them!" Sam says. "They are surfing!"

"I want to try that too!" Mia says. "It looks so cool!"

They run to their mom and dad and ask them if they can surf. Their mom and dad say yes, but they have to be careful and stay close to them. They also have to wear special suits and helmets to keep them warm and safe.

Sam and Mia are very happy. They feel lucky. They put on their suits and helmets and follow their mom and dad to the water. Their mom and dad help them get on the boards and show them how to balance and paddle.

Soon, they catch a small wave and glide along. They feel the wind and the spray on their faces. They laugh and shout with joy.

"Look at us, we are surfing!" Sam says.

"We are surfing, we are surfing!" Mia says.

They have so much fun that they don't want to stop. They surf until the sun sets and the sky turns pink and purple. They thank their mom and dad for the best day ever. They hug and kiss them and say they love them.

They can't wait to surf again.

Once upon a time, there were two little bears who lived in the same house. They were best friends, but they sometimes disagreed.

One day, the two bears had a disagreement about which place to go to that they had never been to before. One bear wanted to go to the beach, and the other wanted to go to the forest.

The first bear said, "Let's go to the beach. It's going to be so much fun!"

But the second bear disagreed. She said, "I think we should go to the forest. There are so many sweet things to discover!"

The first bear put his head in his hands and sighed. He didn't know what to do. Then he had a brilliant idea. "Let's go to both places!" he said with a smile.

The second bear smiled too. She nodded her head in agreement.

They both agreed that it was a sweet idea, and so off they went for an adventure.

Once there was a house. In the house was a fireplace. One day, two children saw the fireplace and wanted to explore it. One of the children saw a stick and decided to poke the fireplace. To the children's surprise, it lit up! The other child was so grateful. They both watched in amazement as the fire danced in the fireplace. They felt so lucky to have the fire to keep them warm and the light to fill the house. The children had never seen anything like it before and they were happy they could now enjoy their special time together.

Lily and Sam were friends who liked to play with cards. They had many cards with pictures of animals, flowers, and cars. Some cards were rare and shiny, and some cards were common and dull. Lily and Sam wanted to have the rare cards, because they thought they had more value.

One day, they decided to play a game with their cards. They each picked five cards from their pile, and showed them to each other. Whoever had the most rare cards would win the game. Lily and Sam counted their rare cards, and saw that they had the same number: two. They looked at their other cards, and saw that they had different pictures.

"I have a card with a lion, and you don't. That means I win!" Lily said.

"No, I have a card with a rocket, and you don't. That means I win!" Sam said.

They argued and argued, and did not listen to each other. They got angry and sad, and stopped playing. They put their cards away, and went to their homes.

The next day, they missed each other, and wanted to play again. They took out their cards, and looked at them. They realized that they had many cards with different pictures, and that they could share them and learn from them. They decided to play a new game, where they would show each other their cards, and tell each other what they knew about them. They learned about lions and rockets, and also about flowers and cars. They had fun and laughed, and did not care about who had the rare cards. They found out that they had more value as friends than as cards. They were happy and sorry, and hugged each other. They played and played, and did not argue. They won the game of friendship.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Max. Max loved to play with his toys all day. One day, Max's mom gifted him a big box of paints. Max was very happy and excited. He opened the box and saw many colors of paints.

Max's mom was very proud of him and hung the painting on the wall. Max was happy and smiled. From that day on, Max loved to mix colors and paint beautiful pictures.

Once upon a time, there was an unknown king. He was an old and powerful king, who ruled over his kingdom with an iron fist. But even he was scared of one thing; the snap. Nobody knew what the snap was, only that it was something to fear.

One day, the king decided to go looking for the snap. He searched far and wide, but he still couldn't find it. He asked many people, but nobody knew what it was.

Just as he was giving up hope, he heard a sound in the sky. He looked up, and there it was - the snap! It looked like a bird, with wings outstretched and a loud cawing noise. The king was amazed, and he watched as the snap flew away. He knew he would never forget the mysterious creature.

The king returned to his kingdom and snapped his fingers - just like the snap he had seen. The people of the kingdom knew something was different, and they began to treat their king with more respect.

The king declared that the snap was his special helper and messenger, and from that day forward, the people of the kingdom saw the snap as a sign of the unknown king's power.

Once upon a time there were two friends named Sam and Jane. Sam was young and adventurous and loved to explore. One day Sam decided to start an adventure. He grabbed a few things and put them in a bag.

He walked to the edge of the forest and looked at the tall trees. He put down his bag, took a deep breath and stepped inside. He started walking and soon saw a big river stretching along the side of the woods.

The river looked mysterious and exciting, so Sam decided to start going down the river. It was an adventurous journey with lots of curves and rocky areas. Sam would splash around and explore all the different things he could find.

Soon Sam got to the end of the river and he saw a clearing. In the clearing were two large stones and in between them was a small path. Sam had no idea what he would find on the path but he was excited to start exploring it.

He put his bag down carefully, and started walking along the path. After a few steps he saw a big waterfall. He had never seen such a thing before. He was amazed and wanted to explore more.

And so, Sam started his own adventure filled with excitement and surprises. He learned something new on every journey and he was never bored. Sam was very happy that he decided to start his journey and explore.

Lily was excited to surf, but she was scared of the big waves. Her dad said, "Don't worry, Lily. I'll be right here with you." Lily felt better and they went into the water. After a while, Lily's dad asked her if she wanted to go back to the shore. Lily replied, "No, daddy. I want to surf more!"

Once upon a time, there was a happy yellow duck. The duck loved to swim in the pond and play with its friends. One day, the duck saw a shiny razor on the ground. It didn't know what it was, so it picked it up and accidentally cut its wing. The duck was sad and couldn't fly anymore.

The other animals in the pond tried to help the duck, but nothing worked. Then, a kind old owl came and said he could restore the duck's wing. The owl used some magic and the duck's wing was fixed! The duck was happy again and could fly with its friends.

But the razor was still on the ground and could hurt someone else. The animals decided to bury it deep in the ground so no one would get hurt. From that day on, the animals learned to be careful and not touch sharp things like razors.

Once upon a time, there was a boy called Jack who had a very difficult time with math. His mommy tried to help him understand the Math better, but it was still hard for him.

One day, Jack got mad because he felt like he wasn't making enough progress. He threw his pencil to the ground and shouted in anger. His mommy said, "It's okay, Jack. Don't be upset - math isn't easy.

Jack looked at his mom and said, "But I'm trying my best! Why can't I do this? His mommy smiled and said, "Sometimes it takes a little bit of extra effort, but I know you can do it. You just need to be patient and keep trying. I forgive you for getting angry.

Jack was happy that his mommy understood and he felt a bit better. He picked up his pencil and tried again. Although math was still difficult, Jack eventually got it. His patience and extra effort paid off!

One day, a little boy named Tim found a box of matches. He thought they were incredible. He wanted to play with them. Tim's mom always told him not to play with matches, but he didn't listen.

Tim went to his room in silence. He didn't want his mom to know. He took a match and struck it on the box. The match lit up with a bright flame. Tim was so excited. He said, "Wow, this is so fun!"

Tim's friend, Sam, came over. Tim showed Sam the matches. Sam said, "We should not play with these. They are not safe." But Tim didn't listen. They played with the matches some more.

The matches made a big fire in Tim's room. The fire grew and grew. Tim and Sam tried to stop it, but they couldn't. The fire was too big. Tim's mom came in and saw the fire. She was very sad and scared.

In the end, Tim's house burned down. Tim and his family had no home. Tim learned a hard lesson. He should have listened to his mom and his friend. Playing with matches was not a good idea.

Once upon a time there was a mighty frog. He loved to jump higher than any other frog. Everywhere he went he made sure he jumped higher than all the other animals. One day he came across a folder on the ground and he saw that it contained a secret.

The mighty frog was very curious and he wanted to see what was inside the folder. He jumped on top of the folder and peered inside. To the frog's surprise he found a pile of rocks in the folder. He figured out that if he used the rocks he could jump even higher than before!

The mighty frog started to form a plan. He gathered the rocks and put them in a pile. He then stood on top of the rocks and then he jumped. With every rock he added, he was able to jump a little bit higher.

Soon the frog was close to reaching the clouds, so close he could almost taste it! But then disaster struck. With one mighty leap, the frog over shot the clouds and he began to tumble down. He fell and fell until eventually he landed on the ground, never to jump again.

The frog had not been careful. He was so busy trying to reach his goal that he had not seen the danger. The mighty frog had been too ambitious!

Once upon a time there was a teddy bear called Tom. Tom loved to bathe in the warm sunshine and drink sweet tea.

One sunny day, Tom had a special treat. His mummy took him for a tea party on the beach. Tom gathered some soft sand near the water's edge and made a cozy spot for himself, then he sat down with a cup of tea.

Tom sipped his tea, feeling the warm liquid slip down his throat. Then he lay back and let the sunshine bathe his body. He imagined he was a dolphin, gliding through the waves.

When the sun started to sink in the sky Tom knew it was time to go home. He said goodbye to his sandy spot and gathered up his tea cup. Waving to the beach he turned and ran back to mummy, feeling full of joy.

Once there was a bear who was deaf. Every day, he tried to solve big and small problems. One day, he saw a giant rock. He didn't know how to solve the problem of getting it out of the way.

The bear thought and thought, but he couldn't come up with a good idea. Then he decided to ask his friends for help. He saw a rabbit, a bird and a sheep nearby and he asked them what to do.

The rabbit said, "Let's dig it out!" The bird said, "Let's fly over it!" The sheep said, "Let's push it down!"

But none of their ideas worked. Then the bear had an idea. He said, "Let's all push together." So the four of them pushed and pushed until the rock rolled away!

The bear was very proud of himself. He had solved the problem! He was so happy that his friends had helped him. From then on, he knew that he could always count on his friends for help.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved playing with her dolls and teddy bears. One week, Lily's mom told her that they would go to the park. Lily was very happy because she loved playing on the swings and slides.

When they got to the park, Lily saw some other kids playing together. She wanted to join them, but they were being harsh and mean to her. They ignored her and wouldn't let her play with them. Lily felt very sad and went back to her mom.

Her mom asked her what was wrong, and Lily told her about the mean kids. Her mom said, "Don't worry, Lily. Let's go find something else fun to do." They went to the other side of the park and found a sandbox. Lily played happily in the sand, and soon some other kids came over to play with her. They were nice and friendly, and Lily had a great time.

Lily learned that sometimes people can be mean, but it's important to ignore them and find something else to do. She was happy that she found new friends to play with, and she had a great week playing in the park.

Lily and Ben are twins. They like to play with their toys in the living room. One day, they see a big box in the corner. They wonder what is inside.

"Let's open it!" Lily says. She runs to the box and pulls the tape. Ben follows her and helps her lift the lid.

Inside the box, they find a lot of shiny things. They are pots and pans and spoons and forks. They are made of metal and have oil on them. The oil makes them slippery and shiny.

"Wow, look at these!" Ben says. He picks up a spoon and a fork and pretends to eat. Lily grabs a pot and a pan and bangs them together. They make a loud noise.

They are having fun with their new toys. They pretend to cook and eat and serve each other. They laugh and smile and talk.

Mom and Dad enter the living room. They see the twins playing with the box. They are surprised and happy.

"Hello, Lily and Ben!" Mom says. "What are you doing?"

"We are playing with our new toys!" Lily says. "They are shiny and oily and noisy!"

"They are not toys, sweetie," Dad says. "They are our new kitchenware. We bought them yesterday. They have oil on them to protect them from rust."

"Oh," Lily says. She looks at the pot and the pan. "Can we still play with them?"

"Of course you can," Mom says. "But be careful not to hurt yourself or make a mess. And when you are done, we can wash them and put them away."

"Okay," Ben says. He puts down the spoon and the fork. "They are fun, but we can share them with you."

Mom and Dad smile. They hug the twins and kiss them. They are proud and cheerful. They love their children very much.

Once upon a time there was a nice little girl called Lucy. She was three years old and had beautiful curly hair. One day, Lucy was feeling very sad. She was feeling lonely and wanted a hug.

She asked her mum, "Can I have a hug?". Her mum smiled, but told her she had to delay asking for a hug until after lunch. Lucy was very disappointed.

Just then, her older brother arrived. He saw her sad face and said: "Come on, I'll give you a hug if you like!" Lucy smiled, and hugged her brother for a long time.

Suddenly, a strange man appeared in the doorway. He was very rude and shouted at them: "What are you two doing hugging? Stop it at once!"

But Lucy's mum and brother were not scared. They told the man that hugs were not bad and that Lucy just wanted a hug. The rude man left, and Lucy got her hug after all.

The end.

It's okay to be brave and try something new, but always be careful."

The moral of the story is: It's okay to try something new, but always make sure you are safe and know what you are doing.

"Wow, look at the flower!" Sara says. "It is so big and bright!"

"Yes, it is!" Tom says. "Do you think it will grow more?"

"I don't know," Sara says. "Maybe we can help it. Let's give it some water and sun."

They run to get a bucket and a cup. They fill the bucket with water from the tap. They pour some water on the flower. They move the pot to a sunny spot.

"There, now it will be happy," Tom says.

They sit and watch the flower. They see a bee come and buzz around it. They hear a bird sing on a branch. They smell the sweet scent of the flower.

"I like the flower," Sara says. "It makes me happy, too."

"Me, too," Tom says. "Maybe we can grow more flowers. We can make our street more beautiful."

They smile and hug. They are happy to have a yellow flower and each other.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play in the park with her friends. One day, she saw a little ant crawling on the ground. She picked it up and asked, "What are you doing?"

The ant replied, "I'm looking for food to bring back to my ant hill. It's hard work, but it's the only way we can survive."

Lily thought for a moment and said, "You're so small and harmless. I'll help you find some food!" So, she looked around and found a small piece of bread. She gave it to the ant and said, "Here you go, little ant. I hope this helps."

The ant was very grateful and said, "Thank you, Lily! You're so kind. I'll remember this and tell all my ant friends about how you helped me." And with that, the ant scurried away back to its ant hill. Lily felt happy that she could help the ant and went back to playing with her friends in the park.

Once upon a time, there was a bird. He loved to dance and sing, but he couldn't find the right rhythm. He tried and tried, but his singing just didn't have the right beat. He was sad because he wanted to dance, but he couldn't.

One day, a magic fairy flew into his room. "My friend," said the fairy, "I can help you find the right beats for your dancing. Just close your eyes, and I will release a spicy rhythm."

The bird closed his eyes as the magic fairy waved her wand. Suddenly, he felt a beat in his head. He opened his eyes and started twirling, bouncing, and clapping with the new rhythm. His heart felt so light and happy!

He thanked the fairy with a big smile and she flew away. From that day onward, the bird danced and sang with perfect rhythm. Whenever he felt down, he would close his eyes and listen to the spicy rhythm the fairy had released.

Sam and Mia are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they see a new boy on the swing. He has a big smile and a red hat.

"Hi, I am Ben. Do you want to play with me?" he asks.

Sam and Mia nod. They welcome Ben and go to the slide. They take turns and have fun.

But then, Sam slips and falls. He hurts his toe. He cries loud.

"Ow, ow, ow! My toe, my toe!" he says.

Mia runs to him and hugs him. She says, "It's okay, Sam. I will get a band-aid for you."

Ben looks at Sam and feels sorry. He says, "I'm sorry, Sam. I didn't mean to make you fall. Can I help you?"

Sam stops crying. He looks at Ben and says, "Thank you, Ben. You are a good friend. Can you get me some water?"

Ben nods. He runs to the fountain and gets some water. He brings it to Sam and helps him drink.

Sam smiles and says, "Thank you, Ben. You are very kind. Do you want to play with us again?"

Ben smiles and says, "Yes, please. I like to play with you and Mia. You are very nice."

Lily and Tom were twins who liked to play by the lake. They had a small boat that they could row together. One day, they decided to go on the boat and explore the water.

"Look, Lily, there are some ducks over there!" Tom said, pointing to a group of birds. "Let's go and say hello to them!"

"OK, Tom, but be careful not to scare them," Lily said, as she helped him row the boat. They got closer to the ducks and smiled at them. The ducks quacked and swam away.

"Hey, where are you going?" Tom called out. "We just want to be friends!"

Lily noticed that the wind was getting stronger and the water was getting choppier. She felt scared and worried. She looked at the shore and saw that they were far away from it.

"Tom, I think we should go back," she said. "It's getting too windy and the boat is rocking too much."

Tom looked around and saw that Lily was right. He felt scared and worried too. He tried to row the boat back, but it was hard to fight against the wind and the waves.

"Lily, I'm sorry, I didn't mean to take us so far," he said. "I hope we can make it back."

Lily hugged him and said, "It's OK, Tom, we'll be fine. We just have to keep rowing and stay calm. Maybe someone will see us and help us."

They kept rowing and praying for help. After a while, they saw a bigger boat coming towards them. It was their dad, who had been looking for them. He waved and shouted, "Lily, Tom, are you OK? I'm here to rescue you!"

Lily and Tom felt relieved and happy. They waved and shouted back, "Dad, we're OK, we're sorry, we got lost!"

Their dad reached them and helped them get on his boat. He hugged them and said, "I'm so glad you're safe. I was very worried about you. You shouldn't go on the boat without me. It's too dangerous."

Lily and Tom nodded and said, "We're sorry, Dad, we won't do it again. We were very scared and worried too. Thank you for saving us."

Their dad smiled and said, "You're welcome, my loves. I'm proud of you for being brave and sticking together. Now let's go home and have some hot chocolate."

Her mom said no, they had to leave some stuff behind, because the new house was smaller. She said Lily could only take one big bag of stuff.

Lily had to choose which stuff to take and which stuff to leave. It was very hard, because she loved all her stuff. She tried to fit as much stuff as she could in her bag, but it was not enough. She had to say goodbye to some of her stuff. She felt like her heart was breaking.

She wept and wept as she hugged her stuff. She said sorry to them and told them she would miss them. She hoped they would find new friends who would play with them and love them. She put them in a box and gave them one last kiss.

Once upon a time there was a silly bear called Tim. He liked to explore the world and find fun new things. One day he went for a walk and noticed a mysterious knob. He tried to turn the knob but nothing happened. He got very frustrated and started to chew it.

Suddenly, something magical happened! The knob opened and Tim was very surprised. Inside there were lots of delicious sweets. He grabbed as many as he could and started to chew them.

No sooner than he started, however, the knob started to close. Tim got scared and tried to run away. But, it was too late and the knob closed before Tim could escape, trapping him inside!

Tim was very frightened and started to cry. Then, just when he thought all hope was lost, he noticed the knob. He quickly realized that the only way to escape would be to chew the knob.

He grabbed the knob and started to chew. Sure enough, it opened and opened wide enough for Tim to escape. Tim was so relieved and ran off, never to touch a silly knob ever again.

Tim and Mia were sad. They did not like the big boy. He was mean and rude. They wanted to go on the slide, but they were afraid of the big boy. He was bigger and stronger than them.
