Sam and Lily wanted to watch a movie. They asked Mom if they could. Mom said yes, but only if they cleaned their room first. Sam and Lily did not like to clean their room. They had many toys and books and clothes on the floor. They thought it was too hard and too boring.

"Let's pretend we are cleaning, but we are not," Sam said to Lily. "We can hide the mess under the bed and in the closet. Then Mom will think we are done and let us watch the movie."

Lily thought that was a good idea. She nodded and smiled. They started to move the things from the floor to the bed and the closet. They did not fold or sort or put away anything. They just made a big pile. They thought they were very clever.

But Mom was not fooled. She came to check on them and saw the mess. She opened the closet and the bed and saw more mess. She was not happy. She shook her head and frowned.

"Sam and Lily, this is not cleaning. This is crazy. You did not do what I asked you to do. You lied to me. Now you cannot watch the movie. You have to clean your room for real. And no more pretending."

Sam and Lily felt bad. They knew they did wrong. They said sorry to Mom and started to clean their room for real. They learned their lesson. They decided to never lie to Mom again. And they hoped that Mom would let them watch the movie another day.

Once upon a time there was a fire. It was very messy. All around it were things that were beginning to rot.

Suddenly, a little girl arrived next to the fire. She was wearing a big yellow hat on her head. She saw how messy the fire was and felt sad.

The little girl decided to help. She got some water from the nearby river and started to put it on the fire. As the water hit the fire, it started to shrink, until it disappeared.

The little girl was happy to see that the fire was not messy anymore. She smiled and cheered. Then, she continued walking, happy and proud of what she had done.

The end.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy had a favorite pair of pants. They were blue and had little stars on them. He wore them every day and loved them very much.

One day, Timmy went to the park to play with his friends. While he was playing, he heard a noise in the bushes. He went to investigate and found a little bird with a broken wing. Timmy knew he had to help the bird, so he picked it up gently and brought it home.

When Timmy got home, he asked his mom for a box to put the bird in. She gave him a little box and Timmy made a cozy bed for the bird with some of his old pants. The bird felt safe and warm in its new little home.

The next day, Timmy went back to the park to play with his friends. He was happy knowing that the little bird was safe and warm in its little bed made of pants. And he knew that he would always be there to help little creatures in need.

Sara and Ben like to play pretend. They have a toy kitchen and a toy shop. Sara likes to be the cook and Ben likes to be the customer. They make up different foods and drinks with their toys.

One day, Sara makes a sign for her kitchen. The sign says "Sara's Cafe". She draws a cup, a cake and a smiley face on the sign. She hangs the sign on the wall with tape. She thinks the sign is very sweet.

Ben sees the sign and walks to the kitchen. He says "Hello, I want to eat at Sara's Cafe. What do you have?" Sara looks at her toys and says "I have tea, juice, cookies, bread and cheese. What do you want?" Ben says "I want tea and cookies, please."

Sara takes a toy teapot and two toy cups. She pours some water from a bottle into the teapot. She pretends it is hot tea. She takes two toy cookies from a plate. She puts them on a napkin. She serves the tea and cookies to Ben on a tray. She says "Here you go. Enjoy your tea and cookies. They are very yummy."

Ben takes a sip of the water and a bite of the cookie. He makes a happy sound. He says "Thank you, Sara. This is very good. You are a great cook. I like your sign. It is very sweet." Sara smiles and says "You are welcome, Ben. I am glad you like it. You are a nice customer. I like your hat. It is very funny." They laugh and play some more. They have fun at Sara's Cafe.

Once, there was a hippo who lived in a big lake. He was happy and loved to play with all his friends in the lake.

One day, the hippo decided to race his friends. So, he asked them: "Who wants to race me around the lake?" His friends all wanted to race, so the hippo said, "Let's get ready, set, go!"

The hippo was the fastest of all his friends. He was very happy and it seemed like he would win the race easily.

But then, he felt something he had never felt before. He felt an earth tremor as he raced. Suddenly, the hippo was stuck and he couldn't move! He was stuck in the mud, and it looked like he wouldn't be able to finish the race.

But then, the hippo realized that the mud was actually very useful. He used it to quickly push himself out of the mud. With a few big pushes, he was out!

The hippo raced to the finish line and won the race! Everyone was very happy for him, and the hippo knew that being useful could help him during a race.

Once, there was a small ship. It was very smooth and could sail on the sea. It was red and blue and everyone who saw it cared for it a lot.

One day, the ship sailed far away, beyond the land and over the waves. But the waters were choppy and it was hard for the small ship to stay stable.

Suddenly, a strong wind blew and the ship was pushed all the way back to land. Everyone who saw it cheered and all cared for it so much.

The ship was safe and sound, no longer pushed by the wind. Everyone felt relieved and happy and they thanked the ship for being so smooth.

The ship was now able to sail far and wide and it was cared for by everyone. They all loved the small, smooth ship.

Tom and Anna are friends. They like to play with toys. One day, Tom has a new toy. It is a magnet. A magnet can stick to some things, like metal.

"Look, Anna, I have a magnet!" Tom says. He shows Anna how the magnet can stick to his car, his spoon, and his fridge.

"Wow, that is cool!" Anna says. She wants to try the magnet too. She looks around for something to stick it to. She sees a trash can. It is metal. It is also smelly.

"Can I stick the magnet to the trash can?" Anna asks.

"No, Anna, don't do that!" Tom says. "The trash can is smelly. It has bad things inside. The magnet will get dirty and smelly too."

But Anna does not listen. She runs to the trash can and puts the magnet on it. The magnet sticks to the trash can. Anna is happy. She laughs.

"See, Tom, the magnet can stick to the trash can!" Anna says.

But then, something bad happens. A big dog comes out of nowhere. It sees the trash can. It smells the bad things inside. It wants to eat them. It runs to the trash can and knocks it over. The trash can falls down. The magnet falls down too. The trash spills out. The dog starts to eat the trash. The magnet gets lost in the trash.

"Anna, look what you did!" Tom says. He is angry and sad. He wants his magnet back. He tries to find it in the trash. But the trash is too dirty and smelly. He cannot see the magnet. He cannot touch the trash. He feels sick.

"Tom, I'm sorry!" Anna says. She is scared and sorry. She did not mean to make Tom lose his magnet. She did not know the dog would come. She wants to help Tom find his magnet. She tries to look for it too. But the trash is too dirty and smelly. She cannot see the magnet. She cannot touch the trash. She feels sick too.

The dog finishes eating the trash. It does not see the magnet. It does not care about the magnet. It runs away. It is happy and full.

Tom and Anna are not happy. They are not full. They are sad and sick. They lost the magnet. They learned a lesson. They should not play with smelly things. They should listen to each other. They should be careful with their toys. They hug each other. They go home. They hope to find a new magnet someday.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to mix juice for her friends. One day, she had a great idea. She wanted to have a juice party!

She invited all her friends to her house. When they arrived, she showed them all the different juices she had. There was red juice, yellow juice, and even green juice! Lily was very generous and wanted to share everything with her friends.

"Let's mix the juices together!" said Lily. Her friends loved the idea. They took turns mixing the juices in a big bowl. The juice became a beautiful rainbow color. Everyone was so happy and they enjoyed the tasty rainbow juice together.

Once upon a time, there was a modern barn. Inside the barn was a nice stall, and a cow was living with her calf. One day, the cow and her calf decided to go exploring. They opened the door of the barn and split apart.

The cow went one way, and the calf went the other way. The calf couldn't find the cow anywhere, so it decided to explore. The calf saw lots of new, exciting things as it looked around. The calf found a big, wide field filled with clover.

The calf played in the field for hours, looking for the cow all the while. Suddenly, the cow heard the calf's loud mooing. She hurried to the spot, and the calf ran over to the cow and hugged her. The cow and the calf were happy to be together again. They hugged and snuggled and ran back to the modern barn together.

Tim and Lily were twins who liked to play with their toys. They had many toys, but their favorite was a big teddy bear that they called Brownie. They hugged Brownie, dressed him up, and took him everywhere.

One day, their mom told them to clean up their room. She said they had too many toys and they should give some away. Tim and Lily did not want to give away any toys, especially Brownie. They hid him under the bed and put some old toys in a bag.

"Mom, we are done!" they said, holding the bag. "Can we go to the park now?"

Mom looked at the bag and was suspicious. She opened it and saw some broken and dirty toys. She frowned and said, "These are not good toys to give away. They are spoiled. You should throw them in the bin. Where is Brownie? He is a nice toy. Someone would love him."

Tim and Lily felt scared. They did not want to lose Brownie. They ran to their room and grabbed him from under the bed. They hugged him and said, "We are sorry, Brownie. We love you. We will never give you away."

Mom followed them and saw them with Brownie. She was angry and said, "You are very naughty. You lied to me. You did not clean up your room. You hid Brownie. You are selfish. You do not deserve Brownie. Give him to me. I will give him to someone who is kind and enthusiastic."

Tim and Lily cried and begged. They did not want to let go of Brownie. But Mom was stronger and took him away. She put him in the bag and left the room. She said, "You are grounded. You cannot go to the park. You cannot play with any toys. You have to stay in your room and think about what you did."

Tim and Lily were very sad. They had no toys, no park, and no Brownie. They wished they had listened to Mom and cleaned up their room. They wished they had been more generous and shared their toys. They wished they had not spoiled their chance to be happy.

Jimmy and his dad were walking down the street. Jimmy asked "What's a hero, Dad?" Dad smiled, "A hero is someone who goes around, filling people with courage and hope."

Jimmy thought and then said, "Can I be a hero too?" Dad replied, "Yes you can, Jimmy. You can help make the world a better place by helping people who are feeling sad."

Jimmy was so excited. He asked, "Can I fill my friends with courage and hope today?" Dad said, "Yes, you can fill your friends with courage and hope and make them feel happy." Jimmy smiled.

Jimmy went up to his friends, who were feeling a little sad. He said, "Don't worry, I'm here to fill you with courage and hope!" His friends were so surprised and filled with joy. They thanked Jimmy for making them feel better.

Jimmy smiled and was proud. He was a hero.

Once upon a time, a mum and her little girl had a very special day. They tidied up their room together, and when it was all done, the mum said "Well done, darling! You worked so hard! I promise that if you keep helping around the house, there will be a special surprise."

The little girl smiled so wide - she loved surprises! She couldn't wait to find out what it was. Every day the little girl helped out around the house, and she smiled each time.

One day, the mum said to the little girl "You've been working so hard! Today your surprise is ready! Go to your room, and you can see it for yourself."

So the little girl ran to her room and she gasped with excitement. There was a brand new toy box, filled with some of her favourite things! She clapped her hands in glee and ran over to give her mum a big hug. "Thank you for my surprise! I'm so happy - I love it!" she said.

Her mum smiled and said "You deserve it, my darling. You kept your promise and now you have your reward. I'm so proud of you!" The little girl hugged her mum and said "I'll always keep my promises!"

The mum and little girl hugged and laughed. They knew that they could always rely on each other, no matter what.

Once upon a time there was a little girl. She was very anxious because she had a very important task to do. Her mommy wanted her to send a scarf all the way across the country to her grandma. The little girl was so scared that something would go wrong.

So she asked her mommy for help. Her mommy said that she wouldn't need to worry because as long as she was careful to put the scarf in the mail with the correct address it would get there just fine.

The little girl was still nervous. She checked the address three times just to make sure. When she was satisfied that she had it right, she held the scarf tightly in her hands and kissed it goodbye. Then she put it in the mailbox and sent it off.

The little girl watched the mailbox until it was out of sight. Now her only worry was that her grandma would like the scarf when it arrived.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and color with her crayons. One day, she went to the store with her mommy to buy more crayons. Lily was so happy and excited to spend her money on new crayons.

When they got home, Lily sat down to color. But she noticed that her favorite blue crayon was missing. She looked everywhere but couldn't find it. Lily felt nervous because she loved that crayon so much.

Lily's mommy asked, "What's wrong, sweetie?" Lily replied, "My blue crayon is gone. I'm nervous." Her mommy said, "Don't worry, we can buy more crayons next time we go to the store. Let's use another color for now." Lily felt better and continued to color with her other crayons.

Once upon a time, there was a girl who was very scared. She lived in a big city, but her family wanted her to have an adventure. They decided to go on a special trip to an island. When they got there, the girl was so excited! She saw the ocean, the sand, and the sun shining down on her.

The girl wanted to explore the island. She went walking and saw all kinds of plants and animals. She even saw a bright, colorful parrot! Suddenly, she realized that she was alone. She was so scared, she didn't know what to do.

Then, a big and friendly bird came up to her. He said his name was Toby and he would help her. Toby showed the girl how to find her way back to her family. They also played games and explored the island together.

Finally, the girl was not scared anymore. She realized that even though she was alone, she was still safe. From that day on, she always felt brave when she went exploring. And she remembered her new friend Toby whenever she felt scared.

Once upon a time, there was a busy bee. Her name was Bee. She was always on the move and doing lots of things. She loved to be busy!

One day, Bee decided to plant a bulb in her garden. She dug a big hole and put the bulb in it. Then she filled the hole with dirt again.

Bee was a little bit afraid that the bulb wouldn't grow. But her friends came to encourage her. "Don't be afraid," they said. "Everything will be alright. It will grow!"

Bee was happy. She watered the bulb every day and waited. It wasn't long before the bulb starting growing and the flowers came! Bee was so happy that her friends were right.

The end.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to eat fruit. One day, their mom gave them a big basket of pears. They were green and juicy and smelled good.

"Can we eat them now, mom?" Lily asked.

"Yes, but you have to share them with your brother. And be careful with the knife, it is sharp. I will cut the pears for you," mom said.

She took out a knife and a cutting board and started to cut the pears into pieces. She gave each twin a plate with some pear slices.

Lily and Ben said thank you and started to eat. They liked the sweet and soft pears. But then, Lily saw something that made her angry. Ben had an enormous pear slice on his plate. It was bigger than hers.

"Hey, that's not fair! You have a bigger pear than me!" Lily said.

"No, I don't. It's the same size. Mom cut them for us," Ben said.

"Yes, you do. Look, it's enormous. You have to give me some of your pear. Or I will cut it with my knife," Lily said.

She grabbed her knife and tried to reach for Ben's plate. Ben moved his plate away and held his knife too. He did not want to share his pear.

"Stop it, Lily. You can't cut my pear. It's mine. Go away," Ben said.

They started to fight over the pear. They waved their knives and shouted at each other. They did not see that mom was watching them.

"Stop! Stop right now!" mom said. She was angry and scared. She took away their knives and their plates. She looked at them with a stern face.

"What are you doing? You could have hurt yourselves or each other. You are not supposed to play with knives. And you are not supposed to fight over food. There are plenty of pears for both of you. You have to share and be nice to each other. Do you understand?" mom said.

Lily and Ben felt ashamed and sorry. They nodded and said sorry to mom and to each other. They hugged and said they loved each other.

Mom smiled and gave them a hug too. She said she loved them and was glad they were okay. She gave them back their plates and their pears. She cut the enormous pear slice in half and gave one piece to each twin.

They ate their pears and thanked mom. They agreed that the pears were delicious and that sharing was better than fighting. They learned their lesson and never fought over fruit again.

Once upon a time, there were two worms who wanted to go on an adventure. They decided to come to the woods to see what they could see.

They looked and looked, but nothing seemed very exciting. Then they found a big hill they wanted to climb.

The two worms started to climb the hill, but it was hard work! They kept trying and trying until they reached the very top. The worms cheered, they were so happy to have been successful!

At the top of the hill, they saw something they had never seen before - a big lake! They swam around and around, until they were so tired that they needed a rest. The two worms curled up in a sunny spot under a tree and soon fell asleep.

The worms had the best time and were so happy that they had come on their adventure. From then on, they often came back to the woods for more adventures!

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to look up at the night sky and see the bright stars and the big round planet. One day, Timmy decided he wanted to go to the planet.

He asked his mommy if he could go, but she said it was too far away. Timmy was sad, but then he had an idea. He found a stick and started to poke the ground. He poked and poked until he made a big hole.

Suddenly, he fell into the hole and landed on a planet. The planet was very hairy! Timmy was scared at first, but then he saw that the hair was soft and fluffy. He played on the hairy planet all day until it was time to go home. When he got back to his own planet, he told his mommy all about his adventure.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Daisy. She loved going to the store with her mom and dad.

One day she asked her dad, "Can I buy something at the store by myself?"

Her dad said "Yes, but only if you tie the knot in the bag properly. Can you do it?"

Daisy was so excited and said "Yes, I can do it!"

So off she went, proudly carrying her shopping bag.

At the store she picked out some cookies and tried to tie the knot in the bag, but it was too hard. She tried again and again, but she just couldn't get it right.

Soon she became frustrated and started to cry. Then her dad came and showed her how to tie the knot carefully. Daisy finally succeeded and was so relieved.

The moral of the story is: never give up, even if things seem too hard!

One day, a playful little monkey was playing in the jungle. He followed the path until he came to a big tree. But there was no victory! He felt so sad and kept looking around.

Just then, he saw another monkey jumping in the leaves of another tree. The playful little monkey wanted to follow the other monkey. He climbed up the tree and saw a delicious banana. He grabbed it and went back down to see the other monkey.

The other monkey smiled and said, "you followed me". That was what he wanted - a victory! The two monkeys hugged each other and celebrated their victory. They laughed and hugged and played together happily.

The playful little monkey never forgot that victory. He followed his new friend every day and enjoyed their time together.

Lily liked to look at the sky. She saw many clouds. Some were big and white, some were small and gray. She wondered what they felt like. She wanted to touch them.

One day, she had a good idea. She asked her dad to help her. He had a big balloon. He filled it with air and tied a string to it. He gave it to Lily. She smiled and said thank you.

Lily held the string and ran outside. She looked for a cloud that was low and close. She saw one that was fluffy and soft. She ran towards it with the balloon. She hoped to succeed.

She reached the cloud and lifted the balloon. She tried to touch the cloud with the balloon. But the cloud was not solid. It was made of tiny drops of water. The balloon went through the cloud and came out wet.

Lily was surprised and a little sad. She did not touch the cloud. She did not succeed. But she learned something new. She learned that clouds were not like balloons. They were different and beautiful.

She looked at the sky again. She saw many clouds. She smiled and said wow. She still liked to look at the sky.

Once upon a time there was a little puppy named Sam. He was very friendly and loved by everyone. Every day, Sam went to the park to visit his friends. He was always so happy when he did.

But one day when Sam went to the park, his friends had all gone home. Sam was so sad. His friends had promised to play with him but they didn't keep their promise.

Sam was so disappointed but he eventually decided to never trust easily. He tried to remain friendly with everyone but he always made sure to check whether they meant it when they promised something.

This made sure that Sam never got his heart broken when his friends had gone away for good. The moral of the story is to always remain friendly, but make sure to keep your promises too!

Once upon a time, there was a squirrel named Sammy. Sammy loved to eat nuts. One day, Sammy was looking for nuts to eat, but he couldn't find any.

Then, he saw his friend Timmy the rabbit. Timmy had a big, shiny nut in his hand. Sammy asked Timmy if he could lend him the nut to eat. Timmy said yes, and Sammy was very happy.

Sammy ate the nut and it was brilliant! He felt so full and happy. Later that day, Sammy found some more nuts to eat. He remembered how Timmy had lent him the nut and decided to lend some of his nuts to Timmy in return. Timmy was very grateful and they became even better friends.

Ben likes to read books. He has many books in his room. He keeps them organized on his shelf. He can find his favorite books easily.

One day, Ben wants to read a book outside. He takes a book from his shelf and goes to the backyard. He sees a gate in the fence. He wonders what is behind the gate.

He opens the gate and walks through. He sees a big park with trees and flowers and swings. He is happy. He finds a nice spot under a tree and sits down. He opens his book and starts to read.

"Hello, what are you reading?" a voice says. Ben looks up and sees a girl. She is holding a book too. She has curly hair and a blue dress.

"I am reading a book about animals. What are you reading?" Ben says.

"I am reading a book about fairies. Do you like fairies?" the girl says.

"I don't know. I have never read a book about fairies. Can I see your book?" Ben says.

"Sure, you can see my book. Can I see your book?" the girl says.

They swap books and look at the pictures. They like each other's books. They start to talk and laugh. They become friends.

They read and play in the park until it is time to go home. They give back their books and say goodbye.

"See you tomorrow?" the girl says.

"Yes, see you tomorrow. I will bring another book. What is your name?" Ben says.

"My name is Lily. What is your name?" the girl says.

"My name is Ben. Bye, Lily!" Ben says.

"Bye, Ben!" Lily says.

They wave and smile. They close the gate and run to their houses. They are happy. They have found a new friend and a new book.

Tim was walking in the park with his dad and he heard a sound. He looked around and saw a group of kids playing together and they were all laughing. They were having a lot of fun and it made Tim smile.

Tim asked his dad, "Can I go play with them?"

His dad said "Sure, you can go and play."

He ran over to them and they said hello. They all had big smiles and laughed at all of Tim's jokes.

One of the kids said, "You have such big ears!"

The other kids all laughed and looked envious. They all wanted big ears like Tim's.

Then the kid with the biggest ears said, "Let's all have big ears."

So they all chuckled, put their hands on their ears and made them big. Everyone laughed and had fun together. They all had big ears and that made everyone happy and excited.

Once there was a person who wondered what was over the hill. The hill was very thick so they couldn't see what was on the other side.

The person started climbing the hill and after some time they made it to the top. They looked around and saw a beautiful garden full of colorful flowers and tall trees.

The person felt so amazed, and they wondered what else could be so special on the other side. They started walking down the hill and came across a small pond. The pond was very quiet and the water was crystal clear.

The person was so amazed by the beauty of nature. They splashed their feet through the cool water and enjoyed the silence around them.

The person looked up into the sky and wondered what else could be over the hill.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to smile and play with his toys. One day, Tim saw a big, good ice cream truck outside his house. The ice cream truck had many colors and played a happy song. Tim's eyes grew wide, and he smiled.

Tim ran to his mom and said, "Mom, can I have some ice cream, please?" His mom looked at him and smiled too. She said, "Yes, Tim. You have been a good boy. Let's go get some ice cream." Tim's smile grew even bigger, and he clapped his hands.

Tim and his mom went outside to the ice cream truck. The nice ice cream man gave Tim a big scoop of his favorite cream. Tim said, "Thank you!" and took a big bite. The ice cream was cold and sweet. Tim smiled and laughed, feeling happy with his good treat.

Once upon a time there was a bear called Brownie. Brownie was the most playful bear in the forest. He would run and scramble around, always looking for something new to play with.

One summer, Brownie met a duck. The duck chattered away and asked Brownie if he could go and explore the forest together. Brownie thought it sounded like fun and said yes.

The two of them had a great time playing and exploring the woods. But one day, Brownie got a bit too playful and accidentally stepped on the duck's foot without meaning to. The poor duck was crying and so was Brownie.

The duck said to Brownie, "You should be more careful, it hurts when you step on me." Brownie, feeling very sorry, said, "I'm so sorry, I didn't mean to hurt you. Please forgive me." The little duck smiled and said, "It's ok, I forgive you."

The moral of this story is that it's important to forgive others and to be careful when playing so that no one else gets hurt.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Mike. Mike was a very troubled boy because he always seemed to make people angry. He did not understand why his actions made people feel unhappy. One day, Mike went on an adventure to find the secret of victory. He set out on a long journey, but after a while he started to get tired. That's when Mike's friend Andy found him.

Andy said, "Mike, why don't you stay here for a while? That way you can have a rest before continuing your journey." Mike was a bit scared, but he decided to stay with Andy. Mike asked Andy, "What is the secret of victory?" Andy smiled and said, "Stay calm and believe in yourself. Then victory will never be far away." Mike thanked Andy and ran off towards his goal, feeling very determined.

Mike kept believing in himself and he eventually reached his goal. When he did, he was rewarded with victory! Mike had learned an important lesson and he was now much less troubled. He realised that with a little bit of faith and courage, he could achieve anything.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy liked to pick flowers in the park. He would pick red flowers, yellow flowers, and even blue flowers. Timmy loved flowers because they smelled nice and looked pretty.

One day, Timmy saw a big post in the park. The post was very tall and had a sign on it. The sign said "Keep the park clean". Timmy thought it was a normal post, but he didn't know what "clean" meant. He asked his mom and she told him that it meant to not leave trash in the park.

From that day on, Timmy made sure to pick up any trash he saw in the park. He wanted to keep the park clean like the post said. Timmy felt happy knowing that he was helping to make the park a nice place for everyone to enjoy.

Once there was a boy named Jimmy. He was 3 years old and he loved to play. Everyday, he had to choose what to play with.

One day, he chose to play with a baseball. He found the baseball in his room and he was so excited. Jimmy liked playing with baseballs because it was fun and exciting.

But then Jimmy got bored with the baseball. He wanted something more fun. So he chose to go play in the park.

Jimmy went to the park and he found lots of fun things to play with. He played on the swing and the slide. He chased after balloons and played tag with his friends.

At the end of the day, Jimmy chose to come home. He was so happy from having such a fun day. He promised himself to never be bored again.

One day, Annie was playing in her backyard. She was picking flowers, looking for bugs and chasing the butterflies. Everything was so colorful and she felt so happy. Suddenly, she saw a big tree and decided to climb it. She reached the top and it felt like she had reached the sky. She looked down at the ground, which seemed very far away.

Suddenly, she heard her mother's voice.

"Annie, come down from that tree!" Mom said.

Annie was scared and started to cry. All of a sudden, her mom said,

"It's okay. I'll catch you if you fall."

Annie was still scared, but she felt safe. She slowly let go and started to fall, but then felt herself being caught by her mom!

Annie laughed and felt relieved. Mom said,

"Let's go inside and have a nice, colorful day together."

Annie smiled and said, "Okay!"

Once upon a time, there was a big jungle. In the jungle, there lived a large tiger. The tiger was very strong and could run very fast. One day, the tiger saw a ball lying on the ground. He picked it up with his mouth and started to play with it. He threw the ball up in the air and caught it with his paws.

Then, a group of children came into the jungle. They were playing and laughing. They saw the tiger playing with the ball and they wanted to play too. The tiger was happy to share his ball with the children. They threw the ball back and forth, having lots of fun.

Suddenly, the ball went too far and landed in a river. The children were sad because they couldn't get the ball back. But the tiger had an idea. He ran very fast and jumped into the river. He swam to the ball and brought it back to the children. They were so grateful and thanked the tiger. From that day on, the tiger and the children played together every day.

Once upon a time there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside. She especially liked to play in her garden.

One day, while she was playing in the garden, Lily found a bracelet lying on the ground. It was a pretty bracelet with silver and pink stones. Lily stopped to look at it. She paused for a moment and then picked it up.

Lily ran to show the bracelet to her mama. Mama was very happy and told Lily she could keep it forever. "It's so lovely!", said Mama.

Lily started wearing the bracelet all the time. Whenever she felt sad or lonely, she would take a look at the bracelet and feel peaceful.

From then on, the bracelet was very special to Lily. Whenever she wore it, it made her feel brave and happy.

Lila and Ben were twins who liked to play with their toys. One day, they found a big box with many new toys inside. They were very happy and wanted to try them all.

"Look, a doll with long hair!" Lila said, picking up the doll. "She is so pretty. I want to hold her and comb her hair."

"No, I want to hold her!" Ben said, grabbing the doll from Lila. "She is mine. You can have the car."

Lila was sad and angry. She did not want the car. She wanted the doll. She tried to take the doll back from Ben, but he was stronger and pushed her away.

"Stop it, Ben! You are selfish!" Lila cried. "You have to share. Sharing is good. Mommy and Daddy always say so."

Ben did not listen. He hugged the doll and said, "No, she is mine. I don't want to share. Sharing is bad. I want to have all the toys."

Lila ran to Mommy and told her what happened. Mommy came to the box and saw Ben with the doll and Lila with nothing.

"Ben, what are you doing?" Mommy asked. "Why are you not sharing with your sister? Don't you love her?"

Ben looked at Mommy and felt ashamed. He loved Lila, but he also loved the doll. He did not know what to do.

Mommy smiled and said, "Ben, you know that toys are not life. Life is more than toys. Life is love, and love is sharing. If you share with Lila, you will both be happy. And the doll will be happy too. She does not like to be held by only one person. She likes to have friends."

Ben thought about what Mommy said. He realized that she was right. He did not want to be selfish. He wanted to be kind. He wanted to make Lila and the doll happy.

He gave the doll to Lila and said, "I'm sorry, Lila. I was selfish. You can hold the doll. I can have the car. Or we can play with both together."

Lila smiled and hugged Ben. She said, "Thank you, Ben. I'm sorry too. You can hold the doll too. I can have the car. Or we can play with both together."

They hugged the doll and the car and played happily. Mommy was proud of them and gave them a kiss. She said, "That's my good twins. You have learned a lesson today. Sharing is good. Sharing is life."

Once upon a time, there was an ancient kitten. She was very sleepy and was hiding. One day, she peeked from behind a tree. She saw a little girl nearby. The girl was holding a toy mouse. The kitten wanted it! She slowly slinked over to the little girl. The girl smiled and held out the mouse. The kitten started to play with it. They were having lots of fun. Then, the girl noticed it was getting late and had to go home. She said goodbye to the kitten and left. The ancient kitten kept playing with the toy mouse until she fell asleep. She was so happy. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little girl called Sarah. She was very cheerful and full of energy.

One day, Sarah and her family moved into a big house. In the bedroom, there was a huge cupboard full of things.

Sarah was so curious. She opened up the cupboard and there was lots of strange things inside.

"What are these things, mummy?" she said.

"These are things we need to weigh," mummy replied.

Sarah was so excited. She used the scales to weigh all sorts of things in the cupboard. She was so proud of herself. Mummy smiled as she looked on.

After weighting all sorts of items, Sarah put away the things and closed the cupboard. She felt so cheerful and was so proud of herself.

The End.

Once upon a time there was a little boy named Jack. He loved to try new things and never gave up. He just always tried his best.

One day, Jack went to his bedroom to find something fun to do. He saw a big drawer in his room that he didn't remember ever seeing before. He tried to open it, but it was too hard. He kept trying and trying, but he just couldn't open it.

So Jack asked his mom for help. She said, "It looks like that drawer is locked shut, so I might need to use a key." She opened the drawer and what came out was bad. Jack saw that inside the drawer was filled with bad stuff like chewing gum and candy.

Jack's mom said, "No, Jack, this stuff isn't good for you. You can't have it."

Jack was sad. He didn't get to do the fun thing he wanted to do and he also couldn't even eat the bad things inside the drawer. But Jack remembered that he was always trying his best, so he said, "It's ok, Mom. I will try something else." And with a smile, he ran off to find something new.

Dad was in the kitchen. He picked up a big, green melon. He smiled and said, "I wish I could have a piece!" Suddenly, there was clapping. Dad looked around and saw his three year old daughter, Lucy, clapping. She said, "Me too, Daddy!" Dad laughed.

He cut the melon into two pieces. He gave one piece to Lucy and she started to eat it. It was juicy and sweet. Lucy smiled as she took bite after bite.

When she was done, Lucy started to clap again. She said, "Yum, Daddy, yum!" Dad smiled and said, "It was very yummy, wasn't it?"

Lucy felt guilty. She said, "Daddy, I'm sorry. I only ate one piece." Dad was surprised. He said, "Why do you feel guilty?" Lucy said, "I should have left the other piece for you."

Dad laughed. He said, "No need to feel guilty, Lucy. I'm happy that you enjoyed the melon!" Lucy smiled and clapped her hands again.

Lily and Max are friends. They like to play with toys. They have many toys in their room. But they also like to see who has more toys. They are competitive.

One day, they decide to count their toys. Lily says, "I have more toys than you. See, I have one, two, three, four, five, six toys. You have only five toys. I win!"

Max says, "No, you don't win. You forgot to count this toy. It is mine. See, it has my name on it. Max. Now I have six toys too. We are the same."

Lily says, "No, we are not the same. That toy is not yours. It is mine. See, it has my name on it too. Lily. You wrote your name on it with a marker. That is not fair. You cheated!"

Max says, "No, I did not cheat. You gave me this toy as a gift. See, it has a bow on it. You said it was for me. You can't take it back. That is not nice!"

Lily and Max start to argue. They pull the toy from each other. They are angry and sad. They forget to have fun.

Mom hears them and comes to their room. She says, "What is going on here? Why are you fighting over a toy? You have so many toys to share and enjoy. You should be happy and kind to each other. You are friends, remember?"

Lily and Max look at Mom. They look at the toy. They look at each other. They feel sorry. They say, "We are sorry, Mom. We are sorry, friend. We don't want to fight. We want to play. Can we play together?"

Mom says, "Of course you can play together. That is what friends do. But first, you have to say sorry to the toy too. You hurt its feelings. It is a nice toy. It wants to be loved by both of you."

Lily and Max say, "We are sorry, toy. We love you. You are a nice toy. You can be with both of us. We can take turns. We can play with you and with our other toys. We can have fun."

Mom says, "That's good. I'm proud of you. Now, give each other a hug and a smile. And then, let's go to the kitchen. I have a surprise for you. I made some cookies for you. They are yummy. Do you want some?"

Lily and Max say, "Yes, please! We love cookies. Thank you, Mom. You are the best. Come on, friend. Let's go. We are hungry."

Lily and Max hug and smile. They hold the toy and their hands. They follow Mom to the kitchen. They are happy and friendly. They are not competitive anymore.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and run around outside. One day, she went to the mailbox with her mommy to mail a letter to her grandma.

As they walked back home, they passed a big temple. Lily asked her mommy what the temple was. Her mommy explained that it was a special building where people go to pray and be peaceful.

Lily thought the temple looked very pretty and wanted to go inside. Her mommy said they could go another time when they were dressed in clothes that fit the occasion.

Later that day, Lily's friend came over to play. They had a fun time together and played with dolls and blocks. As they were playing, Lily's friend said, "I have to mail a letter to my grandma too!" Lily was excited because she just went to the mailbox with her mommy. She showed her friend how to mail a letter and they both felt proud.

One day, Mary went to the park. She saw a jar on the grass. She was fearful because she had never seen one before. She walked closer to the jar to examine it. She tapped it and heard a thump. It was a little scary, so she jumped back.

She looked around, but nobody was there. She stepped closer to the jar again. This time she noticed something strange. There was a tiny noise coming from inside the jar!

What could it be? Mary was a little bit scared, but very curious. She lifted the jar and looked inside.

What she saw made her smile. It was a little baby bird! Mary was so happy to see it that she forgot about being fearful. She held the jar carefully and went to find its mommy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with blocks and would stack them up high. One day, she found a pile of old, dirty socks that her mom asked her to put in the laundry. But instead of doing that, Lily decided to keep them and use them to make a big tower with her blocks. The socks were so disgusting and smelly, but Lily didn't mind. She was having so much fun building her tower. Suddenly, her little brother came in and knocked over the tower, making all the blocks and socks fall to the ground. Lily was sad, but then she realized that it was okay because she could just build another one.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily's mommy asked her to clean up her room. But Lily didn't want to clean up her toys. She wanted to play with them instead. So, she ran away to the attic to hide.

In the attic, Lily found an old treasure chest. She opened it and found a pale necklace inside. The necklace was so pretty that Lily put it on. But then, she heard her mommy calling her name. She knew she had to go back down and clean her room.

Lily went back down and saw her mommy waiting for her. Her mommy requested that Lily clean up her toys. Lily remembered the pretty necklace she found and decided to make a deal with her mommy. She would clean up her toys if she could keep the necklace. Her mommy agreed and Lily happily cleaned up her room. From then on, Lily learned that sometimes it's better to make a deal than to run away to the attic.

Once upon a time, there was a man who was very fast. He wanted to go the fastest he could. So, he sped around and around the street, like a flash of light. Everyone was so surprised to see the man go so fast. He was like a super speeder!

The man kept speeding around and around the street, but then something happened. He drove a little too fast and slipped on the wet street. He flew off his bike and onto the ground.

The man was sad, but luckily he was fine. Someone saw him fall and helped him up. They told him that he was a really fast rider and that he should be a bit more careful!

The man smiled and thanked them. He was happy to know he was fast and that he was okay. He thanked the nice person and hopped back on his bike, this time going a bit slower. He was too fast for his own good. From then on, the man always rode at a safe speed.

Lily liked yogurt very much. She ate it every day for breakfast. She liked all kinds of yogurt: plain, fruit, vanilla, chocolate. She always asked her mom to buy more yogurt when they went to the store.

One day, Lily went to preschool with her favorite yogurt: strawberry. She was happy to see her friends and share her snack with them. But when she opened her lunch bag, she saw that her yogurt was gone. Someone had taken it.

"Who took my yogurt?" Lily asked, looking around. She felt sad and angry.

"I did," said Max, a boy who sat next to her. He had a big smile on his face and a spoon in his hand. He was holding Lily's yogurt. "It looked yummy. I wanted to try it."

"That's not nice, Max," Lily said. "That was my yogurt. You can't just take things that are not yours. You have to ask first. And you have to give it back."

"But I like it," Max said. "It's so good. Strawberry is my favorite. And yogurt is popular. Everyone likes yogurt. Why should I give it back?"

"Because it's mine," Lily said. "And I like it too. And I value it. It means a lot to me. It makes me happy. You can't just take away my happiness. How would you feel if I took something that you value?"

Max thought for a moment. He looked at his lunch bag. He had a sandwich, a banana, and a juice box. He liked his sandwich very much. It had peanut butter and jelly, his favorite. He would not like it if someone took his sandwich.

"I would feel bad," Max said. "I would feel sad and angry. I'm sorry, Lily. I didn't think about how you feel. I was selfish. Here, you can have your yogurt back. And you can have some of my sandwich too. I want to share with you. I want to be your friend."

Lily smiled. She took her yogurt back. She thanked Max for his sandwich. She gave him a hug. She was happy again.

They ate their snacks together. They talked and laughed. They became friends. They learned to value each other. And they both enjoyed their yogurt.

In a forest, there were two friends, a fox and a bear. Every morning they would dance together in the clearing and enjoy the view. But one day, the fox wanted the view of the forest all to himself. He noticed his friend the bear was being humble and letting him be the leader when they danced. The fox became selfish, and he pushed the bear aside and said to him: "I won't share the view with you any more."

The bear was sad and left without looking back. He went back to his den and felt alone without his friend. But then the birds of the forest gave him an idea. He ran to the clearing the next morning and started to dance. He called to the fox, asking him to join him. In the end, the fox joined him and humbly apologized. They danced together once more, watching the view and being friends.

Lily and Ben went to the shop with Mom. They wanted to buy a chair for their room. Mom said they could choose one chair together.

They saw many chairs in the shop. Some were big, some were small. Some were red, some were blue. Some were soft, some were hard.

Lily liked a small chair that was pink and fluffy. She said, "I want this chair, Mom. It is so pretty and cozy."

Ben liked a small chair that was green and wooden. He said, "I want this chair, Mom. It is so strong and cool."

Lily and Ben did not agree. They both wanted their own chair. They started to argue and cry.

Mom said, "Stop, Lily and Ben. You can only buy one chair. You have to share. Why don't you try both chairs and see which one is more comfortable?"

Lily and Ben tried both chairs. They sat on them and bounced on them. They found out that the pink chair was too soft and the green chair was too hard. They did not like either chair very much.

They looked around the shop again. They saw a small chair that was yellow and striped. It was not too soft and not too hard. It was just right.

Lily and Ben said, "We like this chair, Mom. It is so bright and happy. Can we buy this chair, please?"

Mom said, "Yes, you can buy this chair. It is a good choice. You can both use it and enjoy it."

Lily and Ben smiled and hugged Mom. They bought the yellow chair and took it home. They put it in their room and read books on it. They were very happy with their new chair.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. Lily loved to play outside and explore the world around her. One day, she was walking in the park when she saw a big train. The train was making lots of steam and it looked very interesting to Lily.

Lily walked closer to the train and saw a man with a big hat. The man looked very important and Lily was curious. She walked up to the man and said, "Excuse me, sir. What is this big train doing here?"

The man smiled and said, "We are taking people on a ride. Would you like to come with us?"

Lily was very excited and she requested, "Yes, please! Can I ride on the train?"

The man nodded and helped Lily onto the train. As they rode through the countryside, Lily saw lots of animals and trees. She was very happy and nosy, asking the man lots of questions.

After the ride, Lily thanked the man and said, "That was so much fun! Thank you for letting me ride on the train and for answering all of my nosy questions." The man smiled and said, "You're welcome, Lily. Come back anytime!"

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a toy stove that she loved to play with. She would pretend to cook on it and make brilliant pretend meals for her family and friends.

One day, Lily went to the park with her mom and dad. She brought her toy stove along to play with her friends. They had so much fun cooking pretend food and sharing it with each other. But when it was time to go home, Lily forgot her toy stove at the park.

That night, Lily was very sad. She missed her toy stove and her brilliant pretend meals. Her mom and dad saw how sad she was and decided to return to the park the next day. They looked and looked until they found Lily's toy stove under a big tree.

Lily was so happy to have her toy stove back. She hugged her mom and dad and said, "Thank you for helping me find my stove!" From that day on, Lily made sure to always keep her toy stove close by, and she continued to cook brilliant pretend meals for everyone she loved.

Once upon a time there was a man who owned a cart. He wanted to paint his cart so it would look nice. He put all the paint in the cart and wheeled it to the park.

At the park he saw a group of children who were playing together. He asked if they could help him paint his cart. They said "yes!" and ran to him.

Everyone had fun painting the cart. They were using crazy colours like orange and purple. The children were laughing and singing while they painted.

Suddenly, the sun began to set and the children had to go home. The man finished painting the cart, thanked the kids and said goodbye. But when the man opened the paint cans to tidy up, he saw that all the paint was gone!

The man was angry and very sad. He felt so betrayed by the children. He looked at the cart and all he could see was the crazy colours they had used.

The man never trusted anyone ever again.

Jill and Tom were twins who liked to play in the garden. They had a big sandbox where they made castles and roads and bridges. They also had a small flower bed where they planted seeds and watered them every day.

One day, they saw a new flower in their flower bed. It was big and red and had many petals. It looked like a sun. They ran to see it and touched it gently.

"Wow, look at this flower!" Jill said. "It is so pretty and bright!"

"Yes, it is!" Tom agreed. "It is the best flower ever! It bloomed just for us!"

They smiled and hugged each other. They were very happy and proud of their flower.

But they did not know that someone else was watching them. It was a big bee who liked to collect nectar from flowers. He saw the red flower and thought it was rich and sweet. He flew closer and closer, buzzing loudly.

The twins heard the bee and turned around. They saw him coming and got scared. They did not like bees. They thought bees were mean and stingy.

"Help! A bee!" Jill cried. "He wants our flower!"

"Run! Run!" Tom shouted. "He will hurt us!"

They dropped their toys and ran away from the sandbox and the flower bed. They ran to their house and slammed the door behind them. They hoped the bee would go away.

The bee did not go away. He landed on the red flower and drank its nectar. He did not care about the twins or their toys. He only cared about the flower. He thought it was the best flower ever. It was rich and sweet and bloomed just for him.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play outside every day, and she always hoped for a sunny day. One day, the sun was shining bright and the sky was blue, so Lily went outside to play. She ran and jumped and had so much fun.

But then, the sky turned dark and the wind started to blow. Lily's mom called her inside, telling her it was going to rain soon. Lily was sad because she wanted to keep playing. She looked out the window and saw the rain pouring down. It was not a fine day anymore.

Lily went to bed that night, still hoping for a better day tomorrow. But when she woke up, she saw that her favorite toy had been left outside in the rain. It was ruined and she couldn't play with it anymore. Lily was so sad and cried all day. She hoped for a happy day, but it ended up being a very sad one.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Timmy who loved to play outside. He would run around in the grass and jump up and down. One day, he went to the playground with his mommy. He saw a big slide and wanted to play on it.

Timmy climbed up the ladder to the top of the slide. He felt very happy and excited. But then, he looked down and saw that the ground was very distant. Suddenly, he felt scared and wanted to go back down. But it was too late. He slipped and fell down from the slide.

Timmy was very hurt and had to go to the hospital. He learned that it's important to be careful when playing on the playground. From that day on, he never went on the big slide again.

Once there was a little boy named Jack. He wanted to play a game. He asked his mom to play a game with him. Mom said, "I can't play right now, but I know someone reliable who can."

Jack asked, "Who is it?"

Mom said, "It's your dad."

Jack beamed with happiness and ran to his dad. He asked, "Dad, can you play a game with me?"

Dad said, "Of course, I'd love to! What game do you want to play?"

Jack said, "I want to play hide and seek!"

Dad believed him and they started the game. They played for a while and Jack won every round. He was very happy and his dad was proud of him. Jack felt so loved and he had a lot of fun.

It was late in the evening and Paul was feeling very tired. He stretched his arms and yawned. His mom smiled as she looked out of the window at the beautiful landscape.

"Mom, I'm worried," said Paul.
"There's nothing to be worried about, my little one," replied his mom with a gentle voice.

Suddenly, there was a loud noise outside. Paul and his mom both jumped up in fear. Paul started to cry as he saw two robbers entering the house. Paul's mom pushed him away and told him to hide.

Paul did as he was told and hid under the bed. The robbers were filling their bags with all the valuable items they could find.

Paul was very scared but he stayed hidden until he heard the robbers leaving the house. When everything was quiet again, he got out of his hiding place but he didn't see his mom. Tears filled his eyes as he realised that his mom was gone.

Paul was now all alone in the world and his heart was heavy with sadness.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim found a map in his room. The map was old and had a scary picture on it. Tim was scared but wanted to find out what the map showed.

Tim and Sam moved the leaves and opened the box. Inside the box, they found toys and candy. They were so happy and not scared anymore. They played with the toys and ate the candy. The scary map led them to a fun surprise.
