Lily's mommy had a pink knife to cut the sandwich. But when Lily tried to take the knife, she slipped and fell down. Her mommy quickly helped her up and made sure she wasn't hurt.

After that, Lily's mommy cut the sandwich for her and they had a nice lunch together. Lily was happy to be outside on a sunny day with her mommy and eat a delicious sandwich.

Once upon a time there lived a large bear. He lived in a large forest with lots of trees and animals. One day he was very hungry. He wanted to cook his breakfast. He went to find some food.

In the forest he saw two small rabbits fighting. The bear was sad because he didn't like to see animals fighting. Then he had an idea. He asked the rabbits if they would give him some food in exchange for him helping them. The rabbits look at each other and agreed.

The bear started to explain his plan. He said that he could cook some food for all of them, and then they could eat together. The rabbits were excited about the plan but then one of them said "What happens if we fight again?

The bear smiled and said "That won't happen. When we eat together, it makes us friends." The rabbits thought the bear was right.

After the bear had cooked the meal, they all shared it together. The rabbits were so full and happy. For the first time, they decided to share the food instead of fighting. From that day on, the large bear and the two small rabbits were the best of friends.

Molly and her mom drove to the store. As they drove, Molly looked out the window and wondered what she could see. She saw a big red truck with a shiny license plate. She was curious and asked her mom what the license was for.

Molly's mom smiled and explained that the license was like a pass that proved the truck belonged to the man driving it. Molly nodded, but still wondered.

At the store, they passed a man with a foolish look on his face. He was trying to get inside a car but couldn't find the door handle. Molly giggled and wondered what he was doing.

Molly's mom explained that it looked like the man was having trouble opening the car door. She said that it was a foolish mistake but that everyone makes them sometimes. Molly smiled and they went into the store.

As they walked through the store, Molly still wondered what the license was for and why the man couldn't find the door handle. But she enjoyed her day at the store with her mom.

Once upon a time there was the smartest person in the world. He had an idea but he did not know what to do with it. He wanted to make something extra special, but he didn't know what it should be.

So he asked his best friend for help. "Listen," he said. "I have this idea, but I don't know what to do with it. Can you help?".

His best friend thought for a moment, then said, "Yes, I think I have an idea. Let's try it out".

So the two friends worked together and soon they had created something so wonderful that everyone admired their work. Even the smartest person in the world was impressed with the intelligent idea they had come up with.

The moral of the story is to listen to your friends and be open to new ideas. With a little intelligence, amazing things can happen!

Tom and Lily were playing in the yard. They saw a big barrel with a tap. They wanted to see what was inside.

They turned the tap and water came out. They poured the water on the grass, on the flowers, on the rocks. They laughed and splashed each other.

Then they saw a rainbow in the water. It was incredible. It had many colors: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple. They tried to touch the rainbow, but it moved away.

They poured more water and made more rainbows. They were very happy. They did not see Mom coming.

Mom was not happy. She saw the water and the barrel. She said, "Who poured all the water out of the barrel? That was for the garden. Now we have no water left."

Tom and Lily looked at each other. They felt sorry. They said, "We did, Mom. We wanted to see what was inside. We saw rainbows. They were incredible."

Mom sighed. She said, "Rainbows are incredible, but water is precious. Next time, ask before you pour. And help me fill the barrel again."

Tom and Lily nodded. They said, "We are sorry, Mom. We will help you." They hugged Mom and went to get the hose. They learned a lesson.

Tom was a patient boy. He liked to wait for his mom to read him a story before bed. He picked his favorite book, with pictures of animals and colors. He climbed into his bed and hugged his teddy bear.

"Mom, can you read me this book?" he asked, holding up the book.

"Of course, my sweetie," his mom said, sitting next to him. "Let's see, what is this book about?"

She opened the book and started to read. Tom listened carefully and pointed at the animals he knew. He saw a lion, a monkey, a zebra, and a giraffe. He learned some new words, like elephant and hippo.

He yawned and rubbed his eyes. He was feeling sleepy, but he wanted to hear the end of the story.

"Mom, what happens next?" he asked.

"Well, the animals are all friends and they play together in the jungle. They have fun and they help each other. They are happy and they say good night to each other," his mom said, turning the last page.

She closed the book and kissed Tom on the forehead. "Just like we are happy and we say good night to each other, right?"

"Right, mom," Tom said, smiling. "I love you."

"I love you too, my patient boy," his mom said, tucking him in. "Sweet dreams."

She turned off the light and left the room. Tom closed his eyes and cuddled his teddy bear. He thought about the animals and their friends. He yawned again and fell asleep.

Lily liked to draw with her crayons. She had many colors, but her favorite was yellow. She used yellow to draw the sun, the stars, and the flowers. She was proud of her drawings and showed them to her mom and dad.

One day, Lily found a new crayon in her box. It was shiny and silver, and it had a pointy tip. Lily wondered what it was. She picked it up and tried to draw with it, but it did not make any marks on the paper. She pressed harder, but still nothing. She felt the tip with her finger, and ouch! It was sharp!

"Mommy, mommy, what is this?" Lily cried, running to her mom with the crayon and her finger.

"Oh, Lily, that's not a crayon, that's a needle!" her mom said, taking the needle from her and looking at her finger. "You have to be careful with needles, they can hurt you. They are for sewing, not for drawing. Let me see your finger, I'll put a band-aid on it."

Lily felt sorry and scared. She did not mean to hurt herself. She did not know what sewing was. She hugged her mom and said, "I'm sorry, mommy, I thought it was a crayon. I just wanted to draw something yellow."

Her mom smiled and kissed her. "It's okay, Lily, you didn't know. But now you do. Needles are not for drawing, they are for sewing. Crayons are for drawing, and you are very good at it. Why don't you show me your yellow drawings? I'm sure they are beautiful."

Lily smiled and nodded. She took her mom to her room and showed her her yellow drawings. Her mom praised her and hung them on the wall. Lily felt proud again. She loved her mom and her crayons, and she decided to never touch a needle again.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Jack and Jane. They were playing in the park when they noticed two people in uniforms. "Look!" said Jack. "What are they?"

"They are police," explained Jane.

The two friends were eager to learn more, so they ran up to the people in uniforms.

"Do you help people?" Jack asked the police.

"Yes, we help people day and night," the police replied.

Jack and Jane were very happy to know this. Jack then asked if they could help too. The police said "Yes! You can give us a high five."

Jack and Jane happily high-fived the police and started looking for more people to help. They soon found some people who needed help with moving boxes.

Jack and Jane used all of their strength to help the people move the boxes. The people were very grateful and thanked the two friends for their help.

Jack and Jane returned to the park with smiles on their faces. They had been so eager to help and it felt good.

Tom and Jen are friends. They like to play with toys and make things. One day, they find a big box in the park. The box has a sign that says "lab". Tom and Jen are curious. They open the box and see many things inside. They see bottles, tubes, wires, and lights. They see a button that says "on".

Tom and Jen want to see what happens when they press the button. They press it and hear a beep. Then, they see a green smoke come out of the box. The smoke smells bad and makes them cough. The smoke spreads in the air and covers the park. Tom and Jen are scared. They think the smoke is weird and dangerous. They run away from the box and the park.

Tom and Jen go to their homes and tell their moms what happened. Their moms are worried and call the police. The police come to the park and see the box and the smoke. They wear masks and gloves and take the box away. They say the box is from a bad man who wanted to make trouble. They say the smoke is not good for people and animals. They say Tom and Jen are brave and smart for telling them.

Tom and Jen are happy that the police take the box away. They hope the smoke will go away soon. They hug their moms and say they are sorry for playing with the box. They say they will not touch things that are not theirs. They say they will only play with safe and fun things. Their moms say they love them and they are proud of them. They give them cookies and milk and read them a story. Tom and Jen smile and say they are still friends. They say they will play together again tomorrow.

Once upon a time, there lived a small black cat and a big brown dog who were best friends. The cat invited the dog over for lunch.

"I made you some yummy food," said the cat.

The dog was so excited and couldn't wait to see what it was. He arrived at the cat's house and the cat proudly showed him the lunch she had made - it was a big plate of blackberry jelly!

"This looks difficult," said the dog. "How am I going to eat this?"

The cat just smiled. She had a surprise for her friend - a fork and spoon! Now the dog could eat the jelly with ease.

The lunch was a success. Both the cat and the dog had a wonderful time - and the dog learnt an important lesson too. He realized that when things seem difficult, it's best to have a little patience and be prepared to think up creative solutions.

Once upon a time, there was a little fox. The fox lived in a big forest. The fox was very hungry.

One day, the fox saw a yummy red apple on a tree. The fox wanted the apple. The fox tried to bring the apple down. But the fox was too small.

A tall bird saw the fox. The bird wanted to help. The bird flew up and picked the apple. The bird brought the yummy apple down to the fox. The fox was very happy. They both ate the yummy apple and became good friends.

Once upon a time, there was a little angel named Amy. She had an enormous heart and loved to help others. One day, she decided to travel to a faraway land.

Amy met a sad boy named Tom. Tom looked at her and said, "I lost my toy. Can you help me?" Amy, being a helpful angel, said, "Yes, I will help you find your toy."

They looked and looked until they found the toy under a big tree. Tom was very happy and said, "Thank you, Amy!" The little angel smiled and flew back to her home, knowing she had made a new friend.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved animals and wanted to go on a safari. Her mom and dad said they would take her one day.

One day, while they were at the park, Lily saw a bird with a broken wing. She felt guilty and wanted to help it. She urged her parents to take the bird to the vet.

The vet fixed the bird's wing and Lily was happy. She asked her parents if they could go on a safari to see more animals like the ones the vet helps. They said yes and Lily was so excited! She couldn't wait to see all the animals on her safari.

Once upon a time there lived a little girl. She was three years old and liked purple things.

Every day the little girl would ask her mom for a secret. But her mom just laughed and said no.

But one day the little girl went on a magical journey. She found a special purple box that said 'Add Secret'.

Inside the box, she found a purple secret! She was so excited and quickly added it to her collection.

The little girl added so many secrets to her collection that they filled up the whole purple box! Her mom was amazed when she came home to find all the secrets.

The little girl and her mom lived happily ever after with all their purple secrets!

Lily and Tom are playing with their blocks. They build a big tower with many colors. They are happy and proud of their tower.

"Look, Mommy, look!" Lily calls. "We made a tower!"

Mommy comes and smiles. "Wow, that's a very nice tower. You are very good at building, Lily and Tom."

But then, Max, the dog, runs into the room. He sees the tower and thinks it is a toy. He jumps and barks and tries to catch the blocks.

"Max, no!" Tom shouts. "That's our tower!"

But it is too late. Max knocks down the tower and the blocks scatter everywhere. Lily and Tom are sad and angry. They cry and hug each other.

"Bad dog, bad dog!" Lily says. "You broke our tower!"

Mommy picks up Max and puts him outside. She says sorry to Lily and Tom. She helps them pick up the blocks and put them in a tight box.

"Let's go to the kitchen and have some cookies," Mommy says. "Maybe we can build another tower later."

Lily and Tom wipe their tears and nod. They hold Mommy's hand and take a step towards the kitchen. They still love Max, but they hope he will not break their tower again.

Once there was a clever pig. The pig loved to eat oats. He ate oats for his breakfast, lunch and dinner. He was always happy and he liked to be with his friends.

One day the clever pig was walking through the forest when he saw a team of goats. The goats were lost and very scared. The clever pig thought of a plan.

He said to the goats, "Follow me. I will lead you home. The goats followed the clever pig and he showed them the way home. The goats thanked the pig and he waved goodbye.

The clever pig was glad he could help and he kept walking. He ate some oats and was happy to be so clever.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her toys and eat mint candy.

One day, Mia found a small plant in her garden. The plant had a mild smell, and it was very pretty. Mia decided to take care of the plant and water it every day. She loved to poke the dirt around the plant with her little finger to make sure it was soft and good for the plant to grow.

As the days went by, the plant grew bigger and stronger. Mia was so happy to see her plant grow. One day, the plant had a beautiful flower on it. Mia knew that her love and care helped the plant grow. She learned that when you take care of something, it can grow strong and beautiful. And that was the moral value she learned: to take care of the things you love.

Once upon a time, there was a little prince. He lived in a big castle. The prince loved to talk to everyone he met. He wanted to learn new things.

One day, the prince was walking in the castle gardens. He heard a noise coming from a big open door. He was curious and walked inside. In the doorway, he saw a little mouse. The mouse was squeaking and talking.

The prince was very excited. He bent down to talk to the mouse. 'Hello there! What is your name?' he asked.

The little mouse squeaked and said, 'I am Max. What is your name?'.

The prince told Max his name and said, 'Would you like to become my friend?'

Max giggled and said, 'Yes! I would love to be your friend'.

The prince and Max talked and talked. They talked about their days, the castle, and their adventures. They even sang silly songs together!

The prince and Max became best friends. They played in the castle gardens and talked all day.

The End.

Lily and Ben were friends who liked to play with kites. They had a big red kite that could soar high in the sky. They took turns to hold the string and watch the kite fly.

One day, they went to the park with their kite. It was a sunny day with a lot of wind. They found a good spot to launch their kite. Lily held the string first and let the kite soar. She felt happy and said, "Look, Ben, our kite is so high and fast!"

Ben nodded and smiled. He waited for his turn to hold the string. He wanted to feel the kite soar too. But Lily did not give him the string. She said, "No, Ben, it's my turn. I want to play more."

Ben felt sad and angry. He said, "That's not fair, Lily. We have to share. Give me the string, please."

Lily shook her head and said, "No, Ben, it's mine. Go away."

Ben tried to grab the string from Lily. They pulled and tugged. The string snapped. The kite flew away. They watched it disappear in the sky. They felt sorry and cried.

They lost their kite and their friendship. They learned that being selfish and greedy is not sweet.

Once upon a time there was a really nice girl. She liked to spend her days outside in the sunshine. One day while she was outside she found a wet rose growing in the grass. She was so happy and excited to find it that she started to clap and applaud. The girl picked the rose and carried it with her wherever she went that day. It was really special to her and she smiled every time she looked at it.

The rose kept growing bigger and bigger. Soon it was so big and bright that it lit up the whole street. Everyone was so happy and amazed at the beautiful rose that they started applauding for the girl. She was filled with so much joy that she jumped and twirled around in circles.

The girl was so proud of her special rose and she held it close to her heart. She thanked the rose for being so beautiful and she thanked the sky for the rain that made it so wet. She laughed and swayed in the sunshine as she looked up at the sky and thanked it for the beautiful day that had given her the rose.

Once upon a time, there was a boy who loved to create things. He was always making something new and interesting. One day, the boy wanted to make something extra special. So, he asked his mom for some silver. She gave him some silver and the boy went to work. He worked and worked until he created a beautiful silver crown. He was so proud of his creation, he wanted to show it off.

The next day, the boy and his mom went to a party. Everyone was so impressed when they saw the boy's crown. They asked where he got it and the boy said he'd created it himself. Everyone was amazed and asked if he could create something for all of them.

The boy said, "Yes! I can create something for all of you." He showed them all that he was an amazing creator with an interesting knack for silver. Everyone was so proud of him and the boy was very happy with all of the praise.

Sara and Ben are twins. They like to play and have fun. But today is not a fun day. It is a grumpy day. Sara and Ben are grumpy because they have to stay inside. It is raining outside and they cannot go to the park.

They sit on the floor and look at their toys. They do not want to play with their toys. They are bored. They start to argue.

"I want the red car!" Sara says.

"No, it is mine!" Ben says.

They pull the car and make a loud noise. Mom hears the noise and comes to see.

"What is going on here?" Mom asks.

"Sara and Ben are grumpy," they say.

Mom smiles and says, "I have a surprise for you. Come with me."

Sara and Ben follow Mom to the living room. There, they see a big box with a bow. It is a gift.

"What is it?" Sara and Ben ask.

"It is a gift for you. You can open it together," Mom says.

Sara and Ben are curious. They forget their grumpiness. They tear the paper and open the box. Inside, they find a big tent and two sleeping bags.

"It is a camping set!" Mom says. "You can pretend to camp in the living room. You can make a fire, roast marshmallows, and tell stories."

Sara and Ben are happy. They hug Mom and say, "Thank you, Mom! You are the best!"

They set up the tent and the sleeping bags. They find some sticks and paper to make a fire. They find some marshmallows and a plate to roast them. They find some books and a flashlight to tell stories.

They have a lot of fun. They are not grumpy anymore. They are glad. They share the red car and the other toys. They say, "This is the best gift ever!"

One day, Nathan, a three-year-old, went to the beach with his dad. They played tag, built a sandcastle, and Nathan found many shells. But what Nathan wanted most of all was to lift the big, yellow rock.

Nathan's dad could see the excitement on his face and decided to help his son. Nathan put all his strength into it and with his dad's help they were able to lift the rock! Underneath the rock was a spicy snack! Nathan and dad laughed, and Nathan quickly munched it up.

The next day, Nathan wanted to lift the big, yellow rock again and when they arrived at the beach he ran straight to it. But Nathan and his dad could not lift it this time. Nathan began to cry, asking why they could not lift it. His dad then explained, "If you take things without permission, like a spicy snack, you have lost trust. You must earn other people's trust back.

Nathan nodded and with determination, he worked hard to win back his dad's trust. Soon, Nathan and his dad were able to lift the rock again, but this time there was nothing underneath it. But this time, the feeling of getting to lift the rock was the best reward of all.

The moral of the story is to always do the right thing, even when it is hard. This will win you

Mama was busy cooking in the kitchen. She had a big wooden spoon and a heavy pot in her hands. She put the pot on the stove and crouched down. She stirred the food inside the pot and added a special ingredient.

Mama then called her son over. She asked him to get a few spoonfuls of food from the pot. Then she pointed to the table. She said to her son, "Can you put it on the table?".

The boy grabbed the heavy pot with both hands and carefully moved it to the table. He grabbed his spoon and put the food on the table.

Mama smiled and said, "Well done! Now can you help me with the cooking?".

The boy nodded and started stirring the food inside the pot again. He was so happy to help Mama cook.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Vera was walking in the woods. She saw an oak tree and said to it, "I wish I could climb you!" Suddenly, a young bird appeared on a nearby branch. He said, "I will encourage you and help you reach my nest on the top of the oak."

Vera was so excited and started climbing with the help and encouragement of the young bird. Soon enough, she had climbed all the way to the birds nest. She thanked the young bird and waved goodbye, as she went back home with a big smile on her face.

Vera learned that with a little bit of courage and a lot of encouragement, anything is possible. She now remembers the oak tree in the woods and the courageous young bird.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to perform and sing songs. One day, Lily's mom made her a delicious muffin. It was so yummy! Lily thought it was brilliant.

Later that day, Lily went to the park to perform for her friends. She sang and danced, and everyone clapped and cheered. But then, a big storm came and ruined all of Lily's fun. She got wet and cold, and her friends went home.

Lily was sad and hungry, so she went back home. But when she got there, she saw that her little brother had eaten her muffin! Lily was so upset, she cried and cried. The end.

Sara and Tom were playing in the yard. They liked to build things with wood and nails. They had a big box of nails that they found in the garage.

"Look, I made a house for my doll!" Sara said, showing Tom her work. She had nailed some wood pieces together and painted them pink.

"That's fine, but I made a car for my toy!" Tom said, showing Sara his work. He had nailed some wood pieces together and added some wheels.

They stared at each other's work. They both thought their work was better than the other's.

"Your house is too small and ugly!" Tom said.

"Your car is too big and noisy!" Sara said.

They started to argue and yell. They did not want to play together anymore.

Their mom heard them and came to the yard. She saw their work and their angry faces.

"What's going on here?" she asked.

"Sara and Tom are fighting!" they both said.

"Why are you fighting?" mom asked.

"Because my work is better than hers!" Tom said.

"Because my work is better than his!" Sara said.

Mom shook her head. She did not like to see them fight.

"Listen, Sara and Tom, your work is both fine. You both worked hard and used your imagination. You should be proud of what you made, but you should also respect what the other made. You don't have to like the same things, but you have to be kind and polite. Fighting is not nice. Sharing and helping is nice. Do you understand?" mom said.

Sara and Tom looked at each other. They felt sorry for what they said.

"Yes, mom, we understand," they said.

They hugged and said sorry to each other. They decided to play together again. They showed each other their work and said nice things. They even tried to play with each other's toys. They had fun and learned to appreciate their differences.

Mom smiled and gave them a hug. She was happy to see them get along.

The end.

Once upon a time there was a helpful princess. She really loved to go skipping around her castle. Every morning she skipped through the colourful gardens and waved at the friendly birds.

One day the princess skipped into the bedroom and found that the mattress was missing. She was so sad until a helpful little mouse appeared and said:

"Don't worry, Princess! I know where the mattress is. Follow me!"

The princess followed him down the hallway and around a corner, where she saw the mattress tucked away in a corner.

The princess thanked the helpful little mouse and then jumped onto the mattress and started skipping around her bedroom. She was so happy that she was able to find the mattress to skip on again.

The princess smiled the whole day and thanked the helpful little mouse for his help.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Sam. He was a regular boy who loved summer. Every summer his dad would take him to the park. He would raise his arms in the air and yell as he ran until he felt like he was flying.

Whenever he saw other kids playing on the playground, he wanted to join in. But his dad would raise him up and hold his hands until they got to the other side. He would smile and laugh as his dad raised him in the air and gave him a big hug.

Every summer the two of them would have so much fun together. They would play together, laugh together, and even take long walks together. It was the most regular thing for them to do, but it was always special.

Sam loved summer. He loved the sound of birds chirping, the feel of the warm sun and the smell of fresh grass. Most of all, he loved spending time with his dad and raising his arms to the sky.

Anna and Ben like to play with their toys. They have a lot of toys, but their favorite is a big bear. The bear is soft and brown and has a red bow. They call him Spirit, because he makes them happy.

One day, Anna and Ben go to the park with their mom. They bring Spirit with them, because they want him to see the swings and the slide. They have a lot of fun, and Spirit likes the park too. He sits on the bench and watches them play.

But when it is time to go home, Anna and Ben forget to take Spirit with them. They leave him on the bench, and he feels sad and lonely. He hopes they will come back soon.

The next day, a boy named Sam finds Spirit on the bench. He thinks he is a nice bear, and he wants to keep him. He takes him home and puts him in his room. He does not know that Spirit belongs to Anna and Ben.

Anna and Ben are very sad when they realize they lost Spirit. They miss him a lot, and they cry. They ask their mom to take them to the park, but she says it is too late. She says they have to wait until tomorrow.

That night, Spirit feels a strange feeling in his chest. He does not like Sam's room. He does not like his other toys. They are fake and mean. They make fun of him and his bow. They say he is old and dirty. They say he has no friends.

Spirit wishes he could go back to Anna and Ben. He wishes he could hear their voices and feel their hugs. He wishes he could make them happy again. He wishes he could be their bear.

He closes his eyes and tries to sleep. But he can not sleep. He is too sad. He feels his spirit fading away. He wonders if he will ever see Anna and Ben again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, she went to the library with her mom. There, she saw a new novel with a pretty cover. She asked her mom if they could borrow it. Her mom said yes and they took it home.

When they got home, Lily started to read the novel. She read very fast because she was so excited. As she read, the characters in the book started to appear in her imagination. She imagined a princess and a dragon and a magic castle.

Lily loved the novel so much that she read it every day. She was sad when she finished it, but then she remembered that there were many more novels at the library waiting for her to read.
